# THE BOOKS OF 

GALATIANS, EPHESIANS,

PHILIPPIANS, COLOSSIANS {.heading_16}

# Introduction {#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ80.xhtml_Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ4067 .heading_88}

The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Galatians has 6 chapters, 149 verses, and 3,084 words. It is plainly divided into three sections: I--II, the [Personal ]{.class_4095}section; III--IV, the [Doctrinal ]{.class_4095}section; and V--VI, the [Practical ]{.class_4095}section.

The word[ Galatians]{.class_4095} means "the land of the Gauls." There is considerable difference of opinion about this land, although the arguments amount to little or nothing. North and south Galatia are both in the same sector of Asia Minor, which is not as big as France. North central Asia Minor had real "Gauls" in it (Frenchmen!). The Roman province of Galatia---organized in 25 B.C.---included Antioch, Iconium, Lystra, and Derbe, which Paul visited on his first missionary trip (Acts 13--14). The people here are kin to the Kelts (Celts) whose progenitors are also found in England. The "Gallioe" of Europe found in France and Lombardy were called "Galatia" by the Greeks.

Williams' Commentary (1949) does not hesitate to tell us that the Gauls were emotional and fickle! (Did you ever hear of such "racial discrimination" in all your life? Yes, as a matter of fact we have. It was Caesar who wrote that the Gauls were "fickle" and fond of change and not to be trusted! Didn't Caesar make a mistake? Is this not a "racial prejudice"? And what would you know about it anyway since you didn't deal with them?) Alford quotes Thierry as saying that these Gauls were (among other things) inconstant, fond of show, and perpetually quarreling. Is this true of ALL races? (Now let's see you build a "United Nations" out of that one!) Are the Germans ["inconstant"]{.class_4095}? (By answering "some are and some aren't," what have you proved except that you are a coward?) Are the Japanese ["fond of show"]{.class_4095}? (See detailed comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_4095} Gen. 10.)

The date of the epistle is fairly uncertain. Writers' conjectures vary between A.D. 51 and 58. Acts 15:35 is the best possible choice, which would place the date of writing at A.D. 51. The book is written []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ80.xhtml_page_5}AFTER the council of Jerusalem, which is apparent by the remarks in Galatians chapter 2. It is certainly written more than fourteen years after Acts chapter 9. If Paul was saved in A.D. 36 (three years after the Resurrection and Ascension), he wrote Galatians in A.D. 51 or (if we count the "[three years]{.class_4096}" of Gal. 1:18 as an addition to the "[fourteen]{.class_4096}" of Gal. 2:1) in A.D. 54. Dr. E. F. Harrison ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, p. 1283) settles for A.D. 53. Alford cites Galatians 4:13--16 as proof that Paul had already been to these churches twice, but a reading of the passages shows that Paul is referring to his ONE visit. Alford must alter ["at the first"]{.class_4096} to ["at the former time"]{.class_4095} to prove his point. As we have remarked above, this is the unfailing mark of identification for the private interpreter: he must alter the text to prove his theology (see [Rome---The Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_4095} 1970). Dummelow settles for A.D. 55 or 56, and C. I. Scofield pushes it clear out to A.D. 60. Conybeare and Howson settle for A.D. 57--58.

The Epistle to the Galatians is unique on three counts.

1\. Paul did the actual writing himself (Gal. 6:11), whereas he used an "amanuensis" (ain't that a dilly!) for the other letters, writing only the salutation in his own hand.

2\. No "church" is addressed. There are only ["the churches"]{.class_4096} which are addressed (Gal. 1:2).

3\. No one addressed is referred to as a "saint" (Eph. 1:1) or as being ["in God"]{.class_4096} (1 Thess. 1:1) or ["of God"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 1:2).

Because of this third characteristic, one will find verses in the epistle that refer to LOST people as well as to SAVED people. Note Galatians 3:4 and 5:4.

As Romans proves that a man is justified by faith for salvation, the Book of Galatians proves that a man is justified by faith AFTER salvation. Romans is written to counteract the heresy of Acts 15:1, while Galatians plainly answers the doctrines of Catholicism, Seventh-day Adventism, and other Arminian groups as they first appear in Acts 15:5.

None of the major commentators, of course, dare make ANY application of the great truths of the book. It is apparent to a Christian who has served time on the "battlefield" in "hand-to-hand combat" with the enemy that Galatians 1:8--9 damns to Hell the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ80.xhtml_page_6}majority of ordained priests and ministers in any country in the world at any given time in history. But the "commentators" are in the selling business to a large extent, so we should not be surprised to find such passages as Galatians 1:8--9 and Galatians 3:10 and Galatians 5:14 being treated "theologically" in their "doctrinal content" as "related to the great truths of the Gospel imperative," etc., instead of being [explained ]{.class_4095}and [applied]{.class_4095}. The reader will observe that none of the commentators are afraid to APPLY the rules of Greek grammar to the Bible in order to refute the English text, but when it comes to [applying the TRUTH]{.class_4095} to their readers, they are quick to remind us that they are "commentators," not [preachers]{.class_4095}. The only "preaching" that Williams, Clarke, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Bullinger, and E. Huxtable ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}) do is lengthy dissertations on what is wrong with the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 and why their opinions are superior to its text. To those of us who have been "blooded" on a "regimental combat team," this hypocrisy is highly amusing. Nothing could be funnier (or more pitiful) than men correcting God's Authority with the same breath that refuses to correct [heresy]{.class_4095}. If you cannot be specific in applying a text to a contemporary evil, how do you fancy that you can be specific enough to correct the text as it stands? Do you think you are fooling God with this nonsense---or what (Gal. 6:7)?

Luther called the Epistle to the Galatians his "Katherine" (Katherine was his wife). Luther lived and died in Galatians and Romans. (This probably explains why modern Christianity tries to live in Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews.) Galatians is a "near kinfolk" of Romans. The two epistles state the correct Christian doctrines for salvation as they are to be believed and taught until the Rapture. The present departure of the "Christian Church" from correct doctrine to "practical Christianity" and "the universal message of God's love" and "a sweet spirit of ecumenicity" marks the end of Biblical Christianity as the Bible presents it. The Liberals apostatized (1850--1900) under the influence of Rationalism and Deism (1700--1850), but since then the Conservatives have apostatized (1901--1970) under the influence of Christian "education" and the blind guides who rejected the Textus Receptus for the corrupt Alexandrian counterfeits (1850--1930). The greatest apostasy in America before []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ80.xhtml_page_7}the Advent of Christ will be among the members of the Body of Christ who profess to believe in "the fundamentals of the faith."

Nowhere in the New Testament is the truth brought home with more power and more venom than in Galatians (Gal. 1:8--9, 2:16, 3:3, 4:15--16, 5:7, 12, 6:3, 12--13, 15). One can almost hear the rattle of the quill on the papyrus and see the blazing eyes and the knotted forehead of the "[apostle]{.class_4096} [of the Gentiles"]{.class_4096}(Rom. 11:13). There are moments of weeping and tenderness (Gal. 4:5-- 6, 12, 14--15, 5:22--25, 6:1, 10); but the letter is a far cry from any sermon that Weatherhead, Peale, Fosdick, Buttrick, Coffin, or Bishop Pike ever preached. Lurking behind every exhortation, one sees the shadow of the "Master" (who made some "comments" in Matt. 23:1--33!).

Commentators and writers who are obsessed with the "Pauline Complex" (see [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} Note 96, Chap. 6, 1970) have a hard time watering down the "original Greek" to reinstate Paul as a "nice Christian." No "nice Christian" would dare say Galatians 1:8--9, and that is why most Christian educators today never SAY IT. Their incomes depend upon "being nice."

Readers of [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4095} are quite familiar with the attempts of the critical commentators to "dress God up" so he will not offend "important people" at a religious gathering. The originators of this clothing establishment lived in Alexandria, Egypt (A.D. 20--350); we are deeply indebted to them for a "respectable" and "safe" Christianity which is about as Biblical as "The Sun and the Umbrella" (Nels Ferre).

We shall now prepare for the feast! "Come, for all things are now ready!" The table is fully set (Matt. 22:4), the Master is ready to serve (Luke 12:37), and He will sup with us (Rev. 3:20) on this august occasion if we allow Him to do so! Let us sit joyfully and attentively in His presence and eat what He serves us (John 21:9)---without complaining about the cooking (Num. 21:5)! While others starve trying to dissect the meat, peel the potatoes, flavor the salad, heat the tea, clean the silverware, fold the napkins, and look for spots on the drinking glasses, we shall ["eat, O friends; drink, yea, drink abundantly, O beloved"]{.class_4096} (Song of Sol. 5:1)! His words shall taste better than honey (Psa. 19:10) and be more nourishing than []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ80.xhtml_page_8}our necessary food (Job 23:12).

Where Bullinger, Clarke, Dummelow, Jamieson, Henry, Huxtable, Fausset, Lipsius, Ellicott, Brown, Alford, Chafer, Harrison, Simpson, Westcott, Herklotz, Lange, Meyer, Kennedy, Lightfoot, Neal, Michael, Muller, Custer, Vincent, Beare, Dibelius, Hanson, Rendall, Masson, Hogg, Burton, and Ridderbos correct our Holy Bible, we shall (with charity!!) correct them, giving thanks to the same God for equal enlightenment and treating them with the same tolerance with which they treat the Reformation text of God's immortal, infallible, eternal Word.

"Whom shall he teach knowledge? And whom shall he make to understand doctrine? them that are weaned from the milk, and drawn from the breasts\...for by strength shall NO MAN prevail"[ (Isa. 28:9; 1 Sam. 2:9).]{.class_4096}

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml}

# Chapter 1 {.heading_116}

1:1 Paul, an apostle, (not of men, neither by man, but by Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead;)

2 And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:

3 Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,

4 Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:

5 To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen

Most of the salutation is self‑explanatory. Paul is an apostle (not a "messenger" as in Phillip's corrupt translation, 1958), and he always mentions this in any letter where he is writing to someone who is questioning his authority in matters of doctrine (Rom. 1:1; 1 Cor. 1:1; 2 Cor. 1:1; Eph. 1:1; and Col. 1:1). To call the reading "messenger" a translation into "modern English" is nonsense. The word [apostle ]{.class_4095}is still used profusely in the Twentieth Century by radio, TV, newspapers, and magazines. The word [messenger]{.class_4095} no more conveys the sense of the Holy Spirit than a word like "errand boy" or "courier."

Paul's apostleship was by direct revelation (Acts 9:15), and he was chosen by divine appointment in a special sense that not even Peter, James, and John were privileged to share.

["Not OF men, neither BY man, but BY Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). Christ chose the "[twelve]{.class_4096}" (Matt. 10:1--3) when He walked the earth in the flesh, and they were called to follow a Jewish Messiah (Rom. 15:8) who came primarily to minister to Israel (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew, ]{.class_4095}1970: Matt. 3, 10). Paul was chosen by the risen Saviour to follow the God of Israel and minister to the Gentiles (Acts 9:15; Rom. 10--11), and he began a worldwide ministry that is not over yet. His apostleship had no human source (["not of men"]{.class_4096}), nor did it reach him through human instrumentality (["neither by man"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_10}), but it came, rather, from and through Jesus Christ (["but by Jesus Christ")]{.class_4096}. The alteration of the word ["of"]{.class_4096} to "from" and the alteration of the word "[by]{.class_4096}" to "through" by E. F. Harrison [(Wycliffe Commentary) ]{.class_4095}is entirely out of place and means nothing. The text is quite clear as it stands. The corrupt [RV]{.class_4095} (1881--1885) has done the same thing by altering "[by man]{.class_4096}" to "through man," and then "[by Jesus Christ]{.class_4096}" to "through Jesus Christ." These corrections, as all corrections, have no significance whatsoever. The ["dia" is ]{.class_4095}used for both clauses (["BY man" ]{.class_4096}and ["BY Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096}), so at the start the serious student of the Bible should adjust himself to the style of his critical professors. They are NOT going to "go by the Greek" unless they go by it to correct the English. Their "more accurate translations" are nothing but the recorded whims of hypercritical humbugs, and no real scholar could be deceived by them for a minute.

The interpolation "not sent" found in the Roman Catholic "bible" [(Confraternity Revision, ]{.class_4095}1941) is not found in any Greek manuscript in the world. It is a reading quite typical of Roman "scholarship."

The reader should observe that both the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901---or any other edition) and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) have altered the Reformation Greek Text and substituted for it the conjectural emendations of Westcott and Hort. All the translations from the Receptus manuscripts read as the [King James ]{.class_4095}(see [Geneva Bible, ]{.class_4095}1560; [Bishop's Bible, ]{.class_4095}1568; Tyndale, 1525; and the ["Great" Bible, ]{.class_4095}1539).

["Who raised him from the dead" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1). Paul was chosen as an apostle AFTER the Resurrection, whereas the original twelve were all chosen BEFORE the Resurrection. Although Matthias (Acts 1:26) is chosen after the Resurrection, his selection is plainly indirect and can, in a sense, be said to be ["of men"]{.class_4096} or[ "by man." ]{.class_4096}The Resurrection is mentioned in Galatians 1 before Paul has dipped his pen twice in the ink well; for it was the risen Saviour whom he met on the road to Damascus, and it was the risen Saviour (2 Cor. 5:16) who occupied his heart and mind until the day that Nero dropped Paul's head into a basket (2 Tim. 4:1--8).

["And all the brethren which are with me"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2). The "[brethren]{.class_4096}," here, are unidentified but could be any of the brethren of Acts 15 or 16 (see remarks on the date of the writing of Galatians). []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_11}Timothy, Gaius, Aristarchus, Sopater, Secundus, et al., do not show up until Acts 16--20, so those who accept a later date for the writing can include these in the salutation. (A date of A.D. 54 or later is accepted by the Roman Catholic Church. See page 511, [Confraternity Revision, ]{.class_4095}1941.)

["Unto the churches of Galatia" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2). The epistle is not addressed to the "Body of Christ" or to saints or even to Christians; it is addressed to all the local assemblies in Galatia (cf. Rom. 16:16). As such, it contains warnings and rebukes for unsaved people as well as for saved people (see 1 Cor. 16:1). Simon Peter ministers to these same churches (1 Pet. 1:1), in spite of the insistence by Bullinger that Peter and Paul have ministries that are completely separate. The careful reader, who rightly divides "[the word of truth]{.class_4096}," will observe Paul ministering to Jews as well as Gentiles (Acts 13, 18, 28), and Peter ministering to Gentiles (Acts 10) as well as Jews (Acts 2; Gal. 2).

Verse 3 is self‑explanatory.

["Who gave himself for our sins" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). Before commenting on the passage, the student of Greek should make a note of the second phenomenon which commonly occurs in the works of those who hate and despise the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611. Here the Receptus manuscripts give ["huper" ]{.class_4095}(Gk.) for the word ["for." ]{.class_4096}However, Tischendorf, Lachmann, and Tregelles have altered this Greek text to read ["peri" ]{.class_4095}(Gk.). Now, the thing that is "interesting" (scholar's cliché) about this matter is that the word ["huper" ]{.class_4095}is found in the Vatican manuscript ("B"), which was used by the revisors of the [RV]{.class_4095} (1885), the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1952), the [New English Bible]{.class_4095} (1970)---and any other fifty translations at random---to correct the Reformation Bible. On many occasions the testimony of ["B"]{.class_4095} alone was taken to be of such tremendous authority that it was allowed to overrule every other manuscript from every family of manuscripts extant (see absolute proof of this in chapters 3--5 of [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970). Now where [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}([B]{.class_4095}) agrees with the [Authorized Version]{.class_4095} of 1611[, ]{.class_4095}the scholars suddenly decide that it is in error (!), and Bullinger slyly states, "[the texts read 'peri'" (Companion Bible,]{.class_4095} pg. 1749). Well, Doctor, what happened to Vaticanus? Is it not a "text"? Weren't you taught all the way through seminary that Nestle's ["text" ]{.class_4095}was the most reliable? Nestle's []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_12}["text" ]{.class_4095}gives the precedence to Vaticanus, and Eberhard told you that on page [68 ]{.class_4095}of his Introduction!

1\. First phenomenon: alter the English even if the Greek backs it up (see vs. 1).

2\. Second phenomenon: use any manuscript convenient to change the text and throw out your own authority if it agrees with the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611!

(Do you see why there is a need for a [Bible Believer's Commentary]{.class_4095}?!)

["Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present EVIL WORLD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). The text of the Holy Bible---[AV]{.class_4095} 1611---has so worded the passage that any reader can make a present application to his own age and time.

The commentators and revisers have tried desperately to "cool" the text, but it is still hotter than a depot stove. The text, as it stands in the Bible authorized by the Holy Spirit, states the following:

1\. Christ voluntarily gave Himself up to atone for our sins.

2\. He did this so that we could be delivered from the world system in which we live, which is inherently and intrinsically [evil ]{.class_4095}(see comments under Gen. 8:21 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}1970).

This is the negative world view which is found throughout either Testament, in any set of manuscripts. It is not this "big, new, beautiful, wide, wide, wonderful, modern world," but ["this present evil world." ]{.class_4096}The Greek [(poneros) is ]{.class_4095}worse than "bad"; it means downright and outright [wickedness.]{.class_4095}

Now, it is this negative worldview that is so strongly resented by unregenerate scientists, educators, and international Socialists. (The entire book called [The Sure Word of Prophecy \[The Kingdom of God Versus The Kingdom of Heaven,]{.class_4095} 1969\] is devoted to an analysis of this subject.) All evolutionists think [positively]{.class_4095} when dealing with men and history. All Bible believers think [negatively ]{.class_4095}when dealing with the same subjects. All evolutionists (Communist or Catholic) believe in the "potential goodness" of man and the "potential" possibility of "peace on earth" without the\
miraculous intervention of God Almighty dealing with man and history [directly. ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_13}Communists and Catholics (and all evolutionists) resort to "means" and "ways" of attaining peace on earth or eternal life in glory, and the great truths of depravity (Rom. 1--3) are neither investigated nor discussed. On rare occasions when the subject comes up, it is "hush‑hushed" immediately and tempered with some statement such as "but if a man avails himself of the means of grace" or "but if you live up to the light which you have" or "but if we each try to understand each others viewpoint," etc. The truth of the matter is this:

1\. Men are evil (Luke 11:13).

2\. Their hearts are evil (Mark 7:21).

3\. Their nature is evil (Eph. 2:1--3).

4\. Their world is evil (Gal. 1:4).

5\. Their god is evil (2 Cor. 4:4).

Now, these statements alone are enough evidence to consign the writers to perdition in the eyes of deadorthodox Christians; they are enough to consign the writers to a concentration camp in the eyes of a Socialist; and they are enough to consign the writers to a mental ward in the eyes of a psychiatrist. The writers of those words, however, are not the author of this commentary. The writers were Luke, Mark, and Paul---three of the greatest Bible‑believing Christians who ever lived. The statements in Luke and Mark are the heart convictions of the Author of Biblical Christianity. Rome ducks!

The [Confraternity Revision ]{.class_4095}(or the [Rheims Douay, ]{.class_4095}1750) states that Christ only died to save us from the "wickedness of this present world" (i.e., the world is all right! There are just some bad things [in it).]{.class_4095}

Durnmelow flinches!

The worthy doctor tells us that Christ died only to recover us from the evil that ["characterizes" ]{.class_4095}this present world! (Commentary, pg. 947).

J. B. Phillips says that the Greek here [(aion) ]{.class_4095}means "world order," but Dr. Harrison [(Wycliffe Commentary) ]{.class_4095}insists that it can only have a reference to "time." The Rev. Frederick Rendall [(The Expositor's Greek Testament,]{.class_4095} pp. 150--151) tells us that "the world" meant the world which Jesus found existing at the time of His coming.

Having heard the opinions of the Bible rejecters, let us return to the truth.

The entire testimony of the entire Bible would clearly teach that in ANY age, under any condition, at any time after Genesis 1:1, the world is "[evil.]{.class_4096}" Even when Christ is on earth reigning in the flesh with His saints, evil is present (Isa. 26:10; Psa. 110:1--6), and that age will end---as all others---in apostasy (Rev. 20:6--10).

The attempts by the scholars to placate the Devil and lull the Christian to sleep in this century, are not appreciated by the Lord or by any consecrated Christian. This age, as the age of Christ's ministry (Matt. 12:32) and the Tribulation (Matt. 24:3) and the Church Age (Matt. 28:20) and Paul's age (Rom. 12:2) and Adam's age (1 Cor. 2:7) and John the Baptist's age (Heb. 9:26) and any other age, is EVIL.

No age is without evil until Revelation 21:1 (see 2 Pet. 2:12--13). No matter how many times the commentators dodge behind the "Greek" to prove (or suggest) that Galatians 1:4 was a past reference to a past event, the truth of Scripture stands. ["This present evil world" ]{.class_4096}is a reference to 1970--2009, and if your "Greek" doesn't bring that out, you'd better let him go back to cooking in a restaurant. (The remarks by Alford, Trench, and the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}on the subject are not even related to present day Christianity.)

["According to the will of God and our Father" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). Most of the new "Bibles" change the structure of the Greek text to read as Phillips and the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901): ["our God and Father."]{.class_4095}

Now this is not the [literal Greek, ]{.class_4095}nor is it true to the "original Greek" or the "Greek original." The sentence structure of every Greek manuscript ever found (Alexandrian, Byzantine, Caesarean, or Western) reads as the [King James 1611 Authorized Version, ]{.class_4095}so here, the serious student of the Scripture should note the third phenomenon which characterizes the demoniac attack of Twentieth‑Century Conservatives on the word of God.

Phenomenon 3. While criticizing the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 for not rendering a true translation of the Greek Receptus from which it came, the critics never fail to give a false rendering of their own texts if it is convenient.

By now, we have observed three outstanding qualities of modern []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_15}Conservative "scholarship." The reader should learn these and memorize them for future use. When Bible critics desire to destroy the word, they appeal to CLARITY when they refuse to translate accurately, and then they appeal to ACCURACY when the passage needs clarity. (As we proceed through Galatians we shall learn more about the hypocrisy and inconsistency of these "accredited scholars" whose sole purpose in life is to usurp the authority of the Holy Ghost.)

The first Bible to fail to translate the Greek properly here was the Latin Vulgate of Jerome. One will find his error in the Roman Catholic [Confraternity Revision, ]{.class_4095}carefully preserved in the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}(1963) and other Roman Catholic "Bibles."

["To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). The glory belongs to God (1 Sam. 6:5), and where this glory is slighted (Num. 14:22), it departs (1 Sam. 4:22). The glory is never to be given to a Greek lexicon or to a Greek scholar when it comes to matters of interpretation and understanding (Luke 24:45). The Holy Spirit is our teacher (John 14:26), and He will never fail to give the glory to Jesus Christ (John 16:13) and the Father (Col. 3:17).

1:6 I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:

7 Which is not another; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert the gospel of Christ.

8 But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.

9 As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.

10 For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

11 But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.

12 For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.

13 For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews' religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:

14 And profited in the Jews' religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.

15 But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother's womb, and called me by his grace,

16 To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:

Here follows one of those great passages in the Bible that ranks with Genesis 3--4, 12, 22, 24; Exodus 12, 20, etc. The words are plainly the wellsprings of the apostle's heart and soul, and he wastes no time in choosing words or trying to adjust himself to his reader. The passage is the vehement outpouring of a man who is indignant, disturbed, concerned, and jealous of his commission and his charges. It is the truth pitched over the center of the plate waist high, and it demonstrates vividly the difference between the sermon of a God‑called man filled with the Spirit and a man‑called man filled with institutionalized learning. (A man who means what he says and says what he means never sounds like most of the 50,000 writers on the Bible you ever read.)

When a man is hungry, he suddenly has "convictions" about food. When a man is dog‑tired, he has "convictions" about sleep. No living man on this earth who has convictions about the truth ever speaks or writes as the ["]{.class_4095}[scribes and Pharisees.]{.class_4096}["]{.class_4095} Even the Pharisees spoke with more conviction than Kenneth Wuest, Dr. Thieme, and Marshall Neal, when speaking of holy things. Who ever heard of a hungry man saying: "My dear spouse, I do not wish to appear negative or critical in my approach to the culinary processes of this household, but I presume that the absence of victuals on the dining room table is ample evidence that\...." No hungry man ever talked that way in his life. You may as well face it: most of the writers who try to classify themselves with Paul (see "The Pauline Obsession," Note 96 on Chap. 6, [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_17}1970) don't have enough Pauline convictions to sail to Cyprus (Acts 13:4).

["I marvel that ye are so soon removed\...unto another gospel" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). He had gotten a report "by way of the grapevine." The work of Acts 13 and 14 (see especially Acts 14:21--25) had been leavened with the advice of Judaizers (see exact context in Acts 15:1, 5), and Paul now writes (see notes on date of writing in the Preface) to straighten out the young churches.

Scholars make much of the word ["another" ]{.class_4096}in verse 6, but none of them are able to give the believer any more sense from the changes than he can already find in the [Authorized]{.class_4095} text. The meaning is plainly this: "You are listening to something somebody calls 'the Gospel,' but it is NOT 'the Gospel.'" To illustrate the fantastic "foul up" that critics can get into, one should study Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown's Commentary (pg. 1259), where the writers go into great length to explain that the Greek for ["another"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) is not the same word as ["another"]{.class_4096} in verse 7 and that we should be careful to notice that the meaning is a "different" Gospel. Having explained themselves, the writers then turn about‑face in verse 8 and tell us that ["any other gospel"]{.class_4096} is no reference to verses 6 and 7, for in verse 8, it means any "gospel" BESIDES the one you heard preached!

But isn't it amazing how clear verses 6--8 are in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text?

1\. You were exposed to ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4096} (Acts 20:24), and you accepted this Gospel as the truth (vs. 6).

2\. You believed this message to be from God the Father (vss. 4, 6) and accepted it as a work of grace on His part even to let you hear it.

3\. Somebody preached something else to you after I left (Acts 14:23--15:5) and called it "the Gospel," but it was a perversion of the Gospel (vs. 7).

4\. It is a perversion that is characterized by a low‑rating of grace (vs. 6).

5\. If you hear anyone else preach it, including an angel, allow the Lord to damn him good and proper (vs. 8).

Phillips is quite wrong in translating "travesty" for ["pervert" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 7).

The [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) is quite wrong in translating ["removed" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_18}(vs. 6) in the middle voice (with Wahl, Alford, and others), since someone else is the instrument of removing them (see vs. 7).

The [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}nearly has a heart attack at ["accursed" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8) and substitutes for it an expression that neither Paul nor the Holy Spirit had in mind at the time of the writing: ["shall be held outcast" ]{.class_4095}(typical "whitewashing" of a text so that it will not offend polite eyes and ears).

["As we said before, so say I now again" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 9). The reference is used by Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and Rev. Rendall [(The Expositor's Greek Testament, ]{.class_4095}1961) to prove that Paul is referring to some sermon he delivered orally to the Galatians on a previous trip. But the "epistolary plural" (hot dog!!) is used much in Paul's writings (2 Cor. 10:1--4, 13:4, 6--7, 9; 1 Cor. 15:15, 19, 32, etc.).

Paul did NOT tell anyone at Galatia the words of verse 8 when he was with them, for he did not receive those convictions about the Gospel which he "preached among the heathen" until AFTER Galatians chapter 2. You do not find one word in Acts chapters 13 and 14 about a man being damned if he preaches "[any other gospel]{.class_4096}"; as a matter of fact, "[any other gospel]{.class_4096}" does not show up until Acts 15:1. If the "[we]{.class_4096}" were Paul and Barnabas, then Barnabas is found also in Galatians 2:1 getting things straight. An appeal to "[say]{.class_4096}" can't straighten out the problem, for Paul uses "[say]{.class_4096}" for his writings---["so SAY I now again"]{.class_4096} (vs 9). The Judaizers of Acts 13:45, 50--51 were not "perverting the Gospel of the Grace of God"; they were "[contradicting and blaspheming]{.class_4096}" (Acts 13:45).

But we are only toying with the passage. The meat of the passage does not lie in the revisions or in the commentaries; and the changes in the text offered by DeWette, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Alford, Meyer, Wordsworth, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Durnmelow, Bullinger, and Huxtable [(Pulpit Commentary), ]{.class_4095}do not really shed any light on anything except their own estimation of their own abilities.

What the text actually says is this: every preacher, Bishop, Pope, Priest, and minister in the United States who is teaching salvation (or justification) by works---in this age---is damned to Hell.

(Now you didn't find that in the "Greek," did you?!)

If I am making the passage a little too clear, turn to page 152 of [The Expositor's Greek Testament (Vol. ]{.class_4095}III) and see how to handle the passage in a "scholarly fashion" so that no one will get upset. Observe the masterful manipulation of adjectives so that the culprit of verses 8 and 9 does everything EXCEPT go to Hell. It is true that he finds "spiritual death" and "separation from Christ" and loses "Christian blessings" and the "means of grace," but somehow or other thirty years of study in the "original languages" doesn't enable a man to say that the culprit of verses 8--9 burns in the Lake of Fire (Rev. 20:15). What do you suppose is missing in a "Christian education" that so handicaps a Pauline believer that when he follows Paul in the "Heavenlies," he cannot expound Paul's epistles even with the help of the "original"? The "original" says:

1\. Every Pope who ever taught that a man was saved by faith AND works was damned.

2\. Every Church of Christ minister who taught this was damned.

3\. Every Jehovah's Witness, Mormon, Pentecostal preacher, Church of God elder, Methodist, Lutheran, and Episcopal preacher who ever taught it was damned.

Or if they were not actually "damned," that great, educated, refined, sweet, delightful, cultured, dedicated apostle of the Gentiles desired their damnation! The same man who wished them[ "accursed" ]{.class_4096}nearly wished himself[ "accursed"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 9:3)! The trouble seems to be with the word "damn." All of the commentators prefer the milder "condemn." Williams is undoubtedly right in saying that the expression of verses 8‑9 is a PREDICTION as well as a PRAYER.

Now, we have a problem. How do we apply the text?

Was John Wesley (1703--1791) accursed? He certainly preached salvation by faith and works, although the initial salvation was by grace through faith. Wesley, as Peter Cartwright, C. H. Morrison, Culpepper, and Asbury, taught that the believer must work to "endure to the end" (Matt. 24:13) and that anywhere along the road he could lose salvation as quick as Pope John XXIII (1410--1415) lost it. All Episcopalians and Catholics are Arminian in their theology, and none would say that a man is kept by grace after he is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_20}saved, even though he might be saved "by grace through faith" (Eph. 2:1--10).

Now here, having faced the real difficulty in the text, we are in a position to arrive at the real truth of the text, and of course, this truth will be found entirely independently of ALL the Conservative and Fundamental critics of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text. Their lexicons and dictionaries will avail them nothing when asked for the meaning and the truth of the words.

1\. The Gospel, here, is the "good news."

[2. ]{.class_4095}It is the "good news" of Acts 13:38--39, which was preached in Galatia. This is the good news that is reconfirmed by Simon Peter at the council in Jerusalem (Acts 15:11). Regardless of Paul's advanced revelations concerning the life of the believer AFTER he is saved, the message that leads to his salvation is the Gospel referred to in Galatians 1:8--9.

3.[ ]{.class_4095}This exempts the old‑time Methodists from the curse of the passage, at least where any of them preached the blood atonement and the free gift of eternal life by the grace of God. However they may have gotten Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews boggled in their [heads, ]{.class_4095}the Lord blessed their lives and ministries when they preached John [3:16 ]{.class_4095}from the heart---and 99 percent of them did just that.

4\. However, where the "plan of salvation" preached is that if you do this and this and this and this, and keep on doing this, you might be saved, the preacher is clearly damned slap through and through (Matt. 23:15).

To avoid this murderous approach, the commentators have devised "ways and means."

a\. The text is a reference to a historical situation that existed in the past and no longer can be found.

b\. The[ "accursed" ]{.class_4096}is only a desire which God would properly ignore.

c\. If the passage means what it says, you have no right to apply it.

d\. Passages this offensive are only ["Pauline theology]{.class_4095}," not ["the word of God."]{.class_4095}

But the words of LeRoy Wright are appropriate at this point (LeRoy Wright is an obscure independent Baptist preacher in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_21}Mississippi): "If you find that word offensive, you'd better build a fence 'round it." (Or as Dr. Bob Jones Sr. said, "If you don't like what Paul wrote, get on the telephone and call him up in Heaven and tell him about it!")

What the "original Greek" is trying to get across (that has been obscured in the commentaries) is: ["If]{.class_4095} [you tell any sinner there is anyway to get to Heaven outside of the shed blood of Jesus Christ and the free gift of God's grace, God is going to damn you to Hell." ]{.class_4095}Is that clear? Do you want it any clearer? Do you suppose that a study of Trench's "synonyms" will make it any clearer? How clear can you get?

So at the start of the "Epistle of the Apostle Paul to the Galatians," we should have the modern picture of Paul erased from our minds forever. Paul is a vicious, intolerant bigot of the worst sort; his manner of speech is totally lacking any "Christian" love or warmth; and he is as devoid of "the sweet spirit of Christ" as Elijah or John the Baptist. (Would you invite a man to your house for dinner who had already cheerfully damned three‑fourths of the ecumenical council in your hometown?) Alexander Campbell said the "good news" was that if you repented, confessed, believed, were baptized, and held out to the end, you'd make it. I wonder where Alexander is now, if Paul meant Galatians chapter 1 [literally? ]{.class_4095}Every Pope since Innocent I (what a name!) has taught that if you believed, confessed, took the sacraments, obeyed the church, and lived a good life, you would make it. I wonder what ever became of old "Innocent"? Ellen G. White and Judge Rutherford still teach (by their writings) that a man must keep the Ten Commandments (plus some other things) to get to Heaven. What ever became of Ellen and the Judge?

["Though we, OR AN ANGEL FROM HEAVEN\...let him be accursed"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). Paul says it twice (vs. 9) so you can't miss it.

Now, an angel DOES preach "another gospel" in Revelation 14:6, and since all of the commentators missed it fumbling around in their lexicons, it might do well for us to turn to the passage and read it. (It is quite discouraging at times to read the commentators when you know, before hand, that none of them are going to be able to tell you what the Scriptures say about the passage they are discussing! See comments under Eph. 3:18, 4:11). Revelation 14:6 shows clearer than ever the reason why the believer should rightly divide []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_22}["the word of truth,"]{.class_4096} for that angel DOES preach "another gospel," and he is NOT ["accursed."]{.class_4096} He is plainly preaching to another group in another dispensation, and this means that Peter is perfectly in line in preaching WORKS for receiving the Holy Spirit (Acts 2:38), even though this is NOT the "gospel" of Galatians 1:10--14. There are plainly [three gospels ]{.class_4095}between Matthew 10 and Acts 15, and God honours all three of them. Each one is aimed at a different group, and it is not until Acts 15 that everyone officially states that the "Gospel" for [this age ]{.class_4095}is ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4096} (Acts 15:11). Bullinger confounds the time of this revelation with the written record which describes Paul's knowledge of it (see Eph. 3:1--6 and comments).

From Acts 8:35 to the Rapture there is one "good news," and it is not the good news preached by the Communists, Catholics, or educators.

This "good news" is defined in 1 Corinthians 15:1--4. It is not the "good news" that Huxley and Paley preached, nor is it the "good news" that Darwin and Hegel preached, nor is it the "good news" that Lenin and Trotsky preached, nor is it the "good news" that Dewey and Russell preached, nor is it the "good news" that is now being preached by anyone who took any of those men seriously. If the Pauline anathema is inspired, we can understand why modern science, religion, philosophy, and education have no use for the Bible. The clear statement is: ["If ANY MAN preach any other gospel unto you\...LET HIM BE ACCURSED" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 9).

If an angel preached, he could not deliver any greater truths than those found in the book of Romans. If an angel preached, he couldn't prove his own deity as well as Jesus proved His. If an angel preached, he could not make men repent (Rev. 9:21) any more than Jesus did (Luke 13:3). If an angel preached, he could never do as much for the sinner as Jesus did (Phil. 2:6--9) because angels have no blood (Heb. 2:7, 9, 14).

All of the commentators sidestep the cross‑reference in 2 Corinthians 11:4. In that passage will be found not only ["another gospel"]{.class_4096} but ["another Jesus"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}and ["another spirit"]{.class_4096}\![ ]{.class_4095}But there, Paul is speaking sarcastically, and he is actually blaming the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_23}Corinthians for listening to the "other gospel." The gist of the verse is "there is a good possibility that you would go along with another gospel and bear up with those who preached it, so how come you don't tolerate me the same way and listen to me?" No curse is pronounced on those who HEAR ["another gospel,"]{.class_4096} but the damnation is clearly placed on the ecumenical leaders who have been preaching it since the ecumenical council (the "National" Council---formerly "Federal" Council) was founded in the early 1900s.

["For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please MEN? for if I yet pleased MEN, I should not be the servant of Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10). With such an opportunity for "exposition," it is a tragedy that the commentators waste their time on the Greek words in the passage.

Here is a passage that fairly breathes the spirit of Biblical Christianity for nineteen centuries. The verse explains the lives, motives, and actions of every martyr who ever died for Christ; and it explains why men like Billy Sunday, J. Frank Norris, Bob Jones Sr., Sam Jones, Charles G. Finney, Hubmaier, Storch, Stubner, Franke, Zinzendorf, Boehler, Spangenberg, Peter Cartwright, and Mel Trotter will never receive the publicity which the world so gladly allots to Napoleon, Washington, Lincoln, Churchill, Tarzan, Roosevelt, Hitler, Bronco Nagurski, Fulton Sheen, Bishop Pike, Johnny Cash, Lyndon Johnson, Arnold Palmer, Castro, Raquel Welch, Jean Harlow, Bing Crosby, Charlemagne, DeGaulle, Charles Darwin, Mickey Mouse, Pope Paul, Joe Namath, Alexander the Great, Peanuts, Nero, Ted Kennedy, Jack Kennedy, Lil' Abner, and Thomas Aquinas (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy, ]{.class_4095}Bible Baptist Bookstore, 1969).

No man seeking to "persuade men" goes around cursing religious leaders (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew, ]{.class_4095}Matt. 23:1--33 and comments). No man seeking to ["persuade men"]{.class_4096} goes around telling them that they are so rotten there is nothing they can do to save themselves (Gal. 6:12--14). A man who is trying to[ "persuade men" ]{.class_4096}that he himself is ["some great one"]{.class_4096} (Acts 8:9--10) spends his time flattering men (Acts 24:2--8), bragging about the "greatness of man" (cf. Psa. 49:16--20), and adjusting his "convictions" to his audience whenever the situation changes. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_24}("Situation Ethics" have been practiced by politicians more than thirty centuries before any immoral graduate of Columbia University wrote any book about it.)

Paul is obviously NOT a politician (2 Cor. 11:6, 10:10). Paul was in the same class with Elijah and Jeremiah when it came to "pleasing men."

If a Christian is a "man pleaser," he is a God displeaser (James 4:4).

The passages in Romans 15:2 and 1 Corinthians 10:33 have nothing at all to do with the context of Galatians 1. The former passages are discussing the maintaining of a careful testimony before the unsaved and deliberately foregoing "liberties" that might offend a weaker brother in Christ. As far as the MESSAGE is concerned (1 Cor. 1--3), it was preached without fear or favor (Acts 20:27) to any and all; and where it resulted in stoning and lashings (2 Cor. 11:23--28), it was never compromised for anyone (Gal. 6:12).

Human approval never meant anything to Paul (Gal. 2:6) or Jesus Christ (Matt. 22:16). It meant nothing to Luther or John Wesley, and very little to any real Christian who obeyed the first commandment (2 Cor. 10:12--18). Men who are "running for office" or trying to maintain their social image or building religious monuments to prove their "dedication," have to duck, dodge, adjust, compromise, twist, turn, feint, slip, and "finagle" to serve the Lord; but the Christian who is obeying Hebrews 12:1--3 just sails on through without looking to the right or the left. If anything gets knocked down in the way, the Lord has to pick it up.

[Carelessness ]{.class_4095}is not the theme song of dedicated Christianity (Eph. 5:15), but [carefulness in the delivery of the truth]{.class_4095} is not found anywhere in either Testament. Christ makes no effort at all to adjust His message to His listeners (Matt. 23:13--26), nor does He say anything to "influence them to be good Christians." Jeremiah is blatant (Jer. 23), Micaiah is brutal (1 Kings 22), Amos is obnoxious (Amos 7:14--17), Peter is fierce (Acts 5:3--9), Stephen is outrageous (Acts 7:48--53), and Elijah is vicious (1 Kings 18:27). You cannot find the Twentieth-Century type of Christianity in the Bible. The modern "day school," "Christian education," "refined spirit of cultured nicey‑wicies" is foreign to the Holy Spirit. This is obviously []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_25}the work of frightened, nervous, carnal, worldly, money‑obsessed egotists who are trying to worship a WORK instead of God (see comments under Exod. 20:3 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus).]{.class_4095}

You cannot serve God and mammon; therefore, ["the servant of Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10) never gets involved in the business of seeing how many friends he can make or how many people he can placate before they become his enemies. A manpleaser is a God-displeaser (vs. 10).

The [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}(1961), which gave us so much static on "archaic words," has revived the outmoded "currying favor" (vs. 10) for ["do I seek to please men."]{.class_4096} Exactly how "currying favor" is making the passage clearer is a little hard to see, so we now observe point No. I (see Preface) illustrated beautifully. You see, "archaic words" were never an issue; the hypocrites who translated these new "Bibles" simply pretended they were an issue to get you to abandon your authority! Notice that "archaic words" appear with perfect regularity in the [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}(1961)---["interlopers"]{.class_4095} (Gal. 2),[ "abettor"]{.class_4095} (Gal. 2), ["codicil"]{.class_4095} (Gal. 3)---while the publicity campaigns that sell this type of trash go right on talking about "updating the archaic language of the [King James Bible"! ]{.class_4095}But some of us know a soap ad when we see it.

The revisers of the correct Greek text have omitted the word ["for"]{.class_4096} from verse 10, where it reads ["FOR if I yet pleased men."]{.class_4096} This correction, found in the Alexandrian manuscripts, breaks the rhythm of the passage, which is plainly ["For do I now\..." "for if I yet\..." "For I neither received\..." "For ye have heard\...]{.class_4096}" (vss. 10--13). Paul's style is characterized by the repeated use of the word ["for"]{.class_4096} (Gk., ["gar"]{.class_4095});[ ]{.class_4095}and the omission, here, means nothing except that Tischendorf, Lachmann, Tregelles, Westcott and Hort, and Nestle made as many changes in the Holy Bible as they thought they could make and get away with.

Paul could have been called a man pleaser, so he says, ["For do I NOW\...for if I YET pleased men" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10). Of course, even in these days (vs. 13), he professed to be following the light God gave him, according to his conscience (Acts 23:1), and his appeal to the Galatians must be understood in the light of sarcasm. He is saying, "Do you think I'm talking this way because I'm trying to make []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_26}friends? Even if you thought I was running a popularity contest when I was a Pharisee, you sure couldn't accuse me of it NOW! If I were still acting like you thought I was, I wouldn't be telling you what Christ told me to tell you!"

(Phillips, Williams, and Durnmelow do not comment on the significance of the ["yet"]{.class_4096} in the verse.)

Ephesians 6:6--7 drive home the verse and show that even where slaves obey their masters, they are to do it ["as to the LORD" ]{.class_4096}and seek to please HIM by their obedience (see also 1 Tim. 6:1--5). "Manpleasing" is out, and this means that the basic, fundamental approach of modern Christianity is false. First Corinthians 9:19 has been used by this whole "new generation" of "new Fundamentalists" to negate Galatians 1:10, but in 1 Corinthians 9:19 Paul is plainly talking about his CONDUCT, not his MESSAGE or his PREACHING. The preaching that worries about offending polite ears, fashionable whims, fastidious tastes, effeminate socialites, political and racial minorities, and carnal Christians is a God‑dishonoring, blasphemous insult to the Holy Spirit and the word of GOD.

["The servant of Christ"]{.class_4096} is never concerned primarily about the Sunday School attendance, the "higher income brackets" in his church, "tiptoeing through the tithers," the rich "prospects," or the favor of the newspaper in the town in which he lives; he seeks to please God. If God's good pleasure makes him offensive to the community in which he lives, he makes no adjustment whatsoever. Ian Paisley is worshipped by many a "Christian" who would avoid him like the plague if Ian lived in his home town. Men like Jack Hyles are most appreciated by people who do not have to be connected with his church. (We have Christians in Pensacola, Florida, who would pay a high honorarium to hear these men speak, but they would renounce them publicly and privately if they had to be involved in their ministries.) You can always spot the ["men-pleasers"]{.class_4096} among the Conservatives. They never commit themselves orally or legibly to any Bible truth that will affect their income, attendance, or social image.

["But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not AFTER MAN. For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but BY THE REVELATION OF JESUS CHRIST" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_27}(vss. 11--12).

Paul's revelation of the "grace of God" came to him before Acts 15, and the decisions of Simon Peter (Acts 15:11) and James (Acts 15:19) were "late" in the eyes of the apostle to the Gentiles. He was preaching "grace" in the synagogues in Acts 13:39 in Galatia, and ["the dispensation of the grace of God"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 3:2) was delivered to him long before Bullinger imagined (see comments under Eph. 3:1--6). Furthermore, verse 23 shows that ["the faith"]{.class_4096} was being preached BEFORE Acts 9. The message of Isaiah 53 expounded by Philip (Acts 8:30--37) was grace, pure and simple. The eunuch's baptism was no more connected with his justification than that of the Gentile Philippian jailor (Acts 16:30--34).

Peter, James, and John taught Paul nothing about the revelation of the Grace dispensation (Gal. 2:1--6). The Lord Jesus Christ Himself gave Paul this message (Acts 26:16); and Peter, James, and John merely assent to the truth of it in Acts 15:23 and Galatians 2:9.

The corrupt Catholic Bible [(Confraternity Revision, ]{.class_4095}1941) amends its own text (originally the [Rheims Vulgate ]{.class_4095}of 1582) and translates "a revelation" for ["THE revelation." ]{.class_4096}The corrupt [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) omits ["a"]{.class_4095} and ["the,"]{.class_4096} while the corrupt [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) follows the Roman Catholic tradition of ["a."]{.class_4095}

On this verse we see phenomenon No. 4 (see vs. 1), for here good old ["dia"]{.class_4095} pops up again. This time Dummelow and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown translate it as---guess what!---"BY"! This is the same ["dia" ]{.class_4095}that was translated as ["BY"]{.class_4096} in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 in Galatians 1:1, and it is the same ["dia" ]{.class_4095}that the [RV, ]{.class_4095}ASV, [RSV]{.class_4095}, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Dummelow, Bullinger, and everyone else said means "through."

Now make a note of that and don't forget it. And the next time some puffed-up idiot tells you that the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 has not translated its own Receptus "uniformly," call it to his attention that no translator ever translates his manuscript uniformly. If he professes to do so, he is a LIAR (John 8:44!).

Paul certifies that his Gospel is not a man‑made thing, nor is it something that any man passed on to him. ["THE revelation" ]{.class_4096}(correct text) of verse 12 was a definite, specific revelation about the matter which the Lord Jesus Christ gave to him directly at a certain time. If the[ "the"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_28} is not in the Greek text, it certainly should be there in the English translation; and if that seems like "subjective" emendation of text, let it be remembered that every Bible corrupter since 1880 freely emended the text, adding articles and taking them away any place he felt like it. If there is any doubt in your mind about this, study the perversions, mistakes, mistranslations, contradictions, and deliberate alterations of the [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881--1885) text of Westcott and Hort in Matthew 1:1, 11, 24, 2:1, 13, 17--18, 22, 3:8, 16, 4:6, 19, 24, 5:18, 22, 34, 39, 6:4, 8, 24, 30, 7:13, 8:5, 30, 9:1--2, 9, 13, 16, 27, and the other 3,000 places. In these places the serious student of Scripture---who has better sense than to believe that any Bible since 1611 is "more accurate"---will find that words have been added, subtracted, and altered from the Greek text, genitives have been exchanged for possessives, tense and voice have been ignored on the grounds of expediency, tense and voice have been ignored on grounds of "stress," impossible translations have been invented which contradict the text, conjectures of lexicographers have been taken to be fact, alternate translations were chosen to purposely confuse the text, aorists have been translated in the imperfect, words have been borrowed from other accounts and inserted into the text, and "usage" has been alternately accepted and rejected on the grounds of "contemporary usage" and "ancient usage."

Now, this was all done with the most pious "front" imaginable. Where a real Bible believer accepts the article ["the"]{.class_4096} in Galatians 1:12 (even though it is not found in the Greek), the Bible perverter will accept an [RV, ]{.class_4095}an [RSV, ]{.class_4095}an ASV, or a [New ASV ]{.class_4095}that has invented whole clauses missing from the Greek and omitted whole verses that appear in the Greek. Then after doing this the contemptible charlatan will have the gall to say that the article ["the"]{.class_4096} should not be in Galatians 1:12.

If John Wilkes Booth had criticized Lincoln for wearing a top hat, he would have found excellent company among the faculty members of the Conservative Universities in America today.

The Greek original manuscript has ["THE revelation of Jesus Christ."]{.class_4096}

How do we know this? The same way that the conceited asses who criticize the [AV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_29}1611 know that it doesn't!

["THE revelation of Jesus Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12) is clearly the subjective use of ["of"]{.class_4096} (not the objective, as in "love of the truth," the "fear of God," etc.); and although the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}goes to great length (Vol. 20, pp. 19--21) to prove that the verse should match verse 16, the context of verses 8--9 shows that the ["revelation"]{.class_4096} was the revelation of a MESSAGE, not a MAN as in verse 16. Paul got his revelation in a "conference" (vs. 16), not the minute he hit the dirt outside Damascus. To generalize the ["revelation"]{.class_4096} to mean that when Paul had Christ revealed to him he picked up this unique Gospel, entirely overlooks the fact that Paul submitted to Old Testament water baptism as a purification for sins (Acts 22:16) three days after Christ was "revealed to him." The Lord revealed Himself to Paul in many ways (2 Cor. 12:--4), on many occasions (Acts 26:16), and by many means (Acts 27:23). The revelation of ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4096} was on one of these occasions, and it took place before Acts 15 and after Acts 9:1--6.

["For ye have heard of my conversation in time past\...I persecuted THE CHURCH OF GOD" ]{.class_4096}(vs 13). The word[ "conversation"]{.class_4096} is defined in 1 Peter 3:1--4; James 3:13; and Philippians 1:27 as something that can be SEEN. Those who desire to "update" the word usually give us "slime of the purslane" [(RSV, ]{.class_4095}Job 6:6) or "Waheb in Suphah" [(RSV, ]{.class_4095}Num. 21:14) or "satraps and prefects" along with it.

["The church of God," ]{.class_4096}wherever it occurs in the Pauline Epistles (1 Cor. 1:2, 11:22, 15:9, 10:32; 2 Thess. 1:1; 1 Tim. 3:15), is always a reference to the Body of Christ as distinct from Jew or Gentile. This church was in existence before Acts 9, for Paul persecuted it (text) and ["wasted it."]{.class_4096} You will observe that the expression ["church of God" ]{.class_4096}appears in the prison epistles (A.D. 68) long after Ephesians had been written, and it appears in Corinthians (A.D. 57) long BEFORE Ephesians was written (A.D. 61).

This turns out to be "most embarrassing" for the "Bereans" who follow the teachings of Stam and Bullinger. The "church of the body mystery" (Eph. 3:1--6) cannot be ["the church of God,"]{.class_4096} according to Stam and Bullinger. Although these men differ in where they wrongly divide the book of Acts, neither will allow []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_30}["the church of God" ]{.class_4096}of 1 Corinthians 10:32 to be the "church of God" of 1 Timothy 3:15. To Stam and Bullinger there is a Jewish ["church of God"]{.class_4096} that is not the ["body,"]{.class_4096} and then there is a Jew‑Gentile ["church of God"]{.class_4096} that IS the ["body"]{.class_4096} (see lengthy comments under Eph. 3:1--8).

Stam has the "mystery Body" begin in Acts 9; Baker has it begin in Acts 13; O'Hair has it begin in Acts 18; Bullinger has it begin after Acts 28; and if you like, you can make it begin at Matthew 26, John 20, or Acts 2, depending upon how much Scripture you accept or reject!

Now, three facts are clear, and these three facts unseat the teaching of any "body mystery" man who rejects the clear testimony of Ephesians 2:16--20.

1\. Paul persecuted Jesus Christ Himself when he persecuted the Christians of Acts 5‑9 (see Acts 9:4).

2\. The apostles [in this church ]{.class_4095}were one unit "in Christ" and were one with the Father as one Lord, one God. They were put into Christ (John 17:21, 23) with one baptism (Matt. 20:23) apart from water baptism (Eph. 4:5). These apostles were IN Christ and part of Christ (John 17:26).

3\. This ["church of God" ]{.class_4096}had in it Jews (Acts 2:1--36), Gentiles (Acts 6:1), and Jewish proselytes (Acts 2:10); and regardless as to whether or not anyone in it knew of its organic reality as a "Body" (Eph. 3:1--5), it was certainly THERE.

Bullinger, Stam, and O'Hair, as all Hyper‑Dispensationalists, could never get it through their heads that a thing can be present even if it is unrevealed. Every follower of these men in any country makes the same mental error. He assumes that a thing cannot have [existence ]{.class_4095}until it is [revealed ]{.class_4095}(see comments under Eph. 3:1--6).

Paul had plainly persecuted believers "in Christ" before he was ever converted himself.

["In the Jews' RELIGION"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13). The word ["religion" ]{.class_4096}only occurs five times in the Bible. The three Greek words for it are properly translated by the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 translators. The reader may understand that the corrupt rendering of Acts 17:22 in the [RV, ]{.class_4095}ASV, [RSV, NEB, ]{.class_4095}and the rest, is the attempt of a Greek Gnostic philosopher to alibi his ignorance. The translators of these Bibles, as their Greek []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_31}ancestors, were fickle and superstitious (Acts 17:21--22) and not the least bit "religious" except in the bad sense.

1\. Acts 26:5---Orthodox Judaism is a religion.

2\. James 1:26--27---["Religion" ]{.class_4096}is the performance of acts of piety.

3\. Galatians 1:13--14---Orthodox Judaism is a religion.

Nowhere in the Bible does the word ["religion"]{.class_4096} ever refer to salvation, the New Birth, Christianity, or regeneration. The term is one which can be applied to any set of moral standards adopted by any group of unsaved men who are seeking to justify themselves by self‑righteous works (Isa. 64:6).

When Karl Marx said that "religion was the opiate of the people," he told the truth. Undoubtedly the great "religions" of this world have done more to damn mankind than any other factor outside of science and philosophy (1 Tim. 6:20; Col. 2:8). The first thing from which any man must be saved is his religion (Phil. 3:4--8); so Paul is careful to tell you that the ["straitest sect"]{.class_4096} of orthodox Judaism (Acts 26:5) was "manure" in God's sight (Phil. 3:8), in spite of the "modern English" of the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961) which tones the ["dung" ]{.class_4096}down to "garbage." The quickest ways to Hell are Catholicism, Protestantism, Buddhism, Taoism, Confucianism, Mohammedanism, Rosicrucianism, Communism, Mormonism, Seventh-day Adventism, Socialism, Spiritism, Hinduism, Shintoism, and Judaism. Liquor, murder, immorality, and stealing may be more convenient, but they certainly are not as effective. [God had a lot more trouble saving Paul than he did Mephibosheth.]{.class_4095}

Paul now explains what he meant by ["conversation in time past"]{.class_4096}: ["being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). Paul was a sure‑enough ["Hebrew of the Hebrews"]{.class_4096} (see comments under Phil. 3:4--7), and he practiced what he preached (Acts 7:58). Commentators and revisors who have the Pauline Obsession (see Note 96, Chap. 6, [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970) often forget this and hustle Paul into the company of men like Augustine, Barth, Brunner, Tillich, Calvin, Aquinas, Hodges, Warfield, and Leslie Weatherhead without realizing that they are teaming Joe Louis up with Tiny Tim, Norman Vincent Peale, Edgar A. Guest, Albert Schweitzer, and the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_32}Marx brothers.

Paul [killed ]{.class_4095}people (Acts 26:10).

Paul [tortured ]{.class_4095}people (Acts 26:11).

Paul made Christians [curse ]{.class_4095}God (Acts 26:11).

He didn't approve of the Inquisition as Thomas Aquinas (1225--1274) did, he WAS the Inquisition. It would take ten men like Augustine to fill Paul's left sandal, and Barth and Brunner could not have scratched his instep standing on a stepladder.

["Zealous of the TRADITIONS of my fathers" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14; cf. Mark 7:1--13). Paul didn't eat pork (Lev. 11:4--7), he polled his hair without cutting it (Lev. 19:27), he tithed and gave alms (Matt. 23:23), and he obeyed the regulations of the Law (see Phil. 3:4--6). To him, Jesus Christ was an illegitimate bastard, and His mother was a harlot, and His father was a fornicator (John 8:41). He did not hesitate to state this publicly and privately, and he acted on it with the same religious zeal as that of his contemporaries (John 16:2--3; Matt. 27:63). There was no "middle ground" with Paul. Paul would not know "middle ground" (or "ethnic dialogue") if you plastered him with chunks of it as big as a bowling ball. Nowhere between Romans and Philemon are we ever given the picture of a moderate, temperate "mediating theologian," who "reaches his contemporaries" with a "meaningful dialogue" which seeks to "communicate relative values." Paul slams and damns (Rom. 14:23; 1 Cor. 11:29) right and left. He corrects, rebukes (Tit. 1:13), exhorts (Tit. 3:10), brags and boasts (2 Cor. 11:16--28), roars and raves (Rom. 1--3), and carries on like a combination freight train and intercontinental ballistic missile.

If the tradition said "do it," he did it. If the tradition said "believe it," he believed it. If Paul had been a Catholic before his conversion, he would have worn the ashes right smack in the center of his forehead all day "Ash Wednesday" and would have been proud to have worn them. He certainly would not have shrunk from this tradition as Clare Booth Luce, the members of the Kennedy family, and other squeamish, lukewarm, compromising, insincere "Catholics" do.

The ["traditions" ]{.class_4096}were added to the Mosaic Law, and even today, Jews raised on the Babylonian Talmud consider them to be as []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_33}inspired as the Old Testament.

The student of Garner Ted Armstrong---the magazine salesman---should observe that the Old Testament Mosaic Law is called ["the Jew's religion," ]{.class_4096}not the religion of "Judah"; and the oracles were given to the Jews (Rom. 3:1--2), not to "Judah." The anti‑Semitic ideas propounded by [The Plain Truth ]{.class_4095}were the staple diet upon which Adolf Hitler was raised.

The foundation of these ideas is the non‑scriptural, non‑Christian, non‑biblical, non‑factual error that the word [Jew ]{.class_4095}applied only to the tribe of Judah or the two "southern tribes." The word ["Jew"]{.class_4096} includes Simon Peter (Gal. 2:14), who was from GALILEE!

By now, the reader should have learned something about commentators and the truth. The major commentators have agreed on two things:

1\. They will say nothing really nasty or critical about each other and will never apply any truth negatively to one of their competitors.

2\. They will all correct the Holy Bible every chance they get and will create opportunities where they are not apparent.

It is a kind of "mutual honor among thieves" that we find among the critics of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611. You will not be surprised to find that they are highly indignant about any plain application of the plain truth where it deals with THEM. No indignation is ever aroused over perverting the living word ["of the living God"]{.class_4096} (Jer. 23:36).

["But when it pleased God, who separated me FROM MY MOTHER'S WOMB, and called me by his GRACE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15). Williams jumps the gun on Calvinism, and in the mad rush to make the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 say something that neither the Greek nor the English says, he tells us that the expression proves that Paul was "elected" before he was born.

Phenomenon 5. Where all the Greek manuscripts go against the subjective theology of the commentator or revisor, he simply lays his lexicon aside and makes no reference to the Greek at all!

The Greek word for ["separated," ]{.class_4096}here, is ["aphorizo,"]{.class_4095} as it is found in Matthew 25:32; Luke 6:22; and Acts 13:2, 19:9. Not once in either Testament does the word have reference to any [eternal act ]{.class_4095}or []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_34}decree, nor does the text say that Paul was separated BEFORE he was born (see Jer. 1:5). He was separated [when ]{.class_4095}he was born (["from my mother's womb"]{.class_4096}), and he was not "elected" until he "put his hat in the ring." No "election" is eternal, and the word never occurs anywhere but in a time setting (see detailed comments under Eph. 1:4--5 and in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_4095}Exod. 3:19). Williams foolishly calls verses 15 and 16 the foundation verses for the doctrine of election, but of course the word [election]{.class_4095} appears nowhere in the chapter (Gk. [eklektos, ekloge, suneklekte, ]{.class_4095}etc.).

1\. It pleased God to reveal His Son "in Paul" (text) when Paul changed his mind about God's Son (Acts 9:6) and cried, ["Lord, what wilt thou have me to do?"]{.class_4096}

2\. Furthermore, God's Son was revealed ["FROM HEAVEN"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 15:47) before He ever got ["in"]{.class_4096} Paul.

The picture of an automatic salvation, given by Williams, Calvin, Berkhof, Hodge, Dabney, Kuyper, and Bullinger, is not in the least "Scriptural." The Lord appeared twice to Solomon (1 Kings 9:2), and he did nothing about it (1 Kings 11:6). The Lord appeared to Balaam (Num. 22:20) and got no results whatsoever (Jude 11). The Bullingerites and Hyper‑Calvinists would have you think that Jesus suddenly popped up inside Paul's body without his permission and then overcame his will by "irresistible grace" so that he was "born again" without consent! But this is private interpretation. Furthermore, it would be rather stupid to compare the salvation of any Christian from A.D. 70 to 2009 with Paul's conversion experience. God doesn't shoot intercontinental ballistic missiles at jaybirds. Paul was a chosen vessel for the greatest miracle‑working ministry in the Bible outside of the Lord Jesus Christ Himself (Rom. 15:16--19). It would be rather presumptuous to say that every saved sinner was "separated from the womb" in that fashion.

["That I might preach him among the HEATHEN" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16).

Everybody flips.

According to Harrison, Huxtable, Bullinger, the [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881), the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946), Phillips (1958), and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961), the word ["heathen" ]{.class_4096}is highly distasteful and should be altered to ["non‑Jewish" or "Gentile." This ]{.class_4095}is the Roman Catholic reading of Jerome's Latin [Vulgate ]{.class_4095}as found in the [Rheims ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_35}edition of 1582 (and restored to the [NEW ASV ]{.class_4095}by the same type of people). As a matter of fact, the Europeans and Americans who fouled up the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 deeply resent the word ["heathen" ]{.class_4096}being applied to them, so they have erased it everywhere it appears (see Matt. 6:7, 18:17; Acts 4:25; 2 Cor. 11:26; and Gal. 2:9, 3:8)! The Bible perverts who white‑washed this plain English seem to like to pretend that the word ["heathen" ]{.class_4096}can only refer to some South African tribe or some Maori Islander. The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text is correct here, and the motive behind the change to "Gentile" is quite apparent to anyone who has any dealing with the type of proud, self‑inflated stuffed‑shirts that one often finds in "accredited circles." The "heathen" live in New York, Chicago, London, Berlin, Paris, and Los Angeles, as well as in the Congo and New Guinea. Some heathen are civilized and others are not; some are educated and some are not; and some believe the Bible and some do not. The word ["heathen" ]{.class_4096}is an excellent translation of ["ethnos," ]{.class_4095}and in the context it is much preferable to the Roman Catholic reading ("Gentile") preserved in the corrupt ["New" ASV ]{.class_4095}and similar apostate publications.

["To reveal his Son in me" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16). It never pleases God to reveal His Son in anyone until they have believed on Him (John 3:36). The revelation of Christ in the heart of the believer (2 Cor 4:6; John 4:14) always follows conversion (Eph. 5:8) and never precedes it. Verses that thoroughly explain this verse are found in 1 Timothy 1:16; 2 Corinthians 13:3; and Acts 26:17--20.

["Immediately I conferred not WITH FLESH AND BLOOD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16). The last four words are not an accidental placement of nouns to describe the order of Paul's revelations; they are a direct reference to Matthew 16:17 and show clearly that Paul is even with Peter when it comes to revelation. Paul replaces Peter in the book of Acts (note Acts 12 onward) as the "chief apostle" (1 Tim. 1:16). Paul's miracles match Peter's perfectly, as they are recorded in the book of Acts (e.g., Acts 3:1--8 cf. 14:8--10, Acts 5:15--16, cf. 19:11--12, Acts 9:36--41 cf. 20:9--12, Acts 5:3--5 cf. 13:8--11); and the Holy Spirit shows the Christian in that Book that if any apostle could ever be a "pope" at Rome, it would have been Paul (Rom. 15:16 cf. Gal. 2:8), not Peter. No one has to ["rob Peter to pay Paul"]{.class_4095}; the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_36}Scriptures have already committed the theft. Rome still robs Paul to pay Peter; but this is unscriptural stealing, and someday the money will have to be returned (Rev. 17--18).

The risen Saviour who confers with Paul is ["flesh and bones" ]{.class_4096}(Luke 24:39) but not ["flesh and blood," ]{.class_4096}for ["flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God" ]{.class_4096}(1 Cor. 15:50). Paul's revelations are supernatural and as directly given to him as the revelations given to Moses. His knowledge, therefore, of the spirit world, the after life, and salvation was (and shall always be) greater than the combined knowledge of Aristotle, Plato, Jean Dixon, Madalyn Murray, Socrates, Mediums, Darwin, the Tibetan Lamas, Huxley, Spinoza, Kant, Hegel, Nietzsche, James Joyce, William James, Henry James, Dewey, Russell, Hobbes, Hume, Rousseau, Voltaire, Tom Paine, Spengler, Einstein, H. G. Wells, the College of Cardinals, the United Nations, and the Pope.

The reader who has access to Romans, Ephesians, Galatians, and 1 and 2 Timothy has a store of scientific information at hand that will be here after heaven and earth have passed away (2 Pet. 3:12).

1:17 Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.

18 Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.

19 But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord's brother.

20 Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.

21 Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;

22 And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:

23 But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.

24 And they glorified God in me.

We have made our division between verses 16 and 17 because verse 17 goes into a narrative of Paul's travels after his conversion. Up to here, he has been talking about the spiritual issues involved in his conversion and his calling.

["But I went into Arabia, and returned again]{.class_4096} [unto Damascus" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17).              Dummelow suggests that ["Arabia" ]{.class_4096}included Damascus, and proof for this statement will be found on page 29 of Volume 20 of the [Pulpit Commentary. ]{.class_4095}Tertullian, Josephus, and the Apocrypha are cited for the location.

However, the Scriptures are on hand again to shed light on the obscure commentaries. The ["many days"]{.class_4096} of Acts 9:23 match the "many days" of 1 Kings 2:38--39, and in both places the period given is ["THREE years."]{.class_4096} But 1 Kings 18:1 is even better! Here, the ["many               days"]{.class_4096} is[ "three years and six months" ]{.class_4096}(James 5:17) and refers to Elijah the prophet, and Paul says ["AFTER three years"               ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18). Ah, the unsearchable riches of the [King James 1611 AV]{.class_4095}! How unsearchable are its judgments and its ways past finding out! [Paul went to Mt. Sinai.]{.class_4095}

That is where Moses went "in Arabia."

That is where Elijah went "in Arabia."

And that is where Jesus went "in Arabia."

(See detailed account in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew, ]{.class_4095}Matt. 4:1--3, and in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation, ]{.class_4095}Rev. 11:1--8.)

Paul goes into the wilderness and sits down at the feet of the risen Saviour and studies Rebecca, Eve, Rachel, Asenath, and Abigail until he is shown the mystery of the "Body of Christ"! He never once forgets his Old Testament studies during his ministry, and he recommends "Genesis to Malachi" to the born-again believer in the "mystery Body" (see Rom. 15:4).

["Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see PETER"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). While Paul goes up ["to Jerusalem," ]{.class_4096}all the commentators run down to Rome!

According to Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Williams, Ellicott, Bullinger, Huxtable, Harrison, and the [RV, ASV, RSV, ]{.class_4095}and [NEB, ]{.class_4095}the word ["Peter"]{.class_4096} should have been "Cephas." The word "Cephas" is plastered onto the verse 17 times in the comments on the verse by Rev. Huxtable [(Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_38} Vol. 20).

The word [Cephas ]{.class_4095}in Aramaic means "Peter" or "rock"; hence, the word was chosen by the Catholic Church as the proper reading for Matthew 16:18. The [New English Bible is ]{.class_4095}so anxious to get back to Rome that it actually translated Matthew 16:18 as: "You are Peter, the rock." Of course, the Greek text says nothing of the kind.
