The Greek (and Latin) words for [Peter]{.class_4095} and [rock ]{.class_4095}are different words, but in Aramaic, they both can be covered by [Cephas]{.class_4095}. The reader, therefore, should not be surprised to find "Cephas" inserted in the Alexandrian manuscripts ([Aleph, B,]{.class_4095} and [A]{.class_4095}) exactly at the point where ["flesh and blood" ]{.class_4096}appeared. (Note our comments on the direct reference to Simon Peter in Gal. 1:16!) The man to whom God revealed the Deity of Christ, however (Matt. 16:17), was NOT "Cephas." It was ["Peter" ]{.class_4096}in every Greek manuscript of Matthew 16:18 that has ever been found. The corrupt reading of the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095}, therefore, is just one more Roman Catholic attempt to confound the Holy Spirit. The correct word is ["Peter," ]{.class_4096}and the context of Galatians 1:16--18 would require ["Peter"]{.class_4096} if every Greek manuscript but one said "Cephas."

The [AV ]{.class_4095}is absolutely right, and the Alexandrian forgeries of Origen and Eusebius are absolutely wrong. It is a shame and a disgrace that the revision committee of the ["New" ASV ]{.class_4095}didn't have better sense than to preserve this ancient corruption.

["And abode with him fifteen days" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18). It must have been quite a visit. There was much about Christ's earthly life "in the flesh" about which Paul knew nothing. And if he ever picked a companion rightly for finding out about Christ's earthly ministry, Simon Peter was the man!

There is no evidence that Paul knew anything about the Lord's Supper, the agony in the Garden, the Crucifixion, Peter's denial, Judas' betrayal, the feeding of the 5,000, the miracle at Cana of Galilee, or even the details of the Resurrection. Paul may have known something about the crowds and the miracles that followed Christ's ministry (Luke 23:8), and he may have heard a testimony from Nicodemus on one or two occasions (John 7:50--51), but the intimate details of the earthly life of His Saviour were unknown to him. He must have listened in a trance as the old commercial []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_39}fisherman went "back over the ground" and told him about the three years that he had slept outdoors with Jesus, argued with Him before the brethren, eaten with Him at picnics, hiked the dusty roads with Him, prayed with Him, denied Him, and had eventually witnessed His Ascension on the Mount of Olives. You can almost hear Paul---

"What did He talk about when you were alone, Pete?"

"What did He teach you?"

"Did He ever get mad at you? How did He look when He got mad?"

"Could you feel any power around Him? How did you know it was the Lord's power?"

"Did you ever catch Him in a mistake? I mean, didn't He mess up somewhere just one time.

"Did you ever hear Him correct the Bible by referring to some 'original' we don't have?"

"Why did you deny Him? Would you do it again?"

"Com'on Pete, show me the spot! I want to see where He was praying when they came for Him!"

And with tears, Simon Peter would tell the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.

["But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord's brother" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19). There has been enough pen and ink wasted on ["James the Lord's brother" ]{.class_4096}to restock an office supply company. As usual the Devil covers his own "Bible" with a fog of verbiage until the student is lost in hopeless details. The issue is really quite clear.

1\. Roman Catholics do not allow the Lord to have a ["brother."]{.class_4095}

2\. So ["James the Lord's brother" ]{.class_4096}has to be "James the Lord's [cousin" ]{.class_4095}(see Matt. 1:25 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew, ]{.class_4095}1970).

(The "James" of the text cannot be James Zebedee, for he was decapitated in Acts 12:1--2.)

E. F. Harrison and Dummelow "boldly face the text and pass on to the next verse." But Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown zealously defend the Roman Catholic interpretation which was first posited by Jerome in A.D. 392.

Again, the Scriptures are helpful in correcting the commentaries.

1\. Mary has other children besides Christ (Psa. 69:8).

2\. These children are named (Mark 6:3).

3\. The word [cousin ]{.class_4095}is always a reference to a ["cousin," ]{.class_4096}not to a brother (Luke 1:36).

4\. ["James the Lord's brother" ]{.class_4096}became a believer (1 Cor. 15:7) after his other brothers rejected Christ (John 7:5).

["James the Lord's brother" ]{.class_4096}is---by all odds---["James the Lord's brother."]{.class_4096}

The fact that this is not good "Catholic doctrine" should mean nothing at all to the Bible believer, and the fact that the Scofield notes (in either edition) support this Catholic nonsense should mean even less. (The believer should accustom himself to the fact that the greatest and best premillennial scholars in the last half of the Twentieth Century will fall away with the Liberals and Papists and help complete the leavening process prophesied in Matt. 13:33.)

James had to be a "believer," or he would not have witnessed the resurrection of Christ (1 Cor. 15:7). Since he was not converted at the time of John 7:5, we may safely assume that he was converted between the resurrection of Lazarus (John 11) and the Crucifixion. Mary was present at the Crucifixion, and it would be preposterous to imagine that all of Christ's "brothers and sisters" (Mark 6:3) by Joseph were absent. Perhaps the charge given to John (John 19:27) by the dying Saviour caused the conversion of James. Christ did not entrust his mother to her own children (Mark 6:4). He turned her over to the "beloved disciple."

Paul's conversation with James must have been more intimate than those that he had with Peter (not "Cephas"). Two weeks with James, and Paul could have found out nearly every detail of Christ's boyhood and early manhood in the home of Joseph and Mary. It is exactly to this that Paul refers in 2 Corinthians 5:16 when he states ["yea, though WE HAVE KNOWN Christ after the flesh\...."]{.class_4096} He [never knew Him apart from the two weeks mentioned in our text!]{.class_4095}

Not one major commentator from Origen to E. S. English had the slightest idea at all about the connection between these texts. They were all so busy trying to tie theological discussions into the chapter so they would "jive" with chapter 2 in presenting an "apologetic" dissertation on "faith," that they forgot that ["all scripture is given by inspiration of God" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_41}(2 Tim. 3:16), and every verse is as valuable as all the gold bullion in Fort Knox.

["Now the things which I write unto you\...I lie not" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20). It is imperative that the Galatians get it through their heads that Paul's revelation about the Gospel was received DIRECTLY from God at the first and INDIRECTLY from God secondarily at a later date.

A Galatian who read the first 19 verses of the epistle would be tempted to say, "Why, that old liar. He's claiming precedence over Peter, the vicar of Christ, and James, whose own mother gave birth to Jesus Christ!"

In verse 20, Paul has taken an oath: ["before God, I lie not." ]{.class_4096}His reader understands that Paul was an orthodox Jew (vss. 13--14), and no orthodox Jew would think of swearing falsely (Deut. 7:8; 1 Kings 8:31; Josh. 2:17, 20; Judg. 21:5). A real "believer" (Acts 23:21) would have starved before breaking an oath made[ "before God."]{.class_4096} (We may assume that the killers of Acts 23:12--14 either starved or reneged on their oath and thus broke the Law!)

["Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia\...unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vss. 21--22). The events take place in the middle of Acts 9:29--30. Paul goes from Tarsus of Cilicia to Antioch of Syria and evidently sets up churches in those areas, for he "confirms" these groups in Acts 15:23, 41. There are no apostles roving Syria and Cilicia in Acts 9, so Paul could not have learned anything about the Gospel from them at this time.

Rev. F. Rendall [(Expositors Greek Testament,]{.class_4095} Vol. III, p. 157) is careful to tell us that the [King James ]{.class_4095}is wrong in translating ["I\...was unknown." ]{.class_4096}It should have been---oh boy!---"I was becoming unknown." (!) That is quite a trick if you can do it! In the light of 2 Corinthians 6:9, it might be well for Brother Rendall to translate the tense INCORRECTLY so that the "originals" don't contradict!

The ["churches of Judaea"]{.class_4096} (see Acts 9:31) never did get to know Paul personally before Acts 15, because his ["coming in and going out"]{.class_4096} was ["at Jerusalem"]{.class_4096} (Acts 9:28), not throughout Judaea.

Phenomenon No. 6.

6\. Where the correct Greek tense backs up the reading of the [AV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_42}1611, the rules of Greek grammar are ignored, and the commentator invents a tense to match his opinions. You see, here, technically, the verb IS a "perfect," and on the technicality a man could say the [AV ]{.class_4095}was wrong. However, the "technicians" are such consistent liars that when they tell the truth, we should never take it seriously. Observe the following:

1\. ["Gegraptia" ]{.class_4095}(Gk., PERFECT PASSIVE) is translated by Rev. Rendall as ["it IS written" ]{.class_4095}(2 Cor. 8:15). What happened to the penchant for translating tenses correctly, Reverend? How could you have missed ["it HAS BEEN written" ]{.class_4095}after raising such a fuss over Galatians 1:22[ ("agnooumenos")?]{.class_4095}

[2. "Ek tou echein"]{.class_4095} (Gk.:[ ]{.class_4095}"out of the 'to have'") has been translated by Rev. Rendall as "in accordance with your ability." There is no such Greek sentence in any manuscript that has ever been found. The [AV ]{.class_4095}has translated the phrase as ["out of that which ye have"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 8:11) since the ["echein" ]{.class_4095}is plainly the present infinitive of ["echo"]{.class_4095}---"to HAVE."

Rev. Rendall---and any fifty modern [AV ]{.class_4095}critics at random---simply invents Greek words and tenses if they do not appear in the text.

So what appears to be "accurate translating" is often just a cover up for a mangled disorientation of truth. If you do not blanch at altering the [Greek text ]{.class_4095}to suit your fancy, what is wrong with the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators taking the same liberty, especially when their "liberties with the text" bring out the [correct doctrine ]{.class_4095}and truth of the passage? Paul WAS ["unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea,"]{.class_4096} and if they had been "forgetting him," he certainly would have used the word for [forget]{.class_4095} instead of following the stupid phrasing of Rev. Rendall: "I was becoming unknown."

["The churches of Judaea which were IN CHRIST" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 22). The reader will observe that the famous expression of Ephesians, ["in Christ,"]{.class_4096} is used by Paul many years before Acts [28. ]{.class_4095}Paul has Christ in him (Gal. 1:16) and himself in Christ (Gal. 3:27) many, many years before Bullinger gave him credit for it. The[ "mystery" ]{.class_4096}revealed to Paul was certainly revealed many, many years before Acts 18, and to equate the date of the [writing ]{.class_4095}of Ephesians 3:1--6 with the time of the [beginning ]{.class_4095}of the Body is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ110.xhtml_page_43}nonsense. Ephesians is merely a summary statement of Paul's ministry to the Gentiles (see comments under that passage).

Phillips omits the entire expression ["which were in Christ," ]{.class_4096}and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}follows this corrupt leadership. The expression is found in every Greek manuscript extant, in any family of manuscripts from any century, and it is preserved in the highly scientific and accurate [King ]{.class_4095}James 1611 Authorized Version.

Paul is calling to the Galatians' attention the fact that the organized churches of Judaea were already ["in Christ"]{.class_4096} before he himself was ["in Christ"]{.class_4096} (see Rom. 16:7).

["But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith\...they glorified God IN ME"]{.class_4096} (vss. 23--24). The reader should observe that both Christ and God indwell the apostle exactly as Jesus Christ said that they would indwell Peter, James, and John (John 17:21, 23). There is no distinction whatsoever between the "Body placement" of Paul and "the Twelve." Although their ministries and functions differ, they all have ["one Lord, one faith, one baptism"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 4:5); even though Paul is given the revelation of the Jew‑Gentile joint Body, that Body is in existence many, many years before Paul writes Galatians.

Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Bullinger, and Huxtable insist on translating the verbs as imperfect---"was persecuting," "were glorifying." This time Rev. Rendall ignores the verb tenses altogether---after making such a fuss over verse 22---and tells us that they "glorified God," which is the exact [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 reading. The [AV ]{.class_4095}is correct here as in all places, and there is no point in pulling in the imperfect at all. The Reformation text states it beautifully---["persecuted\...destroyed\...glorified"]{.class_4096}! Peter, John, and James (["the Lord's brother"]{.class_4096}) are the bearers of this good news to the churches of Judaea. Paul does not carry the message himself; therefore, he IS "unknown" to them, exactly as the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 states the matter.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml}

# Chapter 2 {.heading_116}

2:1 Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with me also.

2 And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.

3 But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:

4 And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:

5 To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.

6 But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man's person:) for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:

7 But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the circumcision was unto Peter;

8 (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)

9 And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should go unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.

10 Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.

Williams kicks off the criticism by suggesting that we throw our Holy Bible out the window and read the entire chapter as it stands in the corrupt [RV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_45}of 1881--1885. We return the kick by saying that the reader should throw his [RV ]{.class_4095}in the garbage can (where his [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [RSV ]{.class_4095}already are!) and read the entire chapter as it stands in the Holy Bible.

The "visit" of verse 1 is plainly the visit of Acts 15; but this time the visit is not for the purpose of finding out about Christ's earthly life (see Gal. 1:18--19), but for thrashing out problems involved in the conduct of Gentile converts after they have accepted Christ. These problems come to a head in the Christian assembly of Acts 15 and are officially solved there where a unanimous decision is reached regarding them.

The Lord lays it on Paul's heart to make this visit (vs. 2), and he takes a Gentile convert with him: ["Titus, who was with me, BEING A GREEK"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). The entire passage is clear and needs little or no exposition. The Judaizers of Acts 15:1--5 were teaching standard Roman Catholic doctrine still taught today by Episcopalians, Methodists, Seventh-day Adventists, Jehovah's Witnesses, Buddhists, Taoists, Mohammedans and Confucianists:

1\. You have to DO something yourself [to be]{.class_4095} saved.

2\. You have to DO something yourself [to stay]{.class_4095} saved.

That is the sum total of religions, racked out, cashed in, locked up, stashed away, signed, sealed, delivered, and notarized; and if you think any different you're either a coward or a hypocrite---or BOTH.

The specific issue here is CIRCUMCISION, so to avoid making enemies and suffering for Christ "outside the camp" as Paul did (Gal. 6:12), the commentators absolutely refuse to apply the truths of the passage to their contemporary age. Bullinger, Huxtable, Rendall, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Harrison, Lange, Clarke, Ellicott, Williams, and the ["Interpreter's Bible"]{.class_4095} dare not "update" the teaching of the passage. While hollering and screaming about the archaic language of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611, not a man in the bunch can bring anything up to date where it will affect the sale of his books! Ah, faithless generation!

Paul goes up with his buddies (Barnabas and Titus), and the three of them meet the three of Jerusalem (the "Big Three"). They meet behind closed doors (vs. 2---["privately"]{.class_4096}) to discuss the issue of Jewish ritual. They discuss more than circumcision, for they arrive []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_46}at a decision regarding the boundaries of their ministries (vs. 7--10). Paul does not doubt the outcome of the race he is running (1 Cor. 9:26--27), but he is running "in vain" if his work is going to be swallowed up by Judaizers who teach something contrary to his own message of grace. Again the dispensationalist should observe that Paul is preaching pure grace before Acts 15: the events of Acts 13--14 take place more than thirteen years before the Book of Ephesians was written.

Peter, James, and John do not put any pressure on Titus to get circumcised (vs. 3), so it is apparent that Peter is over his ideas about salvation by works, and his "baptismal regeneration" (Acts 2) went with the wind at the time of Cornelius' conversion (Acts 10).

The people who are trying to get Paul "in a bind" on circumcision are ["false brethren"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4) who slipped into the local assemblies and put the pressure on Titus to get him circumcised. The ["false brethren"]{.class_4096} here are obviously the great-great-great-grandfathers of the false brethren who quote Matthew 24:13 and 2 Peter 2:22 to the born-again child of God or try to drag him back to Acts 2:38 after the Holy Spirit has already sealed him (Eph. 1:13).

To overlook the identification of the ["false brethren"]{.class_4096} in the chapter is to confess that one is neither a commentator nor an expounder of the word of God. If all you can locate in the two words is the Greek endings, you should lock up your desk, close your typewriter, and enlist in the Navy. Not one commentator (in the major works) from Origen to English will assist the reader in finding the ["false brethren."]{.class_4096} But marvel of marvels, they are all around us! Every time we lead a soul to Christ they show up! None of them died in A.D. 51--53, and they have multiplied considerably since that time! I wonder why Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Clarke, Lange, Dummelow, Henry, Williams, and the ["New" Bible Commentary]{.class_4095} could not find them?

Those of us engaged in living the words of the Bible (instead of criticizing them) have seen the ["false brethren"]{.class_4096} so many times "spying out our liberty in Christ Jesus" that we wouldn't need to study two weeks of Greek grammar to locate them.

1\. You lead the man to Christ. Up pop the "brethren" of Galatians who say: "You have been misled. We receive Christ every Sunday in the Mass. Return to Mother Church and let her teach you []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_47}['the sincere milk of the word']{.class_4096} and beware of any man who quotes Ephesians 2:8 and forgets James 2:24."

Guess who?

2\. You lead the man to Christ. Up pops a man with a [Watchtower ]{.class_4095}magazine who says: "All is well---there is no hell but the grave. You need to get into Bible study and learn the things that Jehovah would have you to do to merit eternal life."

Guess who?

3\. You lead the man to Christ. Up pops some waterdog who never got out of the tub in Acts 2:38, who says: "You do not have the Holy Ghost, for the Holy Ghost was only promised to those who were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. You have been baptized wrongly, and since the word ['churches' ]{.class_4096}in Romans 16:16 should have been translated 'church,' you have been deceived."

Guess who?

4\. You lead the man to Christ. Up pops some idiot who says: "You must keep the law. If you break one commandment, you are least in the Kingdom of Heaven. If you love Christ, you will keep the MORAL commandments, and one of the MORAL commandments is the ritual observance of a Jewish rest day."

Guess who?

If you guessed one out of four you are ahead of Dummelow, Bullinger, Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Williams, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Huxtable, Harrison, Rendall, DeWette, Lipsius, Jowett, Buttmann, and Alford. They couldn't locate any of the false brethren but the ones who died before A.D. 300.

([And you are trying to learn the Bible from men like that? ]{.class_4095}You would do better studying flannelgraphs.)

Paul ignored these "brethren" (vs. 5) and didn't yield one inch of ground to them. Although he himself was baptized in water (and every Christian in Acts 11 was baptized in water), Paul never made the mistake of connecting water baptism with salvation (1 Cor. 1:17), even if Ananias had done so (Acts 22:16). Never once in his life did Paul teach that a man had to be baptized in water to GET SAVED or to STAY SAVED.

He gave the Christians plenty of advice on what to do after they []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_48}were saved (Eph. 5--6; Col. 3--4; Phil. 4; 1 Cor. 1--14; etc.), but never did he connect their conduct as Christians with their eternal justification by the grace of God through faith in Christ.

["The truth of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5) was to "continue" with the Galatians come hell or high water, and if Paul had yielded on the circumcision issue (with Titus), it would have shaken the faith of the converts in Galatia. Timothy is a different situation (Acts 16:3), for his mother was a Jewess. He submits to circumcision as a testimony to the unsaved Jews in that area. The Galatians were not half-Jew-half-Gentile; they were pure Gentile, or ["heathen"]{.class_4096} as Paul puts it (Gal. 1:16).

["The truth of the gospel,"]{.class_4096} then, is that James 2:24; Acts 2:38; 2 Peter 2:22; Matthew 24:13; 2 Peter 3:17; and Hebrews 6:1--6, 10:26--30, 3:14 are references to something other than New Testament salvation in the Church Age. The ["unlearned and unstable"]{.class_4096} (2 Pet. 3:16) may wrest these Scriptures to ["their own destruction,"]{.class_4096} but the faithful minister of the Gospel is to stand stedfast (Eph. 6:10) and refuse to give them---or their teachers and preachers---one inch of ground (Gal. 2:5). No ecumenical movement is possible to the Bible believer, unless it is the unity of the Spirit (Eph. 4:3) which unites born-again, Bible-believing Christians in Jesus Christ Himself. (The ecumenical movement of 1975--1993 is no more "Christian" than the National Education Association.)

Paul, Titus, and Barnabas have a "pow-wow" with the "big three" and come to the conclusion that Peter, James, and John should continue to minister to Jews in Jerusalem and they (Paul and Barnabas) should continue to minister to the Gentiles (vss. 8--9). Paul and Barnabas split up after this (Acts 15:39--40), and Peter and John both minister to Gentiles after this (1 Peter; Rev. 1), but the emphasis has been fixed. Paul's primary ministry will be away from Jerusalem to the "heathen," and the other apostle's primary ministry will be in Jerusalem to the "saints" (Acts 21:18--20). Two different gospels are not delineated in the conference, and this is apparent from the decisions of Acts 15:19, 23, 28.

["The gospel of the uncircumcision"]{.class_4096} is plainly the Gospel preached to them as a group different from ["the circumcision." ]{.class_4096}That the same message is preached is perfectly apparent from Acts []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_49}15. The Bullingerite conjecture that Peter, James, and John continued to preach Acts 2:38 and James 2 is nonsense. James was written BEFORE Acts 12. (See Scofield's misleading notes again and observe how the authority has been transferred from the original "Peter, James, and John"---Matt. 26:37; Luke 9:28---to Peter, John, and the Roman Catholic "cousin" of the Lord. See remarks under Gal. 1:19.)

(James is clearly written before any revelation of salvation by free grace apart from works, and it is apparent in every line of James' epistle. Consequently, this epistle is the most dearly beloved writing in the entire Bible for the Hell-bound, self-righteous, God-defying, sacramental religionist who desires to pay his own ticket to Glory.)

Peter, James, and John were not able to tell Paul anything he didn't already know (vs. 6); and although they were looked up to by the community at Jerusalem as ["pillars"]{.class_4096} of the church (vs. 9)---not ["princes,"]{.class_4095} as in Roman Catholic mythology---Paul dealt with them on an equal basis (vs. 8) and parted with them on a handshake (vs. 9).

Romans 15:25 shows that Paul kept his word to the "three" (vs. 10) in regard to the poor saints at Jerusalem (see also Acts 11:29--30).

The passage is very clear. As we have said before, it needs little or no "explaining," and it certainly needs no alteration for clarity. However, since all the major commentaries are criticisms of the word of God---except the one at which you are now looking---we cannot be thorough in our notations unless we list the ravages of Conservative criticism as the "fundamental" scholars seek to annihilate the words of the Holy Bible.

1\. ["But neither"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3) should be "not even" ([RV]{.class_4095}, [ASV]{.class_4095}, Dummelow).

2\. ["But neither"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3) should have been "and yet" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}).

3\. ["But neither"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3) should have been "but even" ([RSV ]{.class_4095}1946). (Make up your mind, will ya!)

4\. ["Gave place"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5) should have been "yielded" (Bullinger).

5\. ["Gave place"]{.class_4096} (vs 5) should be "did we yield" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown).

6\. ["By subjection"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5) should be "in the way of subjection" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_50}).

7\. ["Whatsoever they were"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) should be "what manner of men they had once been" (Rev. Rendall).

8\. ["Of"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) should have been "from" (Bullinger).

9\. ["But of those"]{.class_4096} should be "but to be something from" (Vincent).

10\. ["Whatsoever they were"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) should be "what they once" (Lightfoot).

11\. ["By subjection"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5) should be "with the subjection required of us" (Alford).

12\. ["But of these"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) should be "But from those" ([RV ]{.class_4095}1885).

13\. ["They who seemed to be somewhat"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) should have been "the leaders of the conference" ([Phillips]{.class_4095}).

14\. No, they should have been "the men of high reputation" ([NEB ]{.class_4095}1961).

15\. No, they were "reputed as persons of some consequence" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown).

16\. ["In conference"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) should have been "imparted" ([ASV ]{.class_4095}1901, [RV ]{.class_4095}1885).

17\. No, it should have been "added," but one should omit the whole phrase ["who seemed to be somewhat in conference"]{.class_4096} ([RSV ]{.class_4095}1946).

18\. ["Wrought effectually"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8) should only be "wrought" ([ASV ]{.class_4095}l901).

19\. No, it should be "worked" ([RSV ]{.class_4095}1946).

20\. No, both words should be omitted ([Phillips ]{.class_4095}1958).

21\. No, it should have been "entrusted" ([NEB ]{.class_4095}1961).

Now, do you know what a Christian is who abandons his [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 for this kind of trash?[ He is a blank idiot.]{.class_4095}

Do you really want to burn your eyes out reading this kind of tripe, or have you had enough? (I have 300 more corrections to quote in the remaining verses in Galatians! Aren't you ready to holler "Uncle!" yet?)

Shall we try verses 3--8 with the help of the commentators?

Let's try it, shall we?

2:3 [But, not yet, however, rather, neither, or not, Titus who was (being) with, alongside me, being, having been, a heathen Greek []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_51}Gentile convert was forced under compulsion to getting being circumcized.]{.class_4095}

2:4 [And this, that, these, or those, was because of (from) false interloping insidious spies who were had been sham (artificial) brethren sneaking, have been snuck, snooping inside the local assembly (congregation) to observe the freedom which we are (or were) having in Christ that in order for, to, (them or us) might bring us into through a set of rules and regulations like bondage to observances.]{.class_4095}

2:5 [To whom, by whom, in relation to which, under these circumstances listed above, we didn't fall back or change or mind, turning around in one place, giving an inch for any length of time (at least in the aorist tense) no, not even (or or for), possibly any hour, so that (in order to, or from) that through (or by) the gospel you might continue in the gospel you have continued in without anyone harassing or troubling you about (concerning) it (what).]{.class_4095}

Do you know what a Christian is who takes "conservative scholarship" seriously where it alters the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611? [He is a fool.]{.class_4095}

When you get through making a shambles out of the Holy Bible with all this tomfoolery, you will have to erase the verse markings (as [Phillips ]{.class_4095}and the [New English Bible]{.class_4095} do) in order to prevent sane people from identifying the farce!

The alterations in Galatians 2:1--10 by Ellicott, Olshausen, DeWette, Alford, Jowett, Lightfoot, Trench, Vincent, Rendall, Lipsius, Field, Wordsworth, Meyer, and the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} are not merely [worthless]{.class_4095}, they are [ruinous]{.class_4095}.

All was clear without the alteration of a single word. Observe how the [AV]{.class_4095} uses the word ["communicated"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2) in the senses of verbal communication as well as financial help (cf. Gal. 6:6). The word "conversant" can refer to verbal communications as well as to "manner of life" (see Gal. 1:13), and the fact that men have altered the meanings of these Bible words to replace them with the archaic language of Nineteenth-Century Behavioristic Psychology should mean nothing to the believer at all. The words "meaningful" and "relevant" are not understood today by the men that use them with every other breath. If you pinned these men right down, you would find that the two terms are so broad in application that they can be []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_52}made to mean ANYTHING. Not even the word "God" or "universe" is as flexible as the words "meaningful" and "relevant" as they are used by the scientific fanatics and sex obsessional neurotics of today. They are not merely "archaic," they are meaningless. They had no real meaning when they were first coined.

The word ["Cephas"]{.class_4096} is the correct word in verse 9 this time, for it emphasizes the [Jewish quality ]{.class_4095}of the Jerusalem congregation. The corrupt Alexandrian manuscripts (Aleph and B) continue to use [Cephas ]{.class_4095}throughout (Gal. 1:18, 2:11, 14), but they are obviously in error. The error was deliberate and intentional and was designed to encourage the theory that Matthew 16:18 was written in Aramaic (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew]{.class_4095}, Matt. 16:16--18, 1970).

2:11 But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.

12 For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.

13 And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.

14 But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?

Verse 11 takes place around Acts 15:35 and marks the time of a further advance in "non-Jewish" thinking. The problem of works in connection with salvation was thoroughly settled before Acts 15:35, and this is perfectly apparent to anyone but a Roman Catholic, Church of Christ, Seventh-day Adventist, Jehovah's Witness, Buddhist, Taoist, Confucian, Holiness, Episcopalian, Methodist, Evolutionist, or Communist. Now another question arises: what about matters of fellowship between Jew and Gentile?

["But when Peter was come to Antioch"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_53} (vs. 11). There had been Christians at Antioch several years before the council in Jerusalem (Acts 11:26). Furthermore, they had had years of teaching and prophesying (Acts 11:27--28) before the council, and they had had experience in pioneer missionary work among the Gentiles (Acts 13--14). Antioch was very cosmopolitan alongside Jerusalem, so the change in spiritual atmosphere for Simon Peter would have been the equivalent of a Christian "Reformed" preacher in Holland, Michigan, moving to Miami, Florida.

["I withstood him to the face"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11). That is, Paul corrects the "Prince of apostles" and tells him off in no uncertain terms. It would seem that the "Vicar of Christ" got his wires crossed somewhere. If his work in "Rome" was as fouled up as his work in Antioch, someone joined the wrong church! The first "Pope" (that is what a papist believes) not only made mistakes in speech (Acts 10:14) and spiritual discernment (Matt. 16:22) and self-control (Matt. 26:74), but he also was, at least for awhile, a heretic in doctrinal matters. If he had any successors, we should certainly be leary of them. Fortunately, "apostolic succession" of bachelors (Paul was a bachelor!) goes through Paul, not Peter. To be a real "apostolic successor" of Simon Peter, you would have to be a circumcised Jew with a family (1 Cor. 9:5).

["He did eat with the Gentiles\...he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). The reader should observe that THIS separation is not the separation of Romans 1:1--2 nor the separation of 2 Corinthians 6:14--18. It is fashionable nowadays (among ecumenical advocates) to accuse separated Christians of being self-righteous "ultra-separatists" and refusing to eat ["with publicans and sinners" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 9:14; Mark 2:16) in order to "reach them" or "influence them for Christ." However, this accusation comes from men who are twisted and distorted in their thinking; none of them ever really pay any attention to what the Bible says, even when they quote it to prove a position.

When Christ ate with the Pharisees, there wasn't any fellowship (see Luke 11:37, 7:36).

When a Pharisee repented and got right (John 3:1--21), there was []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_54}fellowship, but Jesus Christ was careful all of His life to avoid giving anyone the impression that He was in agreement with the ecumenical group of His day (study Matt. 19--23).

Peter's "separation," here, is a running from an obligation because of ["the "fear of man"]{.class_4096} (Prov. 29:25). Public opinion has reared its ugly head. This is the reason why Liberals and Modernists separate themselves from Biblical Christianity and the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611; they are "men fearers" and "men pleasers" (2 Pet. 2:1--22).

Race-mixers often use this reference to prove that racial integration is "Christian" and segregation is "non- Christian." But again, we should not be fooled by carnal, worldly, men-pleasing reprobates who only quote a verse to prove a position, yet never hesitate to alter any verse at their convenience (Williams, Dummelow, Lipsius, Robertson, Rendall, Warfield, Thieme, Trench, Wuest, Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Alford, Lightfoot, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and Nestle, for example!).

The "integration" found here is an integration of born-again, Bible-believing people in the Body of Jesus Christ (Eph. 1--3), and this is the only Scriptural integration about which the Bible knows anything (1 Cor. 12:12--13). The efforts of the federal government of the United Socialistic Nations to produce a mongrel race of automatons by encouraging and promoting race-mixing, cannot be connected with Galatians 2, unless the "connector" is a spiritual pervert.

Now, Peter has an alibi when he is put on the spot in Acts 11:3, for he was able to tell about the intervention of the Holy Ghost (Acts 10:44) and the marvelous way in which God's grace overruled Acts 2:38 by sending the Spirit directly to the believer [before ]{.class_4095}water baptism (Acts 10:45--47).

However, this time Peter is not on a missionary trip to the heathen; he is fellowshipping in the local church at Antioch with "brothers in Christ." They are all sitting around the table eating fried trout, hush puppies, and seafood gumbo, when a messenger comes in and walks up to Pete and whispers in his ear, "That bunch from Acts 11:2 just pulled up, and they're staying in the motel overnight."

"Uh-oh!" says ["The Vicar of Christ."]{.class_4095} "I'm gonna get my shirttail in a wringer now. Ah, excuse me, gentlemen, I have to make a very []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_55}important phone call!"

(Now if Phillips had THAT, he would have some "living letters," wouldn't he?)

When the word gets around the room after Pete gets up from the table, some of the converted Jews get up with him (vs. 13), and even "the good man filled with the Holy Ghost" (Acts 11:24) gets up and excuses himself. The local residents of Antioch begin to look at one another and make various comments.

"Well, what in the world is eatin' them?"

"Where's the fire, man? You didn't finish your iced tea."

"Well, something must be going on outside. A fight, you reckon?"

"Humph! Never saw Simon Peter quit like that in the middle of a fish-fry. Must'a got indigestion."

Peter has gone to get "Tums for the tummy" because the butterflies in his stomach are having a field day. As he goes out the side door wiping his mouth, he says to himself, "If those birds see me here with these heathen, I'll never hear the end of it! Why did I ever come here in the first place?"

This raises the next issue: does ["the truth of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14) teach that there is a difference between Jew and Gentile (Gal. 3:28) in matters of fellowship, when both of them are in the Body of Christ (Gal. 3:27)?

["I said unto Peter before them all"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). Paul practices what he preaches (1 Tim. 5:20), and he does not hesitate to straighten out the "Vicar of Christ" on his Grace doctrines.

The gist of verse 14 is "If you, being a Jew, are fellowshipping with these Gentiles (and you were before the brethren called) and enjoying it---even though you knew an Orthodox Jew shouldn't be enjoying it---how come you reversed field, brother, and quit enjoying it? Are you trying to teach these Gentiles that Orthodox Judaism is the right way to live?"

Peter never answers the question in the text of Galatians 2. If he did answer, as in Acts 15:11, the answer is not recorded anywhere. Peter refers to his rebuker as ["our beloved brother Paul"]{.class_4096} (2 Pet. 3:15), so he evidently took the dressing-down in good faith and amended his ways. (If the Pope could follow in those steps of Simon Peter, he would have climbed off his throne fifteen centuries back. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_56}No man in this age could sit on any throne---let alone a gold one!---and profess to be a faithful follower of Paul or Christ. Both of them carried CROSSES, not [crowns]{.class_4095}.)

The [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881) and the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) insist that Peter "stood condemned" instead of ["to be blamed."]{.class_4096} Bullinger says that the "texts" read "Cephas" instead of ["Peter"]{.class_4096} and forgets to tell his reader that the "texts" referred to are the ancient forgeries by Origen, Pamphilus, Eusebius, and Marcion. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have decided that "self-condemned" is better than ["to be blamed,"]{.class_4096} and the New English Bible decides that Peter was "clearly in the wrong." Ellicott, Olshausen, Alford, Lipsius, Rendall, and Wordsworth make the usual needless alterations which only remind us of the antics of Clement, Origen, and Jerome. They thought the whole passage was either an act or involved a "deutero-Petros" (see [The Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, p. 162).

One can see at a glance that the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 is far superior to any other translation of the "texts." Peter deserves blame for what he did. He does NOT "stand condemned," and you can be certain that Paul did not "withstand" Peter because Peter "stood condemned." Paul withstood him because he deserved blame. That is the sense, meaning, and interpretation of the text; and if any other interpretation ([RV, ASV, RSV, "New" ASV, "New Scofield,"]{.class_4095} New Morality, "New" Look, "New" Orthodoxy) has been inserted for the reading, it is for the purpose of confusing the reader.

The word ["dissimulation"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13) is the archaic English for "hypocrisy" and is found again in Romans 12:9, where the meaning is evident.

2:15 We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,

16 Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.

17 But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_57}minister of sin? God forbid.

18 For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.

19 For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.

20 I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.

21 I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.

Verse 15 polishes off Garner Ted Armstrong along with all the other British Israelites; for lo and behold, the ["We"]{.class_4096} is a reference to Simon Peter, and Simon Peter was a Galilean from one of the ten northern tribes! Not even his "accent" matched that of a Judaean "Jew" (Matt. 26:73; cf. Gal. 1:13--14). Furthermore, Peter is a Jew ["by nature."]{.class_4096} He is not a "proselyte of the gate" or someone who "became a Jew" (Esth. 8:17) or anything else. Peter was BORN a Jew. ["By nature," ]{.class_4096}he was a Jew before his circumcision, and ["by nature"]{.class_4096} never refers to a political party or a religion anywhere in the Bible (cf. Rom. 1:26, 2:14, 11:24; 1 Cor. 11:14; Eph. 2:3; 2 Pet. 1:4).

Any circumcised descendant of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob---I did not say just "Abraham"!---is a [Jew ]{.class_4095}and is so named in the New Testament. Gerald K. Smith's observations, Winrod's observations, and Armstrong's observations are null and void in the light of the living word of the living God (see Rom. 1:16, 3:29, 10:12, 1 Cor. 1:22--24, 10:32, 12:13; Col. 3:11, etc.). "Judah and Israel" are political distinctions, and appeals made to the use of the word "Jew" in John's gospel are only made by heretics who refuse to quote John 4:22---["salvation is of the Jews."]{.class_4096}

Salvation is NOT of "Israel" nor the "Israel of God" nor the "spiritual Jews" nor the "spiritual Israel." ["Salvation is of the Jews."]{.class_4096}

["Knowing that a man is NOT JUSTIFIED by the works of the law\...for by the works of the law SHALL NO FLESH BE JUSTIFIED"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_58} (vs. 16). Seventh-day Adventists limit the passage to the ceremonial law and refuse to tell their adherents that Sabbath observance is a ceremony. All of the other conservative commentators from 1700--1970 limit it to the Mosaic Law; not one of them dares apply it to any of the churches, preachers, teachers, and religious leaders of their day and time who are plainly teaching salvation BY WORKS (see comments under Gal. 1:8--9). As we have remarked before, it would appear that the commentators have agreed to attack no one but the man who calls the reader's attention to their lack of courage and fidelity to the Bible. They don't make any enemies if they can help it, and to "help it" they would alter every verse in the Bible, if necessary.

This great verse is one of the granite blocks of Christian doctrine placed in the Bible. It clearly shows the following:

1\. There has been a change in dispensational dealing (cf. Rom. 15:16).

2\. Man no longer can share in his own justification (James 2:21).

3\. Justification is immediate and an act of God (Gal. 3:8).

4\. It is obtained by a faith which accepts God's righteousness (Rom. 10:1--5) in place of its own righteousness (Isa. 64:6).

Verse 16 is one of the foundation texts for the Reformation, which temporarily released Europe from the bondage of Roman Fascism. Unfortunately, Europe went to "sleep at the switch" after World War I, and soon the political leaders of Europe---Hitler, Goebbels, DeGaulle, Franco, Mussolini, Willie Brandt, Kiessinger, et al.---showed up: the same crowd that ran it from A.D. 500--1500!

Paul is not confining his theology to the Mosaic Law, even if this is the subject of dispute in the immediate context. Ephesians 2:8--9 and Titus 3:5 show clearly that any works are ["verboten"]{.class_4095} in this age when one is dealing with justification before God (Rom. 4:5).

["Not SINNERS of the Gentiles"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) shows the problem clearly, for a ["sinner"]{.class_4095} in Jewish usage (John 9:24; Luke 7:37) was always an evil person living an immoral life. The term was never used as it is used in Romans 3 until that epistle was written. ["ALL have sinned"]{.class_4096} is something "new" for an Orthodox Jew under the Mosaic Law. David does not cry ["I have SINNED"]{.class_4096} until he has committed adultery and murder (2 Sam. 12:13). Paul is telling Peter, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_59}"You know perfectly well that you and I had to take our place with a bunch of Gentile dogs before God would justify us; and if that is so, what in the world are you doing, now, trying to make a distinction between us and them in matters of right and wrong conduct?" Observe: ["EVEN WE have believed in Jesus Christ, that WE might be justified by the faith of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16). "Even US, Pete, who were not supposed to be 'sinners!'"

It would appear from verse 15 that Paul is now expounding out loud to himself and the Galatians, and that his rebuke of Peter ended in verse 14. (Compare the transition in John 3 from verse 15 to verse 16. Christ often speaks in the third person \[John 3:15--16\]. Paul's break, here, is a little clearer, but he goes on using ["We"]{.class_4096} to include Peter and anyone like him.)

Galatians 3:10--11 is a perfect counterpart to Galatians 2:16, and in these two verses we have the theme of Galatians in a nutshell. Seventh-day Adventists and Orthodox Jews ["are fallen from grace"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 5:4) according to the greatest Jewish Christian who ever lived, for both of them are counting on their obedience to the Law to help justify them in the day of judgment. Appeals to passages in Matthew, Peter, James, and Hebrews are futile. The [obscure ]{.class_4095}can only be interpreted in the light of the [plain]{.class_4095}, and to place Galatians and Romans in a secondary place (alongside Matthew, Acts, and Hebrews) when dealing with the doctrines of salvation identifies the ["unstable"]{.class_4096} (2 Pet. 3:16) immediately. Not a human being on God's earth studied trigonometry before he studied arithmetic, and the world has yet to see a musician who could write a symphony before he first learns the scale. Only in [religion ]{.class_4095}do we find crackpots who relegate the whole Bible to Acts 2:38 or Matthew 16:18 or John 20:23 or Ephesians 4:5 or Ephesians 3:2 or Ecclesiastes 9:5 or Hebrews 6:4--6. Our text is clear (2:16).

In this age, God will not declare any man righteous on the grounds of his obedience to the Law (cf. Phil. 3:4--9 and comments). The verse in Romans 2:13 is a hypothetical statement which states that if a man COULD keep it intact, it would justify him, but two things pop up immediately,

1\. The context of Romans 2:13 is the Gentile obeying the commandments when the Jew doesn't.

2\. The next three verses state that when the Gentile "does the law," he only puts himself under the same condemnation the Jew is under (Gal. 3:13).

["But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ\...is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). There are two readings on the verse, but the latter is preferable. The first thought is this:

"If we find ourselves sinning after we get saved (and justified), does that mean that Christ is the [author of sin]{.class_4095} since He was the one who justified us? God forbid!"

The second is this:

"Now that we are justified, does Christ place us in a position where we become sinners? You do call people who eat with Gentiles[ 'sinners,' ]{.class_4096}don't you (Luke 5:30)? Well, you could not be a sinner in that sense unless Christ is the minister of sin, for He is the one who put you in fellowship with Gentiles who were justified like [YOU ]{.class_4095}are. Is Christ that kind of a ['minister']{.class_4096}? ["God forbid."]{.class_4096}

["God forbid"]{.class_4096} can be translated as "Let it not be!" (or in some translations, ["Away with the thought!"]{.class_4095}) [Phillips ]{.class_4095}has ["of course not!"]{.class_4095} which is much too mild, and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}has ["No! Never!"]{.class_4095} which is better. Neither of them is as good as ["God forbid!"]{.class_4096}; for if God forbids a thing from coming to pass, it will NOT come to pass or come into "being." The ["certainly not!"]{.class_4095} of the [RSV ]{.class_4095}is also a little anemic for the kind of speech that Paul used (2 Cor. 11:6). (Many of the translators like to translate Paul's writings into the cool, subjective, slick vocabulary of their own crowd \[see comments under Galatians 1:8--9\].)

Now Paul turns his guns off Peter and points them at himself.

["For if I BUILD AGAIN the things which I DESTROYED, I MAKE MYSELF a transgressor"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). Peter is to learn from the application, for there is still an indirect reference to himself! Paul has been polite enough to forego saying: "For if YOU build again the things which YOU destroyed, YOU make YOURSELF a transgressor"; but the double application is apparent, because Paul inserted ["We"]{.class_4096} way back at verse 15. The text is transparent. If the saved child of God who has been justified by faith apart from the works of the Law returns to those works or gives any indication (as Peter had done) that those works are connected with [justification]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_61}, he brackets himself. He has stuck himself back under the Law as a sinner trying to get to Heaven by works. Romans 4:5 and Romans 3:26--27 are clear commentaries on the passage. They state the doctrines of salvation so clearly that one marvels at what he sees round about him in America today. No man will ever be justified by keeping the [second great commandment]{.class_4095} (Matt. 22:38--39), and that is the foundation verse for everything that Marx, Trotsky, the Supreme Court, Lenin, Engels; and social welfare ever taught (Mark 12:30--31). A Communist who is an Evolutionist doesn't differ one inch from a Roman Catholic or Seventh-day Adventist when it comes to [self-righteousness ]{.class_4095}or [justification]{.class_4095}. Cain is at home in any crowd, and Cain was a killer (cf. comments under Gen. 4:3--6, [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4095}, 1970).

["For I through the law AM DEAD TO THE LAW"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19). This is the theme of Romans 6--7, which see. None of the commentators give any light not found in Romans. About 59 changes are suggested for Galatians 2:17--19 by Bullinger, Huxtable, Rendall, Harrison, Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Trench, Alford, Lightfoot, Wordsworth, Lipsius, Zockler, and DeWette; and not one of them illuminates anything not already illuminated by the Holy Ghost in the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text.

The Law killed the Christian (Rom. 7:8--9). He was officially declared dead (Eph. 2:1--4). When he accepted Christ, only his [spirit ]{.class_4095}revived (Eph. 2:5--6; John 3:5); but his old dead body (Gal. 2:20) went right on moving (Rom. 6:1--9). His soul was cut loose from this flesh (Rom. 7:1--4) to marry Jesus Christ and become one Spirit (1 Cor. 6:17), yet he still drags the corpse with him to a hole in the ground (or the hole in the sky!!). That corpse was nailed to the cross with Jesus Christ and buried with Him (Rom. 6:1--11). The Christian has no hold on the Law, and it has no hold on him (Rom. 7:1--4). The changing of other laws (Rom. 8:2) into laws of a legal nature to bring him back into bondage (Gal. 5:1) are satanic, whether they originated with Alexander Campbell's "Church of Christ" or the Italian winoe's "Roman Catholic" Church. (Now if that is not in the "Westcott and Hort" Greek text, get yourself a Japanese text.)

The work by E. L. Maxwell ([Born Crucified]{.class_4095}) and the work by Watchman Nee ([Sit, Walk, Stand]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_62}) both deal with the context of Galatians 2:15--21, and either of them are superior to any thing published by the Roman Catholic "Holy See" in the last fifteen centuries.

["I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). I am dead, but I am alive (Rom. 6--7). I am a live [spirit ]{.class_4095}and a dead [body]{.class_4095}. (An unsaved man is a live body and a dead spirit.)

The [RV, ASV]{.class_4095}, Mickey Mouse, [RSV]{.class_4095}, Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Charlie Brown, Goodspeed, [Living Letters]{.class_4095}, Dead Epistles, Mack the Knife, [Riverside]{.class_4095}, [Berkeley]{.class_4095}, and Glenn Miller all translate ["I am crucified"]{.class_4096} as ["I HAVE BEEN crucified."]{.class_4095}

Paul was a ["living sacrifice"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 12:1--3), ["always bearing about"]{.class_4096} in his body the death of his Lord (2 Cor. 4:10) and dying[ "daily"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 15:31). The tense of ["the world is crucified unto me"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 6:14) is the tense of ["I am crucified"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 2:20). A historical crucifixion that lies only in the past ("I have been"---[RV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NASV, NIV, TNIV]{.class_4095}, etc.) is of no more avail to the real Christian than Shamgar's ox goad.

["That I might live unto God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19) is the purpose of the crucifixion, and Paul is speaking in practical terms of a living reality in the present experience of a believer, applying his relationship to a real situation that took place (vss. 13--15). ["I am crucified"]{.class_4096} is his witness to Simon Peter. "I have been crucified" is no more the writing of the Holy Spirit than "I have been eradicated."

["Nevertheless I live; YET NOT I, but Christ liveth in me"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). Some of him is still dead (see lengthy remarks in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4095}, Gen. 2:17). He has a living spirit and a living soul encased in a dead body that is rotting physically (Rom. 7:24) and is perishing ["day by day"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 4:16).

(The insertion of "bodily life" into the passage by [Phillips ]{.class_4095}and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}is an erratum.)

Romans 7:22, 25 is the exact counterpart of this passage in which one will observe three tremendous truths.

1\. The evil that a Christian does is HIS OWN (Gal. 2:18).

2\. Yet it is [not ]{.class_4095}his own (Rom. 7:17).

3\. It comes from something in him that is part of the dead man who was nailed---and still is nailed---to the Cross (Rom. 7:18).

The real "ME" of the Christian---the "ego"---is the New Man born of the Spirit who "lives" (Gal. 2:20), but this "new ME" is Jesus Christ Himself! (cf. comments under Phil. 3:9 and Gal. 6:15).

1\. The good that a Christian does is NOT his own (Gal. 2:20).

2\. Yet it is what the [real ]{.class_4095}"him" desires (Rom. 7:22).

3\. The good fruit comes from something in him that is part of the New Man who rose from the dead (Rom. 7:1--6)!

Paul is not discussing his "bodily life" (as in the corrupt [NEB ]{.class_4095}and [Phillips]{.class_4095}). He is discussing his REAL life, which is Jesus Christ Himself.

Rendall says that he can see "no ground" for rendering ["de"]{.class_4095} (Gk.[---]{.class_4105}☐[☐]{.class_4107}) as ["NEVERTHELESS I live"]{.class_4096}; he would prefer saying "And I live." But here, as in 80 percent of his work, Rev. Rendall is mistaken. ["Nevertheless"]{.class_4096} is the only word that could be used in the context of the passage, for Paul has just made a shocking and remarkable statement---["I am crucified with Christ."]{.class_4096} This calls for some explanation, so the ["nevertheless"]{.class_4096} of the [King James]{.class_4095} 1611 is the correct reading even though the apostate ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} (1963) and the Communistic [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) use Rendall's substitution (Phillips and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}also follow this error).

["And the life which I now live in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). This is what caused [Phillips ]{.class_4095}and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}to translate "bodily life"; and this, of course, comes from deliberate refusal to observe 2 Corinthians 10:3 and Galatians 4:14. This time ["in the flesh"]{.class_4096} has no reference to the activity of the body, but has to do with the fact that the believer's new heavenly life (not "bodily") has been placed into an earthen vessel and must be lived out inside the skin and bone restrictions of a corpse (Gal. 2:20).

Since every commentator from Chrysostom to English refused to identify the word ["flesh"]{.class_4096} as flesh---due to the fear of being called "gnostics"---neither Phillips nor the translators of the [NEB ]{.class_4095}were able to understand the text before us.

The meaning is clear.

"I am hanging on the cross [right this minute]{.class_4095}; and though I am dying a slow agonizing death, in God's sight, I am already dead and buried because I am in Christ and Christ already has been buried. But even though I am a dead man, I am walking around in the body like a zombie because there is something in me that wants to go on []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_64}living, and it wants to go on living for God. This "something" is a risen Lord who was revealed [in me]{.class_4095}. I accepted Him by faith, not by works, and so I continue to live by faith, not by works."

Every real believer is a schizo (Rom. 7:15--23). (You see, the word ["peculiar"]{.class_4096} meant "peculiar" in 1 Pet. 2:9, no matter what your Greek professor told you.)

A Communist said: ["Christ died on His cross, but you make a living off it."]{.class_4095}

Someone has said: ["A Christian worker is good, a worker in Christ is better, but the best of all is Christ IN a worker. "]{.class_4095}

A real Christian can be analyized by his name: "CHRIST" plus "IAN." IAN stands for [I AM NOTHING.]{.class_4095}

When any Christian sits down on a throne and people kiss his ring or his feet and spread carpets before him on which to walk, you can know one thing for certain: that man is no more "Christian" than Judas Iscariot.

Instead of "the church where everybody is [somebody]{.class_4095}," the signs should read: "The church where everybody is [nobody]{.class_4095}" (2 Cor. 4:5).

["I live by the faith of the Son of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). That is, by faith placed in Him as a loving Saviour and a living Lord. And the ["of"]{.class_4096} can be a genitive according to Ephesians 2:8. "The faith which the Son of God gave me is the means by which I live." Dummelow's pitiful exposition of the verse places the whole application into [past history ]{.class_4095}as it was applied to one man who was giving his [personal opinion ]{.class_4095}about the matter. Far from it (God forbid!), Galatians 2:20 is one of the greatest verses in the entire Bible on the relationship of the Twentieth-Century believer to the Lord Jesus Christ.

["I do not frustrate the grace of God\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 21), even if YOU do, Peter!

["\...for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21). Righteousness does NOT come by the Law (Rom. 10:1--4); it comes by imputation (Rom. 4:1--5). It is imputed to no man who is working to gain it (Rom. 4:5). The man who refuses to accept Christ frustrates GRACE; but since this great truth runs contrary to the standard theology of Calvinism ("Irresistible grace," "Unconditional election," etc.), the word ["frustrate"]{.class_4096} must be thrown out of the text as soon as possible by "Fundamentalists" and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_65}"Conservatives" who have been sitting around letting the world go to Hell while they trust God is "calling out the elect."

1\. Phillips: It should have been ["stultify."]{.class_4095} (How's that for "updating the archaic [King James ]{.class_4095}Elizabethan English"?)

2\. Kenneth Wuest: It should have been ["thwart."]{.class_4095} (And you were sucker enough to believe that the[ King James]{.class_4095} was "out-of-date"?)

3\. [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961): It should have been ["nullify"]{.class_4095} ([RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946, also.)

4\. [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881): It should have been ["reject."]{.class_4095}

5\. [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901): It should have been ["make void."]{.class_4095}

Let's just face it, brother. Since 1880, nothing has been going on in the circles of "accredited scholarship" but the confounding and the destruction of the word of God.

"Frustration" is the proper descriptive term where someone prevents somebody from doing what they desire to do. Now, John Calvin (and Kuyper, Dabney, Hodge, Strong, Warfield, Machen, Ross, Gilpin, and the Puritans) would never allow that any man could frustrate God when it came to SALVATION. All of them believed and taught that the "elect" were saved, willing or unwilling, and that "conversion" and "repentance" [followed ]{.class_4095}the "New Birth" (see remarks under Eph 1:5). All of them tried to force every saint into a compulsory conversion as Paul was apparently converted (Acts 9:3--6); that is, knocked down, overcome, blinded, born again, and converted BEFORE repentance or faith. This is the teaching today of all hyper-Calvinists, hardshell Baptists, primitive Baptists, and "Apostolic" groups; and it is propagated by L. R. Shelton, Rolfe Barnard, and Arthur W. Pink.

So out goes ["frustrate"]{.class_4096} from the text, and the substitutions are about as Scriptural as the [Farmer's Almanac. ]{.class_4095}Phillips and Wuest have the general idea, but both of them use archaic language which is not Twentieth-Century usage. The [NEB, RSV, ASV]{.class_4095}, and [RV ]{.class_4095}conjure up a doctrine which is not even imagined by the inspired writer. No man could "nullify" the grace of God if he tried, for it is there whether you reject it or not. The man in the context has not REJECTED grace, for he is a saved man; he has FRUSTRATED grace by implying that the works of the Law are necessary along with it (vs. 12--16). Grace is not able to accomplish her work where she has been mixed with the Law (Rom. 11:6).

The [AV]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ354.xhtml_page_66} 1611 is absolutely correct---100 percent. The "new translations" (anything from 1881 to 2000) are "improvements" in the sense that a washtub is an improvement over an automatic washer-drier.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml}

# Chapter 3 {.heading_116}

3:1 O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?

2 This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

3 Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?

4 Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.

5 He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?

6 Even as Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.

7 Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham.

8 And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, In thee shall all nations be blessed.

9 So then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful Abraham.

Verse one is apparent to anyone but a commentator or reviser. It refers back to Galatians 1:6, and it is no longer lambasting Peter. The gist of it is: "You're acting like a bunch of fools! Who in the world came through there and put a hex on you to get you to turn from the truth of the Gospel and act like you're acting? You heard about Christ's crucifixion; you understood my message about it perfectly; and I described it so vividly you could see it! And my own conduct proved what I just said in Galatians 1:20. How come you dropped [that ]{.class_4095}and started all this 'works' mess?"

(Now that is no "living letter," for the only "living letter" is the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611. If you had the "originals" of Galatians, they would be as impractical as the Greek text of Eberhard Nestle!)

Christ was not only ["set forth, crucified"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_68} among them by sermon, He was ["set forth, crucified"]{.class_4096} by the presence of Paul (Cf. Gal. 2:20 and 2 Cor. 4:10).

Since most of the commentators and revisers missed this obvious truth, they have bitten at some bait offered to them by the dead-orthodox philosophers at Alexandria. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} slyly says: "The genuineness of the words ['among you']{.class_4096} is very doubtful." Consequently, the [RV, RSV, ASV, Phillips, NEB]{.class_4095}, and Dummelow omit them. The majority of Greek manuscripts do NOT consider the words (Gk. ["en humin"]{.class_4095}) to be "doubtful." Two families of manuscripts have them, plus the Old Latin and the Harclean Syriac. Von Soden retains the words, although Westcott and Hort, Tischendorf, and Griesbach ditch them as quickly as Origen took them out of the correct manuscripts. But Origen's meddling with the Authorized Version of A.D. 150 does not stop here. He has also stricken out four more words from the correct text; and this omission is preserved in the corrupt ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} and you-know-what-else---[RV, RSV, ASV, ESV, NRSV, NASV, NAB, NIV, TNIV, CEV, NCV]{.class_4095}, and anything else somebody would like to call "a bible."

The words ["that ye should not obey the truth"]{.class_4096} have been taken out of every "new Bible" since 1880, clearly bracketing their revisers. "Obedience to the truth" is not one of the characteristics of Bible revisers since 1880. The substitution of a foreign text for the Holy Bible---while still professing to be revising the Holy Bible---denominates every "translator" from 1880 to 2000. They are men who [disobey ]{.class_4095}the truth. Therefore, the words of Galatians 3:1 hit them like scalding water poured on a dog, and they holler "the words are not genuine!" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20, p. 117).

Well, to tell [the truth ]{.class_4095}about it, the words ["that ye should not obey the truth"]{.class_4096} are found in the Hesychian family (C), the Syrian family ([Receptus]{.class_4095}), the Vulgate, the Syriac, and the majority of manuscripts. Their "genuineness" is questioned because Jehudi did a lot of knife-work on the New Testament, and the revisers---Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Berkeley, Lamsa, Fenton, Godby, [ASV, RSV, RV, NEB]{.class_4095}, and Little Bo Peep---thought that "mutilation" was [authenticity ]{.class_4095}(see detailed discussion in [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} 1970).

The Galatians, as the modern revisers, had not obeyed the truth. They were frustrating ["the grace of God"]{.class_4096} by toying with the idea that works might add to their righteousness before God. The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 preserves for us the words of the Holy Spirit as they were given to Paul in A.D. 51--53. In spite of Conservative scholarship, the public can still find out what God said about the matter instead of what the faculties at Alexandria and Bob Jones University THOUGHT about the matter.

["This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2). There can be only one answer. Those on the day of Pentecost ["HEARD"]{.class_4096} (Acts 2:37). Those in Antioch of Pisidia HEARD (Acts 13:44). Cornelius came to LISTEN (Acts 10:33), and ["faith cometh by HEARING"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 10:17).

Observe, further, that Paul is not addressing Christians only. The tirade began with ["O foolish Galatians"]{.class_4096}! This allows for the unregenerate in the community to take warning. "You have the Spirit do you? Well, how did you get it? If you got it by the works of the Law, you just THINK you got it!" (cf. Gal. 5:2--4).

Williams bravely suggests that verse 2 destroys the foundations of the Roman Catholic and Greek Orthodox churches (although he is careful not to say "Catholic" or "Orthodox"). However, there is no need to play "Button, Button, Who Has the Button?" with the text. It neatly destroys the foundation of the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} published by evangelical Conservatives, for on page 120 of that work is the statement that the Galatians got the Holy Spirit by [WORKS]{.class_4095}! The writer (Huxtable) has assumed that the Galatians had to have water baptism and laying on of hands (Acts 19:2--6) to receive the Spirit, entirely forgetting that the converts of Acts 19 were Jewish proselytes who had received the message of Luke 3 (John the Baptist) under a dispensational hang-over (Apollos---see Acts 19:1--3). No, there was no "laying on of hands" on Gentiles after Acts 8. The saved Galatians had received the Holy Spirit exactly as the household of Cornelius had received Him---["by the hearing of faith."]{.class_4096}

But why stop now? The text also destroys the foundation of baby-sprinkling in the Lutheran and Episcopalian communions. But why []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_70}stop there? It also destroys the foundation of Karl Marx, Charles Darwin, and the National Education Association; for all of these have assumed that there is only one spirit at work in the universe, and that it is in everyone at the same time---from birth. Our text states that there is a time when you receive God's Spirit, and that if you receive any spirit by any other way than by faith in ["the gospel of the grace of God"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 1:8--9), you are just as lost as the man who preached you the message!

["Are ye so foolish?"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). We have caught our children in the jam again! They translated the first ["foolish"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1) as "idiots" (Phillips) and "thoughtless" (Dummelow); and now, when [the same Greek word shows up,]{.class_4095} Phillips changes it from a noun to an adjective, and Dummelow alters his "thoughtless" to ["folly.]{.class_4095}" (Have you ever heard a man criticize the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 for not being "uniform" in its translation of words? Was he just trying to be funny, or what?)

["\...having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). Yes, according to the standard theology of the Wesleyan Methodists, Nazarenes, Holiness, Pentecostals, Assemblies of God, and "Churches of God."

But the issue is of little importance, for these groups now make up less than 1 percent of professing Christianity. Their preachers have been wallowing in TV for so long that "standards" are a thing of the past. Occasionally, one will see the long dress and long hair, minus lipstick and rings; but now most of the hippies dress in this fashion, and the "holiness" women who still dress this way can usually be spotted by a much easier method than their "peculiar dress." They can be spotted by the stony-faced visage that one often sees on nuns. This "butcher face" is quite characteristic of women active in "church work" in any group where the adherent is trying to perfect holiness by altering her FLESH. A Holiness conference is quite a sight: the dresses are knee length, but they fit like a scuba diver's suit and are so thin a mosquito could fly through them without breaking a wing. There is a noticeable absence of jewelry, but there is no absence of flowers the size of cabbages and shoes that must have been made for a Bourbon Street dancer.

You cannot do anything with the flesh. You can prime it, pamper it, primp it, crucify it, cultivate it, educate it, excercise it, and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_71}suppress it, and it is still flesh (cf. comments under Phil. 3:21---"vile flesh").

No, the Christian life is begun by grace through faith when the Holy Spirit enters and seals the believer (Eph. 1:13). It is, therefore, to be lived by grace through faith (Gal. 3:11) in the power of the Holy Spirit (see Gal. 6:14). (The translation "make a finish"---Roman Catholic [Confraternity]{.class_4095}, 1941---only confuses the text; and of course, it is not the word of God.)

["Have ye suffered so many things in vain?"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4). It would appear that they had suffered persecution because they had believed the Gospel of grace (Gal. 6:12). All this suffering would not benefit them at all if they now abandoned their position and went over to the side of those who were heckling them. The unsaved man will never severely persecute a man who puts WORKS in as a condition of salvation, even where those works do not match his own works.

You see, self-righteousness is universal. It is the result of Genesis 3. Therefore, ecumenical ground can always be found if the religious leader or teacher will only omit ONE thing: [free justification on the grounds of the righteousness of Someone else.]{.class_4095} As long as this is not brought up, the old, dead, decayed, stinking humus of "human nature" can express itself. Even though Catholics, Atheists, Buddhists, Agnostics, Mohammedans, and Taoists may not agree on [what kind of works]{.class_4095} are necessary to justify self, they can all agree that everyone is good enough to do [SOMETHING ]{.class_4095}to justify himself (Isa. 64:6). This explains the persecution that Paul was subjected to all of his life, and the persecution that Luther was subjected to all of his life. They lived by Galatians 1:10 and 6:12. To put it bluntly---

1\. You are no good and neither were your forefathers (negative).

2\. The best you can do will land you flat in Hell (negative).

3\. Unless you find a righteousness superior to your own, you are just as good as in the Lake of Fire with the Devil (negative).

4\. Go on and work at it! The road to Hell is paved with good intentions (negative).

5\. Try your best (Phil. 3:8), and you will fellowship in eternity with whoremongers, liars, and murderers (Rev. 22:15) (negative).

6\. Your only hope is to accept a free gift from God which He will []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_72}not sell you (Rom. 5:15) or let you earn (Rom. 6:23), and for which you can never fully pay Him back! (negative).

Now! You preach THAT and watch how "well-liked" you are in your community! !

That is the message of Romans and Galatians, but modern Conservatives---especially those engaged in building day schools and Christian grade schools---are just as panicky about that message as they are a two-headed rattlesnake. The whole trick today is how to avoid offense so the income will not collapse; that is, the leaders in the apostasy of the Twentieth Century are the educational leaders in the Body of Jesus Christ! (see prefatory remarks).

["If it be YET in vain" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). That is, maybe there is still a chance that you'll get over your fear and return to the right position. Notice the same structure in 1 Corinthians 15:2. Some of the believing was ["in vain" ]{.class_4096}because it was a head belief, not a heart belief (Rom. 6:17, 10:9--10).

["He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit\...doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?]{.class_4096}" (vs. 5). It is taken for granted by all of the commentators (and revisers) that the ["he"]{.class_4096} of the passage is God. The reason for this is verse 14. It is taken for granted (by all) that ["ministering]{.class_4095} the Spirit of God" to a congregation is to be identified with the ["reception]{.class_4095} of the Spirit" (vs. 14) at the time of conversion.

To cover up this obvious disparity, the new "Bibles" have altered the word ["ministereth"]{.class_4096} to ["supplieth"]{.class_4095} ([ASV, RV, RSV]{.class_4095}, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, etc.). The Catholics have rewritten it as "gives the Spirit to you." This reading is followed by the [New English Bible]{.class_4095}, which, of course, is more Roman than any Roman Bible yet printed---see Matthew 16:18. (Phillips joins the Catholic Church at this point.)

But still, the ["among you"]{.class_4096} (which they all threw out in verse 1!) causes trouble, so it must be altered to ["in you,"]{.class_4095} thus completing the private interpretation of the [RV, ASV, RSV, New ASV, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, NEB]{.class_4095}, and the other thirty forgeries. Now that the text has been altered, rounded, shaped, flattened, smoothed, and sandpapered, you will [have to read ]{.class_4095}the ["he"]{.class_4096} as "God!"

Nice work---and nice agreement.
