Having altered the Reformation text to teach the private []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_73}interpretations of the committees who could not understand the text (because they struck out the verse that condemned them---see vs. 1), the alteration is passed on through 100 years (1880--1980) by Dr. Green ([ASV]{.class_4095}) via Bob Jones, Columbia Bible College, and the [Sword of the Lord]{.class_4095} ([these institutions all recommend the ASV or its backslidden counterpart, the "New"ASV]{.class_4095}).

Now, as the text stands, the ["he"]{.class_4096} is no reference to God at all. The ["he"]{.class_4096} is referring to the ["who"]{.class_4096} of verse 1. "God" is not in verses 1--5, and Abraham believing God (vs. 6) had no Spirit "supplied" to him. In verse 8 God is justifying ["the heathen through faith"]{.class_4096} and is not ministering or "supplying Spirit" or miracles to anyone. However, [someone ]{.class_4095}has[ "bewitched"]{.class_4096} the Galatians after Paul left, and [he ]{.class_4095}is "ministering" to them! Furthermore, if he is going to compete with Paul, he is going to have to [work miracles ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 15:19; 2 Cor. 12:12) just like Simon the Sorcerer (Acts 8:9, 13).

Whether or not such a person was actually there is immaterial. Paul is challenging the Galatians and the Judaizers who have "hindered them" (Gal. 5:7). There is no evidence at all that God "worked miracles" among (or in) the Galatians after Paul left. The ["miracles"]{.class_4096} are the apostolic signs given to the apostles, and every Gentile church that had manifestations of them outside of apostolic preaching (1 Cor. 12:10) [had them performed by PEOPLE]{.class_4095} (1 Cor. 12:28), NOT GOD.

The entire body of conservative scholarship, then, may be ignored. The fact that 100 percent of the Fundamental, Conservative, Evangelical, and orthodox scholars agree that the ["he"]{.class_4096} of Galatians 3:5 means "God" means nothing except that it does NOT mean "God." (As we have asked elsewhere, why do you change the text unless you are trying to prove that it says something it doesn't say?) The word is ["he,"]{.class_4096} not "God"; the word is ["ministereth,"]{.class_4096} not "supplieth"; and the miracles were ["among"]{.class_4096} them, not "in" them. [You changed it because it crossed your subjective prejudices.]{.class_4095}

The meaning of the text is clear; and in spite of the bungling of the Greek lexicons, grammars, commentators, revisers, editors, teachers, preachers, and faculty members, the thought is as follows:

"Who is that bird up there that got you off the track? Is he a miracle worker? [I was! ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_74}Is he a channel through whom you are receiving the truth? Is he preaching in the demonstration and power of the Spirit? (1 Cor. 2:4). All right, if he is, is he carrying on his ministry by following the Old Testament Mosaic Law, or is he carrying on his ministry by preaching the truth of the Gospel and living by faith?"

(If your "Greek Testament" failed to pick up that line of thought, you would do well to trade it in for a copy of the [Training Union Quarterly for Juniors]{.class_4095}.)

["Even as Abraham"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). Now there is where all of the commentators hit the skids. They reasoned that since God promised to supply the Spirit to Abraham's seed (vs. 14)---and this was connected with justification by faith (vs. 8)---verse 5 simply had to fit into the subsequent context. It never occurred to them for a moment that it fitted the PRECEDING context, for the words ["Even as"]{.class_4096} followed!

But the ["Even as"]{.class_4096} is plainly a reference to ["the hearing of faith"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5) just mentioned and has no connection at all with ["He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5).

If this seems a little "stretched," the reader should carefully study the comment by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (Commentary, pg. 1265) where the commentator has attempted to force the two verses together. The reasoning is that just as Abraham obtained justification by faith, you obtain the Spirit by faith; and since God supplied the justification to Abraham, He [supplies ]{.class_4095}the Spirit to the Galatians. But one can see at a glance that [this ]{.class_4095}is the reasoning that is "stretched." Justification is not a ["supply,"]{.class_4095} nor is it a ["ministry"]{.class_4095}; it is a judicial act of God whereby He declares the sinner to be righteous (Rom. 3:26). Furthermore, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown completely ignored the middle of verse 5---["and worketh miracles among you."]{.class_4096} The commentators sense their inconsistency and bow out with the word "obtained"---substituted for "supplied"---[although they had just altered the correct text]{.class_4095} (of vs. 5) [from ]{.class_4095}["ministereth"]{.class_4096}[ to "supplieth!"]{.class_4095}

Typical.

Once you start butchering the words of the Holy Spirit, you have to keep on till you have nothing but mincemeat---[or baloney.]{.class_4095}

["Even as Abraham believed God, and it was ACCOUNTED TO HIM FOR RIGHTEOUSNESS"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). No Holy Spirit is "supplied" to Abraham. None of the commentators ever got the significance of John 3:3, 5 where it dealt with David, Moses, and Abraham; for none of them were born of the Spirit, and none of them were "sons of God" (Gal. 4:6). The only point where Abraham's salvation matches the salvation of the Galatians was on the point of [imputed righteousness ]{.class_4095}given by [faith ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 4:1--5). In all other points they differ.

1\. Abraham was [physically ]{.class_4095}circumcised; the Galatians were [spiritually ]{.class_4095}circumcised (Col. 2:11).

2\. Abraham was not in the Body of Christ; the Galatians were.

3\. Abraham went to Paradise in Abraham's Bosom; the dead Galatians went to the third heaven.

4\. Abraham had no completed atonement for his sins; the Galatians did.

5\. Abraham's soul was stuck to his body; the Galatians had their souls cut loose from their bodies (Col. 2:11; Rom. 7:1--4).

The 500 scholars who tried to equate verse 5 with God's dealing with Abraham in verse 6---in regard to the ministry of the Holy Spirit---did not spend much time reading the Bible, at least not seriously.

The only similarity between verse 5 and verse 6 is that whoever is ministering to the Galatians must be preaching salvation[ "by the hearing of faith," ]{.class_4096}for that is how Abraham believed God (cf. Gen. 15:6; Rom. 4:1--5).

["Know ye therefore that they which are of faith, the same are the children of Abraham"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). Whoever is ministering to them (vss. 1, 5) is NOT a "child of Abraham" (John 8:39, Rom. 11:1--4) unless he received the Spirit by faith; and they---the Galatians---will never be counted ["children of Abraham"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29) unless they have received the Spirit by faith. It is apparent that the reference is to [spiritual ]{.class_4095}children of Abraham, not [physical ]{.class_4095}children (vs. 16).

The reader should not be upset by the use of the word ["children"]{.class_4096} for the Greek word "sons," used by the Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}edition and its twin sisters the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946), for the same two corrupt Bibles ([ASV ]{.class_4095}and [RSV]{.class_4095}) have refused to translate the word for[ "child"]{.class_4096} ([paida]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_76}, Gk.) in Acts 4:30, where it dealt with the Deity of Christ, and have altered it to "servant"! The accusation, therefore, that the [AV ]{.class_4095}has translated nouns incorrectly is simply more rock-slinging from people who live in glass houses (cf. comment under Gal. 3:29).

Phenomenon No. 7.

7\. Fault is found with the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 for refusal to translate the word ["huios"]{.class_4095} as "son" every time, and yet, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV]{.class_4095} also refuse to translate it "son" every time! Observe Acts 5:21; Matthew 27:9; Luke 1:16; Acts 7:37; etc.

You see, the real difference between "modern Bibles" and the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 is that "modern Bibles" are not Bibles and the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 is a Bible. "Uniformity of translation" is not an issue, nor has it ever been. Those who complain about it are just as guilty as anyone else, and if you don't believe that, you are either ignorant or crooked. [The evidence can be produced in court.]{.class_4095}

["Children of Abraham"]{.class_4096} is the correct expression, and the substitution of the word "sons" means nothing except that someone is always trying to get rid of the authority of the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text. The Catholic Bible goes a step further and says that Abraham was not declared to be "righteous" at all; rather "it was credited to him as [justice]{.class_4095}," whatever in the world that means!

["And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the heathen"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). The Scriptures evidently have one of the attributes of God (cf. Rom. 9:17)! At times, Paul (as any "Bibliolater"!) seems to have the nasty habit of inserting the word ["scripture"]{.class_4096} instead of the word "God." The Scriptures raise people up (Rom. 9:17), foresee future events, and preach to people BEFORE they are written! There was no "book of Genesis" at the time of Genesis, and the only person who spoke these words was the Lord God Almighty, who here is called[ "THE SCRIPTURE."]{.class_4096}

Now, we cannot have this! If anyone knows the attitude of the revisers and commentators towards the Scriptures, they know that it is intolerable to think that the Scriptures could be anything more than poorly copied transcriptions of some original nobody ever found. Psalm 138:2 is never taken at face value by any major commentator from Origen to English, for all "accredited scholars" []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_77}are terrorized by the thought that intellectuals might think they really BELIEVED the Bible as it stands. This would cast them out of the "Scholars' Union" (as Dr. Hills so aptly puts it) and effect their incomes and social images terribly. And horror of horrors! They would no longer be considered as intelligent men by [Communists and Atheists]{.class_4095} (and that is very important these days!).

Dummelow and E. F. Harrison duck the first twelve words in the verse. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown duck the first twelve words in the verse. Phillips irons the problem out by adding ["in the words spoken to\...."]{.class_4095} Only Williams tells the truth of it. Williams simply states that the Bible preached the Gospel before the Scriptures had an existence! (How on earth could a man make a statement like that and then not explain it?)

But now the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} shows up, like Sir Galahad on the white horse, to rescue us from exalting the Scriptures too highly, for here (Vol. 20, p. 125) we are told that the word ["scripture"]{.class_4096} was not a substitute for the One who spoke to Abraham---although that is what the text says. It is rather "put in place of the announcement which Scripture records as having been made." Now doesn't that clear up things?

If this is true, how do you explain the "announcement" as having the ability to "FORESEE"? Our text says the Scripture foresaw what God would do!

No, you might as well quit. Paul nearly equates the Scriptures with God on two occasions and possibly a third (Heb. 4:12--13), and the small ["word" ]{.class_4096}(not the Incarnate Word) of Psalm 138:2 is ["magnified"]{.class_4096} over ["the name which is above every name"]{.class_4096} (Phil. 2:9). This explains beyond further discussion why the old [AV]{.class_4095} 1611[ King James ]{.class_4095}edition of the Holy Bible has been the target of man's hatred since the day it arrived. Conservatives and Fundamentalists despise it with the same fury and envy that Catholics and Communists despise it. (When a "new version" comes out, it is never compared to any other version but the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611. In the last half of the Twentieth Century, even Nestle's Greek text has been joined to the [King James]{.class_4095} text in one edition so that it \[Nestle's\] will be able to stay in business!)

["And the scripture, foreseeing that God would justify []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_78}the HEATHEN through faith, preached before the gospel unto Abraham, saying, IN THEE SHALL ALL NATIONS BE BLESSED"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). Alford, DeWette, Vincent, Meyer, and Rendall have some changes they would like to make on the text; but of course, they are of no consequence and are not worth reproducing.

The verse is the signal for Augustine (A.D. 354--430), Jerome (A.D. 345), and the other "Christian" evolutionists to drive home the ancient blasphemy known as "Postmillennialism." The reasoning is as follows:

1\. The Gospel of Galatians 1:8--9 and 1 Corinthians 15:1--4 is [the same one ]{.class_4095}that the Scriptures preached to Abraham in Genesis 12:1--3.

2\. Therefore, the literal and physical blessings which are promised to the children of Abraham (Isaac, Jacob, and Israel) were transferred to the New Testament Christians and are now [the property of the church.]{.class_4095}

3\. Wherever these promises refer to a literal restoration of Israel in the physical land of Palestine (Isa. 2, 11, 65; Joel 2, etc.), they are to be "spiritualized" according to the allegorical method taught at Alexandria (Origen) and applied to the Roman Catholic Church.

The Reformers took almost the same view; and consequently, we find the "Covenant Children" of the Dutch Reformed, Christian Reformed, and Reformed Churches (see Holland, Michigan) still using baptism in the place of circumcision.

Here, we come upon Phenomenon No. 8: No English scholar since 1880 who rejected the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 can read an English Bible.

You see, immediately following one of the strongest possible descriptions of the authority of the Scriptures, the Holy Ghost decided to test DeWette, Dummelow, Jamieson, Clarke, Fausset, Lange, Brown, Ellicott, Lipsius, Rendall, Dr. Green, Meyer, Alford, Olshausen, Davidson, Ruckert, Vincent, and Huxtable to see if they could read ENGLISH. They couldn't!

[Genesis 12:3 is not mentioned in Galatians 3:8. ]{.class_4095}Galatians 3:8 has nothing at all to do with Genesis 12:3. This is obvious to a fourth-grade student of English.

Genesis 12:3 says ["families." ]{.class_4096}Galatians 3:8 says ["nations."]{.class_4096}

You say, "Well, why haggle over terminology. Don't the words []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_79}mean the same thing?"

But my dear fellow, you cannot back out now after finding fault with the terminology of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 on words like "but" instead of ["neither"]{.class_4096} and "yet" instead of ["still"]{.class_4096} and "was" for ["have been."]{.class_4096} [You old GNAT STRAINER, you]{.class_4095} (Matt. 23:24).

You see, the highly scientific and accurate [King James Bible]{.class_4095} has here "rightly divided" two great truths which have always been and will always be separate truths. It has done this by using two different words, and the fact that every cross-reference Bible from the Hexapla to the ["New ASV"]{.class_4095} refers the reader to Genesis 12:3 means nothing at all. [There is no reference whatsoever to Genesis 12:3 in Galatians 3:8;]{.class_4095} and if you confound the cross-references, you have disarranged the truth of God as well as[ "the truth of the gospel."]{.class_4096}

The reference in Galatians 3:8 is clearly to Genesis 18:18, AFTER the promise of the chosen seed (Isaac) who appears in the context of Galatians 3! To confound the [physical ]{.class_4095}promises of [physical ]{.class_4095}blessings to the [literal ]{.class_4095}seed of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob with the promise of the Spirit to the [spiritual seed of Abraham]{.class_4095}---Isaac, typifying Jesus Christ---is one of the greatest, deepest, most profound, intense, and far-reaching errors that any heretic ever espoused.

Is it not a "stultifying thought" (to quote Phillips; see comments on Gal. 2:21) to realize that not one major English reviser or commentator, since Paul, could read? [Not even Bullinger could find the reference!]{.class_4095} So help me, he cites Genesis 12:3!

Now, the serious student of the Bible, who has learned to ignore any and all corrections of the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text, sees immediately that not only is the word ["nations"]{.class_4096} missing from Genesis 12:3, but no ["seed"]{.class_4096} is mentioned in Genesis 12:1--3. The Holy Spirit has carefully guarded the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 from the outrageous muddle into which the ["New ASV"]{.class_4095} and other translations got.

The ["seed"]{.class_4096} of Galatians 3:16, 19 is the seed promised in Genesis 15:5 and mentioned in Genesis 18:18. That is "ex cathedra" interpretation from the Throne of God; or (as some of the black sheep say), "If any man believe that Galatians 3:8 is a reference to Genesis 12:3, LET HIM BE ANATHEMA" (Council of Trent, 1546).

["Preached before THE GOSPEL"]{.class_4096} is a reference to some good news preached to Abraham. It was the New Testament Gospel only in [general typology ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_80}and only then in reference to one part of the New Testament Gospel: the death of Christ to make the Spirit available (vss. 12--16). The Lord does NOT preach the death, burial, and resurrection to Abraham as such, although He foresees the death, burial, and resurrection of His Son at this time. Abraham only "sees Christ's day" (John 8:56) in the sense that he manifested that he had enough faith to believe in the physical resurrection of Isaac (Heb. 11:17--19)---and what reader could miss the reference in Genesis 22:18! [It is the cross-reference to Genesis 18:18 and Galatians 3:8.]{.class_4095} Note, further, that Abraham still varies from our salvation in that his JUSTIFICATION (James 2:21) is connected with Genesis 22, while his RIGHTEOUSNESS is found in Genesis 15!

Ah, the unsearchable riches of the King's English! How scientific it is compared with the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} and the ["New" English Bible]{.class_4095} and the "New" Morality!

["So then they which be OF FAITH are blessed WITH faithful Abraham"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). The verse is self-explanatory. If you want the SPIRITUAL BLESSINGS he got---imputed righteousness and justification---you must exercise faith as he did: i.e., faith in what God SAID. (If God said ["nations"]{.class_4096} in one place and ["families"]{.class_4096} in another, you'd flat better believe what He said or you're just liable to miss a blessing, doctor!)

3:10 For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, Cursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them.

11 But that no man is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, The just shall live by faith.

12 And the law is not of faith: but, The man that doeth them shall live in them.

13 Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree:

14 That the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

15 Brethren, I speak after the manner of men; Though it be but a man's covenant, yet if it be confirmed, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto.

16 Now to Abraham and his seed were the promises made. He saith not, And to seeds, as of many; but as of one, And to thy seed, which is Christ.

17 And this I say, that the covenant, that was confirmed before of God in Christ, the law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, cannot disannul, that it should make the promise of none effect.

18 For if the inheritance be of the law, it is no more of promise: but God gave it to Abraham by promise.

Now we are in a position to understand the remainder of the chapter. As long as the reader insists on ignoring the Reformation text and reading ["families"]{.class_4096} for ["nations"]{.class_4096} and running to the wrong cross-references, he will learn nothing about the ["strong meat"]{.class_4096} of the word (Heb. 5:14). What follows is now well defined; and it completely contradicts the eschatology (teachings on prophecy) of the Alexandrian school led by Philo, Saccas, Plato, Origen, Clement, and Pantaenus, and espoused by Calvin, Berkhof, Augustine, Jerome, and the Ecumenical Council of 1980.

1\. There are two issues---physical and spiritual.

2\. The spiritual issue is not connected with the physical, material blessings promised to Israel in the Old Testament, except where they apply to the Millennium (vss. 18, 29).

3\. The ["seed"]{.class_4096} is spiritual, the ["children"]{.class_4096} are spiritual, and ["the promise of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} is spiritual (vs. 14).

4\. The ["promises"]{.class_4096} (plural) of verse 16 include physical as well as spiritual blessings, but the physical blessings go to the believing remnant of Israel in the Old Testament (Rom. 3:1--3) and to the restored remnant in the Millennium (Rom. 11:12, 15).

5\. The ["promise"]{.class_4096} (singular) of verse 14 is the subject of Galatians 3:2--3, 5 and 4:6.

["For as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse: for it is written, CURSED IS EVERY ONE that continueth not IN ALL THINGS which are written in the BOOK OF THE LAW to do them"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_82} (vs. 10; cf. Deut. 28:61, 27:26). The writer of the [LXX]{.class_4095}, writing more than 100 years after the death of Paul, has altered the text of Deuteronomy 27:26 to match Paul, but this is Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Alexandria. It is designed to kid the faculty into thinking that [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}came from a Greek Old Testament that "Christians" used. (There is more evidence that bunnies come from Easter eggs.)

The verse is quite clear. The man is cursed who KNOWS the Law (Rom. 7:7), and the comment of the ecumenical council (John 7:49) is just as wrong as wrong can be. ["All things which are written in the BOOK OF THE LAW"]{.class_4096} is quite a "chaw" (updated English!) to put it conservatively.

["The book of the law"]{.class_4096} covers everything in the book of Deuteronomy and often everything in the Pentateuch (note Josh 1:7--8, 8:31--32; Luke 2:22-27, 24:44; 2 Kings 14:6, etc.). As we have said before, Galatians 2:16 has reference to a great deal more than some ceremonial law given to Israel. The moral commandments are in ["the book of the law,"]{.class_4096} and ["the book of the law"]{.class_4096} is chock-full (updating the English!) of rules and regulations on conduct towards man and beast. If you are trying to get to Heaven by WORKS, make sure that bacon, oysters, lobsters, rabbit, crabs, catfish, and pork chops never appear on your table (Lev. 11). Make sure you don't shave when you get up in the morning (Lev. 19:27), and don't hold any grudges (Lev. 19:18). If you got tattooed while you were in the Navy (Lev. 19:28), you'd better get a deer hunter to skin you---otherwise, you're as good as in Hell with the door shut and the key thrown away!

["But that NO MAN is justified by the law in the sight of God, it is evident: for, THE JUST SHALL LIVE BY FAITH"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11; cf. Rom. 4:2; Gal. 2:16). The Seventh-day Adventist cannot worm out of it, although he tries. First, he says the Law is the [ceremonial law]{.class_4095}, which it is NOT (see previous verse). Next he says that Christ PLUS the Law justifies, which it does NOT (see Gal. 5:4 and comments). Then he segregates Matthew 5:19 and 1 John 2:4 from ["the book of the law"]{.class_4096} and insists that the ["commandments"]{.class_4096} hold over but the ["law" ]{.class_4096}doesn't. By now he has done enough sky-diving acrobatics to put Barnum and Bailey out of business. The ["commandments"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_83} were on stone (2 Cor. 3:7) and went with ["the book of the law"]{.class_4096} (cf. comments under 2:17--21).

["The just shall live by faith" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11) has been altered by the omission of the word ["his"]{.class_4096} found in Habakkuk 2:4. We shall comment on this in a moment.

Bengel and Alford are responsible for the corrupt reading of the [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881), which see. Canon Cook and Delitzsch give us some hot air about what the "original" manuscripts said on Habakkuk 2:4, but since neither of them had access to them, the remarks are rather pointless. The [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) reading does not even quote the passage or make an attempt to do so, and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961) is way out in left field with a private interpretation that would make the Assumption of Mary sound like Bible doctrine.

Now, the word ["his"]{.class_4096} is missing from Paul's quotation out of Habakkuk.

Why?

Phillips says nothing. Williams says nothing. Bullinger says nothing. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown say nothing. Harrison says nothing. Dummelow says nothing. Rendall says nothing. No explanation is to be found in the works of Lipsius, Trench, Alford, Meyer, Ruckert, DeWette, or Vincent.

Now, wouldn't you think that if a man had the self-confidence and the audacity to correct the Bible every other word that he could contribute something when a [real problem ]{.class_4095}showed up? Isn't this a marvelous opportunity for Origen and Eusebius to go back into Habakkuk and erase ["his"]{.class_4096} so it will match Paul---exactly as they did with every other so-called [LXX ]{.class_4095}"quote" in the New Testament?

David W. Kerr ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}) does not even attempt to discuss the omission, and yet he spends more time and paper expositing the verse (in Habakkuk) than Harrison ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}) does on Galatians 1:8--9 combined!

What happened to all the Hebrew and Greek scholarship on such short notice?

Paul purposely omits ["his"]{.class_4096} in ["the just shall live by HIS faith"]{.class_4096} and does it knowing that the ["his"]{.class_4096} is in the verse he is quoting. And after having gone harum-scarum through Galatians correcting God Almighty every time He opened His mouth, here []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_84}stand the commentators like mute Communists pleading "the Fifth Amendment." Phenomenon No. 9.

9\. No commentator (or Greek professor) who corrects the [AV ]{.class_4095}text really has ANY insight into the word to start with: he is as void of spiritual revelation as Elymas (Acts 13:11).

Fortunately, the Bible believer has access to the greatest Commentator, Reviser, Translator, Teacher, and Etymologist the world has ever known: the Holy Spirit, who wrote the Book.

a\. ["Faith"]{.class_4096} in the Old Testament is manufactured by the individual (Heb. 11:1--39), and it is attributed to him as part of his own doing (vs. 12).

b\. The personal element enters since salvation under the Law is a combination of faith and works (James 2--3; Rom. 10:5).

c\. ["Faith"]{.class_4096} in the New Testament is the gift of God (Eph. 2:8--9; Gal. 2:20); and not even the faith exercised in Christ can be called a WORK (Rom. 10:1--6).

The omission, therefore, of ["his"]{.class_4096} from Habakkuk as quoted in verse 11 and Romans 1:17 is intentional, guided, directed, inspired, and purposeful. It is no accident, it is not a "free quotation," and it is not a quotation from some screwed-up [LXX]{.class_4095} that nobody ever found. The answer to the problem is found by comparing Scripture with Scripture. The "original Greek" yields no answer at all, and the men who bellow the most about the "original Greek" haven't the ability even to discuss the problem.

Phenomenon No. 10.

10\. Where neither Greek nor Hebrew yields any light, return to the [King James 1611 Authorized Version]{.class_4095} (not the [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095}, which has tried to reprint the [ASV ]{.class_4095}under the [AV ]{.class_4095}title), and the Holy Spirit will give you the light that is missing in "the originals"!

["And the law is not of faith: but, The man that DOETH THEM shall live in THEM"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). The [ASV]{.class_4095} (and [New ASV]{.class_4095}), along with its apostate sisters the [RV ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV]{.class_4095}, translates as the Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}Edition: ["he who doeth"]{.class_4095} for ["THE MAN that doeth."]{.class_4096} True to their eternal inconsistencies, the same translations write ["THE MAN that doeth"]{.class_4096} in Romans 10:5 where they all profess to be citing the same quotation---Leviticus 18:5. The []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_85}Greek is a masculine aorist singular participle and can be translated either way; however, the fact that none of the revisers ([RV, ASV, RSV, NEB, Confraternity, Phillips]{.class_4095}, etc.) took any care [to translate the aorist correctly]{.class_4095} shows they were just straining for gnats to start with. The [Septuagint ]{.class_4095}writers, after reading Romans 10:5, went back and rewrote Leviticus 18:5 so you would think that Paul was quoting Alexandria; but the rewrite did no good, for this time the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095} crowd left the [Septuagint]{.class_4095} out!

You will observe further that not one critic in the bunch was bothered by the ["them"]{.class_4096} (plural) referring to the Law (singular: Greek---["nomos"]{.class_4095})! [Isn't it strange what you can put up with in the AV 1611 when you have to?!]{.class_4095}

Casting aside the "paid performers," let us look at the text.

The Old Testament saint under the Law must perform the works (Deut. 28:14) as an evidence of his faith (James 2:22). These works do not justify him (vs. 11) unless faith accompanies them (Heb. 11:39--40). He lives by doing (Ezek. 18), and when he quits "doing" (Psa. 51:11), he has "had it" (Judg. 16:20). God can take the Spirit from him permanently (Saul) or temporarily (Samson) or not at all (David), but even under the Law exceptions are made (2 Sam. 12:13). Grace is everywhere manifest in the life of Samson who never repents, confesses, or restores anything one time in a lifetime of continued transgression. But "Eternal Security" is unknown in the Old Testament apart from the Psalms of David (Psa. 91:14--16), who was given ["sure mercies" ]{.class_4096}(Acts 13:34) that other men were not given (2 Sam. 7:14--15). Even in the Old Testament ["the just"]{.class_4096} lives ["by faith,"]{.class_4096} but it is ["his faith"]{.class_4096} (Hab. 2:4); whereas the New Testament believer is living ["by the faith OF THE SON OF GOD, who loved\..."]{.class_4096} (Gal. 2:20).

What the commentators and Bible revisers made a mess of, the plain English straightens out.

["Christ hath redeemed us from the CURSE of the law, being made a CURSE for us: for it is written, CURSED is every one that hangeth on a tree"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13). The 39 stripes laid on the sinner (2 Cor. 11:24) under the 39 Old Testament books---which end with ["a CURSE"]{.class_4096} (Mal. 4:6)---match ["the curse of the law"]{.class_4096} in the 39th verse of the 13th chapter of Acts. (See the discussion of "13" in [The Mark of the Beast]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_86}, 1970, Chap. 4.)

The Seventh-day Adventist amends the text to read: "Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the CEREMONIAL LAW of Leviticus"; and of course, this identifies the Seventh-day Adventist. He is a private interpreter like the Popes. The Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}(1941) "Bible," in the interest of getting rid of the "archaic" language, tells us that a man is cursed "who hangs on a GIBBET." (Try that one on the "mod" generation!)

All of the "new" translations follow Rome (which is to be expected), and we find the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, along with the other apostate "Bibles" ([RV, RSV, Phillips, NEB]{.class_4095}, etc.), saying that it should be "became" a curse, instead of ["BEING MADE a curse."]{.class_4096} The participle comes from a deponent verb and can be translated either way, but the observant student will notice that Rev. Rendall ([Expositors Greek Testament]{.class_4095}) will deliberately translate a deponent verb as PASSIVE or MIDDLE when the [AV ]{.class_4095}has translated it active. The purpose is (always) to destroy the Reformation text.

Fortunately, we have 2 Corinthians 5:21 "in the original" to help us translate. Christ was ["MADE to be sin for us,"]{.class_4096} and He was ["MADE a curse for us."]{.class_4096} The word ["ginomai" ]{.class_4095}(Greek) is used in this sense more than fifty times in the New Testament. [The ASV itself has translated the word as "made" in John 1:3, 10, 5:6, 9:14, etc.]{.class_4095}

What was the purpose of translating it as "became" here, instead of ["being made"]{.class_4096}? The purpose was to destroy the authority of the Reformation text, as we have said time and time again, and will say time and time again till our breath leaves us. We are dealing with hyper-critics in the realm of Bible revision. (The word "scholar" should never be degraded to that classification.)

Deuteronomy 21:23 is the verse cited, and it tells everything clearly.

Christ obeyed the Law and did not deserve the death of Deuteronomy 21:18--21, although those were the charges (Matt. 26:65)! He should NOT have hung on a tree, but He did. Therefore, His death was either a mistake (Acts 2:23!) or a vicarious death for Barabbas! Barabbas was a thief (John 18:40), Judas ["was a thief"]{.class_4096} (John 12:6), and Christ was taken "as a thief" (Luke 22:52). [What are you?]{.class_4095} Christ's death, here, is a payment (Col. 1:14) to redeem people []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_87}"in hock" (Heb. 9:15; Rom. 3:25). He was forsaken of God (Matt. 27:46) as He became the personification (John 3:14) of sin itself (2 Cor. 5:21), and if you ever stand sinless before God, you will have to accept THAT substitute before the undertaker gets your corpse.

["That the blessing of Abraham"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). ["The blessing," ]{.class_4096}here, is the imputed righteousness of verse 6 and the justification of verse 8.

["Might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the PROMISE OF THE SPIRIT through faith"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). The second clause is a second promise which was NOT connected with Abraham. It is connected with Luke 24:49 and Acts 1:4, and only applies to the spiritual seed of Abraham at a much later date than Moses or David. It is connected with the[ "foreseeing" ]{.class_4096}of the Scripture back in verse 8 and is foreshadowed in Joel 2:28--29.

The ["we"]{.class_4096} of the verse includes Paul and the Galatians. The coming of the Spirit is connected with verse 13, for the Holy Spirit could not come to Jew or Gentile until the Crucifixion. Christ had to be ["glorified"]{.class_4096} before the Spirit came (John 7:39), and there was to be no ["glory"]{.class_4096} ahead of the ["sufferings"]{.class_4096} (1 Pet. 1:10--12).

Williams is much in error in stating that the reference deals with the promise of the Spirit TO Abraham. Abraham was given no Spirit, nor was he promised any Spirit (cf. comments under vss. 5--6).

The "hanged one" (Jewish [Talmud]{.class_4095}---Tolvi or Tolui) is consigned to Hell by the Rabbis (Trypho, in Justin Martyr's work), but those of us who know the "hanged one" know that He got out of Hell a good while back and took the keys with Him when He went (Rev. 1:18). After descending (see Eph. 4:8--10), He ascended and [sent forth the Spirit ]{.class_4095}(Acts 2:1--38) which the Gentiles (Acts 10:44) receive by faith and faith alone.

["Brethren, I speak after the manner of men"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15; cf. 2 Sam. 7:19; Rom. 6:19). "I'm going to give you a commonplace, ordinary, everyday illustration."

["Though it be but a man's covenant, yet if it be CONFIRMED, no man disannulleth, or addeth thereto"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). In the interests of helping us with "archaic words," the [NEB]{.class_4095} (1961) has translated "\...or add a CODICIL." What a "codicil" is, is beyond me, but I am sure that ["addeth thereto"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_88} is just as good!

["Now to Abraham AND HIS SEED were the PROMISES made. He saith not, And to SEEDS, as of many; but as of one, And to thy SEED, which is CHRIST"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown get things snarled up, as usual, and insist that the word ["promises"]{.class_4096} was only the repetition of the same PROMISE. Exactly how this is done in the face of a verse that is calling the reader's attention to the importance of observing singular and plural (["seed"]{.class_4096} and ["seeds"]{.class_4096}) would be mystifying to anyone except someone who had noticed the hatred that Conservative scholars have for the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text. The promise of Genesis 12:3 is NOT the promise of Genesis 18:18 and Genesis 22:18 (see comments under vs. 8).

The reasoning is as follows:

1\. ["The PROMISES" ]{.class_4096}(plural) were given to the ["SEED."]{.class_4096} The PROMISE of Genesis 12:3 was not given to the ["SEED" ]{.class_4096}(singular) which referred to Christ.

2\. The PROMISE (singular) of Genesis 12:3 went to the physical offspring of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. This is perfectly apparent from Genesis 13:15--16 and Numbers 24:9.

3\. The PROMISE (singular) that ["the nations"]{.class_4096} would be blessed (Gen. 18:18, 22:18) was made to Isaac as a type of Christ. This promise was a promise connected with belief (vs. 6), imputed righteousness (vs. 6), and justification (vs. 8).

4\. The [spiritual ]{.class_4095}inheritance, then, is that the Gentiles who exercise faith as Abraham exercised it, will be justified (vss. 25, 29).

5\. The [physical ]{.class_4095}inheritance (vs. 18) states that the ["SEED"]{.class_4096} of Abraham will inherit the land (Gen. 12:1, 15:18), and this SEED can include ["joint-heirs"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 3:29; Rom. 8:17) as well as the tribes of Israel (Psa. 22:30--31).

[The issues are physical and spiritual]{.class_4095} (cf. comments under vs. 10).

Now,[ "the covenant"]{.class_4096} was made with Abraham BEFORE the Law (Gen. 15, 18, 22); therefore, no man ["addeth thereto"]{.class_4096} (the Law could add nothing) and ["no man disannulleth"]{.class_4096} (the Law could take nothing from it).

["The covenant, that was confirmed before of God IN CHRIST"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_89} (vs. 17). Isaac stands for Christ in the context; and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are in gross error in asserting that [Christ was IN Abraham.]{.class_4095} There is no indication that Jesus Christ was in ANYBODY before Acts 2. ["Christ in you"]{.class_4096} (Col. 1:27) is a New Testament doctrine that follows the Ascension. (See detailed doctrinal analysis in Gen. 2:7, [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4095}, 1970.) This problem caused Origen to erase ["in Christ"]{.class_4096} from the "original manuscripts," and Origen's corruption is still found in the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095}, [Vaticanus]{.class_4095}, the [RSV]{.class_4095}, [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}, the [RV]{.class_4095}, [Alexandrinus]{.class_4095}, and the whole family of dead-orthodox apostates.

["In Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17), in this passage, is like the ["in Christ"]{.class_4096} of Ephesians 1:20 and Colossians 1:15--18, and refers to God doing what He did in the Old Testament THROUGH Christ. This does not mean that Abraham or Isaac were ["in Christ,"]{.class_4096} nor does the text suggest that. The text said, ["GOD IN CHRIST" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17).

["The law, which was four hundred and thirty years after, CANNOT DISANNUL, that it should make the promise of none effect" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17). (For the time element, see the detailed discussion in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus]{.class_4095}, 1972, Exod. 1:8.) ["The promise"]{.class_4096} here is any promise that God made to Abraham, although he is about to apply the statement of verse 14 (["the promise of the Spirit"]{.class_4096}) to the argument. However, the next verse still uses the word ["inheritance"]{.class_4096} which can refer to a physical promise (Gen. 15:18). Paul is still using an illustration, and when the words are pressed, the reader is in danger of thinking that the physical blessings promised to Abraham's literal ["seed"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 4:13) are to be SPIRITUALIZED. That, of course, is a trap God set for the conceited and the ignorant (Rom. 11:25--29).

Now we have the complete sense. [Promises ]{.class_4095}were made to Abraham. Some of these were physical, some spiritual. All of them were made to the ["SEED,"]{.class_4096} and since the[ "SEED"]{.class_4096} (Isaac) was a type of Christ, it is apparent that what God did before the Law was a reference to some operation He was going to do AFTER the Law was fulfilled (vs. 13). Both dispensations are controlled by grace; and where grace is in charge, the Scriptures are always pointing to Christ (Eph. 1:17--23). The spiritual promises, then (made in regard to Isaac), had a future reference to Jesus Christ. The coming of the Law []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_90}could not add or subtract from those promises. Furthermore, the physical promises (Gen. 15:18) were made with reference to Christ (at least after Genesis 12:1--3) for the word ["seed"]{.class_4096} pops up in Genesis 12:7! The literal inheritance on earth (2 Tim. 2:12) was promised to Abraham more than 430 years before the giving of the Law (Gen. 15:18); therefore, even though literal Israel is [temporarily displaced ]{.class_4095}and set aside (Rom. 11:13--24), it must eventually receive the inheritance (Rom. 11:26--29), and God will use ["hunters" ]{.class_4096}and ["fishers"]{.class_4096} to accomplish this result (Jer. 16:16).

["For if the inheritance be of the LAW, it is no more of PROMISE: but God GAVE IT TO ABRAHAM BY PROMISE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18)! Having examined the unsearchable riches of the Holy Bible, we shall now return to the easily calculated bankruptcies of the modern "Bibles."

1\. ["In Christ"]{.class_4096} has been removed from every Bible printed since 1880.

2\. Christ wasn't God's[ "seed"]{.class_4096}; He was God's "off- spring" (Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV]{.class_4095} of 1946).

3\. The Law wasn't ["added"]{.class_4096}; it was "enacted" (Catholic [Confraternity]{.class_4095}).

4\. There is no ["seed" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16), only "descendants" (Dummelow).

5\. There is no ["seed,"]{.class_4096} only an "issue" ([NEB]{.class_4095}, 1961).

6\. The Law (vs. 19) "came into existence" ([Phillips]{.class_4095}, 1958).

7\. ["The inheritance"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18) "accrues" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}).

8\. ["Covenant"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) should be "testament" (Olshausen, Ruckert).

9\. "Free gift" should be added to verse 18 (Vincent, Lightfoot).

10\. ["Because of transgressions"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19) should be "to check transgressions" (DeWette, Ruckert, Rendall).

11\. ["Covenant"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) should have been "will" (Catholic [Confraternity]{.class_4095}).

12\. Bullinger tells us that the "texts" read "Christ Jesus" for ["Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14) but forgets to tell you that here Nestle ([Novum Testamentum Graece]{.class_4095}, 1960) has rejected the [Receptus ]{.class_4095}reading for [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}. Here, the two forgeries from Alexandria have been accepted by Nestle over the witness of four families of manuscripts: the Caesarean ([Theta]{.class_4095}), the Syrian ([G]{.class_4095} and [Receptus]{.class_4095}), the Hesychian ([A ]{.class_4095}and [C]{.class_4095}), and the Western ([D]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_91})---with the majority of the manuscripts. The serious student of the Scripture should observe this, for here the [ASV ]{.class_4095}has rejected Nestle and [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}because this time they read with the [King James Bible]{.class_4095}! [("Interesting," isn't it?)]{.class_4095}

3:19 Wherefore then serveth the law? It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator.

20 Now a mediator is not a mediator of one, but God is one.

21 Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could have given life, verily righteousness should have been by the law.

22 But the scripture hath concluded all under sin, that the promise by faith of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believe.

23 But before faith came, we were kept under the law, shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

24 Wherefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

25 But after that faith is come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.

26 For ye are all the children of God by faith in Christ Jesus.

27 For as many of you as have been baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

28 There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither bond nor free, there is neither male nor female: for ye are all one in Christ Jesus.

29 And if ye be Christ's, then are ye Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise.

From here on Paul is discussing spiritual things [only]{.class_4095}, and any []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_92}references to the physical inheritance of Abraham (Gen. 15:18) or his[ "seed"]{.class_4096} (Gen. 28:13--15) are put aside. Observe verses 21--25, etc. We are now dealing with FAITH in its relationship to LIFE.

["Wherefore then serveth the law?" ]{.class_4096}Paul answers immediately:[ "It was added because of transgressions, till the seed should come TO WHOM THE PROMISE WAS MADE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19).

Then it ceased its usefulness when Jesus Christ showed up (vs. 13)---["Added\...TILL THE SEED SHOULD COME."]{.class_4096} The spiritual promises made to Abraham must be connected with Isaac and Isaac only, for he---not Jacob---is a type of Christ. The Law was added, and ["it was ordained BY ANGELS in the hand of a MEDIATOR"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19). There was no mediator between God and Abraham (Gen. 15:18, 22:13--15). The two made a covenant without a third party intervening. Moses was the mediator between God and Israel (Exod. 4:16).

The Law, then, is a temporary expedient which ends with John the Baptist (Luke 16:16) and is nailed to the cross (Col. 2:14).

a\. The Law does not contradict the promise (Gal. 3:21).

b\. The Law is ["holy, and just, and good"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 7:12).

c\. It was given to show a man his true nature (Rom. 7:7).

d\. It showed the Jew his own sinfulness (Gal. 3:22).

e\. There is no difference, then, between Jew and Gentile as far as sin goes (Rom. 3:9).

f\. Therefore, there is no difference (Peter!) as far as grace goes (Gal. 3:28).

The law was given to TEACH; therefore, it is a ["schoolmaster"]{.class_4096} (vs. 24). Those who learn from it must learn that God's standards are too high to meet and that every class, in every age, in every grade, flunked the final.

["Till the seed should come"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19)! The Chosen Seed of Israel's Race (Gen. 3:15) graduated "Magna Cum Laude," and if He takes the examinations in your place, you'll pass with flying colors!

["Now a mediator is not a mediator of ONE, but God is one"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). You don't need a middleman unless two parties are involved. Christ is the "one Mediator between God and man" (1 Tim. 2:5); but once a man is saved, there is no "Mediator," for the text []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_93}does not say that Christ is "one Mediator between God and the child of God." We are one with Christ, being in Christ and part of Christ (Eph. 2:6; Col. 1:27). God deals as directly with us as He dealt with Abraham, whom He called ["my friend"]{.class_4096} (Isa. 41:8 cf. John 15:15). God is ["one" ]{.class_4096}(Deut. 6:4--5). He dealt [directly ]{.class_4095}with Abraham, but He dealt [indirectly ]{.class_4095}with Israel (Exod. 3--24); therefore, the Angel in the bush (Acts 7:30) ordained the Law (Acts 7:35) in the hands of Moses (Exod. 32:15--16). The whole affair could not take away or add one thing to the Abrahamic promises given to Isaac, who stood as a type of Jesus Christ.

["Is the law then against the promises of God?"]{.class_4096} Paul answers immediately, ["God forbid!"]{.class_4096} (vs, 21; cf. 2:17).

["It was added because of transgressions"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19). It was added because God was dealing with a nation of people who had been integrated with Africans for over 200 years and had picked up their habits, manners, morals, and possibly their speech (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus]{.class_4095}, 1972, Exod. 16--19). The thunders of Sinai were given to cool off a nation of people who had lived like dogs for more than three generations and still carried their gods with them (Acts 7:43) long after they crossed the Red Sea (Josh. 24:23). There was no fit vehicle for the ["seed"]{.class_4096} of Genesis 3:15 when Israel came out of Egypt. Furthermore, God did not produce "Isaac" until the flesh was dead (Rom. 4:19), so Jesus Christ does not show up until Israel's spiritual life and national life are completely "shot." The pitiful remnant (Luke 2:25, 36) in Israel at the time of the First Advent amount to LESS than 600 (1 Cor. 15:5--6) after three and a half years of miracles, signs, and wonders! By conservative standards, that is less than one percent.

["BECAUSE of transgressions"]{.class_4096} is the correct translation ([AV]{.class_4095} 1611), and other private interpretations should be classed as conjectures and opinions.

["IF THERE HAD BEEN a law\...righteousness SHOULD HAVE BEEN by the law"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21). This is one of those beautiful verses like John 21:25 (proof text for the Roman Catholic Apocrypha and church councils) and Hebrews 6:4--6 (proof text for losing salvation in the Pentecostal and Holiness groups). ["IF there had been\...."]{.class_4096} It is very similar to Romans 9:22 (proof text for hyper-Calvinists) and is faintly reminiscent of ["IF ]{.class_4100}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_94}[thou be the Son of God\..."]{.class_4096} (Matt. 4:3, 6).

In other words, if a law had been given which had the power to bestow life, then righteousness would have come in with that law, and the seed would not have had to come (Gal. 2:21). But the Law could not give life (Rom. 8:3) because the human beings to whom it was given, could NOT keep it (John 7:19). The reader who reads his Old Testament will remember that Moses (the Law) couldn't take the children of Israel into the Promised Land (Deut. 34); it took Joshua ([Jesus!]{.class_4095} See [AV 1611]{.class_4095} in Acts 7:45) to do it\![ "The lawgiver" ]{.class_4096}(Num. 21:8) broke all Ten Commandments (Exod. 32:19)!

Then the man who thinks he is "keeping the commandments" (Ellen G. White and the Seventh-day Adventists) is kidding no one but himself. You never kept them, and neither did your mother or father or grandmother or grandfather. Moreover, your kids will do worse than you are doing right now, for ["the scripture hath concluded all under sin"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22)[.]{.class_4096}

["All" ]{.class_4096}mean[s Y]{.class_4095}OU. The Scriptures of Deuteronomy and Leviticus settled the matter, for no Jew who ever lived, could keep every ["jot and tittle"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 5:18). This was manifested by the yearly atonement for sins (Heb. 10:1--3).

The Roman Catholic who offers his sacrifice every Sunday (a counterfeit of Mal. 1:11, which speaks of the Millennium) does exactly the same thing. His Mass is a confession that he has NOT kept all of the commandments and, further, that he has no effectual, eternal sacrifice given once and for all (Heb. 10:8--12) in which to trust (Heb. 10:14)[.]{.class_4096}

["That the PROMISE BY FAITH of Jesus Christ might be given to them that believ]{.class_4096}e" (vs. 22). The reference to the "inheritance" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown) is a little off base, as this promise is plainly the promise of verse 14---the Holy Spirit (cf. vs. 2). Paul has been heading this way for a long time (between vs. 2 and vs. 22). The entire passage has been an illustration comparing the Mosaic Law with the Abrahamic promises. We are ["heirs"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29) to JUSTIFICATION (vs. 8) primarily, not the Palestinian inheritance promised Abraham in Genesis 12:3, 7. The "coming of faith" (vs. 23) matches the "coming of the Spirit" (vs. 14), and both words are found in verse 14 and verse 2[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_95}[.]{.class_4096}

["But before FAITH came]{.class_4096}" (vs. 23) will be a reference to the [age ]{.class_4095}o[r dispensatio]{.class_4095}n of faith, no[t personal]{.class_4095} faith, for many Old Testament saints had personal faith (Heb. 11; cf. comments under 3:11). Paul is using the word in the historical sense, which is perfectly apparent by what follows. What follows is history---from Exodus 20 to Acts 9.

["We were kept under the law"]{.class_4096} (vs. 23). The ["we"]{.class_4096} is a saved Jew or Gentile, according to what follows (vss. 24--27). ["Shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed"]{.class_4096} (cf. Prov. 29:21). The Law makes sure that nobody finds a "loophole" until after Calvary. All the loopholes and knotholes are plugged up, for the Mosaic Law would make Hammurabi's Code look like a Girl Scout oath. There are no "loopholes" in this Law, and where the Pharisees thought they found them (Mark 7:7--13), they were only rejecting[ "the commandments of God."]{.class_4096} The sinner is imprisoned or surrounded by the Law, and he cannot get out of it. He cannot keep it, he cannot obey it, and he cannot rid himself of it (except through LSD, psychiatrists' couches, Freudian psychology, sex education, SIECUS, self-hypnosis, heroin, stereophonic "riffs" \[try that one for size!\], and the HEW board of the United Nations. Even then his conscience survives the onslaught, and in the stillness of the night tells him: "You aren't fooling anybody but yourself, son").

In Bay Minette, Alabama, a friend of mine was serving on the police force. One night he and three other lawmen surrounded a shack where a crap game was in full swing. There were only two doors to the shack, which was a single room with [boarded windows]{.class_4095}. Two lawmen came in one door and two in the other. The men kneeling on the floor in front of them kept right on rolling, betting, and snatching for nearly five minutes after they entered. Suddenly in the crowd of about ten men, one old darky lifted up his head and saw the badge and uniform standing across the room from him. For a moment, he blinked; then he tried to speak. As the game went on ("Eighty-kate! Box cars! Snake eyes, \*\~!\$\~o! You're faded! I'll take half of it! Crapped out! \$!\~%! Com'on baby, come to papa! etc."), he stammered and pointed, now to his buddies and now to the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_96}doorway. It was twenty seconds before he became vocal: "L-l-l-l-l, heyya-hey-h-h-h-h- [DE LAW!"]{.class_4095}

Dice, coins, cigarettes, matches, and chewing tobacco went all over the room; and everybody wheeled and headed for the exit. But there were only two exits, and each one was blocked by two six-foot patrolmen with clubs and pistols. One old Afro-American (that is not the original Greek!) got stuck between the legs of one of the officers; looking wildly about, he said, "Lawsy mercy! Heah ah is needin' to go somewheah, [and ain't got no place to go!"]{.class_4095}

You see, the Law had him hemmed in. [And that's right where it's got YOU.]{.class_4095}

["Shut up unto THE FAITH which should afterwards be revealed. Wherefore the law was our SCHOOLMASTER to bring us unto Christ, that we might be JUSTIFIED BY FAITH"]{.class_4096} (vss. 23--24). And there is the whole thing as plain as the nose on your face. The Law was a teacher, and everyday it taught the same lesson---["you are a sinner."]{.class_4095} It kept this curriculum through 1,400 years (or more) of history and absolutely prevented any man from justifying himself (Luke 16:15). Even those who walked blameless in the Law (Luke 1:6; Phil. 3:3--6) still had to trust the shed blood of the lamb; and all the lambs in Asia, Africa, and Europe could not REDEEM the transgressions which were under the first covenant (Rom. 3:25). There is only one door out of the classroom, and the class will not dismiss until Revelation 21. Christ is ["the door"]{.class_4096} (John 10:9). You can no more "go home for recess" through Buddha, Lao Tse, or Mohammed than you can bust your way out of a bank vault with a toothbrush.

Before finishing the chapter, we should briefly review the inaccuracies of the [AV ]{.class_4095}critics as they seek to muddle the sense of the passage. The [RV ]{.class_4095}has reread ["shut up"]{.class_4096} from verse 23 back into verse 22 (although this was not in the least necessary) and has made the Scripture do the shutting up instead of the Law. Its sister translation, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), does the same thing. The [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) uses the word ["consign," ]{.class_4095}Phillips has ["imprisoned,"]{.class_4095} and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}says simply that the Scripture has ["declared"]{.class_4095} the world to be in subjection to sin. The reading ["shut up"]{.class_4095} used by the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} is, of course, the [Roman Catholic ]{.class_4095}Latin [Vulgate ]{.class_4095}of Jerome, still found in the [Roman Catholic Confraternity Edition]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_97} (1941). Given the fact we have proved conclusively in [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_4095} (1970) that the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV]{.class_4095} are [Roman Catholic ]{.class_4095}Bibles, the reader should never be shocked to notice the constant agreement between their texts.

The corrections suggested by Lightfoot, Meyers, Alford, Lipsius, Ellicott, Wordsworth, Jowett, Vincent, Rendall, and DeWette are not worth reproducing.

["But after that faith is come, we are NO LONGER under a schoolmaster"]{.class_4096} (vs. 25). Therefore, the Christian is no longer under the Law (Rom. 6:14), for he is dead to it (Gal. 2:20) by his unity with Christ (Rom. 6:8--16). He has no relation to it (cf. comments under Col. 2:14), and any and all attempts to use it, or any part of it, for justification are worthless (Gal. 5:4).

We now enter the great "Hall of Heresy"---the last five verses in this chapter, where we find the five proof texts for the five great heresies of the Church Age.

1\. Verse 25 is used to teach [antinomianism: ]{.class_4095}no restraint on Christian conduct at all after salvation.

2\. Verse 26 is used to teach the ["Fatherhood of God and the Brotherhood of Man"]{.class_4095} by quoting only half the verse.

3\. Verse 27 is used to teach [baptismal regeneration ]{.class_4095}by altering the context of the passage from the Spirit (see vss. 2--3, 5, 14, and 4:6) to physical sacraments.

4\. Verse 28 is used to teach homosexuality and [mongrelization of the races.]{.class_4095}

5\. Verse 29 is used to spiritualize the prophets so that the Church gets the blessing promised to Israel: [Postmillennialism.]{.class_4095}

Here at the very center of the textbook on orthodox doctrine, five heresies find their starting points. (Our commentators are not lacking in heresy, even if they are lacking in fidelity.)

Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown says that water baptism is a "means" of ["spiritual transference"]{.class_4095} from one state into a "living union" with Christ! The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} states that water baptism brings us into union with Christ, into union with life, and that the "SACRAMENT" (as the [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} puts it, pg. 1174) of water baptism puts men into Christ (Vol. 20, pp. 141--[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_98}142). Dummelow says the same thing (Commentary, pg. 952), after saying that union with Christ is obtained by "faith" (!) and that water baptism only ["symbolizes"]{.class_4095} our union with Christ. But this is the old booby-trap that finished off Irenaeus (A.D. 130202), Justin Martyr (A.D. 100--165), and Origen (A.D. 185--254) before Augustine (A.D. 354--430) tried to add predestination to it (see [A History of the Christian Church]{.class_4095}, Walker, pp. 162--165). Calvin faithfully carried on the nonsense, and it has been preserved throughout the Church Age by Alexander Campbell and the Popes of Rome.

"You are saved by faith, but faith is [demonstrated ]{.class_4095}by baptism. Babies---to enter the covenant---need the [sign of the covenant]{.class_4095}, but the sign of the covenant is baptism. So the sign puts you in, but it [might not be real]{.class_4095}. If it [is ]{.class_4095}real, the sign [does ]{.class_4095}something; but if is [not ]{.class_4095}real, it doesn't. If you are not elected, it will not work; but if you [are]{.class_4095}, it [will ]{.class_4095}work, etc., etc."

But the truth is, it was men of this caliber who worked out America's foreign policy between 1945 and 1970. No amount of theological hogwash will conceal the glaring truth: we are dealing with men who refuse to read English (cf. comments on Col. 2:8 and Eph. 4:9). Water baptism puts you into union with---water. (I don't recall in 1,900 years of history where it ever put you "into" anything else.)

Fancy how the text would read if the context were NOT spiritual! ["There is neither\...MALE NOR FEMALE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28)?

Now how in creation could you say that the baptism of verse 27 was physical? [There isn't one reference to physical things anywhere in the five verses!]{.class_4095}

The ancient heresy of baptismal regeneration is still taught throughout America today in the "Church of Christ," and its "elders" repeat and repeat and repeat the accumulated errors of eighteen centuries of blundering miscalculations. No "Campbellite" could preach ["the truth of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 2:5, 14) if he studied ten years in Seminary.

["For ye are all the children of God\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 26), but the verse does not stop there. The Liberal in the Ecumenical Council stops there, and every Socialist in the world (who is still a Deist) stops there; but the verse continues without any regard for the roadblocks: []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_99}["by faith in Christ Jesus."]{.class_4096}

People are NOT all ["children of God."]{.class_4096} The substitution of ["offspring"]{.class_4095} by the [RSV ]{.class_4095}in Galatians 3:19 for ["seed"]{.class_4096} tells us all we need to know about the international Socialists who translated it, for ["offspring"]{.class_4095} is the word used for ALL MEN in Acts 17:29 where Paul was speaking to the unregenerate "intellectuals" of his day. Men are ["offspring,"]{.class_4096} but they are neither ["children of God" ]{.class_4096}nor ["sons of God"]{.class_4096} until they are born again and adopted into God's family (see remarks under Eph. 2:1--3 and Gal. 4:6--7). ["The Brotherhood of Man"]{.class_4095} is one of the greatest religious blasphemies ever taught by the Christ-rejecting, Bible-despising, pagan devotees of international Socialism.

There is no evidence anywhere in the Scripture that all men are God's children. The only verse the Liberal could find in the Gospels was Matthew 23:9, which was given to Jewish disciples [before the Cross. ]{.class_4095}In that same chapter, Jesus is calling the international Socialists (see vs. 15) "children of serpents" (vs. 33)! The children of Satan (John 8:44) are ["children of wrath"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 2:3), and many of them are[ "twofold more the child of hell" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 23:15) than the Liberal preacher who turned them to social issues (Rev. 3:14--17) instead of the word of God. Men like Abbie Hoffman and the "revolutionary" campus speakers (1960--1970) are the products of the Liberal pulpit. According to the word of God, they are no more ["children of God"]{.class_4096} than Bloody Mary, Pope Leo I, Pope Innocent III, Ignatius Loyola, Adolph Hitler, Fidel Castro, John Wilkes Booth, Francisco Franco, Carl Chessman, Pope Paul VI, or Boss Pendergast. [They all went to the same church!]{.class_4095}

["There is neither Jew nor Greek"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28). Here is a real text for an indiscriminate race-mixer. But observe again the roadblock placed by the Holy Spirit to keep the Socialists in their place---["male nor female."]{.class_4096} Plainly, people do not lose their physical characteristics (in regard to gender) when they become converted. If you are going to ignore the physical distinctions of RACE on the grounds of the verse, you are going to have to let men and women use the same washrooms.

Now, "modern education" has gone a long way in this direction. By developing manly women ("women's liberation") and womanly []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_100}men ("fruit," I believe, is the "original Greek"), the integration problem has been solved to the point where single "couples" can live in dormitories "on campus," provided their parents are stupid enough to pay their expenses. Of course, God always saves a joker for the last hand; and in spite of every attempt to level the races and sexes into a passive, mongrel race of automatons, two salient truths remain:

1\. The men cannot yet bear children.

2\. The women have to take ways and means to keep from getting pregnant.

You see, you don't beat God out no matter which way you approach. (See detailed analysis in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4095}, 1970, Gen. 3:1--20.)

["Bond nor free"]{.class_4096} keep their physical compartments under grace (see 1 Tim. 6:1--4 and comments under Eph. 6:5--7). If you want to go back to 1860--1865 for an argument, the Scriptures will give you one. What the federal government did was to take the Black man off the farm and put him in the "ghetto." The Congress and the Supreme Court are now arming him to use him for the purposes of international Socialism, but if Russia ever takes over America, most of them will be liquidated. There are still "bondmen" in 2010 (see Rev. 6:15, 18:13).

["For ye are all one IN CHRIST JESUS"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28). Don't leave out the last three words. The Scripture knows of NO unity outside of the unity of Christians IN CHRIST (John 17:21). Ecumenical unity of apostate churches and economic unity of socialistic institutions are a horse of another color---pale green (Rev. 6:8).

["And if ye be Christ's, then are ye Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29). Now Paul has finally come to it. This is what he was trying to say all along, ever since he mentioned the Spirit (vs. 14). The man, who exercises faith in Christ, receives the Holy Spirit as part of a spiritual inheritance that began with Abraham's justification and imputed righteousness.

Galatians 4:3--7 goes right back over this same ground again (see comments). Paul is driving it home with everything he can get his hands on. You are Abraham's SPIRITUAL SEED, because the ["seed"]{.class_4096} of verse 16 is SPIRITUAL. "In Christ" believers are one Spirit (1 Cor. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ537.xhtml_page_101}12:13), and one Spirit put them into Christ (Eph. 4:4) apart from water baptism or heathen "sacraments." "In Christ" there is no difference SPIRITUALLY between men and women, slaves and masters, Jews and Gentiles (vs. 28). If you are "in Christ," you are entitled to the spiritual promises which God gave to Abraham's SEED (Isaac), for you ["are\...Abraham's seed"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29).

Further, the heirs to the Millennial inheritance (see comments under Gal. 5:21 and Col. 3:24) have to be [spiritual children ]{.class_4095}before any literal, physical, visible Kingdom comes (see detailed account in [The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_4095}, Bible Baptist Bookstore, 1969). Nowhere at any time is it possible to spiritualize the physical promises and make them fit the verse. If the inheritance is literal, you become an heir to it with Christ (Rom. 8:14--17) by joining up with ["the commonwealth of Israel"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 2:11--12). If the inheritance is [spiritual]{.class_4095}, you become an heir to it IN Christ (Gal. 4:6) by joining the Body of Christ at your conversion. Any and all deviations from these truths are private interpretations.

Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown give us an amazing private interpretation on verse 28 by saying that the verse is referring to the bodily resurrection where male and female lose their distinctions! [Every angel in the Bible is a male]{.class_4095} (Gen. 19:1--5; Judg.13:3--11; Luke 24:4, 23; Rev. 21:17; and Gal. 4:14). Again, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown insist on altering ["children"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26) to "sons," and then they turn right around and say the same word (Gk. ["huios"]{.class_4095}) should be "children" (Commentary, pg. 1283)! [Very typical.]{.class_4095}

Bullinger, DeWette, Trench, Vincent, Meyer, Alford, Wordsworth, and Rendall make the usual alterations, which are, as usual, pointless. The reader will find the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth with the sense, the complete sense, and nothing but good sense in the [AV 1611 text as it stands. It has stood 360 years in spite of Conservative scholarship.]{.class_4095}

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml}

# Chapter 4 {.heading_116}

4:1 Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;

2 But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.

3 Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:

4 But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law,

5 To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.

6 And because ye are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, Abba, Father.

7 Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

In the previous chapter, the Law was likened to a schoolmaster who "led" and to a patrolman who "locked up." Now the Law is likened to a "tutor" or "governor" (vs. 2) who is in charge of a child until it he full grown.

["That the heir, as long as he is a CHILD, differeth nothing from a SERVANT, though he be LORD of all; But is under TUTORS AND GOVERNORS until the time appointed of the father" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1--2). The apostles are called ["servants"]{.class_4096} (John 15:15) until the day of the Crucifixion. Christ Himself is the "servant of Jehovah" (Isa. 42:1) and "learns obedience" as a Son (Heb. 5:8), even though He was "Lord of all' (Col. 1:15). Furthermore, Israel was born as a ["son"]{.class_4096} when the nation was in servitude as a SLAVE (Exod. 4:23). Israel had to "learn obedience" under the tutorship of the Law (vs. 5).

"Growing up" is a universal operation, and no one is exempt if he lives a normal life.

Eliezer has charge of Abraham's possessions, even though all things were delivered to [Isaac ]{.class_4095}by his father (Gen. 24:36). Throughout the Bible, the [King James ]{.class_4095}text delivers a light and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_103}understanding on the passage that is unknown to the commentators. In a discussion running over 700 words, the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}does not even give the reader one Biblical truth concerning the passage. In six verses of the chapter, Dummelow cannot even find the Old Testament cross‑references, and not even the [Cross Reference Bible ]{.class_4095}(pg. 2202) is able to assist the student in the simplest truths of the passage. When we approach the commentators (critics), we find ourselves engulfed in technical terminology with cross-references to other Greek words which mean nothing at all in expounding the passage properly. Perhaps the most muddled of the muddled commentators is Rev. Rendall [(Expositor's Greek Testament) ]{.class_4095}who wastes half a page with some doggerel about Roman citizens, Asiatic Greece, Greek cities, guardians for orphan children in Rome, and God knows what. Rendall denies that there is any reference in the Bible bearing on the first two verses (Ibid., pg. 175) and demonstrates for us, for the 300,000th time, how inept and helpless Greek scholars are when faced with the plain truth.

The ["tutors and governors" ]{.class_4096}of the passage are the rules and regulations of the Mosaic (and Levitical) Law, and ["the time appointed of the father" ]{.class_4096}is obviously the ["fulness of the time" ]{.class_4096}in verse 4.

In spite of Ramsay's discoveries---that the children in Syria were put under GOVERNORS (Dummelow's Commentary, pg. 953)---the [RV, RSV, ASV, ]{.class_4095}and other corrupt publications insist on altering the word to ["trustees" ]{.class_4095}and ["stewards."]{.class_4095} But the Lord always has a loaded pair of dice. The word[ steward]{.class_4095} simply runs the reader back to Genesis 24:2 and shows that the reference is to Hebrew custom, not Roman; therefore, Rev. Rendall wasted a mess of ink.

Other changes have been made to obscure the Reformation truth as much as possible. Among them will be found the following:

a\. ["Term"]{.class_4095} and ["Date"]{.class_4095} inserted for ["the time appointed."]{.class_4096}

b\. ["Guardians"]{.class_4101} inserted for ["tutors."]{.class_4096}

c\. ["Infant" ]{.class_4095}written in for ["child" ]{.class_4096}(Ellicott, Vincent, Lightfoot).

d\. ["I mean"]{.class_4095} used instead of "[Now I say"]{.class_4096} ([RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946).

e\. The Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}erases any possible cross‑reference to Christ by translating the word ["lord"]{.class_4096} as ["master"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_104} (vs. 1).

The correct reading, of course, is found in the [Authorized Version, ]{.class_4095}1611. The archaic ["minority" ]{.class_4095}(vs. 3) used by the [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}(1961) is quite typical of versions that complain about the "archaic words" in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611.

["Even so WE, when WE were children, were in bondage under the ELEMENTS of the world" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3). Again he is speaking [historically. ]{.class_4095}There is a sense (Rom. 7:5--9) in which the convicted sinner personally goes through this experience (Acts 9:1--5) before he finds Christ, but the context is plainly: "We Jews and Gentiles used to go by rules and regulations; and these kept us shut up, hemmed in, boxed in, and cut off from self-justification, while they also checked our lusts and frustrated our flesh. We were in bondage to these elemental rules and regulations (Col. 2:8), and thus both Jew and Gentile find our counterpart in Israel under the Law (Rom. 2:12--16)."

The critics don't like the word ["elements."]{.class_4096} They deeply and bitterly resent the liberty the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators took in translating ["stoicheion" ]{.class_4095}(Gk.) as ["rudiments" ]{.class_4096}in Colossians 2:8 and ["elements" ]{.class_4096}here. But this deep, hypocritical bitterness should never bother the Bible believer for a minute, for              it will be observed that time and time again the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}(and others like them) translate a word anyway they jolly well please. (See documented proof in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}1972.)

Now the Holy Spirit had a purpose here in translating the word as ["elements," ]{.class_4096}exactly as He had a purpose in translating the word as ["rudiments" ]{.class_4096}in Colossians (which see). In Colossians, the discussion about the ["rudiments"]{.class_4096} concerns the basic teachings of unregenerate mankind as they are related to[ "philosophy"]{.class_4096} and ["tradition"]{.class_4096} (see comments). In Galatians, the ["elements" ]{.class_4096}refer to the fundamental laws of ["touch not; taste not; handle not"]{.class_4096} (Col. 2:20--21) which everyone in the world observes to some extent (Rom. 2:15).

The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 reading, therefore, is highly scientific and shows that the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators have done scientific research into the true meaning of the text far beyond any of their Nineteenth‑ and Twentieth‑Century competitors. The [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [New ASV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_105}are shallow beyond description when compared to the brilliant translating of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 committee.

As a matter of fact, the occasional "uniformity" displayed by such cheap "Bibles" as the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(any edition of either) is one of the outstanding marks of their translator's lack of spiritual insight and understanding of the word of God. "Uniformity" is not one of the Lord's attributes; for even though He is the same "yesterday, today, and forever," His works and words display a complexity and diversity (Psa. 147) which the most brilliant minds have never been able to catalogue accurately. (The theory of "uniformity," to tell the truth, is the evolutionary theory of Paley and Lyells!)

["But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son, made of a woman, made under the law" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). Even though Israel as a nation was a ["son"]{.class_4096} (Exod. 4:22), her individual members were never "sons of God." The misleading cross‑reference to Isaiah 43:6 in the footnote of the [Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_4095}(any edition) on Genesis 6:4 doesn't solve anything, for the passage in Isaiah is addressing the nation corporately. There are no "sons of God" in the Bible before Acts 2, unless they are ANGELS (Job 38:6--7, 1:6, 2:1).

In order for an individual Israelite to become a "son of God," God's Son had to come and repeat the history of the nation. This is why Jesus Christ had to be called ["out of Egypt."]{.class_4096} (But all the commentators missed the cross-reference in Matt. 2:15 because they were busy resisting the Holy Spirit.)

The Son of God is ["sent forth" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4) in spite of the omission of the second word (["forth"]{.class_4096}) in the [NEB]{.class_4095}, [Phillips, ]{.class_4095}and the Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}edition. The word ["made,"]{.class_4096} used twice in the verse, is the proper word (see [Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_4095}Vol. 20, pp. 182--183). The substitution of ["born"]{.class_4095} destroys the crossreferences to 1 Corinthians 15:45 and John 1:14.
