The Roman Catholic Bibles all use ["born"]{.class_4095}:[ ]{.class_4095}first the [Confraternity; ]{.class_4095}then the [ASV]{.class_4095}, the [RV, ]{.class_4095}the [RSV, ]{.class_4095}and the rest of the Catholic Bibles, including, of course, Phillips and the ["New" English Bible. ]{.class_4095}(As we have proved before in [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_106}are Roman Catholic editions, even though Rome may not appreciate the classification. You can be certain that Dr. Rice and Dr. Weniger do not appreciate it!)

The Lord Jesus Christ comes down under the curse (3:10, 13) so He can take the slave's place in the yoke of bondage (vs. 4). To do this, He must know and appreciate the "infirmity of the flesh" (Heb. 7:28) and "learn obedience" (Heb. 5:8) exactly as a mortal learns it (Phil. 2:8). He, therefore, is made a faithful High Priest, taking on Him ["the seed of Abraham"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 2:9--16). Being tempted (Heb. 2:18) as any son of Adam, He suffers (Heb. 2:10) in the slave's place! \[WELL, ALLELUIA!!\]

When a translator in Africa was trying to find the proper word for [redeem]{.class_4095}, a native gave him a word which meant "to take your neck out." When he was asked to explain this curious substitution, the native said: "Years ago when the Arabs began the slave trade, they would come through here and lead away our people by putting their necks into wood collars and chaining the collars together. There have been times when a prince would volunteer to take the place of a captive and would walk up to the line and allow the slave trader to release the prisoner and put [his neck ]{.class_4095}in the yoke instead! That is the nearest thing I can give you to ['redeem.'"]{.class_4096}

["To redeem them that were under the law"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). The Lord of Glory serves the table (Luke 22:27) and girds His loins to wash the feet of His "masters" (John 13:4--14! Well, glory!). He is not merely[ "born"]{.class_4095}[ ]{.class_4096}of a woman; He is ["MADE"]{.class_4096} flesh (John 1:14), ["MADE"]{.class_4096} to be sin (2 Cor. 5:21), ["MADE"]{.class_4096} to be a curse (Gal. 3:13), ["MADE"]{.class_4096} a living spirit (1 Cor. 15:45), and ["MADE unto us righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption" ]{.class_4096}(1 Cor. 1:30). Thank God for the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text, given by the authority of the Holy Ghost, and preserved in spite of the bungled, tangled, confused, indiscriminate tampering of hyper‑critical egotists!

["To REDEEM them that were under the law, that WE might receive the adoption of sons" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5). The transference of ["we"]{.class_4096} and ["ye"]{.class_4096} in verses 5--6 throws Alexandria off the main line; and the [RSV, ASV, RV, ]{.class_4095}etc., are caught in the "hot box."

["And because YE are sons, God hath sent forth the Spirit of his Son into YOUR hearts, crying, Abba, Father" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_107}The Alexandrian faculty will never allow the Holy Spirit to translate anyway but "uniformly," so they have altered the ["YOUR hearts" ]{.class_4096}to "our hearts" so it will match the ["we"]{.class_4096} of ["we might receive"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). (Students of [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis ]{.class_4095}have seen this little act performed so many times they usually go to sleep before the intermission. God's "little helpers" at Alexandria are always trying to protect His "testimony" with Plato, Aristotle Anaximander, Euripedes, Aristophanes, and Socrates.)

Naturally, the [RSV, ]{.class_4095}ASV, [RV, NEB, Confraternity, ]{.class_4095}and other Catholic Bibles make the substitution, along with Lightfoot, Alford, Wordsworth, Vincent, Lipsius, Bullinger, Dummelow, Williams, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Harrison, Rendall, and the other "helpers." But as we have said before, "the Lord has a monkey wrench for every nut," and He will not fail to provide one on this occasion. Observe this:

["Wherefore THOU art no more a servant\..." ]{.class_4096}(vs. 7)! What happened to "US" and "OUR" and "WE," professor?

You see, you fouled up the detail again. That is twice per verse for 31,102 verses. Isn't that some kind of a record? Doesn't that entitle you to play in the "Super Bowl"?

The thoughts being carried through by the Holy Spirit in the [AV ]{.class_4095}text over the objections of the "Conservative" commentators, revisers, translators, and Greek expositors are these:

1\. WE were all in a mess---Jew or Gentile (vs. 3).

2\. WE all needed to be redeemed (vs. 5).

3\. YOU[ ]{.class_4095}Gentiles got in on it too (vs. 6).

4\. Therefore [YOU ]{.class_4095}are sons of God (vs. 7).

Now what could be any plainer than that?

The fact that Alexa[ndrinus, Sinaiticus, Vaticanus,]{.class_4095} and [Cantabregensis ]{.class_4095}read "our" in verse 6 for ["your" ]{.class_4096}means nothing except that [A, B, Aleph,]{.class_4095} and [D ]{.class_4095}are never to be taken seriously where they attempt to overrule the Holy Spirit of God. To the skeptical reader who still thinks that [A, B, C, D, ]{.class_4095}E, and [P]{.class_4095} must be allowed to take the stage, let us suggest that he READ his Bible first.

a\. ["YE knew not"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8).

b\. ["YE did service"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8).

c\. ["YE have known"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9).

d\. ["YE observe days"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_108} (vs. 10).

e\. ["YE know how"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13).

Now, what in "tarnation" (Sanskrit for "hell") would lead any fool to think that the word ["our" ]{.class_4095}belonged to verse 6? The first person plural is not the subject of a single thought in a single sentence anywhere between verse 6 and verse 13. The second person is the proper subject every time, under every condition. Phenomenon No. 11.

Where Aleph, [A, B, C, D,]{.class_4095} E, F, G, H, 1, J, K, L, M, N, O, P, etc., read against the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, charitably overlook their stupidity and "pray for them."

["Abba, Father" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6) shows that the Gentiles have been adopted into a Jewish setup, for the word ["Abba" ]{.class_4096}is a Hebrew word. Therefore, to read ["your"]{.class_4096} as "our" in the same verse is absolutely ridiculous. [Any Jew knew the word!]{.class_4095}

["Abba, Father" ]{.class_4096}is the cry of a child who has been adopted into another family that speaks another language. For an understanding of adoption (Rom. 8:15), the reader should lay the commentators and revisers aside and give his undivided attention to Exodus 4:22; Hosea 11:1; Romans 11:7; Hosea 1:9; Matthew 3:17; Hebrews 5:8; Psalm 2:7; Acts 13:33; Romans 1:4; and Ephesians 1:5.

And now if you really want to get stuck in the mud up to the axles, try the [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}(Catholic) [Bible ]{.class_4095}which has gone from ["you"]{.class_4095} in verse 6, to[ "our"]{.class_4095} in verse 6, to ["he"]{.class_4095} in verse 7! One bunch became sons, another bunch got the Spirit, and another guy got set free who wasn't there! [(The AV is not the only Bible that is "hard to understand"!)]{.class_4095}

Having made a shambles of the correct text, Alexandria cannot stop. It is rather like a man throwing baseballs at milk bottles in a fair booth. Once you see one or two of them tumble, you cannot resist the temptation to get all three of them. So here, the corrupt scribes of the corrupt school at the corrupt city (who corrupted the manuscripts) have omitted the words ["through Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 7) from the text. Nestle refuses to tell his readers that [Aleph ]{.class_4095}([Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}) reads as the [King James, ]{.class_4095}and quietly puts the sign for ["Hesychian"]{.class_4095} manuscript that agrees with the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611. (P46 \[papyrus\] also omits ["through Christ." ]{.class_4096}Of course, this is to be expected. See []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_109}[The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970, pg. 85.)

Again, we must look to the Roman Catholic Church for this omission. You will find that the Latin Vulgate is the source of this "better reading" and that the Jesuit [Rheims ]{.class_4095}edition of the Catholic hierarchy (1582) is exactly the same as the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} (1963)[, ]{.class_4095}and other Catholic publications. (What scholars call "newer manuscripts" and "information unavailable to the [King James ]{.class_4095}translators" is often a reference to the Roman Catholic Latin of 1582, which any [King James ]{.class_4095}translator could have picked up for the price of a tomcat. The serious student of the Bible should understand that very often ["modern scholars" ]{.class_4095}are simply ["ancient liars.")]{.class_4095}

4:8 Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.

9 But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?

10 Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.

11 I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.

12 Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.

13 Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.

14 And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

15 Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.

16 Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?

17 They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.

18 But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_110}thing, and not only when I am present with you.

Now things get moving. Up to here, with the exception of one or two outbursts (1:8--9, 2:17, 19, and 3:3), Paul has been teaching Bible to his new converts, using the wording found in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, not the Roman Catholic Latin of Jerome as found in the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095}, etc.

["Howbeit then, when YE KNEW NOT God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8; cf. Col. 2:16). The first seven words are explained in the next verse---["\...now, after that ye have KNOWN God, or rather ARE KNOWN OF GOD"]{.class_4096}---implying that the unsaved are NOT personally known to God until they are elected, and everyone of them is elected in TIME (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_4095}1972---Exod. 3:19, 7:3).

The service performed was to idols (Acts 14:11--18, 17:22--30; Psa. 96:5, 97:7) and the ["elements"]{.class_4096} mentioned above (vs. 3). The idols are not real ["gods"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 10:20), but the statement in 1 Corinthians 8:5 refers to something else (see Psa. 82). To avoid the truth pointed out by the reference, nearly all of the critics (called "commentators") assume that the ["gods"]{.class_4096} of John 10:35 are HUMAN JUDGES. You will find this private interpretation preserved through nineteen centuries of church history by Conservatives and Catholics who are blind to the truth of Scripture. The truth of the matter is there ARE "other gods," and they are mentioned many times in the Scriptures (Gen. 3:5; Exod. 22:28 \[often confused by using the last half of the sentence!\]; Deut. 12:3; Jer. 10:11). Images are made representing these[ "gods,"]{.class_4096} but these ["gods"]{.class_4096} are plainly present in Genesis 1, 3, 6, and later. There ARE "gods by nature." They have been here before, and they will show up again (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}1970, Gen. 6:1--6); but there is no room to discuss the subject here.

["Ye have known God, or rather are KNOWN OF GOD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 has a nasty habit of booting Calvinism around. The "elect" were ["without God"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 2:12),[ "children of wrath" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 2:3), foreigners and strangers to ["the covenants of promise"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 2:12), and unknown to God (Gal. 4:9) until they []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_111}show up "in Christ" (see comments under Eph. 1:4). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown beg out by substituting "love" for "know" and then running it back to eternity.

["How turn ye again to the weak and beggarly ELEMENTS" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 9). The [RSV ]{.class_4095}and the [NEB]{.class_4095} have invented a peculiar term for ["elements"]{.class_4096} which they call "elemental spirits." But the [NEB ]{.class_4095}translators hastily explain that this is not a reference to "spirits," but actually to "ideas"! The [RSV ]{.class_4095}does not get out of the trap, however, because it made the mistake of translating ["them"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8) as "beings." We seem to be dealing with men who flinch and feint at the truth of Genesis 6:1--6 but don't seem to be able to follow through once they get moving.

["Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10). They must have been good Episcopalians! (Ash Wednesday, Maundy Thursday, Catfish Friday, Bad Saturday, Sunday Monday or Always, Blue Monday, Shrove Tuesday, and Tennessee Saturday Night!)

The Galatians once served idols which were not real[ "gods"]{.class_4096} but only represented the ["gods"]{.class_4096} of Genesis 3 and 6. They now propose to go back and serve rules and regulations that are just as dead as the idols (Col. 2:14--17). You would not believe that a man who had been crucified with Christ at Calvary and had experienced the power of the Holy Spirit (3:5) could be fooled into thinking that Sabbath observance was connected with salvation, but these poor fickle Galatians (see Preface!) actually thought that Sabbath observance was part of "justification." ["Ye OBSERVE DAYS" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10).

Some of the more zealous of them (vs. 17) also set aside Christmas and Easter, and a few more picked up Good Friday and Ash Wednesday. Before they got through, they had more special days than a Trappist Monk. There was "God bless Galatia Day" and "Remember the Gala Day" (a fort that held out against the Germans!) and "Fourth Recessional Advent Day before Shrove Tuesday" and "National Black Muslim Day" and "Francois De Burgundy Day" (a Frenchman who died of drinking too much wine at one sitting), etc., etc.

They observed months and years, exactly as they are found in Leviticus 23 and 25. (I cannot read the account without being reminded of the gnome: []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_112}["Thirty days hath Septober, April, June, and No Wonder. All the rest eat peanut butter, except grandma---and she rides a bicycle.")]{.class_4095}

The matter of special "days" is settled in Romans 14, and although the Seventh‑day Adventists go right on ignoring the truth, the truth still stands (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}Gen. 2:1--2, 1970).

We are reminded again that the correct interpretation of Galatians 3:5 is that there is someone at work in the Galatian community, teaching and preaching among them. The ["months" ]{.class_4096}of the passage (vs. 10) shows a knowledge of the Jewish "new moons" (1 Sam. 20:24), and the ["they"]{.class_4096} of verse 17 is plainly a reference to the[ "who"]{.class_4096} of Galatians 3:1.

["I am afraid\...lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). This ties in exactly with the comments made under 2:2 and 3:4, which see.

["Brethren\...be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye]{.class_4096} [have not injured me at all" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12). Rev. Rendall is terribly upset by what he calls "the severing of connections" between this verse and the previous context (Ex[positor's Greek Testament,]{.class_4095} pg[. ]{.class_4095}177). He calls this doing "great violence" to the Greek text. In view of the fact that Rev. Rendall and his friends---any commentator or reviser between 1880 and 2010---do "violence" to the Reformation text about twice every verse, the Reverend's consternation should call for nothing but indifference. You will observe that Rev. Rendall simply supplies Greek verbs where he feels they are needed ([Ibid.]{.class_4095} pg. 177). But all of the [AV]{.class_4095}'s critics do the same thing.

Phillips, the [ASV]{.class_4095}, the [RSV, ]{.class_4095}the [RV, ]{.class_4095}and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}cannot bring out the sense of the verse; and since the alibi for publishing each one of these phony "Bibles" was that the [AV ]{.class_4095}was not CLEAR, we shall take some liberty to "clarify."

"Do what I'm doing, gentlemen. You don't see me running around with a palm leaf on the table and ashes plastered all over my forehead. Why, looka here! I became like YOU when I got saved. I dumped all this sacramental‑ritual mess. We're both saved sinners; we both got saved the same way. How come you can't follow my conduct in regard to formalism? You haven't messed me up by carrying on like you've been doin'! []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_113}[You just messed yourself up!"]{.class_4095}

(How's that for preserving tense, voice, number, gender, and supplying "verbs" that are "required in the context"?)

Ellicott, Dummelow, Lange, Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown all missed the sense of ["ye]{.class_4096} [have not injured me at all"]{.class_4096};[ ]{.class_4096}and all of them were so anxious to tie it to what follows (vss. 13--15), they ignored the context in which it appeared---["Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years." ]{.class_4096}Our critics seem to have picked up the "galluping hefalumps": a rare disease which forces people to push every verse into the verse AHEAD of it. Observe this phenomenon among "the afflicted" in Galatians 3:5 and 3:18--19 and 4:5--6. It is a common affliction among men who are in a hurry to alter the truth.

["Ye have not injured me at all" ]{.class_4096}is a reference to their conduct in the preceding verse. It is no more connected with "you didn't injure me the last time I preached there" (all the commentators) than Billy James Hargis is connected with Weatherman (1969--1972).

["Ye know how through INFIRMITY OF THE FLESH I preached the gospel unto you at the first" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 13). Paul was with the Galatians in the same condition that he was with the Corinthians (1 Cor. 2:1--8), and his infirmities (2 Cor. 12:5--10) dogged him to the chopping block (2 Tim. 4:6--8). ["The beloved physician"]{.class_4096} (Col. 4:14) goes with him to the end (2 Tim. 4:11), and his "temptation in the flesh" is obviously the ["thorn"]{.class_4096} of 2 Corinthians 12:7.

Paul is getting ready to make a personal appeal instead of a doctrinal appeal (as he has done for the last two chapters). The idea will be: "You didn't reject me before. You followed me then, even though I was weak and sick. So how come you have turned on me?" (vs. 16)

["And MY TEMPTATION which was in my flesh ye despised not" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). Well, something has gone wrong with our Bible again! (Isn't it a tragedy that we poor, ignorant, stupid, Bible‑believing people can't read one paragraph in "our Bible" without some educated ass trying to tear it up for us?)

The Lord makes the mistake (!) of using the word ["peirasmon" (Gk]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_114}.) one time in a different sense than a temptation to sin, so Alexandria has a spasm and changes[ "my" ]{.class_4096}so it becomes "your"; i.e., somebody else's temptation.

Now the word ["temptation," ]{.class_4096}here, is obviously a reference to a [bodily trial. ]{.class_4101}Even Lachmann (who deeply resented the [AV ]{.class_4101}1611) sees this (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown Commentary, pg. 1271). If Lachmann had missed it, then 2 Corinthians 12:7--10 would have defined it anyway! But since the verse is hard to read in the "original" (!), the "original" has been altered so the Lord will not be accused of being "vague."[ Aleph, A, B, D, ]{.class_4101}and [F ]{.class_4101}read ["your temptation"]{.class_4101} instead of ["MY temptation."]{.class_4096}

The "oldest manuscript," however, does not say "your." The oldest manuscript---P46---says ["MY"]{.class_4096} as the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611.

Phenomenon No. 12.

12\. [Where the oldest manuscripts read as the King James, the Conservative scholars often throw them out.]{.class_4095}

Paul was received in spite of his ["temptation"]{.class_4096} in the flesh. It is called [his]{.class_4095} ["temptation"]{.class_4096} because the temptation was in relation to HIM.

["But received me as AN ANGEL OF GOD, even as Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). The ["New" Scofield Reference Bible---]{.class_4095}which [is ]{.class_4095}a disguised [ASV]{.class_4095}---moves blithely over the text and forgets that it had just made such a stink about ["an"]{.class_4096} and ["the"]{.class_4096} in regard to "the Angel of the Lord" (see references in Matt. 2:13; Acts 27:23, etc.). Here they had just altered ["temptation" ]{.class_4096}to "trial," and the ["angel"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}slipped up on them before they could call him "sexless" (see Scofield note, pg. 11). This angel, as all angels, was just as male as Jesus Christ. The Greek construction [(which nobody commented on this time) ]{.class_4095}indicates that the angel here is the Lord Jesus, exactly as He was in Acts 27:23! [EVERY ANGEL IN THE BIBLE IS A MALE.]{.class_4095}

["Where is then the blessedness ye spake of?" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15). Or to quote the Roman Catholic [Confraternity, "Where then is your SELF CONGRATULATION?"]{.class_4095} The [RSV ]{.class_4095}eradicates the blessing by saying, ["What has become of the satisfaction you FELT?"]{.class_4095}

Naturally, the [American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}(any edition) and the [Revised Version ]{.class_4095}(1881) follow the Roman Catholic version (1582): ["that gratulation of yourselves." ]{.class_4095}(["Gratulation"?]{.class_4095} Did they thank []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_115}themselves or grind themselves to granules or what?)

But to those of us who have received "blessings" from hearing a real preacher preach the truth, there is no problem with the text. We wouldn't congratulate ourselves if[ "pou]{.class_4095} [on" ]{.class_4095}(Alexandrian alteration of the text) was sprinkled all over the epistle. The Galatians congratulated nobody. They said: "Boy, Paul, you're really a blessing, brother! Man, I gotta blessing out of that last message! Preach on, Paul. If I get blessed any more, I'm gonna get raptured ahead of time." But since no reviser or commentator since 1880 had enough Christian experience to understand that kind of ["blessedness,"]{.class_4096} we are wasting our time trying to correct them.

["Ye would have PLUCKED OUT YOUR OWN EYES" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15), which would indicate that the eye trouble (see Acts 9:8--9) of 2 Corinthians 12:10 (["infirmities"]{.class_4096}) was connected with the ["thorn" ]{.class_4096}of 2 Corinthians 12:7. Notice how the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 preserved the proper crossreferences in Joshua 23:13 and Numbers 33:55.

["Am I therefore become your enemy, because I]{.class_4096} [tell you the truth?" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16). Well, it might turn out that way (see 1 Kings 18:1--2). Ahab always did look at Elijah as an enemy (1 Kings 21:20), because ["friendship of the world is enmity with God" ]{.class_4096}(James 4:4). The nice, respectable, monument-building, affluent Christian of the high income bracket would kill the Bible‑preaching prophet if he had the opportunity, for real preaching is death to worldly honor and support.

The word ["because"]{.class_4096} ([AV ]{.class_4095}1611) is much clearer than the lame ["by"]{.class_4095} used since 1881 by all the assembly line---[RV, ASV, RSV, NEB, ]{.class_4095}etc.

["They ZEALOUSLY AFFECT YOU\...it is good to be ZEALOUSLY AFFECTED" ]{.class_4096}(vss. 17--18). Mistakes are like rabbits. All you have to do to start a string of slanders and misrepresentation is just pull one person's ear who doesn't have any sense, and they will take care of the multiplication. Thus we find in verses 17 and 18 one of the most remarkable mistranslations ever attempted, faithfully passed on from the [RV ]{.class_4095}to the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}to Alford, Vincent, and Wordsworth, to the [ASV]{.class_4095}, and thence to Lightfoot, DeWette, Rendall, and Green, and right on down to the [RSV ]{.class_4095}with Ellicott, Dummelow, Williams, Jamieson, Fausset, and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_116}Brown, Kenneth Wuest, and others who know how to do nothing but "ape" their idols.

Now, the text as it stands ([AV ]{.class_4095}1611) is very clear.

1\. It is good to get stirred up (be zealous) over ["a good thing"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18).

2\. It is good to be affected in this manner by anyone (vs. 18).

3\. But somebody is affecting you in the wrong way (vs. 17).

4\. They are stirring you up about something (vs. 10) that is not ["a good thing" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17).

5\. This is done so that you will get "cut out of the herd" (vs. 17) and will have an affect on them, which is described exactly in 6:13.

With something that clear, how do you suppose the following thing happens?

1\. [ASV]{.class_4095}: ["They zealously SEEK you"]{.class_4095} and ["to be zealously sought"]{.class_4095} (vss. 17--18).

[2. RSV: "They make MUCH OF YOU"]{.class_4095} and ["it is good to be made much of."]{.class_4095}

3\. Rendall:[ "They courted you"]{.class_4095} and ["it is good to be courted."]{.class_4095}

[4. RV: "Zealously seek"]{.class_4095} and ["zealously sought."]{.class_4095}

5\. [Pulpit Commentary: "They admire you in no good way."]{.class_4095}

6\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown: (as Rendall).

7\. Williams: (as Rendall).

Now, try each of those readings throughout both verses (17 and 18) and then face God at the judgment and tell Him (the Author of the Bible!) that you understood it better after 1881. After making his change, Phillips (["win over"]{.class_4095} for ["affect") ]{.class_4096}cannot go on with the change and drops it altogether at verse 18. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}actually inserts ["envy"]{.class_4095} for ["zealously affect"]{.class_4096} and[ ]{.class_4096}then changes the voice of the verb in verse 18 to make sense.

Wasn't the whole thing pretty clear before any of you messed with it?

What in thunder is so difficult about: "These people are burning up with enthusiasm in their efforts to get you back under the Law, and they do it to cut you off from me and my teaching so they can glory in your enthusiasm that has been turned in another direction. It is okay to be stirred up with enthusiasm if the enthusiasm is placed in the right thing (Rom. 10:1--3!)---whether I am around or not."

(If you can get it any plainer than that, publish your own Bible.)

The Galatians were being AFFECTED by zealots who wanted them back under the Law. These zealots were EFFECTIVE in their work to some extent, or Paul would never have gotten so upset about the danger. The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text is as clear as a watchmaker's lens, and there has never been any improvement over it by any man, dead or alive, in the last 300 years.

Cheerleaders are chosen for their ability to ["zealously affect"]{.class_4096} a crowd, and the most fanatical enthusiasts you will ever see (outside of St. Peter's Square) are the crowds that gather on Sunday afternoons in America to see the Browns, the Jets, the Dolphins, the Rams, the Indians, the Colts, the Forty-Niners, and the Green Bay Packers collide. These fanatical, screaming, raving, sobbing, moaning, howling mobs have been ["zealously affected"]{.class_4096} but ["not well"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). You could not get five of them to weep over a man going to Hell, to shout over a sinner getting saved, to moan over their sins, or to rave about Jesus Christ.

People ["zealously affected"]{.class_4096} in ["a good thing" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18) are never terribly upset about Arnold Palmer missing a putt on the eighteenth green, Joe Namath slipping in the mud, or 0. J. Simpson getting tackled behind the line of scrimmage. There are a lot of "unholy rollers" in America whose fanaticism would make a Holiness tent revival sound like an Episcopalian funeral. Many a young lady in America can dance until two in the morning and come home with blisters on her feet, but she couldn't walk five blocks to a prayer meeting. There are "fanatics" and then there are fanatics.

4:19 My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,

20 I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.

21 Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?

22 For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.

23 But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.

24 Which things are an allegory: for these are the two covenants; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.

25 For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.

26 But Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

27 For it is written, Rejoice, thou barren that bearest not; break forth and cry, thou that travailest not: for the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband.

28 Now we, brethren, as Isaac was, are the children of promise.

29 But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit, even so it is now.

30 Nevertheless what saith the scripture? Cast out the bondwoman and her son: for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman.

31 So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.

Now begins a dissertation on Isaac and Ishmael, as Paul summons all the illustrations he can from the Old Testament. Paul takes for granted (as Tertullian) that everybody in Galatia knows about Abraham, Isaac, Ishmael, and Hagar. This has led the dead‑orthodox apostates in "Conservative" schools to assume that there must have been some Greek Bible (Old Testament) all over Asia, and this Greek "[Septuagint]{.class_4095}" must have come from some dead‑orthodox, apostate Conservatives like those who taught at the dead‑orthodox apostate school at Alexandria. But this is the same old song (see [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, 1970) ]{.class_4095}that dead‑orthodox apostate Conservatives have been singing since Origen emasculated himself to gain Heaven by works.

The truth of the matter is that Homer was availing himself of the stories about Samson and Jonah before Ptolemy Philadelphus (309--[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_119}246 B.C.) thought about perverting the Old Testament. Scholars are naive, indeed, to believe that the living words of the living God published throughout the universe (Psa. 19:1--4) had to depend on some dead‑orthodox apostate Conservative in Egypt for their transmission. You can be certain that Nebuchadnezzar's captain (Jer. 40:1--5) knew more Bible truth than Socrates or Plato, who lived more than 200 years later.

["My little children" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19).[ ]{.class_4095}He had led them to Christ (see 1 Cor. 4:15), and he was their "spiritual father" (see [2]{.class_4095} Tim. 1:2). A[ ]{.class_4095}good spiritual "father" wears the ordinary everyday clothing of his day and time, travels extensively to preach the Gospel of the Grace of God, magnifies the Lord Jesus Christ and the word (1 Thess. 2:13), and raises his converts to anticipate the Second Coming of Jesus Christ (1 Thess. 1:9--10). A spiritual "father," who clothes himself in the "garbage of a monk" (to quote one high‑school student!) and compasses land and sea ["to make one proselyte"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 23:15) and magnifies the authority of a Church hierarchy and Church traditions, is about as good a spiritual "father" as the[ "father" ]{.class_4096}of Judges 17:10.

["Until Christ be formed in you"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19).[ ]{.class_4095}Notice that Paul "travails" to bring forth children, exactly as Israel will (Isa. 66:8--12) and exactly as Sarah and Hagar did (vss. 22--27); then he must travail again for their growth. The verse matches Galatians 3:5 (see comments); Philippians 3:10; and 1 Corinthians 4:15.

Christ IN the believer is not Christ FORMED in the believer (Eph. 4:13--16).

There is very definitely a "second work of grace" (Rom. 1:11) done in a saved sinner subsequent to his conversion. Misconceptions about this work do not nullify the truth of it. As Bob Jones, Sr. used to say: "Some folks are so busy arguing about what to name the baby, they forget to have the baby."

1\. The old nature is NEVER "eradicated" in any work of grace; the Christian carries this nature to the grave (Rom. 7:15--25; 1 John 1:8). In spite of the best intentions of Bud Robinson (a sweet soul!) and the "Wesleyan Methodists," the old nature is part and parcel of the child of God, and it eventually lands him in the funeral home.

2\. The gift of tongues is nowhere associated with a "second work of grace" in the Bible. ["Tongues are for a sign"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_120} (1 Cor. 14:22) and are the LEAST gift that a Christian can receive (1 Cor. 12:28--31).

3.[ ]{.class_4096}Manifestations of the power of the Holy Spirit are not always proof of "Christ formed" in the believer, for the most carnal Christians who ever lived (1 Cor. 5--6) had the most "manifestations" (1 Cor. 12:1--11).

However, where Christ is ["formed"]{.class_4096} in the believer, certain manifestations are bound to follow; for where Christ is[ "formed"]{.class_4096} in the believer, the believer is ["filled with the Spirit" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 5:18) and is controlled by the Holy Ghost. (The word is "Ghost"---the bodily shape of a dead man! Any and all changes in the "New Scofield Bible" and Bullinger are to be ignored.)

A. Such a Christian will bear spiritual fruit continually (Psa. 1:1--3).

B. Such a Christian will be able to take persecution cheerfully.

C. Such a Christian will not be deceived into hitchhiking on theological "hobbyhorses" which Pentecostals, Jehovah's Witnesses, Hyper‑Calvinists, Catholics, Seventh‑day Adventists, Mormons, Bullingerites, and "Charismatic" groups gallop through town.

D. Such a Christian will be able to claim Romans 8:28 in dire emergencies.

E. Such a Christian will speak the word boldly (Acts 4:31).

F. Such a Christian will give cheerfully and liberally and will not only read the Bible but will meditate in it and memorize it.

Certainly, in this age, such a Christian will never abandon the doctrine of free salvation and justification by the grace of God for the legalizing tendencies of self‑righteous people who are "trying to keep the Ten Commandments."

Although every child of God has the Holy Spirit, not every child of God is ["filled with the Spirit" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 5:18). Only where the body cavity is filled with the Holy Spirit can it be said that "Christ is formed" in that Christian.

Turning from the truth for a moment to the conjectures of the critics of the Bible we find this:

1\. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961) overthrows the whole truth of the passage by stating that the Christian is to take on the "shape" of Christ. This is not the sense at all, for this plainly matches the [NEB's ]{.class_4095}boner in 1 []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_121}Corinthians 11:1, where it presents Shelton's teaching in clear print---["example."]{.class_4095} That is, the ecumenical Socialists who promote the ["New" English Bible ]{.class_4095}believe that a man is saved by SHAPING his life after Christ's life and following Christ as an "example." (Observe how the corrupt ["New" ASV ]{.class_4095}and old [ASV ]{.class_4095}both change ["followers" ]{.class_4096}to "imitaters" in the same verse---1 Cor. 11:1.)

2\. The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}displays the theological whopper of the ages. On page 198 (Vol. 2), it tells us that although the Galatians had been baptized in water---referring to 3:27!---they were NOT "sons of God" (see Gal. 3:26) until Christ "had been really formed in their hearts." This is the sacramental predestination of Augustine (Walker, A [History of the Christian Church, ]{.class_4095}pg. 165) perfectly presented, and it matches the official theology of the "Reformed churches" of Holland, Michigan. Their theology is this: "Water baptism saves you and is necessary for salvation; BUT! you may not get saved when you get baptized, although you will be later; BUT! you may not be later if you are not one of the elect; BUT! covenant baptism makes you one of the elect; BUT! this doesn't happen when you get baptized, although\...!" (If you know of a quicker way to get to Hell, don't take it!)

3\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown cannot stand the doctrinal truth of the passage and quickly alter it so that it will not have any connection with the Holy Spirit dwelling IN the Christian. We are rather to believe (if we are stupid enough to believe the commentators) that Paul was just trying to get the Galatians to "think" and "talk" Christ continually.

4\. Dummelow offers the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}some competition, if the two of them are trying to see who can make the biggest mess out of the text. Dummelow (Commentary, pg. 954) states that Paul desired to "reconvert" them to Christ! Oral Roberts, A. A. Allen, Judge Rutherford, Reverend Ike, Daddy Grace, Father Divine, Ammie S. McPherson, Jack Coe, Ewing, and the College of Cardinals might go along with that; but there is not one case of "reconversion" of any individual to Christ in the New Testament.

It is useless to list the changes given by DeWette, Vincent, Lipsius, Rendall, Lightfoot, Alford, Meyer, et al., on the passage, so we shall dispense with them.

Verse 20 is self‑explanatory. Paul feels like he is in danger of estranging himself from them further by being unable to fully express himself. Any letter, regardless of its tact or warmth, is cool and objective alongside the spoken word when the speaker is face to face with his "addressee." If Paul could have talked with them personally, he would have had less "doubts" (see the verse) about their real condition. As it is, he deeply doubts their condition, and he wonders if many of them are really saved at all (see 5:4).

Verse 21 is self‑explanatory, and the method of address is commonly used in America in the Twentieth Century. "Oh, you want to volunteer? Well, those of you who are so gung‑ho about it, have you ever checked that branch out? Don't you know what they got ready for ya?" "You gonna get a job there? Well, tell me something, do you know what a guy hasta do there to get a raise?" "All right, all you birds that want Social Security, you tell me what the law says about 'withholding!'" etc.

["For it is written\..." ]{.class_4096}(vs. 22). What follows is a set of parallels which can best be understood by lining them up side by side.

1\. Isaac versus Ishmael.

2\. Sarah versus Hagar.

3\. Mt. Zion (Heb. 12:22) versus Mt. Sinai.

4\. New Jerusalem versus earthly Jerusalem.

5\. Freewoman versus bondwoman.

6\. Born after the promise versus born after the flesh.

7\. The New Testament versus the Old Testament.

8\. Children of promise versus children of bondage.

9\. Grace versus Law.

The dissertation is called ["an allegory" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 24). The reader who is unfamiliar with such terms (and such terms as personification, simile, metaphor, synecdoche, metonymy, hyperbole, etc.) should read [Pilgrim's Progress ]{.class_4095}(John Bunyan) to get the idea. An allegory is quite similar to a [parable,]{.class_4095} although, technically, the two are not identical figures of speech.

Nothing in the passage needs lengthy exposition. The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text, as always, preserves the sense better than any of the "Bibles" manufactured since 1800. All is transparent.

The believer who believes on the chosen seed (personified by []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_123}Isaac), the Lord Jesus Christ, is born from above (vs. 26). His mother is not "Mary, the mother of Jesus," but NEW JERUSALEM (vs. 26). [Faith ]{.class_4095}is not ["the mother of us all,"]{.class_4096} although this is where Polycarp (A.D. 100) first began to bring the Scriptures into line with the Religious Whore on the seven mountains (Rev. 17). It is a small step from "Faith is the mother of us all" to "THE FAITH is the mother of us all" to "THE CATHOLIC FAITH is the mother of us all." (Polycarp, Irenaeus, Clement, and Justin Martyr finish the process.)

Being born from above, the Christian's mother is free. She is not Hamitic; she is Shemitic. Hagar, as her kinfolk, was a[ "servant of servants" ]{.class_4096}(Gen. 9:25) in spite of the work of the international Socialists on the Supreme Court. The fact that servants occasionally ride on "horseback" (Eccl. 10:7), like they appear in [Life ]{.class_4095}and [Look ]{.class_4095}magazines, means nothing except that [Life ]{.class_4095}and [Look ]{.class_4095}magazines are in competition with the Holy Spirit.

The believer has been set free from the Law (Gal. 5:1); therefore, he ceases to hear the words that came from thunder, smoke, fire, and darkness (Exod. 19--20) at the foot of Sinai. Instead, his windows are always open (Dan. 6:10) towards Jerusalem (Col. 3:1--2), and he is a bondslave to Jesus Christ (Phil. 2:12), not to the Law (Rom. 7:1--10).

His father is Abraham (3:29), who is a type of God the Father (Luke 16:24); and his brother is Isaac (3:16), who is plainly a type of Jesus Christ (Gen. 22:1--12). While he walks in the Spirit and is lead by the Spirit (Rom. 8:1--11), his stepbrother Ishmael (picturing Jews still under the Law) is still in bondage, and earthly Jerusalem is "bound" until the Advent of the second Isaac (Isa. 54:1).

Paul has taken Isaiah 54:1 by accommodation (exactly as he took Hab. 1:5 by accommodation, as well as Hos. 1:10 and 2:23) and applied it to the conversion of the Gentiles in the Church Age---not the Millennium. Now, the passages in Hosea 1 and 2, and Isaiah 54 MUST refer to the Millennium if the "Church Age" was a mystery unknown to the prophets. Therefore, the discriminating student of doctrine must do one of two things (quickly!) with Paul's misuse of these verses, and these two adjustments will determine whether many students graduate as a Bullingerite or a "Ruckmanite," at least in regard to the verse cited here.

1\. Bullinger: To avoid the embarrassment of Paul's []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_124}accommodation, the Body of Christ is lifted clear out of the book of Acts and placed beyond Acts 28. Thus, any passage in Galatians or Romans which speaks of Gentile conversion (and accommodates Isaiah, Hosea, or any prophet) can be said to be a truthful application in view of the facts that the Church was NOT present and "Millennial" conditions were still "at hand." Observe how this MUST be done with Galatians 4:27, for the verse in the context of Isaiah 54 is the Jewish remnant that survives the Tribulation. Paul uses it for Gentile converts who have been saved by grace through faith in ["the truth of the gospel," ]{.class_4096}which excludes all other "gospels" (Gal. 1:8--9).

2\. Ruckman: All Scripture is not only profitable for DOCTRINE, but is also profitable ["for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness"]{.class_4096};[ ]{.class_4096}therefore, to limit doctrinal passages to one application and one only is to carry "right division" beyond the limits of the Holy Spirit's intentions. Such a dissection develops a cold, heartless, passionless, impractical, theoretical, limited Christianity. Paul was never guilty of this. Paul---our example (1 Tim. 1:16)---never hesitates to apply the Scripture freely. The fact that the verses don't always match the exact doctrinal situation (Acts 13:40--41) doesn't bother him in the least (and it is not going to bother me!). If a misapplication is going to result in the teaching of a LIE, that is something else. If the misapplication is going to obscure a greater truth, that is something else. But where the Old Testament passages are applied for preaching purposes to reprove, correct, and instruct in righteousness, the passages aimed at the remnant at ["the end of the world"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 28:20!) can certainly be applied to Gentiles saved in the Church Age. [Paul applies them ]{.class_4095}(Acts 16:33, 18:8).

To be a consistent hyper‑dispensationalist, therefore, a man cannot stop at Acts 9 [(Stam) ]{.class_4095}or Acts 13 [(Baker) ]{.class_4095}or Acts 18 [(O'Hair). ]{.class_4095}He must continue past the book of Acts, or he cannot get rid of water baptism, for Paul does not hesitate to apply "Jewish Tribulation passages" to Gentile converts who are IN CHRIST by ONE SPIRIT (3:27). Matthew 28:19--20 may be primarily a Tribulation passage (see comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew, ]{.class_4095}1970), but this in no way nullifies its application to Gentile converts. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_125}After all, it is aimed at GENTILES. Here (vs. 27), the passage (Isa. 54:1) is not even for Gentiles!

Williams, a hyper‑dispensationalist, sees this problem immediately and deliberately avoids it by refusing to connect verse 27 with verse 26. This is done in spite of the ["for"]{.class_4096} which begins the sentence (vs. 27). Sensing that he has erred, Williams hastily shifts ground (Commentary, pg. 916) and comes out with the incredible statement that all Christians are not in the Church, for the Church was "not of promise" but Jerusalem was!

This is the bumfoozle (I always did like Pogo's vocabulary better than Rev. Huxtable's!) in which Bullinger finally got snarled. You have "Christians" IN CHRIST by ONE BAPTISM (3:27--28) who are one in Christ and have lost their racial distinctions (3:28), but these Christians are not in the one Body of the Church---even though all the things said about one are true of the other! This type of flim‑flam comes from insisting that the revelation recorded in Ephesians was not GIVEN until the [writing ]{.class_4095}of Ephesians (see comments under Eph. 3:1--4). Stam and O'Hair split the difference and pretend that although Paul applied the Old Testament prophets to the Church, you cannot apply Matthew 28 to the Church. (Exactly how this garbled logic is arrived at, I have no idea.)

The reader should observe how carefully the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 has added ["that was born" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 29) in [italics ]{.class_4095}so you would know that the words are not in the Greek manuscripts. The [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946), of course, is a false bible, for it refuses to let you know when English words have been added to complete the meaning. Now here, the addition of the English words should have been in italics so that if anyone was hairsplitting doctrine, they would not have been deceived into thinking that ANYONE was ["born of the Spirit" ]{.class_4095}in the Old Testament, for none of them were (see comments under 3:6--9). John the Baptist may have been "FILLED with the Spirit" from his mother's womb (Luke 1:15), and Jeremiah may have been ["sanctified"]{.class_4096} before he was born (Jer. 1:5), but the New Birth by the Holy Spirit (John 3:5) is unknown, for it cannot take place until AFTER the crucifixion (see Psa. 22:30--31; John 3:3, 5).

But by far the most interesting thing about the passage is its political overtones as they affect the United Nations Assembly of 1980--2010. [The Arabs have no business in Palestine]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_126} (vs. 30). (See extensive treatment in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}Gen. 13:15 and 15:18.)

That is, you cannot be a "BIBLE BELIEVER" without getting persecuted on POLITICAL GROUNDS. Any Christian who ducks this Biblical truth has betrayed the words of the Holy Spirit by which he came to know Jesus Christ. In the Dark Ages the Roman Church murdered Jews by the thousands, made them wear yellow clothing, put them in ghettos, and called them "Christ killers." During those years no Christian could have gone along with this activity unless he was a [Bible‑rejecting Christian. ]{.class_4095}In the 1960s the Popes at Rome absolved Israel of any guilt in Christ's death. No Christian would have tolerated those pronouncements unless he was a [Bible‑rejecting Christian. ]{.class_4095}In the first half of the Twentieth Century, Pius XII stated officially that Palestine belonged to the Arabs (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy,]{.class_4095} 1969, pg. 106). You would have to be a [Bible‑rejecting Christian ]{.class_4095}to believe that.

As a matter of fact, [85%]{.class_4095} of the Conservative leaders in America today are [Bible‑rejecting "Christians." They ]{.class_4095}have been born again by the Spirit of God, but they believe only in "the fundamentals of the faith." Aside from that, they reject whole‑heartedly the Bible teachings on the following:

1\. Salvation in the Tribulation.

2\. The second coming of Satan.

3\. Integration.

4\. The authority of the Reformation text.

5\. The perversity of scholars.

6\. The true identification of the Roman Church.

7\. The authority of the [AV ]{.class_4095}Bible as it has been preserved by the Holy Spirit apart from Eighteenth‑, Nineteenth‑, and Twentieth‑Century "scholarship."

[8. ]{.class_4095}Revisions and revisers.

["Geshem the Arabian"]{.class_4096} (Neh. 6:1) has neither part nor lot in Palestine. If anyone reading this is an Arab, all we can say to you is: "If you had any sense, you would submit to the authority of the word of God \[Josh. 1:7--8\] and never think for a minute that history is going to turn out any other way than how God SAID it was going to turn out" (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy,]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_127} 1969).

If the [King James 1611 Authorized Version ]{.class_4095}says that the Arabs will be cast out of Palestine and the Jews will dwell in it forever, the matter is signed, sealed, and settled; your opinion about the matter (or the opinion of the United Nations about the matter) is not even worth considering. The men who ["divide the land for gain"]{.class_4096} (Dan. 11:39) between A.D. 1980 and 2010 will[ ]{.class_4095}only delay matters one or two years longer. Where the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 speaks, the nations obey whether they are willing or not.

In dealing with the Greek text, the following errors of the revisers and commentators may be noted:

1\. Manuscripts [Aleph, C,]{.class_4095} and [G ]{.class_4095}(Origen in the lead) have taken the word ["Agar" ]{.class_4096}out of verse 25. [Vaticanus, Alexandrinus, ]{.class_4095}and [D]{.class_4095} left it in but had to mess up the text some way, so they substituted the word ["de" ]{.class_4095}(but) for ["gar" ]{.class_4095}(for). 2\. The first person plural ["we"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28) has been retained in the Receptus with [Alexandrinus, C,]{.class_4095} and the majority of manuscripts, while the corrupt [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and P46 (see Hill's analysis of the papyrus on page 165 of [Believing Bible Study, ]{.class_4095}Christian Research Press, Des Moines, Iowa, 1968) have altered it to "ye" (i.e., ["Now YE\...are the children of promise"]{.class_4095}) .

Rendall [(Expositor's Greek Testament,]{.class_4095} pg. 181)[ ]{.class_4095}finds the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 intolerable at this point (as we have found him to be intolerable at about any point!) and insists that the ["gar" ]{.class_4095}of the [Receptus ]{.class_4095}is a primitive error in "transcription" because the word [Hagar]{.class_4095} CANNOT mean Hagar the woman! Rev. Rendall's reasoning on this is that Hagar could not be Mt. Sinai (note ["Agar is mount Sinai"]{.class_4096});[ ]{.class_4096}however, this overlooks the fact that Paul told us at the start that he was giving ["an ALLEGORY"]{.class_4096} (vs. 24). Observe in verse 24 ["mount Sinai\...which is Agar." ]{.class_4096}Exactly why the reverse is not true is strange indeed. Rev. Rendall seems to have a hard time with third‑grade English.

3\. The word ["all"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26) is not found in the corrupt Alexandrian manuscripts ([B ]{.class_4095}and [Aleph]{.class_4095}), although [A ]{.class_4095}still reads as the [Receptus]{.class_4095}.

Dummelow wastes time to tell us that the context of Isaiah 54 is [earthly ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ786.xhtml_page_128}Jerusalem and then suggests that Paul was using a "rabbinical style" when he spiritualized the passage. That is, Dummelow doesn't know where he is going at death or how to get there. If this seems a little harsh, the conscientious student who "looks well to his going" should turn to page 1,090 in Dummelow's Commentary. There he will see that "New Jerusalem" is NOT a real city, nor does it have real measurements, nor does anyone live in it, nor are the stones which compose it real stones. Dummelow, following in the steps of Origen and Eusebius, thought that New Jerusalem was "figurative." If you are still in doubt, read Dummelow's comment on John 14:1--3. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown go right on with this song and dance by stating that the New Jerusalem of our text is the "Messianic theocracy." # Chapter 5 {.heading_116}

5:1 Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.

2 Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.

3 For I testify again to every man that is circumcised, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

4 Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.

5 For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.

6 For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.

Readers of the new corrupt bibles---[ASV, New ASV, RSV, New RSV, RV, NEB]{.class_4095}, etc.---will observe that the first verse in the passage has been garbled beyond recognition. Rev. Rendall speaks for the entire group of dead-orthodox apostates by saying "in the original text" ([Expositors Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 183), so forth and so on. The gullible Greek student is supposed to read right across outrageous lies like this as though he was looking at something akin to truth. [The truth of the matter is Rev. Rendall has never seen the "original text," and neither has any man who made one change in the AV 1611 text.]{.class_4095} Marcion and Origen have both messed with the text (Nestle's, pg. 486), and all of the Hesychian manuscripts have butchered it (see [Which Bible?]{.class_4095} by David Otis Fuller, 1970, pp. 101--104).

The [RSV ]{.class_4095}reading here would lead the student to believe that the purpose of the Atonement was only for "freedom." Verse 13 corrects this heresy. Neither Wuest nor Dummelow can get the sense of the passage, and Phillip's alteration of ["wherewith Christ hath made us free"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1) to "freedom that Christ won for us" is extremely anemic. The suggested improvements on the passage by Lightfoot, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_130}Lipsius, Meyer, Wordsworth, Moberley, Humphry, Ellicott, Bullinger, and Huxtable are along the same line as the improvements made on "That Old Black Magic" when Spike Jones recorded it. (Spike Jones and Red Ingle \[1945--1947\] had a lot in common with Alford, Trench, Thayer, Westcott, Lightfoot, Hort, and Philip Schaff.) Again, all is clear.

The Christian should stand and ["stand fast\...in the liberty"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1) that Christ made for him (Rom 7:1--3). He is not to go back under the ceremonial observances of the Law, for they have nothing to do with his justification (vs. 4). The legalizers who count on these things have been knocked from "amazing grace" into a "floating opportunity" (as one of the more erudite brothers put it!).

["Christ shall profit you nothing"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2). The verse is aimed at Galatians who have not yet received Jesus Christ (see comments under 1:2). The context of this verse, as verse 4, is clearly to an [unsaved man]{.class_4095}. The refusal of any of the commentators to make this distinction is quite typical of the vicious attacks the Conservatives have made on the word of God. Hell is neatly covered up by such "commenting," and even the great theme of Galatians---justification by faith---is obscured by intimating that verses 2 and 4 are aimed at Christians (see comments under vs. 4).

The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} likens the people of verse 2 to "Jewish CHRISTIANS" (Vol. 20, pg. 239). Dummelow likens them to Christians who have "renounced allegiance" to Christ. The Bible believer is to ignore both of these "comments" exactly as he would ignore the comments of Abbie Hoffman or Angela Davis (1969--1971) on "moral standards" or the comments of Darwin, Huxley, Haeckel, or the National Education Association on "future history."

Jesus Christ can profit no man who is counting on the Law for his justification. Such a man has ["fallen from grace"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4), for if he ever starts counting on the Law to save him, he must go "whole hog" (vs. 3) and keep ALL of it---not just the seventh day and the "Ten Commandments" as Ellen G. White taught.

["For I testify AGAIN\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 3; cf. comments under 1:8--9). The meaning, here, is that if a man gets circumcised in order to be justified in God's sight, he is LOST. Williams does not help matters a bit by teaching Arminianism on page 916 of his commentary: "you have been loosed from Christ" is certainly not the sense. If it [were]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_131}, those who teach salvation by works would be right, for if a Christian could be LOOSED from his sins and then LOOSED from Christ, he is [falling in and out of salvation. ]{.class_4095}That is exactly how Catholics, Episcopalians, and Methodists have taught verse 4 (in the context) ever since Origen abandoned the grace of God for his own brains.

["Christ is become of no effect unto you\...ye are fallen from grace"]{.class_4096} (vs 4). This is one of the great "Arminian" passages in the Bible used by Pelagius (A.D. 400--450), Arminius (1560--1609), and John Wesley (1703--1791) to prove that a born-again child of God can lose salvation and go to Hell after he becomes part of Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 12:13).

To enforce this non-biblical heresy, the Revision Committee of 1881 set a precedent for all future heretics to follow (and one should observe that around 30,000 errors were smuggled into this committee for future use and that commentators and revisers since that time have copied anywhere from 5,000 to 30,000 of these errors). Having botched verse 2 and having refused a correct translation of verse 1 (note Rev. Harrison's statement about verse 1 in the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1294), verse 4 suddenly looms with an impossible exposition.

I am sure that when the dead-orthodox apostates and unregenerate Unitarians and Deists on the [RV ]{.class_4095}1881 committee began tampering with the passage, they had no idea where it would end, but it ends where only leaven can end (vs. 9)---slap through the whole lump.

Faced with the expression ["fallen from grace,"]{.class_4096} the grace-rejecting humanists of the [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881) decide that the "Christian" in the passage must have lost his salvation. Like any legalizing Judaizer of the time of the epistle (A.D. 51--53), the Campbellites and Catholics grab the passage to prove that a Christian CAN "fall from grace." (Observe the same heresy discussed and settled in Acts 15:1, 5, 11.) But the [RV ]{.class_4095}translators (as their idolizers) were never too sure of spiritual unity with Jesus Christ to start with. Years and years of irrational thinking, vicious criticism, envy and hatred of the Reformation text (see pp. 173--205, [Which Bible?,]{.class_4095} David Otis Fuller, 1970), and frustrated ambition must have quenched the Spirit so []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_132}deeply in those of them who were regenerated that their sense of eternal security and union with Christ must have been nearly blank. Men as stupid as some of the commentators (see their inane remarks under 3:27) could hardly be expected to expound any Greek text of Galatians 5:4 correctly.

This time every Greek manuscript (uncial and cursive) in all four families--- papyrus or vellum---read the same way! Horrors! Being unable to alter "the Greek text," the [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881) translators hit upon a capital idea: they translated the first part of the verse as "ye are SEVERED from Christ\...." This clearly nullifies any promise in 1 Corinthians 12:13, for if you can be [severed FROM Jesus Christ after you become a part of Him]{.class_4095}, you can go to Hell [after ]{.class_4095}being saved ["by grace\...through faith."]{.class_4096}

Behold, what hath man wrought! In the midst of an epistle whose chief theme is free salvation and eternal security by the grace of God, up pops the Judaizer disguised as a Conservative "Fundamentalist"! Phenomenon No. 13.

13\. When it comes to Bible translating and revision, [profession of orthodoxy ]{.class_4095}means nothing [in a pinch.]{.class_4095} (Kenneth Wuest will alter Greek words \[see comments under 1 John in his work\] to prove something that isn't so.)

Now that the monkeys of 1885 have monkeyed with the text, here come the monkey tracks!

2\. Huxtable: ["severed."]{.class_4095}

3\. [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946):[ "severed."]{.class_4095}

9\. Alford, Lightfoot, Meyer, Ellicott: "severed."

Well, maybe [they ]{.class_4095}got "severed" from Christ or might get "severed," but Paul changes the person of verse 4 from ["you"]{.class_4096} to ["we"]{.class_4096} in verse 5, so ["we"]{.class_4096} will take the liberty of using the God-honored text of the Reformation for which men bled and died.

1\. Tyndale (1525): ["Christ shall profit you nothing."]{.class_4096}

2\. [Great Bible]{.class_4095} (1539): []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_133}["Christ shall profit you nothing."]{.class_4096}

You see, the [ASV, New ASV, RV, RSV, New RSV]{.class_4095}, and [NEB ]{.class_4095}are not [Bibles]{.class_4095}. [They are not revisions of the Bible. They are not continuations of Bible revision. They are not connected with the English Bible.]{.class_4095}

Every Bible since 1880 contains 5,000 or more of the 30,000 deliberate alterations of text first used (instead of the Bible) by the [RV ]{.class_4095}committee of England under the suicidal leadership of Lightfoot, Westcott, Hort, and Ellicott.

A "New"ASV (recommended by Bob Jones University) is no more an English Bible than a "new" detergent.

Setting aside the deletory (and desultory) alterations of the truth, let us look at the Bible and see what is going on.

a\. There is someone who [cannot benefit ]{.class_4095}from the effects of Christ's atonement and free salvation.

b\. The people who cannot benefit from it are people who are trying to [justify themselves by works]{.class_4095}.

c\. If there are any of those people among ["the]{.class_4096} [churches of Galatia"]{.class_4096} (see comments under 1:2), they are not [in grace]{.class_4095}, and they most certainly are not ["in Christ"]{.class_4096} (3:28).

d\. A believer in Christ can never be "severed" from Christ (Rom. 8:35--39), or Christ's "body" would be marred again (Heb. 10:10--14). There are no schisms in the spiritual Body of Christ (see comments under Eph. 1:10, 14). The thought of "severance" from Christ cannot even originate in the mind of a consecrated Christian, nor did it ever originate in the mind of the apostle of the grace of God, nor would the Holy Spirit have put such a thought in the mind of any saved sinner after Galatians 1:8--9. The word [sever]{.class_4095} comes from Satan, the master Bible critic (Gen. 3:1) and arch-deceiver (2 Cor. 2:11). Its appearance in every Bible between 1880 and 1980 means nothing except that the final apostasy has set in and that those who recommend the ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} are part of that apostasy---for monetary reasons (1 Tim. 6:5).

e\. Gentiles were told to "continue in grace" (Acts 13:43) even before they were saved, and Romans 11:11--25 is a dissertation on []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_134}the truth that God offers grace to Gentile nations whether the individuals in those nations have accepted His Son yet or not. Those who reject Christ (as here) after the grace has been offered (as here) are ["fallen from grace"]{.class_4096} (as here).

The text is a gem. It stands in the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 flashing in the glory and beauty with which the Holy Spirit first endowed it---a diamond among diamonds---apart from any and all changes by any and every scholar of any generation since it was penned by the inspired apostle. The readings of the new "Bibles" are a fatuity and a blasphemy to the Holy Spirit who wrote the Book.

(You say, "The Greek word \[["katergethete"]{.class_4095}\] means\...." Yes, but, you silly ass, it also means [what it says!]{.class_4095} You see, you were dumb enough to believe Thieme, Wuest, A. T. Robertson, and "the leading scholars," etc., without checking page 219 of the Freshman's Greek Interlinear (only kidding! [The Analytical Greek Lexicon]{.class_4095}) which states that the primary meaning is "to render useless or unproductive," or "to render powerless." That is, ["Christ is become of no effect unto you\...."]{.class_4096} When in doubt, throw Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Dummelow, Williams, Trench, Driver, Lipsius, Rendall, Weniger, Harrison, Huxtable, Bullinger, Meyer, Lightfoot, Neal, Hort, Wuest, Custer, and Dr. A. T. Robertson O-U-T! [OUT!!]{.class_4095})

["For WE\..."]{.class_4096} (ain't that beautiful!?). ["For WE through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). Here the proper Greek reading of the previous verse is settled, for Paul changes the second person plural to the first person plural and adds ["through the Spirit."]{.class_4096} You see, the grace-rejecting people of verse 4---as the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and [NEB ]{.class_4095}translators---[had no Spirit!]{.class_4095} Rev. Huxtable ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}) is still "holding out" to the end (Vol. 20, pg. 240), and so he says that the ["we"]{.class_4096} of verse 5 is a reference to those who are still abiding in Christ! Those in verse 4 are to be compared with Romans 5:2 (also Williams) and 2 Peter 3:17, and are in danger of "losing it."

Again, the commentators begin to run all over the pasture like a bird dog trying to find a covey. Dummelow says the people of verse 5 are waiting for "justification" (!) (see Rom. 5:9), and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown agree with this bizarre interpretation. Imputed righteousness is ignored by the [New English Bible]{.class_4095} (1961) and Rev. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_135}Rendall. All of these "Conservatives" seem to feel that righteousness and justification are something you wait for after you get saved. None of the commentators but Dr. Harrison ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1295) are able to find the truth of the matter. (Phillips hints at it in his translation of 1958.)

But the ["hope"]{.class_4096} of our passage is the ["hope"]{.class_4096} of Titus 2:13; 1 John 3:1--3; Romans 8:10--11; and Colossians 1:5, and it has nothing to do with imputed righteousness and free justification by faith.

Since many of the commentators and revisers were postmillennial and amillennial (Schaff), they completely misconstrued the "Greek" of the passage and reconstructed the words of the Holy Spirit to mean that[ "by faith"]{.class_4096} we are waiting to get justified at some later date. This is not the meaning (nor the words) of the sentence. The meaning is clearer in the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 than anywhere: "We are in Christ, and it is through the Holy Spirit that we wait and walk while we are looking forward to the ["blessed hope" ]{.class_4096}when our righteousness will appear and the end result of our faith will be manifest where you can SEE it."

["For IN JESUS CHRIST\...faith which worketh by love"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). Notice that the Galatians (as the Ephesians) are "in Christ," and they (as the Ephesians) have been baptized into Christ by one Spirit, and they (as the Ephesians) have been saved by grace and justified by faith. This is important in correcting the errors of Bullingerism, which confound the [date of the writing ]{.class_4095}of Ephesians with [the time of the committal ]{.class_4095}of[ "the]{.class_4096} ["dispensation of the grace of God"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 3:2).

The verse needs no commenting. External conditions are no longer relevant to salvation. You can't brag about circumcision; you can't brag about uncircumcision (Gal. 6:15). The Lord ignores either condition (Rom. 2:25--29) and looks only on the heart. In the believer---note ["in Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096}---the Lord looks for faith which manifests itself in obeying the first two commandments (Rom. 13:8, 10).[ "The fruit of the Spirit is LOVE" ]{.class_4096}(see comments under 5:22), and cutting yourself or trying to get others to cut themselves (vss. 11--12) will not profit a Christian, nor will it justify an unsaved man (vss. 2--4). Observe that from here on, the discourse is aimed at [the believer ]{.class_4095}in the churches (vss. 7, 13, 16, 18, 24).

5:7 Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?

8 This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.

9 A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.

10 I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.

11 And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.

12 I would they were even cut off which trouble you.

All the commentators are very diligent to translate ["etrechete"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) as "ye were running" (see Phenomenon No. 6) and are just as diligent in erasing the ["anekopsen"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) of ["who did HINDER you"]{.class_4096} and inserting ["enekopsen"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) meaning "who did DRIVE you back." Having done this, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [RV ]{.class_4095}go right on using the word ["hinder"]{.class_4096} used by the [King James]{.class_4095} 1611, [after taking the Greek word (from which the AV was translated) and altering it to the word found in Sinaiticus and Vaticanus!]{.class_4095}

So here we have an amazing phenomenon. The [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 has translated the word as[ "hinder,"]{.class_4096} exactly as it was translated in Romans 15:22; 1 Corinthians 9:12; and 1 Thessalonians 2:18, and yet the Greek word was spelled differently. How does one account for the fact that the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 got the right translation (according to the usage of the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and the [NEB]{.class_4095}) when it didn't have the Greek word which the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and [NEB ]{.class_4095}used? According to Huxtable, the [Receptus ]{.class_4095}word meant "driven back." But the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 translators translated it ["hinder."]{.class_4096} Could it be that the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators know what the correct meaning of a word is even when they don't have access to the original word? [(Now there's a thought.)]{.class_4095}

The Christian begins to run the race (Heb. 12:1); he knows the Starter and the Finisher (Heb. 12:2); he must get all of the weights out of his pockets before he starts (Heb. 12:6); and he must run to win (1 Cor. 9:24), not to "place" or "show." The course must be finished, and ["the faith"]{.class_4096} must be kept while running (2 Tim. 4:6--8). (I didn't say you had to keep ["exercising faith"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_137} to get to Heaven.) [Obedience ]{.class_4095}to the truth is clearly described in what follows (vss. 13--26). [Obeying ]{.class_4095}["the truth of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 6:17) is not the same thing as [continuing ]{.class_4095}in the ["truth of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 2:14), for the latter consists of living as though you believed that your justification was a past event accomplished at Calvary. Many Christians get bogged down in the sand before they get to Romans 12!

The article in the expression ["THE truth"]{.class_4096} has been taken out of the correct text by Nestle, even though it is found in three families of manuscripts, the majority of manuscripts, and the oldest papyrus of the text (P46---see Phenomenon No. 6).

Verse 8 is self-explanatory.

["A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). Here stands another one of those tremendous truths found so frequently in the Bible (see comments under 1:8--9). The principle is a universal truth. It is most appropriate at this point in the epistle because the translators, revisers, and commentators have already leavened the book enough to make 400 loaves of "Sunbeam bread," and not a single loaf has enough nutrition in it to keep a rat alive three days---let alone a sheep.

[Where do you stop the leavening process?]{.class_4095} If you begin self-justification with circumcision, do you stop there, or do you adjust your diet to Leviticus 11? Having done that, can you omit the feasts of Leviticus 23 and 25? Having done that, can you avoid the laws on purification in Leviticus 10--16?

Once you put [sex education ]{.class_4095}into the schools, where do you stop? High school? Junior high? Grade school? Having indoctrinated the children in sex techniques and the use of contraceptives and prophylactics, do you fail to go on and teach them Lesbianism, beastiality, sadism, and masochism? If you emphasize something that TV and magazines have already over-emphasized (to make money---1 Tim. 6:10), where does it end? In Sweden, "sex education" is now the main INDUSTRY, and pornographic literature is not merely allowed or recommended, it is promoted and encouraged. Sweden has the highest rate of V.D. and suicide of any Western nation in the world.

When armed troops succeed in getting one Negro into a College, cannot armed troops get ten of them in? Then why not 500? Then why not 500 into a high school? Then why not 50,000 into grade schools? [Then why not all-Negro schools backed up by the Army? ]{.class_4095}The two most segregated places in the world are Eutaw, Alabama, and Washington D.C., for [all colored politicians are RACISTS.]{.class_4095} Where they get into office, no colored man votes for a white candidate. White may vote colored, but colored will not vote white (see Cleveland, Philadelphia, Washington, or any place else). If you can force a private school to admit Negroes under threat of removal of their tax-exempt status, can you not also do it with a CHURCH? Didn't your ancestors leave Europe to get away from a State that meddled in matters of private worship? Where do you stop the process once you start it?

You don't. It leavens the whole lump.

Where do you stop Bible revision?

You say, "Well, what if the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators had taken that attitude; where would the [AV ]{.class_4095}be today?"

But questions like that show a very naive attitude. The [AV ]{.class_4095}translators did NOT approach the Bible or the subject of Bible revision in the manner of the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and [NEB ]{.class_4095}translators; and if you don't know that as well as you know that you are saved, you shouldn't be discussing the subject of Bible revision with anyone. The original ["New" Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} was not the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, although it said that it was. The ["New ASV"]{.class_4095} is not a revision of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611, although it claims to be. And the [RV ]{.class_4095}and [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1952) are no more revisions of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 than [All Quiet on the Western Front ]{.class_4095}is a revision of [A Christmas Carol.]{.class_4095}

If 1 John 5:7 doesn't belong in, what about Romans 8:1? If neither belong in, what about Mark 16:9--20? And what about Matthew 6:14? And what about Acts 8:37? And tell me something, Jack the Ripper; if you can take out two words, can't you take out three? And if you can take out three, what is wrong with five? And if you can take out one verse, why not 10 verses? 30 verses? 100 verses? Why not 2,000 verses? What is the difference, essentially, between Thomas Paine's attitude toward the Bible and Kenneth Wuest's? When John R. Rice recommends the ["New ASV,"]{.class_4095} what has []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_139}he done that Pope Paul VI didn't do when he recommended "Tobit"? [They both came from the same set of manuscripts!]{.class_4095} (See [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} 1970.)

["A little leaven"]{.class_4096} (not a lot, brother!). It was ["a LITTLE LEAVEN"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 13:33) that mother Rome placed among ["the children of the kingdom"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 13:38) ["till the whole was leavened."]{.class_4096} Readers stupid enough and gullible enough to believe that the faculty members of Moody Bible Institute and Bob Jones University are to be trusted in matters of "textual emendation" should give careful thought to the scholarly work by Dr. Wilkinson published in [Which Bible?]{.class_4095} (David Otis Fuller), in chapters 1 and 2 of that work. The Jesuit attempts to replace the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible were carried out in the English Revision Committee of 1881, and apostasy has characterized Conservative scholarship since that day. Nearly 100 years of apostasy have progressed since the carrying out of the Jesuit commission through Westcott and Hort. You will not find any "new" bibles on the market that are not Roman Catholic through and through. ["A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump."]{.class_4096}

Since the word of God is likened to BREAD, how do you suppose the major commentators---everyone inclusive from 1880 to 1980---could not find anything to say about Galatians 5:9 where it dealt with the very words on which they were commenting? With a flippant "false doctrine," the ["leaven"]{.class_4096} is identified by the commentators without so much as a nod of the head at the committees who made 30,000 changes in the text of the Holy Bible! Dummelow tells us that leaven is good and that it pictures [the spread of the Kingdom of God ]{.class_4095}(Commentary, pg. 956)! If such a monstrous private interpretation is true, we have had nearly 100 years since 1881 to "spread the Kingdom," and it must just about be here because of the wonderful leaven put in the loaf!

["A little leaven LEAVENETH THE WHOLE]{.class_4096} [LUMP."]{.class_4096}

The most dangerous bible is not the [New English Bible,]{.class_4095} for that is a ton of leaven. The most dangerous bible is not the [RSV]{.class_4095} (1952), for that is 1,000 pounds of leaven.
