The most dangerous bible is not the ridiculous paraphrases published as [Good News for Modern Man.]{.class_4095} The most dangerous bibles contain ["A LITTLE LEAVEN."]{.class_4096} The most dangerous bibles are bibles like the [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} and the [New ASV,]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_140} for these bibles are handmade for dead-orthodox suckers who desire to hear or tell something ["new" ]{.class_4096}(Acts 17:21) without leaving their profession of fidelity in the "inspired originals."

["I have confidence in you\...he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10). The Galatians are troubled. Somebody by the track is heckling them as they take the hurdles, and perhaps someone is throwing tin cans and bottles on the track (beer cans and wine bottles!) in an effort to slow them down or get them to get off the track. [Money ]{.class_4095}hinders the Christian from running the race. [Success ]{.class_4095}often has a desultory effect on the runner. [Relatives ]{.class_4095}can hinder the new Christian with every type of Satanic criticism, suggestion, advice, and counsel. [Sorrow ]{.class_4095}can hinder the race, as bereavement and poverty often make it impossible for the runner to see the obstacles through a prism of tears, and the lame feet (Heb. 12:13) often stumble on the cinder track where pain and sickness have bruised the spirit of the runner. [Marriage ]{.class_4095}can be a great hindrance (1 Cor. 7:32--33); and God only knows how many men and women failed to "finish the course with faith" (2 Tim. 4:7--8) because of a complaining, whining, carnal, critical, worldly, materialistic partner who cared for nothing but the gods of [Life ]{.class_4095}and [Look ]{.class_4095}magazines and the applause of a world gone mad on sex and money.

["He that troubleth you"]{.class_4096} can be a number of people, although the immediate reference, here, is to the legalizers who are trying to get them to perform works as a part of justification. Verse 10 states that Paul is encouraging them to take the right position by saying that he has "confidence in them," and that they will take no other view about the matter than the one Paul has: ["that ye will be none otherwise minded"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10).

["And\...if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11; cf. 1:10). If Paul was still preaching circumcision (as he did before Acts 9), the legalizers wouldn't be cussing him up and down the country and trying to get his brains knocked out every time he preached (Acts 13:45; 14:19).

["I would they were even cut off which trouble you"]{.class_4096} (vs. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_141}12). There are differences of opinion about the meaning of the verse, and some of the commentators go to some length to prove that the "original" meant: "I wish they would cut off their own reproductive organs while they are making such a fuss over circumcision." Jerome, Ambrose, Augustine, and Chrysostom take this view; but throughout their works one senses the ominous influence of the dead-orthodox apostate who DID emasculate himself---Origen (A.D. 185--254). The [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and [NEB ]{.class_4095}carry out this thought, although Rev. Rendall tactfully glosses over the text without comment.

All of this has an aura of "Alexandria, Eusebius, Augustine, [Septuagint]{.class_4095}, [ASV]{.class_4095}, John Calvin, Philip Schaff, and 'the recension at Antioch'" about it that any real believer could pick up without a Geiger counter.

The probable meaning is: "I wish God would cut these agitators off from you where they couldn't upset you any more." The reader who depends on the Holy Spirit for matters of interpretation cannot fail to notice the English texts of 2 Corinthians 11:1 and 1 Corinthians 4:8, which contain the same Greek word (["ophelov"]{.class_4095}). The "apocopi of Cybele" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20, pg. 247) may be a nearer way to finding the truth than trying to dig up the lost books of the library at Alexandria, but the Holy Spirit probably isn't going to put you to all that trouble if you are really seeking the truth.

5:13 For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.

14 For all the law is fulfilled in one word, even in this; Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

15 But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.

16 This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh.

17 For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.

18 But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.

What follows is a study of liberty under grace compared with liberty under the Law. The drift of the passage is apparent in every line. You are free and are no longer bound by any formal regulations, but this doesn't mean you can "let down the bars" like Epicurus and the Hedonists and take up "situation ethics." You are still in the flesh (geographically---see 3:6--10), and you can make a mess of things (like David or Lot) even though you are saved by grace and [kept ]{.class_4095}by grace. Nothing is obscure in the entire passage, and the futile (and hopeless) "corrections" made in the [New ASV]{.class_4095} and other corrupt bibles mean nothing at all, except that the monumental work of the 1881 committee succeeded in polluting the whole stream of "scholarship" for 100 years.

Now, liberty has laws which accompany it (James 2:8, 12). Even though it follows[ "the law of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:2) and is free from rules and regulations, it demands certain things of the man who has been "set free" (Gal. 5:1). Free men who abuse the privileges of liberty suffer for their violations (1 Cor. 11:30), and sometimes they die (Rom. 8:13). (The Campbellite perversion of this truth is arrived at by taking Rom. 8:2 and making it a law LIKE the Old Testament ceremonial laws. To the contrary,[ "the law of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} is an [automatic ]{.class_4095}law that works automatically in regard to those who have the Spirit.) Compare verse 18 in this context with Romans 8:14. You are ["led"]{.class_4096} whether you follow the leadership or not (cf. comments under Phil. 2:13).

The mistranslation of the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and the [RV]{.class_4095} (1881)---["called for freedom"]{.class_4096}---has been commented on before under verse 1. But they continue with the error, although the context of Galatians 5 shows that nobody was called "FOR freedom." Christians have been set free (vs. 1) and saved so they can escape the bondage of self-righteousness (2:21), but they were called TO ["serve one another"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13) and were saved FOR the Lord (1 Cor. 6:13). The readings, therefore, of the [RV ]{.class_4095}and the [ASV ]{.class_4095}are to be classified with the [NEB ]{.class_4095}which reads[ "set us free TO BE free men."]{.class_4095} The Lord did nothing of the kind (see 1 Cor. 7:21), and the antinomianism of the translators is quite apparent in their mishandling of Galatians 5:1.

Jeremiah 7:9--10 is a beautiful comment on the passage.

Verses 13 and 14 need no comment; they are found in another []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_143}setting in Romans 13:8--10.

["But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). "Dog eat dog" is the common expression, and the word ["backbiters"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 1:30) is still used today in common language everywhere. There is no need to change any words in the first part of the verse; the warning is all so clear. Christians who spend all their time arguing and fighting each other only destroy their own testimonies (Gen. 13:7--8 and comments). For example, when the entire body of Conservative scholarship gave itself over to attacking the God-given [Authorized Version]{.class_4095}, it only succeeded in doing three things:

1\. It emptied fundamental churches.

2\. It turned young people to Rome or Moscow for authority.

3\. It caused Christians to let dust gather on their Bibles.

Any man who tries to correct the authority by which God saved him and called him into Christian service is a [half wit.]{.class_4095}

Verses 16 and 17 leave nothing to the imagination. The Christian has two natures which are "contrary" to one another. The Christian is prevented first by one and then by the other from doing what first one and then the other desires to do (cf. Rom. 6--7). The word ["lusteth"]{.class_4096} is the same word found in 1 Corinthians 10:6 and Matthew 5:28, and no amount of "updating" can alter the meaning of the word. The word means to "greatly desire" anywhere that it is used (Rom. 1:24, 6:12, 7:7, 13:14, etc.). The Holy Spirit desires what the flesh abhors, and the flesh desires what the Holy Spirit abhors (Luke 16:15); and these items are listed in the next ten verses. The only way the Christian can avoid spending a lifetime running errands for a corpse (Rom. 6:2--6) is to ["walk in the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16) while his regenerated spirit (John 3:5--7) is still located ["in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 10:3). [Every Christian is a true schizo]{.class_4095} (see Gal. 2:20 and comments).

The Christian who is ["led by the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (judicially---Rom. 8:14) is not under the Law (judicially---Rom. 8:1). Furthermore, the Christian who follows the leadership of the Spirit (practically---vs. 18) is not under the condemnation of the Law (practically---Rom 8:1, 13). The [RV, ASV,]{.class_4095} and [NEB]{.class_4095}, with Phillips, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Weymouth, Berkeley, the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(any edition), and the other fifty "translations," all fail to understand these Christian []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_144}doctrinal truths; so all of them blithely knock out the second half of Romans 8:1 thinking they are doing the Holy Spirit a favor. The correct reading is found in the [King James 1611]{.class_4095}, as it is found in [Aleph ]{.class_4095}(Alexandrian family), [D ]{.class_4095}(Western family), [P ]{.class_4095}(Antiochan family), and the vast majority of Greek manuscripts.

The machinations of the Bible-rejecting Conservatives are as follows:

a\. The ["flesh"]{.class_4096} is only the ["lower nature,"]{.class_4095} not the flesh [itself ]{.class_4095}(Phillips and the [NEB]{.class_4095}).

b\. The flesh is "the sinful impulses" (Dummelow).

c\. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} manages a mighty mangling of verse 17 to read "to the end that what things soever ye fain would do, those ye SHALL NOT DO." (Fatalism if you ever heard it!)

d\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown resent ["is fulfilled"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14) because it was translated from ["pleroutain"]{.class_4095} instead of the Vatican's ["peplerotai."]{.class_4095} (But this is Origen and Pamphilus messing with the Bible and trying to "help God out." "Has been fulfilled" is the doctrinal statement of a Postmillennialist trying to apply Matthew 5:18 to the First Advent. The Law has NOT been fulfilled, and the reading of [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}here is a glaring forgery.)

e\. The flesh is human nature "estranged from God" (Williams).

f\. The minor changes suggested by Rendall, Meyer, Lightfoot, Lipsius, Ellicott, Wordsworth, Trench, Bullinger, and other "Conservatives" are not worth listing.

5:19 Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,

20 Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,

21 Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.

22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,

23 Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.

24 And they that are Christ's have crucified the flesh []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_145}with the affections and lusts.

25 If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.

26 Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.

Lust GETS; love GIVES. Then the secret of the victorious life lies not in "eradication" or "suppression" of the old nature, but counteraction. If the Christian is walking in the power of the Spirit, he is obviously not controlled by the flesh. At any moment he is under the control of one or the other.

Because this is true, a check list is now given to the Galatians to help them tell when the Spirit is running them and when the flesh is running them.

["Now the works of the flesh are MANIFEST"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19). That is, there is not a great deal of need for giving the list because ["the works of the flesh"]{.class_4096} are pretty apparent to anybody. As a matter of fact, the first four ["works of the flesh" ]{.class_4096}are so manifest that they are fleshly in the extreme. (The careful reader will preserve the distinction between WORKS and FRUIT \[vs. 22\]. Many self-righteous sacramentalists think because they have WORKS as an [evidence of faith ]{.class_4095}that they have [saving faith]{.class_4095}. Saving faith is connected with FRUIT, and WORKS can be ["dead works"]{.class_4096} \[Heb. 9:14\]. Tit. 2:14 and Eph. 2:10 in no way absolve the self-righteous, unregenerate, Hell-bound sinner from the demand for FRUIT. See John 15:1--6.)

Nearly all the commentators explain adultery as a married transgression while fornication is a "single" transgression. But of course, this is Webster's definition, not the Holy Spirit's. Those foolish enough to adopt it, have to make Matthew 19:5--9 look like the battle of Jutland before they emerge with an exposition (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew,]{.class_4095} Matt. 19:1--12). In the Bible, ["fornication"]{.class_4096} is the ACT itself at any time (1 Cor. 6:18), and[ "adultery"]{.class_4096} is the breaking of the marriage contract or spiritual declension from God or a LOOK (Matt. 5:28). Webster needs to be "revised."

["Lasciviousness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19) (Greek, ["aselgeia"]{.class_4095}) is outrageous intemperance of any kind and ranges from inordinate affections (Col. 3:5) to sodomy and extreme forms of sexual perversion (Lev. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_146}20:16).

["Idolatry" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20) is defined as ["covetousness"]{.class_4096} by the same apostle (see comments under Col. 3:5), but naturally, the word also includes the idolatry of the Greek scholars at the University of Athens (Acts 17:23).

["Witchcraft"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20) is the ["pharmakeia"]{.class_4095} (pharmacies!) of Revelation 9:21 and includes the use of LSD, heroin, marijuana, and opium in religious exercises. All drug addiction is connected with "seeking the truth," and the attempts of modern educators to isolate the addict from spiritual realities and spiritual truths is hopeless. The addict is a "seeker"; and he chooses the "experience" or the "happening" or the "moment of truth" with his pot, weed, grass, snow, etc., to escape the responsibility of living in the condition in which Genesis 3 placed the race. The addict's [fear ]{.class_4095}of "reality" is more sensible than the Liberal preachers [description ]{.class_4095}of "reality," for all Liberals are evolutionary Communists in their approach to social issues and society. The jungle society of today is the result of Genesis 3, and the addict usually finds out the nature of that society before the Liberal preacher does. He takes the wrong escape (as Saul did---1 Sam. 28), but he at least senses the hopelessness of life without the Comforter (John 14:26).

In the Bible, ["witchcraft"]{.class_4096} is associated with female practitioners (Deut. 18:10; 2 Chron. 33:6; Exod. 22:18; Nah. 3:4). The early Puritans were stupid enough to think that the Kingdom had come (they got this from John Calvin) and that, therefore, the rules which applied to [the theocracy of Israel ]{.class_4095}could be placed on [the community of New England]{.class_4095}. This led to the Salem "witchcraft" trials which ended where Servetus (Calvin's theological opponent) ended---the deaths of Bible--believing Protestants by a Church-State. [You see, the official theology of Catholicism on prophecy is the same as John Calvin's. ]{.class_4095}(See [The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_4095}, 1969.)

["Hatred, variance, emulations" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20). The first word is apparent. The next two words describe wranglings and jealousies. (Origen and the school at Alexandria have messed with the spelling of the words \[see Nestle's text\].)

["Wrath, strife, seditions, heresies"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20) are allusions to fits of temper, quarreling, stirring up rebellion against established []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_147}authority (especially in the church---1 Tim. 5:17), and following theological ways (vss. 1--4, for example) which are contrary to the revealed truth of the word. What Rome calls a "heretic" is not what the Bible calls a heretic, and vice versa. (Biblical salvation is called ["heresy"]{.class_4096} \[Acts 24:14\] by sacramental legalists. The Waldensians and Albigenes were called "heretics" by the wine-headed Italian princes who pretended to be ["vicars of Christ."]{.class_4095})

"Creationism" is heresy to an evolutionist. Catastrophic geology is heresy at the University of Chicago. Segregation is heresy to race mixers. Isolationism is heresy to international Socialists. The Bible was heresy to Voltaire, Rousseau, Tom Paine, and Madalyn Murray (cf. comment on Prov. 15:8 and 29:27 in [The Bible Believeis Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_4095} 1972). It works both ways.

An "infidel" and a "heretic" are two different birds. An infidel does not believe revealed truth. A heretic [accepts ]{.class_4095}revealed truth and then [alters ]{.class_4095}it and twists it to serve his own ends. Postmillennialism, Amillennialism, Baptismal Regeneration, and the "Assumption" of Mary are four of the biggest and most outstanding heresies which pass off as "Christian" doctrines. The [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV]{.class_4095} were not translated by [infidels]{.class_4095}; they were translated by [heretics]{.class_4095}.

["Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings "]{.class_4096} (vs. 21). Nestle has omitted ["phonoi"]{.class_4095} (Greek for "murders") from his text on the dubious authority of two corrupt manuscripts. These are the same two manuscripts that omit the ending on Mark 16---[Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}. The "fathers" who approve of the omission are Origen, Marcion, Jerome, and Augustine.

However, the [King James ]{.class_4095}preserves the reading of three families of manuscripts, plus the vast majority in any family---[A, C, D, E, F, G, K, L]{.class_4095}, etc.

The ["revellings"]{.class_4096} are quite contemporary and go on every weekend in the apartments of Washington D.C., the penthouses of New York, the private homes of Beverly Hills, and the dormitories of every major College in the United States. "Orgies" is as good a term as any, and the activity is four times as prevalent in America today as it was in 1900. Then it was ten times as prevalent as in 1700.

["Of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_148} (vs. 21). Now, for the second time, there is a clear reference to past preaching as in 4:13--16, not Galatians 1.

["That they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21). The English text flabbergasts the expositors and commentators so thoroughly this time that they admit their ignorance and do not even make any attempt to talk about "better texts," "archaeological discoveries," "oldest manuscripts," "the texts," "the recent editors," and all of the other lying stratagems that are normally resorted to in order to nullify the [AV 1611]{.class_4095} text. Rendall starts by writing a Greek word (["proeion"]{.class_4095}) and then finds himself standing out in a Bible Sahara alone without a thought, a text, an idea, a companion, a canteen, or supplies. He gazes across the vast horizons of Scriptural truth for several minutes and suddenly remembers that he couldn't read English to start with, so he'd better just forget the whole mess and pretend he's somewhere else.

This is quite typical of ALL commentators who reject the [AV 1611]{.class_4095} as an infallible text. When really faced with a genuine problem, they are not merely unable to mine out "nuggcts" from the "riches of the Greek text," [they are unable to break the ground deep enough to find water ]{.class_4095}(2 Pet. 2:17).

Dummelow skips comment. Bullinger's cross-reference (App. 114) gives the wrong kingdom (!). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown give two cross-references without defining either one. Rev. Harrison ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}) gives 1 Corinthians 6 but refuses to define the kingdom. Williams---who has been going by Bullinger---finds himself "up a creek without a paddle," so he skips the entire verse. And Rev. Huxtable ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20) gives all the cross-references [without believing any of them]{.class_4095}. The references he gives are to the Millennial reign in Palestine after it has been restored to Israel, [but Huxtable was a POSTMILLEN-NIALIST!]{.class_4095} Isn't it a pity that the "original manuscripts" can give us no light! And the truth of it is that if the original manuscripts were found, they couldn't give one scholar under heaven any light where that scholar rejected the [AV 1611]{.class_4095}. Nestle, who has access to P1--P75, [Aleph, A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, K, L, M, N, P, S, T, U,]{.class_4095} the Coptic, Gothic, Sahidic, Syriac, Bohairic, [Psi, Phi, Pi, Lambda, Omega, Theta, Delta, Gamma, ]{.class_4095}and the Latin, old [Itala]{.class_4095}, [Vulgate]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_149}, and the editions of Griesbach, Lachmann, Von Soden, Tischendorf, Weiss, Westcott and Hort, and Tregelles, cannot produce one Greek word or one Greek verse that would aid ANY reader in finding [the first basic truth ]{.class_4095}of the passage.

Phenomenon 14. Many times the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 is far superior to any manuscript extant, and on occasion it would be superior to the original manuscripts if they could be produced. A man who makes a resolution to the effect that he believes the unfound, unknown, unavailable "originals" are God-breathed and then refuses to make a resolution that he has available an infallible Book preserved without error, is a [leader in the apostasy of the "Body of the mystery" ]{.class_4095}(cf. comments under Eph. 3:1--6).

Now! Placing [all ]{.class_4095}of the major commentators, leading revisers, and best-known expositors carefully in the trash can---where they should have been to start with---we shall consult the Infallible Oracle to obtain infallible truth. (To paraphrase Dorothy Parker: "These commentators', revisers', and expositors' remarks should not be dismissed lightly; they should be thrown away with great force!")

1\. ["The kingdom of God"]{.class_4096} in the passage is not the Kingdom of God mentioned in Romans 14:17, for the one in Romans is a spiritual Kingdom into which a man is born (John 3:3, 5) in this age. The Kingdom of God, here, is an INHERITANCE (cf. Matt. 25:31--34).

2\. This Kingdom is earned by service (see Eph. 5:5 and Col. 3:24). It is not the "free gift by the grace of God" which placed the believer into the Kingdom of Colossians 1:13.

3\. This Kingdom of God, as the one in Acts 1:3, has to do with the appearance of the Kingdom (note ["see"]{.class_4096} in John 3:3--5) when Christ returns (cf. Luke 19:11--27).

4\. [Therefore, no Postmillennial commentator and no Amillennial commentator could interpret the passage correctly even if he had the "ORIGINALS" in his lap.]{.class_4095} (Correct interpretation and ability to expound the truth of the Scriptures does NOT lie in formal training in linguistics, archaeology, and etymology. What is purposely obscure to Kenneth Wuest is very clear to any Bible believer with an eighth-grade education---provided he believes what he reads.)

5\. The Premillennialists missed the passage because many of []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_150}them knew that they had done some of the things listed in the passage (!), and they could hardly confess to their readers that they were going to be knocked out of Luke 19:17. (Bullinger would not allow Luke 19:17 to apply to a Christian anyway, so that took care of him!!)

The "riches" of the "nuggets" in the "original Greek" are found [only in the AV text of 1611.]{.class_4095}

a\. You are saved by grace through faith, but you will not reign on earth with Christ (Rev. 5:10) unless you [suffer ]{.class_4095}with Him (Rom. 8:17).

b\. You can be denied [this inheritance ]{.class_4095}(2 Tim. 2:12), although you cannot be denied salvation (2 Tim. 2:13) once you are "in Christ" (1 Cor. 1:7--9).

c\. Your service for Christ (Col. 3:24), then, determines your earthly rewards in the Millennium (Luke 19:17). As Esau (Heb. 12:16), you can lose this inheritance (Heb. 12:17), [for it is conditioned on WORKS]{.class_4095}.

Isn't it amazing how much light "de Bible do shed on de commentatahs"? When in doubt, throw the commentaries out!

["But THE FRUIT of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22), not "the WORKS of the Spirit," although that is what one would expect by a comparison with verse 19. Here the Lord is showing the Christian another difference between the old and new nature. You worked for Hell before you were saved (Rom 6:23---["the WAGES"]{.class_4096}), but now God works in you while you let Him work (Phil 2:13). Thus, the new life is characterized by FRUIT, not "works" (Rom. 4:5; cf. Eph. 2:9--10 for the ["works"]{.class_4096}).

["Love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22). That is, all the things that the world seeks for, all the things the unregenerate worship, all the things that are useful and practical, and all the things that it takes to solve every problem with which the world is confronted.

Hippies, Beatnicks, and the [National Education Association ]{.class_4095}are hung up on ["love"]{.class_4096}---meaning "fornication." The Liberals have been preaching "love" so long (while arming Black Muslims and Communists for riots) that some people think God is a pucker-mouthed Santa Claus trying to see how many elections He can win by kissing babies. First Corinthians 13 is the greatest thing ever written, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_151}according to Bible-rejecting preachers in the National Council of Churches (Ecumenical Council), but either chapter near it (12 or 14) was "Paul's opinion" or "Pauline theology."

With all the static about "peace," you would think that some of the members of the United Nations might be interested in Galatians 5:22. It is "peace, peace, peace" from morning to night; and of course, ["there is no peace"]{.class_4096} (Isa. 48:22) because the Prince of Peace (Luke 2:14) came to bring a sword (Matt. 10:34), not peace. No ["swords" ]{.class_4096}can be turned into plowshares (Isa. 2:4) until He is ON THE THRONE (Luke 1:32). Therefore, to inscribe Isaiah 2:4 before the United Nations building in New York---[yes, it's there!]{.class_4095}---is like inscribing ["Blessed are the dead which die in the Lord"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 14:13) on the coffin of Ho Chi Minh.

The Jews got upset, so Liebemann wrote [Peace of Mind]{.class_4095}. The Catholics got upset, so Sheen wrote [Peace of Soul]{.class_4095}. The Protestants got upset, so Graham wrote [Peace with God]{.class_4095}. (The original is in Rom. 5:1 and Col. 1:20---["peace through the blood."]{.class_4096})

["Peace" ]{.class_4096}is the only thing that every crooked politician and unregenerate religious leader can talk about [constantly ]{.class_4095}without losing his popularity, so all of them talk about it (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy,]{.class_4095} Bible Bapist Bookstore, 1969). "Peace" is kind of like the weather: everybody talks about it, but nobody does anything about it. "Peace talks" are what politicians carry on so their countries can build up armies and navies. Only Russia and Red China (1950--1980) seemed to grasp the essential truth of history which is so graphically stated by the Prussians: ["In case of rain, the war will be held in the auditorium."]{.class_4095} While America expended her strength (and taxed her people to death) on a "war for peace" (1960--1970), Red China and Russia saved their men and munitions and got ready to fight a "war for WAR."

No nation really believes in "peace" enough to disarm, and any nation that professes to disarm is either lying or else is INSANE. Having rejected the peace of God (John 14:1--4), this world is destined to[ "wars and rumours of war"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 24:6) until Revelation 20:9. No amount of synthesizing, "communicating," integrating, leavening, leveling, or "class equalizing" is going to effect the Bible prophecy a whit.

["Love, joy, peace"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_152} (vs. 22). The Holy Spirit in the believer causes him to love God (Rom. 5:5) and his [fellow man ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 13:10--14). It causes him to love [God's people ]{.class_4095}(John 14:17, 26, 15:12, 17) and [God's word ]{.class_4095}(John 15:7). And he loves these items in their proper order. He is enabled to love [his wife as himself ]{.class_4095}(Eph. 5:23--28), and she is enabled to love her husband (Tit. 2:4) through the Holy Spirit (Rom. 5:5) where other ways are impossible. This love is ["the fruit of the Spirit,"]{.class_4096} not the result of meditation on the "Fatherhood of God and the Brotherhood of man." It is not the result of a round of drinks or a "trip" on drugs, and it is not in the least connected with the syrupy, suave, unctuous, flacid remarks found in [Unity ]{.class_4095}magazine, [Guideposts]{.class_4095}, and [The Upper Room. ]{.class_4095}Where the Spirit has filled a Christian, there is a love for lost men and women (Psa. 126:5--6), and there is a demonstration of 1 Corinthians 13 from the HEART, not from the MIND.

["Joy"]{.class_4096} is the chief element missing from American Christianity (of any brand). In some of the largest churches in America, one is impressed with a peculiar hypnotic, formal, stiff ritualism that reminds one of the Catholic "mess." ("Mess" means [food]{.class_4095}---that is what is served in the "mass." The word "mass" is an abbreviation for "dismissal," but the word is a misnomer: the word is ["mess."]{.class_4095}) Our Fundamental churches are stuffed with Christians who never experience "joy" inside the walls of their places of worship. They shout at the ball games, they laugh at the TV comedians, they lust at the musicals showing every night on television, they weep over "The Great Beyond" and "The Blue Horizon" and "Happiness is a thing called Unhappiness" (or as a colored lady said: "Ah likes to see dem aftehnoon soap operas on de TV, cause dats when de white folks suffah!"); but they cannot make a holy grunt under the preaching of the truth, and they can't even laugh with joy when a sinner takes his bleeding feet off the hot pavements of Hell and falls into the arms of Jesus Christ (Luke 15:23).

If true belief is from the heart (Rom. 10:9--10) and obedience must come from the heart (Matt. 22:37) and the heart determines ["the issues of life"]{.class_4096} (see Prov. 4:23 and comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_4095} 1972), what do you suppose God thinks of a Christianity that can only be stirred up to "worship" []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_153}by LSD, jazz combos, African music, Watusis, and "love-ins"? Is a Fundamentalist any different from a heathen when it takes a pay raise, a diploma, a Rose Bowl game, a "fish fry," or a 300-foot-long hot dog (!) to interest and excite him? Isn't the [heart condition ]{.class_4095}the same in both cases?

Real ["joy" ]{.class_4096}causes [rejoicing with other Christians ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 12:15), [rejoicing when a soul is converted ]{.class_4095}(Luke 15:7), [rejoicing at the destruction of Rome ]{.class_4095}(Rev. 19:1--3; missed that one, didn't you), [rejoicing at the return of Israel to Palestine ]{.class_4095}(Psa. 122:6), and [rejoicing in a present salvation ]{.class_4095}(1 Pet. 1:8).

When the Christian has peace with God through Jesus Christ (John 14:1--4), he is able to exhibit the greatest of the Divine attributes mentioned to Moses (Exod. 34:6)---["longsuffering."]{.class_4096}

The ["gentleness"]{.class_4096} of the verse does not imply docile or passive conduct, or the manner of Kahlil Gibran, Norman Vincent Peale, or Fulton Oursler. The Liberal's concept of "gentleness" means "yield to the enemy and let him get away with murder (Matt. 5:39), especially when I am in league with the enemy!" You never saw a Liberal council, church, or [minister ]{.class_4095}"turn the other cheek" to a man like Carl McIntire or Ian Paisley (1950--1970). There is nothing "gentle" about the tactics used by Liberals and Catholics when trying to get Fundamentalists off a radio station or when trying to prevent churches from leaving presbyteries or associations. (All Liberals are hypocrites when it comes to ecumenicism, and none of them practice what they preach.)

["Goodness, faith"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22). The reader should observe that the first three bunches of fruit are INWARD, the second three are OUTWARD, and the last three are GODWARD. There is nothing meek about Paul when he is preaching the Gospel (vs. 23---["meekness"]{.class_4096}), and his "fear and trembling" (1 Cor. 2:3) is the trembling of a man concerned about God using his message, not the trembling of a man worrying about offending his audience (see 1:8--9). ["Goodness"]{.class_4096} covers the whole life of outward acts toward men both lost and saved (1 Tim. 2:1--3; 2 Thess. 2:17; Tit. 3:1; 1 Pet. 3:16). The Christian must be gentle in his personal dealings with mankind; he must "do good" in relation to other men; and he must put up (["longsuffering"]{.class_4096}) with their revilings, indifference, unfair dealings, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_154}slander, persecutions, and "dirty dealings." None of this should compromise his message or his witness or his testimony. The teaching that the "nice Christian" should not "offend" anyone with strong language is not found in the passage.

["Faith, meekness, temperance"]{.class_4096} (vss. 22--23). These show the relationship of the believer to the Lord. He should exercise faith in [God's promises ]{.class_4095}and faith in [God's providences ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 8:28). He should receive instructions from the Lord meekly and submissively and not in the spirit of Simon Peter (the first "vicar of Christ"!) who used to say: ["Not so, Lord!"]{.class_4096} (Acts 10:14), ["Be it far from thee, Lord!" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 16:22), ["Thou shalt never wash my feet!" ]{.class_4096}(John 13:8), etc. The correct response should be ["Speak, Lord; for thy servant heareth"]{.class_4096} (1 Sam. 3:9). ["Temperance"]{.class_4096} implies [self-control ]{.class_4095}and shows that 1 Corinthians 9:25 should be the guideline for all Christians who are filled with the Spirit.

["Against such there is no law"]{.class_4096} (vs. 23). The things listed above are not condemned by any law, and they are contrary to nothing in the Law. Where the believer "walks in the Spirit" (vs. 16) and bears ["the "fruit of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22), there is neither judicial nor practical condemnation (see remarks under vs. 17). The Lord is the vine; we are the branches (John 15:5). We only [bear ]{.class_4095}up the clusters; He [produces ]{.class_4095}the clusters. Our bad works are the works of our own flesh which take place when we yield to it and give our will's consent to its works. Our [good works ]{.class_4095}are the result of the Holy Spirit's "fruit bearing" within us when we yield to the Spirit and give our will's consent to His fruit.

All of this can be grasped at one reading without the aid of one lesson in Greek.

Nevertheless:

1\. The word ["faith"]{.class_4096} should have been "respect," because it didn't refer to God; it referred to man (Rev. Rendall).

2\. It shouldn't have been ["faith"]{.class_4096}; it should have been ["fidelity"]{.class_4095} ([NEB ]{.class_4095}and Phillips).

3\. ["Gentleness"]{.class_4096} should have been ["kindness"]{.class_4095} (Alford, Phillips).

4\. ["Faith"]{.class_4096} should have been "trustfulness" (Conybeare, Howson).

5\. ["Gentleness"]{.class_4096} should have been "benignity" (Jamieson, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_155}Fausset, and Brown). (This, by the way, is the reading of the Jesuit [Rheims ]{.class_4095}version of 1582!)

6\. ["Gentleness" ]{.class_4096}should have been[ "kindness"]{.class_4095} ([RV]{.class_4095}, [ASV]{.class_4095}, and [RSV]{.class_4095}---same book, different editions).

And Lipsius, Wordsworth, Meyer, Lightfoot, and Ellicott make the usual alterations which mean nothing.

The fruits of the Spirit are for CHARACTER while the gifts of the Spirit (1 Cor. 12) have to do with POWER; hence, all carnal Christians are bucking for the "gifts" of 1 Corinthians 12 before the fruits of Galatians 5.

Now for the prejudiced reader who is tired of the detailed manner in which we have negated the other versions, let us see why we have been so particular in commenting about "works and fruit" in the preceding passages. Here before us lies the [Confraternity Revision of the Roman Catholic Church]{.class_4095} (St. Anthony Guild Press, 1941). Having absolutely refused to observe the wording chosen by the Holy Spirit in any set of manuscripts, the reader finds the Catholics saying (footnote, pg. 523) that the people who PRACTICE THE VIRTUES listed in Galatians 5:22--23 are under the law of Christ!

1\. You don't [practice ]{.class_4095}FRUIT; you bear it.

2\. You don't [practice ]{.class_4095}JOY; you experience it.

3\. You don't [practice ]{.class_4095}MEEKNESS, unless you are a hypocrite.

4\. The Holy Spirit bears Fruit in those who have the Spirit and those who WALK IN THE SPIRIT.

What you ["practice"]{.class_4095} is about as relevant to the passage as practicing baseball or football. You see, whoever wrote the footnote to the Catholic Bible knew nothing about the fruit of the Holy Spirit. He was someone who was trying to counterfeit the fruit [without the root ]{.class_4095}(Matt. 13:25).

["And they that are Christ's HAVE CRUCIFIED THE FLESH with the affections and lusts"]{.class_4096} (vs. 24; cf. Tit. 2:14). The reader should observe that this "crucifixion" is [self-inflicted.]{.class_4095} The one in Galatians 2:20 was inflicted by God Himself when the believer was united with Christ, but this one is the believer applying that truth (Rom. 6:4--8) to his present wrestling with the flesh. When Paul says ["I am crucified"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 2:20), he means literally, actually, in God's sight, he is dead and nailed (cf. Rom. 6:6) and will remain so until physical death (Rom. 7:24). When he says []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_156}["have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts,"]{.class_4096} he is speaking of a real Christian (2 Tim. 2:19) who has put into practice what God has declared to be so (Rom. 6: 11).

["Affections and lusts"]{.class_4096} covers the tremendous field which TV capitalizes on twenty-four hours a day (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_4095} 1972). The purpose of commercial advertising is to arouse or create [covetousness]{.class_4095}. ["Affections and lusts"]{.class_4096} are the two desires that attend coveting (see remarks on Col. 3:5). You [like ]{.class_4095}it or you [love ]{.class_4095}it. You want it or you desire it. You feel kindly disposed toward it, or you are crazy about it. Where the affections are set on things above (see Col. 3:1--3), the flesh is crucified [in the present tense.]{.class_4095}

Lamsa (from the Aramaic, supposedly) omits the word[ "crucified,"]{.class_4096} but we would expect this from such a sterile translation as Lamsa's, which perverts Matthew 27:46 to: ["For this was I kept"]{.class_4095}!

Crucifixion is a violent, shameful, painful, lingering death. There is, therefore, something about the Christian life (Phil. 1:6) that is a slow agony (Rom. 7:24--25) in spite of the fact that ["we are more than conquerors" ]{.class_4096}(Rom. 8:37) and[ "God\...always causeth us to triumph"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 2:14). Where the body is a true ["living sacrifice"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 12:1--2), it must perish, hanging on nails before the pitiless gaze of an unsympathetic world. The "crucified life" may last a year or fifty years or seventy years, but it is characterized in every century by public ridicule, painful experiences, religious persecution, betrayal of friends and loved ones, and fruit from the dying kernel (John 12:24). The best expositions of the text will be found in the works by E. L. Maxwell, Ruth Paxson, Andrew Murray, and Watchman Nee. The major commentators have little or nothing to offer at this point.

["If we live in the Spirit]{.class_4096} \[and we do, for we are "in Christ"\][, let us also WALK IN THE SPIRIT"]{.class_4096} (which we often do NOT; cf. vs. 16). We are in the flesh [geographically ]{.class_4095}(2 Cor. 10:3), but not [spiritually ]{.class_4095}(1 Cor. 6:17). Therefore, we really live (Rom. 8:9--10) ["in the Spirit."]{.class_4096} If this is so (see first clause), then ["let us also walk in the Spirit."]{.class_4096}

Williams throws the [AV]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ999.xhtml_page_157} 1611 out the window and tells us, dogmatically, that the verse "should read" as the [RV ]{.class_4095}1881. The [RV ]{.class_4095}(as its apostate children and grandchildren, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}1901 and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}1946) has changed the ["in"]{.class_4096} to ["by."]{.class_4095} Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown also adopt this scribal blot, and then they have the brass to inform the reader that "by" is what the Greek says! The "Greek" says nothing of the kind. Every manuscript ever found says[ "en"]{.class_4095} (Gk.), which Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown translated as "IN" in Romans 8:9.

As a matter of fact, the [ASV, RV, RSV]{.class_4095}, the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, and other caricatures of the Bible, all translate ["en"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) as[ "in"]{.class_4095} in the companion passage.[ "By the spirit"]{.class_4095} merely erases the cross-reference to Romans 8:9. Phenomenon No. 15.

15\. Very often the critics of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 will lie about what a Greek word "means" and then alter it to suit their own fancy. ["En"]{.class_4095} cannot only mean "by" but also "among," "upon," "before," "in respect to," "in the case of," "on the occasion of," "on the ground of," "with," "equipped with," "furnished with," etc.

To say that "the Greek should be 'by' not ['in']{.class_4096}" is to say: "I don't like the [King James, ]{.class_4095}so I'll just pretend it's wrong." (And that really is what lies behind all this big, new, modern, scientific "research" that goes on. A nasty old bulldog busted somebody's kiddy car.)

["Let us not be desirous of vain glory\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 26). The verse needs no exposition. It is aimed at Christians like those in the Corinthian church (1 Cor. 1--3), and it cautions them against being proud, puffed up, and opinionated. Envy and strife are "out" for the believer. Any attempt to meet the requirements of Jewish legalistic standards will only breed that type of fellowship so common in "holiness" churches: "Did you see that dress she was wearing!?" "Sure she dyes her hair; can't you tell?!" "I did NOT cut my hair! It comes down to my ankles!" etc. The Amish and the Mennonites have been known to have church "splits" over buttons, laces, seams, radios, tractors, collars, rings, and plowshares. (One cannot help but think of the Quaker who said to George Whitefield: "If thou wilt not quarrel with me about my broad brim, I shalt not find fault with thy clerical robes. Give me thine hand, George!")

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml}

# Chapter 6 {.heading_116}

6:1 Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.

2 Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.

3 For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.

4 But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.

5 For every man shall bear his own burden.

6 Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.

The [NEB ]{.class_4095}gets off to a flying start by implying that some Christians are ["endowed"]{.class_4095} with the Spirit and some are not! (This is the logical outcome of rejecting the position of the believer ["in the Spirit." ]{.class_4096}See comments just made under 5:25.) The [RV, ASV, ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV]{.class_4095}, with their corrupt counterparts, now demonstrate for us Phenomenon No. 16.

16\. Insist on "exactness" to change the [AV ]{.class_4095}and then forget exactness when being exact will MATCH the [AV.]{.class_4095}

Here, the word ["paraptoma"]{.class_4095} (Gk., ["fault"]{.class_4096}) has been translated as ["trespass"]{.class_4095} in the [RV, ASV, RSV, ]{.class_4095}and [New ASV. ]{.class_4095}The same word, however, has been translated as ["sins"]{.class_4095} in James 5:16 to match the Jesuit edition of the Roman Catholic Bible of 1582, which uses James 5:16 as a proof text for the confessional! When we examine Kurt Aland (Greek New Testament, United Bible Societies, 1966, pg. 789) and Nestle [(Novum Testamentum Graece, ]{.class_4095}1960, pg. 488), we find the word ["hamartias" ]{.class_4095}(Gk., "sins") has been inserted into the text without any manuscript evidence. We are to assume---according to Nestle's explanation on pages 78 and 79 of the English preface---that the [King James ]{.class_4095}reading ["paraptomata" ]{.class_4095}(Gk., ["fault") ]{.class_4096}is found only in the [Receptus ]{.class_4095}manuscripts and that [Aleph, B,]{.class_4095} etc., all say[ "sins"]{.class_4095} (["hamartias"]{.class_4095}) as the Roman Catholic Jesuit Bible. However, Aland gives no explanation for the substitution, after giving []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_159}[seventy‑five references to the difference in spelling between "anaplerosete" and "anaplarosate" in Galatians ]{.class_4095}6:2! Isn't it rather strange that a "scholar" would devote seventy‑five notations to manuscripts and versions on the spelling of ["fulfil"]{.class_4096} and then forget to give one reference on a substitution where Roman Catholic doctrine has been inserted into the Holy Bible in place of the Reformation text? And you call this "scholarship"?

The [AV ]{.class_4095}consistently translates the word ["paraptomata" ]{.class_4095}as ["fault" ]{.class_4096}in both places (Gal. 6:1 and James 5:16). The[ ASV, RSV, RV, ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}are not even consistent where they made the Catholic substitution!

Here is a Christian who is caught in a ["fault."]{.class_4096} A ["fault"]{.class_4096} is a basic problem that deals with basic character, not an overt transgression properly called a "sin." The ["fault" ]{.class_4096}lies in the timber itself and is inherent in the grain. The besetting sin of Hebrews 12:1 is much closer to the meaning than an accidental "slip" of a brother. The brother thus tempted (vs. 1) has gotten out of fellowship with the Lord, and his ["fault" ]{.class_4096}has been discovered by the "brethren." He is to be restored as the man of 2 Corinthians 2:6--10 was restored. Those who undertake to "straighten him out" had better do it carefully (and prayerfully), for the Adversary (Job 1:8) is ready with: "They're talking mighty big for a bunch of sinners! How about letting me put on them what I put on him!" Matthew 7:1--5 and Proverbs 24:17 are commentaries on the operation. David is overtaken by Nathan, and the "restoration" is not "You deserve to die, and God is going to kill you, and it serves you right!"; but rather, ["The ]{.class_4096}[Lord ]{.class_4103}[also hath put away thy sin; thou shalt not die" ]{.class_4096}(2 Sam 12:13; cf. 1 John 5:17).

["Bear ye one another's burdens\...For every man shall bear his own burden" ]{.class_4096}(vss. 2--5). We have omitted much of the passage to show the contrast between verse 2 and verse 5. Verse 2 is talking about helping a brother with a burden in addition to your own burden, which you should carry yourself (cf. Jer. 23:33--38, 10:19). As someone has said: "You [cannot spell 'BROTHERS' without spelling 'OTHERS.'"]{.class_4095} ["The law of Christ"]{.class_4096} is superior to Moses' Law (John 7:23) and goes beyond "God's law" (Rom. 7:22--25). ["The law of Christ" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_160}(see comments on 5:13--18) is the law of love (John 15:12), and helping others bear their loads is part of it. The greatest burden-bearer who ever lived was the Sin‑bearer (Isa. 53:3--10) who said, ["My yoke is easy, and my burden is light" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 11:30). The psalmist tells us to cast our burdens on the Lord (Psa. 55:22) while we are helping others bear theirs. Never should we be guilty of "burden‑loading" like the Pharisees of Matthew 23:4.

["For if a man think himself to be something\... he deceiveth himself" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3; cf. 1 Cor. 8:2). If a man thinks he is spiritual (vs. 1) when he is not, he is deceiving himself, but he is not deceiving God (vs. 7). If a man thinks he is something when he refuses to help a brother with a load (vs. 2), he is nothing. If a man will not bear his own burdens (vs. 5), he is nothing, and if a man thinks he is something just because he borrowed somebody else's glory, he is nothing.

["But let every man prove his own work"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4) by the word of God and by the Lord Himself (2 Tim. 2:15). He should not be comparing it with somebody else's and then thinking he is a big shot because his work looks better than the man's next to him (2 Cor. 10:12). The man whom the Lord commends is approved (2 Cor. 10:18), not the man who commends himself. When the Lord commends a man, the man knows it, and this is the cause for the rejoicing mentioned in the verse. Thus, real joy comes from knowing you have done what God told you to do, regardless of what any man thought about it or regardless of how it stacked up alongside any other man's work.

["Then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). Often Christians borrow each other's "alleluias" and pawn them off as their own. Get your own "alleluia" and get your own victory; you cannot live off another person's victories forever. ("We sure are shaking this bridge," said the flea on the elephant as they went over it together.)

(Phillips tries to update things in vs. 5 with ["every man must shoulder his own pack"]{.class_4095}; but "tote his own load" is a little clearer, or "carry your own end of the load," or "shell out your quota," if you are really going to mess the Bible up good.)

["Let him that is taught\...communicate unto him that teacheth" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). The word ["communicate" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_161}is used in the sense of talking back and forth (2:2) or "helping people out with material goods" (Phil. 4:15; Heb. 13:16). These meanings are clear in the context; there is no need to mess up the text just because someone is too lazy to look at an alternate reading in a margin. Romans 15:27 is the Holy Spirit's comment on the passage (cf. Phil. 4:14).

6:7 Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.

8 For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.

9 And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.

10 As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.

11 Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.

12 As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.

13 For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.

14 But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.

15 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth any thing, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.

16 And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.

17 From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.

18 Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

Verse 7 has been the text for more sermons than you could count in several weeks. It would not be exaggerating it a bit to say that a sermon on verse 7 has been preached somewhere on an average of ten times a week every week since 1611. Many of us who were evangelists for years preached on the text as many as ten times a year. There are still---in spite of the tremendous apostasy following the advent of television (1950)---more than 100 full‑time evangelists in the United States alone. Eighty thousand sermons on the text, since 1611, would be a very conservative estimate.

["Be not deceived"]{.class_4096} is a commandment, not a suggestion or a piece of "constructive criticism." You should not kid yourself about the results of sowing to the flesh or the Spirit, and you CANNOT kid God about it. ["God is NOT mocked." ]{.class_4096}The hedonists (the Hugh Hefners and Joe Fletchers of every age) may treat sin lightly or flippantly or even hilariously, but the end result is the same---CORRUPTION. ["He who laughs last, laughs best" ]{.class_4095}(Prov. 1:24--33), and the last laugh is God's (Psa. 2:4). Darwin, Huxley, Paley, Lyell, Haeckel, Engels, Marx, Trotsky, Einstein, Dewey, Russell, Gentile, and Dr. David Reubin were deceived; however, they received commandment [not to be deceived.]{.class_4095}

["BE NOT DECEIVED"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). The commandment is written to ["a man"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7), and ["he that soweth" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8) clearly indicates a universal application. The fact that you do not believe the Bible is immaterial. You still reap what you sow. ["God is not mocked."]{.class_4096}

[Furthermore, ]{.class_4095}you reap MORE than you sow. Nobody reaps one grain of corn from one grain of corn; a stalk comes up with ears of corn on it. [Furthermore, ]{.class_4095}you don't expect to reap apples where you sowed wheat, and you don't expect to reap grapes where you sowed barley. Jeremiah 12:13 and Hosea 8:7 violate the natural law, but that is because the sinners in the passage who defied God violated the natural law before they sowed (Isa. 24:5--6). [Furthermore, ]{.class_4095}you often reap what someone else sows (John 4:36--37), and what you sow, someone else may get to reap (John 4:37--38). [Furthermore, ]{.class_4095}there is a season to reap and a season to sow (Eccl. 11:1--6), and you cannot delay the harvest when it comes (Prov. 22:8).

Sow liquor---reap drunkards. Sow Hollywood---reap divorces. Sow TV---reap sex perversion. Sow Africans---reap a mongrel race. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_163}Sow infidelity---reap suicides.

["Be not deceived; God is not mocked"]{.class_4096}---even if YOU are. Hypnotize yourself, fool yourself, kid yourself, tell yourself the picture isn't that bad. Make up some elaborate alibi about your case being different from anyone else's, etc.; and do you know what will happen?

You'll reap. ["For WHATSOEVER a man soweth, THAT shall he also REAP" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 7).

And thank God it works both ways! (2 Cor. 9:6; Eph. 6:8). Many of us wore out our "constitutions" before we got saved and have been living on our "bylaws" for many years, and yet God has graciously given us time to sow something good before calling us out of the field. Every truth preached was good seed (Eccl. 11:1--6), and someday you will harvest it. Every deed done out of a genuine love for Christ was good seed, and someday you will harvest that. Every time you magnified the Lord, stood up for Him before His enemies (Psa. 94:10), bragged about Him (Psa. 35:27), and praised Him for His mercy (Psa. 103), you sowed a crop that someday will bloom.

["He that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit REAP LIFE EVERLASTING" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8). What we do in the flesh for the flesh will be counted as errands run for a corpse (Rom. 6:3--8). What we do in the Spirit for the Lord will be counted as ["gold, silver, and precious stones" ]{.class_4095}(1 Cor. 3:12). "Reaping" everlasting life matches the[ "laying hold"]{.class_4095} of I Timothy 6:12, 19. In either case, the reaper already has eternal life (1 John 5:12--13) but comes to a full enjoyment of it with all that has accrued to his own individual account (1 Cor. 3:11--14) since he first trusted Christ.

The reader should observe that the flesh is ours---"[HIS flesh," ]{.class_4096}but the Spirit is God's; hence---["THE]{.class_4096} [Spirit," ]{.class_4096}not "HIS spirit." (Both Phillips and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}obscure the sense of the passage; and, of course, the changes by Rendall, Huxtable, Conybeare, Howson, Alford, Trench, Meyer, and Wordsworth are---you know what.)

Opportunities for sowing come and go. As Talmage said, "You might as well go gunning for the larks that whistled in the meadow last year as fetch back a lost opportunity and bag it" (see vs. 10).

["And let us not be weary in well doing\...As we have therefore OPPORTUNITY, let us DO GOOD UNTO ALL MEN, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_164}especially unto them who are of the household of faith" ]{.class_4096}(vss. 9--10). The worker is encouraged to keep on sowing in the right field. We often get ["weary in well doing," ]{.class_4096}and the Devil makes it certain that ["well doing"]{.class_4096} is always more tedious than "bad doing." Stolen apples usually taste better than the ones you buy at the market (see Prov. 9:17 in [The Bible Believers Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}1972). And as a wheelchair patient said who was just trying out his new chair, "Well, I'll be doggone. Looka there! I can go backerds bettern I kin go forerds!"

["In due season" ]{.class_4096}is the Lord's season, and until then we are to be instant ["in season, out of season"]{.class_4096} (2 Tim. 4:1--4). If we faint (Prov. 24:10), then we cannot sow (Eccl. 11:1--6)---no sowing, no crop!

The opportunities for soulwinning will not always be here. In Spain, they are gone and have been gone since A.D. 500. The Basques in Spain, in 1971, were being executed by a Church‑State under one of the greatest Fascist dictators who ever lived. The Ulster Protestants of 1970 were being assassinated and stoned for trying to do what Catholics had done in Ulster for years---publicly parade down the streets. In America today (1997), the government is bankrupting every private religious institution that refuses to admit Negroes for Christian training. The opportunities will not always be here.

Opportunities to show kindness to your parents will not always be here; some day they will lie under the dirt. Opportunities to receive Christ will not always be here. ["As we have therefore OPPORTUNITY, let us do good."]{.class_4096}

You will never get completely over a misspent youth. If you are still young, you'd better start to clean things up now while you have the opportunity, for ["God is not mocked\...he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption" ]{.class_4096}(vss. 7--8).

The[ ]{.class_4095}"let us not flag" of the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}is hardly the way to explain anything. Soden's alterations, Winer's alterations, Lightfoot's alterations, Lipsius' alterations, with the changes suggested by Conybeare, Ellicott, DeWette, and Jowett, mean nothing one way or another and can be dispensed with without losing any of the "deeper meaning" of the passage.

["Let us do good UNTO ALL MEN" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_165}(vs. 10; cf. Tit. 2:5; 1 Thess. 3:6, 12; Eph. 6:8--9). ["Doing good"]{.class_4095} [is sowing, ]{.class_4095}and yet the reader should not fail to keep up with the changes in "person" and "number" in the [AV ]{.class_4095}text. Any man will reap corruption from sowing to his flesh, but every man will NOT reap everlasting life by ["doing good." "Do‑gooders" ]{.class_4095}(Gal. 5:4) have already been cancelled out of the book of Galatians; the people, here, who are told to ["do good" ]{.class_4096}are saved people "in Christ" in whom Jesus Christ dwells. (["The household of faith"]{.class_4096} is in Eph. 2:19 and 1 Tim. 3:15.)

["Ye see HOW LARGE A LETTER I have written unto you with mine own hand" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). The Jesuits have always objected to this translation because their fabrication of 1582 [(Rheims), ]{.class_4095}printed to compete with Erasmus' [Receptus]{.class_4095}, has written ["what manner of LETTERS I have written to you."]{.class_4095}

Since all commentators and revisers since 1881 are Roman Catholic in their attitude towards the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text---and especially the ["New" ASV ]{.class_4095}revisers---we should not be surprised to find the Vatican-\
approved Papal text preserved in Phillips (1958), the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961), the [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881), the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946), and the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), and condoned by Dummelow, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Jamieson, Fausset and Brown, Williams, Vincent, Alford, Ellicott, Neal, Bullinger, Weniger, Meyer, Wordsworth, Custer, Lightfoot, DeWette, Westcott and Hort, and other envious, bitter fanatics who despise the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}Bible[. ]{.class_4095}Harrison (Fuller Theological Seminary) says piously that the [King James ]{.class_4095}reading is not a good "rendering." [The College of Cardinals says, "Amen." ]{.class_4095}Isn't it a pity that God the Holy Spirit cannot preserve His inspired word without the help of those whose lives and talents are dedicated to altering it?

But again, the entire body of Catholic, Protestant, and atheistic scholarship is "fudging." Why all the "accuracy" this time about the dative plural of ["gramma" ]{.class_4095}(Gk., "letter") after omitting [whole words ]{.class_4095}(Gal. 5:21) and [phrases ]{.class_4095}(Gal. 5:1) and changing ["voice" ]{.class_4095}(5:12) to prove theological points? After tearing out an admonition to ["obey the truth" ]{.class_4096}(3:1---every translation since [1881), ]{.class_4095}who are you to go around blabbing about "singulars and plurals"? Having swallowed the camel, are you going to expect us to give you audience while you []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_166}strain the gnat?

If it was ["large letters," ]{.class_4095}what would that mean? Wasn't the trouble stated perfectly by Rev. E. Harrison when he complained that it could not mean ["large a letter" ]{.class_4096}because Galatians "is not a large letter"? Isn't that the real reason why all of them changed it? And if not, would you read Rev. Huxtable's old cliche: "Paul doesn't use the word to mean letter anywhere else, so\...." Neander, however, disagrees with the Catholics, Phillip Schaff, Lightfoot, Alford, Dr. A. T. Robertson, Westcott and Hort, Dr. Green, et al., and accepts the English translation as correct. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown state that it must be "large letters" to prove that Paul wrote it himself---not through an "amanuensis." Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown call this a "token," showing that Paul wrote in his own hand. But this will not do at all, for the "token," according to the One who inspired the "originals," was ["the salutation of Paul with mine own hand" ]{.class_4096}(2 Thess. 3:17), not "how large LETTERS I have written."

The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}states the matter very clearly when it states that the [AV ]{.class_4095}translation gives the sense CORRECTLY, although it "cannot be defended as a LITERAL translation." But why would anyone who knows God or the Bible waste time to go for a "literal translation" every time? None of the revisers do! The [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_4095}did not give us a "literal translation" of [Hades,]{.class_4095} [Sheol, ]{.class_4095}or [Gehenna. ]{.class_4095}As a matter of fact, they didn't give us ANY translation in ten passages\![ ]{.class_4098}[Aren't we something to talk about "singulars" and "plurals"?]{.class_4095}

You see, when dealing with religious hypocrites, one should always remember that first and foremost (above every consideration) is their desire to rid the world of the [King James 1611 Authorized Version. ]{.class_4095}Any expediency will do. To be brief, observe how the original Mafia (the committee of 1881 who set the pace) handles some of the Bible while bellyaching about the singular and plural of[ "gramma"]{.class_4095} in Galatians 6:11.

1\. There isn't one single article in any [Greek text of Matthew 1:1,]{.class_4095} yet the [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RSV]{.class_4095}, and [RV]{.class_4095}---after hollering about "articles" in the [King James---]{.class_4095}insert THREE articles for "expedience sake." [None of them are in italics.]{.class_4095}

2\. The words of the Holy Spirit---["about the time" ]{.class_4096}(Matt 1:11)---are made to read[ "at the time of,"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_167} and yet, the sense of the Greek is "ABOUT THE TIME OF"!

You see, nobody is literal except when he wishes to alter the AV text.

3\. The word ["child"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 2:13), referring to Jesus Christ, is the same word for ["child" ]{.class_4096}(Gk., "[paida") ]{.class_4095}in Acts 4:27, 30; yet the gentlemen so concerned about "literal translation" and "more accurate translations" have slapped ["child" ]{.class_4096}clean out of the text where it was connected [with Christ's Deity ]{.class_4095}(Acts 4:27!). They have translated it by a different word so you would [question ]{.class_4095}His Deity!

And some incredible ass on the faculty at BIOLA, Wheaton, Moody, Columbia, Fuller, Dallas, BJU, Tennessee Temple, or Pensacola Christian College will now tell us that ["letter" ]{.class_4096}should be "letters" in Galatians 6:11!

4\. All of the modern corruptions---[ASV]{.class_4095}, [RSV, NEB, RV, ]{.class_4095}etc.---translate[ "the evil one"]{.class_4095} (Matt 6:13) for ["evil,"]{.class_4096} and yet, the very same construction is found in Matthew 5:39 and is translated as ["him who is evil" ]{.class_4095}or ["one who is evil." ]{.class_4095}Is this "LITERAL" translating?

5\. How on earth do you get ["righteousness"]{.class_4095} out of ["dikaiosunen" ]{.class_4095}(Gk., Matt. 6:1), and yet ["your right mind"]{.class_4095} [(RSV) ]{.class_4095}and ["to soberness"]{.class_4095} [(RV ]{.class_4095}and [ASV) ]{.class_4095}in 1 Corinthians 15:34? And now you would correct the Book that saved us and blessed us and tell us that ["letter"]{.class_4096} should be "letters"? Go stick your head in a bucket of water three times and pull it out twice.

When in doubt, throw the lexicon out: O‑U‑T, OUT!

Paul is plainly saying: "This is a long letter for me to write because writing is tough on my eyes (4:15), but I am taking the time out to do it so you can see how concerned I am. I am not waiting for an 'amanuensis.' And if the [RV ]{.class_4095}or the [ASV ]{.class_4095}or the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}(or any other [ASV) ]{.class_4095}translators put an 's' on this here word ['letter,']{.class_4096} [ask them why they forgot to put one on]{.class_4095}[ 'sabbath']{.class_4100}[ in Matthew 28:1. If ]{.class_4095}there is anything I can't stand, it's an inconsistent hypocrite!"

["As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should SUFFER PERSECUTION FOR THE CROSS OF CHRIST" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12). Or "in the original," it reads: "As many as desire to strut their education constrain you to mix your [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible with the ['New ASV]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_168},' only lest they should suffer persecution from the dead‑orthodox apostates who are working on the next revision!" We have commented on the text before under 1:10 and 4:17.

["For neither they themselves\...keep the law\...that they may glory in your flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 13). No Seventh‑day Adventist keeps all the Law any more than any Old Testament Jew kept all the Law (John 7:19). Even where outward conduct was ["blameless"]{.class_4096} (Luke 1:6), the first commandment was violated regularly, and probably the tenth one as well. The only purpose in getting the new convert to move back toward the Law is to brag about it: "Looka here! We got one of those Baptists to see the truth!" Bullingerite churches, Jehovah's Witness churches, Seventh‑day Adventist churches, and Campbellite churches are composed---90 percent---of former members of Baptist churches who were "zealously affected" and "saw the light." Catholics will still be lauding Cardinal Newman [after ]{.class_4095}the rapture.

The outward change (circumcision) can cause people to glory in the[ "flesh,"]{.class_4096} but the inward change (vs. 15) can only cause glory in the Spirit (1 Cor. 1:31).

["But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world IS CRUCIFIED UNTO ME, and I UNTO THE WORLD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). Paul speaks of the cross as representing the finished atonement of Christ and its affect on the Christian. There are four aspects to this "cross."

1\. The believer is crucified with Christ (Gal. 2:20) [in the present.]{.class_4095}

2\. Christ Himself was crucified [in the past.]{.class_4095}

3\. The world is crucified to the believer, for it is dead (John 17:9) and lies under the wrath of God (2 Pet. 3:7--10).

4\. The believer is crucified to the world, for he has been ruined in relation to it (John 16:1--4), and he is a ["savour of death unto death"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 2:16) to those who love it (2 Tim. 4:10).

It is dangerous to translate ["is crucified" ]{.class_4096}as ["has been crucified" (ASV, RSV, RV, ]{.class_4095}Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, the [Amplified New Testament, ]{.class_4095}etc.), for this gives the Christian the false impression that the problem of his relationship to the world is a[ ]{.class_4095}past event settled once and for all, and that, therefore, there is no need to claim the reality of the relationship every day (see comments under []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_169}Phil. 3:10). (Cf. comments on tense under Gal. 2:20.)

["For in Christ Jesus neither CIRCUMCISION availeth any thing, nor UNCIRCUMCISION, but a NEW CREATURE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15). The corrupt translators who corrupted the English Bible in 1881 (with the corrupt [RV) ]{.class_4095}have stricken out ["For in Christ Jesus," ]{.class_4096}and the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and other corrupt modern "translations" follow suit. Neither the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) nor [Good News for Modern Man ]{.class_4095}(1966) have the missing words. The premillennial "Fundamentalist" from Moody Bible Institute, Kenneth Wuest, also knocks ["Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4096}out of the translation. ["Christ Jesus"]{.class_4096} is found in the Hesychian, Western, Caesarean, and Syrian families of manuscripts exactly as the Holy Spirit has preserved it in the [Authorized Version of 1611.]{.class_4095}

The man who is ["IN Christ Jesus"]{.class_4096} (as the correct text states) is ["a new creature" ]{.class_4096}(2 Cor. 5:17). Since this creature is a spiritual creation (John 3:3--6), physical operations have nothing to do with it; therefore, circumcision is a moot point. The "new translations" have misread the passage entirely and have deliberately stricken out ["Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4096}to teach a private interpretation that Paul did not have in mind when he wrote the text.

The intelligent believer---who has better sense than to think that Kenneth Wuest (and those like him) know what they are talking about---will observe that the context of the passage is a [saved man ]{.class_4095}in Christ (vs. 11) who is warning [saved men ]{.class_4095}in Christ (vs. 13) about the danger of bragging over physical things (vs. 15). Paul has more reason to brag than any of them, since he bore physical marks in his body (vs. 17) which spoke of a [consecration ]{.class_4095}far beyond circumcision. The conjecture of the Revision Committee of 1881, carried out so rigorously by the [ASV ]{.class_4095}of 1901 (and the [New ASV]{.class_4095}), was based on the idea that God demanded a "new creation" of the [unsaved man. ]{.class_4095}This is not the subject of the passage. The original corrupter was the writer of P46 and [Vaticanus]{.class_4095}, and these two corruptions were adopted and promoted by Augustine (see[ Nestle's,]{.class_4095} pg. 489, footnote).

["And as many as walk according to this rule\...and upon the Israel of God" ]{.class_4096}(vs 16). Rev. Harrison [(Wycliffe Commentary, ]{.class_4095}pg. 1298) has caught the sense of the expression which [Good News for Modern Man ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_170}(1966) entirely misses (cf. Rom. 2:28--29; 1 Pet. 1:1, 2:10). The entire Body of Christ [(Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_4095}pg. 313) is not intended, but rather those saved Jews who were teaching salvation by grace apart from circumcision, for this was the doctrinal conclusion reached by the assembly at Jerusalem in Acts 15.

["I bear in my body the MARKS of the Lord Jesus" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17). The ["marks"]{.class_4096} are plainly a reference to the beatings, stonings, and whippings of 2 Corinthians 11:23--28.

The meaning of the admonition is pretty clear. "Don't let me hear any more criticism of my ministry up in your area from men who don't know what they are talking about. If you think circumcision is 'mark ing the flesh,' I'll show you some real 'marks'!" (As someone has said, ["The more MARX, the less MARKS." ]{.class_4095}No real Marxist was ever whipped for being faithful to the Lord Jesus.)

Paul, like Richard Wurmbrand, could take off his shirt and show people a thing or two if they got talking about the "flesh" too much. One hundred and ninetyfive lashes with a whip (2 Cor. 11:24) are going to leave some ["marks."]{.class_4096} Paul had the ["stripes" ]{.class_4096}(Isa. 53:5) laid on him just like they were laid on the Lord Jesus (1 Pet. 2:24).

Although Paul's blood was able to "heal" no one, it was caused to ooze out of lacerated wounds, punctured flesh, and bruised tissue (2 Cor. 11:24). A man who has been ["stoned"]{.class_4096} and ["beaten with rods" ]{.class_4096}(2 Cor. 11:25) is going to bear some marks for life. No movie made about Paul ever reproduced the picture faithfully. Paul's physical appearance at the close of his ministry would have resembled a retired pro‑football tackle who had spent his time in retirement serving as a punching dummy for a heavyweight boxer.

The "stigmata" of Roman Catholic mythology is entirely out of the question, although the Antichrist may reproduce the phenomenon to convince people he is the Messiah (see [Mark of the Beast,]{.class_4095} 1960)[.]{.class_4095}

The [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RV, RSV, ]{.class_4095}Dummelow, Kurt Aland, Nestle, and ["Good News"]{.class_4095} (1966) all omit the ["Lord" ]{.class_4096}from the verse; and Bullinger and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown condone the omission. Phenomenon No. 17.

17\. After stating explicitly that one of the rules for translating would be "to chose the reading characteristic of the writer's style," []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_171}the modern translators ignore Paul's "style" altogether and make him say something unlike his style. Although Paul uses the word ["Jesus"]{.class_4096} alone about twelve times, he uses ["Lord"]{.class_4096} or ["Christ"]{.class_4096} with the name over 140 times!

["Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18). ["The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096} is clearly defined in 2 Corinthians 8:9. The verse is self‑explanatory. The ["spirit" ]{.class_4096}of the text is the human spirit (1 Thess. 5:23; 2 Tim. 4:22) which has been regenerated by the Spirit of God (John 3:5).

Here ends the Epistle to the Galatians. Its teaching is clear. A man is saved by grace through faith and is kept by a God‑given faith which God Himself imparts to the man at the New Birth. The saved sinner is "IN" Jesus Christ and is justified by Christ's finished atonement, apart from the works of the Law. Not even circumcision, which preceded the Law, has any bearing on justification now. All "gospels," therefore, which exhort the sinner to any kind of works in order to obtain justification from God are "gospels" proceeding from the mouths of lost men (Gal. 1:8--9). Quoting James 2:24 solves no problem for the grace‑rejecting apostate, for no apostate who ever used James 2:24 could tell you what James 1:1 meant or why Abraham's justification came AFTER his imputed righteousness (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}Gen. 15:6, 1970).

CHECK LIST

At this point, it is in order to insert a list of the seventeen phenomena which we have run across in our study of Galatians. This list may be used as a point of reference while studying Ephesians, Philippians, and Colossians whenever the reader encounters the same phenomena. As we have indicated before, one of the outstanding characteristics of 98 percent of the modern revisers and commentators is that they are basically LIARS. When checked out thoroughly according to their own rules (by which they have altered the [Authorized Text), ]{.class_4095}they stand out vividly as brilliant hypocrites who can no more practice what they preach than Karl Marx.

[No. 1. ]{.class_4095}Alter the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text even where [the Greek supports it.]{.class_4095}

[No. 2. ]{.class_4095}Throw out the "oldest manuscripts" if they back up the [AV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_172}text.

[No. 3.]{.class_4095} Give a false rendition of the "oldest manuscripts" while criticizing the [AV ]{.class_4095}for doing the same.

[No. 4. ]{.class_4095}Translate the same Greek word a different way after complaining about the "lack of uniformity" in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611.
