[No. 5.]{.class_4095} Where all manuscripts back up the [AV ]{.class_4095}reading, the commentator or translator [invents a Greek word ]{.class_4095}found in none of the manuscripts.

[No. 6. ]{.class_4095}Absolute refusal to translate tense or articles correctly after complaining about the [AV]{.class_4095}'s refusal to do it.

[No. 7. ]{.class_4095}Where "uniformity" will back up the Deity of Christ, it is dropped, and another word is substituted which will obscure the Deity of Christ.

[No. 8. ]{.class_4095}Many of the scholars since 1881 cannot find the English references to the English verses which explain the text, since they have abandoned the English text as being "faulty."

[No. 9.]{.class_4095} Many correctors of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text have no insight whatsoever into the word, and this is manifest when dealing with the simplest problems in exposition such as a change in quotation between the Testaments.

[No. 10. ]{.class_4095}The Holy Spirit will often put light on the English text that is obscured in the Hebrew and Greek texts.

[No. 11. ]{.class_4095}Sometimes, ALL of the "oldest manuscripts" are in error and should be ignored.

[No. 12. ]{.class_4095}Even the oldest papyrus manuscripts are rejected by the [AV]{.class_4095}'s critics, on occasion, where they agree with the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text of 1611.

[No. 13. ]{.class_4095}Conservatives will abandon their profession of orthodoxy in a "pinch."

[No. 14. ]{.class_4095}A man who rejects the [known AV 1611 ]{.class_4095}for the [unknown ]{.class_4095}(and unobtainable) "originals" is often tongue‑tied when called upon to expound the truth of a verse that deals with Bible prophecy.

No. 15. Very often, the critics of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 will simply lie to their students about the meaning of a Greek word, being confident that their students will not check the word in a lexicon.

[No. 16. ]{.class_4095}Claim to know what the "original" says.

[No. 17. ]{.class_4095}Sometimes, the critics of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 will lie deliberately []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1194.xhtml_page_173}about their methods of translating or revising, and this can be proved in court with reproductions of their works.

Hereafter, we will not waste a great deal of time describing the genuflections and somersaults of the Bible's enemies ("Evangelical Conservatives" foremost!) as they seek to undermine the authority of the Holy Spirit. We shall simply note their exegetical gyrations with "No. 1," "No. 6," or "No. 15," etc.

All of them---from 1881 onward---are guilty of anywhere from one to seventeen of these violations. The excuses they use to cover up their hatred and infidelity are the same ones which were so aptly mouthed by the Revision Committee of 1881 (see Prefatory remarks).

The true Bible‑believing Christian may treat them (and their constituents) exactly as he would treat a rattlesnake or a rabid fox.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml}

# E[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_page_174}phesians {.heading_86}

## Introduction {#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ4075 .heading_1314}

One of the best ways by which the believer can identify apostates is by noting their remarks concerning the books of the Bible where these remarks touch "sound doctrine." For example, not one major commentator out of ten will mention the dangers connected with the book of Acts, the Epistle to the Hebrews, and the Gospel of Matthew when the student is attempting to define true Christian doctrines. The writers will waste page after page going into the historical backgrounds of the books and then waste reams of paper correcting the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text, but they will utterly fail to mention where and how the books have been used by "Christians" to create the contemporary religious scene in which the reader finds himself.

This is very significant. It means that the vast majority of commentators, revisers, and Bible teachers, since 1881, have lost contact with the age in which they live and do not even know how the Bible affects the community around them. We are not speaking here of "Liberals" or "Neo‑orthodox" preachers; we are speaking of premillennial "Conservatives" or amillennial "Evangelicals."

The book of Ephesians is an excellent example. In the introductions written for this book by Alfred Martin, Trench, Lipsius, Rendall, Dummelow, Williams, Bullinger, Clarke, Lange, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, W. G. Blaikie, and the "leading, well‑known scholars," one will find no mention of two outstanding doctrines which have affected the Body of Christ negatively for more than 200 years. The Greek scholars are not even able to theorize about these two doctrines, although both of them have been drawn from the Epistle to the Ephesians. And in vain will the seeker of truth find any information about the connection between Ephesians and these two doctrines from the "accredited scholarship" of any generation. These two doctrines were drawn from Ephesians 1 and Ephesians 3, and they are:

1\. Hyper‑Calvinism.

2\. Hyperdispensationalism.

Both heresies develop an impractical, detached, indifferent "head Christianity" which splits theological hairs and produces Christians who are about as "Pauline" as Calvin and Bullinger. People obsessed with the "Pauline Complex" (see note 96, chapter 6, [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence) ]{.class_4095}assume that John Calvin was a "Pauline type" Christian because of the spread of orthodox Christianity in Scotland and England through his teachings. People assume Bullinger was a "Pauline type" Christian because of his preoccupation with the Pauline Epistles especially the "Prison Epistles." However, ["by their fruits ye shall know them"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 7:20). One sermon by Jonathan Edwards (1703--1758) is no standard by which to judge, for Tennant and Whitefield (1714--1770) both preached a moderate Calvinism. Puritan New England never produced a Billy Sunday or a David Livingstone, and the notes in the old [Scofield Reference Bible---]{.class_4095}not the[ ]{.class_4095}"New" one---were written by MODERATE Dispensationalists. Hyper‑Calvinism and Hyper-dispensationalism never produced anything but a smug, self‑satisfied superiority that secretly patted itself on the back and said to the Body of Christ, "We have found the truth, and you still remain in error."

The Bible believer should be careful to note that to "find" this "truth," the Hypers had to change the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 in more than 5,000 places. Dabney, Strong, Kuyper, Hodge, Calvin, Gill, Berkhof, Stam, O'Hair, Bullinger, Baker, et al., could not find "the truth" in the Bible of the Reformation. They---as every Liberal preacher in the United States and Europe---had to alter some portion of the Holy Bible to find "the truth." Although their paces vary, they all got their feet shod in the same blacksmith's shop before they started down the track. They all began in Ephesians (cf. comments under 1:4--5, 11, 3:1--6).

Ephesus was originally a seaport. The Cayster River eventually blocked its channel to the ocean, and it became a third‑rate harbor. The city was quite cosmopolitan in the time of Paul; and it boasted of the largest theatre in Asia Minor, reputed to have a capacity for seating 50,000 people. In addition to this structure for[ "lovers of pleasure"]{.class_4096} (2 Tim. 3:4), Ephesus had a temple which was 425 feet long and 220 feet wide, supported by 127 columns nearly 60 feet high. It was made of marble (Pliny, [Natural History]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_page_176}, xvi., 40, 213) and was dedicated to Diana.

Paul's work there (before the writing of his epistle) is described in Acts 18--20. Eusebius states that the Apostle John stayed at Ephesus before his banishment to Patmos. When John writes to ["the church of Ephesus"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 2:1--6), he tells of a church that was hard working and faithful to the word and had failed only in keeping its ["first love"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 2:4).

Paul is in jail when he writes the epistle (Eph. 3:1, 4:1), and most of the historians agree that it was written during the first imprisonment of Acts 28. Its affinity with the Colossian epistle is apparent in every line (cf. Eph. 1:7 with Col. 1:14; Eph. 1:10 with Col. 1:20; Eph. 3:2 with Col. 1:25; Eph. 5:19 with Col. 3:16; Eph. 4:32 with Col. 3:13, etc.).

DeWette, Baur, Schwegler, and Davidson have questioned the authorship of the epistle; and Dummelow follows the party line in telling us that some time and space must be spent answering these "Christian" infidels because of their "competent" scholarship (Dummelow's Commentary, pg. 958). Readers more experienced in dealing with "competent scholarship" know that "competent scholarship" in the Biblical field very rarely means anything, and where correction of the word of God is concerned, it means nothing---[absolutely nothing.]{.class_4095}

We know Paul wrote it because he dictated most of his epistles (see Gal. 6:11 and comments), and in this epistle, he tells us that it is he---Paul---who is doing the talking (3:1 and 1:1). We do not know what kind of "scholarship" DeWette, Schwegler, Baur, and Davidson possessed, but certainly it was not related to Biblical or Christian scholarship. Many linguists and etymologists mistake formal [education for ]{.class_4095}spiritual insight (cf. comments under Eph 1:3, 4).

The best way to answer such "competent scholarship" is to point out that a "backlash" is no harder to untangle than a "bird's nest," although the latter is usually associated with an open‑faced reel. (You have to be "competent" to understand any of that!)

The Bible believer will be stultified to find that every introduction to the "Ephesians" he picks up has some nonsense in it about an "encyclical letter." He also will be surprised to notice that []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_page_177}Kenneth Wuest is in agreement with Origen and Westcott and Hort on the words ["at Ephesus" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 1:1), and so he puts them in brackets---as also Kurt Aland and Nestle. This time, [Good News ]{.class_4095}for [Modern Man ]{.class_4095}(1966) is more orthodox than Kenneth Wuest (Moody Bible Institute, Teacher Emeritus), for it, at least, says, ["who live in Ephesus."]{.class_4095} But Phillips (1958) lays all of Origen's cards on the table by telling us that the Epistle to the Ephesians was not written to the saints[ "at Ephesus" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 1:1) at all; rather, it was written for anyone in "other places where this letter is read." (This is the famous "encyclical" letter mentioned above.)

Now, where does all this stuff and nonsense come from? It comes from [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}, the two corrupt Alexandrian manuscripts which were idolized by Eusebius, Augustine, Casper Gregory, and Dr.\
A. T. Robertson. These two manuscripts omit the words, according to the dictates of Marcion the Heretic (A.D. 160) and the great dead‑orthodox apostate Origen (A.D. 184--254).

Why were the words omitted? To understand this, one must have an understanding of the mind so vividly described in Proverbs 18:1--2. This type of mind has an affinity for texts like John 21:25. The same type of mind would think that the "Dead Sea Scrolls" were a magnificent discovery. It is the type of mind that would add Apocryphal books to the Old Testament, and it is the same type of mind that would be tricked into thinking that there must be a "Book of Enoch" somewhere since "Jude mentioned it." ([P.S., Jude did NOT mention it.)]{.class_4095}

This peculiar type of mind is always looking around to find [The Book of Jasher ]{.class_4095}or [The Book of the Wars of the Lord, ]{.class_4095}etc. It is easily identified by one outstanding characteristic: [it rejects present, revealed truth and continues to search for something more authoritative to justify the rejection. ]{.class_4095}Some of its outstanding representatives are Dr. A. T. Robertson, Kenneth Wuest, R. B. Thieme, Alford, Thayer, Milligan, Souter, Deismann, the Popes, Augustine, Lipsius, Trench, Cardinal Spellman, Ramsay, Garstang, Thulock, Delitzsch, Keil, the Rosicrucians, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, W. F. Albright, the spiritists, Westcott, Hort, Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, the Satanists, agnostics, Calvin, atheists, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_page_178}Dummelow, F. F. Bruce, Clarke, Lange, Custer, Berkhof, Machen, Neal, Warfield, Griesbach, Gregory, Lachmann, drug abusers, Tischendorf, Nestle, nuclear physicists, [and the last 20,000 authors you just read.]{.class_4095}

To understand the omission of ["at Ephesus"]{.class_4096} from [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}(and P46), you must understand the double depravity that attends the intellectual mind (Prov. 18:1--2). The words ["at Ephesus"]{.class_4096} were taken out of the Holy Bible because of the phrase ["the epistle from Laodicea" ]{.class_4096}found in Colossians 4:16.

Origen reasoned that if the Bible mentioned any book, that book had to be available. For after all, did not he, Origen, have access to the entire Bible? It would be unthinkable that he, Origen, would be ignorant of so important a document as ["the epistle from Laodicea"]{.class_4096}! Why, how would he answer his pupils at Caesarea when they asked him about ["the epistle from Laodicea"]{.class_4096}? Who ever heard of a real scholar saying "I don't know"? Quick! Find the "Epistle of the Laodiceans"! Find the "Book of Enoch"! Invent one if you can't find one! (See comments on "anthropology" in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}chapter one.)

The similarity between Ephesians and Colossians is striking, as we have said before; therefore, the most natural thing to do would be to declare that the "Epistle to the Ephesians" is ["the epistle from Laodicea." ]{.class_4096}But how can one do this if the "inspired originals" say ["the saints which are at Ephesus"? ]{.class_4096}Easily. Take out ["at Ephesus,"]{.class_4096} and you have a letter addressed to the Laodiceans, or at least an "encyclical letter" which can be read anywhere. This supplies the missing "epistle" which God nearly left out of the Book due to an oversight which, fortunately, was amended by Origen.

This is what is behind all of the tomfoolery that you will read in Rev. Salmond's Introduction [(Expositor's Greek Testament) ]{.class_4095}and the lengthy notations in the [Pulpit Commentary (Vol. ]{.class_4095}20, Intro., iv--v). Rev. Salmond has the gall to tell us that the testimony of Marcion the Heretic (A.D. 160) and the two corrupt Alexandrian manuscripts puts the "strength of the case" on the side of omitting ["at Ephesus." ]{.class_4096}How this "strength" overrides the testimony of Chrysostom [(who was not a "heretic") ]{.class_4095}and the Alexandrian, Syrian, Caesarean, and Western families of manuscripts, plus the majority of manuscripts, is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_page_179}a little vague.

[​]{.class_4102}It is true that Schmidt, Weiss, Abbott, Reuss, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Bengel, Hort, Neander, Harless, Olshausen, and Woldemar accepted the corrupted text as it appears in [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}; but the "strength of the case" clearly lies in the arguments presented by Dean Burgon (1813--1888) in his thorough analysis (pp. 169, 187, [The Last Twelve Verses Of Mark, ]{.class_4095}1959 edition). This analysis goes far beyond the superficial theorizing of Rev. Salmond, who cites only the works of Tertullian, Origen, and Basil (pp. 229--230, [Expositor's Greek Testament). ]{.class_4095}Among other pieces of damaging evidence which the Holy Spirit has mustered against the "searchers for the truth" (Prov. 17:24) is the heading to the Ephesian epistle. In both [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus, ]{.class_4095}the heading says: ["TO EPHESIANS"! ]{.class_4095}Origen forgot to erase the heading!

You see, the Lord's omissions are as inspired as His revelations. The boyhood and early manhood of Jesus Christ are as great a matter of interest to the Holy Spirit as the accounts of the Crucifixion and Resurrection. The same Holy Spirit who told you to trust in what He said, thought it proper not to reveal certain things to you, and these "omissions" are as INSPIRED as the revelations. But these "omissions" are what fascinate the Christ‑rejecting, Bible‑hating, authority-despising, intellectual egotist. He is obsessed with them. Nay, they mean more to him than all the revealed truth in the world, for if he could only find them (Prov. 17:24), then he would be superior to his stupid brethren who do not have access to the "hidden wisdom"! The Gnostics did not die out with Valentinus (A.D. 160) and Philo (20 B.C.--A.D. 50).

["The epistle from Laodicea" ]{.class_4096}may or may not be in the Bible, and if it is, you wouldn't know anything more than if it wasn't. The [Book of Jasher ]{.class_4095}may or may not be in the Bible, and if it was, you couldn't prove it. If it wasn't, it would have no business being there. But this is not the "intellectual" approach. This type of faith is called a "closed mind." The "open mind" rejects revealed truth (which is apparent by the fact that every man subscribing to the "encyclical theory" rejected the [AV ]{.class_4095}as it stands) and occupies itself with the "search for truth" which will allow it to reject revealed truth even further.

For example, the attempts to "restore the originals" are all made by men who deeply and bitterly resent the power of the [King James 1611 Version. ]{.class_4095}Those who are "seeking the truth of the originals" [have already rejected the truth of the revealed and preserved text. ]{.class_4095}They are wasting your time and my time and God's time, and you are a blank fool if you give them any consideration.

The keynote of the book of Ephesians is ["spiritual blessings"]{.class_4096} and ["heavenly places."]{.class_4096} Such words as ["riches," "fulness," "riches of his glory," "exceeding riches of his grace," "abounded," ]{.class_4096}etc., show that Paul is in fine humor when he writes. He is in somewhat the condition of the martyr in the Leonine dungeons, who opened his letters with: ["From the delectable orchards of the Leonine gardens!"]{.class_4095}

Since the book deals with spiritual realities and "heavenly places," it is a perfect pitfall for the Christian who desires to become "so heavenly minded he is no earthly good." The Hyperdispensationalist grabs Ephesians 3 and spends the rest of his life emphasizing a truth that was revealed more than nineteen centuries ago, and the Hyper‑Calvinist grabs Ephesians 1 and spends the rest of his life trying to play "God" and think like God thought before Genesis 1 (which is extremely difficult to do!). Those experienced in "hand to hand" combat with the enemy (Eph. 6:10--17) know that neither Hyper‑Dispensationalists nor Hyper-Calvinists have much to say about Ephesians 5 and 6. Ephesians 5 and 6 are [practical.]{.class_4095}

Ephesians was written between A.D. 55--58 (McGiffert), 60 or 61 (Meyer), 62 (Zahn), 61--63 (Lightfoot), 75--80 (Ewald), 80 (Scholten), 100 (Holtzmann), or 130--140 (Baur, Davidson). You may waste considerable time (and eyesight) trying to figure the date out, but again, the internal evidence of the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text far outweighs the guesses of its critics. Paul is in jail; he is writing after Acts 20; and the tie‑in with Romans 16:25 is so apparent that no one would imagine the epistle was written in Caesarea, but in Rome itself.

Bullinger has a field day in Ephesians. Since the epistle will NOT teach his dispensational system, he alters the word ["prophets" ]{.class_4096}to "prophetical writings." [(Companion Bible,]{.class_4095} pp. 1759, 1764). This must be done since the cliché of Bullinger and Stam was: "Israel is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1307.xhtml_page_181}the subject of PROPHECY; the church is the subject of REVELATION." Since ["the whole family in heaven and earth" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 3:15) messes up some theories about the "body mystery," Bullinger has to devote half a page (p. 1771) to altering the [King James ]{.class_4095}text so it will reinforce his private interpretation. Since Romans 16:25--26 prove that Paul already knew of the "Body mystery" during the Acts period, Bullinger hastens to tell us that Romans 16:25--26 was added to the book of Romans at the time Paul wrote Ephesians!

Proof? ["The original manuscripts prove this\..." (Companion Bible,]{.class_4095} pg. 1694). (You see, they'll do it every time! \[See [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970.\].) Every writer who uses the expression "the original manuscripts" knows perfectly well that he is LYING when he says it. But they will go right on with it, year in and year out, in every "Fundamental" seminary in the United States and Europe just like they thought they were fooling somebody.

Bullinger insists that Ephesians 3:3 is a reference to the postscript added to Romans 16:25--26, and yet the thing is incredible, for the Roman epistle was in circulation all over Europe three years before Ephesians was written. To get out of this obvious muddle, Bullinger tells us ([Companion Bible,]{.class_4095} pg. 1694) that Paul knew about the "Body mystery" before he wrote Ephesians (i.e., before Acts 28) but couldn't write about it. However, this only muddles things worse, for the ["one body"]{.class_4096} and the ["one baptism"]{.class_4096} are found in [the First Epistle to the Corinthians]{.class_4095} (1[ ]{.class_4095}Cor. 12:12--13) written near the time of Acts 19. Furthermore, Bullinger later tells us that the[ "one body"]{.class_4096} of the "Body mystery" did not begin until Acts 28! As we have said before, Stam dates the Body at Acts 9, while rejecting water baptism which continues through Acts 18. Baker begins the Body at Acts 13, while rejecting water baptism, which continues through Acts 18. O'Hair began the Body at Acts 18 so he could get rid of water baptism. Bullinger has the Body beginning at Acts 28 and being revealed before Acts 20, but it was not described in writing until after Acts 28, although it IS DESCRIBED in 1 Corinthians and Romans, written BEFORE Acts 28. (Somebody doesn't like the Baptists!)

If one did not know the "brethren" better, one would think they resented the ministries of J. Frank Norris, George Truett, Billy Graham, Charles Haddon Spurgeon, Lee Scarborough, W. B. Riley, Haidemann, Beauchamp Vick, John Rawlings, Harold Henniger, Art Wilson, Lester Roloff, T. T. Martin, W. A. Criswell, M. R. DeHaan, Charlie Fuller, B. H. Carrol, Jack Hyles, Lee Roberson, B. B. Crimm, B. R. Lakin, Jim Mercer, Fred Brown, John R. Rice, Jesse Hendley, E. J. Daniels, Hyman Appleman, and Mordecai Ham. Why the panic over a water baptism that follows conversion? Paul didn't panic.

The book of Ephesians has 6 chapters, 155 verses, and 3,022 words.

In its present form, the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text of 1611 is perfectly clear and needs no alterations of any kind. The dead‑orthodox apostates of 1881--1981 find fault with it on an average of five times per verse, which makes more than 700 suggested changes. All of these may be dismissed without any polite consideration, and the "competent" scholarship behind them may be ignored without any bad after‑effects whatsoever. We will point out the errors of the [AV ]{.class_4095}critics as they occur, and we will refer the reader to the appropriate number on the "Check List" (see note at the end of Galatians) where he may locate the particular phenomenon being demonstrated.

Where an "accredited" scholar is so egotistical that he feels he is competent enough to correct the Holy Bible, we shall naturally feel competent enough to correct him.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml}

## Chapter 1 {.heading_116}

1:1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, to the saints which are at Ephesus, and to the faithful in Christ Jesus:

2 Grace be to you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in Christ:

4 According as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and without blame before him in love:

5 Having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,

6 To the praise of the glory of his grace, wherein he hath made us accepted in the beloved.

["Paul, an APOSTLE of Jesus Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1) has been altered to ["Paul, an AMBASSADOR of Jesus Christ"]{.class_4095} by Kenneth Wuest (New Testament, 1961). The translation, of course, is a fatuity. Wuest translates the same word as ["apostle"]{.class_4095} in 2 Corinthians 12:12 (Phenomenon No. 4). The [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}(with Phillips, the [RV, RSV, ASV]{.class_4095}, and ["Good]{.class_4095} [News") ]{.class_4095}reads ["Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4095}instead of ["Jesus Christ," ]{.class_4096}for the inversion is found in Origen's ["Vaticanus"]{.class_4095} and the corrupt Western "[D]{.class_4095}" manuscript.

["By the will of God ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1) indicates the directive will of God (Acts 9:15) and not the permissive will, as in James 4:15.

["To the saints WHICH ARE AT EPHESUS"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). The words ["at Ephesus"]{.class_4096} (Gk., ["en Epheso"]{.class_4095})[ ]{.class_4095}have been taken out by Marcion and Origen and left out in [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}.

["And to the faithful in Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1). Phillips omits the last three words this time, but most of the new "Bibles" ([Good News, NEB, ASV, RSV, RV, ]{.class_4095}and Kenneth Wuest) read as the [King James]{.class_4095}---["Christ Jesus."]{.class_4096}

The ["saints"]{.class_4096} are the[ "faithful"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_184} mentioned in the verse, and the construction of the sentence is a Hebrew parallelism similar to that of Matthew 21:5, which see.

["Grace\...peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2). The word ["grace"]{.class_4096} occurs twelve times in the book of Ephesians and here refers to grace dispensed from day to day (Heb. 4:16). It is not a "state of grace" ([Confraternity ]{.class_4095}New Testament, 1941, pg. 525, footnote) out of which the believer may fall, but grace given from day to day to match the trials of the believer (2 Cor. 12:7--10). God the Father gives the grace (1 Cor. 1:4, 3:10), and the peace is Christ's (John 14:27). Those of us who have "grace and peace" have God for our Father (1 Cor. 8:6) and Jesus Christ for our Lord (Eph. 4:5).

["Blessed be the God and Father\...who hath blessed us with all SPIRITUAL BLESSINGS in HEAVENLY PLACES in Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3). Seven of these blessings are named (1:4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 13, 14), and the expression ["heavenly places"]{.class_4096} is mentioned four times in the epistle (1:3, 20, 2:6, 3:10; five if you count the "original Greek" for ["high places"]{.class_4096} in 6:12). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have an excellent comment on the text (Commentary, pg. 1279), and it is a pity that they had to waste any paper with serious discussions of the[ ]{.class_4098}theories of the 1881 Revision Committee. Their comments here (Eph. 1:3) are a fine piece of exposition on the English text. With the exception of the "citizenship" (substituted for ["conversation" ]{.class_4096}in Phil. 3:20), the material is excellent.

The meaning is clear. We are seated "upstairs" at this moment (Eph. 2:6). Being "in Christ," we are present before the throne (1 John 4:17), and as He could be in two places at the same time (John 3:13), so we now occupy two localities (1 Cor. 6:17; Eph. 2:6, 5:30).

The ["heavenly places" ]{.class_4096}are real places and are certainly not the "heavenlies," although this will be the gist of the new translations. To segregate real science (the statements of the [King James Authorized Version) ]{.class_4095}from Twentieth-Century science (1 Tim. 6:20), the postmillennial and amillennial "Christians" have sought to convince the believer that the literal, physical Heavens are not a real locality in the divine plan. The believer, rather, is to be content with "spiritual blessing in the heavenly realm" [(NEB), ]{.class_4095}or "every spiritual gift in the heavenly WORLD" (!) [(Good News), ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_185}or "every SPIRITUAL blessing in the heavenly realm" [(Amplified New Testament). ]{.class_4095}Dr. Alfred Martin speaks on behalf of the mutilators who took out part of John 3:13 by telling us that we are NOT in heaven and that the words ["heavenly places" ]{.class_4096}are "misleading" and refer only to our "association" in Christ!

These "Christians" who are [only on earth ]{.class_4095}must be a peculiar breed. We saved Christians are joined to a Body whose Head is in Heaven. We share this heavenly position as part of the Body. We are ["one Spirit"]{.class_4096} with a Spirit who fills the universe, and we are only ["absent from the Lord"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 5: 1--10) in regard to our physical body. The statement of Ephesians 1:3 was plainly ["spiritual blessings." ]{.class_4096}Nothing physical was mentioned in regard to ["blessings."]{.class_4096} Our [physical blessing ]{.class_4095}is future (1 Cor. 15:49--57), but we have already "gone home to glory" in the Spirit. The insertion of ["places" ]{.class_4096}by the [King James ]{.class_4095}translators shows that they had a grasp of New Testament truth denied to Dr. Martin (Professor of Old Testament Synthesis at Moody Bible Institute) in the Twentieth Century.

Kenneth Wuest's translation of the verse is "out of this world," even if it didn't get all the way to ["the third heaven"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 12:2). We should "eulogize" the one who conferred "benefactions" on us, according to Brother Wuest. How this is an improvement over the text of 1611 is a little hard to see. However, those who take their Bible studies seriously have learned to take Wuest with a ton of salt.

1\. On pages 292--293 of Wuest's New Testament, one will find the verse numbers given in consecutive order as though all the verses were there. However, Wuest, as the Roman Catholic [New Jerusalem Bible, ]{.class_4095}has omitted a whole verse [without notifying the reader. ]{.class_4095}Acts 8:37 is missing in both translations, while the correct number system was kept to lead the reader to think he had a whole New Testament (Phenomenon No. 16).

2\. In Matthew 16:18, Wuest has invented a private interpretation which is so far astray from any Greek text that a real Greek scholar could not even recognize the translation. The nearest thing to it is the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946). (Phenomenon No. 15).

3\. The Deity of Christ is attacked in 1 Timothy 3:16 by erasing the word ["God"]{.class_4096} (exactly as it was erased in the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_186}[New English Bible, Good News, Phillips, ]{.class_4095}the [RV, ASV, RSV, ]{.class_4095}and the Roman Catholic [Confraternity New Testament ]{.class_4095}of 1941). Who would guess that Moody Bible Institute approved of Arian translations that were in line with Rome? (Phenomenon No. 7).

["According as he hath CHOSEN US IN HIM before the foundation of the world\..." ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). At this point, John Calvin and Berkhof went to pieces, and Arthur W. Pink and L. R. Shelton have been trying to pick up the pieces ever since.

Now, we needn't waste a lot of time trying to explain the text. It is apparent from the remarks made by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (Commentary, pg. 1280). The forcing of the word [foreknowledge]{.class_4095} to mean ["foreORDAINED"]{.class_4095} (Kenneth Wuest, pp. 366, 549---Phenomena No. 5 and No. 15) shows that someone is trying to get you ["in Christ" ]{.class_4096}before you or Christ show up! Rev. Salmond's exposition of the passage is an improvement over those given by Bengel and Calvin ([Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 248); but we still see---lurking behind every word---the thought that perhaps the believer was ["in Christ" ]{.class_4096}before he was ["in Adam"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 15:22).

Instead of wasting a lot of paper and ink with obtuse analysis, let us state the facts squarely.

1\. No man is ["IN CHRIST"]{.class_4096} until he is born again.

2\. Every man was ["IN ADAM"]{.class_4096} when Adam died.

3\. The "elect" were not[ "IN CHRIST"]{.class_4096}; they were ["aliens," ]{.class_4096}["alone in the world,"]{.class_4095}[ "children of wrath," "dead in trespasses and sins," "having no hope," "without God,"]{.class_4096} and ["unknown"]{.class_4095} (Gal. 4:7--9; Eph. 2:1, 3, 12).

4\. The Greek word for ["chosen"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 1:4) is found in Acts 13:17; Mark 13:20; John 15:19; Romans 9:11, 11:5; 1 Corinthians 1:27, etc.; and there is not one case where it occurs that it ever refers to anything DECREED ["before the foundation of the world."]{.class_4096} Neither Jacob nor Israel were ["chosen\...before the foundation of the world." ]{.class_4096}Neither was Paul, nor was Abraham.

5\. The believer was chosen ["FROM the beginning," ]{.class_4096}according to 2 Thessalonians 2:13. How is he then elected ["BEFORE the beginning"?]{.class_4095}

6\. If election is eternal, how were you SANCTIFIED in [eternity ]{.class_4095}(1 []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_187}Pet. 1:2) when Ephesians 2:12--13 clearly states that you were anything but sanctified?!

7\. In Matthew 22:14, the CALL precedes the "election."

But we are only irritating the Calvinists. Why not finish the matter?

If you were ["in him]{.class_4096} \[Christ\][ before the foundation of the world,"]{.class_4096} you plainly fell "out of Christ" in Genesis 2 and got "into Adam"; whereupon, you fell "out of Adam" at your conversion and got back ["IN CHRIST." ]{.class_4096}[Why not fall again? ]{.class_4095}(Such momentum deserves repetition!)

Observe how the plain English clears up the obscure Greek used by Kenneth Wuest (Moody Bible Institute). (Even [Good News for Modern Man ]{.class_4095}caught the sense of the verse better than Wuest or Dr. Martin.)

1\. The English that clears up the Greek is found in 2 Timothy 1:9 (Phenomenon No. 8). There, we are told that ["before the world began" ]{.class_4096}(as Eph. 1:4), God gave us certain things; but these things were only ["given us IN CHRIST JESUS" ]{.class_4096}(see text), and, therefore, to get them, you had to get ["in"]{.class_4096} Him!

2\. Observe the close similarity between the wording of 2 Timothy 1:9--10 and Ephesians. 1:4--5.

The meaning, then, is clear. ["Before the foundation of the world,"]{.class_4096} God decreed that He would choose no one in this age to become ["holy and without blame"]{.class_4096} (you[ ]{.class_4096}see, the commentators forgot the purpose of the election, as usual!!) unless they were ["in Christ." ]{.class_4096}Jesus Christ was to be the medium through which all blessings would be dispensed (vs. 10), and we can only be ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). You were not ["in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}before Genesis 1:1, nor was anyone else. You were not ["in Christ" ]{.class_4096}before Genesis 1:1, nor was anyone else. Christ had no "Body" until Acts 2 into which you could enter (John 17:23); therefore, the entire system of Calvinism---where it deals with election---is a nullity.

Predestination (vs. 5) always follows FOREKNOWLEDGE (Rom. 8:29), and election always follows FOREKNOWLEDGE (1 Pet. 1:2). For this reason---the truth---Wuest, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, and Rev. Salmond have invented a new word for "foreknowledge." []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_188}This word is "efficient decree" or "efficient foreordination." No Greek text speaking of "foreknowledge" has any such word in it (Phenomenon No. 15). The private interpretations of these men are attempts to read Calvin into the words of the Holy Spirit, and the Holy Spirit will have nothing to do with it.

["Having predestinated us unto the adoption\...according to the good pleasure of his will" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5). Dr. Martin gives us Calvin's position admirably (Wyc[liffe Commentary, ]{.class_4095}pg. 1303); i.e., God chooses some to be saved and doesn't choose others.

This is the root of Calvin's system; and by coupling the verse with Romans 9:16, 18, the great superstructure of Limited Atonement, Irresistible Grace, Unconditional Election, and Total Depravity was erected. The system contains the most remarkable theological contradictions known to man, and the whole Aristotelian philosophy behind the theology is a distortion of reasoning processes. The real Calvinist reasons as follows:

a\. I cannot understand what God did and why He did it because He did it arbitrarily "according to the pleasure of His will" (Eph. 1:5).

b\. Since God does these things without my consent, He makes the decisions for me (Rom. 9:16); therefore, I have no will of my own before I am saved.

c\. Therefore, God elects a certain number, regardless of their will in the matter, and atones for this number and this number only, since His will would be frustrated if He atoned for any more than He is going to save.

d\. It would be teaching salvation by works to assume that God had any basis for choosing the elect; therefore, I leave it to Him and simply shrug my shoulders and say: "God had His own purpose which no one can know. See how humble I am?"

But all of this breaks down when dissected.

If you can't understand God's mind or His purposes before Genesis 1:1, what are you doing telling us that He "decreed" certain things before Genesis 1:1? All of God's decrees are eternal according to Berkhof. How did Berkhof find that out (see Rom. 11:34--35)?

You have already assumed that God would be frustrated if someone rejected His atonement, and you have assumed that the atonement was limited when the Scriptures state that it was NOT []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_189}(Heb. 10:29; 2 Pet. 2:1). You seem to profess to know a great deal about God's "purposes" while rejecting His clear INTENTIONS (2 Pet. 3:9)! As a matter of fact, all Hyper‑Calvinists profess to know exactly why the elect get saved and the reprobate don't; but none of them connect it with the[ "will,"]{.class_4096} although God places the whole emphasis on the ["will" ]{.class_4096}(Isa. 14:13--14; John 5:40; Matt. 23:37; Rev. 22:17; Acts 7:51) when it comes to accepting or rejecting salvation.

The truth is that God KNOWS (Rom. 8:29; 1 Pet. 1:2) who will accept and who will reject. Those who will accept are "elected" and "predestinated" to be ["conformed to the image of his Son"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:29) and ["unto the adoption of children" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 1:5).

Now, we need to eliminate the nonessentials as quickly as possible, for Calvin's system is an intricate mass of complexities designed to wear out the reader until all he can do is throw up his hands and say, "tis the will of Allah! Kismet!" The root trouble is a [denial of free will ]{.class_4095}where it is asked to accept Jesus Christ (John 1:11--13). To put it clearer, Calvin denied the truth that an unsaved sinner could accept or reject Christ of his own free will. If the sinner was "elected," he was overpowered and quickened by the Holy Spirit without the consent of his will (Berkhof, [Systematic Theology,]{.class_4095} pg. 483), and if he was not one of the elect, he was dead anyway and could not will to accept Christ if he had decided to! Calvin's "corpse" is held responsible for [rejecting ]{.class_4095}Christ, but is not responsible for [accepting ]{.class_4095}Him!

Hyper‑Calvinists deeply resent this "oversimplification" of their position and insist that such a description is "misrepresentation," "slander," etc.; but the briefest study of any Systematic Theology by any Reformed theologian will show that this is the position exactly. (Counter positions are presented, but this only makes a contradictory system. No system in the world has more contradictions in it than the one presented by Berkhof, Dabney, Mauro, Gill, Lowth, Patrick, Hodge, Calvin, Shelton, and A. W. Pink.)

The Bible position is as follows:

1\. God knew who WOULD and who WOULD NOT accept His Son [before they did.]{.class_4095}

2\. On the basis of this FOREKNOWLEDGE, God predestinated those who WOULD to be[ "conformed to the image of his Son"]{.class_4096} at []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_190}a future date.

3\. No human merit was involved, and Calvin's insistence that [a willingness to accept Christ ]{.class_4095}is "human merit" or "good works" is the height of absurdity.

The pseudo‑Calvinists (e.g., Kenneth Wuest), [who believe in free will, ]{.class_4095}have to erase the word [FOREKNOWLEDGE,]{.class_4095} while the real Calvinists have to erase free will if they retain the word [FOREKNOWLEDGE]{.class_4095}. You say, "Is it that simple?" That's all there is to it.

Since the [results ]{.class_4095}of receiving Christ are good, Calvin insisted that no man could receive Christ of his own free will, for this would be "good works." To uphold the doctrines of "Total Depravity" and the deadness of the sinner, Calvin eradicated man's will, never noticing that the WILL was not a subject of depravity. The fact that a man can accept or reject anything (including the convicting work of the Holy Spirit---Acts 7:51) shows "depravity" extends only to man's mental, emotional, and spiritual makeup. The "will" is a [function of the mind; ]{.class_4095}it is not a thought, feeling, or inspiration that passes through the mind. God foreknew who WOULD and who WOULD NOT accept His Son, but that in no way affects their responsibility. You either have or you haven't.

["To the praise of the glory of his grace, wherein he hath made us accepted in THE BELOVED" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). The expression ["the praise of the glory of his grace" ]{.class_4096}is found here to mark God the Father. ["The praise of his glory"]{.class_4096} is used in verse 12 to mark God the Son, and again in verse 14 to mark God the Holy Spirit.

It was God's grace that enabled any man to find Christ (Eph. 2:8--9), and it is therefore God's grace that leads any man to accept Him. He (Christ) is the ["beloved"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 3:17, 12:18), and we are NOT ["accepted"]{.class_4096} until we are ["IN HIM"]{.class_4096} (see comments under vs. 4). Since this interpretation is arrived at by a Scripture‑with-Scripture comparison, and since it clearly indicates the proper method of interpreting verse 4, we should not be surprised to find every reviser since 1881 pouncing on the text like a ravenous wolf---in sheep's clothing!

1\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}[(Good News, ]{.class_4095}1966).

2\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_191}[(Amplified New Testament, ]{.class_4095}1958).

3\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}(Phillips, 1958).

4\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}[(NEB, ]{.class_4095}1961).

5\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}[(RV, ]{.class_4095}1881).

6\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}[(ASV, ]{.class_4095}1901, and [New ASV, ]{.class_4095}1963).

7\. You are NOT ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}[(RSV, ]{.class_4095}1946).

8\. You are NOT ["accepted in me beloved"]{.class_4096} (Kenneth Wuest, 1961, Teacher Emeritus, Moody Bible Institute, Chicago, Illinois).

If the new "Bibles" are true revisions of the Holy Bible, how does one account for the fact that you ARE ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}in the following:

1\. Tyndale (1525).

2\. The [Great Bible]{.class_4095} (1539).

3\. The [Geneva Bible ]{.class_4095}(1560).

4\. The [Bishops' Bible ]{.class_4095}(1568).

?????????????????????????????

What line of Bibles are the "new" translators following? [Why, that's easy. Their readings are found in the Jesuit publication of 1582 (Rheims Version)!]{.class_4095}

Wuest, Thieme, Rendall, Clarke, Lange, Blaikie, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and the faculty at Bob Jones University simply traded your Reformation Bible in for the Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}edition of 1941, which has the same reading!

The new "Bibles" are NOT revisions of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 or "the Greek." [They are revisions of Jerome's Latin Vulgate. ]{.class_4095}Whenever you find these Protestant "Esaus" selling your birthright for a mess (not "mass") of pottage, you know where to look---Alexandria.

The lengthy analysis by Rev. Salmond ([Expositor's Greek Testament, ]{.class_4095}pg. 253) tells us what we knew before we picked up his book. The new readings are based on the corrupt text of [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}; the men responsible for substituting this text for the true text of the Holy Bible were Westcott and Hort, Lightfoot, Ellicott, and Tischendorf. This is the "competent" scholarship one hears so much about. The scholars who translated the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 [had this text before them ]{.class_4095}exactly as it appeared in [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_192}, and not a man on the committee was stupid enough to believe that this text was the word of God. Phenomenon No. 17.

The scholars lied to you when they told you the text of the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) was based on manuscripts unknown to the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 translators. [The ASV manuscripts were in print in 1582 in the official Jesuit Bible of the Roman Catholic Church!]{.class_4095}

1:7 In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, according to the riches of his grace;

8 Wherein he hath abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence;

9 Having made known unto us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he hath purposed in himself:

10 That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one all things in Christ, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth; even in him:

11 In whom also we have obtained an inheritance, being predestinated according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will:

12 That we should be to the praise of his glory, who first trusted in Christ.

13 In whom ye also trusted, after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise,

14 Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, unto the praise of his glory.

Now that the reader has been thoroughly alerted to the dangers of "competent scholars" by the "bestknown scholars" restoring "the original text," we shall spend less time criticizing them and more time in the actual exposition of the text. (It will suffice to say that every Bible revision from 1880--1980 is a Roman Catholic production.)

["In whom we have redemption THROUGH HIS BLOOD\..." ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_193}(vs. 7). Notice how this fits the preceding verse exactly, if the preceding verse is left as it stands in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text. We could not have been ["accepted" ]{.class_4096}without forgiveness and redemption. In the Old Testament, they were "forgiven" (Exod. 34:7) but not "redeemed" (Heb. 9:15) in the final sense (Rom. 3:24--25). Our redemption is ["through his blood," ]{.class_4096}as we find it in Colossians 1:14. (Salmond, Meyer, Lightfoot, Lipsius, Trench, Alford, Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Berkeley, Goodspeed, [Good News, RV, RSV, ASV, New ASV, NEB, ]{.class_4095}Clarke, Lange, Ellicott, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, and Satan all omit ["through his blood" ]{.class_4096}in Col. 1:14, which see.)

At this juncture (1:7), [Good News for Modern Man ]{.class_4095}suddenly gets skittish and decides that the word ["blood" ]{.class_4096}is archaic. The apostates who translated this ["Good News"]{.class_4095} have taken "the blood" out of the verse and replaced it with "the death" of Christ. There are no Greek manuscripts that read in this fashion. But Greek manuscripts have never been any problem with any reviser since 1881. ["Good News" ]{.class_4095}has also taken the blood of Christ out of Acts 20:28; Romans 5:9; and Ephesians 2:13.

When people are short of blood, I believe they are called "anemic."

That describes the new "Bibles" exactly.

["Having made known unto us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). Then it not only pleased God (vs. 5) to predestinate us, it also pleased God to show us WHY He predestinated us (vs. 9). Calvin won't stand for that, yet there it is! We are predestinated because [we have been redeemed and forgiven ]{.class_4095}(vs. 7), and this made us ["accepted in the beloved" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). Peter did not know this in Acts 3:19, for he speaks of a future "blotting out of sins"; whereas Colossians speaks of a [past ]{.class_4095}["blotting out"]{.class_4096} (Col. 2:14).

The mystery of God's will is explained in what follows. ["That in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one ALL THINGS IN CHRIST, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth; even IN HIM" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10; cf. 2:7). ["\...ALL THINGS in Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10) is a general designation and matches the use of the word ["all"]{.class_4096} in Mark 1:5, 1 Corinthians []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_194}9:22, and 1 Corinthians 15:27--28, especially.

The ["things"]{.class_4096} that are gathered together are gathered together ["in Christ" ]{.class_4096}(note the emphasis---["EVEN IN HIM"]{.class_4096}). Unsaved people, demons, devils, and fallen angels are not[ "in Christ,"]{.class_4096} nor will they ever be. ["All things" ]{.class_4096}is a reference to all of God's dealings, doings, designs, and dispensations. He has determined that the Lord Jesus Christ will be the capstone of these plans. When Eternity begins (Rev. 21--22), the Lamb will be the center of attraction (Rev. 21:23); those saved from the Old Testament, the Church Age, the Tribulation, and the Millennium will gather around Him (cf. remarks on 3:15).

["The dispensation of the fulness of times" ]{.class_4096}lies yet in the future (Acts 3:21), and Christ's First Advent was to begin a series of ages which will terminate in Revelation 21 (cf. comments under 3:9). Two heresies are derived from the passage.

1\. [Postmillennialism: ]{.class_4095}i.e., our time is gradually stretching out till it automatically becomes ["the fulness of times,"]{.class_4096} and then everyone will be converted to Christ (see [Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_4095}Vol. 20, pg. 4 on "Ephesians").

2\. [Universal salvation ]{.class_4095}(Origen, Bullinger): i.e., ["all things"]{.class_4096} refers to sinners on earth, demons, devils, fallen angels, and people in Hell.

However, the subject is ["things"]{.class_4096} ([AV ]{.class_4095}1611), not "people"; and one of those things is listed immediately---["an inheritance."]{.class_4096}

["In whom also we have OBTAINED AN INHERITANCE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11; cf. vs. 14). A Postmillennialist or Universalist will lose his chance to make the ["things" ]{.class_4096}of verse 10 "people," if the[ "things"]{.class_4096} are truly [things]{.class_4095}. Hence, nearly every "Bible" on the market since 1881 alters ["obtained an inheritance" ]{.class_4096}to "were made a heritage" or "were made an inheritance." To justify this, the Postmillennialists quote Deuteronomy 4:20. But this is an obvious[ faux pas. ]{.class_4095}The Holy Spirit, in the English text (Ruth 4:6), is careful to tell us that an inheritance is [marred if it is abandoned. ]{.class_4095}God has never abandoned Israel (Rom. 11), and they are still His inheritance (Jer. 10:16; Matt. 21:38).

This reading of the verb in the passive voice was first done by the Jesuit priests in 1582, and Westcott and Hort reproduced this []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_195}Catholic translation in 1881 where it was passed on diligently by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (1910), the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946), the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), Kenneth Wuest (1961), Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer (BJU), arnd other Jesuit sympathizers. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}and Phillips are closer to the meaning of the text than ["Good News." ]{.class_4095}(The [Amplified Version ]{.class_4095}contains [both readings ]{.class_4095}so that graduates from Bob Jones University and Tennessee Temple will be able to play ball on [both teams ]{.class_4095}in the ecumenical council!)

We are a ["purchased possession"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14), not an ["inheritance,"]{.class_4096} and we get an inheritance because we were "adopted" (vs. 5). Any Greek text that fails to notice these two salient truths and speak accordingly must give way before the English (Phenomenon No. 8).

["Being predestinated according to the purpose" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). Calvinists mistranslate the verse to read: "you were predestinated to be saved according to the arbitrary purpose of God when He willed for you to get saved 'after the counsel of His Will'---not yours."

The careful reader will observe that the word "predestinate" was attached to the following:

1\. Being like Christ [in the future ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 8:29).

2\. Being adopted as children [upon our acceptance of Christ ]{.class_4095}(Eph. 1:4--9).

["That we\...who first trusted in Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12). Paul includes himself in a group that trusted Christ before the Ephesians trusted in Christ. This, of course, knocks Bullinger's system "galley west," for it means that Paul was not in the Body of Christ unless the Body started BEFORE Acts 18--19! This makes Paul a member of two bodies if the Body of Christ did not begin until AFTER Acts [28 (Companion Bible,]{.class_4095} pg. 1761). The ["we"]{.class_4096} of the verse is plainly the ["we"]{.class_4096} of 2:10, 14, 16, and 18. Observe how the ["we"]{.class_4096} of verse 11 includes Jew and Gentile, the ["we"]{.class_4096} of verse [12 ]{.class_4095}is the Jewish believer, and then both are included in the ["our"]{.class_4096} of verse 14.

["In whom YE ALSO TRUSTED, after that YE HEARD THE WORD OF TRUTH, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye BELIEVED, ye were SEALED WITH THAT HOLY SPIRIT of promise"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13). A sinner hears the word (John 5:24); he believes the truth of the Gospel (Gal. 1:8--9); []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_196}this causes him to trust Jesus Christ as his Saviour (Rom. 10:13); and upon this act of his WILL, God circumcises the sinner (Col. 2:11--12) and seals him just like you would seal up a bottle of preserves. He is preserved ["until the REDEMPTION of the purchased possession"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14), and ["sealed unto the day of REDEMPTION"]{.class_4096} (4:30).

All is clear. The believer's body is ["bought with a price"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 6:20), and his physical body becomes God's possession (1 Cor. 6:19). ["The purchased possession"]{.class_4096} will be redeemed at the Rapture (1 Cor. 15:50--56),[ ]{.class_4095}although the believer's soul was redeemed (Eph. 1:7) the moment he trusted Christ.

Dr. Alfred Martin steps into the room a moment to demonstrate for us Phenomenon No. 16. Dr. Martin tells us that in the "original," the adjective (Greek, ["hagio"]{.class_4095})[ ]{.class_4095}is "emphatic." (Having never seen "the original," why do you suppose they go right on with it?)

Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown now enter with a correction of the [AV ]{.class_4095}text which professes to reproduce "the Greek." But in the Greek reproduction (Commentary, pg. 1281), they did not translate the ARTICLE before ["promise"]{.class_4096}! (Or for that matter, the one before ["grace"]{.class_4096} in Eph 2:8; see Phenomenon No. 17. Williams tells us the [AV ]{.class_4095}should read as the [RV ]{.class_4095}in verse 13, which means, of course, that it should NOT.)

["Which is the earnest of OUR INHERITANCE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). The Holy Spirit was promised (see comments under Gal. 3:14), and when He comes, He bears witness (Rom. 8:16) of our adoption. Being adopted, we have the full assurance that when Christ appears (1 John 3:1--3), we shall receive the full inheritance to which a real Son is entitled (Gal. 4:1--6 and comments). Part of this inheritance is a sinless, 33-year‑old, incorruptible, male body (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew, ]{.class_4095}Matt. 22:30).

We are to read "unto" instead of ["until" ]{.class_4096}according to Dr. Blaikie ([Pulpit Commentary), ]{.class_4095}and the good Doctor has called our attention to the fact that the ["until" ]{.class_4096}of the [King James ]{.class_4095}is "not textual." However, it is "clearer"; and since Dr. Blaikie would even add Greek words to the text [that are not there ]{.class_4095}to make it "clearer" (see his note on vs. 13: ["este"), ]{.class_4095}we can be assured that he is charitable enough to allow ["until" ]{.class_4096}for "unto" here and in Philippians 1:10. (Again, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_197}Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have some good comments to make on verses 13--14, and they give some excellent cross‑references. It is a shame that any of them had to follow the blind leaders of 1881 to any distance in writing their commentary. If you omitted the "rathers" in Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, you would have a fair commentary.)

["Until the redemption of the purchased possession" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). Observe that we are NOT the "Lord's inheritance" (see comments under vs. 11), in spite of the fact that the vast majority of scholars, commentators, and revisers insist that we are. Instead, we are a ["purchased possession" ]{.class_4096}which awaits physical redemption. The reader should observe the mess that Bullinger gets into on verse 18 when he attempts to line up verse 18 with the standard "accredited interpretation" of verse 11.

1:15 Wherefore I also, after I heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus, and love unto all the saints,

16 Cease not to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers;

17 That the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him:

18 The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of his calling, and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,

19 And what is the exceeding greatness of his power to us‑ward who believe, according to the working of his mighty power,

20 Which he wrought in Christ, when he raised him from the dead, and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places,

21 Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world, but also in that which is to come:

22 And hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be the head over all things to the church,

23 Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_198}all.

(We shall ignore the mass of changes suggested by Alford, Ellicott, Meyer, DeWette, Davidson, Salmond, Riddle, Luken, Schneiderman, Abbott, Bullinger, Robinson, Bengel, and Wordsworth \[the [Cross Reference Bible, ]{.class_4095}pg. 2210)\], as we have learned on at least 200 other occasions that these men simply do not know what they are talking about nearly 40 percent of the time.)

Verse 15 is self‑explanatory and matches 1 Thessalonians 1:3--10.

Verse 16 is self‑explanatory and matches 1 Thessalonians 1:2 and Colossians 1:9.

Now follows Paul's intercessory prayer for the saints at Ephesus (cf. also 3:16--21 for a similar prayer).

1\. That you might have ["the spirit of wisdom and revelation" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17).

2\. That ["the eyes of your understanding" ]{.class_4096}should be[ "enlightened"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18).

3\. That you might ["know what is the hope of his calling"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18).

4\. That you might know ["the riches of the glory of his inheritance" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18).

5\. That you might know ["the exceeding greatness of his power" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19).

The rest of the passage describes the One whose power is available to the Christian, the One who will supply him with the petitions listed above. All is as clear as the Coral Sea on a calm day in July, and nothing could be more ridiculous than the attempts of the new "versions" to "clarify" the passage.

To begin with, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}(and similar dead‑orthodox, apostate corruptions) omit the ["the"]{.class_4096} before[ "spirit" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17), insisting that since "the article is not in the Greek texts"---Phenomenon No. 17---it should be omitted. Those of us who have dealt with the exegetical crooks in the "Scholar's Union" know exactly how to evaluate a statement like this. It is about as significant as saying, "In Marcion's library is a book describing how the seventh aeon of the fifth order of higher spirits became a Universal Monad." Every translation on the market inserts ["the"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_199} in the same verse before ["knowledge," ]{.class_4096}and it is not in any Greek text extant (cf. 2:10 for an example of omitting "the" when it is there).

You see, when you deal with a "corrector" of the [King James 1611 Holy Bible, ]{.class_4095}you must assume, to start with, that he is a LIAR, and if you don't, you will do him an injustice. No translator translated the ["the"]{.class_4096} before "heart" (Gk., ["kardias"]{.class_4095} in Nestle's) in the next verse. You see, "articles" are added or subtracted at the caprice and whim of the reviser, while he complains about the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators doing the same thing! (Hypocrite!)

At ["understanding" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18), the [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RV, ]{.class_4095}and [RSV, ]{.class_4095}in the interests of "making the Bible clearer" and "getting rid of the archaic Elizabethan English," suddenly translate the word ["kardias" ]{.class_4095}literally and give us "the eyes of your hearts." [What does this mean? ]{.class_4095}Aren't we getting "true to the original Greek"?! (By the way, the [TR]{.class_4095} "original Greek" says "[dianoias"]{.class_4095}---"[mind]{.class_4095}"---not "heart" as in Nestle's. That is why the [KJV]{.class_4095} said ["understanding."]{.class_4096}) But isn't it a little late to start translating LITERALLY (to change the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text) when the [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RV, RSV, New Jerusalem Bible, ]{.class_4095}etc., refused to translate ["orthotomounta" ]{.class_4095}(Gk., 2 Tim. 2:15) as ["RIGHTLY DIVIDING"' ]{.class_4096}and went, instead, back to the Jesuit Bible of 1582 to give us ["handling"]{.class_4095}? ["Orthotomeo" ]{.class_4095}means, primarily, ["to CUT straight." What ]{.class_4095}happened to our "literal" translators? \[Phenomenon No. 4.\] Any alibi to change God's word!

["The eyes of your understanding" ]{.class_4096}are plainly the ["eyes"]{.class_4096} of 1 Samuel 14:27 and Luke 24:31. The Christian should desire ["the spirit of wisdom and revelation" ]{.class_4096}in the knowledge of God. This knowledge (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}Prov. 1:2--7, 1972) should give the Christian an intimate knowledge of his Saviour as a friend and companion as well as a Lord and Saviour. As a matter of fact, this basic definitive ["knowledge"]{.class_4096} of God is another one of the missing elements in modern "Fundamentalism," for the great passages that deal with God's [personality and ways of doing things ]{.class_4095}are not found in the New Testament; [they are found in the Old Testament. ]{.class_4095}You see, the One who is unchanging "yesterday, today, and forever" is a real Person. He has a [personality, ]{.class_4095}with ways of thinking, ways of reacting to situations, ways of handling problems, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_200}ways of discussing things, habits of planning, habits of response, and habitual expressions of a sense of humor. [Ignorance of God ]{.class_4095}is one of the outstanding marks of the revisers and commentators since 1800, and it is manifest in every line of their works.

As a matter of fact, the basic theory behind all revision since 1800 shows that none of the revisers knew anything at all about God's nature---[practically]{.class_4095}. No man who had ["knowledge"]{.class_4096} of God---in the sense that Paul is praying about---would even THINK that ["Vaticanus"]{.class_4095} and ["Sinaiticus"]{.class_4095} were reliable manuscripts. The matter would not even be open for discussion. Manuscripts containing spurious books as part of the canon (secreted in the confines of the private domicile of the Roman hierarchy), which matched the style of Marcion and Origen, could not be taken seriously by a Bible believer who knew ANYTHING about God's nature, character, personality, or methods of dealing with men and nations. Many times, even "godly men" of outstanding character and pious deportment are unable to "get to know God" in any other way than through their personal DEVOTIONS, but this is not[ "the knowledge"]{.class_4096} of which Paul is speaking. Paul is speaking about ["knowledge OF HIM."]{.class_4096} The briefest study of Isaiah 40--53 and Job 38--42 will yield more ["KNOWLEDGE OF HIM" ]{.class_4096}than four years of Seminary and 400 hours of "private devotions." The God of Isaiah and Job is the God who was manifested in the flesh (1 Tim. 3:16, [AV ]{.class_4095}1611), and a theology that limits "present truth" for the "church of the one Body" (Bullinger) to the Pauline Epistles is a God‑dishonoring theology.

["That ye may know what is the HOPE OF HIS CALLING\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). The Hope of our calling is Christ Himself (Heb. 6:18), for He is the One who will meet us in the air (Tit. 2:13) and fulfill the[ "hope" ]{.class_4096}of Romans 8:20--24 (Cf. 4:4; Col. 1:5, 23, 27 and comments).

["The riches of the GLORY OF HIS INHERITANCE in the saints" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18). Now Williams and Bullinger---both Hyperdispensationalists---blithely step off "the deep end" and sink out of sight in "the unsearchable depths of the spiritual heavenlies." They tell us that the "church" is the inheritance of God the Father! (Williams Commentary, pg. 920). Bullinger has to run to the corrupt [Septuagint ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_201}to prove that the church "will be" His inheritance "in heaven" [(Companion Bible, ]{.class_4095}pg. 1761).

But alas, verses 11, 14, and 18 are a reference to the same thing in the same context and have nothing to do with God inheriting anything. First Peter 1:4 states the matter clearly; but since Williams, Baker, Stam, Bullinger, and O'Hair thought 1 Peter was a "Jewish epistle" and had no application to the Body, the reference is completely overlooked. God the Father HAS an inheritance (Ruth 4:1--6; Psa. 28:9, 33:12, 68:9, 78:62, 78:71, 94:14, 106:5, 40; Isa 19:25; 47:6, 63:17; Jer 10:16, 16:18, 51:19), and to overlook this central Bible truth, which is mentioned more times than the word ["dispensation"]{.class_4096} or the word ["mystery," ]{.class_4096}is an unforgivable oversight on the part of Bullinger and Williams, which clearly shows their bent to private interpretation. Hyperdispensationalists, then, are not to be trusted in their handling of Ephesians. This is quite apparent by the matter mentioned above.

["His mighty power, Which he wrought in Christ, WHEN HE RAISED HIM FROM THE DEAD\...Far above all principality, and power, and might, and dominion\...And hath put ALL THINGS UNDER HIS FEET\...head over ALL THINGS to the church, WHICH IS HIS BODY" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19--23). The power which raised Christ from the dead resides in the physical body of the believer (Rom. 8:10--11). The passage is dealing with [travel in outer space ]{.class_4095}(cf. comments on 3:18). The power that raised Jesus Christ from the dead, raised Him clean out of this world's atmosphere, ionosphere, and exosphere; on out through the solar system and the "S"‑shaped nebula; out through the galaxies and nebulae and star clusters; and on up past Alpha Draconis through the "empty space over the north" (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}Gen. 1--2).

The comments by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown on the passage are excellent. With the exception of one or two minor corrections that they attempted to make on the Holy Bible, the comments are as fine a piece of work as you will find anywhere (cf. comments under Phil. 2:9 and Col. 1:16).

The power that put the Lord Jesus in His present GEOGRAPHICAL LOCATION (vs. 20; Col. 3:1) resides on this earth in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_202}"earthen vessels" (1 Cor. 3:16). The inference is clear. If Christ did it, [you can do it! ]{.class_4095}And there is no way on earth to avoid this conclusion. The prayer is that the child of God will see this truth, grasp it, and lay hold of it in such a way as to revolutionize his character and conduct (cf. 3:18, 20 and comments). Every Christian is an [astronaut ]{.class_4095}who has been commissioned and empowered to travel at a speed of 100,000 light years per second. He will leave the feeble efforts of Cape Canaveral so far behind that if they reached Venus next year, the feat would be of no significance whatsoever. No Bible believer has to bow and kiss the "feat" of science, [even where science is right.]{.class_4095}

The passage is not merely dealing with the regal headship and lordship of Jesus Christ in [the realm of the Spirit; ]{.class_4095}it is dealing with ATOMIC POWER in the realm of SPACE EXPLORATION (cf. John 19:9). Where it occurs again in Ephesians 3:18, every commentator from Origen to E. Schuyler English missed the reference. [Any kind of "science" ]{.class_4095}will destroy belief in the [AV ]{.class_4095}text if it is used as a basis for truth instead of a [cross-reference.]{.class_4095}

When the [Amplified New Testament ]{.class_4095}hits the passage, the translators "freak out." Since these translators followed the allegorizing tendencies of Alexandria (see John 17:17; 1 Thess. 2:13), the term ["heavenly places" ]{.class_4096}upsets their boat every time it comes down the channel. In 1:3, it is "the heavenly REALM"; in 1:20, it is "the heavenly PLACES"; and in 2:6, it is "the heavenly SPHERE." (Having a little trouble there, Doctor?) But these grammatical inconsistencies are always to be expected from Postmillennial expositors who are trying to teach their theology via "translations." You will observe in the Preface to the [Amplified Version ]{.class_4095}that Daniel 12:4 has been altered to read as [a positive statement ]{.class_4095}by inserting ["of the Lord"! ]{.class_4095}Daniel 12:4 is the corollary to 2 Timothy 3:7, and it is about as "positive" as [From Here to Eternity ]{.class_4095}by James Jones.

Now, Jesus Christ is ["above all."]{.class_4096} The word ["above,"]{.class_4096} here, is used in the sense of geographical location (cf. Ezek. 28:14) as well as spiritual Headship. He is "over the universe" [geographically ]{.class_4095}and [directionally, ]{.class_4095}and He is "over everything in the universe" in relation to [power ]{.class_4095}and [authority. ]{.class_4095}Demons, devils, angels, cherubim, seraphim, and archangels are subject to Him (Col. 1:16--19). Thrones, dominions, principalities, powers, nations (Matt. 28:19--[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_203}20), men, beasts (Isa. 11:1--12), plants, oceans, mountains (Psa. 148), stars, galaxies, planets, atoms, protons, neutrons, and electrons (Heb. 1:2--3) exist and subsist by His directive or permissive will; and He has been exalted to a place above every name (Phil. 2:9) in the Church Age, the Tribulation, the Millennium, or Eternity. ["All hail the power of Jesus' name, let angels prostrate fall!" ]{.class_4095}Only His ["word" ]{.class_4096}(Psa. 138:2) was ever exalted above His ["name,"]{.class_4096} and that is why His ["word"]{.class_4096} is the perennial and eternal target of self‑inflated egotists who hide behind the name of "Conservative," "Evangelical," or "Fundamentalist."

You see, if you can correct His ["word"]{.class_4096} (Psa. 138:2), you have indirectly placed YOUR AUTHORITY above the supreme Authority of Heaven and earth (Eph. 1:21)! Therefore, there is no essential difference at all in the manner of approach (or the motive behind the approach) of the Roman Catholic Popes to the authority of the word of God and the manner of approach used by Dr. A. T. Robertson, Kenneth Wuest, R. B. Thieme, Warfield, Machen, and any 300 "leading scholars" who ever lived since 1880.

["All things" ]{.class_4096}are ["under his feet"]{.class_4096} (in the geographical sense), and the earth is merely His ["footstool"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 5:35). All of His enemies have not yet been conquered (1 Cor. 15:26), and all of them have not yet "kissed His feet" (Psa. 2:12; 110:1--6); but [they will ]{.class_4095}(cf. comments on Phil. 2:9).

He is the "Head" of the church and the only "Head" the church has ever had or ever will have. There is no "visible head" to this Body, for you enter this Body by the Spirit (Eph. 4:4) and are baptized into it by the Spirit (1 Cor. 12:13). Since ["God is a Spirit" ]{.class_4096}(John 4:24), the Head must be a [spiritual ]{.class_4095}Head. Our "Head" is in Heaven, and we can no more drown in perdition (1 Tim. 6:9) after becoming a part of Christ than a man can drown standing in five feet of water with his head sticking out of it from his shoulders up. (Those dead-orthodox apostates who were "severed" from Christ \[see comments under Gal. 5:4\] have a problem, but we saved people are breathing the air of[ "the third heaven"]{.class_4096} \[ 2 Cor. 12:2; Eph. 2:6\]!)

["To the church, WHICH IS HIS BODY" ]{.class_4096}(vss. 22--23). Notice the definition. ["The church"]{.class_4096} is not a political hierarchy, a reform society, a social club, a fraternity, a house of merchandise, an []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1350.xhtml_page_204}"involved commitment," a place of amusement, or "a spokesman for the underprivileged." ["The church"]{.class_4096} is[ "HIS BODY."]{.class_4096} Christ's earthly body was a body of humility and meekness. He sought the lost, believed the word, never condoned formalism or ritualism, referred to Canaanites as ["dogs"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 15:22--27!), blasted the ecumenical council of His day, and wore no crown but a crown of thorns. [IS THAT A DESCRIPTION OF YOUR CHURCH?]{.class_4095}

Observe the peculiar twist given to the verse when it is quoted by a Hyperdispensationalist. (Careful students of truth may have noticed a remarkable phenomenon which accompanies any distortion of truth: i.e., ["the HOLY ghost" ]{.class_4095}for "the Holy Ghost" \[a "Holiness" pronunciation\] and ["BupTIZE" ]{.class_4095}for "BAPtize" \[a Campbellite pronunciation\]. Instead of saying ["the CHURCH, which is his body," ]{.class_4096}the Hyperdispensationalists pronounce it as ["thechurchoftheONE-BODY." ]{.class_4095}Notice that this slaughter of the King's English is done to produce the impression that there is in the New Testament a church which is of "another body." Since this is Bullinger's doctrinal conviction, the phrase is always read in that fashion.

["The fulness of him that filleth all in all" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 23). Kenneth Wuest copies the Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}(1941)---["all with all"]{.class_4095}---and Alford, Bengel, Ellicott, Meyer, Wordsworth, and Salmond recommend this Roman Catholic reading. The [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RV, ]{.class_4095}and [RSV ]{.class_4095}retain the reading of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, which, of course, is correct.

The spiritual Body of Jesus Christ is the Church; His physical body is seated at ["the right hand of the Majesty on high"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 1:3, 12:2; Acts 2:34). His spiritual Body is His ["fulness"]{.class_4096} that ["filleth all in all" ]{.class_4096}(cf. comments under 3:18). The One who fills the universe (Heb. 1:10--12) lives in His Body, which is the Church; and He is ["the fulness of the Godhead bodily"]{.class_4096} (cf. comments under Col. 2:9).

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1507.xhtml}

## Chapter 2 {.heading_116}

2:1 And you hath he quickened, who were dead in trespasses and sins;

2 Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that now worketh in the children of disobedience:

3 Among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts of our flesh, fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind; and were by nature the children of wrath, even as others.

4 But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us,

5 Even when we were dead in sins, both quickened us together with Christ, (by grace ye are saved;)

6 And both raised us up together, and made us sit together in heavenly places in Christ Jesus:

7 That in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through Christ Jesus.

The words ["hath he quickened"]{.class_4096} have been supplied by the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 translators properly in line with verse 5. Even the [RV, ASV, ]{.class_4095}and [RSV ]{.class_4095}see the need for the addition, although the [RSV ]{.class_4095}is not as honest as the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, for the [RSV ]{.class_4095}makes the addition in regular print, [not in italics. ]{.class_4095}Kenneth Wuest does the same thing.

["Were dead IN trespasses and sins"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1) is wrongly translated as "with reference to" (Kenneth Wuest). In line with this attempt to overthrow the evangelical doctrines of the New Testament are the [Amplified Version ]{.class_4095}and [Good News for Modern Man. ]{.class_4095}They give us as variants ["BY your trespasses and sins" ]{.class_4095}and ["BECAUSE of your disobedience." ]{.class_4095}But all three of these private interpretations are attempts to dodge Genesis 3. You were IN Adam. You were ["IN your sins"]{.class_4096} (John 8:24). You were DEAD in them (vs. 5). They were a part of you, and you were a part of them. You were []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1507.xhtml_page_206}stuck to them as man and wife stick in a fleshly joining (Rom. 7:1--2), and the only way you got "loosed from them" (Col. 2:9--12) and got out of them into something else (Rom. 7:3--4) was by the New Birth. If any "translation" alters ["IN"]{.class_4096} to "because," "for," "with," "by," "at," "in reference to," or "in relation to," you can scratch that translation. Until you are ["quickened" ]{.class_4096}by the Spirit (vs. 5), you are IN your sins and trespasses---[geographically ]{.class_4095}and [spiritually ]{.class_4095}(cf. comments under Gal. 5:16).

The "deadness" of the unconverted sinner led Berkhof, Calvin, Hodge, Dabney, A. W. Pink, L. R. Shelton, Gill, and others to assume that he cannot act responsibly. That is, he cannot receive Christ (see 1:4) because he is DEAD. But this assumption is irrational in the extreme. If he cannot receive, he cannot reject. (You never saw a dead man reject anything!)
