All of them suddenly forgot their "Greek," their "original manuscripts," their "scientific researches," their "recent discoveries," their "improved translations," and their "clearer renderings." They just put the pedal on the floor and followed the car ahead of them (Phenomenon No. 9).

The prayer which Paul prayed was never answered---at least where it touched the lives of 30,000 Bible commentators and critics. He was praying that they would ["COMPREHEND" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18), but none of them even tried to work on the problem, let alone ["comprehend"]{.class_4096} it.

[The "genitive" for verse 18 will be found in the immediate context. ]{.class_4095}It is given in verse 15. It is mentioned [again ]{.class_4095}in verse 10. It is mentioned [again ]{.class_4095}in 1:20--21, and [again ]{.class_4095}in 2:6, and [again ]{.class_4095}in 1:3, and [again ]{.class_4095}in 1:10! Now tell me how a man with a doctorate in Greek or Semitic languages could have missed that? He could not have missed it if he had TRIED.

The only way a man could have missed the missing "genitive" would have been by approaching the word of God with a haughty, disdainful attitude [and the intent of a man desiring to replace its authority with his own. This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.]{.class_4095}

[​]{.class_4102}The ["breadth, and length, and depth, and height"]{.class_4096} are the same[ "height"]{.class_4096} and ["depth,"]{.class_4096} mentioned in Romans 8:39. Paul lived all his life on this planet with a cosmic orientation that located positions in the universe. We have neither time nor paper to give the "lay out," but the Lord answered Paul's prayer for some of us. We do ["COMPREHEND" ]{.class_4096}the dimensions, and we know the directions and distances within the dimensions (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}Gen. 1--2).

We are strangers and sojourners on foreign soil. We are walking on a peanut of a planet which is on the back end of an S‑shaped nebula where our solar system makes up one of several hundred. This nebula is only one in several thousands or millions. Directionally, it is SOUTH of the glorified body of Jesus Christ, who is seated at the right hand of a throne which is [north ]{.class_4095}of this nebula. At any time in Paul's life, he could point to the place where he was "caught up" (2 Cor. 12:1--4). He was so aware of the size, shape, location, and atmosphere of that place, that he said he would rather die immediately and go there than go on living (Phil. 1:21, 23; 2 Cor. 5:8).

This realization makes a different kind of a Christian from those who translated the Bible since 1880 (and those who have sought to destroy it since it was written). This type of Christian is described in Colossians 3:1--4; and even though he is called a "crackpot" by "the majority of scholars," he knows where his body is in the universe, where his Head is in the universe, the approximate distances between them, the Body that fills this immense abyss (called ["the deep"]{.class_4096} in Gen. 1:2), and his oneness with this Body as a member of it (Eph. 5:30). [The EXACT measurements for the length, breadth, depth, and height of this abyss are to be found in Exodus 22--40! ]{.class_4095}But who is sufficient for these things? Certainly not Origen, Philo, Eusebius, Jerome, Augustine, Pamphilus, Valentinus, Symmachus, Theodotian, Aquila, Marcion, Calvin, Luther, Wesley, Aquinas, Abelard, Duns Scotus, Paley, Lyell, Ptolemy, Copernicus, Galileo, Kepler, Trench, Alford, Thayer, Robertson, Davis, Milligan, William Culbertson, Moulton, Rendall, Gregory, Einstein, Von Braun, Westcott, Hort, Clarke, Lange, Meyer, Lightfoot, Scofield, English, Gaebelein, John Walvoord, McClain, Wilbur Smith, Clarence Mason, Pember, Larkin, or the faculty at Wheaton or Bob Jones University!

Petition No. 5: That you might ["know the love of Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19).

6\. And that you ["might be filled with ALL THE FULNESS OF GOD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19).

Most of the petitions are self‑explanatory, and only the fourth petition remains inscrutable through the centuries. Petition No. 2 refers to the believer's personal relationship to Christ as someone []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1639.xhtml_page_240}who has moved in to share a home with him. The "indwelling" is not [conditioned ]{.class_4095}on faith or being in "a state of salvation" (see 2:5), but refers to Someone who is already dwelling and has come to stay (John 14:16). A life of faith will not grieve the [Dweller ]{.class_4095}(Eph. 4:30; Gal. 2:20). As a wounded soldier once said to a surgeon who was trying to extract a bullet from his ribs after the battle of Waterloo: "Dig a little deeper, and you will find the Emperor."

In connection with verses 18 and 19, the student should read 1 Corinthians 2:16; Proverbs 25:3; Colossians 2:9; and Ephesians 4:9, 13.

The reading of the [NEB, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, "Good News," ]{.class_4095}and the [Amplified Version ]{.class_4095}on verse 18 may be rejected without consideration. All three of these apostate versions insert, as part of the Holy Bible, the private interpretation we have mentioned. This private interpretation, stretching across nineteen centuries, is no more "Scripture" than Tobit, Judith, or Bel and the Dragon.

Petition No. 3 is the subject of 1 Corinthians 13.

Petition No. 5 is a petition ["to know the love of Christ"]{.class_4096} which is beyond human comprehension, and the thought is quite similar to the "peace which passeth understanding" in Philippians 4:7. That is, it is BETTER than understanding. The love of Christ manifested to the sinner is better than knowing reams about "original manuscripts" and "articles" and "tenses" in the "original."

Petition No. 6 matches 5:18 and Galatians 5:16, 4:19, which see.

According to Colossians 2:9, the ["fulness of the Godhead bodily" ]{.class_4096}is available to the Christian. This accounts for the remarkable promise which follows:\
["him that is able to do EXCEEDING ABUNDANTLY above all that we ask or think, according to the power THAT WORKETH IN US" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20; cf. Rom. 15:18).

Verse 21 is self‑explanatory. The ["world without end" ]{.class_4096}is the new earth of Revelation 21:1--2, and it will go on out into the "age of the ages" forever. Christ is working IN the believer, He is working FOR the believer, and He is working NOW ["according to the power that worketh in us." ]{.class_4096}This is the power mentioned in 1:19, and again, the context is traveling in outer space! He went up through the literal depths to the literal height, and you will too if you are a bornagain child in the[ "family"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1639.xhtml_page_241}!

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml}

## Chapter 4 {.heading_116}

4:1 I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called,

2 With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love;

3 Endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.

4 There is one body, and one Spirit, even as ye are called in one hope of your calling;

5 One Lord, one faith, one baptism,

6 One God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all.

Having made a first-rate mess of the ["mystery,"]{.class_4096} Bullinger is now in a position to get rid of water baptism once and for all. The private interpretation placed on the passage before us is so obvious that one would be surprised that "Bereans" could find enough support to start a Bible school.

["I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1; cf. 3:1 and comments). ["Walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). The Christian's career or "job" is obeying Christ. His work by which he makes a living on this earth is really only his "hobby." All other occupations are "avocations," and the Christian's serious life work has nothing to do with astronomy, physics, agriculture, commerce, electronics, the building industries, trades, art, music, literature, science, education, politics, business, economics, civil service, or the military. These are "incidentals" in the life of the real child of God, and his ["calling"]{.class_4096} is a high, heavenly, and ["holy calling"]{.class_4096} (2 Tim. 1:9) for a prize of the highest calling possible (see Phil. 3:14 and comments).

["With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2). Every descriptive word in the passage is talking about the Christian's dealings with his fellow Christian. It will take ["lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering"]{.class_4096} to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_243}["keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3 cf. Col. 3:12; Gal. 6:1), for God's people are hard to get along with and rarely overlook faults and sins in their own kind which unsaved people would overlook altogether, or even praise as virtue! If Bob Jones Sr., J. Frank Norris, Dallas Billington, Jack Hyles, Jack Wyrtzen, Billy Graham, Theodore Epp, M. R. DeHaan, Carl McIntire, John R. Rice, B. B. Crimm, Billy James Hargis, Walter Wilson, Billy Sunday, and Hyman Appelman all had to share the same room, there would be blood running out under the door in less than 48 hours! People with convictions about the Bible and the ministry have convictions about [their clothes ]{.class_4095}and [their food]{.class_4095}. If you don't believe it, see Lester Roloff!

["THE UNITY OF THE SPIRIT in the bond of peace"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). The Holy Spirit has saved the members of the church (Eph 1:4--14) and has placed them in the Body as it pleased God (Eph. 2:20--22) and has equipped each of them for the right function (1 Cor. 12:1--13). This unity must be kept in daily living as the Christians deal with each other. ["The bond of peace"]{.class_4096} will prevent contentions from breaking out (Rom. 14). The binding power of peace is present where there is unity-preventing eruptions like Paul himself was prone to (See Acts 15:39)! The believer is asked to KEEP what God has already set up.

Plainly, then, the context of the passage is UNITY. But at this point, Stam, Bullinger, Baker, O'Hair, and some of the "Darbyites" suddenly decide that the context is a NEW REVELATION that supersedes an old one, and they then DISUNITE the members of the one Body!

Every Hyperdispensationalist in the world rejects water baptism on the grounds of verse 5. The reasoning is as follows:

1\. Since the verse says ["ONE baptism,"]{.class_4096} there cannot be TWO. (Error: there are SEVEN! See Matt. 3:11; Acts 2:38; Matt. 28:19; 1 Cor. 10:1--2; Matt. 20:22--29.)

2\. Since there is only one baptism that saves (1 Cor. 12:13), no Christian should be baptized in water for the ["ONE BAPTISM" ]{.class_4096}is Spirit baptism. (Error: Peter, James, John, and Paul were all baptized by the Spirit; and they were all baptized in water.)

3\. The correct meaning of verse 5 is: "I [used to think ]{.class_4095}that there []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_244}was more than one baptism---back in my ignorance when I got saved---but now that I have had the advanced revelation of the "Body mystery," I realize there is only ONE." (Error: this is the wildest private interpretation imaginable, for look what it does to the other two items in the same verse! "I used to think there was more than one Lord, but NOW I see there is only ONE"; "I used to think there was more than one faith when I got saved, but since my advanced revelation, I realize there is only ONE"?)

You see, the context of Ephesians 4 has nothing to do with [kinds ]{.class_4095}of Lords, Gods, and baptisms; it has to do with the [number ]{.class_4095}of Gods, Lords, and baptisms. There are many "faiths," but there is only ONE real one. There are many lords and gods (2 Cor. 4:4; Psa. 82:6; 1 Cor. 8:5), but there is only ONE real one. There are SEVEN baptisms, but there is only one [real ]{.class_4095}one.

The result of throwing out water baptism on the grounds that Ephesians 4:5 is an "advanced revelation" that supersedes all others completely combobbulates (that's a good one!) the rest of the Scripture.

The phenomenon of [Acts 2:2--4 ]{.class_4095}was a baptism by the Spirit (Acts 1:5).

The phenomenon of [Acts 10:44--47 ]{.class_4095}was a baptism by the Spirit (Acts 11:16--17).

[The Corinthians]{.class_4095} were baptized by the Spirit (1 Cor 12:13)!

[The Ephesians,]{.class_4095} who were saved before Acts 20, were baptized by the Spirit (Eph. 4:5).

[Paul includes himself]{.class_4095} with the Corinthians as baptized by the Spirit (1 Cor. 12:13), [and they were saved in Acts 18.]{.class_4095}

It is of this baptism that Paul says ["one baptism."]{.class_4096}

There are not two SPIRIT baptisms; there is ONE. Therefore, to use the verse as an alibi to divide the Body of Christ and overthrow the ["unity of the Spirit"]{.class_4096}---look at the context!---by saying that it negates the water baptism of Peter, James, John, Paul, the Corinthians, and the Ephesians is to violate the commandment of the context---["ENDEAVOURING TO KEEP THE UNITY OF THE SPIRIT IN THE BOND OF PEACE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3).

Is Paul trying to get the Ephesians of Acts 19 to fight the Corinthians of Acts 18 because the latter had been baptized in water? []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_245}[Did you ever meet a Stamite or a Bullingerite whose entire ministry wasn't doing just that!?]{.class_4095}

No, it won't work. Baptism is never said to be a "sign"; and Bullinger, Baker, and Stam all had to change the word ["washings"]{.class_4096} in Hebrews 9:10 to fit their theological formats. To say that water baptism disappeared with tongues (on the basis of 1 Cor. 14:22) is to say something that neither Paul nor God ever suggested. ["Tongues are for a SIGN"]{.class_4096}; water baptism is a "FIGURE." Next step: [Stam and Bullinger must get rid of 1 Peter 3:21!]{.class_4095} But those of us who have served our time with the forward units and the regimental combat teams know a gold-bricker when we see one. This spiraling, circular, snake-like slithering from verse to verse in order to escape the truth is not the least bit characteristic of a [Bible-believing Christian]{.class_4095}. In "action," we have found that the movement is the standard drill for the Campbellites ("Church of Christ"), the Russellites ("Jehovah's Witnesses"), and the Whiteites ("Seventh-day Adventists"). It is a characteristic of all people who are trying to make the Scriptures teach what they do not SAY.

["There is ONE BODY"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4). There never have been two. There never has been one body in the book of Acts and another [outside ]{.class_4095}it. Paul is in the Body, and he is in the book of Acts. Peter, James, and John are IN Christ and IN God and are INDWELT by the Holy Ghost (John 17:21--26). There is ONE BODY; the same Spirit who baptized Peter, James, and John into it at Pentecost, baptized the Corinthians into it in 1 Corinthians 12:13, and the whole congregation was baptized in water to show their Spirit baptism (cf. Acts 10:44--48).

["ONE SPIRIT"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4). Not a Holy Spirit who baptizes people and a Holy Ghost with which the Son baptized people. (Oh, yes, we've heard all the tricks!)

["Even as ye are called in ONE HOPE of your calling"]{.class_4096} (vs 4; cf. comments under 1:18).

["ONE LORD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). Obviously the Lord Jesus Christ.

["ONE FAITH"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). Obviously that of 1 Timothy 4:1 and 2 Timothy 4:7.

["ONE BAPTISM"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). Obviously that of 1 Corinthians 12:13, although Blaikie tells us ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20, pg. 147) that []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_246}this baptism is the "initiatory rite" whereby we enter the visible church! He believes the word ["one"]{.class_4096} means that the one act of sprinkling the baby does the job!

["ONE GOD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). Obviously the one of 3:14.

["Who is above all]{.class_4096} \[as in 1:20\][,]{.class_4096} [and through all]{.class_4096} \[as in 1:23\][,]{.class_4096} [and in you all]{.class_4096} \[as in 3:19\][."]{.class_4096}

All of the apostles, including Paul, Barnabas, Silas, Sosthenes, Timothy, Apollos, Junia, and Andronicus (see Williams' Commentary, pg. 925), were immersed in water AFTER their spiritual baptism. [Segregating them from the "twelve" ]{.class_4095}proves nothing at all. The "twelve" were baptized in water, and the "eight" were baptized in water. If you have not been baptized in water, it is because you belong to a "Body mystery" that is so great a mystery that Peter, James, John, Paul, Silas, Barnabas, Sosthenes, Timothy, Apollos, Junia, and Andronicus all missed it.

When Stam and Bullinger get through with Ephesians 1--4, the ["mystery"]{.class_4096} is more mysterious than when God revealed it to Paul, and the heavenly spiritualities of the Spirit are so heavenly spiritual that the Devil himself couldn't find out who went where or how they got there.

4:7 But unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

8 Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.

9 (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?

10 He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)

11 And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers;

12 For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:

13 Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:

Verse 7 is self-explanatory. Christians vary in the amounts of grace they receive (2 Cor. 12:9), exactly as they vary in the amounts of faith they receive (Rom. 12:3). Christ has the gifts without measure (John 3:34), but He measures them out in the next few verses.

["Wherefore he saith, When he ASCENDED UP ON HIGH"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). We are dealing with outer space travel again (cf. 1:20 and 3:18).

["He led CAPTIVITY CAPTIVE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). First exodus of scholarship!

["What is it but that he also DESCENDED FIRST INTO THE LOWER PARTS OF THE EARTH?"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). Second exodus of scholarship!

["He that descended is the same also that ASCENDED up far above all heavens]{.class_4096} \[there are three of them---2 Cor. 12:2\] [that he might fill all things"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10; cf. 1:23 and 3:18 with comments).

But we left the convoy back at Howard Johnson's before we ever got to verse 10, so let us go back and see how many flat tires, busted radiators, split blocks, burned valves, stripped gears, and broken solenoids we can find.

1\. Christ did not go down INTO the earth---[although Matthew 12:40 says that He did!]{.class_4095}---He just came down "to the very earth" ([NEB]{.class_4095}, 1961).

2\. Christ did not go down INTO the earth---[though Acts 2:27, 31 state that He did!]{.class_4095}---He merely came down to "the depth of this world" (Phillips, 1958).

3\. The captives of verse 8 were not people---[though Matthew 25:36, 43 says they were!]{.class_4095}---they were merely "sin," "death," and the "curse" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, pg. 1289).

4\. The captives of verse 8 were not people---although 1 Peter 3:19, 4:6 says they were!---they were "the powers of darkness" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 148).

5\. The captives of verse 8 were "that which captured us" (!!) according to Dr. Martin ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1310).

6\. The descent of verse 9 cannot be descent into Hades---[though Matthew 12:40 and Acts 2:27, 31]{.class_4095} says it was!---so the word ["first"]{.class_4096} is taken out of the verse ([RV ]{.class_4095}1881, [ASV ]{.class_4095}1901, [RSV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_248}1946, and Dummelow)!

7\. The captives are "vanquished foes" ([Amplified Version]{.class_4095}), but these foes are "sin," "death," and the "Devil" (Matthew Henry, also)!

Now, isn't this interesting? Death is not destroyed until [Revelation 20:14 ]{.class_4095}(see 1 Cor. 15:26). The Devil is not out of the picture until [Revelation 20:8--10]{.class_4095}, and yet all of the monkeys who monkey with the Bible are telling us that sin, death, and the Devil were LED CAPTIVE! What did the Lord Jesus [do ]{.class_4095}with SIN when He got it back up ["above all heavens"]{.class_4096}? [That is where verse 10 says He went!]{.class_4095} What do you suppose Jesus was doing hauling death and the Devil up to ["the third heaven"]{.class_4096} with Him?

Let us turn to the [Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}! Surely Rev. Salmond will be able to assist us with his vast learning and his knowledge of the "original language"! How about it, Rev?

Well, the Reverend wastes two pages listing every source he knows of and comes to the conclusion that Jesus Christ did NOT pay a visit to Hades (Volume Three, pg. 327); He merely came [down to this earth ]{.class_4095}from Heaven.

But now there is "all hell to pay" (as the G.I.s of World War I put it), for if Jesus Christ did [not ]{.class_4095}go down to Hades, then Matthew 12:40 has no meaning, Acts 2:27, 31 have no meaning, 1 Peter 3:19, 4:6 have no meaning, and (horrors!) what does THIS mean?

["So Christ was once OFFERED TO BEAR THE SINS of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time WITHOUT SIN unto salvation"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 9:28).

If Christ bore our sins---and He did, if Christ became SIN for us---and He did, if Christ became a CURSE for us---and He did, if Christ became as a SERPENT in our place---and He did, how does He ["appear the second time WITHOUT SIN"]{.class_4096} [unless He deposited His burden somewhere]{.class_4095}? If He was no longer bearing sin when Mary desired to touch Him (John 20:17),[ what did He do with your sins]{.class_4095}? WHERE ARE YOUR SINS THIS MINUTE?!

They were ON Christ, and He bore them IN His body (Isa. 53:4--5; 2 Cor. 5:21; 1 Pet. 2:24). If He did not go into ["the heart of the earth"]{.class_4096} and preach to spirits in prison (1 Pet. 3:19), what did He do with your sins? [THEY WERE ON HIM WHEN HE DIED!]{.class_4095} Furthermore, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_249}it was Christ's SOUL (Isa. 53:10) that was the offering for sin, and His SOUL was ["sorrowful, even unto death"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 26:38).

Mark it down; the writers of the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, the [Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, and Dummelow's Commentary have no more idea of what they are talking about than Tom Paine or Voltaire.

You see, the Body of Christ is not so much plagued these days with "Liberals" and "Neo-orthodoxy" as it is with Fundamental, Premillennial, Conservative INFIDELS.

Christ was [already on earth ]{.class_4095}when He said that He would be (in the future) ["three days and three nights in the heart of the earth."]{.class_4096} When He came up through the bars (Jon. 2:6), He brought the keys with Him (Rev. 1:18), so the GATES of Hell---[caught you in unbelief again!]{.class_4095}---could not keep the "CAPTIVES" enclosed! This explains why the Premillennial "Teacher Emeritus of New Testament Greek" at the Premillennial Moody Bible Institute (named after Dwight L. Moody) could not find the [Freshman Greek ]{.class_4095}meaning of the [Freshman Greek ]{.class_4095}word in his first-year [Freshman grammar ]{.class_4095}for ["gates" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 16:18--19). (No modern Greek professor or reviser consistently translates all of his Greek correctly, no matter what his profession of faith is. He translates it to suit his fancy. Phenomenon No. 1.)

Notice, also, the absolute refusal of Dr. Wuest to translate ["kosmon"]{.class_4095} as ["world"]{.class_4096} in John 18:36. Even further, notice his blatant denial of the difference in source between the two [births ]{.class_4095}of John 3:3--5. Dr. Wuest tells us that the "source" of both births is the same! ([The New Testament: An Expanded Translation,]{.class_4095} pg. 215.) That is exactly what Alexander Campbell taught, [and it has been the official teaching of the Roman Catholic Church for fifteen centuries.]{.class_4095}

["And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). What follows is the result of Christ's ascension. He ascended more than a month before Acts 1 (see John 20:17), and at this time, He took the souls of the Old Testament saints up with Him, or at least ["many"]{.class_4096} of them (Matt. 27:51--53). Being "redeemed," they could no longer stay in an intermediate state, and the modern revisers and translators since 1880---any of them---unwittingly left the Old Testament saints in "Abraham's Bosom" by spiritualizing the word ["captivity."]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_250} (Rather stupid, don't you think, for a man who professes to believe in the ["inspired originals"]{.class_4095}?)

With the coming of the Holy Spirit (Acts 2), ["gifts"]{.class_4096} are given, and the[ "gifts"]{.class_4096} come with the ["gift of the Holy Ghost"]{.class_4096} (Acts 2:38). Although the apostles are "chosen" in Matthew 10, they are not "empowered" (Luke 24:49) until Acts 2. The reader should observe that the ["gifts"]{.class_4096} here are PEOPLE, not "tongues," "miracles," "healing," etc., as they are found in 1 Corinthians 12. By reinserting ["gifts unto men"]{.class_4096} at verse 11 ([NEB, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, Good News, Amplified]{.class_4095}), the new translations obscure the sense entirely, for the "gifts unto men" of Psalm 68:18 are not the [men ]{.class_4095}given to the Church in the verse we are now studying:

["AND HE GAVE SOME, apostles"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11). Christ gave some men the ability to be apostles and others the ability to become prophets. Observe (to Bullinger's confounding) that ["apostles and prophets"]{.class_4096} (see 2:20) are to minister to the [one Body ]{.class_4095}of the "Body mystery" (see vs. 12).

[Where are the ]{.class_4095}["prophets"]{.class_4096}[ in the Berean assemblies? ]{.class_4095}Where are the ["apostles"]{.class_4096}?

If this age began with Ephesians, after Acts 28, what happened to the men given to it as gifts? Bullinger and Stam are gung-ho on ["pastors and TEACHERS,"]{.class_4096} but there is about as much "evangelism" among Hyperdispensationalists as there are [King James ]{.class_4095}Bibles among Jesuit priests.

Dr. Martin solves the problem by saying that ["apostles and prophets"]{.class_4096} were both "temporary offices" ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1311). Bullinger tries to ignore the problem, and in his 189th Appendix ([Companion Bible]{.class_4095}), he simply states the function of each office without even attempting to explain why the ["apostles and prophets" ]{.class_4096}are no longer with us. Both Bullinger and Williams assure us that the[ "apostles"]{.class_4096} in the passage could have no reference to the "Twelve" since, according to Williams and Bullinger, the "Twelve" are not in the Body of Christ. But again, this has a certain odor about it that reminds one of dead shrimp left in the sunlight too long. As a matter of fact, the last time we saw ["apostles and prophets"]{.class_4096} was in Ephesians 2:20, and they were part of the ["commonwealth of Israel"]{.class_4096} (2:12) and dated their apostleship from the CRUCIFIXION []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_251}(2:13--16), not from Paul in Acts 9.

Furthermore, Bullinger, Stam, Baker, O'Hair, and the "Grace" schools again forgot to check the cross-references in English! Observe 1 Corinthians 12:28---["apostles\...prophets."]{.class_4096} The "Church," here, is said to be the ["body" ]{.class_4096}(1 Cor. 12:27),[ "and God hath set\...FIRST apostles."]{.class_4096} There is no record of Silas, Timotheus, Junia, Apollos, Sosthenes, or Barnabas being ["set"]{.class_4096} in the Church; but there is a major passage telling how Peter, James, John, Thomas, Bartholomew, et al., were ["set"]{.class_4096} in the Church---Acts 2! Paul says that these men ["were apostles BEFORE" ]{.class_4096}he was (Gal. 1:17). If they were ["before"]{.class_4096} him and[ "set\...first"]{.class_4096} in the Church, why would any blockhead think the "Church" began in Acts 9, let alone Acts 28?!

No, the truth is that the [apostles ]{.class_4095}of Acts 1--10 were necessary during the formative years of the Church as it began to grow. The [prophets ]{.class_4095}(Acts 11--28) were necessary for its maturity preceding the completion of the New Testament canon; and since that time, ["some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers."]{.class_4096} Chronologically, the last group ministering to the body before the Rapture will be ["pastors and teachers,"]{.class_4096} and the reader can see that there is no comma ("in the originals" as Wuest, Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Dummelow, Rendall, Blaikie, Martin, Trench, Lightfoot, Robertson, Meyer, Wordsworth, Olshausen, and Westcott and Hort would say!) after ["pastors."]{.class_4096} The pastor is to be a "teacher" (2 Tim. 1:11; 1 Tim. 3:2), and a pastor who cannot teach the word of God---as well as hold hands with old ladies, politic with members he is about to lose, smile at people coming in the door, encourage members to tithe by eating cake and drinking coffee with them---has no more business in the pulpit than he does in a barroom. (For study, cf. Matt. 5:5; 10:8; Acts 1:22; Mark 16:15; Acts 21:11; 2 Tim. 4:5; 1 Tim. 5:17; Acts 23:1; Ezek. 34:23; Acts 20:28; 1 Pet. 5:1.)

["Evangelists"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11) are a rare breed and only the most famous ones are known: Billy Graham, Billy Sunday, Bob Harrington, Dwight Moody, Dave Wilkinson, John R. Rice, Charles G. Finney, Jack Van Impe, Hyman Appelman, etc. However, there are scores of full-time evangelists quietly plowing, sowing, planting, and reaping, year in and year out. They are not pastors "swapping pulpits," as is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_252}so often the case; but they are men who are on the road day and night, 30--48 weeks a year, holding meetings wherever God opens the doors. A few of these were Glenn Schunk, Don Wilson, Don Chenoweth, Phil Shuler, Jim Mercer,\
B. R. Lakin, Fred Brown, E. J. Daniels, Carl Woodbury, John Leland, "Monk" Parker, and Bob Persson. Of course, today, there are many others (e.g., Sam Gipp, Dave Spurgeon, et al.).

["For the perfecting of the saints, FOR THE WORK OF THE MINISTRY, for the edifying of the body of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). The [King James ]{.class_4095}reading is highly preferable to the next nearest fifty translations. The men given to the church as ["gifts"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11) are to do the following:

1\. Perfect the saints.

2\. Work as ministers.

3\. Edify the Body of Christ.

It is true that the work of the gifted men is to perfect the saints so that they (the saints) can minister and edify others, but both meanings are in the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 as it is stated. Nearly every version since 1880 gives the latter meaning only, instead of the three listed above.

["Till we all come in the UNITY OF THE FAITH, and of the knowledge of the Son of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13; cf. 3:16--20, 1:23, and 4:3 with comments). ["Unto a PERFECT MAN, unto the measure of the stature of the FULNESS OF CHRIST"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13; cf. Col. 1:24; Rom. 8:29; 1 Cor. 2:6; Phil 3:15; Gal. 4:19). After telling us that the [AV ]{.class_4095}is "wrongly translated" ([Expositor's Greek New Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 332), Rev. Salmond pulls the old "new Humanity" bit on us that Dummelow pulled off in 2:10, which see. Theodotian, Calvin, and Meyer think the perfection spoken of is the perfection of Romans 8:29, which is unattainable until the Rapture. Jerome, Luther, Chrysostom, and DeWette take it to be a goal that can be attained in this life. (So also Williams and Bullinger.)

Before expositing the infallible [AV ]{.class_4095}text, we may take note that 18 [changes ]{.class_4095}have been suggested by the scholars between verse 8 and verse 14. These men are Robinson, Harless, Soden, Davidson, Luken, Riddle, Meyer, Alford, Bengel, Abbott, Salmond, Ellicott, Wordsworth, DeWette, and Schnedermann. The sincere student of the Bible may overlook [all eighteen proposed changes ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_253}and will certainly be better off if he does.

A. The Body of Christ will reach "maturity" in the next twenty years. Its "wheat" will be sure enough "wheat," and it will match the performance of the early church in the first two centuries of church history. The ["perfect man"]{.class_4096} and the ["stature of the fulness"]{.class_4096} is an indirect reference to the Body as ["one new man"]{.class_4096} (2:15), and its "growing" (4:16) will be over when the trumpet sounds (1 Thess. 4:15--16).

B. The individual Christian has been furnished with gifted men (vs. 12) so that he can ["grow in grace, and in the knowledge of our Lord"]{.class_4096} (2 Pet. 3:18; Eph. 1:17, 3:19). His goal for "growth" should be the same as Paul's (Col. 1:24; 1 Tim. 6:19; Phil. 3:10--14).

["The stature of the fulness of Christ"]{.class_4096} implies a Christ-likeness in the believer---not an imitation of Christ (see 5:1)---which avails itself of everything that the risen Saviour has for it (3:16--21). Christ only reaches his full[ "stature"]{.class_4096} in the body of the believer in whom He is ["formed"]{.class_4096} (see comments under Gal. 4:19).

4:14 That we henceforth be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, and cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;

15 But speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, even Christ:

16 From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.

The first verse in the section is an inspired description of the activity of Bible translators and revisers, Bible teachers, and Bible commentators. No one but God Himself could have described the activity of 1880--2010 (in the Biblical realm) in the words that follow.

1\. ["Tossed to and fro"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). Today, the [RV, RSV,]{.class_4095} and the [ASV]{.class_4095}; tomorrow Phillips, [Living Letters]{.class_4095}, [Good News]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_254}, and the [Amplified]{.class_4095}; the day after that Lamsa, Weymouth, Moffatt, the [NEB]{.class_4095}, and the [New ASV]{.class_4095}.

2\. ["Carried about with every wind"]{.class_4096} (vs 14). Postmillennialism today, Amillennialism tomorrow, post-Tribulation rapture the next day!

3\. ["Sleight of men"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14), as in "sleight of hand," "the hand is quicker than the eye," etc. This is the professional dice thrower gaffing or loading the dice, the professional cardsharp dealing the second card or the bottom card, the professional magician trying to convince you that [men ]{.class_4095}are called "gods" (see [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 261) when the quotation (Psa. 82) doesn't even mention the matter.

4\. ["Cunning craftiness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14): Trying to prove that the ["clouds"]{.class_4096} of 1 Thessalonians 4:17 are "masses of saints" who LOOK LIKE clouds (Kenneth Wuest, New Testament, pg. 482)! Making you think that Paul quoted a pre-Christian [Septuagint ]{.class_4095}in Ephesians 4:26 when the writers of the [Septuagint ]{.class_4095}are actually quoting from books written 200--400 years after the New Testament was completed (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis]{.class_4095} and [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_4095}, 1970).

5\. ["They lie in wait to deceive"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). Exactly. That is the whole idea and that was the purpose to start with. To this day, you can find the prefaces to [RVs, ASVs]{.class_4095}, and [RSVs]{.class_4095} which talk about their texts merely being "revisions" of the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible and not "new" or "different" "Bibles." The men who write these prefaces KNOW they are lying when they write them, and they know that [the text of 1880 has made 30,000 changes in the text of the AV 1611.]{.class_4095} The men who retranslated the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 and called it a [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} knew perfectly well they were not printing the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text, but they say on their frontispiece that it IS the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text! ["THEY LIE IN WAIT TO DECEIVE."]{.class_4096} When Kenneth Wuest takes out a whole verse of Scripture and yet refuses to [number the verse he takes out]{.class_4095}---that is, he retains the [King James]{.class_4095} [number system ]{.class_4095}without the [proper number ]{.class_4095}of verses (as in Acts 8:37)---he has worked himself into this bracket: ["THEY LIE IN WAIT TO DECEIVE."]{.class_4096}

The mature Christian (vs. 14) is[ "no more"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_255} a child (vs. 14), and you couldn't fool [any ]{.class_4095}"mature Christ-ian" with the trash that has been published since 1880. You could fool a [philosopher ]{.class_4095}with it, or a Greek [scholar]{.class_4095} or a book [publisher]{.class_4095} or a "[genius ]{.class_4095}in the original tongues" or a [professor ]{.class_4095}of New Testament Greek or some incredible boob like Westcott or Hort, but a "mature Christian"? Never. You could fool a Conservative, soulwinning, born-again Fundamentalist before you could fool a mature Christian.

When Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer (representing the official viewpoint of Bob Jones University, Greenville, SC) alter the [AV ]{.class_4095}text again and again and again---see the dead-orthodox publication [Biblical Viewpoint]{.class_4095}---by referring to ["THE]{.class_4095} Greek text," it only fools the young ministerial student who has been tricked into attending the University because it had a reputation for believing in the infallible, absolute authority of the Bible, etc.

Any mature Christian knows that there is no such animal as "THE Greek text," and the men who use this Satanic cliché no more believe what they are saying than when they say: "We believe the Bible IS the word of God." They believe that [some ]{.class_4095}Bible [somewhere ]{.class_4095}(no longer existing) WAS the word of God, and they know this to be their true conviction when they lie publicly to get students!

"THE Greek text" cannot be the "originals," for these men [don't have the originals. ]{.class_4095}"THE Greek text" cannot be the [Receptus]{.class_4095}, for Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer [recommend the New ASV ]{.class_4095}which is the[ Vaticanus-Sinaiticus]{.class_4095} amalgamation of Aland, Metzger, Westcott and Hort, Jerome, and Augustine (see [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} 1970). "THE Greek text" exists nowhere but in the deluded imagination of the dead-orthodox apostate, [for there are scores of Greek texts ]{.class_4095}available to any student---Tischendorf, Mill, Walton, Griesbach, Stephanus, Elzevir, Nestle, Westcott and Hort, Moulton, Souter, Lachmann, Von Soden, etc.

These dead-orthodox apostates ["lie in wait to deceive"]{.class_4096}; and with ["cunning craftiness"]{.class_4096} learned ["by the]{.class_4096} [sleight of men,"]{.class_4096} they destroy the ministerial students' faith in the living words of the living God. To a mature Christian, such double-faced hucksters are a joke.

["But SPEAKING THE TRUTH in love"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). It is bad enough to hate the [AV]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_256} 1611, but when you hate it and pervert it at the same time (John 17:14; Jer. 23:36), how do you claim to be a "mature Christian"? Are you not still a child---a baby (1 Cor. 3:1)? The word of God is[ "truth" ]{.class_4096}(John 17:17), and we are to speak it as ["the oracles of God"]{.class_4096} (1 Pet. 4:11). We are to speak it ["in love."]{.class_4096} (Most Fundamentalists major in ["speaking the truth"]{.class_4096} [without love]{.class_4095}, and the Liberals speak love [without the truth]{.class_4095}, but the "happy medium" is what is desired.)

Notice the context---["GROW UP"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). Grow up! Stop flinching, dodging, and breaking out into a cold sweat every time "scholarship," "science," or "education" waves its magic wand at your[ King James 1611 AV]{.class_4095}! Your Book can take care of itself! You don't have to defend a roaring lion. Jes' turn 'im loose! If the faculty at Bob Jones University thinks your lion is senile and decrepit and has lost all of his jaw teeth, let him out, man! Turn him loose! You'll be more than "gratified" with the "performance."

It was the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 that upset Madalyn Murray, Abbie Hoffman, Rap Brown, Stokely Carmichael, Marshall Neal, Timothy Leary, Westcott and Hort, Voltaire, Spinoza, Darwin, Leibnitz, Tom Paine, Pope John XXIII, A. T. Robertson, Lenin, Marx, Trotsky, Bertrand Russell, John Dewey, Robert Sumner, Noel Smith, R. B. Thieme, Huxley, Gus Hall, and Kenneth Wuest. Turn him loose! You talk about a show! ["From whom the whole body fitly joined together\...maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16). Observe that the Body of Christ is "growing" exactly as the building of 2:21 is growing. Furthermore, this Body must grow in size, for there were more members in it in A.D. 90 than there were in A.D. 70, and there were more members in it in 1900 than there were in 1400. It is true that the immediate context is [individual members ]{.class_4095}growing so that the Body will grow into "maturity" (in the sense of Christ-likeness), but a static body into which members are placed (Bullinger and Calvin) is not the point of the passage.

4:17 This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,

18 Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:

19 Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.

20 But ye have not so learned Christ;

21 If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:

22 That ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;

23 And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;

24 And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.

25 Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.

26 Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:

27 Neither give place to the devil.

28 Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.

29 Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.

30 And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.

31 Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:

32 And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.

The entire section is dealing with practical Christian living, and for this reason, one will find that most Calvinists and Bullingerites stop in chapter 3 or at chapter 4:6 in expounding Ephesians. Occasionally, a brave soul will mention something in chapter 5 or chapter 6 (or the section before us), but "honorable mention" is all that any Calvinist or Bullingerite ever allowed the practical section.

["That ye henceforth walk not AS OTHER GENTILES WALK"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17), showing clearly that even though the saved Ephesian is "in Christ" and therefore has lost his "Gentile" nameplate (1 Cor. 12:13; Gal. 3:28), his PHYSICAL body is still a [Gentile ]{.class_4095}body, not a [Jewish ]{.class_4095}body (see Gal. 3:28; Rom. 3:1--2). Racial distinctions are mentioned right along through the New Testament, even where the passages are speaking of born-again people in one Body (Rom. 11). It is only when speaking of the spiritual STANDING of the believer that the Holy Spirit erases racial demarcations. Since all the new "Bibles" are for the purpose of promoting religious Socialism, all of them omit the word ["other"]{.class_4096} and give instead the archaic Jesuit rendering of the Roman Catholic, Dark-Age [Rheims Version ]{.class_4095}of 1582. (Observe the same attempt to integrate races by omitting the word ["blood"]{.class_4096} in Acts 17:26.)

The ["New" ASV]{.class_4095} (1963) is just as corrupt as the old [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) when it comes to these things, and one should never make the mistake of thinking that simply because 95 percent of the "Conservatives" in America recommend either version that they have any idea of what they are doing at all. Dead-orthodox apostates have been aping the Jesuits since 1881, and their recommendations are not to be taken seriously by conscientious students of the Holy Scriptures.

In what follows, we see the Divine estimation of Frenchmen, Spaniards, Italians, Americans, Englishmen, Russians, Germans, Irishmen, Africans, and Asiatics.

1\. ["Vanity"]{.class_4096} dominates their minds (vs. 17).

2\. Their understanding is ["darkened"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18).

3\. They are ["alienated from the life of God" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18).

4\. This is because there is ["ignorance"]{.class_4096} in them (vs. 18).

5\. This ignorance is deliberate and intentional (vs. 18).

6\. They are ["past feeling"]{.class_4096} when it comes to spiritual truths and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_259}spiritual issues (vs. 19).

7\. They have given themselves over to fornication, gluttony, cannibalism, sex perversion, deviation, and sensual pleasure (vs. 19).

8\. They are greedy (["schwein"]{.class_4095}) and unclean (["hund"]{.class_4095}) (vs. 19).

The description is also the description of the leading educators of the National Education Association (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972, Prov. 19:27). These ["blind leaders of the blind"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 15:14) have a regular creedal statement which is as dogmatic as the Council of Trent (1546). They believe that empirical science is the supreme authority, that God is part of the universe, and that both He and the universe are eternal. They believe that "growing," "changing," and "adjusting" are the supreme good, and that ANYTHING is "relative" or "meaningful" if it is relative to [anything ]{.class_4095}or means [anything ]{.class_4095}to [anybody]{.class_4095}. They believe all truth is relative, the Bible is an omnibus of fairy stories and legends, and you couldn't burn in a lake of fire forever if you tried. They believe in criticizing the Bible, but criticism of somebody's "faith" is taboo. They are all international Socialists, and the only "heaven" they know is "pie on the earth by and by" through the efforts of blind men as blind as they are. Socrates, Aristotle, Plato, and Weiskaupf kick off for them; Charlemagne, Clovis, Pepin, Rothschild, and the Popes intercept the passes for them; Darwin, Huxley, Lyell, Paley, Kepler, Newton, Bacon, Comte, and Spinoza do the running; Leibnitz, Hegel, Royce, Dewey, Rockefeller, Russell, Einstein, and JFK do the place kicking for the extra point; and Gentile, William James, Henry James, Hobbes, Hume, Carlyle, Marx, Freud, Lenin, Trotsky, Engels, and the Health, Education, and Welfare Board carry the ball over the goal line. Nothing in the Super Bowl could compare to it!

They are BLIND; hence, they say, "Well, I just can't [see ]{.class_4095}it. I just don't look at it that way!" They are ["past feeling"]{.class_4096}; hence, they say, "Well, there must be more to it than that (John 3:16)! I don't FEEL anything!" They are IGNORANT; therefore, they say, "Where did Cain get his wife? How did the animals get in the Ark? What about the heathen who haven't heard?" They are vain; hence, they say, "I think; therefore, [I AM. Man]{.class_4095} is the measure of all things, etc." They []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_260}are unclean; therefore, they say, "What is wrong with it? A little bit doesn't hurt. We know when to quit. My conscience doesn't convict me (Tit. 1:15!)!" And they are DEAD (vs. 18; Luke 9:60); hence, they worship [LIFE ]{.class_4095}magazine and watch "This is your Life" and buy "life insurance" and say "LIFE begins at forty" and try to prolong life or find the source of life on the moon (Rom. 10:4--7).

That is, the godless, depraved, Diana-worshippers of First-Century Ephesus would have great ["gemuflekeit"]{.class_4095} with the wretched, abased, dolly-worshippers of the United Nations today. Human nature never changes. The corrupt humus of the Ephesian craftsmen (Acts 19:27) is the corrupt humus of the greatest religious and political leaders of the Twentieth Century. You only imagine it otherwise because your ["understanding"]{.class_4096} is ["darkened"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18).

(The changes suggested by Bengel, Rendall, Blaikie, Martin, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Clarke, Lange, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Meyer, Wordsworth, Salmond, Schaff, Alford, DeWette, Schnedermann, Riddle, and Ruckert are not worth recording.)

["But ye have not so learned Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). Hence, the country expression: "That preacher done LEARNED me Jesus," or (in the original!) "That preacher done LERNT me Jesus." (In Kentucky and the Ozarks, they say "LARNED"; but Bengel, Rendall, Blaikie, Martin, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Clarke, Lange, et al., were not ["learned"]{.class_4096} enough in the "original languages" to make application of the text!)

Verse 21 interprets for us:[ "If so be that ye have heard him, and HAVE BEEN TAUGHT BY HIM." ]{.class_4096}You did not learn verses 17--19 from Jesus Christ; you learned them from your teachers in College and high school, from the network shows on TV, and from the newsstand!

After the usual blather about the [AV ]{.class_4095}not translating tense, case, and number correctly, the [NEB ]{.class_4095}and Phillips refuse to translate ["Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20) as the object---although the word is in the accusative. Phenomenon No. 3. Several of the commentators resent the Ephesians being taught ["BY"]{.class_4096} Christ and hearing Him (vs. 21), so they decide, instead, that the Ephesians were just taught "about Christ" and "heard about Him" from someone else. To arrive at these misreadings, the commentators had to ignore all of the Greek texts []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_261}of any family. Phenomena Nos. 1, 2, and 5.

["That ye put off concerning the former CONVERSATION"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22; cf. 2:3). The word ["conversation"]{.class_4096} is used by Shakespeare (2 Henry IV, v 5), and yet to this day, no one has "revised it" or "updated it." Why? How is it that an original by Michelangelo is too sacred to "revise" and a play by Shakespeare is so holy that not one word can be changed, and yet the Holy Bible that made England and America the greatest powers in the world has to be altered to suit someone's fancy?

["The OLD MAN, which is corrupt"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22). Here, the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961) comes home with a real blooper, for the deluded scribe has written "lay aside the OLD HUMAN NATURE." I would like to see you try it (Rom. 7:24)! You won't "lay aside human nature" until the undertaker comes for your corpse.

Kenneth Wuest now decides that he has not been called to translate Greek after all, and after writing ten pages (Preface) on how much more "accurate" his "expanded translation" is going to be, he simply ignores every Greek text extant and translates ["man"]{.class_4096} ([anthropon]{.class_4095}) as "self." This is NOT the Greek word for "self." The [RSV ]{.class_4095}tries "new nature" (which is not a Greek translation of any kind), and Phillips suggests "new life" (which is not a Greek translation of any kind). Phenomenon No. 3.

But the reason why our brethren are having so much trouble with the passage is found in verse 24: ["put on the new man." ]{.class_4096}You see, ["the new man"]{.class_4096} is Jesus Christ Himself (1 Cor. 15:47; Col. 3:10), and the text (vs. 24) is stating that the original image of Adam---before he fell---was made in the image of Jesus Christ, who Himself is the ["image of God"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 1:2--3; 2 Cor. 4:4). Romans 13:14 explained the verse exactly, and not even Bullinger could find the English cross-reference! The logical terminus of this "messing with the word" is the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961), which goes "all out" with the "new-nature-life-self" bit and winds up by saying that the new nature "shows itself" by living a "just" and "devout life." But this is not the writing, wording, spelling, meaning, sense, or teaching of the passage. The ["new man"]{.class_4096} (Jesus Christ) is created in ["righteousness and true holiness."]{.class_4096} And it is this ["new man"]{.class_4096} that the Christian is to ["put on."]{.class_4096} He is to be worn as a new suit of clothes, and ["the old man"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_262} ("Adam") is to be hung up in the closet---[without ]{.class_4095}the moth balls.

(Wuest's habit of separating the adjective from the noun \[vs. 24\] is carried out here, giving us "and holiness of truth." But, of course, this means nothing in getting the sense of the passage. Furthermore, Kenneth Wuest refuses to separate the adjective from the noun in 5:6, although the two constructions are identical. Phenomenon No. 4.)

["Deceitful lusts"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22) are still corrupting a nature which was corrupt when it was born (Rom. 3:9--20). Lust is [deceitful ]{.class_4095}in that it:

a\. Figures a way to obtain what it wants, so making Self the determining factor in a decision.

b\. Professes to be willing to "quit" when it has "had enough," but it never has enough (Eccl. 6:7)!

c\. The processes of rationalization and self-justification which accompany the "lust-sin-death" cycle (James 1:15) will cause a man to deceive himself (Gal. 6:7) so he can obtain the desired object.

When ["the new man"]{.class_4096} is donned, [nineteen changes ]{.class_4095}come with the change of apparel (Isa. 61:10). They are as follows:

1\. The Christian should put away lying (vs. 25). The verse is self-explanatory (Zech. 8:16; Rom. 12:5). We are members of the same body. This time, the word ["neighbour"]{.class_4096} refers to another Christian "in Christ." Lying to God is worse than lying to the "brethren" (Acts 5:3--4), and many a child prayed for snow so "the pretty flowers could come up next spring" when what he had in mind was using a new sled. It is extremely difficult to be honest at ALL TIMES. The Christian who will not admit [this ]{.class_4095}is somewhat of a liar himself.

The innuendoes, overtones, nuances, and hidden traps in the average conversation these days are so numerous that it reminds one of the conversation between two Japanese businessmen who met at the train station in Tokyo. One of them asked the other where he was going on business that day, and he replied, "Kobe." The other became livid and flew into a rage, screaming, "Oh, you despicable liar! You told me Kobe so I would [think ]{.class_4095}you were going to Osaka! But I have made many inquiries this week, and I know you really [are ]{.class_4095}going to Kobe! Oh, you deceiver!"

Modern Christian lying usually begins with "we feel led of the Lord" or "remember us in your prayers" or "we ran 500 in Sunday school last week." (To the untutored this means: "I wanted to do it," "we need some money," and "we ran 400 in Sunday school.") Most women are notorious liars when it comes to age. Someone has aptly said, "The longest year in a woman's life is that year between 25 and 35," or "A woman's best ten years are between 30 and 31." The woman who will not lie outright will profess to have blond hair when it is dirty brown, will profess to have the measurements of Miss America when they are more like Jackie's measurements: Jackie Gleason. (And the men are not immune. Many a man who looks like a Greek god in a Hart Schaffner Marx suit, looks like a Greek restaurant after he takes off his clothes.)

The greatest liars are Bible revisers and Greek "scholars." Bobbie Baker and Billie Sol Estes would have a long way to go before they could catch up with Westcott, Lightfoot, Hort, Schaff, Sanday, Trench, F. F. Bruce, Alford, Farrar, Lachmann, Tischendorf, Origen, Eusebius, and C. H. Dodd. The original critic lied straight out (Gen. 3:1--4), but not before he questioned the authoritative text (Gen. 3:1).

2\. The Christian should ["speak\...truth"]{.class_4096} (vs. 25).

3\. ["Be ye angry, and sin not\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 26). In spite of the fact that the verse is illustrated by the life of Jesus Christ Himself (Matt. 21:12; Mark 3:5), the corrupt New [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1963) and old [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), recommended by the faculty at Bob Jones University, reproduced the Roman Catholic reading of the Jesuit priesthood ([Rheims]{.class_4095}, 1582) in Matthew 5:22, which not only nullifies Ephesians 4:26, but also makes a sinner out of Jesus Christ. However, these monstrous blasphemies have never bothered the Greek faculty at any "Fundamental" school, so don't look for anyone to get upset about it. (The [New ASV]{.class_4095} \[1963\] is the 1582 Jesuit Bible of the Latin Church.)

The Christian is to get angry at times (Tit. 1:13; Matt. 23:15; 2 Cor. 13:2), but he is not to ["sin" ]{.class_4096}when he gets angry, ["for the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God"]{.class_4096} (James 1:20) and ["anger resteth in the bosom of fools"]{.class_4096} (Eccl. 7:9). Jesus was angry at nothing but sin, and angry at no one but the religious leaders of the ecumenical council of His day. His words to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_264}harlots (John 4:6--26) and beggars (Mark 10:52) were much more charitable than His words to Doctors of Law and Rabbis (Matt. 23:2--36).

4\. ["Let not the sun go down upon your wrath"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26). The "never go to bed" of Phillips is just a little too much, and it justifies the remark made by someone that [Living Letters]{.class_4095} and Phillips are COMMENTARIES on the Scripture and have nothing to do with the Holy Bible itself.

Twelve hours is long enough to be sore at anybody. If you go on with it, you will lose sleep. You may get ulcers from a grudge if you keep nursing it (Lev. 19:18). If you should [die ]{.class_4095}in your sleep, you will have to hit the whole mess again at the Judgment Seat of Christ (Matt. 18:18, 1 Tim. 5:25), so "knock off" with it before sunset.

5\. ["Neither give place to the devil"]{.class_4096} (vs. 27). You will do this if you keep on nursing the grudge (cf. 2 Cor. 2:10--11). The Devil will take advantage of it and magnify it (1 Kings 21:1--10) until it is all out of proportion, and it might terminate in murder (2 Sam. 18-19) or something just as bad (Gen. 37)!

6\. The sixth admonition that comes with ["the new man"]{.class_4096} is: ["Let him that stole steal no more"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28).

7\. The seventh is: ["let him labour, working with his hands\...that he may have to give"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28). All is transparent. The Bible encourages men to work (2 Thess. 3:10), not steal (Exod. 20:15). (See comments on Exod. 20:15 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus,]{.class_4095} 1972.) Loafers like Lenin, Hoffman, Trotsky, Rap Brown, and Marx---who couldn't work a 20 hour week, let alone a 60 hour week---have no business telling any workers to "unite"; and they have no more business telling laborers and farmers what kind of an economic system they need than Pete Maravich has telling Jack Nicklaus how to drive with a No. 3 wood.

The real Christian goes beyond the requirements of providing for his own house; he works so that ["he may have to give to him that needeth"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28). Any group of Christian believers who obey the New Testament can give a much more convincing demonstration of [Utopian ]{.class_4095}Communism than any group in Russia or Red China (cf. Acts 2--4).

8--9. The eighth admonition is against ["corrupt communication"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_265} (vs. 29), and the positive half of the verse (the ninth admonition) encourages ["that which is good\...that it may minister grace unto the hearers"]{.class_4096} (cf. 5:3--4 and comments).

["Corrupt communication"]{.class_4096} includes the passing on of rumors, the inventing of slander, the dirty joke, the "off color joke" (see 5:3--4), and any language that is foul, filthy, rude, or obscene. ["Good words and fair speeches"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 16:18) do not remedy the problem, for ["good words and fair speeches"]{.class_4096} are quite characteristic of the most corrupt "communicators" on earth (see Phil. 3:21 and comment). The word ["corrupt"]{.class_4096} is the famous critic's word for the [Textus Receptus ]{.class_4095}of the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible, and if the reader is bored with the constant repetition of this word in our commentary, he may attribute it to the fact that the author was bored long before the reader was with the endless appearance of the word where the "leading" revisers, theologians, commentators, and "manuscript detectives" used it in referring to the Holy Bible. Westcott and Hort went a little further and called the Bible of Billy Sunday, J. Frank Norris, Dr. DeHaan, Bob Jones Sr., Mordecai Ham, David Livingstone, Jonathan Goforth, Gipsy Smith, Sam Jones, Charles G. Finney, George Whitefield, and David Brainerd, "vile" (see [Which Bible?]{.class_4095}, David Otis Fuller, 1970, pg. 223).

The [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 has never been guilty of a ["corrupt communication" or ]{.class_4096}a corruption or a corrupt text. The commuters, who "communicated" with the American public between 1880 and 1980, were professional corrupters from the top of their heads to the soles of their feet---in spite of the admonition: ["Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29).

In 1961, I saw a man 81 years old get saved in a meeting in Statesboro, Georgia. He had been raised as a simple-minded farm boy, without guile or sophistication. He became deeply convicted during a revival meeting when he was fifteen years old and nearly trusted Christ. During this period, he heard a Bible-believing preacher tell one of the dirtiest jokes he was to hear in his entire lifetime. All conviction left him, and it left him for 66 years. At 81, he told me that under my preaching, he had felt the same old conviction that touched his heart at fifteen, and he had nearly run []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_266}down the aisle during the invitation in the fear that it would leave him again and never return.

10\. The tenth warning is: ["grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption"]{.class_4096} (vs. 30; cf. 1:13--14).

Since the Holy Spirit is a person (John 16:13), He can be grieved (here), resisted (Acts 7:51), and lied to (Acts 5:3). The Christian is ["sealed unto the day of redemption"]{.class_4096} exactly as the unsaved sinner is sealed unto the day of perdition (2 Pet. 2:9). The "preserves" will be opened in that day (Psa. 97:10), and "perseverance" is no problem for the believer in Christ. God preserves Him (1 Cor. 1:7--9). The substitution of "with a view to" (Wuest and Rendall) and ["for the day"]{.class_4095} ([NEB]{.class_4095}, 1961; [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095}, 1958; [RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946) and ["the personal pledge of your eventual"]{.class_4095} (Phillips, 1958) adds nothing to the text, or the sense of the text, but confusion.

The Holy Spirit is grieved with foolish talking and jesting, sin and uncleanness, all unrighteousness, lying, loafing, exaggerating, self-pity, lack of concern, lack of gratitude, lack of fruit (see Gal. 5:22 and comments), grumbling, murmuring, stinginess, jealousy, false accusations, lack of faith, refusal to testify, indifference to the Bible, perversion of the truth, failure to praise God, and [worrying]{.class_4095}. Paul and John Wesley probably caused less grief to the Holy Spirit than any two men who ever lived (see comments under Gal. 5:16).

["The day of redemption"]{.class_4096} is[ "the day of Christ"]{.class_4096} (see 2 Cor. 1:22 cf. 1 Cor. 1:8; Phil. 1:10, 2:16; 2 Tim. 4:8; 2 Thess. 2:2; 1 Cor. 4:5). This is not "the day of the Lord," and the Bible believer should never be deceived by such misleading publications as [The Blessed Hope]{.class_4095} (George Ladd, Eerdmans, 1956, pp. 92--93) into thinking that the days are identical. "God" is not "Heaven," kingdom is not ["kingly power"]{.class_4095} (see [RSV]{.class_4095}, Luke 23:42), "Lord" is not "Christ," and peanut butter is not orange marmalade. (Ladd, as F. F. Bruce \[[The Gospel of the Kingdom]{.class_4095}\], is simply fulfilling the prophecy of vs. 14.)

Now follow six negative admonitions: the Christian is to get rid of---11.[ "Bitterness,"]{.class_4096} 12. ["Wrath," ]{.class_4096}13. ["Anger,"]{.class_4096} 14. ["Clamour,"]{.class_4096} 15. ["Evil speaking," ]{.class_4096}and 16. ["All malice." "Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous" ]{.class_4096}(Prov. 27:4), bitterness is sin (Heb. 12:15), []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_267}clamor is one of the works of the flesh (Eph. 4:31 cf. Gal. 5:21), evil speaking is a violation of Ephesians 4:29, and malice is part of the old life (Tit. 3:3). (Cross-references which give light on the terms are found in Acts 8:23; Rom. 3:14; Heb. 12:5; James 3:11; Rev. 16:19; Heb. 5:7; Mark 3:28; 1 Tim. 6:4; and Col. 3:8). Malice is an attitude; clamor is the result of anger and wrath. Evil speaking can cause bitterness. Bitterness is a stimulant to wrath and anger. (Not one suggestion by one Greek scholar from 1612 to 1980 is any improvement on the text.)

17\. ["Be ye kind,"]{.class_4096} 18. ["Tenderhearted,"]{.class_4096} 19. ["Forgiving one another." ]{.class_4096}What should be the easiest thing to do is often the hardest. Personal hurts and personal injuries are to be overlooked, forgotten, put away, or ignored altogether (Col. 3:13; 1 Pet. 2:21--25). The tendency to retaliate or "strike back," where one has been slandered or reviled, is part of the old humus of human nature. The passage is not referring to being sweet towards critics who hate and attack God's word (note Tit. 1:10--12, 1 Tim. 1:20, Matt. 16:23, Acts 5:1--11), but it is a personal reference to personal dealings (exactly as the commandment of Exod. 20:13 is). Christians are very unkind to one another; they are not in the least "tenderhearted" in the middle of church splits, doctrinal controversies, and "business meetings." The ones who talk the most about "courtesy, love, politeness, and soft words" are usually the most vicious and venomous in an out-and-out "showdown."

Billy Sunday and Sam Jones were cultured gentlemen alongside Bishop Pike, Bishop Inge, Poteat, Harkness, Fulton Sheen, and the leaders of the ecumenical council (1970--1990). You've never seen real malice until you have watched the movements of Liberals and Catholics trying to shut down a radio station owned by Carl McIntire or J. Harold Smith, or a newspaper published by Ian Paisley.

["Forgiving one another"]{.class_4096} should come easy in view of the fact that ["God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you"]{.class_4096} (vs. 32). But many Christians were saved before they were fifteen years old, and it is very hard to convince them that God had to forgive them of anything really BAD. These Christian-nurtured, Christian-raised, Christian-trained "Christians" are often the most vicious foes of Biblical preaching that one can imagine. They hold grudges for []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1764.xhtml_page_268}decades and even pass them on to their children. A man like John Newton ("Amazing Grace") or Mel Trotter can obey the admonition of verse 32 much easier than a man who has been raised in a Christian day school and has never done anything worse than lie about a report card. At least this is the infallible truth according to Jesus Christ (Luke 7:47).

I remember a vivid scene from eighteen years ago in Macon, Georgia. I was eating dinner with a Christian couple when their son (age 8) came running into the dining room, red-faced and bawling at the top of his lungs. When his mother had calmed him down, it came out that his older sister (age 12) had slapped him after some kind of an argument. The mother asked him gently, "But honey, didn't you remember that verse we learned yesterday about ['be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another.'"]{.class_4096} The boy interrupted with another burst of agony and cried, "Mama, I'm trying to[ 'be ye kind one to another,']{.class_4096} but she don't wanna ['be ye kind one to another'"]{.class_4096}! (I think that pretty well states the case.)

Throughout the 19 items (vss. 25--32), the scholars make various changes, but they amount to little or nothing---as usual. Counting all of the emendations suggested on the passage, we would have to make seventy alterations of text. The wisest thing to do (as always) is simply to deposit the "new" translations (with the commentaries) in File 13.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml}

## Chapter 5 {.heading_116}

5:1 Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children;

2 And walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour.

3 But fornication, and all uncleanness, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you, as becometh saints;

4 Neither filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not convenient: but rather giving of thanks.

5 For this ye know, that no whoremonger, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God.

6 Let no man deceive you with vain words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience.

7 Be not ye therefore partakers with them.

Nearly all of the new "Bibles" have followed the Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}edition (1941) by altering ["followers" ]{.class_4096}to "imitators." This [In His Steps]{.class_4095} type of business is clearly stated in the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961), which often is more honest than the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}in its clear statement of the Liberal's theology. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}says that we must try to be[ "like"]{.class_4095} God (also ["Good News"]{.class_4095}). Phillips suggests that we ["copy"]{.class_4095} God, while the [Amplified ]{.class_4095}(with the [RV]{.class_4095}, [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RSV]{.class_4095}, [New ASV]{.class_4095}, [New Jerusalem Bible]{.class_4095}, [New American Bible]{.class_4095}, Kenneth Wuest's [Expanded New Testament,]{.class_4095} and other corrupt publications) has ["imitators." ]{.class_4095}These renderings are "most unfortunate," to use the hypocritical cliche of the revisers (who, if they said what they meant, would say: "I hate this [AV ]{.class_4095}reading and can't stand it!").

An IMITATOR is a [counterfeiter]{.class_4095}. An [imitation ]{.class_4095}Bible ([ASV, RV, RSV, NAB, NEB]{.class_4095}, AWOL, CIA, FDR, RFD, etc.) is not the real thing. An [imitation ]{.class_4095}church (Rev. 17) is not the Body of Christ. An [imitation ]{.class_4095}apostle (2 Cor. 11:13--15) is an ambassador of the Devil, and the greatest "imitator" of God who ever lived [is Satan]{.class_4095} (see [Mark of the Beast, ]{.class_4095}1960, chaps. 3--4).

We are to FOLLOW God (text) and FOLLOW Paul (see 1 Cor. 11:1), and we are to "imitate" nobody. We are to follow God in His ways of forgiveness (4:32), we are to follow Christ in His example of suffering (1 Pet. 2:21), and we are to follow Paul in matters of testimony (1 Cor. 11:1).

We are to "imitate" nobody. The "imitators" are the Catholics and Liberals who originated, presented, publicized, and promoted an [imitation ]{.class_4095}Christianity with an [imitation ]{.class_4095}Bible and an [imitation ]{.class_4095}Saviour.

You may discount every translation on the market from 1800 to 2000 on the basis of Ephesians 5:1 alone. A man who would, here, choose "imitate" before "follow," [when both words are given ]{.class_4095}(pg. 200, [Analytical Greek Lexicon]{.class_4095}), has already showed his colors: [he is an imitation Christian.]{.class_4095}

Note that the verb ["walk"]{.class_4096} is used in the context (vs. 2): ["And walk in love\...."]{.class_4096} When you ["walk,"]{.class_4096} you [follow ]{.class_4095}somebody; you do not [imitate ]{.class_4095}them unless you are a [counterfeit ]{.class_4095}trying to pass off as a genuine something-or-other. Thomas á Kempis' "Imitation of Christ" is fine for Catholics and Liberals, but it is no course for a Bible-believing Christian to "imitate" or even consider following. Imitations are a dime a dozen.

["A sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2; 2 Cor. 2:14--15). God was pleased with this sacrifice, and it was a SACRIFICE (Heb. 10:8--12), not a martyr's death. God accepted it as He accepted Noah's (Gen. 8:20--22) and Abel's (Gen. 4:4), but of course, this "offering" spoke of better things than the blood of Abel (Heb. 12:24).

We are not to "imitate" Christ's love, although that is what the [New ASV]{.class_4095} and the Roman Catholic Bible tell us to do; we are to [bring forth love as a fruit of the Holy Spirit ]{.class_4095}(see comments under Gal. 5:22). [Imitation ]{.class_4095}fruit is a dime a dozen. You can hang oranges and apples all over a dead oak tree till it looks like a polka-dot tent, and it still can't [BEAR fruit. ]{.class_4095}(And if you are stupid enough to think that "imitators" is the correct translation of Ephesians 5:1, you are probably dumb enough to think that a [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} is the [King James AV ]{.class_4095}1611!)

["But fornication, and ALL UNCLEANNESS, or covetousness, let it not be once named among you\..."]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_271} (vs. 3). Following the corrupt Jesuit revision of 1881, Lachmann, Tischendorf, Tregelles, Westcott and Hort, and Kenneth Wuest have placed the ["all"]{.class_4096} (Gk. ["pasa"]{.class_4095}) AFTER ["uncleanness,"]{.class_4096} although neither Kurt Aland nor Nestle is willing to give any manuscript evidence for the change. (You may assume that it is a private conjectural emendation of the dead-orthodox apostates based on their own fancy.) Phenomenon No. 3.

Saints are not merely advised against [doing ]{.class_4095}certain things (Acts 15:28), they are further advised to avoid even [discussing ]{.class_4095}them. The three forbidden subjects are ["fornication" ]{.class_4096}(which matches the ["whoremonger"]{.class_4096} of vs. 5), ["uncleanness"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}(which matches the ["unclean person"]{.class_4096} of vs. 5), and ["covetousness"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}(which matches the ["idolater"]{.class_4096} of vs. 5; see Col. 3:5). To match these three sins, represented by three persons, we have three types of conversation:

1\. ["Filthiness"]{.class_4096} for the[ "whoremonger"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4).

2\. ["Foolish talking"]{.class_4096} for the[ "unclean person" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4).

3\. ["Jesting"]{.class_4096} for the ["idolater"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4).

Now the Lord is often sarcastic (Amos 4:4) and often speaks with bitter sarcasm (Mal. 3:15). Furthermore, He deliberately exaggerates (Luke 13:32; Matt. 19:24; Luke 18:25) and often gives out with understatements (Acts 19:23). Again, the Bible doesn't fail to present scores of humorous situations---Genesis 27:22, 31:48--49; 1 Samuel 15:13--14; John 13:4--9; Luke 19:3--4; James 1:23--24, etc. Jesus has His "tongue in His cheek" on more than one occasion---Matthew 5:15, 6:26, 7:9--10, 7:15--16, 16:3; Luke 10:21. A sanctified sense of humor is a great asset to any Christian and especially to a minister or an apostle (note Acts 16:37).

But "foolish talking" and "jesting" refer to a more trifling, banal type of conversation. Ribaldry, buffoonery, trifling witticisms without point and false refinement are the things referred to. There are conversations that are composed of nothing but nasty, stupid, silly, flippant remarks; and they edify no one. A laugh is needed occasionally to get one through the rough places in life (Prov. 17:22), but getting a laugh just for the sake of getting a laugh---Skelton, "Laugh In," Gleason, Benny, Hope, Abbott, Costello, Ed Wynn, Gomer Pyle, Joe Penner, Smothers Brothers, Fred Allen, Flip []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_272}Wilson, Lucy, Jonathan Winters, Dave Gardner, Danny Kaye, Sal Mineo, the Three Stooges, etc.---is something else. Somebody asked a famous comedian if he had no other ambition in life than just to make people laugh. He replied that he had and that it was a very serious ambition. When asked what it was, he said, "I have always wanted to throw an egg into an electric fan." Winters, Gardner, and Alan King (as Will Rogers) are often firing pointed daggers which drive home a truth while the recipient is doubled-up in laughter; this is passable for the Christian. Sam Jones, Dwight Moody, and Billy Sunday all had a sanctified wit which they used for the glory of God.

Nearly all "jesting" winds up in "dirty" jokes, and one does not have to listen to many comedians through a period of years to trace the degenerative power of the old corrupt humus. Bob Newhart on his first "long play" is not the Bob Newhart who emerged from the TV series. The Red Skelton of the 1940s was not the Red Skelton of the 1960s. As Will Rodgers once remarked after hearing a filthy story told by an after-dinner speaker, "I guess his mind is like a race horse. It runs best on a dirt track." If it's "doubtful," it's dirty. Little pornographic "best sellers" by Dr. David Reuben do not hit the White Throne Judgment in any better condition than [Playboy]{.class_4095}, even if a medical doctor wrote them. [No medical doctor has any business telling everything he knows about everyone any more than a lawyer or a priest does.]{.class_4095} Dr. Reuben (like Adolph Hitler) was a cheap opportunist.

It is not "becoming" for saints to talk like the leaders of SIECUS and the SDS, and it is not ["convenient"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4) any more than the doubtful things of 1 Corinthians 6:12. It will serve as an antidote for aimless conversations that eventually become morbid and vulgar. ["Giving of thanks"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4) is always becoming for a saint, and it is always "in place."
