["For this ye know\...hath any inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5; cf. comments under Gal. 5:21). Williams has well noted that this verse messes up some Catholic theology. The Mohammedan teaching and the Papal teaching is that soldiers who "die in combat for their country" get an automatic passport to Heaven. To the contrary, you cannot even get a passport to the Kingdom of God if you practice the vices listed above (see []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_273}Heb. 12:16--17). Covetousness is classified with immorality, vice, sensuality, and whoremongering. Exactly where this puts 99 percent of the world's "kingdom builders" is not hard to guess. There is not one prayer in the Bible for "peace to come on this earth" apart from the Advent of the Lord Jesus Christ, and those who covet peace and prosperity for "all free men everywhere" are IDOLATERS! You were told to have ["NO OTHER GODS"]{.class_4096} (Exod. 20:3) before ["the true God"]{.class_4096} (1 John 5:20). He did not say "bad gods"; He said ["NO OTHER GODS"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 22:37). Peace and prosperity (with [sex ]{.class_4095}and [education]{.class_4095}) are the leading gods of all "one worlders."

["Let no man deceive you with vain words"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6).[ "Many"]{.class_4096} will be deceived (Matt. 24:5, 24) because ["many false prophets"]{.class_4096} will arise (Matt. 24:11) and ["deceive many"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 24:5) and ["many"]{.class_4096} will follow them (Matt. 24:10).

["Cometh the wrath of God upon the children of disobedience"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6; cf. 2:2 and comments). ["The wrath of God"]{.class_4096}---not "dreadful judgment" ([NEB]{.class_4095}, 1961)---falls on unsaved people who covet, fornicate, and live filthy lives (Gal. 6:7). What SIECUS, Joe Fletcher, the HEW board, the NEA, Hugh Hefner, Freud, and David Reuben say about the matter is immaterial (Gal. 6:7), for they, as the seducers of 2 Timothy 3:13, have been deceiving others for so long they are self-hypnotized themselves. Their opinions are to be considered with the same amount of respect that one would study the opinions of a two-year-old Hottentot on IBM computers.

The warning in the next verse, ["Be not ye therefore partakers with them,"]{.class_4096} warns the Christian not only to abstain from the acts listed in verses 3--5, but to avoid fellowship with anyone who does those things or who tries to talk you into thinking that God will overlook those things. He will not overlook them (cf. vs. 11). To make the younger generation think that God WILL overlook them, the following help has been enlisted by educators and scientists.

1\. A whole series of "realistic" novels which emphasize [luck ]{.class_4095}instead of [cause ]{.class_4095}and [effect]{.class_4095}: Stephen Crane, E. M. Remarque, Henry James, Faulkner, James Jones, Norman Mailer, Maugham, James Baldwin, Hemingway, Steinbeck, etc.

2\. A whole shelf full of philosophers who devoted their lives to proving that nothing was good or bad [in itself]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_274}, and that there were no absolute moral standards: Leibnitz, Sarte, Huxley, Peirce, Hegel, Dewey, Whitehead, Russell, Freud, Spencer, Nietzsche, et al.

3\. A College curriculum for every State College in America that refuses even to MENTION Divine retribution or Divine omniscience or personal accountability to God.

4\. A zoo full of Darwinian anthropoids who are only accountable to "nature" for their misdeeds: Darwin, Lyell, Paley, Huxley, Haeckel, Einstein, Comte, Fichte, Hobbes, Lamarck, and the United Nations Assembly.

Over and against these stands the [Authorized King James 1611]{.class_4095} Holy Bible. It says that ["the wrath of God"]{.class_4096} is upon those who practice these things, and that ["the wrath of God"]{.class_4096} is on the Christ rejector (John 3:36). The empirical-existential-atomistic-critical-positivistic PROOF that the [AV ]{.class_4095}is infallible in the matter is 6,000 years of insane asylums, field hospitals, first-aid stations, penitentiaries, concentration camps, "tiger cages," jails, and graveyards (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_4095}, 1969). ["Be not YE therefore partakers with them"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7).

5:8 For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord: walk as children of light:

9 (For the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth;)

10 Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord.

11 And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather reprove them.

12 For it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret.

13 But all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light: for whatsoever doth make manifest is light.

14 Wherefore he saith, Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.

15 See then that ye walk circumspectly, not as fools, but as wise,

16 Redeeming the time, because the days are evil.

17 Wherefore be ye not unwise, but understanding what []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_275}the will of the Lord is.

18 And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess; but be filled with the Spirit;

The entire passage is on the Christian's relation to the unsaved people in the world around him. It is an exhortation for separation and sanctification, and it points out the difference between a dead-orthodox apostate who is sound asleep in the cemetery (vs. 14) and the Christian who is "following" (not "imitating"!) the Lord in a circumspect walk (vs. 15).

["For ye were sometimes darkness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8; cf. 1 Thess. 5:1--9). Ephesians 2:11--12 defines the expression, and it is elaborated on in John 1:1--10. ["But now are ye LIGHT in the Lord"]{.class_4096} (cf. 1 Thess. 5:5; Rom. 13:12). ["Walk as CHILDREN OF LIGHT,"]{.class_4096} as contrasted with ["children of disobedience"]{.class_4096} in verse 6.

["Children of light"]{.class_4096} implies the following:

1\. The light of the world is Jesus Christ.

2\. The word of God is light.

3\. The children have been [born ]{.class_4095}of the light.

4\. A man is born again by the word and by the Lord Jesus.

5\. A man must receive [spiritual light ]{.class_4095}before this can take place.

6\. A man thus born should love the light after his New Birth.

7\. A man thus born should [follow ]{.class_4095}the light and [walk ]{.class_4095}in the light.

There is no darkness in the city to which the Christian is traveling (Rev. 21:25); he, therefore, should love light rather than darkness (John 3:19--20). (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown and Williams have some Scriptural comments \[for a change\] to make on the passage.) The ["children of light,"]{.class_4096} here, are never to be confused with the apocryphal "sons of light" and "sons of darkness" mentioned in the Dead Sea Fairy Tales.

["For the FRUIT of the Spirit is in all goodness..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). The verse plainly shows that the word "imitators" should never have been used in verse 1---see any corrupt translation since 1700. You CANNOT "bear fruit" by [imitating ]{.class_4095}anything. When you ["follow the Lord"]{.class_4095} and "walk in the light" (vs. 8), you bear fruit; but the dead-orthodox apostates who translated 5:1 as "imitators" have no fruit, at least not[ "the fruit of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9).

["By their FRUITS ye shall know them"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_276} (Matt. 7:20), and the [fruits ]{.class_4095}of the Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Berkeley, Phillips,[ New Jerusalem, New American, RV, ASV, RSV, New ASV]{.class_4095}, Fenton, Lamsa, [NEB]{.class_4095}, etc., are:

1\. Lack of belief in the authority of the Bible.

2\. Lack of confidence in the Bible.

3\. Indifference to many Bible doctrines.

4\. Abandonment of Biblical Christianity.

5\. A wholesale return to Rome or a defection to Communism.

6\. A generation of preachers who don't know their mouths from a hole in the ground. (This is not the ["original,"]{.class_4095} but it is a ["reliable translation"]{.class_4095}!)

["Proving what is acceptable unto the Lord"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10). Well, any translation just mentioned is not ["acceptable unto the Lord,"]{.class_4096} and the fact that any 35 of them COMBINED cannot replace the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 should be a fair notice from the Lord that He thinks they STINK. But "hope springs eternal in the human breast," so with the same attitude that Darwin and the Postmillennialists had (see [Which Bible?]{.class_4095}, David Otis Fuller, chap. 6), the modern "Fundamentalist" goes right on "proving what is acceptable to the Devil." [ANY NEW TRANSLATION IS ACCEPTABLE TO THE DEVIL, AND HE WILL USE ALL OF THEM.]{.class_4095}

["What is acceptable UNTO THE LORD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10). The Lord always places near some verse like this--- something that will cause the dead-orthodox apostates and their Jesuit friends to ["stumble at the word" ]{.class_4096}(1 Pet. 2:8). Therefore, the reader should give careful attention to the construction of the context. He has been talking about the separation of the ["children of light"]{.class_4096} from ["the children of disobedience"]{.class_4096} and the absence of any fellowship between them. When these admonitions are [obeyed]{.class_4095}, the Holy Spirit in the believer can "bear fruit" (cf. 2 Cor. 6:14). Since every translator since 1880 had ["fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11) and violated the commandment of verse 7, they had to do [something ]{.class_4095}with the passage. They did it!

You will be surprised (or perhaps not too surprised) to find that the word ["Spirit"]{.class_4096} has been knocked clean out of verse 9, and that the word ["photos"]{.class_4095} (light) has been inserted against the authority of []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_277}the oldest papyrus manuscript extant---P46! Phenomenon No. 2.

"Fruit of the light" is NOT a Pauline expression. It is NOT Paul's style. And as we have already noted (vs. 17), a real "modern, scientific investigator" will never hesitate to LIE outright in dealing with an author's [style]{.class_4095}. There is no such thing as ["fruit of the light"]{.class_4095} anywhere [in either Testament]{.class_4095}. The expression is arbitrary fabrication, pure and simple, and is divorced from any truth revealed in any of the 31,000-plus verses in the Bible. The corrupters who make Paul use this non-biblical, non-Pauline expression are now listed:

1\. The [RV ]{.class_4095}(1881).

2\. The [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901).

3\. The [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1963).

4\. The [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946).

5\. Phillips (1958).

6\. [NEB]{.class_4095} (1961).

7\. Jesuit Bible (1582).

8\. Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}(1941).

9\. Williams (Commentary, pg. 926).

10\. Blaikie ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20, pg. 209).

11\. Dummelow.

12\. [The Roman Catholic Latin Vulgate of the Dark Ages.]{.class_4095}

You see, no modern "Bible" printed since 1611 is a "Holy Bible." The English Bibles of 1525, 1535, 1539, 1540, 1560, 1562, 1568, and 1602 read as the [AV]{.class_4095} of 1611: ["For the fruit of the Spirit is\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 9).

[There has not been one revision of the Holy Bible since 1800. ]{.class_4095}The new "Bibles" are [the restored text of the Roman Catholic Vulgate ]{.class_4095}(via Origen in Alexandria, Egypt); and if your teacher, preacher, pastor, or professor doesn't know that, he is either lazy, ignorant, or crooked. (All three of these qualities are listed as SINS in the book of Ephesians!)

Having eliminated the Author of Ephesians (the Holy Spirit) and replaced Him with the abstract neuter---"Light"---the new "Bibles" now step into an exegetical quagmire (vs. 13) and come out with fifteen separate private interpretations out of twenty corrupt translations.

["And have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11). The works are so connected with the workers (see 1 Cor. 6:9--10) that when the works are "reproved" (text), the workers are reproved (vs. 12).

["For it is a shame even to speak of those things WHICH ARE DONE OF THEM IN SECRET"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). The Christian should not partake of the "sins of other men" (1 Tim. 5:22), and the only "speaking" he should do, is when he reproves ["those things which are done of them in secret"]{.class_4096} and rebukes the one who practices them (1 Tim. 5:20).

The word ["reprove"]{.class_4096} has been altered to "expose" by ["Good News,"]{.class_4095} the [NEB]{.class_4095}, and the [RSV]{.class_4095}, in line with the Roman Catholic Bible of 1941. The reader should notice that the subject and object of the reproving is ["THINGS"]{.class_4096} (vss. 11--13). You do not have to ["speak of those things which are done of them in secret"]{.class_4096} to REPROVE them. As a matter of fact, all you have to do [is turn on the light]{.class_4095} (John 3:17--21)! John the Baptist reproves Herod (Luke 3:19) without going into detail about the activities of Herod's bedroom. The content matter of a real preacher's sermon (2 Tim. 4:2) is a "reproof" to the works (and the workers) of darkness. This means that the main subject matter of Ephesians 5:11--13 is [the preaching of the word of God under the power of the Holy Spirit]{.class_4095} or the public witness of the word of God from one person to another. Have you ever noticed how unpopular Bible verses are in a night club or at a Rotarian's luncheon?

You see, all of the commentators took the "light" (which they inserted for the Holy Spirit in vs. 9) to be somebody [imitating Christ's life]{.class_4095}! Phillips states this modernistic theology perfectly, and the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} assents (pg. 1313---that's quite a number!). The gist of the new "Bibles" and dead-orthodox apostate commentators is that the Christian should shut his mouth tightly and let his LIFE rebuke and expose the works and workers of darkness. This explains why the word was changed from ["reprove"]{.class_4096} to "expose" (Dummelow) and why the [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095} includes [both words ]{.class_4095}so as not to offend the Liberal or the Lord!

Having taken this approach on the runway, the planes now land, coming in from all directions north, south, east, and west. If you are []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_279}looking for "clarity in Bible exposition," don't look for it in the pile-up that now takes place!

1\. If a thing is clearly revealed, it BECOMES light (Phillips).

2\. Anything illumined is ALL LIGHT! ([NEB]{.class_4095}).

3\. Light MIGHT turn something it shines on into light (Phillips).

4\. If anything is [visible]{.class_4095}, it is light ([RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946)! (How about DUNG, doctor?)

5\. [Everything ]{.class_4095}made plain is light (Kenneth Wuest). (How about "Peanuts" by Shultz?)

6\. Anything [enlightened ]{.class_4095}is light (Olshausen). (Buddha?)

7\. You are an [enlightened ]{.class_4095}one if you are made manifest (Bengel).

8\. What you can perform openly is [light ]{.class_4095}(Rendall).

9\. Light is the element which makes all things clear ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}).

10\. Everything made manifest is [light ]{.class_4095}(Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown).

Do you at last understand why the Roman Catholic Church has taught for fifteen centuries that the Bible is "hard to understand" and that you must come to "Mother Church" for the correct interpretation?

Isn't it a pity that God couldn't speak as clearly as Lange, Ellicott, Clarke, Meyer, Wordsworth, Alford, Trench, Lightfoot, and ["Living Letters"]{.class_4095}? How do you reckon the Trinity was able to get along between 1611 and 1880 without all of these "helps to the reader"? Isn't it a shame that Wesley, Whitefield, Frelinghuysen, Tennant, Webb, Evans, Livingstone, Cartwright, Morrison, Culpepper, Sam Jones, Bob Jones Sr., Frank Norris, Mordecai Ham, Beecher, Torrey, Talmage, McCheyenne, Finney, Spurgeon, Moody, Sunday, and Martin Luther didn't have access to the brilliant expositions of these erudite scholars? My, what a revival we have coming with all of this improved translating! If the Reformation stirred the world for Christ, imagine what an "outpouring of the Spirit" we are going to have under Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Fenton, the [ASV]{.class_4095}, the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, the Gorgeous New ASV, and the [Official Standard Ecumenical Accredited Restored American Revised Standard United Nations' Roman-Protestant VERSION!]{.class_4095}

Shall we lay aside the kiddy cars, rattles, play pens, and pacifiers []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_280}and see if the Lord God of Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Peter, James, John, and Paul has something for us?

1\. If a rebuke or reproof makes a thing [manifest]{.class_4095}, then that rebuke or reproof is like a [light ]{.class_4095}shining on the hidden object.

2\. ["The light"]{.class_4096} of verse 13 is the word of God. Obscene how every commentator and reviser since 1611 ignored the GENDER of ["whatsoever"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13)---Greek: [phaneroumenon.]{.class_4095}

3\. The light is the ["truth"]{.class_4096} (John 17:17), and the [neuter ]{.class_4095}truth is the [word of God ]{.class_4095}(John 6:68) composed of WORDS which are ["life"]{.class_4096} and ["spirit"]{.class_4096} (John 6:63).

Where the Christian shuts his mouth, the Light of the world should give light around him. But in the text (vs. 13), he is to [open his mouth; when he does, he speaks ]{.class_4095}["as the oracles of God"]{.class_4096} (1 Pet. 4:11).

Since not one of these translators, revisers, commentators, or teachers since 1880 took the [AV ]{.class_4095}seriously, not one of them could read the text in English [or Greek ]{.class_4095}as it stood. This accounts for the numerous alterations of the "scholars" on the passage. A scalded cat runs in any direction.

Christ does not shine "ON" you ([NEB]{.class_4095}, Phillips, [RV]{.class_4095}, [ASV]{.class_4095}), but this is the logical terminus of the Bible perverter who takes the word ["light"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13) to be a reference to the Word. (Cf. any work by Barth, Brunner, or any introduction to any new translation on the market.) ["Christ shall give thee light"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14), but this ["light"]{.class_4096} is the BOOK I HAVE IN MY HAND (Psa. 119:105).

Note how carefully the Holy Spirit---who was "eliminated in the semi-finals" (vs. 9) by the Fundamentalists and Conservatives---has guarded the truth of the word: ["Proving what is acceptable"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10), ["understanding what the will of the Lord is"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). The context has been bracketed by admonitions to [find out what God wants]{.class_4095}. Tell me something, Doctor. [Where does a man go to find out what God wants]{.class_4095}? (Well, he sure doesn't go to an [American Standard Version]{.class_4095} or an [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095}!) (Isn't it remarkable how clear the "archaic Elizabethan English" is along side "the living language in the living idiom of the modern man"?)

The Christian is to search the word of God for truth and light, and he is to put this light on the works and workers around him who are in darkness. This light will "make manifest" [works ]{.class_4095}and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_281}[workers ]{.class_4095}(John 3:17--21). The Christian "sleeper" (any scholar since 1900 of any profession, with the possible exceptions of J. J. Ray, Benjamin Wilkinson, David Otis Fuller, and Dr. Edward Hills) who is lying down in the hole with the DEAD (vs. 14) orthodox apostates is told to get out of the dirt and the Lord will show him a thing or two he couldn't learn trying to "find the originals." The wise Christian (vs. 15) is not ["unwise" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17), but walks circumspectly (carefully---vs. 15) and uses his time wisely (vs. 16) ["because the days are evil"]{.class_4096} (regardless of the optimistic viewpoint taken by the ecumenical council, the United Nations, the Vatican, and other "kingdom building" organizations).

Peter Cartwright knew how to apply verses 11--13. At a barn dance in Kentucky, the itinerant circuit-rider put some "light" on the works and workers of darkness that E. Schuyler English, Gaebelein, Thieme, and Wuest couldn't find. When Peter entered the barn during a driving rainstorm, a young snip came up to him and invited him to dance with her. (Peter in his early manhood was a handsome, dark-eyed, dark-haired giant.) He responded by seizing her hand in a grip like a vise and saying very loudly: "Ah would be glad to accept this here dance with you, young woman, but ah makes it a practice nevah to do anything without first seekin' the Lord's blessing on it!" Whereupon he knelt-in the middle of the dance floor, and in a voice like thunder, he prayed one of those old circuit-riding, camp-meeting prayers that are now considered to be "in extremely bad taste" by the faculty members of Bob Jones, Tennessee Temple, Southeastern, Southwestern, Dallas, Fuller, Biola, Columbia Bible College, Philadelphia, Pillsbury, and Union Theological Seminary. ["Oh, God, have mercy on these poor, Hell-bound\...!"]{.class_4095} The young lady struggled like a hooked eel to get away, but to no avail. The dancing stopped, the music stopped, several of the musicians dropped their instruments and "absquattulated," and several professing Christians by the "refreshment stand" began to look around like they were trying to find their family Bible. Peter Cartwright arose from the floor and preached 40 minutes. There were 14 people saved and a church was started in the barn. (That is what verses 11--13 mean, and any interpretation that doesn't put that kind of a construction on the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_282}passage is a nonentity.)

Verse 14, as 1 Corinthians 15:34, is speaking of dead CHRISTIANS, not sinners ["dead in trespasses and sins."]{.class_4096} Dead [Christians ]{.class_4095}are characterized by great faith in science, philosophy, scholarship, linguists, education, and tradition---and no FRUIT. Our text tells them to wake up (vs. 14), or they will blow up (vs. 16; cf. Gal. 1:4; Rom. 13:11; Col. 4:5).

The paraphrase (vs. 14) is from Isaiah 60:1, and the reader should observe again that Paul does not hesitate to apply the sayings of the prophets [to the Body of Christ.]{.class_4095} This by itself is sufficient proof that Bullinger, Stam, Baker, and O'Hair were NOT followers of Paul or Pauline teaching. Pauline teaching often applies the Old Testament to the Body of the "mystery." "Present truth" is not Ephesians; "present truth" for the Christian is [Genesis to Revelation.]{.class_4095}

["Redeeming the time"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16). The admonition makes it clear that it is a sin for a Christian to waste time. Kill time and it will kill you. Someone has written: "Lost, one golden hour studded with 60 diamond minutes. There is no reward offered for it, for it is lost forever." "Time will get us all," said an ancient writer. Ben Johnson had these words on his watch: "Work, for the night is coming when no man can work" (see John 9:4). Sir William Russell handed his watch to the executioner when he mounted the scaffold and said, "You may have this; I will no longer need it. From now on I will be dealing with eternity." (For further study on ["redeeming the time,"]{.class_4096} cf. comments under Prov. 18:9 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972).

A preacher can memorize Scripture while he is shaving. A woman can study the Bible while drying her hair. A man can read tracts while eating lunch. A man can pray while he is answering his mail. A man can witness while he is coming home from work. A preacher's wife can clip out sermon illustrations while he is on visitation. It is possible to write out names, addresses, and telephone numbers while one is talking over the phone about something else.

A man who lives doing one thing at a time is [wasting ]{.class_4095}time. A man who spends an hour a day watching TV has ruined 7 percent of his life. Any man with one issue of [U.S. News and World Report]{.class_4095} could []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_283}pick up anything in the way of "news" in less than 30 minutes, and the items would cover every event for two weeks. Newscasts are nothing but repetition of old news. (And 80 percent of that was not really "news" when it was picked up and misrepresented to appear sensational.)

It would be safe to say that any educated man in America could listen to one 15 minute newscast a week, and he would miss nothing of any importance. What people ignorantly mistake for "relevant issues" are nothing but the news media trying to make a living by stirring up and developing [controversial ]{.class_4095}issues. There have not been ten major events in world politics or economics in the last year. The news media merely created the impression that something big was going on.

Wars are not really [news]{.class_4095}; they are the daily way of living and have been for more than 5,000 years. Riots and revolutions are to be expected in any community or nation that goes by the standards of science and education instead of the Bible. Death and disease are the standard law of life and always have been, and depressions and inflation are the natural result of a nation abandoning the word of God (Prov. 14:34).

If the really "newsworthy" items had been stacked up through the Twentieth Century, they would have totalled about 700 items. That is 700 for seventy years; ten per year is a high average. One newscast every month would take care of the matter without much trouble. But how can you make a living doing that!?

Christians are notorious wasters of time. The industry of men like William Carey and women like Wesley's mother is a rebuke to the Twentieth-Century Christian. Livingstone worked a twelve hour day as a boy and then went to school three hours every night. Edison got by on four hours of sleep a night. The modern "Christian" spends that much time watching soap operas and ball games!

We waste time when we play games of chance. (Games of skill develop brains, reflexes, and coordination.) We waste time when we read too many novels, for the word [novel]{.class_4095} simply means something that is not true. We waste time when we sit around over coffee and cake gossiping about other Christians, and we waste time when we spend more than 30 minutes with a secular newspaper or magazine. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_284}There isn't 30 minutes worth of sound reading in any daily newspaper in the world. [Look ]{.class_4095}and [Life ]{.class_4095}are about as "worthwhile" for a child of God as the Karma Sutra and [Droll Stories]{.class_4095} (Balzac).

["The will of the Lord"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17) comes in three categories (see Rom. 12:2) and is knowable (Col. 4:12).

["And be not drunk with wine, wherein is excess"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). [I'll give you three guesses what is going to happen. ]{.class_4095}No, that is too much charity! I'll give you ONE guess.

If you were a dead-orthodox apostate leaning toward Rome, what would YOU do with the verse? Well, you would make [some attempt ]{.class_4095}to keep wine in your pantry exactly like the Pope did when he had his Old Testament (any edition of any Catholic Bible) end with a verse on WINE DRINKING! (See Prov. 20:1, 23:31, and comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972.)

1\. Getting drunk is [debauchery]{.class_4095}, but wine is all right as long as you don't get drunk ([RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946)!

2\. The same reading came from the Roman Catholic [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}of 1941.

3\. Don't ["get drunk"]{.class_4095} ([Amplified Version]{.class_4095}) because "getting drunk" is debauchery.

4\. It is the ["state of intoxication"]{.class_4095} that is dangerous (Wuest, 1961).

5\. The excess is not in the wine but only in "wrong [use ]{.class_4095}of the wine" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown).

6\. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}and ["Good News"]{.class_4095} flinch, hit, waver, pass, and feint at the verse without being able to go clearly one way or another.

But how could any scholar translate ANY Greek text properly if [his church used intoxicating liquor in the Communion service]{.class_4095}? The trouble has never been with tense, voice, gender, case, and "articles." The problem has always been with winoes and liquor heads who rejected Isaiah 5:22; Matthew 26:29; Deuteronomy 29:6; and Genesis 40:10--11. First Timothy 5:23 is not "[wine ]{.class_4095}used aright" (see Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown Commentary, pg. 1295), for that passage deals with WINE AND WATER, as in medicine with an alcoholic content. You see, when a man wants to "imbibe," he cannot even figure out one syllable words. [Wine ]{.class_4095}is not "wine and water mixed." Nor does the reference in Ephesians have anything to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_285}do with sick Christians suffering from stomach troubles! (Phenomenon No. 17.)

No, the ["excess"]{.class_4096} is in the [wine]{.class_4095}, not the [drunkenness]{.class_4095}. Observe how clearly the "original" (to quote the ancient lie) brings this out---Greek: ["oivo en ho."]{.class_4095} This is the reading of [every Greek manuscript ]{.class_4095}in all four families, [uncial or cursive]{.class_4095}. However, this time, Kenneth Wuest decides that [his English]{.class_4095} is better than the [Greek text]{.class_4095} he has been using for nine books to correct the Reformation Bible! Phenomenon No. 5.

["But BE FILLED WITH THE SPIRIT"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18; cf. Gal. 4:19 and comments). You are going to be ["filled"]{.class_4096} with something (Luke 2:40; John 16:6; Rom. 1:29, 15:14; 2 Cor. 7:4; Acts 2:4, 3:10, 5:17, 13:45, etc.), so you might as well be filled with the Holy Spirit. If you are filled with the Spirit, you will resemble a drunken man (Acts 2:13). You will have boldness (1 Kings 22:14!). You will want to share your bottle" (John 2:10) with everyone (Hab. 2:15). You won't be able to keep your mouth shut (Acts 2:18), and your "liquor" will affect your WALK! (Watchman Nee has an excellent work on the verse entitled [The Release of the Spirit.]{.class_4095} A Christian doesn't quit drinking! He just changes bottles!)

A Spirit-filled Christian is the rarest animal found on earth today. Having learned how to counterfeit ["the fruit of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 5:22) without the [filling ]{.class_4095}of the Spirit, modern Conservatives and Fundamentalists have raised a generation of Christians who appear to be Spirit-filled; but the appearance is merely the result of applied psychology, Christian education, moral discipline, cultivation of personality, and artificial facial expressions. The modern Christian is "nice." He is not effective. He is a "great guy," but he has no power that Norman Vincent Peale and Dale Carnegie did not have. Neither of them professed to know anything about the New Birth or the book of Ephesians.

5:19 Speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;

20 Giving thanks always for all things unto God and the Father in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ;

21 Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God.

22 Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as unto the Lord.

23 For the husband is the head of the wife, even as Christ is the head of the church: and he is the saviour of the body.

24 Therefore as the church is subject unto Christ, so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing.

25 Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it;

26 That he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,

27 That he might present it to himself a glorious church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without blemish.

28 So ought men to love their wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his wife loveth himself.

29 For no man ever yet hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it, even as the Lord the church:

30 For we are members of his body, of his flesh, and of his bones.

31 For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and shall be joined unto his wife, and they two shall be one flesh.

32 This is a great mystery: but I speak concerning Christ and the church.

33 Nevertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself; and the wife see that she reverence her husband.

The main section deals with the relationship of the Church to Christ, and this is likened to the relationship between a man and his wife. The first two verses are still dealing with the Spirit-filled life.

["Speaking to yourselves in PSALMS and HYMNS and SPIRITUAL SONGS"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19). Speaking and singing (vs. 19) are included in the exhortation, and both are recommended as an expression of the joy that comes from being []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_287}["filled with the Spirit"]{.class_4096} instead of being filled with wine. Phillip's lame ["let the Spirit stimulate your souls" ]{.class_4095}is not a translation of ["be filled with the Spirit"]{.class_4096} at all. It is Phillip's sermon note on an idea he got from reading the Bible.

The [Amplified Version]{.class_4095} is fearful of the "Campbellite" overtones of the verse, since it has been used for eighty years as a "prooftext" by the water-dogs to prove that musical instruments are not to be used in church services. So the [Amplified ]{.class_4095}has inserted ["and instruments"]{.class_4095} into the text! If this is legitimate, how about the following: ["Singing, dancing, rocking, twisting, frugging, and rolling, with guitars and electronic devices, making acid rock and hard rock with your torso to the great new 'Christianity'"?]{.class_4095} Why stop with ONE addition (cf. comments under Gal. 5:9)?

Three kinds of music come under the heading of "Christian music."

1\. ["Psalms"]{.class_4096}: these were arranged in poetic form in the old hymnals of the Presbyterian church. The Lord Jesus sang one of the Psalms the last night He was on the earth before His crucifixion.

2\. ["Hymns"]{.class_4096}: these are collections of sacred songs that have become established through the years. They form a sort of Christian [classical ]{.class_4095}music.

3\. ["Spiritual songs"]{.class_4096}: these appear as "solos, duets, trios, and quartet" music and are contemporary with each decade. Among them are "It Took a Miracle," "It's Real," "Ship Ahoy," "The Great Judgment Morning," "I Am Amazed," "He Touched Me," "Till the Storm Passes By," "Only a Vine," "The Stranger of Galilee," etc.

Spiritual songs may include most of the works of Bach, Handel, and Haydn. This is apparent to anyone who spends much time with classical music (cf. Gen. 4:21 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_4095} 1970).

The Christian's "singing" and "melodies" are to come from his heart (vs. 19), not his mouth (see comments under 2:4). They are to be ["to the Lord"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19) like everything else (Col. 3:23). The everlasting howling and wailing about the wife who left the note on the table, the drunkard about to jump out the window, the murderer hiding in Miller's cave, the respectable prostitute before the Harper []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_288}Valley PTA, the ring found in the wrecked automobile, the dancing girls at "Sam's Place," and the shimmy-shaking, mini-skirted whore walking down the street are NOT the outbursts of the Spirit-filled life. (You would think that this would be apparent, but modern "youth movements" are attracting their crowds and becoming "meaningfully involved" only by incorporating African sex music into their "worship services." They are "spirit-filled," but of course, there always have been [two spirits]{.class_4095}! See comments under 2:2.)

You see, when you sing "There is no other way but the way of the cross," you must finish with "then I bid farewell [to the way of the world]{.class_4095}." You cannot sing "Beneath the cross of Jesus I fain would take my stand," unless you finish the selection with ["content to let the world go by."]{.class_4095} And there is no use singing "My Jesus I love Thee, I know Thou art mine," when you are unwilling to sing "for Thee all the follies of sin I RESIGN." [A positive Christianity without a negative counterbalance is the religion of Satan.]{.class_4095}

["Giving thanks always for all things"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20; cf. 1 Thess. 5:18). ["Submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21). (See lengthy comments on "the fear of God" in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus,]{.class_4095} Exod. 20:20, and [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_4095} Prov. 1:7.)

At this point, Rev. Salmond steps forward to tell us that the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 "must give way" before the ancient Alexandrian corruption found in the Roman Catholic [Vulgate]{.class_4095}. The Jesuit priests have written[ "Christ"]{.class_4095} for ["God"]{.class_4096} in Ephesians 5:21, and this is the standard Roman Catholic Latin reading. It was revived by Westcott and Hort, Schaff, and Lightfoot in 1881, after lying where it belonged---in the garbage---for more than eighteen centuries. The expression ["the fear of Christ"]{.class_4095} is obviously a Roman cliché, and it is as foreign to Paul's style and manner of expressing himself as could possibly be imagined.

This obvious forgery (["the fear of Christ"]{.class_4095}) is found in the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and similar corrupt publications, and it is found in [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}. The expression is a true "original," for it is no more connected with Pauline writing than an expression like "total involvement" would be connected with the writing style of Victor Hugo.

Paul wouldn't have thought to say such a thing, and if he [had thought of it]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_289}, he certainly would never have written it down. LTTrGrWHRVELiMeGoAbSchonLipOlSalLueRidDeWRoHaVinWo (to cite the [Cross Reference Bible]{.class_4095}, which uses the [ASV ]{.class_4095}text!) may have spent a combined 400 years studying the Pauline epistles, but they certainly never learned enough about Paul's style to identify his writing at fourteen inches! The [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1963) also approves of this Roman Catholic corruption of text.

["Wives, submit\...as unto the Lord"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22). Now if you want a hard verse, there is one! (Cf. Col. 3:18; Gen. 3:16; 1 Pet. 3:1--7.)

Of course, the context is [saved husbands ]{.class_4095}and [saved wives ]{.class_4095}(see vss. 23--26). But even the unsaved man has the authority in his home (1 Pet. 3:1--2), and those who cannot "rule" the roost, "ruin" the roost. Many a family stayed together only on the agreement that the woman would make 51 percent of the decisions.

An ancient legend states that St. Peter set up two lines at the gate of heaven: one was for men who ran their own families, and the other for men whose wives ran them. Only one man was standing in the line for men who ran their own families. When St. Peter asked him why he was standing there, he said, "Because my wife told me to stand over here." (You know, "the man is 'the head of the house,' but the NECK turns the head," and "the views expressed by the head of the house are not always those of the MANAGEMENT," etc.)

Now, the [first ]{.class_4095}admonition was impartial. It was for [all Christians ]{.class_4095}to submit to each other (vs. 21). However, when dealing with a husband and wife situation, the first admonition is always given to the WOMAN (note, Gen. 3:13, 16; Col. 3:18; and 1 Pet. 3:1--4). The reason for this is explained in 1 Timothy 2:14.

Submission to [any ]{.class_4095}authority is one of the most disagreeable things about life. It is within human nature to resist authority of every kind and never submit unless forced to. Even where women desire to be ruled (Gen. 3:16), it is only to alleviate themselves of the responsibility for making decisions. As one woman said when asked if she had a hard time making up her mind: "Well, [yes ]{.class_4095}and [no]{.class_4095}."

The spirit which causes unregenerate Blacks to commit vandalism on College campuses is the same spirit (Eph. 2:2) that causes Dr. A. T. Robertson to correct the [King James]{.class_4095} [AUTHORIZED ]{.class_4095}text. (I realize that the average "Christian" cannot see this, but the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_290}average Christian is neither mature \[Eph. 4:14\] nor spiritual \[1 Cor. 3:1--3\] nor used to ["strong meat" ]{.class_4096}\[Heb. 5:14\].) [Covetousness ]{.class_4095}is equal to [immorality ]{.class_4095}in God's sight (see Eph. 5:5). The spirit that causes Abbie Hoffmans and Angela Davises and Jerry Reubens to curse parental and police authority is the spirit that caused Kenneth Wuest (Teacher Emeritus, Moody Bible Institute) to strike out Matthew 23:14 and then publish a verse-numbered text that refuses to list the omission! Rebels against authority have the same approach to Bible translating that the Weatherman Organization (1969) had to law enforcement. The man who resents highway patrolmen and policemen with German shepherds (Rom. 13:1--4), is like the man who resents the authority of the [King James 1611]{.class_4095} Bible.

Some women have husbands whose attitude toward them and their children is so unreasonable and intolerable that even the law would condone their NOT submitting. Some women's husbands are so UNLIKE the Lord (vs. 22) that they could picture Tiny Tim as John Wayne before they could picture their husbands as Jesus Christ. Still, ["the husband IS THE HEAD OF THE WIFE,"]{.class_4096} and this is made very clear in 1 Corinthians 11:1--10. The woman with a husband like Errol Flynn or Dean Martin must either "tough it out" (1 Cor. 7:13) or get a temporary separation (1 Cor. 7:11). If she is saved, she must do with prayer and submission (1 Pet. 3:1--4) what she is not allowed to do with tongue and temper. If her husband deserts her (1 Cor. 7:15) or sues for divorce (1 Cor. 7:15), she may get providential release or relief, but unless God works one of these things out, she is to "submit" herself ["as unto the Lord"]{.class_4096} (see Col. 3:18). Many times, this is nearly as difficult as it is for Ham to submit to Japheth (1 Tim. 6:1--4)! Hence, "women's liberation" matches "civil rights" and international Socialism.

The woman "in submission" is the subject of Proverbs 31. The woman "liberated" is the woman described in Proverbs 9:13--18. Since the Bible clearly teaches segregation, obedience, submission, and [spirituality]{.class_4095}, the prime goal of American education is integration, disobedience, liberation, and [carnality]{.class_4095}.

["For the husband is THE HEAD OF THE WIFE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 23; cf. 1 Cor. 11:3). A series of parallels now follow, and the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text presents the comparisons as clear as any fifth-grader could want []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_291}them.

1\. The first man, Adam, had a wife (vs. 31).

2\. She was part of his flesh and bones (vs. 30).

3\. They were ONE flesh, which was MALE flesh (vs. 31, Gen. 5:1--3).

4\. This was the original "ideal marriage" (Matt. 19:3--9), so it is a picture of a proper marriage relationship between two saved people (vs. 22).

5\. It was also a picture of the relation of Christ's Body (vs. 30) to Him and His relation to it (vs. 23).

6\. As Adam died to get his body (Gen. 2:21), so Christ died to get His body (vs. 25).

7\. A man should love (vs. 25), nourish, and cherish his wife (vs. 29) since she is a part of his body (vs. 28).

8\. A man's body should be cleansed and sanctified (vs. 26), and Christ's Body should be holy, glorious, and "without spot or blemish" (vs. 27).

The Christian man should love God enough to die for Him (Rev. 2:10), he should love his brethren enough to die for them (John 13:35), and he should love his wife enough to die for her (vs. 25). This ancient love-death cycle, begun in Genesis, is as perennial as ["wars and rumours of wars"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 24:6). Every woman wants a man who loves her enough to die for her (1 Tim. 2:13), so the sex-murder ("love me enough to kill for me") is the everlasting theme song of the magazine stand. "Smack-smack, bang-bang" describes 90 percent of the content of television.

Christ is the head of the Body (1:22) and the Saviour of the Body (vs. 23).

When Paul says ["as the church is subject unto Christ,"]{.class_4096} he is plainly speaking of an ideal state. The Body of Christ has been about as obedient to its Head as Zsa Zsa Gabor was to her first three husbands. The Church [should be ]{.class_4095}subject to Christ, exactly as a woman [should be]{.class_4095} in subjection to her husband. Israel was given a bill of divorcement which put her asunder from her husband (Hosea 1--3); and if it were not for the fact that each individual believer is an organic part of Christ's spiritual Body (see 1:13--23), the "Church" of the "Body Mystery" would have gone through the circuit court []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_292}several centuries back!

["Christ also loved the church, and gave himself for it"]{.class_4096} (vs. 25). The verse settles beyond any question the matter of the local church and the ["church, which is his body."]{.class_4096} To say that Christ died for the [local church ]{.class_4095}would be nonsense. The local church includes [unsaved church members]{.class_4095}, and the first New Testament local church had a devil for a treasurer. If the response is made that Christ died for the [unsaved too]{.class_4095}, then there would be no sense in the statement of verse 25 to start with. We know that Christ died for sinners (1 Tim. 2:6), and we know that God "gave Him" (John 3:16) for the world and not for "the members of His Body" only. The statement clearly matches the one of John 13 which states that although Christ loved ALL of his disciples (John 13:1), He loved one in a special way (John 19:26). This clearly identifies John as a type of the BODY! (Cf. comments under Rev. 4:1--3, [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation]{.class_4095}, 1970.)

Calvin reads verse 25 without looking at John 3:16; and when he does look at John 3:16, he interpolates: "God so loved the [Gentile elect ]{.class_4095}that whosoever of the [Gentile elect ]{.class_4095}believeth\...etc." But this is just Tom Paine and Rousseau and Voltaire with a little "Christian profession" added to fool suckers. The text doesn't SAY that.

["Cleanse it with THE WASHING OF WATER BY THE WORD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26). Martin and Williams spiritualize the water and call it "the word" as in John 15:3 and John 13:1--10; however, Martin's cross-reference was a bad error as the sprinkling he refers to (Ezek. 36:25) will be literal water. Still, this is a better attempt at exegesis than Kenneth Wuest's ridiculous construction that says the water was in "the sphere of the Word."

Still, the verse is a duzey (Latin: "Deusenberg," Hebrew: "humdinger," Greek: "Lallapalloozer," Sanskrit: "ring-tailed bobcat"). Moffatt said the Church got cleansed [when her members got baptized ]{.class_4095}"in the bath of baptism." How is that for partial cleaning? One cell at a time through a period of twenty centuries! She must have smelled like a hippy because nearly all of her members from A.D. 350 to A.D. 1600 were [sprinkled]{.class_4095}! Do you know of very many people who get clean from somebody splashing water on their forehead at a "laver"?

Rev. Salmond finds his own madness in the text. After tying and untying himself with granny knots, half hitches, barrel knots, and square knots, he finally comes to the conclusion that God promised in His word to cleanse the Body by water baptism; and this water baptism removed its "guilt" ([Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, Vol. 3, pg. 369)! Question: when a man can elaborate on the Greek of Xenophon, Homer, Herodotus, Sophocles, and Euripedes (pg. 368) and compare Greek with Hebrew (pg. 369) while discussing the scholarship of Ellicott, Moule, Haupt, Meyer, Alford, Ruckert, Bleek, Moulton, and Winer, how is it that he could think that water baptism had [anything to do with a sinner's guilt]{.class_4095}? Phenomenon No. 9.

But on it goes. Dummelow says that water baptism sanctifies the Church. Neander, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown insist that it is water baptism, although the word [baptism ]{.class_4095}occurs nowhere in the chapter. Phillips refuses even to translate the word ["water"]{.class_4096} (which occurs in all manuscripts of any family), but the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961) runs you right back to the kitchen sink and dunks you in again (or throws water at you; I don't know which).

But suppose we accept this Roman-Catholic-Campbellite private interpretation? Did you ever stop to think what it would mean [in the context]{.class_4095} in which it appears? Why, the ["washing"]{.class_4096} was not merely done to ["sanctify" ]{.class_4096}and ["cleanse" ]{.class_4096}the Church (vs. 26), it was done to present the Church without ["spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing\...holy and without blemish"]{.class_4096} (vs. 27)! Tell me something, worthy gentlemen. When did ANYONE'S water baptism (aspersion, immersion, sprinkling, dunking, or diving) make them ["without blemish"]{.class_4096}?

You see, Rev. Salmond got lost in the woods, and if you are ever stupid enough to follow a man simply because he is a Ph.D. in Semitic languages or a Ph.D. in Greek grammar with twenty years of formal education in an accredited institution, you deserve to get lost in the woods! Rev. Salmond, as Daniel Boone, would say that he had never been "lost" but that one time he had been pretty "confused" for about three weeks. (Rev. Salmond was confused most of his life.)

No, the text is not about water baptism, and neither is the near or remote context. Dummelow and Moffatt, as Alexander Campbell and Augustine, simply read "water" every time it said ["baptism"]{.class_4095} (see Gal. 3:27) and "baptism" every time it said ["water"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_294} (here). (This is what is known as a "WB syndrome" or "splashing fixation." The "Sons of the Pioneers" \[1940--1950\] had a song about this. I cannot recall all the words, but I remember one choice verse that says, "Don't listen to him, Dan, he's a devil not a man, and he's spread the burning sand with WATER--\[water!\]--cooooool water!")

Christ gave Himself for the Church (vs. 25) so that HE, [not someone else]{.class_4095}, might be able to minister to it. His ability to ["sanctify and cleanse it"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26) comes from the fact that He [bought it]{.class_4095} and [paid for it]{.class_4095}, just like Boaz bought Ruth (Ruth 4:10). The reader should observe that the Holy Spirit has preserved the word ["purchased"]{.class_4096} in both places (Ruth 4:10; Eph. 1:14)!

The Lord Jesus [is doing something to the Church NOW]{.class_4095} so she will be spotless THEN (vs. 27) at her presentation day (Rev. 19:7--8). He is washing the Body with a kind of water which is ["by the word"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26). John 13:4--15 shows clearly what is meant by the passage. The "brethren" should minister the word to each other to clean each other (John 13:14), and husbands should minister the word to their wives to keep them clean. A man takes care to keep his own flesh clean (vss. 28--29), and, therefore, he should minister to his wife!

Exactly as the scholars kicked the Holy Bible aside for "light" (see comments under 5:13), they now kick the Holy Bible out for "water baptism." The corrupt [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} even goes to the length of calling water baptism a "sacrament" in the footnote to Acts 8:12! Imagine C. I. Scofield---a Brethren!---approving of THAT note!

You get "wrinkles" from sitting too long in one place, and "wrinkles" have to be "ironed out." There will be a lot of "ironing out" at the Judgment Seat of Christ. "Spots" come from the world (James 1:27) and have to be washed out or dry-cleaned. (The Baptists wash 'em, and the Methodists dry clean 'em, and no man can "join the Lord's Army" by joining the "Church of Christ" because that is the Lord's NAVY. The choice words of an old Negress are no more out of keeping with the text than the remarks of Dummelow, Moffatt, and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown. She stood on the bank and watched all three of her sisters getting baptized. The first "surfaced" with "Glory to God!", the second with "Alleluia!", and the third with "Praise de Lawd!" Frantic for something different to say (as she was []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_295}dunked), the fourth sister broke the "shining river" with "MERRY CHRISTMAS!" Ephesians 5 is talking just as much about Christmas as it is about water baptism.)

What follows is clear. Adam and Eve were the same [flesh and bones]{.class_4095}, although Eve had no blood before Genesis 3:6. Christ has [flesh and bones]{.class_4095}, but no blood, when He comes up from the tomb (Luke 24:39). A man and wife are the same [flesh and bones, ]{.class_4095}even when they are apart; since this is a mystery, it is a perfect illustration of a Christian on the planet Earth, [in the back end of the universe]{.class_4095}, who is ONE with a risen Saviour now seated [more than 50,000,000 light years above the solar system]{.class_4095} (see extended comment under 1:20 and 3:18).

["This is a GREAT MYSTERY"]{.class_4096} (vs. 32), so Bullingerites talk about nothing but it. Divine predestination is nearly as mysterious, so that is all Calvinists talk about (see notes under 1:4--5 and 3:1--6)! However, the mystery, here, is not that Jew and Gentile will be in one body; the mystery, here, is how two individuals, hundreds of miles apart (or billions of miles apart), can both share the same ["flesh and bones."]{.class_4095} The serious student of Scripture will observe the anti-Bullingerite statement of Paul: ["This is a great mystery: BUT I SPEAK CONCERNING CHRIST AND THE CHURCH."]{.class_4096} The ["church"]{.class_4096} referred to here was spoken of in verse 31, and verse 31 is a reference to Adam and Eve! [This would indicate that Christ's Body originated at His DEATH]{.class_4095} (see 2:13--16).

You see, O'Hair and Stam did [not ]{.class_4095}follow Paul. And now Williams, who talked so much about the "twelve apostles and the eight apostles" and the "subject of revelation and the subject of prophecy," meets his Stalingrad. The English text will [not ]{.class_4095}meet the demands of his Hyperdispensationalism; neither will the Greek text; and neither will the German, Latin, Gothic, Coptic, Sahidic, Bohairic, Yugoslavian, or the Chinese text. So Williams [rewrites Ephesians ]{.class_4095}and makes verse 32 read: "This secret is great---I mean the secret concerning Christ and the church."

[You see, most of the greatest, premillennial, orthodox "Conservatives" in America today are Bible-rejecting heretics]{.class_4095} (see Preface). Phenomenon No. 13.

The word ["This"]{.class_4096} in verse 32 was a direct reference to verse 31, and the conjunction ["but"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_296} (found in every Greek family of manuscripts extant) separates the two thoughts. Not even ["Good News for Modern Man"]{.class_4095} (1966) destroys the sense of the verse as Williams has done.

The only difference between Tom Paine and Theodore Epp, where the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 is involved, is the amount of Scripture they altered or threw away. [One was an atheist, and the other was a Premillennial soulwinner.]{.class_4095} Isn't it amazing how many "odds and ends" a [King James]{.class_4095} Holy Bible can unite?

Tom Paine had no quarrel with some "inspired" manuscripts which nobody ever found.[ His quarrel was with a 1611 AV.]{.class_4095} When Theodore Epp abandons his [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, which was bought with the blood of the martyrs ([Which Bible?]{.class_4095}, D. O. Fuller, pp. 107--117), and reads from the laminated, contaminated, corrupt, leavened, milked-out, watered-down [Amplified Version]{.class_4095}, he is only saying "Amen" to Tom Paine's point of view. One may go to Heaven and the other to Hell, but they both had the same spirit when it came to the God-honored text of the Reformation Bible.

["Nevertheless let every one of you"]{.class_4096} (vs. 33), which only confirms the [King James]{.class_4095} reading further. Verse 32 has to be a reference to verse 31, or there would have been no point in saying ["Nevertheless."]{.class_4096} The word has been placed to indicate that although Paul had been speaking of the flesh and bone joining of the Body of Christ to Christ Himself, what he said about "nourishing" and "cherishing" (vs. 29) is still applicable to every saved man and wife, "in the flesh."

["Let every one of you IN PARTICULAR so love his wife even as himself"]{.class_4096} (vs. 33). Conversely, the wife is told to love her husband (Tit. 2:4).

["And the wife see that she reverence her husband"]{.class_4096} (vs. 33). Note, again, how the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 corrects the errors of the editorial committee of EITHER [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095}. The word ["reverence"]{.class_4096} is not the word [fear,]{.class_4095} and [fear]{.class_4095} is exactly what is warned against in the husband-wife relationship (1 Pet. 3:6). Now notice how the English corrects the Greek in these matters. The "fear of the Lord" is not "a reverential trust" (Heb. 12:28), for the two words are used as opposites; wives are to ["reverence"]{.class_4096} (text) but not to [fear.]{.class_4095} You do NOT [fear]{.class_4095} when you ["reverence"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ1946.xhtml_page_297}; therefore, the Scofield note (in either edition) [is an attempt by Fundamental, Evangelical, Premillennial Conservatives to overthrow the teaching of the Holy Bible]{.class_4095} (see comments under Exodus 20:20 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus,]{.class_4095} 1972). Phenomenon No. 13.

In conclusion, the Bible believer should notice that the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) and its corrupt sister-bibles (the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, 1963, and the old [ASV]{.class_4095}, 1901) have omitted the definitive statement of the entire passage. All three of these corrupt corruptions (plus the [NEB]{.class_4095}, Phillips, [Good News]{.class_4095}, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, [Vaticanus]{.class_4095}, [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}, Dummelow, Lachmann, Tischendorf, Tregelles, Westcott, Hort, and Methodius) omit ["of his]{.class_4096} [flesh, and of his bones." ]{.class_4096}Naturally, the first man to cut this portion of scripture out was ORIGEN.

## Chapter 6 {.heading_116}

6:1 Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right.

2 Honour thy father and mother; which is the first commandment with promise;

3 That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.

4 And, ye fathers, provoke not your children to wrath: but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the Lord.

5 Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;

6 Not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but as the servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart;

7 With good will doing service, as to the Lord, and not to men:

8 Knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth, the same shall he receive of the Lord, whether he be bond or free.

9 And, ye masters, do the same things unto them, forbearing threatening: knowing that your Master also is in heaven; neither is there respect of persons with him.

Having dealt with husbands and wives, the text now takes up instructions for ["children," "fathers," ]{.class_4096}and ["servants." ]{.class_4096}Although the [RSV]{.class_4095}, [NEB, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, Good News, ]{.class_4095}etc., translate the word ["servants" ]{.class_4096}as "slaves," none of the translators believe 1 Corinthians 7:20--22 or 1 Timothy 6:1--3; so the change was useless unless the purpose of the translator was to prove the Bible WAS outdated!

["Children, obey your parents IN THE LORD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1). It is assumed that the parents are saved, as in 5:21--22. The problem arises immediately: "What if they are not?" (Many a teenager has had to wrestle with this problem, and any pastor has had literally scores of them bring up detailed questions about the relationship.) Since the problem does arise, Origen immediately erases []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_299}["in the Lord" ]{.class_4096}so the problem will not be brought up! [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}preserves this corruption for us, and yet, this time, it is such an obvious erratum that not even the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}dare reproduce it. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}and ["Good News" ]{.class_4095}are not afraid to erase the words, however, exactly as Lachmann did. (Westcott and Hort put them in brackets and Phillips ducks gracefully out with some hypothetical guesswork about the Lord setting parents over children, or something like that.) The [Amplified, ]{.class_4095}as usual, keeps everybody happy by retaining the reading so that Conservatives like Theodore Epp and Bob Jones III will think well of it; then it explains the expression away so that people like Origen and Hort will think well of it! The expression ["in the Lord" ]{.class_4096}never meant "as His representatives" anywhere in any Bible ever printed. [You are in Him, or you are out of Him.]{.class_4095}

Again, the English sheds light on the fanatical ravings of the Conservative critics. The case is the same as 5:22. Saved or unsaved, Exodus 20:12 stands as it stands in this context: ["Honour]{.class_4096} [thy father and mother\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 2). (Note, again, how an Old Testament regulation given to the nation of Israel is also given to the "Body of the mystery" as "present truth." "Present truth" is often Proverbs and Exodus! Tell that one to Baker the next time you see him!)

Until the child is a "man" (Numbers 1:2--3, ["twenty years old"]{.class_4096}; U.S.A., eighteen years old), he is under submission (Luke 2:51). Unless his parents command him to do something directly contrary to the clear teaching of God's word (Exod. 20:12), he is to obey. The same thing goes for the wife (!), and the same thing goes for the servant of servants (6:5). The only problem with the text was the problem that always arises in the mind of a rationalizing philosopher who resents an authoritative command. The Holy Spirit has preserved a remarkable comment on ["Children, obey your parents in the Lord." ]{.class_4096}He has written, ["FOR THIS IS RIGHT." ]{.class_4096}Origen, Lachmann, Methodius, and Vaticanus are [wrong.]{.class_4095}

["Which is the first commandment with promise" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2). There are no promises connected with the first four commandments, for the mercy of the Lord connected with the second commandment is merely an absence of punishment.

["That it may be well with thee, and THOU MAYEST LIVE LONG on the earth" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_300}(vs. 3).

According to George Gallup:

1.[ Protestant ]{.class_4095}ministers live longer than priests.

2\. Honor students live longer than athletes.

3\. Professional people live longer than laboring people.

4\. Married men live longer than single men.

5\. Bricklayers and mechanics live longer than most other laboring men.

6\. Longest life expectancy will belong to a female child who is the second child in a [Protestant ]{.class_4095}family.

7\. Shortest life expectancy is that of a European Monarch.

8\. A rich rural area in a [Protestant ]{.class_4095}country will have older citizens than any other place in the world.

9\. The longest lives (on an average) are lived in the rural areas of the Midwest United States---Iowa, Nebraska, Illinois, Kansas, and Oklahoma.

(Evidently the Roman Catholic Latin [Vulgate]{.class_4095}, reproduced in the [New American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}(1963), isn't able to produce very good fruit!)

["And, ye fathers, provoke not your children to WRATH: but\...the nurture and admonition of the Lord" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). You can't teach an old dog new tricks, but you can [help the pups. A ]{.class_4095}family [altar ]{.class_4095}will usually [alter ]{.class_4095}a family, and although not every family that prays together "stays together," it will work 90 percent of the time.

The word ["nurture" ]{.class_4096}is defined with the passages in Luke 23:16; Hebrews 12:5--8; and Judges 8:16. You nurture a child with discipline and punishment. You admonish him with words and instruction. Observe how superior the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 is here to the "original Greek," for in Hebrews 12:5--8 both methods of "pedagogy" are given:

1\. Chastening---["scourgeth."]{.class_4096}

2\. Chastening---"[rebuked."]{.class_4096}

Paul gets some "nurture" in Acts 16, and David gets some "admonition" in 2 Samuel 7:7--12. (When in doubt, read the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible and leave the Greeks stuck in the grease.)

["Provoke not your children to wrath\..." ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). The child []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_301}who is constantly upset, disturbed, angered, and frustrated will seek love and comfort with the "new revolutionaries" (the old Bolsheviks) or the "hippies" (the dope‑heads) or the "avant garde of the new morality" (sex-obsessional neurotics) or "musical communication through ethnic dialogue" (African fleshpots). One extreme is too much yelling and ordering around, and the other is too much petting and mildness. The Lord whips (negative) those whom He loves (positive). Too much whipping produces a rebel or a bum, and too much loving produces a criminal or a [leech ]{.class_4095}(see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}1972, Prov. 22:16).

["Servants, be obedient to them that are your MASTERS\...as UNTO CHRIST" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5). Notice how the admonition matches 5:22---["AS UNTO THE LORD."]{.class_4096}

This is not an employer‑employee relationship, but a slave‑master relationship. Regardless of anyone's opinion about the Civil War and slavery, certain facts are true and no amount of lying will alter the truth.

1\. The New Testament tells the bond servant to stay where he is (1 Cor. 7:20--22) and serve.

2\. The New Testament tells the preacher that if he does not preach this, he is ["proud, knowing nothing" ]{.class_4096}(1 Tim. 6:1--4).

3\. The New Testament recognizes the bond between a saved master and a saved slave (1 Tim. 6:2; Eph. 6:9) and gives instructions relating to it.

4\. Slavery was a [Hamitic ]{.class_4095}institution from the start, and the Arabic slave traders (from Ishmael) were selling Africans before Englishmen or Dutchmen had enough ships to bring home a load of bananas.

5\. The Africans who were brought to America between 1700 and 1900 not only have "ghettos" and "rat control" to deal with, they also have two‑car garages, swimming pools, Mitchell spinning reels, deep freezes, automatic dryers, air‑conditioned bedrooms, and many of them drive Cadillacs and Buicks. You hear a lot of "racists" talk about the "great debt the South owes the Negro" for having "used him." That debt has been paid back at least TWICE before 1940. If there is any doubt in your mind about this, offer ANY Negro a free trip back to Nigeria or the Gold Coast and watch what happens!

We are not going to start a Civil War again. The Civil Rights Bill started it again, and the bloodiest race riots America ever saw took in America after 1964. However, this civil war was not started by Simon Legree whipping poor "Uncle Tom." It was started in Russia and carried out through FDR, the leftwing Supreme Court, the Congressmen of 1950--1960, and the [major television networks.]{.class_4095}

Onesimus in bonds had more liberty than MLK Jr. did riding Kennedy's private airplane (1 Cor. 7:20--22).

["Not with eyeservice, as men‑pleasers\..." ]{.class_4096}(vs. 6). That is, don't wait till the overseer or the "boss man" comes by before you pick up the shovel or the hoe. The Christian's "work" is "overseen" by God Almighty (1 Cor. 7:23), and he will receive a reward for what he does ["in singleness of\...heart, as unto Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5).

Dr. Martin is rather misleading when he tells his reader that the gradual abolition of slavery was a "by product" of Christianity. The abolition of slavery in the United States was a political movement with an economic motive behind it, and the chance references (and appeals) to "Christian conscience" by the preachers of that period were ANTI‑CHRISTIAN pronouncements, which is apparent by the text before us. If Dr. Martin means [anti‑biblical Christianity, ]{.class_4095}he told the truth, but he has intimated that true Biblical Christianity led to the abolition. It did not. Furthermore, Dr. Martin has not been reading his Bible nor his history books. The greatest slave traffic in the world took place in Europe more than 50 years [after ]{.class_4095}the "Emancipation Proclamation," and there were more slaves in Europe between 1938 and 1950 than there were in North America between 1800 and 1900. Furthermore, there are millions of slaves in slave camps [today, ]{.class_4095}and there will be more in the future (Rev. 6:15).

No institution was "set aside," let alone "abolished." You simply exchange methods, change definitions, and alter the outward appearance; then the chumps and suckers who want to believe things are getting better take the bait. Martin Luther King Jr. "had a dream" (see Jer. 23:25). But the only part of his dream that will come true is the part that dealt with a [mongrel race ]{.class_4095}of passive automatons (Rev. 6--19).

Verses 7 and 8 are self‑explanatory. Observe ["BOND OR FREE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8).

["And, ye masters, do THE SAME THINGS UNTO THEM, forbearing threatening: knowing that YOUR MASTER also is in heaven; NEITHER IS THERE RESPECT OF PERSONS WITH HIM"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9). The last clause is the "Christian alibi" used by race mixers and international Socialists for integration. The reader will observe that everywhere the expression occurs in the Bible (2 Chron. 19:7; Acts 10:34; 1 Pet. 1:17; James 2:9; Rom. 2:11; Col. 3:25), it is speaking of God's attitude towards individual men on SPIRITUAL ISSUES. The unregenerate ministers of the NAACP (2 Cor. 11:12--15) and the ecumenical council (Matt. 23:1--13) can only privately interpret these passages in the context of a SOCIAL ISSUE or a FLESH ISSUE (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy, ]{.class_4095}1969). Not one time does God or the Bible even SUGGEST that ["all men are created equal" ]{.class_4095}in the sense that Lincoln and the Black Muslims (and the French Revolutionists!) use the expression (see [Segregation or Integration?, ]{.class_4095}1982).

We may have to tolerate the enforcing of international Socialism on us by the Bible‑rejecting, scienceworshipping sinners of our age, but we do not have to lend our [support ]{.class_4095}to the movement, [encourage ]{.class_4095}the movement, speak [kindly ]{.class_4095}to those who do, have [fellowship ]{.class_4095}with those who believe in it, make any attempt to justify it with Scripture, or pretend for a minute that it has anything to do with [Biblical Christianity.]{.class_4095}

Those who yap the longest about racial integration send their children to private schools (see any Congressman in Washington, D.C.), and the stupid Christians who talk about the "Fatherhood of God and the Brotherhood of Man" have never led a Negro to Christ in a lifetime. You see, "bigots" are not limited to Bible‑believing Fundamentalists! Some of the most narrow‑minded, intolerant bigots you will ever meet in your life are the Liberal preachers and Liberal professors of the Twentieth Century. Their intolerance can only be matched by the furious hatred which Conservative scholars manifest towards the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text of the Reformation Bible.

Our text says that a "master" should treat a "servant" right. He is not to browbeat him or threaten him. He is to treat him like a human being [if he is lost, ]{.class_4095}and like a Christian brother [if he is saved ]{.class_4095}(Philemon). The treatment which international Socialists dish out to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_304}Christians in Russia, Romania, Yugoslavia, Hungary, China, Latvia, and Lithuania is quite typical of people who believe that ["all men are created equal." ]{.class_4095}Uncle Tom got better treatment from Simon Legree than Richard Wurmbrand got from the men who believed Marx and Darwin.

Observe how Communist organizations like the Supreme Court, the NAACP, CORE, and the National Educational Association always [overlook ]{.class_4095}the "saved‑ lost" division of Scripture. When they speak of "unity" and "integration," they are never referring to the things spoken of [in the Bible. ]{.class_4095}The Bible has no "unity" or "integrating of the believer" with ANY man, of ANY color, if that man has never been born again (2 Cor. 6:14). That is the Christian position. The position of the Liberal preacher is the position of Engels, Weiskopf, and Marx. None of them recognized any SPIRITUAL distinctions, for none of them were ["born of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (John 3:3--6).

6:10 Finally, my brethren, be strong in the Lord, and in the power of his might.

11 Put on the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

12 For we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the darkness of this world, against spiritual wickedness in high places.

13 Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

14 Stand therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, and having on the breastplate of righteousness;

15 And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;

16 Above all, taking the shield of faith, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked.

17 And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God:

18 Praying always with all prayer and supplication in the Spirit, and watching thereunto with all perseverance []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_305}and supplication for all saints;

19 And for me, that utterance may be given unto me, that I may open my mouth boldly, to make known the mystery of the gospel,

20 For which I am an ambassador in bonds: that therein I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.

Now follows a great militant passage on the spiritual warfare of the believer. His leader is a ["captain" ]{.class_4096}(Heb. 2:10), and this captain ["is a man of war" ]{.class_4096}(Exod. 15:3) whose troops must ["endure hardness as a good SOLDIER of Jesus Christ" ]{.class_4096}(2 Tim. 2:3). They fight ["a good fight" ]{.class_4096}(2 Tim. 4:7) which is called a ["war"]{.class_4096} (1 Tim. 1:18). No matter how many conscientious objectors and 4‑Fs there are in the ranks, the real Bible believer is a trained fighter equipped to stand toe to toe with the adversary and "slug it out."

["Be strong IN THE LORD, and in the power of HIS MIGHT" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10). Our sufficiency is plainly of Him and not ourselves (2 Cor. 3:5), and we put ["no confidence in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (Phil. 3:3). The man who marches out to this battlefield had better not rely on his education, wisdom, talents, money, brains, culture, background, breeding, reputation, character, or natural abilities. His adversary is ["a roaring lion" ]{.class_4096}(1 Pet. 5:8; 2 Tim. 4:17) and has successfully defeated every man that ever met him on the field of battle; i.e., every man but ONE (Col. 2:15).

Gideon's "300" didn't go into action with M‑18s, 75mm recoilless rifles, bazookas, grenades, napalm, lugers, Mausers, Krags, Enfields, Springfields, bayonets, trench knives, Thompson sub‑machine guns, axes, spades, BARS, mortars, tanks, artillery, planes, and switchblades; and neither do we (2 Cor. 10:4).

["Put on the WHOLE ARMOUR OF GOD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). For a moment we shall dispense with the endless nonsense put out by Trench, Alford, Ellicott, Wuest, Thieme, Robertson, Goodspeed, Meyer, Wordsworth, Ruckert, Lipsius, Rendall, Blaikie, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, Lange, Clarke, Westcott and Hort, Riddle, Salmond, Vincent, DeWette, Olshausen, the faculties of Bob Jones, Fuller, Dallas, and Moody, and the [New ]{.class_4095}ASV, old ASV, [RV, RSV, NEB, ]{.class_4095}AWOL, NRA, PO, RFD, PDQ, etc., and attend to the words []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_306}of truth.

The armor that is described is plainly the armor of a First‑Century Roman foot soldier---an infantryman. This is the "foot soldier" or "dogface" of timeless fame. His fighting is called ["the Queen of Battles," ]{.class_4095}although he is only a pawn on the chessboard of Division Headquarters and Army Headquarters. However, as in chess, he often is responsible for the checkmating of the king, and often he does not fall on his "square" before he has unhorsed a knight or dethroned a bishop. Since all wars are over [real estate, ]{.class_4095}all wars are eventually settled by this pawn. You cannot run a country until you [rule it; ]{.class_4095}you cannot rule it until you [possess it; ]{.class_4095}you cannot possess it until you are [ON it; ]{.class_4095}and you cannot get ON it without fighting [on your feet. ]{.class_4095}"The Queen of Battles" only appears as a "queen" to men like Von Moltke, Clausewitz, Patton, Rommel, and those who are either inexperienced or else hard ened to the most terrible sights. The tough old liquorhead W. T. Sherman took one look at the terrain in front of him at Atlanta (1864) and said grimly: ["WAR IS HELL." ]{.class_4095}When an infantryman hollers ["Give 'em hell!" ]{.class_4095}he is desiring to give his adversary something as near to Hell as you will ever get it this side of the Lake of Fire. All of the elements are there---the confusion (James Jones, [The Thin Red Line), ]{.class_4095}the cursing (Norman Mailer, [The Naked and the Dead), ]{.class_4095}the screaming and crying (E. M. Remarque, All [Quiet on the Western Front), ]{.class_4095}the hopelessness (Willie Heinrich, [The Crack of Doom), ]{.class_4095}the fire (Fehrenbach, [That Kind of a War), ]{.class_4095}the never dying WORM (Stanley Pavillard, [Bamboo Doctor), ]{.class_4095}and, throughout, all the misery, discomfort, insanity, and filth of the battlefield (Wolff, [In Flander's Field).]{.class_4095}

Do you wonder why there are not enough Christian soldiers in America today to form one Regiment? You can't get a choir to sing "Onward Christian Soldiers" any more because there are too many [conscientious objectors ]{.class_4095}in the choir! Our modern "Christian" schools are not training SOLDIERS; they are training [executives, kiddy‑teachers, ]{.class_4095}and [administrators. ]{.class_4095}And they are doing this with a full knowledge of Ephesians 6, written to the "Church of the Body mystery, etc."
