Tell me something! When was the last time you met a Christian []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_307}who reminded you of a FOOT SOLDIER!? Well, Paul was that kind of a Christian. [What kind are YOU?]{.class_4095}

A soldier should have guts (vs. 13). He should hold his ground and "dish it out" like Audie Murphy (1 Pet. 5:9). He should fight when he is scared to death (2 Sam. 10:12). And he should contend for every foot of ground like it was worth \$200,000 an acre (2 Sam. 23:11--12).

The individual soldier is the decisive element in combat, all other things being equal; and even where they are NOT equal (Kharkov, Gettysburg, Moscow, etc.), a fiercely contested piece of ground (Antietam, Belleau Woods, etc.) can wreck the plans of a whole opposing army. There is no place in the history of the world where the individual soldier is able to do more for his "country" (Phil. 3:20) than in Christian warfare. The effect of men like Paul, Wesley, John Huss, Dean Burgon, Bob Jones Sr., J. Frank Norris, Peter Waldo, Billy Sunday, Martin Luther, and Jonathan Goforth on the history of mankind is incalculable. Their opposition to the forces of evil and darkness affected unborn generations and the eternal destinies of millions of people. Not even Rommel, Charlemagne, Napoleon, Model, Von Manstein, Patton, Truscott, Stilwell, Mao Tse, Bedford Forrest, Ney, Sgt. York, Jeb Stuart, Jackson, Lee, Von Kleist, Kesselring, and Runstedt are in the same class with Huss, Savonarola, Hubmaier, Fox, Whitefield, Tennant, Morrison, Earle, Gardiner, Wurmbrand, Paisley, Peter Waldo, Wycliffe, Patrick, Alban, Columba, and Raymond Lull.

["PUT ON THE WHOLE ARMOUR OF GOD THAT, YE MAY BE ABLE TO STAND AGAINST THE WILES OF THE DEVIL" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). He has principalities, powers, thrones, Princes, Kings, Mayors, Popes, Bishops, philosophers, scientists, armies, navies, kingdoms, councils, and educators at his disposal (Rev. 12:9). He can muster a host in 24 hours that Russia could not muster in fifty years. His hordes stretch through the centuries; and the roll call shows that among the atheists, perverts, whoremongers, idolators, demons, devils, liars, extortioners, embezzlers, murderers, sadists, and blackmailers are also enlisted Conservative Bible scholars, Fundamental Bible teachers, born‑again "Christians," moral philanthropists, godly ascetics, mystical spiritualists, soulwinning []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_308}pastors, healing evangelists, and even David (2 Sam. 11), Peter (Matt. 16:23), Paul (Acts 15:39), and Moses (Num. 20:12).

["War hath no fury like a noncombatant"; ]{.class_4095}consequently, those who are most anxious to "restore the original" and "preserve the sense" are the most warlike of all the Christians. Their ministry is sabotaging the frontline troops, eating up all the "C‑rations" before they get to the front, cutting off the supply lines, fouling up the communication lines, and strutting around on the parade ground in the "original uniforms." The greatest Banzai charges in Christendom are those all‑out frontal attacks on the [AV ]{.class_4095}Bible conducted by the faculty members of Christian schools, who were converted by it. (A man who has been in [real combat ]{.class_4095}has better sense than to field‑strip his weapon in the middle of a counterattack!)

The armor comes in seven pieces:

1\. A loin covering.

2\. A breastplate.

3\. Boots.

4\. A shield.

5\. A helmet.

6\. A sword.

7\. Leg‑guards (greaves).

The last item is not mentioned, for this soldier is KNEELING to receive the cavalry charge and the flying shafts of the enemy's artillery. His knees, therefore, are covered (see vs. 18).

There is no "back plate" mentioned, for this soldier is supposed to "face up to it" and confront the enemy, not retreat from him. This carries two implications with it. First, if he turns and runs, he will get shot in the back (Bull Run, 1861). Second, if he presses the attack and gets way out in a salient in front of his troops, his own men will shoot him!

It is obvious that Burgon, Waldo, Miller, Hills, Ray, Fuller, Wilkinson, Luther, Wesley, Whitefield, Sunday, Torrey, Finney, Scofield, Larkin, Pember, Paisley, Wurmbrand, Popov, and Watchman Nee got out on the No. 1 and No. 2 "scout" position! Most of the troops stay behind the "line of departure," and many of them would rather stay in the "Regimental Bivouac Area."

No retreat. Nothing like the colored man who heard the bullet [twice ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_309}when it was shot at him: once when it passed him, and once when he passed it! Nothing like the Afro‑American who couldn't stand the "talking bombs" on Tarawa because they said, "B‑o‑o‑o‑oye‑ee‑e, you ain't never goin' back to Ala‑BAM‑a!!" Nothing like the dark Major in World War I who said to his troops, "Ah, gentamun, de 'telligence say dat in 'bout foah hours de Germans is gwine to attack, and ifen dey do, we is sipposed to retreat. Ah's a little lame in mah left laig, so ah thinks ah will start NOW."

They moved 10,000 white troops in behind some colored troops in the front line trenches during a major battle in World War I. That night, peeking over the parapet at the flare‑illuminated "no‑man's land," one Hamite said to another: "Hey, man, what you think de headlines is gwine to say tomorrow after dis heah battle?" With eyes staring wildly into the dark, the other said, "Ah doan know 'bout you, man, but if you feels like ah feels, dey is gwine to say '10,000 white men trampled to death!'" (It wasn't quite that bad, but about 2,000 were overrun the next day! You see, Ham is the knife fighter, fist fighter, and razor fighter of the ring, the stadium, and the alley. Crawling around at night in front of Japheth's Wermacht is "something else.")

["STAND AGAINST" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). The figure is the ancient battle line drawn up to withstand an attack. The men stand shoulder to shoulder (or shield to shield) in a phalanx, or square. Their "colors" or "standards" are with them. From this ancient picture come our hymns on warfare: ["Sound the Battle Cry," "Hold the Fort," ]{.class_4095}etc. There have been few songs written since 1900 that deal with Christian warfare. Did the soldiers all retire to the old folks home?

Modern warfare is a flexible, fluid, mobile, deadly game of hide‑and‑seek; and the man who advances "as skirmishers" with a "banner" is a blank fool. Even in the Civil War, the standard bearer was learning that "the old grey mare ain't what she used to be," and Hitler's tragic defense of Stalingrad was due to his World War I concept of "FORTS"; hence, "Fortress Stalingrad." [Fortress Stalingrad ]{.class_4095}looked like a modern Bible conference: the defenders were encircled, infiltrated, outflanked, out‑equipped, FREEZING, starving, and abandoned by OKW and the high command. (See chap. 5, [Mark of the Beast,]{.class_4095} 1960.)

But in a "British Square," every man is essential. If one man breaks ranks, there is a hole. Even in a modern assault, if one rifle squad falls back, the other advancing units can be outflanked. If one tackle fails to block, the defense can "blitz" the quarterback. The individual soldier is essential to the defense of a line. His morale, courage, and determination will usually decide the issue.

When Trench, Alford, Wuest, Gaebelein, English, Epp, Fuller, DeHaan, Thieme, Wilbur Smith, John R. Rice, Wordsworth, Vincent, Bob Jones III, Rendall, Robertson, Salmond, Machen, Warfield, and Zodiates ["give place to the devil"]{.class_4096} (see 4:27) and break ranks, the whole line wavers and is in danger of collapsing. If any man on the line abandons the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611[ ]{.class_4095}of the Reformation, he steps out of ranks and leaves his brother exposed to the enemy fire. Every criticism and correction of the text is a compromise with ["the wiles of the devil," ]{.class_4096}and the real soldier is ["not ignorant of his devices" ]{.class_4096}(2 Cor. 2:11). His first attack is on the Bible (Gen. 3:1); not on salvation, the New Birth, the Deity of Christ, the blood atonement, the Resurrection, the Second Coming, separation, consecration, soulwinning, or any other thing you might wish to think up [in order to take his side against the word of God.]{.class_4095}

["For we wrestle not AGAINST FLESH AND BLOOD\...SPIRITUAL WICKEDNESS IN HIGH PLACES" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12). This is the prince of Ephesians 2:2 and John 12:31, 14:30, 16:11 and Daniel 9--11. His demoniac satraps and satellites (Dan. 10:13, 20) operate the present world system (Luke 4:6). Did you ever wrestle a full-grown, wild, African lion (1 Pet. 5:8)? If you did, the [memory ]{.class_4095}of the wrestling match would serve in place of a "return card" (Job 41:8). You'll need something better than a toothpick or a butter knife if you are going to win this one!

["STAND\...WITHSTAND\...STAND\...STAND"]{.class_4096} (vss. 11--14).

Don't slide back. Stop this everlasting "covered withdrawal to the secondary positions." This is one time you are to fight it out like Adolph told the Wermacht and the SS troops to do on the Russian front. You are to fight to the last bullet, and if you don't want to shoot yourself with the last one, take another one of the ["principalities"]{.class_4096} with you.

How cheap and artificial most of our TwentiethCentury Christian leadership appears when measured by the lives of []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_311}[unregenerate German soldiers! ]{.class_4095}In the winter of 1944, the Russians found a nineteen‑year‑old noncom in a gun pit outside of Smolensk. He had lost all of his gun crew. His 88mm was out of commission. He had fired all the rounds from the cartridge cases of his dead squad. He had fired all the rounds in his own Mauser and his Luger. One arm was a bloody stump in a sling. He was sitting on the rim of the frozen gun pit, in thirty degrees below zero, without a serviceable weapon of any kind. When the Ruski put their bayonets on his chest and told him to raise his hands, he spit on them!

When was the last time you met a Christian with morale like that? ["STAND\...STAND\...STAND!"]{.class_4096}

In the battle for the Reichswald forest, a Germanspeaking Englishman was wounded. During the German counterattack, he "played possum" and watched the counterattack out of one eye half closed. He saw a German Major charging through the snow who suddenly stopped in his tracks. The man made a noise somewhere between a belch and a grunt, and then he hit the ground. A "Bren" gun had stitched him. The Englishman did not turn his head but continued to play possum. Three minutes later, the prone figure of the Major---not thirty feet from him---arose and stood on its feet, a mass of bloody clothing. The figure shook for a moment, then stiffened, and right there, in a driving snow storm, that German officer clacked his heels, saluted to the snow, and (in [Hoch Deutsch]{.class_4095}) gave his rank, name, and unit, and reported the hour of his death. With a mighty "Heil Hitler!" he fell straight long in the snow.

When was the last time you met a Christian with tenacity like that? You see, that type of Christianity is the sworn enemy of the type of Christianity that clamored for "Bible Revision" between 1800 and 1980. The men of the [RV ]{.class_4095}and [ASV ]{.class_4095}revision committees were no more "soldiers" than they were apostles. You would get better soldiers out of J. Frank Norris' Sunday school than you would get at the table of any "revision committee." The revision committees of 1880--1980 were "Campfire Girls."

["Wherefore take unto you the WHOLE ARMOUR OF GOD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13); not just the defensive armor (vs. 16) and not just the offensive armor (vs. 17). All combat is composed of two elements: []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_312}offense and defense. No boxer or bayonet fighter abandons his guard altogether. In kendo, kureneko, aikido, karate, kakuto, judo, jujitsu, gung fu, and kung fu, the blocks are as important as the holds, strikes, blows, throws, and "securing." The fencer has parries as well as thrusts; the knife fighter has deflections as well as slashes. The Christian who has nothing but FAITH will never "get a lick in" for God in a century. The Christian who has nothing but the SWORD will get torn all to pieces in the battle. You need both.

["STAND THEREFORE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). Don't give God Almighty this blather about the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible being the word of God, "EXCEPT for the chapter and verse numbers, paragraph headings, italics, non-Byzantine readings, spurious words, additions from other books, the ending on Mark 16, the man at the pool (John 5:3--4), the comma of 1 John 5:7, and the 'late manuscripts' from which it came." STAND, MAN! What kind of a soldier are you to turn tail like a scared jackrabbit every time some educated ass brays out of an air‑conditioned Seminary classroom! Do you wear ["The Red Badge of Courage"]{.class_4095} (Stephen Crane) or "The White Feather of Cowardice"?

["STAND THEREFORE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). Stand where Herr Luther stood; not on the "fundamentals," but on the word of God! Look at yourself! You stand there, fully equipped and clothed (2 Tim. 2:1--4), armed for battle, with the Captain of the Lord's host (Josh. 5:15) as your commander. And three minutes before the assault troops hit you, some company clerk behind you says, "There is no such thing as an infallible Bible!" And what do you do? You chickenhearted, white-livered, yellow‑bellied, spineless girl scout! You throw down your SWORD (vs. 17), drop your shield (vs. 16), and run barefooted (vs. 15) into the arms of the enemy, hollering: "You win! You win! [You're educated! ]{.class_4095}I'm not!"

Do you see why God made Gideon dump 31,000 volunteers? If I had to fight alongside of a coward like you, I'd just as soon put you out of your misery before the fight starts. You might drop your shield on my foot and break my instep before the action!

["Loins girt about with TRUTH" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). The strength of a man is in his loins, and the servant "girds his loins" to serve (John 13:4--5). This calls for an open, honest, frank, candid man who is girded with sincerity, dependability, and integrity. (Anything as []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_313}complicated as the revisers' explanations of the Bible are obviously the products of a complex, synthesized, integrated, leavened mind girded with obtuse, ambiguous, double‑minded conundrums designed to DECEIVED.)

["The breastplate of righteousness" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14). This is personal righteousness and matches the practical admonition of Ephesians 4--5 to put on something that God has already given. The believer already HAS God's righteousness (Rom. 10:1--4; 1 Cor. 1:30), but now he is to wear it into battle.

["And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15; cf. Rom. 10:15). All of the scholars distort the truth of the passage because not one out of ten of them is preaching the Gospel to anybody. They get rid of the command to "be prepared to PREACH THE GOSPEL" by calling the[ "OF"]{.class_4096} in the verse a "genitive of origin." Consequently, Salmond, [NEB, ]{.class_4095}Phillips, and Dummelow, with Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, [RSV, ]{.class_4095}etc., are unable to give any intelligent exposition of the verse.

The verse is telling the Christian to be prepared to preach ["the gospel of peace"]{.class_4096} wherever his feet take him (see Exod. 13:4--9). But the last forty critics we have mentioned not only did not DO this, they were not even PREPARED to do it.

The late Dr. Walter Wilson, or the old Welshman Billy Bray, knew what the text meant, but of course, they received their instruction on it by the Holy Spirit through the [AV ]{.class_4095}text. Kenneth Wuest takes the view of the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) and other Communist publications. The only difference he allows is a "firm foundation" for "equipment." Neither word is in any Greek text.

John Wesley would witness to highway robbers as they pilfered his saddle bags. Beza would witness to Nuns, Priests, and Cardinals in the Queen's court. Torrey would hit sinners with the Gospel on a dance floor; and I (by the grace of God only) have "socked it to them" in the middle of a skating rink, in downtown restaurants, at a drive‑in theater, on a tennis court, in barber shops, in poolrooms, and on one occasion in the interior of a wrecked car after a collision. There are Christian soldiers today (not many of them) who are witnessing in prisons, on beaches, in barrooms, in courts of law, and during fire fights. But none of these men are kin to Lightfoot, Clarke, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_314}Lange, Dummelow, Ellicott, Trench, Driver, Gesenius, Keil, Delitzsch, Schaff, Wuest, Robertson, Warfield, Lipsius, Wordsworth, Westcott, Hort, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Alford, Vincent, Moulton, Milligan, Meyer, Abbott, Ruckert, Blaikie, or any translator on the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}committee. There are "soldiers," and then there are ["rookies."]{.class_4095}

["Above all, taking the SHIELD OF FAITH, wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16). They had "fire fights" long before gunpowder showed up, and the flaming missile was a common sight in the Near East long before the days of the Caesars. Arrows and spears carried flax or wool soaked in oil and set afire (Thucydides, Diodorus, Cicero, Herodotus, et al.). These were the first "flame‑throwers" or "napalm bombs," and they burned men just like a phosphorus grenade burns them today. (Men have always been wise about killing and self‑preservation. Twelve‑year‑old Viet Cong natives are just as wise in these matters as any top ten scientists in the Pentagon.)

Job knew something of the "fiery darts." Jeremiah had his share slung at him. Paul used to collect them (2 Cor. 11:23--27). The Lord Jesus Christ took the whole arsenal full in the chest (Isa. 53; Col. 2) and recovered (Heb. 12:24) with nothing but a few minor scars.

[Poverty ]{.class_4095}tests our faith. [Bereavement ]{.class_4095}tests our faith. [The Devil ]{.class_4095}tests our faith with doubts about the word and God's faithfulness. [Sickness ]{.class_4095}and [loneliness ]{.class_4095}try our faith. A Christian who goes into this fray without a shield is "asking for it," and he may be compared to a Marine who goes into action on Guadalcanal without Atabrine tablets or a mosquito net.

["And take the helmet of salvation, and the SWORD OF THE SPIRIT, which is the WORD OF GOD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17). All soldiers have a helmet of some kind. The green berets and the "tam‑o'‑ shanters" (Aussie hat) are all right for a few, but helmets have been worn from Cain to Cairo by soldiers, sailors, marines, astronauts, and airmen. You can recover from an amputated hand, foot, arm, or leg; but an amputated head is a little hard to "heal up." First Thessalonians 5:8 is the companion passage.

But once again we have stumbled into the Lion's den: ["THE SWORD OF THE SPIRIT"!]{.class_4096} The word [sword]{.class_4095} is the word [WORD]{.class_4095} with an "S" on the front of it. ["The]{.class_4096} [sword of the Spirit" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_315}is the two‑edged weapon of Hebrews 4:12 which the saint is to carry in his hand (Psa. 149:5--6) and wield in such a way as to make the blood spout (Jer. 48:10; 2 Sam. 1:15). Those fearful of using the weapon (Judg. 3:21) have no business in the ranks, and those who devote their lives to taking the edge off it (the majority of the scholars, teachers, revisers, or commentators since 1880) should have it stuck in their guts instead of their scabbards.

If the word is a sword and you are commanded to use it (Luke 4:4), what are you going to do if [you can't find it? ]{.class_4095}Well, you are going to be just like Saul the night that David came and stole his spear (1 Sam. 26:12). You are going to arise and shake yourself like Samson (Judg. 16:20) and ["wist not"]{.class_4096}---that is the "original"!---that the Lord ditched you before you got into the action.

[What is the word of God? ]{.class_4095}Is it the message (Barth and Brunner)? Is it the Fundamentals (Gaussen and Wilbur Smith)? Is it something God wrote but lost because He wasn't able to keep it (John R. Rice, Noel Smith)?

Help us out, brave comrades! We are outnumbered 4,000 to one. Our rations are stale. We have summer uniforms to wear in December. Our dead and wounded have decimated our ranks so that 80 percent of us are not fit for combat. Our supply lines have been cut off! Our motor pool is bombed out. Our left flank has collapsed. Our ammunition dump exploded. The enemy is attacking in force! The artillery curtain is moving forward in "creeping fire"! Our barbed wire entanglements have already been blown to smithereens and half the land mines we planted have already been detonated. Quick, [brave comrades, HAND US A WEAPON!]{.class_4095}

And the faculties at Bob Jones, Tennessee Temple, Moody, Dallas, Fuller, San Francisco, Southwestern, Southeastern, BIOLA, Philadelphia, Columbia, Trinity, Wheaton, and Pillsbury hand us a [BEAN BAG! ]{.class_4095}Well, we don't need it. If they are really serious about their offer, we suggest that they get out of Christian work at the first opportunity possible and take up knitting or crocheting. We need [soldiers ]{.class_4095}out here, not "Whistler's Mother."

Now, when our Company Commander (Heb. 2: 10!) was interdicted with automatic weapons (Luke 4), He said: ["IT IS WRITTEN\...IT IS WRITTEN\...IT IS WRITTEN" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_316}(Matt. 4). But if He had had the learning and advantage of the "great strides in archaeological research" and "recent manuscript discoveries," He could have said: ["A better rendering would be\..." or "it is unfortunate that this word was translated thus and so\..." or "this does not bring out the meaning of the original\..." or "the word is interpolated from Fourth Pogo 13:666\..."]{.class_4095} or ["the translation is impossible and must give way to Elephantinus or Canine-canus," ]{.class_4095}or any other number of witty and impressive things. But no, our Company Commander (Heb. 2:10!) had only one sword on His person, and He drew it and struck (Luke 4:4) and struck again (Luke 4:8). When it was called to His attention that His sword was dull and made out of the wrong kind of steel (Luke 4:10), He did what? [He struck again ]{.class_4095}(Luke 4:12).

Ehud didn't have to [justify ]{.class_4095}his dagger. All he had to do was [use ]{.class_4095}it (Judg. 3:21).

You "judge men by their responses" (Dr. Bob Jones Sr.), and the response to a thrust from a good old, archaic, Elizabethan [King James 1611 AV ]{.class_4095}is, "My [God! I've been hit!" ]{.class_4095}(If you don't believe it, read the commentaries. That old, black‑backed, 66mm canon has sent more men back to the Aid Station than Napoleon, Hitler, Caesar, Genghis Khan, Charlemagne, Alexander, Eisenhower, Zhukov, and Gustaphus Adolphus ever looked at. And a lot of them [died ]{.class_4095}on the way.)

Now, do you think if we were in physical combat, those of us who are "on the firing line" would trade our Springfields, Mausers, M‑18s, and Colts, for slingshots, butter knives, serving spoons, toothpicks, and penknives? ["THE SWORD OF THE SPIRIT, WHICH IS THE WORD OF GOD" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17).

You still think I am preaching instead of telling the truth, don't you? All right, then, observe what happens when this old 1611 [AV ]{.class_4095}verse pierces the critic to the soul! [The Holy Spirit has no sword; ]{.class_4095}He merely [supplies ]{.class_4095}or [gives ]{.class_4095}one! 1. Phillips, 2. [NEB, ]{.class_4095}3. [Good News, ]{.class_4095}4. Williams, 5. [Amplified ]{.class_4095}("[wields]{.class_4095}"), 6. Rendall, 7. [Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_4095}8. [Wycliffe Commentary, 9. ]{.class_4095}Dummelow.

This time, only Wuest goes along with the [AV, ]{.class_4095}and this is probably because the Greek construction is so plain that the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_317}have to leave the text as it stands. However, Von Soden and Olshausen make the Spirit to be the "word." If the committees of the [RV ]{.class_4095}and [ASV ]{.class_4095}were put on the spot, they would holler "genitive of origin" and leave you in the same mess in which Custer was left on the Little Big Horn.

You see, the Holy Spirit does not [furnish ]{.class_4095}us or supply us with "a sword." [The word of God IS THE SWORD of the Holy Spirit. ]{.class_4095}The Spirit has a sword with which he punctures people, slashes people, stabs people, hacks people, chops people, slices people, and pierces people (see Col. 2:8 and comments). The Spirit "furnishes" [nothing, ]{.class_4095}nor does any Greek text on earth say anything like that. Such a reading allows the reviser to hand you his Jesuit production and say: "Here, use this one. God has promised to furnish you with a [sword." ]{.class_4095}But this is not the Holy Bible's text. The text says that God the Holy Spirit has a sword, and it is ["THE SWORD"]{.class_4096}; or as J. J. Ray so aptly put it: [God Wrote Only One Bible.]{.class_4095}

Now, this is a commentary on Ephesians, not a book on manuscript evidence; but for the unlearned and ignorant Ph.D.s and M.A.s who have misled you, you should recommend the following "devotional studies."

1\. [God Wrote Only One Bible ]{.class_4095}(J. J. Ray).

2\. [Which Bible? ]{.class_4095}(David Otis Fuller).

3\. [The King James Version Defended ]{.class_4095}(Dr. Edward Hills).

4\. [The Revision Revised ]{.class_4095}(Dean Burgon).

5\. [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence ]{.class_4095}(the author).

You see, some of us KNOW what is going on, and we know what HAS gone on. We are not trying to brag about our knowledge or trying to strut it, although it seems that way to the prejudiced bigots who have been promoting the new translations. We are simply calling the Christian's attention to the fact that [every Bible from 1880 to 1980 is an attempt to reinstate the Jesuit Bible of 1582. ]{.class_4095}If you don't KNOW this, you [should. ]{.class_4095}If your professor doesn't KNOW it, he has no business teaching Bible in a Christian institution, and he is just as much a fifth column traitor to the cause of Christ as the Liberals who preached in Trinitarian pulpits between 1800 and 1900.

The apostasy in this century is not in Union Theological Seminary. It is in the Body of the Lord Jesus Christ.

["The sword of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} is "[the word of God]{.class_4096}"; "[the word of God]{.class_4096}" is ["the sword of the Spirit."]{.class_4096} We [have ]{.class_4095}that word, so we have the sword. When Robert Sumner tells us that he believes the "Bible" is inspired but the [King James]{.class_4095} Bible[ ]{.class_4095}is NOT "infallible," it means nothing at all. It simply means that he, as scores and scores of dead‑orthodox apostates, has made his own [education ]{.class_4095}and [intellectual ability ]{.class_4095}the final court of appeals, for he, as scores and scores of dead‑orthodox apostates, [has no infallible Bible. ]{.class_4095}The "Bible" of which he speaks is an unread, unknown, unattainable Bible of past oblivion; and his belief, therefore (where it deals with THE BIBLE), is merely a resolution that he has no infallible authority by which to go except his own opinion.

That is exactly where Karl Marx and Tom Paine stood. These people had a [sword; ]{.class_4095}it is "the sword of reason, which is found in the theories of [science ]{.class_4095}and [philosophy" ]{.class_4095}(see Col. 2:8 and comments). If you go into battle with this "sword," you may make a living, but God Almighty will have some things to show you (1 Cor. 2:1--13) when you try to explain your rejection of His Book (1 Thess. 2:13).

["Praying always with all prayer and supplication\...for all saints; And for me\...that I may OPEN MY MOUTH BOLDLY\..."]{.class_4096} (vss. 18--19). Paul needed prayer as much as Demas or John Mark, and he knew it (cf. Acts 6:4, 4:29; Rom. 15:30; Psa. 140:7). Any man can preach the Gospel while the wind blows from the right direction (Eccl. 11:1--6), but when the gales are up and the elements begin to howl (Matt. 14:24, 30), it takes a supernatural boldness "[to make known the mystery of the gospel.]{.class_4096}" "[The righteous are]{.class_4096} [bold as a lion" ]{.class_4096}(cf. Prov. 28:1 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}1972), and throughout the history of the church, they stand and roar while their "brethren" run from phantoms of their imagination.

Savonarola said it publicly, while Pope Alexander VI plotted privately.

Luther jeopardized his life, while Pope Leo X hid behind "Holy Mother Church."

Burgon blasted the [RV ]{.class_4095}committee publicly, while Westcott and Hort smuggled the Latin [Vulgate]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_319} into the committee privately.

Richard Wurmbrand called Graham's hand on the "invitation to Christians" at the Berlin Congress (OctNov, 1966), but Theodore Epp didn't dare even DISCUSS the subject.

While Carl McIntire was losing influence, prestige, property, rights, and liberties, Pope Paul VI was reaping the income from the sale of tickets at Yankee Stadium.

For every George Wallace, there is an Agnew.

For every Sam Jones, there is a "Henry Ward Beecher."

For every Paul, there is a Demas.

For every Bob Jones Sr., there is a Bishop Pike.

For every C. I. Scofield, there is an E. S. English.

For every J. Frank Norris, there is a George Truett.

For every Martin Luther, there is a Melanchthon.

For every Patton, there is an Eisenhower.

For every Stillwell, there is a Marshall.

For every Truscott, there is a Lucas.

For every R. A. Torrey, there is a Gipsy Smith.

For every Billy Sunday, there is a Norman Vincent Peale.

For every J. J. Ray, there is a Benjamin Warfield.

And for every Oliver Greene, there is a T. W. Wilson.

While Paul is ["an ambassador in bonds" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20), the Campbellites and Baptist churches that split off from the church in Antioch (Phil. 1:15) are "adding new members" to their "fastest growing, friendliest churches in town" (Phil. 1:18). And when Paul gets out, he will have learned NOTHING, for he will speak to Nero (2 Tim. 4:1--8) with the same tone of voice he spoke to Felix, Festus, and Agrippa (Acts 24--26). "Some men never learn!"

If men are bold to sin, we should be bold to rebuke sin. In view of the context (the soldier), the quiet Christian, in this age, should receive a dishonorable discharge. After all, they are supposed to SHOOT sentries who go to sleep in combat zones! ["WATCHING thereunto\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 18).

["Praying always with ALL prayer"]{.class_4096} implies praying on all occasions, under every condition, and praying all kinds of prayers (1 Sam. 12:23; 1 Tim. 2:1, 8).

Paul is through with his military discourse. In his day, the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_320}"troops" probably accepted the admonitions as a German SS regiment would have accepted orders from Sepp Dietrich. However, "times have changed" (as SIECUS says!), and NOW the response of the troops is much closer to the response of hippy draftees who have been told they cannot smoke in their bunks after Taps.

Corporal Wuest thinks that the general orders should have read "[pernicious one]{.class_4095}" (vs. 16) instead of ["the wicked."]{.class_4096} Corporal Weigle ([RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946) thinks ["fiery"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16) should have been "[flaming]{.class_4095}." ["Salvation" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 17) should have been "[saving faith]{.class_4095}" according to Private First Class Frances Siewert [(Amplified Version). ]{.class_4095}["Darts" ]{.class_4096}should have been "[missiles]{.class_4095}" according to Buck Sgt. Phillips, but it should have been "[arrows]{.class_4095}" if we are to believe Staff Sgt. Dodd [(NEB, ]{.class_4095}1961). We are to "[accept]{.class_4095}" salvation "[as a helmet]{.class_4095}" according to 2nd Lt. Robert Bratcher [("Good News]{.class_4095}"). ["High places"]{.class_4096} should have been ["heavenly places"]{.class_4095} if Captain Schaff is right ([ASV]{.class_4095}, 1901); and if 1st Lt. Blaikie is right [(Pulpit Commentary), ]{.class_4095}the "heavenlies" are not even a reference to a geographical location; they are, rather, the [heavenly thoughts ]{.class_4095}that you have about spiritual things!

Boy, are we ready for an attack! You talk about [organization ]{.class_4095}and [coordination! ]{.class_4095}Man, we got it!

But this is only the beginning. You see, Privates DeWette, Lipsius, Rendall, and Abbott made some more changes in the orders. Corporals Lightfoot, Bengel, Meyer, and Wordsworth added a few original thoughts to them; and Sergeants Vincent, Riddle, Salmond, Schnedermann, Robinson, Ruckert, and Bullinger also stuck their oar in. As this "crack regiment" moves forward "like a mighty army" to assail the foes of God and truth, its "attack briefing" now reads like this:

"Your main objective or target, primary [or secondary, ]{.class_4095}may [or may not ]{.class_4095}be on your right or left flank, [unless ]{.class_4095}the enemy decides to attack. In which case, withdraw or retreat behind Hill 407 or Hill 306 or Hill 221 on grid square 24---[generally.]{.class_4095} In case of artillery or strafing attacks, you may [or may not ]{.class_4095}take cover in the valley to your right or left front, if you get across the stream or river in front or behind you. Each soldier is encouraged to use his initiative and private judgment in determining all operations not covered in these [suggestions."]{.class_4095}

Sounds like the battle plan for the Anzio beachhead, doesn't it? [Do you know what happened at the Anzio beachhead ]{.class_4095}(Jan. 1944)?

6:21 But that ye also may know my affairs, and how I do, Tychicus, a beloved brother and faithful minister in the Lord, shall make known to you all things:

22 Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that ye might know our affairs, and he might comfort your hearts.

23 Peace be to the brethren, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

24 Grace be with all them that love our Jesus Christ in sincerity. Amen.

The great epistle now ends with a personal note and a word of recommendation for Tychicus (2 Tim. 4:12), who is an Asiatic (Acts 20:4). Verse 21 is self‑explanatory, and the student should observe the use of the word ["all"]{.class_4096} (as in ["shall make known to you ALL things"]{.class_4096}). Tychicus certainly did not tell them how many hairs the Sioux chief in South Dakota had on his head, nor did he tell them Cordell Hull's telephone number. The word ["all"]{.class_4096} is sometimes a reference to "all without distinction" (see Col. 4:7, 9; Mark 1:5) rather than "all without exception" (as in Rom. 3:23).

Verses 22 and 23 are self‑explanatory. True love is accompanied by FAITH (vs. 23), and there is a difference between those who [profess ]{.class_4095}love for the Lord Jesus and those ["that love our Lord Jesus Christ IN SINCERITY" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 24). If a man really loves God, it ["is known of him"]{.class_4096} (1 Cor. 8:3), for real love cannot be hidden any more than strong perfume (John 12:3).

Since this one word ["sincerity"]{.class_4096} shows the Bible believer the difference between real Biblical Christianity and dead orthodoxy, every translation on the market gets rid of the word as quick as a shot out of a gun.

You see, [no translator since 1881 was sincere in his work of translating, for his MOTIVE was negative, critical, and destructive and came from an envious and bitter heart. ]{.class_4095}The surest proof that this is so is the sudden and startling absence of the word ["sincerity"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_322} from:

1\. The [American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}(any edition ever published).

2\. The [Revised Standard Version ]{.class_4095}(any edition ever published).

3\. [Good News for Modern Man ]{.class_4095}(1966).

4\. The [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}(1961).

5\. Phillips (1958).

6\. [Amplified New Testament ]{.class_4095}(1958).

7\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (Commentary, pg. 1299).

8\. [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}(pg. 261).

9\. Bullinger [(Companion Bible, ]{.class_4095}pg. 1771).

What inspired these men to get rid of the word ["sincerity"]{.class_4096}? [Simple:]{.class_4095} THE OFFICIAL ROMAN CATHOLIC BIBLE PUBLISHED BY THE JESUIT PRIESTS IN 1582. If you don't believe it, buy a copy of the ["Rheims" ]{.class_4095}(1582) and compare it with any edition of the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901 or 1963) recommended by the deluded and apostate "Conservatives" at Bob Jones University and Moody Bible Institute.

These dead‑orthodox apostates, who "contend for the faith," are unwittingly leading the Body of Christ right slap back into the Dark Ages by hoisting a competing authority up to the level of the Holy Bible. Rome does not deny the Fundamentals or "the Word of God"; she simply [elevates something else to an equal position of authority. ]{.class_4095}This is the habit of the faculty members of Bob Jones, Tennessee Temple, Dallas, Fuller, Fort Worth, and Wheaton; they simply elevate the Roman Catholic [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) to a place of equal or superior authority to the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 and then talk about "contending for the faith." They are contending for Rome.

The great [ASV ]{.class_4095}hobbyhorse is a hobbyhorse that all of these faculties ride to death. They are as badly hung up on this hobby as Pink was on Calvinism or Bullinger was on Dispensationalism. This religious "hobby" is so prominent at any of these schools that the student may find himself in classes where the Roman Catholic [ASV ]{.class_4095}(and ["New" ASV]{.class_4095}) is used to correct the Holy Bible as many times as twice per verse.

Furthermore, no amount of light or facts can unseat these blinded, deluded fanatics from their theological hobbyhorse. It is essential to their prestige and religious image that they keep promoting the apostate [ASV ]{.class_4095}(or ["New" ASV, ]{.class_4095}or "Brand New []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2158.xhtml_page_323}Spanking New ASV") at the expense of the Bible, for this lays the emphasis on [Greek scholarship ]{.class_4095}and [Christian education" ]{.class_4095}instead of on [the Holy Spirit and the Reformation text. ]{.class_4095}This is Satan's position.

Knowledgeable people who are acquainted with the facts of church history and manuscript evidence know that the theological hobby of these dead‑orthodox apostates is bound up with their [income ]{.class_4095}and their [egos. ]{.class_4095}No true Bible‑believing Christian would abandon the word of God to ride such a hobbyhorse, even if the race was called "contending for the faith." These antics are the stock and trade of men who are contending for [human learning ]{.class_4095}and [human wisdom. ]{.class_4095}The [ASV ]{.class_4095}hobbyhorse is a dead horse, and it will lie dead in the stable "till hell freezes over."

You see, as we have said before (and will say until God calls us home), "The apostasy in the Twentieth Century is not in the ranks of the Liberals or the Neo‑orthodox; the apostasy is within the ranks of the [born‑again, saved, Premillennial, soulwinning Christians." ]{.class_4095}It is much, much, much later than you think.

["Grace be with all them that LOVE OUR LORD JESUS CHRIST IN SINCERITY. Amen"]{.class_4096} (vs. 24). Here ends one of Paul's greatest epistles. It shows the heavenly (geographical) position of the Christian as well as his spiritual standing in Christ. It presents the Christian as a WARRIOR from outer space, sent to wage war against the principalities and powers who assailed Jesus Christ (Col. 2:15). Unity is an essential in an effective military outfit, and so the epistle has gone into great lengths to draw the troops together (Eph. 1--4). The warrior's moral life has been discussed (Eph. 3--5), as well as his position and warfare.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2349.xhtml}

# Philippians {.heading_86}

## Introduction {#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2349.xhtml_Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ4083 .heading_1314}

Philippi was named after the father of Alexander the Great, the famous "Philip of Macedonia." It guarded the eastern frontier of that country and lay athwart the pass which led from the interior to the Aegean Sea. The word [Philippi]{.class_4095} means "a lover of horses."

At the time of Paul's first visit to Europe (around A.D. 52), Philippi was a Roman military colony where the colonists were free citizens enjoying exemption from poll tax and tribute, but who had the right to hold land. The Latins, there, outnumbered the Greeks, and it was "Roman" from head to foot. There were no synagogues in the city to mention at this time, and the "place of prayer" was on a river bank (Acts 16:13).

Paul's visit was a memorable one, as it was inspired by a vision. His first series of revival meetings ended with both he himself and his song leader in the "hoose gow" (Aramaic for "jug"). Paul traversed Macedonia twice between this time and the time of the writing of the epistle (A.D. 62), and the date of writing is so close to that of Ephesians and Colossians that it is hard to tell which was written first. Lightfoot and Bullinger would both tend to push Ephesians to the last---for different reasons---but they probably are both wrong.

The tone of Philippians is the relaxed tone of a man who has said and done what he should have said and done; and there is no crusade, doctrinal disturbance, controversial issue, or "mystery to be revealed" anywhere in the epistle. It is the freest of all of Paul's writings, the most informal and personal, and the most truly "epistolary" in style.

Paul is in prison at Rome, awaiting trial following his incarceration mentioned in Acts 28, and this is apparent from Philippians 1:13 and 4:22. There are many dead-orthodox apostates who conjecture that Paul was in prison in Caesarea at this time (Bottger, Holtzmann, et al.). Robert Mounce supposes that it came from an Ephesian imprisonment on the grounds of 1:25 and 2:24. (The idea being that if Paul planned to return east from Rome to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2349.xhtml_page_325}revisit Philippi, he would not be going WEST \[Rom. 15:28\] where he planned to go.) But all of this is, of course, some scholar with nothing to do but play around with ideas while he is destroying the faith of his students in the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 Bible. Mounce, as any 2,000 like him, quit believing and started "playing" many, many years ago; that will be quite apparent as we proceed into the epistle.

Bauer, DeWette, Holsten, Heinriches, Koppe, Clemen, and Volter (and other Bible-rejecting "Christians") have questioned the authorship (or complete authorship) of the epistle. Their volumes on the subject lie where they should lie---in a subterranean, stagnant vault reserved for worthless books.

The commentaries on the epistle by Meyer, Holeman, DeWette, Van Hengel, Wiesinger, Neander, Haupt, Rilliet, Ellicott, Wordsworth, Alford, Gwynn, Eadie, and Lightfoot contain about 200 errors; and the inability of these masterminds of Christendom to expound the epistle is so painfully apparent in every line of their works that the reader is advised to save himself the embarrassment of reading them. Others (Weiss, Vincent, Haupt, Franke, Lipsius, Klopper, Wohlenberg, Bullinger, and Beet) have attempted (with the men listed above) to destroy the authority of the epistle but the Bible believer should never take the efforts of such men seriously. The vast majority of them, in any age, from any persuasion, of any ability, were merely etymologists and linguists. Their spiritual power as preachers or expounders of truth was always about thirty feet below the low-tide mark.

The church at Philippi was one of Paul's favorite churches. It had seen him suffer (Acts 16), and twice it had helped Paul in Thessalonica after his departure (4:16). They were not infected with Judaizers, nor by any serious doctrinal problems. They had no grave cases of immorality (as at the church at Corinth), and with the exception of "personality problems" (4:2) and an occasional Judaizer (3:2), the church was in good shape.

The keynote of the epistle are ["ALL"]{.class_4096} and ["REJOICE."]{.class_4096} (Williams \[Commentary, pg. 929\] has an excellent outline of the book which would be useful to anyone interested in outlining books.) The book has 4 chapters, 104 verses, and 2,183 words. It was written near A.D. 61 or 62, although the scholars differ as to their guesses.

We shall not take time in our comments to go through all of the 200 errors of the "leading scholars," but will only occasionally point them out under "Phenomena," as we did in the commentary on Ephesians. (The list of Phenomena will be found at the end of the commentary on Galatians.) There are actually fewer changes in Philippians than in Ephesians or Colossians, but this is only because the subject matter of Philippians is primarily PRACTICAL instead of DOCTRINAL. Wherever doctrinal epistles show up, all of the dead-orthodox apostates assume they are "suspect"; therefore, they go to work tooth-and-nail to substitute their own private interpretations (Mark 7:9,13) for Bible doctrine. This is quite apparent to any objective researcher who has studied the last two books---Galatians and Ephesians. (It is so very apparent that we can safely say that only a man with a vicious subjective prejudice would fail to grasp the truth of it.)

Our habit, here, will be as it has been in the commentaries on Genesis, Exodus, Proverbs, Matthew, Revelation, Galatians, and Ephesians. We shall abide by the God-honored text which the Holy Spirit has authorized---over the objections of Conservatives, Liberals, Catholics, atheists, Illuminati, Evangelicals, agnostics, Fundamentalists, Communists, exponents of Neo-orthodoxy, and drug addicts, alike.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml}

## Chapter 1 {.heading_116}

1:1 Paul and Timotheus, the servants of Jesus Christ, to all the saints in Christ Jesus which are at Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:

2 Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,

4 Always in every prayer of mine for you all making request with joy,

5 For your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now;

6 Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ:

7 Even as it is meet for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart; inasmuch as both in my bonds, and in the defence and confirmation of the gospel, ye all are partakers of my grace.

8 For God is my record, how greatly I long after you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ.

9 And this I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all judgment;

10 That ye may approve things that are excellent; that ye may be sincere and without offence till the day of Christ;

11 Being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.

Paul includes Timothy's best wishes in the opening of the letter, although Timothy, of course, is not in jail with him at this time.

["To all the saints\...bishops\...deacons"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). (Cf. Acts 20:17; 1 Pet. 5:1; 1 Tim. 3:1--7; Tit. 1:5--7; Acts 6:1--6; and 1 Tim. 3:8--13.)

The ["bishop"]{.class_4095} is a guardian or inspector. This is apparent from the passages where the word [elder]{.class_4095} occurs. The bishop is plainly an []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_328}ordained elder (Acts 20:17, 28) in charge of "feeding the flock" and acting as "overseer" of the vineyard (1 Cor. 9:7--10). The "Bishops" of the Catholic and Methodist churches have nothing to do with anything even suggested in the Scripture. All ["bishops"]{.class_4096} and ["elders"]{.class_4096} help oversee LOCAL CONGREGATIONS, and there are several of them in each locality (Acts 20:17). The bishop is the "pastor" of Ephesians 4:11, and if the pastor is to be ["apt to teach"]{.class_4096}(1 Tim 3:2) and a "teacher" (Eph. 4:11), then every Roman Catholic Bishop since Gregory the Great is in the wrong office and has deceived himself about his calling.

Rome has never produced ONE great teacher since Augustine, and Augustine was as hopelessly fouled up in heresy and blasphemy as any three men who ever lived (Walker, [A History of the Christian Church]{.class_4095}, pp. 164--165). Among other things, he was an evolutionist and worshipper of Plato (Col. 2:8 and remarks). Any Roman Catholic in the Twentieth Century can learn more about the Bible from [one book ]{.class_4095}by Clarence Larkin (a converted Baptist, formerly an Episcopalian) than he can learn in the whole library of Abelard, Anselm, Augustine, Jerome, and Thomas Aquinas.

The "deacon" is a "waiter" or "attendant" whose job is to "minister" (Gk., ["diakanon"]{.class_4095}). He is a ["runner through the dust"]{.class_4095} (literally) and handles the carrying out of suggestions and recommendations made by the elders or the church itself (Acts 6:1--6). Nowhere does the Bible ever intimate that you are to make deacons out of the rich businessmen in your community so you can build nicer looking buildings, nor does it ever suggest that a deacon's function is to "politic" in business meetings and handle the finances of the church.

A deacon (as an elder or bishop) has a [spiritual calling ]{.class_4095}to a [spiritual job]{.class_4095}, and the requirements for bishop and deacon are so similar you could hardly discern the difference between them (1 Tim. 3:1--13). Deacons should be the LEADERS in hospital visitation, prayer meetings, greeting people, maintaining good relationships between the people and the pastor, and carrying out the distribution of goods and help to poor Christians (Acts 6:1--6).

Verse 2 is self-explanatory. (Wuest's insertion of "tranquilizing" right before ["peace"]{.class_4096} doesn't help matters a bit, in view of the last ten years! The "church leaders and helpers" of the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_329}[Good News for Modern Man]{.class_4095} is about as accurate as translating "Father and Son" as "Daddy and Kiddy.")

Paul thanks God every time he calls the Philippians to remembrance (vs. 3), and when he prays for them (vs. 4), he does it joyfully: ["making request with JOY."]{.class_4096} This last word is the keynote of the epistle. It crops up in 1:25, 2:2, 2:17--18, and 4:1. In addition, you have ["rejoice"]{.class_4096} in 1:18, 2:16--18, 2:28, 3:1, 3:3, 4:4, and 4:10. For a "jail bird," Paul is in pretty good spirits. The reason for the joyful prayer is their ["fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). Here was one bunch who got started right and stayed right and were not "hindered" (Gal. 5:7 and comments) in their race. They had fruit to show that their fellowship was unbroken (1 Cor. 1:9; 2 Cor. 13:14; Phil. 4:10, 15; and 2 Cor. 9:13). ["Fellowship in the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5) includes:

1\. Believing what he preached.

2\. Passing this message on to others.

3\. Supporting him financially while he preached it to others.

4\. Supporting him verbally before his enemies.

5\. Praying for his success in preaching the Gospel.

6\. Showing concern for his ministry when anything went wrong (1:12--18).

Every real minister whom God ever called to preach had a few "Philippians" behind him. If the Lord had not provided these Philippians, along with the Bible and the Holy Spirit, many a successful ministry would have been "[without form, and void; and darkness\...upon the face of the deep.]{.class_4096}"

["Being confident of this very thing"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). Now follows one of the greatest promises in the Bible on the eternal security of the believer.

["He which hath begun a good work in you WILL PERFORM IT UNTIL THE DAY OF JESUS CHRIST"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). The reader should review the comments under Ephesians 4:30 which deal with the "day of the Lord" and reestablish himself in Biblical truth. "[The day of Christ]{.class_4096}" (as in 2 Thess. 2:2) is NOT "the day of the Lord," in spite of Mounce's dogmatic error ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1321).

A small boy at a prayer altar was heard praying: "Lord, make a good job of me! Make a good job of me!" Well, the passage says that He will do it (cf. 1 Cor. 1:6--9). In spite of Jehovah's Witnesses, Catholic Priests, Lutheran Bishops, Methodist Communists, "Church of Christ" and Mormon elders, and Pentecostal and Church of God preachers, God will ["perform it until the day of Jesus Christ."]{.class_4096} If James and Peter (James 2:21--26 and 2 Pet. 2:20--22) have something else in mind, they will have to stand in line until Paul gets through. The One who works IN the Christian (see 2:13 and comments) placed Himself within the Christian (see Col. 1:27 and comments), and He will not "knock off for lunch," cut down to a "twenty hour week," take a paid vacation, quit before the whistle blows, or "retire at fifty." He will work on and on until His workmanship is ["faultless before the presence of his glory"]{.class_4096} (1 Thess. 5:23; Jude 24).

Paul says that it is right for him to have this joy about Philippi since the converts there are ["in"]{.class_4096} his heart (vs. 7). They have been "[partakers]{.class_4096}" with Paul, in or out of prison, as he has stood up for the Gospel (vs. 7). This is manifested by the financial help they have given him on other occasions (see Preface). Zahn, Sohm, Hoffmann, Harnack, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown suggest that we change the text (vs. 3) to "on account of your whole remembrance of me." This is significant, for it now leads Kenneth Wuest to reverse the whole thought of ["for me to think this of you all, because I HAVE YOU IN MY HEART"]{.class_4096} to ["you are holding ME IN YOUR HEART."]{.class_4095} This reading is also encouraged by the [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095}, in spite of the fact that it does not occur in one Greek manuscript of any family or period. (The word ["YOU"]{.class_4096} is in the accusative case, and the word "[YOUR]{.class_4095}" is a fabrication pulled out of thin air (or hot air).) Phenomenon No. 5.

The word "[perfect]{.class_4095}" has been thrust into the Bible at verse 6 for ["perform"]{.class_4096} by the apostate [RV ]{.class_4095}committee of 1881--1885, and it is preserved in the [ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and by Ellicott and Alford. (The [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095} splits the difference, as usual, and tries to work it in with "develop.") The insertion of a "perfecting" instead of a "performance" is distinctly out of keeping with the rest of the epistle (2:13, 3:12--15). Paul is not "[perfect]{.class_4096}" (3:12), and nobody is perfecting him. He is striving [towards ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_331}perfection, but the One IN HIM ["worketh\...mightily"]{.class_4096} in the sense of WORK (Col. 1:29). This is ["performance,"]{.class_4095} not ["perfecting."]{.class_4095}

["How greatly I long after you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). The reader sees immediately what has happened to the word ["bowels."]{.class_4096} It originally referred to the upper and lower viscera, including any organ within the body cavity. However, "medical science" has now confined the word to a function of the lower viscera in getting rid of waste material. This is quite typical of "word evolution," and it is one of the evidences of man's depravity. The careful student of Scripture who discerns the SPIRITUAL ISSUES behind revisions, translating, preaching, and application of terminology has observed that [AV ]{.class_4095}Bible words are the favorite targets of Satan; and he has never stopped once in 300 years in seeking to get rid of them. Observe the following: 1. ["Bishoprick"]{.class_4096} (Acts 1:20), 2. ["Ass"]{.class_4096} (Gen. 22:5), 3. "[John]{.class_4096}" (Rev. 1:1), 4. "[Peter]{.class_4096}" (1 Pet. 1:1), 5. "[Privy]{.class_4096}" (Acts 5:2).

The word [bowel]{.class_4095} is kin to [bowl]{.class_4095} and has always been a reference to the hollowed-out INSIDE of an object. This is how the word is used in Scripture, and it indicates the emotions that are connected with the inner man as they affect the actions of that person. The modern use of the term, of course, is a perversion of this truth. Paul longs to see and have fellowship with the Philippians.

This longing to be with them is a godly love inwrought by the Holy Spirit; it is patterned after the compassion (1 John 3:17) of Christ Himself. The [Pulpit Commentary's]{.class_4095} reference to the "heart and liver" (like the old "kidney-rein" routine in the Old Testament) doesn't mean anything. The "[bowels]{.class_4096}" of Christ are the inner feelings of Christ in His present state. In His present state (Eph. 1--2 and comments), His Body fills the universe. It is from WITHIN this Body that Paul speaks. (Don't look for that in a major commentary; you won't find it. Critics of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 can't read!)

Five "requests" (see vs. 4) have been sent up to the Lord for the Philippians.

1\. ["That your love may abound yet more and more"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9).

2\. ["That ye may approve things that are excellent"]{.class_4096} (vs. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_332}10).

3\. ["That ye may be sincere\...till the day of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10).

4\. ["That ye may be\...without offence till the day of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10).

5\. ["That you be filled with the fruits of righteousness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11).

Love without ["knowledge"]{.class_4096} and ["judgment"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9) is the kind of love which America has promoted, exalted, recommended, and worshipped since 1918. The two words are connected in the Greek texts but are [separated ]{.class_4095}in [Good News For Modern Man]{.class_4095} (1966), because ["Good News"]{.class_4095} is primarily an interdenominational hippy publication. The [New English Bible]{.class_4095} (1961) has a "wild one." It says that the prayer is that the Lord will give them the gift of true "DISCRIMINATION"! (Wow! That's really "up to date," don't you think? Imagine an international Socialist like C. H. Dodd putting THAT in a "Bible"!)

["That ye may approve"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10; cf. 1 Thess. 5:21). Love that is able to "discriminate" (and if a man cannot "discriminate" between two things that are different, you'd better keep him off the highway!) can find out what is [excellent ]{.class_4095}and what is [not]{.class_4095}. Again, this prayer was never answered (see comments under Eph. 3:14--15), for 95 percent of the commentators, revisers, and scholars, thought that the Westcott and Hort text of 1881 ([Vaticanus]{.class_4095} and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}) was an "excellent text." No Bible believer in his right mind would have considered [either ]{.class_4095}manuscript to be even "fair" manuscripts, let alone "excellent ones." As a matter of fact, a man would not need love, judgment, discrimination, approving, proving, comprehension, nor precise knowledge to see immediately that [Vaticanus]{.class_4095} and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095} are [Gnostic depravations ]{.class_4095}of the crassest sort. The "scholarship" behind them only proves that 95 percent of modern scholarship spends its time out of contact with truth and reality and spends most of its time with destructive criticism.

The things that are ["excellent"]{.class_4096} are listed in chapter 4 and 1 Corinthians 13. It is quite in keeping with the apostasy among Conservatives that Mounce, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Dummelow, and Caffin ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}) were not able to give any Biblical reference to the "excellent things." Dummelow drops clean out and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_333}tries "the things that DIFFER" instead of the things that are ["excellent,"]{.class_4096} but since Dummelow could not even tell the difference between Spirit baptism and water baptism (see Gal. 3:27 and notes), the substitution is rather ludicrous.

["Sincere and without offence"]{.class_4096} is the desired condition for the Christian at the Judgment Seat of Christ (see Col. 1:10, 22 and comments). Again (see the massacre of "[sincerity]{.class_4096}" in Eph. 6:24), the word ["sincere"]{.class_4096} arouses the wrath of the insincere professional critics. OUT it goes from the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961). OUT it goes from ["Good News"]{.class_4095} (1966). OUT it goes from the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946). OUT it goes from Dummelow. OUT it goes from Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown. OUT it goes from the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} (pg. 1321). And OUT it goes from Wuest's translation (pg. 459). If we are to take the profession of these Bible enemies at face value, we are to believe that they will arrive at the Judgment Seat "PURE" (see texts) without having to be ["sincere."]{.class_4096} (That is quite a thing you have going there, Doctor! Let me know how it comes out!)

["Filled with the fruits of righteousness"]{.class_4096} can only take place where the "fruit-bearer" is filled with the One who brings forth the fruit (see comments under Gal. 5:22 and Eph. 5:1). These fruits are ["by Jesus Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11), and God gets the glory and the praise for them. (Wuest's alteration of verse 10 means nothing, so we shall not reproduce it.)

1:12 But I would ye should understand, brethren, that the things which happened unto me have fallen out rather unto the furtherance of the gospel;

13 So that my bonds in Christ are manifest in all the palace, and in all other places;

14 And many of the brethren in the Lord, waxing confident by my bonds, are much more bold to speak the word without fear.

15 Some indeed preach Christ even of envy and strife; and some also of good will:

16 The one preach Christ of contention, not sincerely, supposing to add affliction to my bonds:

17 But the other of love, knowing that I am set for the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_334}defence of the gospel.

18 What then? notwithstanding, every way, whether in pretence, or in truth, Christ is preached; and I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.

Now Paul refers to his own problems and informs the Philippians of his present condition. While doing this, he cheerfully encourages them not to worry about it since "things are going fine," as far as the Gospel goes, and his imprisonment has inspired others to preach and has served as a testimony to his own faithfulness. Paul believes what he wrote back in verse 6. Knowing that the "[work]{.class_4096}" is INSIDE, that it is a guaranteed work, that its Author is God, and that it will be carried out by God, he is whistling "Beulah Land" in his cell instead of whistling "Birmingham Jail."

["The furtherance of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12) shows that the Gospel is going further than he had expected it to, and his jail sentence had only helped matters instead of hindering them. Now everybody in the palace at Rome KNOWS why he is in jail (vs. 13), although at the time of Acts 28, things were a little muddled.

Nearly all of the scholars object to the translation of ["to praitorio"]{.class_4095} as ["the palace."]{.class_4096} Many of them seem to feel that the word should have been left untranslated and simply transliterated as "the Praetorium." Accompanying this grumbling resentment is also the feeling that the "poor dumb" translators of 1611 didn't know what the Praetorium Guard was. Hoffmann, Abbott, Haupt, and Vincent insist that it is ["those forming the guard."]{.class_4095}

Following this brilliant conjecture, the [ASV, New ASV, RV, RSV, New RSV, New Jerusalem]{.class_4095}, Roman [Confraternity]{.class_4095}, and the [Amplified New Testament ]{.class_4095}step into line. But this time, the chaos among the critics is so great that no real case for the change can be proved. The Catholic [Rheims ]{.class_4095}says ["courts."]{.class_4095} The [NEB ]{.class_4095}says ["headquarters."]{.class_4095} Phillips says ["the whole imperial guard."]{.class_4095} [Good News]{.class_4095} conjectures ["palace guard."]{.class_4095} Dummelow interprets it as ["corps of troops."]{.class_4095} Ramsay and Mommsen suggest ["the judicial authorities."]{.class_4095} Alford, Meyer, DeWette, Von Soden, and Lipsius guess that it is ["the barracks or camp";]{.class_4095} and Bengel, Chrysostom, Mynster, and Theodoret say that it is ["the emperor's palace."]{.class_4095}

Tell me something? Doesn't the [AV]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_335} word cover ALL POSSIBILITIES? [Then why was it altered?]{.class_4095}

Consider! The men who altered it did not even have enough respect for each other to allow a reading that would cover their opponent! Wouldn't you think that the enemies of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 would at least allow one word to remain in, since it backed up [all ]{.class_4095}of their conjectures? No, the old despised, hated, ridiculed, abused [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 is so furiously detested that the scholars will even [cut their own throats to get rid of ONE WORD.]{.class_4095}

Leave the text as it stands. Paul's bonds were ["manifest in all the palace."]{.class_4096} ["ALL"]{.class_4096} means "all in the palace," and ["ALL OTHER PLACES"]{.class_4096} takes care of everybody in a "barracks or camp" outside the palace, and that is that. Phenomenon No. 8.

["Waxing confident\...are much more BOLD TO SPEAK THE WORD WITHOUT FEAR"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). Then Paul's prayer request (Eph. 6:18--20) was not only answered for him, but for his converts as well! Two classes of Christians are now inspired to "shovel in the coal" ("put the pedal on the floor," "pull out the stops," "pour on the steam," "step on the gas," "lay it on thick," "throw the fat on the fire," etc., "The New New New Amplified New New Testament," 1999).

1\. Those who said to themselves: "If he can take it, I can take it. He is not going to get ahead of me (1 Cor. 9:24) and get the prize. If he can do it, I can do it (Eph. 6:10)! Here I come, Paul!"

2\. Those who said to themselves: "Now you see what happened to Paul! That is proof that he was wrong and we are right. His Sunday School attendance has dropped, and he has lost three members who make over \$8,000 a year. This proves that our Bible teaching is correct, and now we need to redouble our efforts while he is in jail. When he gets out, he'll know that we were right if we can get a 40 percent increase in the Junior Department and a 60 percent increase in the Young Married Couples." (You see, it is NOT ALL in the "Greek original"!)

["Some\...preach Christ\...of envy and strife"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). The Bullingerites are trying to get members to leave the Baptist churches; that is their MOTIVE in preaching Christ. Calvinists are trying to prove that evangelism is heresy; that is their motive in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_336}preaching. Campbellites are trying to rebaptize Methodists, Baptists, and Presbyterians to get their money; that is their motive in preaching. A man's motive in preaching is soon apparent by the amount of time he spends on a particular theological "hobbyhorse." No man preaching from the [right motive ]{.class_4095}ever wastes his ministry on [one doctrinal peculiarity.]{.class_4095}

The Bible is a gold mine of information on motives, purposes, intents, goals, plans, sports, science, marriage, divorce, education, separation, experience, sin, mistakes, war, philosophy, prophecy, prayer, salvation, nature, economics, politics, hypocrisy, humor, history, and demonology; no man can PREACH it properly if he spends even 10 percent of his ministry on ["Greek originals," Sovereignty, Water Baptism, the Body Mystery, or the Baptism of the Holy Ghost.]{.class_4095} These "hobbyhorses" mark the preacher as one motivated by ["ENVY AND STRIFE,"]{.class_4096} not "COMPASSION AND BURDEN." ["The Body mystery"]{.class_4095} takes up less than one-fiftieth of the New Testament. Water baptism is mentioned fewer times than prayer, the Second Coming, consecration, sanctification, witnessing, Bible study, godly living, the Antichrist, or salvation. "Limited Atonement" and "Irresistible Grace" are [implied]{.class_4095} (not mentioned!) in less than 2 percent of the New Testament verses. And Jesus Christ never made any correction of the Hebrew Old Testament in three years of ministry. You will not find the expression "a better translation should be" ONE TIME in four Gospels! A ministry composed of these subjects is the ministry of a [failure in the ministry.]{.class_4095}

The greatest failures in the ministry are the hobby- horse riders who devote their lives to altering the [AV ]{.class_4095}text with "the original Greek." ["Envy and strife"]{.class_4096} are the obvious motives behind the work of these men, as they deeply and bitterly resent the power and authority of the Holy Bible in its [Authorized]{.class_4095} form. They are not merely at strife with [the Body of Christ]{.class_4095}; they are at strife with the [Holy Spirit.]{.class_4095}

Quite typical of these hobbyhorse riders are Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer of Bob Jones University, who have devoted their time and ministries to overthrowing the [King James]{.class_4095} text by way of a "Biblical Viewpoint." One may safely say that the hobby which these men []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_337}follow is the hobbyhorse that Greek faculties have been riding since Origen was elected president of the University of Alexandria. This hobbyhorse is the most popular of all the theological hobbies, and those who cater to it can be found in every theological camp in Christendom. You see, even Bullingerites, Calvinists, Campbellites, Russellites, Catholics, Fabians, Socialists, Communists, atheists, Protestants, Jews, and Satanists enjoy altering the text of the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible.

Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer, following the universal tradition of dead-orthodox apostates, will subjugate every major doctrine, every verse, and [every word ]{.class_4095}in the Bible to their particular "hobby." Their hobby, as the hobby of Westcott and Hort, Machen, Warfield, Gregory, Moulton, Souter, Metzger, Aland, Nestle, and ORIGEN, is defying the authority of the English Reformation text and overthrowing it by subjecting it to their own private critical interpretations. The final court of appeals for any man listed above was neither the Bible nor "THE Greek text"; [it was his own opinion of the material found in the Greek lexicon. ]{.class_4095}The hobby of 90 percent of the Conservative Schools in America is [the destructive criticism of the Reformation text.]{.class_4095}

["The one preach Christ OF CONTENTION, NOT SINCERELY\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 16). This time, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and[ RSV]{.class_4095} put in the word ["sincerely"]{.class_4096}! (See vs. 10 and comments.)

The ["Christ of contention"]{.class_4096} is the Christ of the sermons by Mormons, Seventh-day Adventists, Jehovah's Witnesses, Hypercalvinists, "revolutionaries," and Hyperdispensationalists. It is a Christ who may "show or place," but He never "wins." He is always competing with days of observance, predestination, body mysteries, water pools, "social involvement," tribulation passages, buried gold plates, or (in the Catholic Communion) with Blessed Mary, Blessed Joseph, and Blessed John the BAPTIST (oops!). If the cry of the world is ["Sir, we would see Jesus" ]{.class_4096}(Johm 12:21), it is a cry that falls on deaf ears many times.

The preachers of verse 16 are doing what they do because they hate Paul and are envious of his power and influence. His ministry is a success; theirs is a failure. If he had his head cut off (A.D. 62), then their ministry would have been shot. Some churches and schools []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_338}only work to compete with somebody they don't like.

["But the other of love, knowing that I am set for the defence of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). So Paul has some friends in spite of his "police record," and they stick with him. They preach Christ because they know that that is what Paul would want them to do, and that is what they know [he would be doing ]{.class_4095}if he were "out on bail."

["Every way, whether in pretence, or in truth\...rejoice\...rejoice"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). This is the right attitude of the true God-called preacher. Regardless of anyone's motives, as long as Jesus Christ is getting some publicity, praise the Lord! If it takes envy and strife to get some of the mossbacks and deadheads in a church back on the ball for Jesus, then "let the good times roll!" If a passive, cold, backslidden Christian cannot get interested in Daily Vacation Bible School (DVBS) or visitation unless he is inspired to do it to get even with a preacher he doesn't like in another church, then give him the inspiration! In the day of judgment (see vss. 19--20), the hated preacher will get the reward anyway, so "let'er rip"!

The people who love me will bear fruit, and I will get a share in their work because I inspired them; and the people who hate me will bear fruit (to prove they are spiritual!), and I will get a share of that because I was the reason they "ministered!" You can't lose! ["AND I THEREIN DO REJOICE, YEA, AND WILL REJOICE."]{.class_4096}

1:19 For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer, and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,

20 According to my earnest expectation and my hope, that in nothing I shall be ashamed, but that with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ shall be magnified in my body, whether it be by life, or by death.

21 For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.

22 But if I live in the flesh, this is the fruit of my labour: yet what I shall choose I wot not.

23 For I am in a strait betwixt two, having a desire to depart, and to be with Christ; which is far better:

24 Nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you.

25 And having this confidence, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all for your furtherance and joy of faith;

26 That your rejoicing may be more abundant in Jesus Christ for me by my coming to you again.

We must go ahead with the exposition immediately, for the thought is connected with verse 18. Nearly all the commentators divide the section at verse 18, but this is because they have failed to take the "long look" they were told to take in verses 6 and 10. (We will list the errors of Lipsius, Wordsworth, Wuest, Luken, Lumby, Wiesinger, Davidson, Alford, Rendall, Ellicott, Westcott, Hort, Lightfoot, Meyer, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown at the conclusion of the section.)

["For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19) is a reference to [spiritual things]{.class_4095}, not to somebody "getting out of jail." In spite of these obvious connections, the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} recommends the apostate [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) which has taken away Paul's salvation and made it a ["deliverance."]{.class_4095} (Note how the [RSV ]{.class_4095}did this same thing in Zech. 9:9 when dealing with Jesus Christ.) Dummelow finds the truth, for a change, and gives 1 Corinthians 9:23 and 2 Timothy 4:18 as cross-references.

["And the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 19; cf. 4:19). The spiritual need will be met in jail (Phil. 4:11); and the fruit of the Spirit will be manifest in ["the]{.class_4096} [day of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vss. 6, 10), not the "day of the LORD."

["According to my earnest expectation and my hope, that in NOTHING I SHALL BE ASHAMED"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20; cf. Rom. 1:16). He will not be ashamed of being arrested and thrown in jail and will look at the whole thing in the light of Romans 8:28 and 2 Corinthians 11:23--33. He will witness to the Highway Patrolman when he is writing out the ticket. He will witness to the Sheriff while he is being handcuffed (Acts 21:37). He will handle the matter with at least as much courage as a demon-possessed Communist (Angela Davis?) and will witness ["with all boldness, as always"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20). The change of scenery will not change his approach.

["Christ shall be magnified in my body"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_340} (vs. 20), and one might say that this was Paul's first and supreme passion throughout a lifetime (Gal. 2:20 and comments, with Col. 1:18 and comments). Paul magnified Christ with chains rattling on his wrists. Moody pled like a father; Jonathan Edwards argued with a severe argument; Bunyon wrote a sublime allegory; Talmage handled it as an artistic poet; Payson wept, leaning on the pulpit; Billy Sunday riveted red hot texts into his congregation; and Whitefield, as an actor, overwhelmed his audience with pathos, terror, and drama. But they all desired for Christ to ["be magnified,"]{.class_4096} and they magnified Him.

["Whether it be by LIFE, or by DEATH" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20). It didn't make any difference how the "trial" came out at Rome. Paul was praising God on the way to the chopping block (2 Tim. 4:6--8); and if they had whacked off his head here, he would have been ["with Christ; which is far better"]{.class_4096} (vs. 23). All a guillotine can do for a Christian is give him a "shortcut" to Glory.

They threatened a French Huguenot with exile, and he said, "My home is in Glory." They threatened him with loss of goods, and he said, "My treasures are laid up in Heaven." They threatened him with imprisonment, and he said, "I'll be freer in chains than you'll be out of them" (John 8:34--36). Finally, the officials roared, "We'll cut off your head!" To which he replied, "If you do, my soul will get home to Heaven quicker than his Majesty's horses can get to the palace gate."

["For TO ME to live is CHRIST, and to die is GAIN"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21). We cannot stop here; it is too good to stop! ["A desire to depart\...WHICH IS FAR BETTER"]{.class_4096} (vs. 23). (Cf. 2 Cor. 5:1--8 and 1 Thess. 5:9--10.)

There is no mistaking about the mind of the apostle. He is a "suicidal maniac." He got up there one time (2 Cor. 12:2) and didn't care to come back at all; and ever since he got back, he was having "accidents" (2 Cor. 11:23--33) in the hopes that somebody would [overdo it!]{.class_4095}

1\. A dead Christian is far better off than a live one.

2\. He is not unconscious; he is LIVING with Christ (1 Thess. 5:9--10).

3\. He does not go into the "grave," in spite of Russell and Rutherford's inane perversions of the truth.

4\. He is ["absent from the body"]{.class_4096} and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_341}["present with the Lord"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 5:1--8).

5\. He is ["with Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 23).

6\. His death is a ["gain"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21).

This immediately places the Christian beyond hope of recovery in the eyes of science, religion, psychology, psychiatry, Protestantism, Buddhism, Taoism, Communism, education, Catholicism, Judaism, Confucianism, and 95 percent of any of the political, religious, or scientific leaders in any age.
