You see, the Bible-believing Christian KNOWS where he is going at death (1 John 5:13), and he knows the [direction ]{.class_4095}and [distance ]{.class_4095}that he will travel (Eph. 3:18 and comments) and what is [awaiting him ]{.class_4095}at his destination (see comments on Col. 3:1--3). This ["is the spirit of prophecy" ]{.class_4096}(Rev. 19:10) which the world has not (John 16:13) and cannot receive (John 14:17). It means that when a man is saved, he is forever segregated (willingly or unwillingly) from the world system and everything in it.

No man on earth can say that "dying" is ["far better"]{.class_4096} than [living ]{.class_4095}unless that man is either [sick mentally]{.class_4095} or a [Bible-believing Christian.]{.class_4095} [You see now why the HEW board of the United Nations places both men in the same bracket!]{.class_4095}

Death is not GAIN for a Communist. It is not GAIN for a Catholic. If it were GAIN for an unregenerate suicide, he could not say ["far better"]{.class_4096} or ["with Christ"]{.class_4096} unless he lied openly. No Jehovah's Witness would say that death was ["far better"]{.class_4096} than living. That profession is reserved for the saved child of God.

The other alternative to the "[far better]{.class_4096}" position is "living in the flesh" (vs. 22), "abiding in the flesh" in order to serve others (vs. 24), and to bear fruit for Christ (vs. 22). Paul is torn between the two alternatives, but if it was left entirely up to him, he would choose to go home and be with the Lord (vs. 23). The only thing that kept him chained to the earth was the "need" that people had for his message and ministry. (If that were the only thing that kept a modern minister chained to this earth, America would not be in the pitiful condition it is in today; however, the modern minister \[saved or lost\] is usually chained to TV programs, relatives, houses and lands, building a career, promoting programs and attendance, and trying []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_342}to cooperate with his denomination so he can "retire" with a little nest egg.)

Verses 25 and 26 are self-explanatory. Paul is going to stay on earth (this time) to minister to the Philippians and help them to grow in joy and faith. Even though he may stay in jail for awhile, he looks forward to an indefinite date in the future (vs. 27) when they will rejoice greatly on seeing him.

Now this is the "meat" of the passage. The [AV ]{.class_4095}critics, of course, will call this "milk" and then direct you to that mass (and maze) of entangled Greek grammar and hypothetical conjectures, under the guise of showing you the real "meat." What follows, however, is not "meat" (and if it were, it would not pass any government inspection at the packing house). What follows is clabbered skim milk.

First of all, the reader should notice how that almost 100 percent of the scholars attribute Paul's quotation of verse 19 to Job 13:16 [in the LXX]{.class_4095}. The [authority ]{.class_4095}for this blatant lie is an Old Testament Greek manuscript written more than 100 years [after Paul's death]{.class_4095} (see [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} 1970, chap. 4).

Secondly, Dummelow tells us that the two alternatives in verses 21--23 were "balanced" so that Paul had [no preference]{.class_4095} (Commentary, pg. 972)!

Thirdly, we learn from Rev. Kennedy that verse 17 should have been verse 16 and verse 16 should have been verse 17; yet Rev. Kennedy [does not dare make the change.]{.class_4095} Having told us that [Aleph, A, B, D, E, F, G, P,]{.class_4095} etc. transpose the verses, Kennedy goes by the [Receptus]{.class_4095}!! Phenomenon No. 17.

Now, this is "most interesting" (to cite the dead-orthodox cliché), for it shows that whoever "messed" with [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095} and [Vaticanus]{.class_4095} (and influenced the authors of [A]{.class_4095} and [D]{.class_4095} to follow him) was deeply disturbed by the references to ["envy and strife"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) and "sincerity" (vs. 16) in the text. This obvious distortion of order in [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}and [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}is even more apparent when one compares 16 and 17 with verse 15. The order in verse 15 is:

1\. ["Envy and strife."]{.class_4096}

2\. ["Good will."]{.class_4096}

This is the exact order of the Textus Receptus in the next two verses:

1\. ["Contention"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_343} (vs. 16).

2\. ["Love"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17).

You see, "the most authentic manuscripts" and "the oldest manuscripts" and "the best manuscripts" are sometimes nothing but a great big HORSELAUGH. Phenomenon No. 11.

Mounce's translation ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1323) of verse 23 is about as "modern" as the [Clementine Vulgate.]{.class_4095} He has told us that Paul's quandary is over being "immobilized" by "two opposing considerations." (Real plain, eh?)

Bullinger has a massive footnote on verse 23 to the effect that Paul was not speaking of "death" but of a "rapture." How this is done in view of ["to DIE is gain"]{.class_4096} is a little hard to say, but when you get to know the [AV ]{.class_4095}critics, you expect spells of irrationality at regular intervals.

John Calvin says, "For to me Christ is gain," which is no rendering at all. Wuest has Paul "striking his tent" and being held "perpendicular" and winds up by giving us the impression that Paul desired to STAY more than he desired to GO ([Expanded New Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 461).

In order to justify his "rapture" (see Bullinger, above), Bullinger has destroyed the English of ["to die is gain"]{.class_4096}; so he tells us on page 1774 of his [Companion Bible]{.class_4095} that [Christ would gain]{.class_4095} by Paul's death! Isn't it strange how a man's heart motives can affect his Greek scholarship? The intelligent reader will dismiss these suggested corrections to the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text and attribute all of them to ["strife," "envy,"]{.class_4096} and ["contention"]{.class_4096} (vss. 15--16).

1:27 Only let your conversation be as it becometh the gospel of Christ: that whether I come and see you, or else be absent, I may hear of your affairs, that ye stand fast in one spirit, with one mind striving together for the faith of the gospel;

28 And in nothing terrified by your adversaries: which is to them an evident token of perdition, but to you of salvation, and that of God.

29 For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ, not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for his sake;

30 Having the same conflict which ye saw in me, and now hear to be in me.

Paul is going to be spared this time, and he will be around to minister for another few years---about seven more. He is abiding ["in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (vs. 24) although he is ["not in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:8--9); and although he is ["in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 10:3), he does not walk ["after the flesh."]{.class_4096} (You see, no interpretation but the plain meaning of the plain English will yield any sense---see Col. 2:17. If you do not have the GEOGRAPHICAL location of the Christian in SPACE, you do not have his spiritual location IN CHRIST. Compare Josh. 24:14 and Eph. 5:30.)

["Only let your conversation\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 27; see note on Eph. 2:3). ["Be as it becometh the gospel of Christ\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 27; cf. Gal. 1:7, 11). In the passage, the[ "gospel"]{.class_4096} is used not only for the substance of 1 Corinthians 15:1--4 (which see), but it also stands for the entire body of truth that accompanies the conversion of the sinner. This body of truth is found in Galatians and Romans. A man who [believes ]{.class_4095}these truths should [live ]{.class_4095}like he believes them.

["That ye stand fast in ONE SPIRIT, with ONE MIND striving together for THE FAITH of the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 27). This is the practical way of saying Ephesians 4:1--6, which see. The[ "striving"]{.class_4096} is not to "get saved," like the Campbellite elders misinterpret the passage; nor is it to "stay saved," as the Seventh-day Adventists privately interpret the passage; but it is to make an effort to believe the same truths as one body and to ACT in accordance with that belief, even though such conduct will cause persecution and slander---see the next three verses.

["And in nothing terrified by your ADVERSARIES: which is to them an EVIDENT TOKEN OF PERDITION, but to you OF SALVATION, and that of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28). At this point, we arrive at one of the most remarkable cases of perversion among "competent scholars" found anywhere in either Testament. The words have been so constructed that the dead-orthodox apostates are UNABLE to make any sense out of them. Now, when a dead-orthodox apostate like Kenneth Wuest, Dr. A. T. Robertson, Moulton, Milligan, Souter, Thieme, or the "best-known scholars" get []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_345}into this kind of a predicament, they simply say: "The text is obscure," or "It is probably corrupt" (see Col. 2:23 and comments). The reasoning, here, is very simple, and it follows the pattern set down by Origen (A.D. 185--254) more than eighteen centuries back.

1\. [I]{.class_4095} am able to judge the Scriptures and interpret them.

2\. I can do this because of my superior [education and ability.]{.class_4095}

3\. Therefore, if I [cannot ]{.class_4095}understand a verse, [there is something wrong with the verse.]{.class_4095}

4\. Therefore, I will [rearrange the verse]{.class_4095} so that I can explain it.

This is the exact layout for every dead-orthodox apostate from Origen to E. Schuyler English, and the format is also the official format of the Roman Catholic hierarchy. Rome approached the Scriptures this way from the first time that Jerome perverted them to the last ["New Jerusalem Bible"]{.class_4095} on the market.

The word "courage" has been inserted (either directly or by implication) in every major commentary on the market, and the word ["your"]{.class_4095} has been placed before ["salvation"]{.class_4096} in every major translation since 1880. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown state it well by saying that ["which"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28) means "your not being afraid." The [Amplified ]{.class_4095}lays out the whole private interpretation by saying that the fearlessness of the Philippians was a clear sign and proof that their adversaries were going to be destroyed and that the adversaries knew this. The [RSV ]{.class_4095}calls the sign an ["omen,"]{.class_4095} but all take the same point of view: i.e., your courage is convincing your adversaries that they are damned to perdition, and it is proof [to them]{.class_4095} that you are saved.

Nothing could be further from the truth, whether that truth were the truth of history, theology, practical Christianity, or Bible doctrine. Paul's [courage ]{.class_4095}never convinced [any enemy ]{.class_4095}throughout a [lifetime ]{.class_4095}that [he was saved]{.class_4095} and [they were lost.]{.class_4095}

Not one commentator or reviser in the last 100 years noticed verse 30: ["Having the same conflict which ye saw in me."]{.class_4096} The teaching, therefore, of Rendall, Mounce, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Moulton, Milligan, Lange, Neal, Clarke, Souter, Custer, Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Lipsius, Haupt, Riddle, Salmond, Wordsworth, Lightfoot, Alford, Bengel, Wuest, and the committees of the [RV, RSV, ASV, New ASV, New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095}, and [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_346} is a total blank. It is a perfect example of Roman revisers trying to invent a "Christian teaching" which the hierarchy will officially adopt.

To enforce this official blooper, the Greek ["humin"]{.class_4095} (["to you"]{.class_4096}) has been dropped and the ["humon"]{.class_4095} ("your") of [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}has been adopted. The theory that the ["which"]{.class_4096} is a reference to "courage" is based on the inability of the translator and reviser to find the antecedent. Notice how the same thing happened (identically!) in Ephesians 3:19 when the "brains" found a passage they couldn't "master."

But the trouble lies much deeper, for Bible trouble is always, primarily, HEART trouble. The inexperienced, naive, and gullible revisers of 1881, 1901, 1946, 1952, 1963, 1966, and 1969 could never understand this kind of passage, for they have had no [practical experience ]{.class_4095}in what Paul is talking about. The [AV ]{.class_4095}translators DID. You see, it has been assumed by all that scholarship and science equip a man to understand the Scriptures if the reviser is a saved man. This is the position taken by the revisers of the [Scofield Reference Bible.]{.class_4095} This overlooks the fact that [Pauline Christians]{.class_4095} are what God ordered (see comments under Eph. 5:1 and 6:10--17). How does an [un-Pauline]{.class_4095} Christian go about expositing a verse that deals with suffering reproach (vs. 30) for the Gospel's sake (vs. 27) before a heathen populace (vs. 28) that believes that Christians are ATHEISTS (Acts 19:26)?

You see, the truth of it is that none of the major commentators listed in this commentary (or any of the revisers) knew anything about [real opposition ]{.class_4095}from unsaved people. But the most ignorant believer who spent time in witnessing would have no trouble at all in grasping the text, without any "Greek helps."

The truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth is as follows:

1\. All heathen adversaries of the Gospel think that when a Christian suffers (vs. 30), it is because [he ]{.class_4095}is cursed of God and headed for perdition (Job 2:9). (Could God write it any clearer? Wouldn't you be a consummate idiot to think that Greek or Hebrew would make it any clearer?)

2\. Nearly all Christians who suffer---while being ridiculed by the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_347}lost---tend to lose their courage, and at times they become terrified with the thought that [God has abandoned them]{.class_4095} (Lam. 3:18--40).

3\. Paul is encouraging the Philippians to go right on with it and ignore the suffering (as he had to do! Phil. 1:12, 18, Acts 16:28--30) and accept the [suffering ]{.class_4095}(1 Pet. 2:19) as a sign of God's [blessing ]{.class_4095}(Luke 6:22) and as evidence that they are saved (Heb. 12:4--8), even though their adversaries accept the [same token]{.class_4095} (John 9:2) as proof that they are [lost ]{.class_4095}(vs. 28).

[But you cannot get that reading unless you have obeyed God in the matter of an aggressive, militant witness in the middle of the heathen populace in which you live!]{.class_4095} Revisers are about as "militant" as the Campfire Girls. And Greek scholarship is totally incapable of producing the truth in this text.

Where did the ["humon"]{.class_4095} originate? It originated AFTER the Council of Nicaea (A.D. 325); it originated AFTER Constantine succeeded in pulling the suckers into the ecumenical council. The reading originated with dead-orthodox apostates who went along with Rome to avoid persecution. Philippians 1:28 became a "back number" after the Edict of Milan (A.D. 313), and the apostate "Conservatives" at Nicaea were stupid enough to think that their "bold stand" was proof to the heathen that the heathen were lost and that they---the apostate Conservatives---were saved! You couldn't make a worse mistake if you filled up your gas tank with Hadacol.

1\. David's troubles---caused by his adversary (2 Sam 12--13)---are proof to the adversary that David is damned (2 Sam. 16:8).

2\. Job's troubles---caused by his adversary (Job 1--2)---are proof to his wife that he is damned (Job 2:9).

3\. The blind man's troubles (John 9:1) were proof to the disciples that he or his parents had something to do with perdition (John 9:2).

4\. But above all! Jesus Christ's suffering under His adversaries (Psa. 22:1--15) was an evident token to them that He was "cursed of God" and a blasphemer headed for Hell (John 8:13), who should be STONED (John 8:59) for His "courage."

That is, everywhere, [throughout any passage in either Testament,]{.class_4095} this [basic, fundamental, Christian truth ]{.class_4095}is as clear as the Bodensee on a cloudless day in May. What God does to bless, correct, instruct, and save His people (Heb. 12:4--8; Rom. 8:35--36) []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_348}is proof to the lost that those people are DAMNED.

The reason why every major scholar from Eusebius to English (who despised the [Textus Receptus]{.class_4095}) could not understand the passage is because every one of them thought that they were impressing the heathen. They were impressing nobody but the poor, deluded, deceived ministerial students who were "green" and inexperienced in the methods, motives, tactics, and operations of Satan.

Their alteration of the truth is a disgraceful and sickening thing to behold. Observe how the very next verse, in English or GREEK, confirms the correct interpretation. ["For UNTO YOU"]{.class_4096} (Gk.: ["hoti humin,"]{.class_4095} exactly as the ["humin"]{.class_4095} of verse 28---["but TO YOU of salvation"]{.class_4096}).

["For unto YOU it is given\...to SUFFER FOR HIS SAKE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29). The ["hoti"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) shows clearly that the ["which"]{.class_4096} of verse 28 is connected not only with the proper attitude of the Christian (["in nothing terrified"]{.class_4096}), but has to do with the suffering of the Christian while he is being ridiculed by the adversary (cf. 1 Pet. 3--4)!

["Having the SAME CONFLICT\...to be IN ME"]{.class_4096} (vs. 30). Paul's sufferings convinced no one that he was saved and they were lost, and not even Agrippa figured that he was going to Hell simply because Paul was bold to speak while in chains. Festus took Paul's boldness to be "an evident token" of his [insanity ]{.class_4095}(Acts 26:24), and the Judaizers took it to be an evidence of his [weakness and rudeness]{.class_4095} (2 Cor. 10:10, 11:6). You don't know much about human nature or the Bible if you are fool enough to believe that your "courage" will convince an unsaved man that he is going to Hell. The Holy Spirit does this work (John 16:8), and if ["Felix trembled"]{.class_4096} (Acts 24:25), it was the Holy Spirit who shook him. For every man who trembled under a bold testimony, there were 100 that rose up in self-righteous indignation to get rid of it (see Acts 13, 18, 21).

The "same conflict" was seen in Paul when he first came to Philippi and is now being seen again, following his imprisonment in Rome. The Philippians, then, are being arrested and threatened with imprisonment, and this only adds weight to our remarks above. Their witness didn't convince anyone that they were saved and their adversaries were lost. The adversaries took for granted that the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2367.xhtml_page_349}Philippian Christians were forsaken by the gods and abandoned (Psa. 89:38--45), and they were being "worked over" just like the Sanhedrin had worked Stephen over. The ["evident token"]{.class_4096} is exactly the reverse of what Lightfoot, Schaff, Robertson, Davis, Tregelles, Gregory, Wordsworth, Vincent, Alford, Lipsius, Van Hengel, Buttmann, Wiesinger, Davidson, Meyer, Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Rendall, Mounce, and Caffin ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}) thought it to be. And their misreading of the text is revolting when one sees their reason for the misreading---lack of experience and immaturity.

Among the numerous errors placed over the infallible text are:

1\. No salvation (vs. 28); only ["victory"]{.class_4095} ([Good News,]{.class_4095} 1966).

2\. ["Constancy and fearlessness"]{.class_4095} have been added by the [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095} (1958) without any manuscript authority for the words to be supplied.

3\. ["Not caring two straws FOR your enemies"]{.class_4095} (Phillips, 1958) is good preaching for a Nineteenth-Century English audience, but it has nothing to do with Bible translating.

4\. The "attempts" of the adversaries would prove to them that they were only leading them to destruction (Mounce, [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1324).

5\. ["Token"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28) should have been "omen" ([RSV]{.class_4095}, 1946).

6\. Your adversaries could find out they were damned "if they only could see it" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown Commentary, pg. 1304).

7\. ["Given"]{.class_4096} (vs. 29) should have been "bestowed" or "graciously bestowed" (Lipsius, Lueken, and Schnedermann), while Meyer, Alford, and Davidson like to think of it as "WAS granted."

Anything but believe the text.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml}

## Chapter 2 {.heading_116}

2:1 If there be therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels and mercies,

2 Fulfil ye my joy, that ye be likeminded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind.

3 Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.

4 Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.

The entire chapter deals with humility in service, in one form or another. The great passage on the humility and exaltation of Christ is found between verses 5 and 11, and nowhere in Philippians is the vicious, bigoted prejudice of the narrow-minded, intolerant enemies of the Bible any more manifest than in this section.

["If there be therefore any consolation\...comfort\...fellowship\...bowels and mercies" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1; cf. 1:8 on ["bowels"]{.class_4096}). It is taken for granted that these things certainly are to be found[ "in Christ,"]{.class_4096} and there is no questioning as to their presence. The first-class conditional clause is simply: "If these things are there, then---['Fulfil ye my joy'"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2).

The substitution of ["exhortation"]{.class_4095} for ["consolation"]{.class_4096} ([ASV]{.class_4095}, 1901) is further off base than the [RSV ]{.class_4095}reading: ["encouragement."]{.class_4095} (The comments of Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are excellent, for a change, on the passage, and the cross-references given---1 Cor. 12:4; 2 Cor. 13:14; Phil. 1:9---are very proper.) If the Philippians have experienced the consolation and comfort of Christ (2 Cor. 1:3--7), they should be able to "get together in unity" (see vs. 2; Gal. 6:1--2; 1 Pet. 3:8). Not only should this be an incentive, but the love and mercy (vs. 1) they have received from Christ and the fellowship they have with Him through the Spirit should enable them to put away any schisms (as 4:2) and have the ["SAME LOVE, being of ONE ACCORD, of ONE MIND"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2).

Mounce, who is a sort of Wuest-commentator, prefers for us to be "knit" together in one "soul" (vs. 2). Exactly how this is done when every soul is a separate "ego" or ["eimi"]{.class_4095} (Gk.), is a little difficult to see. The words ["mind"]{.class_4096} and ["accord"]{.class_4096} ([King James]{.class_4095} text) carry out the thought much better. (Mounce's conjecture came from Bishop Ellicott \[[Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 59\].)

Nothing is more pleasing to a father and mother than to know that their children are living harmoniously without bickering and fighting (2 John 4), and nothing could please a spiritual father (1 Cor. 4:15) more than to know that his converts were standing for the truth without fighting among themselves. And this, of course, is the rarest of things. "Civil wars" are the common lot for the "soldiers of faith," and the Body of Christ has seen many more revolutionaries hollering for their "rights" than it has seen trained shock-troops charging the real enemy.

The people who divided the Body of Christ were John Mark (Acts 15:37--39), Demas (2 Tim. 4:10), Diotrephes (3 John 9--10), the "hobbyhorse riders" (Acts 20:30), the Roman Catholic politicians (Rev. 2:6, 14--15, 20), and the revision committees of 1881, 1901, 1946, 1952, and 1963. The revision committees did, by far, the best job because they forced Christians to fight over ["authority."]{.class_4095} Rome was never able to steal the Christian's faith in the Bible, but the revision committees pulled off the stunt. (Where the atheists, Communists, evolutionists, and Papists failed, the editors of the [New Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095} succeeded admirably.)

["Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). Nothing is to be done to strut the flesh, display the intellect, obtain coveted articles, get the preeminent place, promote self, or split the church into factions (Gal. 5:26; 1 Cor. 3:3; 3 John 9--11). The exchange of "thinking" for "doing" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown) only destroys the sense of the verse. The [mind ]{.class_4095}which promoted the [action ]{.class_4095}is mentioned in the second clause. That is made clear by what follows, for the illustration that follows is the type of MIND that led Jesus Christ to DO what He DID. Therefore, the exchange of "thinking" for the [AV]{.class_4095}'s italics only shows the lack of spiritual insight on the part of the critic.

The [RV]{.class_4095}'s substitution of ["faction"]{.class_4095} for ["strife"]{.class_4096} is also needless. The [RV]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_352} committee was trying to prove that it was "uniform," so it translated ["eritheia"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) as ["faction"]{.class_4095} every time it showed up. But as we have said before, absolute uniformity, in every case, would prove nothing except the desire of the translator to overthrow the Received text and the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611. The Holy Spirit Himself is not always "uniform" (see citations in Acts 13:40--41, 7:16, 43, etc.), and the attempts of Origen and Eusebius (A.D. 200--350) to force Him to be uniform by inventing an ["LXX" ]{.class_4095}doesn't fool anyone but Swete, Robertson, Neal, Custer, Davis, Moulton, Milligan, et al.

Since this freedom of translation is allowed, the vicious, prejudiced, bigoted critics of the [AV ]{.class_4095}text---Robertson, Davis, Moulton, Milligan, Souter, Westcott, Hort, Weigle, Schaff, et al.---cry: "Then why do [you ]{.class_4095}object to [us ]{.class_4095}taking liberties in our translations, and why are you so pickish on [words]{.class_4095}?!" Answer: "Very simple. [YOU threw the first stone.]{.class_4095} YOU are the mudslinger. You are the innocent Adolph, criticizing Poles for [abusing 'German citizens.']{.class_4095} You are the innocent Castro, determined to 'liberate' Cuba."

If [you ]{.class_4095}can change the words, [the AV translators]{.class_4095} [can change them, and they have the liberty to do so WITHOUT YOU OPENING YOUR YAP ABOUT IT ONE TIME. ]{.class_4095}If you are going to prove that [they ]{.class_4095}were wrong and every word should be translated "uniformly," then the first time[ you fail to translate a word uniformly,]{.class_4095} you have disqualified yourself as a scholar and have shown your colors for what you are: A DISGRUNTLED, FRUSTRATED, DEAD-ORTHODOX APOSTATE that a real Christian would not follow sixteen feet.

You see, neither the [RV ]{.class_4095}nor the [ASV ]{.class_4095}are "uniform." The [RV ]{.class_4095}just happens to be "uniform" on ["faction,"]{.class_4095} so they insert it into James 3:14. Now go look at James 3:14 and see how much sense this makes! "Faction in your heart"? Why, [Webster's Dictionary]{.class_4095} says that ["faction"]{.class_4095} is "a [group of people ]{.class_4095}who have common aims usually dissident from the main body!" Well, tell us something, Doctor. If one has a "FACTION" in his heart, is it a small one or a large one? [Two people or ten?]{.class_4095} A thousand? How did they crawl into your heart when the only things mentioned in the verse are ADJECTIVES? Shouldn't a man learn English before he starts to translate [Greek]{.class_4095}? (It would seem not.) Phenomenon No. 9.

["Let each esteem other better than themselves"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3 cf. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_353}Rom. 12:10; Eph. 4:2). This is the greatest "cure all" ever suggested for problems in the local church. The only trouble with it is that it somewhat resembles the Law (cf. Rom. 13:8--9). It is ["weak through the flesh"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:3; Gal. 3:19). Have you ever been in an assembly where every Christian considered any of the rest of the believers BETTER than himself? Now, that would be an assembly!

Mounce hastens to prevent any assembly from getting that way by telling us that ["better"]{.class_4096} doesn't mean "superior," but only "worthy of preferential treatment." (Isn't that sweet!) Is that what Christ had in mind when He showed up (see the next two verses)? [Then what do Luke 22:26--27; John 3:16 and Matthew 10:24--25 mean?]{.class_4095}

["Good News"]{.class_4095} is still not convinced, so the verse is destroyed by making it a negative statement: ["never thinking that YOU are better than others."]{.class_4095} (Fine. Now we are all [equal ]{.class_4095}just like Marx, Langston Hughes, Gus Hall, and Engels wanted it!) But any Greek text emphasizes the "superiority" of the matter; and not even the reactionary [ASV, RV, RSV,]{.class_4095} and [Amplified ]{.class_4095}are deceived into accepting the reading of the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}.

["Look not every man on his own things"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4; cf. Gal. 6:2 and comments). That is, don't spend all your time thinking about yourself and magnifying your own strong qualities and achievements, ["But every man also on the things of others"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4). Look out after your neighbor's interests (Rom. 15:2) as well as your own (1 Cor. 8:13). Philippians 2:21 is a perfect illustration of someone who ignores the exhortation of this passage.

2:5 Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus:

6 Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God:

7 But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant, and was made in the likeness of men:

8 And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, and became obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.

9 Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name:

10 That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth;

11 And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

The passage is called "poetry" by the writer for the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} (Robert Mounce, Ph.D., Associate Professor of Biblical Literature and Greek, Bethel College and Seminary, St. Paul, Minn.). Serious students of the Bible who take their work to heart recognize immediately this old Judges 5/Deuteronomy 32 gimmick, which originated in the halls of Gottingen, Heidelberg, Stuttgart, and Bonn and was carried out in the Old Testament text of the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1952). [All Liberals believe that poetic passages are to be taken figuratively and cannot be used to teach doctrine.]{.class_4095} This is the [religious conviction]{.class_4095} of all Liberals; and it is a [creedal dogma ]{.class_4095}at Harvard, Yale, Princeton, Union, Chicago, Columbia, and Colgate-Rochester.

Williams is absolutely correct in overruling Brother Mounce and stating that verses 9--11 declare "the Deity of the Lord Jesus Christ." This passage is on the two natures of Christ, and it was thrashed out before A.D. 500 in the controversies that arose around the Monophysites, Nestorians, Arians, Monarchians, Modalists, and Ebionites.

["Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5).[ "This mind"]{.class_4096} is plainly referring to the attitude which Jesus Christ had toward service and obedience (see vss. 7--8). When Paul says ["we have the MIND of Christ" ]{.class_4096}(1 Cor. 2:16), he is referring to the same thing. Incidentally (!), there is [no article ]{.class_4095}in 1 Corinthians 2:16 for ["THE mind,"]{.class_4096} yet the "uniform" [ASV ]{.class_4095}and the "uniform" [RV ]{.class_4095}and the "uniform" [RSV all put the article in]{.class_4095}! Phenomenon No. 4.

The change of ["mind"]{.class_4096} to "inner disposition" ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}) is just some more of the same old baloney dished out for people who can't afford pork chops. An "inner disposition," these days, can mean anything from gastritis and excess bile to traits of withdrawal and tendencies to suicide. ["Attitude" ]{.class_4095}(Phillips and ["Good News"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_355}) is better than "inner disposition," but both of these paraphrases erased the cross-reference to the same mind in 1 Corinthians 2:16. (You see, you "can't win for losin'." Once you've made ONE change, you are committed to Satan's work, and you will jolly well serve your time till he discharges you.)

["Who, BEING IN THE FORM OF GOD, thought it not robbery to BE EQUAL WITH GOD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). This should settle any argument in the future about any Greek word some infidel dug up to overthrow your faith in 1 Timothy 3:16. Whatever doubts Origen, Eusebius, Augustine, Pamphilus, Valentinus, Marcion, and other pagan Greek philosophers may have had about Christ's Deity, they should have been dispelled with verse 6. You see, the Scriptures are always so clear on [some things ]{.class_4095}that the verses have to be [changed ]{.class_4095}to prevent you from understanding them. Now, some things are clear in spite of the fact that the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901 or 1963) and the [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946 or 1952) have done their best to alter the truth of these texts.

1\. Christ is not only ["the FORM of God," ]{.class_4096}He is ["THE IMAGE OF GOD"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 1:2--3; 2 Cor. 4:4; Col. 1:13--15).

2\. He was not only ["EQUAL WITH GOD"]{.class_4096} (John 8--10), but claimed the same [title ]{.class_4095}(Isa. 9:6), [power ]{.class_4095}(Matt. 26:64), [attributes ]{.class_4095}(John 3:13), [authority ]{.class_4095}(Matt. 25:31--32), and [nature ]{.class_4095}(Col. 1:16--17; John 1:1--4) of God.

3\. Christ was ["GOD\...MANIFEST IN THE FLESH"]{.class_4096} (1 Tim. 3:16) and brought God's BLOOD (Acts 20:28) with Him. (The alterations of these texts by the dead-orthodox conservatives who translated the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, 1963, means nothing one way or another. [All ]{.class_4095}dead-orthodox Conservatives resent the Reformation Received text.)

4\. Christ never hesitated to let people worship Him as God Almighty (Rev. 21:7), the ["Alpha and Omega"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 22:13), ["the beginning and the ending"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 1:8), and never told any man to get off his knees if that man was worshipping Him (John 20:28; Matt. 14:33).

Now that much is clear. Christ was God or He was a liar. Jesus Christ was the Creator of Genesis 1:1, or He was a [religious crook]{.class_4095}. If He wasn't God in the flesh, He was the poorest, punkest, sorriest []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_356}"revolutionary" that ever misled ruined mankind; and you would be a blasphemous fool to mention His name in prayer or praise, or recommend His "teaching" to anyone but a drunken Confucianist. Why recommend a liar and a blasphemer to your children? [A Jew wouldn't!]{.class_4095}

No, you can't beat this thing with a stick. Jesus Christ was either who He professed to be (Matt. 11:27) and who Paul knew Him to be (Eph. 1:10), or He was a third-rate religious quack trying to impress people because He was too lazy to hold down an honest job. There is no middle ground, and the great Author of Absolute Truth and Absolute "Polarities" (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972) has fixed one issue so that you cannot ride sidesaddle. You get on or you get off. The Liberal who holds up Christ as an [example ]{.class_4095}is a duped dunce. Christ was the [poorest ]{.class_4095}example of a religious leader who ever lived---[bar none---if He were not God of very God, manifested in the flesh.]{.class_4095} I wouldn't preach one more message on the Bible or Christ (1 Cor. 15:15--30) if I thought Jesus Christ was a man like Buddha, Mohammed, Confucius, Lao Tze, or Moses. [I've got better sense.]{.class_4095}

["But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). And now the fat hits the fire! Some superstitious idiot dug up the Greek word ["edenosen"]{.class_4095} and sidetracked forty generations of Christians, arguing about the ["kenosis"]{.class_4095} theory. (See the ridiculous discussion documented in [Systematic Theology]{.class_4095}, Berkhof.) At the sudden appearance of the "original word" in the "original tongue," all of the troops panic; and there is a pell-mell, slam-bang race for dugouts, foxholes, fallout shelters, two-man slit trenches, and concrete bunkers. In the panic, everyone forgets verse 6 and verse 9, and the way they carry on---Liberals, Catholics, Conservatives, Fundamentalists, and Neo-orthodox alike---you would think that the German artillery had opened on Verdun. The truth of the matter is that Celsus (A.D. 180) and Porphyry (A.D. 232--303) just tossed some popcorn on the table.

1\. Christ wasn't ["in the form of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6); He simply had God's[ "nature"]{.class_4095} ([NEB]{.class_4095}, 1961, C. H. Dodd).

2\. Christ wasn't ["in the form of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6); He ["had always been God by nature"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_357} (Phillips, 1958).

3\. Christ wasn't ["in the form of God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6); He merely possessed the "essential qualities of God" ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1324, Dr. Mounce).

4\. He was not ["in the form of God"]{.class_4096} (vs 6), but only had the ["essential attributes"]{.class_4095} because this is the way [Aristotle ]{.class_4095}(!) uses the word ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 59)!

5\. Christ only refused to ["grasp"]{.class_4095} equality with God ([RV, ]{.class_4095}1885; [ASV, ]{.class_4095}1901; [RSV, ]{.class_4095}1946).

6\. Jesus Christ is not ["Lord to the glory of God the Father"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11); He is ["in the glory"]{.class_4095} ([Confraternity]{.class_4095}, pg. 547, Rome).

7\. Christ did neither "empty Himself" nor make ["himself of no reputation"]{.class_4096}; He ["EXINANITED"]{.class_4095} Himself (Jesuit [Rheims ]{.class_4095}of 1582---[the Bible that the ASV, RV, and RSV are trying to restore!!]{.class_4095})!

8\. Christ was deprived of His divine attributes though not His "intrinsic Deity" (Dummelow, Commentary, pg. 973). (How, then, did He curse a fig tree, become transfigured, and sit in Heaven while He was talking with Nicodemus? How did He still the elements?)

9\. No one is to get upset about ["the name of Jesus"]{.class_4096}; it is only ["in]{.class_4095} the name" of Jesus that God has done anything drastic (vs. 10; [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1325).

10\. Christ wasn't ["equal with God,"]{.class_4096} but He [could]{.class_4095} [have been]{.class_4095} if He had tried! ([Good News]{.class_4095}, 1966, pg. 443).

But the best is for last!

11\. Kenneth Wuest (Moody Bible Institute, [Expanded Translation]{.class_4095}, 1961, pg. xv, Preface) tells us that the Name above every name (vs. 10) is NOT[ "the name of JESUS!"]{.class_4096} Now, there is a "nugget" if you ever saw one. [Every Greek text in the world reads as the King James 1611]{.class_4095}. What happened to the "originals" which Brother Wuest mentioned on page vii of his Preface? Phenomena No. 2 and 5.

Having presented the great ecumenical magpie nest---Conservatives, Fundamentalists, Catholics, Liberals, and book sellers alike---let us look at the passage before us through the eyes of a Bible believer instead of a Bible critic, and through the eyes of a Christian instead of the eyes of a puffed-up educator (1 Cor. 8:1).

["Who, being in the FORM of God\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). The Greek word (in any manuscript) means ["form."]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_358} Christ was God's body (John 1:1--3, 14, 18) in God's shape (Heb. 1:1--3), and He filled the universe (Heb. 1:10--12) as He NOW fills it (Eph 1:20--23). He abandoned this ["form"]{.class_4096} and changed it for another ["form."]{.class_4096} This ["form" ]{.class_4096}was a 33-year-old male Hebrew, born of a Jewish woman.

You cannot divorce the ["form"]{.class_4096} from the attributes, qualities, or anything else; and the attempts of the translators, commentators, and revisers to do so only show their allegorizing, spiritualizing tendencies. These were the tendencies that ruined their whole life's work when they came to Ephesians 3:18, which see.

["Thought it not robbery to be EQUAL with God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). The word ["harpagmos"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) is an [act of robbery]{.class_4095} (Bullinger, [Companion Bible]{.class_4095}, pg. 1774), not a "grasping hold" on something which someone already HAS. So here we have Phenomenon No. 3. Because the scholars (any, of any persuasion) could not [divorce the time element between the two clauses in verse 6,]{.class_4095} they had to [force ]{.class_4095}the Greek word to mean [what it does not say]{.class_4095}. If He had the Deity---[AND THAT IS CLEAR!]{.class_4095}---He didn't have to "snatch at it violently" (Kennedy, pg. 436) or "clutch" at it (Wuest, pg. 462). (Rev. Kennedy gives up altogether and comes to the conclusion that the "equality" is a FUTURE thing that has [not yet taken place]{.class_4095} \[Kennedy, [Expositor's Greek New Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 437\]! But this shows that a "competent, accredited, reliable, etc., scholar" admits [of two different time elements in the clauses]{.class_4095}! Isn't it amazing how the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 translators had all of this figured out ahead of 1900 and without any of the information that the modern editors profess to have?)

Plainly, Christ professes equality with God [when He is on earth]{.class_4095} (John 1:18, 3:13, 5:22, 6:46, 8:58, 9:38)! And even plainer, all of His enemies thought He was a [God-robber]{.class_4095} (John 10:33). [THEY CRUCIFIED HIM BETWEEN THIEVES!]{.class_4095}

Ah, the unsearchable riches of the archaic Elizabethan English! How profound are its clauses and its meanings past finding out!

You see, when our accredited faculty hits a snag, they do one of three things: they say that the passage is "corrupt" (e.g., Col. 2:23), or they take the verse out of the context to explain it (e.g., Gal. 3:5), or they try to force the verse into the immediate context to make it "make sense" (e.g., here).

The great lessons which the Bible-believing Christian should learn from this are:

1\. The "majority of leading scholars" do not trust the [AV ]{.class_4095}text as far as they can run without legs.

2\. The "majority of leading scholars" never consult the Holy Spirit about any problem that arises when dealing with the Bible, which the Holy Spirit wrote.

3\. The "majority of leading scholars" have the utmost confidence in their own education and talents to force God's word to meet the demands of their own opinions.

The great truth of the text (vs. 6) was deliberately CLOSED to the greatest Conservative, Catholic, Liberal, and atheistic scholars who ever lived. While they professed to be searching for the "deeper things" and the "hidden meaning" (shades of Origen!), they simply dished you up a plate of sour collards and moldy corn bread.

["But made himself of no REPUTATION"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). That is the correct reading if you ever saw it! No pride of birth (Mark 6:3), no pride of upbringing (John 1:46), no pride of scholarship (John 7:15), no pride of independency (Luke 8:3), no pride of ability (John 5:19), no pride of success (John 7:5), no pride of bigotry (Mark 9:39), no pride of sanctity (Luke 15:2), no pride of resentment (Luke 13:34), no pride of looks (Isa. 53:2), and no REPUTATION (Matt. 11:19) but a BAD ONE (Luke 23:1--2)! ["He humbled himself"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8).

["And took upon him the form of a servant"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). The form he took was the form of a male, Hebrew carpenter, born under the Law. When He wasn't waiting on hospital beds (Mark 2:1--12), He was waiting on tables (John 13:3--11). (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown \[Commentary, pg. 1305\] give the reader a beautiful description of the [King James]{.class_4095} text, and Bullinger's note on verse 7, for a change, shows wisdom and spiritual understanding.)

Christ takes the long journey down from Alpha Draconis to this solar system in seven humiliating steps:

1\. ["Made himself of no reputation."]{.class_4096}

2\. Took on ["the form of a servant."]{.class_4096}

3\. Took on the likeness of man.

4\. Was ["found in fashion as a man."]{.class_4096}

5\. ["Humbled himself."]{.class_4096}

6\. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_360}["Became obedient."]{.class_4096}

7\. ["Became obedient unto\...the death of THE CROSS."]{.class_4096}

There was no gradual humbling: no landing first on Saturn and then at Rome and then on to Jerusalem before going to Bethlehem. There were no "progressive stages" in His abasement: no stepping from the Trinity to the post of the Archangel, to the Angel of the Lord, to the Roman Emperor, to Herod, to the Chief Priest, etc. No, it was one mighty downward fall from Glory to Gethsamene, from Heaven to Hell (Acts 2:27, 31---see comments under Eph. 4:9), from the Father's house to Satan's seat, from the ivory palaces to the stables of Bethlehem, and from the praises of cherubim, seraphim, and angels to the curses, boos, hisses, and blows of depraved men (Heb. 5:8). This was the only man in the history of the universe who ever chose His time, place, and manner of birth!

If you have the "mind" He has (vs. 5) and are "conformed to his image" (Rom. 8:29), then you have no reputation, you have been set at nought, you have been slandered and vilified, you have suffered without complaining, you have been classed with criminals and demons, you have gone till you have fallen on your face, you are outside the camp bearing "His reproach," and you are no more related to the "modern Christian" than John Knox was related to Captain Kangaroo.

Jesus spoke of Himself as a servant in a YOKE (Matt. 11:29). He spoke of Himself as a waiter (Luke 22:27). Isaiah calls him "the Servant of the Lord" (Isa. 52:13, 53:11, etc.).

["And was made in the LIKENESS OF MEN"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). He had arms, hands, feet, eyes, ears, nose, etc., as an ordinary man has them. He inhaled and exhaled when He breathed. He ate His food and digested it, and---marvel of marvels!---He was a real baby who cried when He was hungry and depended on a daughter of Adam for His early nourishment! This is called a ["GREAT\...MYSTERY"]{.class_4096} (1 Tim. 3:16), and it is past finding out.

God dependent on a woman? Did you ever tend to a baby? [Can you imagine the Creator of Heaven and earth being in that kind of a condition? ]{.class_4095}You see, when the Lord pulled off [the incarnation]{.class_4095} (John 1:14), He confounded the wisdom of every philosopher, scholar, or scientist (1 Cor. 1--2) who ever lived or ever will live. ["The foolishness of God is wiser than men"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_361} (1 Cor. 1:25).

["And being found in fashion AS A MAN"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). That is, He got hungry (Matt. 21:18; Luke 4:2) and thirsty (John 4:7, 19:28) and tired (John 4:6). He wept (John 11:35), He rejoiced (Luke 10:21), He got angry (Mark 3:5), and He DIED (1 Cor. 15:3). Altizer and Nietzsche were a little late in posting their notices: "God is dead." The day God died, the heavens put on sackcloth and ashes and the chandeliers in the sky went out (Matt. 27:45). If God had not been a TRINITY, Golgotha would have been the end of world history. We know when God died, where He died, how He died, and for what He died; and I dare say that is a good bit more than "Zarathustra" (Nietzsche) got ahold of. (Yes, we understand the pragmatic reasoning behind the modern saying "God is dead"; we just aren't dumb enough to think that changing the LABEL on a bottle changes the CONTENT. It's curdled milk either way.)

["Even the death of the cross"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). This death was a painful, shameful, lingering death (see Gal. 2:20 and comments). It was a substitutionary death (1 Pet. 3:18), a vicarious death, a propitiatory death (1 John 2:1--2), a bloody death (Heb. 9:14), a violent death (Acts 7:52), and an effective death (Rom. 5:6--10).

["The death of the cross"]{.class_4096} is the Roman "X" death, devised by fallen man. It represents the attempt of man to negate or scratch out the truth of God. There are three crosses standing for three "Xs," and these three "Xs" crop up throughout history in the form of KKK, JFK, Hex, Hex, Hex (Gk.: 666, Rev. 13:18), tit-tac-toe, and the two fingers crossed or separated (see [Mark of the Beast]{.class_4095}, 1960).

The death of the cross is a typical Darwinian innovation which does not appear---according to all geologists and anthropologists---until man has had at least 2,000,000 years to "evolve" to "higher stages." It consists of a man being tied or nailed to a sharpened stake, cross bar, or "X" form so that he suffers excruciating agony for several hours or days before he blacks out into a coma. Among other choice things attending this death are acute dehydration, dislocated arms and shoulders, torn tendons or ligaments, gangrene, high fever, muscle spasms, cramped muscles, excretion or vomiting, and hysteria.

Many times, the victims would be fully conscious after hanging []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_362}on their stakes for 48 hours, and sometimes the birds would begin to eat their flesh before they had lost enough blood to pass out completely. Some crucified victims were alive after two days and nights on the cross. It was Christ's humble obedience which led Him [knowingly ]{.class_4095}(Matt. 16:21) to this kind of death.

In "Spartacus," the hero is forced to fight his best friend in a duel to the death. Each of them is trying to kill the other one so that the slain man will not have to endure the torture of crucifixion. Spartacus wins---or loses. He wins the duel and is crucified.

[But Jesus Christ VOLUNTEERED]{.class_4095} (Heb. 10:7) [for His ENEMIES]{.class_4095} (Rom. 5:10)! (WELLLL, GLLLOOOR-REEEE!!!)

"See from His head, His hands, His feet, sorrow and love flow mingled down; Did ere such love and sorrow meet, Or thorns compose so rich a crown? When I survey the wondrous cross on which the PRINCE OF GLORY died, my richest gain I count but loss, and pour contempt on all my pride!" "I saw one hanging on a tree in agony and blood, who fixed His languid gaze on me as near His cross I stood!" "So I'll cherish the old rugged cross, till my trophies at last I lay down; I will cling to that old rugged cross, and exchange it some day FOR A CROWN!"

["Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him "]{.class_4096} (vs. 9)! He took seven steps down, so now He takes seven steps up!

1\. ["Highly exalted."]{.class_4096}

2\. ["A name\...above every name."]{.class_4096}

3\. Universal dominion.

4\. Over everything in heaven.

5\. Over everything on earth.

6\. Over everything under the earth.

7\. Absolute Lordship.

Joseph went from "slam city" to the throne of Egypt. Jesus goes from a garden tomb to ["the third heaven"]{.class_4096} (John 20:17).

["A name\...the name"]{.class_4096} (vss. 9--10). This Name is the "power of attorney" (see Matt. 1:21, 18:20, 19:29; Heb. 13:15; Mark 9:41; Eph. 6:10; Rom. 15:9; 1 Cor. 5:1--4, etc.). The name is ["Jesus,"]{.class_4096} regardless of Kenneth Wuest's opinion about the matter---see page 461 of [The Expanded Translation]{.class_4095}---and this is the Name of names.

It is a more imperial name than [Caesar.]{.class_4095} It is more eloquent than [Cicero.]{.class_4095} It is more musical than [Beethoven.]{.class_4095} It is holier than [Mary]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_363} or [Giuseppi Rancalli]{.class_4095} or [Giovanni Montini. ]{.class_4095}It is a beautiful name; it is an easy name. It is whispered by children at bedtime; it is called out in the darkness of hospital rooms at night; it is murmured under shell-fire on the battlefields; it is sounded forth publicly from the pulpit of every true minister that is left; and it is called upon tearfully by the sinful wretch who escapes the pollutions of this world at a prayer altar and "tastes and sees" ["that the ]{.class_4096}[Lord ]{.class_4103}[is good"]{.class_4096} (Psa. 34:8 cf. Rom. 10:13).

"There have been names that I have loved to hear, but never has there been a name so dear to this heart of mine, as the name divine, the precious, precious NAME OF JESUS!"

Lost men can say "God" easily, and some of the bolder ones can say "Christ"; but the only way to say "JESUS," [if you are lost]{.class_4095}, is to use the name as a cuss word---as thousands of Roman Catholics do daily---or use it to refer to an [earthly man ]{.class_4095}who lived in Palestine. Paul applies the human name "JESUS" to the RISEN LORD, and that is what cuts off Kenneth Wuest's water! The name which Jesus "possesses" (see Wuest, pg. 463) is [Jesus]{.class_4095}. The devil is a god (2 Cor. 4:4) and a Christ (Ezek. 28:14), but he is not the [Lord's Christ]{.class_4095} (Acts 4:26), and he is not "JESUS." There is only one "Lord" whose name is JESUS, and that is [the Lord Jesus Christ.]{.class_4095}

The translators who altered ["at"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10) to "in" on the basis of the "Greek," simply lied to you again (see comments under vss. 4--6). It can be translated either way, and this time, again, the [AV ]{.class_4095}translators have far and away the best of the choice. No unsaved man or demon (see vs. 10) ever bends his head, knee, wrist, neck, or finger ["in the name"]{.class_4095} of Jesus. ["In the name"]{.class_4095} is a privilege reserved for [saved people ]{.class_4095}(John 14:14). The [RV ]{.class_4095}(1885) and the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) both say ["in," ]{.class_4095}and the [Amplified ]{.class_4095}says ["in"]{.class_4095} and ["at"]{.class_4095} (!). But why not go to the "original"? The original ["in"]{.class_4095} is found in the Jesuit Bible of the Roman Catholic Church [published more than twenty years before the AV was published]{.class_4095} ([Rheims]{.class_4095}, 1582).

Once again, we have caught the best brains of the day (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown also recommend the Jesuit reading) sliding by the truth because of an irreverence and indifference to the text. This verse is referring to a [future date ]{.class_4095}(Rev. 20). Verses 10 and 11 mean []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_364}exactly what they say and are to be taken literally in spite of the "poetry" bit suggested by the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, which, by the way, is carried out in the [NEB]{.class_4095}---[the NEB has the whole section written out as blank verse!]{.class_4095}

Face the text head-on. Someday after this heaven and earth have melted with fervent heat (2 Pet. 3:12) and the dead are left standing in open space (Rev. 20:11--15) with nothing to hold up their bodies but the word of God (Heb. 1:3), the records are opened (Dan. 7:10) and the "little horn" (Dan. 7:11) gets his payoff, and the dragon gets his (Rev. 20:10).

The curtain is about to fall. Everyone is on stage. The orchestra has stopped. There is a ghastly hush. The last review has reviewed the lives, thoughts, motives, speech, and deeds of 90,000,000,000 people. The examining (Job 26:2--4) and [cross-examining]{.class_4095} (Psa. 51:4; Rom. 3:4--missed that one, didn't you, you old four-flusher!) is over. Every mouth has been stopped (Rom. 3:19); all the questions have been answered (Matt. 22:15--46). Time has run out, and there stand around 80,000,000,000 people about to experience what they were taught (and believed) could never happen. But now, [everybody is a "convert"]{.class_4095}! You can't find a skeptic or an agnostic in the pack! There is the rim of the universe; there is the Lake of Fire about 3000 times as large as the sun---plenty of room for all!---and now the actors must take a BOW!

From the Throne of Glory THE NAME is proclaimed. And do you know what will happen? Well, bless your soul, every atheist, agnostic, skeptic, infidel, Catholic, Taoist, Communist, Buddhist, Confucianist, Mohammedan, Socialist, Brahman, existentialist, Hindu, Jew, Protestant, Satanist, cultist, pragmatist, spiritist, Unitarian, Deist, pantheist, and polytheist in the universe will "hit the deck" and cry: ["JESUS CHRIST IS LORD!"]{.class_4096}

Over there is Langston Hughes, kneeling. Over there is Gus Hall. Over yonder is Tom Paine and Voltaire. Over here is Thomas Altizer and Bishop Pike. In front of us are Rousseau and Hobbes. Over there are Humes and Huxley; behind them Paley and Lyell. Behind us are kneeling Einstein and Bertrand Russell, and on our right are Mohammed and Gautama Buddha. On our left are Darwin and Karl Marx, and in front of them are Judas and Pharaoh.

Since we saved, born-again, blood-bought, Bible believers have already "bowed the knee" (Gen. 41:43), it won't bother us in the least. It would be a pleasure---ANY TIME!

And before that curtain bangs on the stage and the lights go out, that great Prince of Darkness, the veteran of ancient days, that old, battle-scarred, pockmarked, weather-beaten genius of Ezekiel 28 who ["deceiveth the whole world"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 12:9) enters and kneels; and then, before all of his children, cohorts, followers, principalities, powers, admirers, victims, and worshippers, he cries: ["JESUS CHRIST IS LORD!"]{.class_4096}

[Some of you trusted the wrong "Christ"]{.class_4095} (see [Mark of]{.class_4095} [the Beast]{.class_4095}, 1960).

It would be a pity at this point to return to a discussion of the thoughts of Caffin, Mounce, Lipsius, Phillips, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Moffatt, Rendall, Meyer, Wordsworth, Luken, Van Hengel, Buttmann, Bengel, Lightfoot, Nestle, Westcott, Hort, Aland, Davidson, Lange, Clarke, Ellicott, and the "leading scholars" on the text, so let us "leave well enough alone." (As a Southern Belle once said, "You all done ruint it umpteen dozen times already! Cantcha leave it 'lone?")

Someday, everything that breathes will be a Bible-believing Christian, but that "conversion" will be too late to affect the eternal destinies of those involved (Heb. 9:27). Bow the knee NOW or bow it THEN: you [will ]{.class_4095}bow it. Confess Christ NOW or confess Him THEN: you [will ]{.class_4095}confess Him, and you will do it ["to the glory of God the Father"]{.class_4096} who gave Jesus ["the]{.class_4096} [preeminence" "in all things"]{.class_4096} (Col. 1:18), ["for]{.class_4096} [in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily"]{.class_4096} (Col. 1:15--19, 2:9 and comments). I am glad my knees gave way under me at 9:30 in the morning, the 14th of March, 1949, at the corner of Gregory and Palafox in downtown Pensacola, Florida. [Any "repeat performance" is always a genuine pleasure]{.class_4095}.

2:12 Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.

13 For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_366}do of his good pleasure.

14 Do all things without murmurings and disputings:

15 That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world;

16 Holding forth the word of life; that I may rejoice in the day of Christ, that I have not run in vain, neither laboured in vain.

Having finished the great theological and doctrinal discourse on the Person and Deity of Jesus Christ as they were related to His humility and exaltation, Paul now returns to the "earthlies" and applies his text to the Philippians.

["Work out your own salvation with fear AND TREMBLING"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). We have emphasized the last words so the believer will not be seduced by the liberal Scofield note (pg. 607, footnote, [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_4095}), which speaks of "reverential trust" (see detailed analysis in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, Prov. 1:2--7, 1972). ["Trembling"]{.class_4096} is not a very good description of "reverential trust" nor "reverential piety," and the attempts to water down the expression ["the fear of the ]{.class_4096}[Lord]{.class_4103}["]{.class_4096} are attempts by dead-orthodox apostates to make the wrath of God acceptable to all classes of readers so they will not accuse the scholars of being "fanatics" or "bigots." (This is very, very typical of the whole Conservative movement since 1900.)

["Wherefore\...work out your own salvation"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12), since your Saviour is "God of very God" and supreme Lord of all and will win the battle thumbs down, in the clear, with no "left overs." Since every one of your enemies will have to confess Jesus Christ publicly and verbally, there shouldn't be any doubt in your mind about His "power of attorney" where it is related to YOU.

["For it is God WHICH WORKETH IN YOU"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13). This is the verse that is omitted in the creedal statements of Methodists, Catholics, Episcopalians, and "Holiness." (Not that it makes any difference anymore. Not even the Baptists and Presbyterians have any real convictions left about anything anymore, so [creedal statements of belief ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_367}really mean nothing and have meant very little since around 1950. Most Baptists and Presbyterians believe in television and [Life ]{.class_4095}magazine.)

By isolating verse 13 from verse 12, the Arminians developed a system where you "work AT your own salvation" instead of ["work OUT your own salvation"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). But as we have said before (Gal. 3:11 and comments; Eph. 2:8--9 and comments), "The Christian does not work to GET saved, neither does he work to STAY saved; he works because he IS saved." You cannot[ "work out"]{.class_4096} something unless it is "worked in," and God ["in you"]{.class_4096} (Col. 1:27) is working [through you ]{.class_4095}(Eph. 1:19, 3:20 with comments) to bring forth fruit for His own glory. This clear presentation of truth, found in the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}Holy Bible, should deter anyone from misunderstanding the operation.

But in spite of the clarity, Calvin, Berkhof, Dabney, Hodge, Kuyper, and the Reformed churches of Holland, Michigan, take the passage to mean: "For it is God who WILLS instead of you" (Rom. 9:16) and "He is doing His good pleasure in you regardless of YOUR will." But of course, the verse says nothing of the kind. Berkhof ([Systematic Theology]{.class_4095}, pg. 471) applies the verse to the UNSAVED sinner, ["dead in trespasses and sin,"]{.class_4096} and uses it to prove that the "elect" receive Christ by proxy, God doing the WILLING for them. (See warning notes in the Introduction to Ephesians of this Commentary on Hyper-Calvinism.)

The proper commentary on verse 13 is found in Hebrews 13:21; Ephesians 3:7; Titus 2:11; Isaiah 26:12; and 1 Corinthians 15:10. Dr. Mounce goes a little beyond Scofield in trying to get rid of ["the fear of the Lord"]{.class_4095} and tells us that ["fear and trembling"]{.class_4096} is merely an "idiomatic expression" which shows a "humble frame of mind." The answer to this type of private interpretation is "HOGWASH." [Living Letters]{.class_4095} (1962) offers us the same nicey-wisey, prissy-wissy interpretation by telling us that ["fear and trembling"]{.class_4096} really means "deep reverence."

You don't have to read many Conservative scholars to realize that they are at loggerheads with a man who was a type of Christ and who wrote nearly three-fourths of the book of Psalms (Psa. 147:11). Isaiah eliminated [Living Letters]{.class_4095} and the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} with []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_368}one stroke when he wrote Isaiah 66:2. Those of us who have spent much time dealing with these "deeply pious" people, with "humble frames of mind" in their "reverential trust," know that they are some of the greatest Bible-rejecters in the world. Their whole approach to Christianity (see Eph. 6:10--17 and comments) is unscriptural, and their attitude toward the ["word of life"]{.class_4096} mentioned in the context of Philippians 2:13 is as despicable as the attitude of Cassius Clay toward the white race. (All Negroes who are in the spotlight of publicity are RACISTS. You never met one in your life who wasn't!)

["Do all things without murmurings and disputings"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14; cf. 1 Cor. 10:3--11; John 7:12; Acts 6:1). ["That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). The goal is to have enough unity among yourselves (see 2:3) so that the Christ-rejecting community will have no charge to bring when such a charge involves internal strife in the local church (1 Pet. 2:12; 2 Pet. 3:14; Deut. 32:5; 1 Thess. 5:15).

The ["crooked and perverse nation"]{.class_4096} is Rome or Greece (see comments under Eph. 2:1--3)! The commentators were tricked into thinking the reference was to [Israel]{.class_4095}, due to the cross-reference to Deuteronomy 32:5. However, there wasn't one synagogue in Philippi, and there are only seven verses in the whole Epistle (1:14--15, 3:2, 3, 4, 5, 6) that deal with the "Judaizers." Again, we have encountered Phenomenon No. 8.

The word in Deuteronomy was not ["nation"]{.class_4096}; it was ["generation." ]{.class_4096}The generation referred to is the generation of the "end time" in the Great Tribulation, which is apparent in every line of Deuteronomy. The ["nation"]{.class_4096} in Philippians is in the Church Age. One can easily understand how 100 percent of the modern editors and commentators made the mistake, for Ethelbert Bullinger states that both words are identical (margin, pg. 1776, [Companion Bible]{.class_4095}). But "God" is not "Heaven" and "seed" is not "seeds" and "nation" is not "family" (see Gal. 3:8 and comments). A man who would make ["nation"]{.class_4096} identical to ["generation"]{.class_4096} has no business calling himself any kind of a "dispensationalist"!

The word for ["generation"]{.class_4096} in the Greek ([geneas]{.class_4095}) is found in all of the Greek manuscripts, so here we have a plain case of Phenomenon No. 10, which see. The English text sheds light on []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_369}doctrinal truth not found in the Greek manuscripts! But this happens quite often and comes as no surprise to the student who has spent time in the word of God instead of the writings of its enemies.

The ["perverse NATION"]{.class_4096} is equated with the "world" right in the verse: ["among whom ye shine as lights IN THE WORLD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). This is not Israel.

["Holding forth the word of life\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 16). The Philippian Christian is to hold out the Bread of Life to the hungry, lost, dead, perverse, crooked, lonely, sinful people (Eph. 2:12) around him. ["The word of life"]{.class_4096} is:

1\. The word that [gives ]{.class_4095}life.

2\. The word that is [alive]{.class_4095}.

3\. The word that came from [the life]{.class_4095} (John 1:4).

4\. The word that [lives ]{.class_4095}forever.

5\. The word that tells of [eternal life.]{.class_4095}

Since these are five of the greatest and most precious truths regarding the living word ["of the living God"]{.class_4096} (Jer. 23:36), Dr. Mounce ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1325) immediately tears them out of the apostle's mouth and tells us that it is merely the word that "brings life." But this is the same old crap ([LXX ]{.class_4095}Greek for ["refuse"]{.class_4095}---see Phil 3:8 in the [RSV, RV, ASV]{.class_4095}, any edition) we ran into when we tried to arm ourselves with ["the sword of the Spirit"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 6:17). It seems that "genitives" are a universal expedient for ["making the word of God of none effect"]{.class_4096} (Mark 7:13)! The corrupt ["Good News"]{.class_4095} takes Barth's position and offers us ["the message of life." ]{.class_4095}You are to keep ["the word of life"]{.class_4096} to yourself, according to the [RSV ]{.class_4095}of 1946 ("[Holding fast"]{.class_4095}), and Phillips doesn't get the sense much better.

Typical treatment of the passage will be found in the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} (Caffin). Observe: ["Ye may be"]{.class_4096} should be "ye may BECOME" (vs. 15), ["shine"]{.class_4096} should be "are seen" (vs. 15), ["lights"]{.class_4096} should be "luminaries" (vs. 15), ["holding forth"]{.class_4096} should be "possessing" (vs. 16---Meyer), ["I have not run"]{.class_4096} should be "I DID not run" (vs. 16), etc.

Once again, at verse 16, we encounter the ancient heresy preserved by dead-orthodox apostates from 1885 to 2000. (We have mentioned this before under Eph. 1:14 and 4:30 with Phil. 1:10.) Bishop Lightfoot says that the expression ["the day of Christ"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_370} (vs. 16) is "peculiar to this epistle" and should be identified as ["the day of the Lord."]{.class_4095} But Lightfoot, as George E. Ladd ([The Blessed Hope]{.class_4095}), is just as confused as Bullinger was when he said that ["nation"]{.class_4096} meant ["generation,"]{.class_4095} and just as confused as Campbell was when he said that ["baptism" ]{.class_4096}meant ["water]{.class_4095}," and just as confused as Pope Paul VI was when he said that ["Peter"]{.class_4096} means ["ROCK"]{.class_4095} You see, "birds of a feather flock together"!

Furthermore,[ "the day of Christ"]{.class_4096} is NOT "peculiar" to Philippians; it is found as plain as the nose on your face in 2 Thessalonians 2:2!

["The day of Christ"]{.class_4096} plainly deals with the Judgment Seat of Christ, where the editors, commentators, revisers, and translators will be judged---if they are saved at all! Hence, the sudden "tight lips" when Philippians 2:16 is approached. The alert Christian will notice that the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} is unable to write [one line]{.class_4095} on the meaning of verse 16! Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown give the proper cross-references (1 Thess. 2:19; 2 Cor. 1:14) but bypass any comments. Dummelow is mute (Commentary, pg. 974), and the amazing Dr. Mounce slams the door shut on the verse with some irrelevant mumbling about Deismann's ["cloth getting torn"]{.class_4095} and Herklotz's statement on Paul's "mixed metaphors"!

It would appear that none of our friends are ready to hit the Judgment Seat of Christ. But this is apparent in every line of their work, for the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} has stricken out the word ["Christ"]{.class_4096} in Romans 14:10; Dummelow has done the same thing; and the [RV, ASV, RSV, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[,]{.class_4095} [NEB, Good News]{.class_4095}, Moffatt, Weymouth, Goodspeed, Berkeley, and [Twentieth Century ]{.class_4095}have also done the same thing. Somebody is in for a surprise (2 Cor. 5:11).

["The day of Christ"]{.class_4096} is the day when a man's converts will appear, and their fruit will be examined. Since the only convert who can bear fruit is the one who receives ["the word of life"]{.class_4096} (here)[ "as it is in truth, the word of God" ]{.class_4096}that ["worketh also in you"]{.class_4096} (1 Thess. 2:13 cf. Phil. 2:13), the modern editors and commentators would just as soon forget the whole scene and pretend that it doesn't exist. This is the reason why the word ["Christ"]{.class_4096} was knocked out of Romans 14:10 by the Conservatives of the [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1960), and why "Lord" was inserted here and in 1:10 for ["Christ"]{.class_4096} by George E. Ladd and Bishop "Lightfoot." []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_371}[Greek scholarship has nothing to do with it.]{.class_4095} Phenomenon No. 13.

Laying aside the backslidden Conservative apostates, we learn:

1\. The day of Christ will reveal Paul's work to the fullest (1 Cor. 3:5--8).

2\. At this time, the genuineness of his ministry will be apparent (1 Cor. 4:4--5).

3\. His converts will prove his own fidelity to ["the word of life"]{.class_4096}; and if they prove faithful, he will ["rejoice in the day of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16).

4\. Converts who refuse to "hold forth the word of life," after they have been saved by it, are a heartache and a sorrow to the man who tried to train them. Hence, Paul is concerned about "running in vain" and "laboring in vain," for these are two figures of the ministry found in 1 Corinthians 9:7, 24.

The conjectures and comments of Lipsius, Bullinger, Clarke, Lange, Ellicott, Schaff, Origen, Lachmann, Tischendorf, Tregelles, Alford, Schnedermann, Luken, Vincent, Bengel, Lightfoot, and "the leading scholars" are a total BLANK.

Origen believed in salvation by works (Princip. 3.5.3, Koetschau, 1913). Lachmann and Tischendorf were Amillennialists. Schaff did not believe in the Rapture, Tribulation, or Millennium. Bengel and Lightfoot didn't know enough about practical Christianity to preach on the street, and not a man in the list would know a "crown" from an "inheritance."

The exaggerated reverence and honor given to these men by the faculty members of conservative schools is part of a gigantic Madison Avenue "Scholars' Union" campaign to support an [educational system]{.class_4095}. The faithful student of God's word should never take such men seriously in any discussion where real Bible truth is being sought for in the realm of doctrine.

The alteration of ["lights"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) to "luminaries" to match the corrupt [LXX ]{.class_4095}of Origen (A.D. 200--500) does nothing for "enlightening" anyone with "light" at all. Christ is[ "the light of the world" ]{.class_4096}(John 9:5), the word is a light (Psa. 119:105, see comments under Eph. 5:9), the Christian is a light (Matt. 5:14), and ["whatsoever doth make manifest is LIGHT"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 5:13). A []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_372}"luminary" is often a reference to an ecclesiastical or philosophical "big shot," and the word is entirely out of place in any discussion of truth in the Twentieth Century. It is extremely "archaic," and the fact that Kenneth Wuest uses it means nothing. Even the "stars" and "beacons" used by the [NEB, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, Good News]{.class_4095}, etc., are clearer than "luminaries." "Luminary" is the dead language of dead-orthodox apostasy, and it has no place in anybody's vocabulary after the Seventeenth Century. The [AV ]{.class_4095}translators knew that in the Seventeenth Century! !

["The word of life"]{.class_4096} (not the "word that bringeth life") is to be held up and out like a torch in an outstretched arm. This indicates plainly that the French lady in the harbor of Staten Island has the WRONG BOOK in her hand. An "eternal flame" (JFK's grave) that points the way to [New York ]{.class_4095}is about as helpful these days as a road marker directing the driver to a garbage dump. The only city in the USA more filthy and depraved than New York is Washington D.C., and every President since Roosevelt has made him an "alternate White House" to get out of the Black section of the Capital. (You have to hire policemen from OUT OF THE STATE to come and work in Washington. That is not "racism"; that is [a solid fact]{.class_4095}, provable in a law court.)

A nation that abandons ["the word of life"]{.class_4096} for the torch of the "revolutionary" (France revolted, remember?) is a lost nation (Psa. 9:17). To be perfectly honest, someone should write "Burn, baby, Burn!" at the base of the "torch" on Staten Island. [Bombings ]{.class_4095}and [burnings ]{.class_4095}are the outcome of the rejection of Genesis 3, according to Dr. Shadduck Ph.D., in his work on evolution ([Puddle to Paradise]{.class_4095}), [written in 1925.]{.class_4095}

2:17 Yea, and if I be offered upon the sacrifice and service of your faith, I joy, and rejoice with you all.

18 For the same cause also do ye joy, and rejoice with me.

19 But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.

20 For I have no man likeminded, who will naturally []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_373}care for your state.

21 For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ's.

22 But ye know the proof of him, that, as a son with the father, he hath served with me in the gospel.

23 Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soon as I shall see how it will go with me.

24 But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.

25 Yet I supposed it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, and companion in labour, and fellowsoldier, but your messenger, and he that ministered to my wants.

26 For he longed after you all, and was full of heaviness, because that ye had heard that he had been sick.

27 For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.

28 I sent him therefore the more carefully, that, when ye see him again, ye may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.

29 Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness; and hold such in reputation:

30 Because for the work of Christ he was nigh unto death, not regarding his life, to supply your lack of service toward me.

The epistle now assumes a very personal tone as Paul deals with the ministries of his companions (Timotheus, vs. 19, and Epaphroditus, vs. 25). Most of it is self-explanatory, and the changes in the text of the "recent editors" will be omitted this time, since by now the reader is probably bored to death with them. Once you are used to hot homemade bread out of the oven (Luke 15:16--17), cold baloney out of the deep freeze does not exactly start the saliva running!

["If I be offered upon the sacrifice and service of your faith\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 17), which is as much as saying, "If I have to die in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_374}order to produce in you the kind of Christians you ought to be (Rom. 12:1--3; Gal. 4:19), I am willing." Their ["faith"]{.class_4096} includes their whole attitude toward service (see comments under 1:11). They are serving and sacrificing as every Christian should (1 Pet. 2:5---["spiritual sacrifices,"]{.class_4096} not "masses" or "messes").

["I joy, and rejoice with you all"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). He is happy to die with them and for them, and any sacrifice he has to make in order to make their offering[ "acceptable unto God"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 12:1 cf. Phil. 1:11, 19) is al right with him.

["For the same cause"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). They should be just as happy about it as he is.

["But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19). The verse is self-explanatory.

["For I have no man likeminded\...for YOUR STATE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20; cf. comments under 2:3--4).[ "For all seek THEIR OWN"]{.class_4096} (vs. 21).
