["For ye know the proof of HIM\...he hath served WITH ME in the gospel"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22). Therefore, Timothy does care for their things (vs. 20) and the things of Jesus Christ (vs. 21). ["As a son with the father"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22) is commented on under 1 Corinthians 4:15--17.

Verse 23 is self-explanatory. Verse 24 is self-explanatory.

["Yet I supposed\...to send to you Epaphroditus, my BROTHER\...COMPANION\...FELLOWSOLDIER\...MESSENGER\...that MINISTERED to my wants"]{.class_4096} (vs. 25; cf. 4:18). A real "brother in Christ" is five things to a real minister of the Gospel.

1\. He is a ["brother"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 12:50). This means that there is blood relationship and birth relationship (John 1:12--13).

2\. He is a ["companion,"]{.class_4096} which some "brothers" (Cain and Abel, Jacob and Esau, Isaac and Ishmael) are NOT.

3\. He is a ["fellowsoldier"]{.class_4096} (see remarks under Eph. 6:10--17); and he will not break ranks, steal your canteen, foul your rifle, hold back while you are advancing, fail to cover your flank, refuse to share his rations, betray you to the enemy, go to sleep on watch, or let you take all the fire.

4\. He is a ["messenger"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 8:23). He bears "glad tidings of great joy" (Rom. 10:12--15), and he is described in Proverbs 25:25.

5\. He is a ["minister" ]{.class_4095}(Acts 13:2; Rom. 15:27). []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_375}["For he longed after you all\...because that YE HAD HEARD that he had been sick"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26). This is some more of the "high ground" (like 1 Cor. 10:29). Epaphroditus is not worried about [being sick]{.class_4095}; he is afraid that some other Christian [will worry about it]{.class_4095} if he hears about it! (See Abimelech in Judges 9:54 for a peculiar twist of the same idea.)

["For indeed he was sick nigh unto death\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 27). Both Paul and Timothy got sick on occasion (1 Tim. 5:23, 2 Cor. 12:9--10). ["But God had mercy on him" ]{.class_4096}(cf. 2 Cor. 1:8--10). ["And not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow"]{.class_4096} (cf. 2 Cor. 1).

["I sent him therefore the more carefully"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28). Compare 4:6 and comments for ["carefully."]{.class_4096}

Paul was anxious to send him to them as soon as possible so they could see for themselves that he was no longer sick. This would cause them to rejoice and that would start the chain reaction that would make him (Paul) more cheerful---["that I may be the less sorrowful"]{.class_4096} (vs. 28).

Verse 29 is self-explanatory. Verse 30 is self-explanatory. Epaphroditus does what the Philippians were unable to do (4:10), and he nearly dropped out on the way trying to do it (cf. Acts 15:26; Rom. 16:2, 4).

Now, if the reader would like to get bogged down in interminable discussions of nonessentials, he may waste a few hours with the "modern editors," "scientific exegetes," and "commentators." The total number of changes suggested for the fourteen verses comes to over 200, if we synthesize the comments and revisions of [Living Letters]{.class_4095}, the [Companion Bible]{.class_4095}, the [NEB]{.class_4095}, the [RSV]{.class_4095}, the [RV]{.class_4095}, the [ASV]{.class_4095}, the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, Caffin, Mounce, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Wuest, [Rheims]{.class_4095}, [Confraternity]{.class_4095}, Rendall, [Good News]{.class_4095}, Phillips, Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Lightfoot, Bengel, Kurt Aland, Nestle, Westcott, Hort, and the other destructive critics of ["the word of life"]{.class_4096} (who are commonly called "the best known scholars").

["Sacrifice"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17) should have been "libation," ["service"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17) should have been "priestly service," ["good comfort"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19) should have been "cheered," ["companion in labour"]{.class_4096} (vs. 25) should have been "fellow worker," ["more carefully"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2534.xhtml_page_376} (vs. 28) should have been "more quickly," ["naturally care"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20) should have been "care truly," ["full of heaviness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26) should have been "sore troubled," ["sick"]{.class_4096} (vs. 27) should have been "ill," ["full of heaviness"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26) should have been "homesick," ["nigh unto death"]{.class_4096} (vs. 30) should have been "risked his life," ["longed"]{.class_4096} (vs. 26) should be "had longed" etc., etc. (It is too bad God never learned how to speak or write!)

If you want diversity, fragmentation, insecurity, instability, looseness, lack of purpose, pointlessness, aimlessness, and vagaries, try any major commentary or any translation printed since 1880. If you want the truth, get ahold of a [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible[.]{.class_4095}

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml}

## Chapter 3 {.heading_116}

3:1 Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe.

2 Beware of dogs, beware of evil workers, beware of the concision.

3 For we are the circumcision, which worship God in the spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh.

4 Though I might also have confidence in the flesh. If any other man thinketh that he hath whereof he might trust in the flesh, I more:

5 Circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, an Hebrew of the Hebrews; as touching the law, a Pharisee;

6 Concerning zeal, persecuting the church; touching the righteousness which is in the law, blameless.

The first section is a justification of Paul's ministry and proof to anyone that he is able to compete with anyone who brags about works.

["To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1). The new translations [(]{.class_4095}Phillips[, NEB, ]{.class_4095}and [Good News) ]{.class_4095}twist the sentence so that it appears that the subject of ["the same things" ]{.class_4096}is what follows---a warning about Judaizers. However, the meaning is clearly: "It doesn't bother me to keep on telling you to rejoice, even though I have mentioned the word and its cognates seven times already---1:4, 18, 25, 26, 2:2, 17, 28---so I am going to mention it some more---3:1, 4:4, and 4:10---and that will be a safeguard for you!"

As a matter of fact, where there is [rejoicing, ]{.class_4095}there cannot be grumbling and murmuring (2:14), and this will save [burying ]{.class_4095}some corpses (1 Cor. 10:5--12). Where there is joyful thanks (Rom. 1:8), there is little apostasy, ["for the joy of the ]{.class_4096}[Lord ]{.class_4103}[is your strength" ]{.class_4096}(Neh. 8:10), since it is the second fruit of the Holy Spirit (see comments under Gal. 5:22).

The Coca Cola company has plastered []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_378}["The pause that refreshes" ]{.class_4095}and ["Have a Coke" ]{.class_4095}on every highway and road stop from Fairbanks to Patagonia, and every grocery store and juke joint from Uelen (Siberia) to Cape Town. They never feel ["grievous"]{.class_4096} about repeating it over and over and over. They dim it into your skull until you walk into a drugstore and say, "I'll have a Coke," without even being conscious of the fact that you blew your mind the last 35 times you saw "Have a Coke." You don't want a gummy glass of soda water! No "soft drink" is a thirst quencher.

You see, the Christian should develop the habit of [rejoicing ]{.class_4095}(Phil. 4:4) just like Coke addicts develop the [drug habit. ]{.class_4095}And ["it is SAFE"]{.class_4096} for Christians to develop this habit (see text).

["Beware of dogs"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2; cf. Rev. 21:8, 22:15; Matt. 7:6, etc.). The dogs are obviously two‑legged, and the immediate reference is to dogs who practice "mutilation" (["beware of the CONCISION"]{.class_4096}). Since the word is defined in the next verse, the numerous treatises on it are worth little or nothing. ["For we are the circumcision\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 3).

Men are like DOGS. Dogs have many good qualities (see [Some Dogs I Have Known ]{.class_4095}by Bob Shuler), but a dog is still a dog, and a dog [will eat vomit ]{.class_4095}(2 Pet. 2:22). Some men are like [collies---]{.class_4095}slow, patient, kind, lovable, gentle, and friendly. Some men are like [chihuahuas---]{.class_4095}snappy, irritable, nervous, noisy, wildeyed, cowardly, and a nuisance. Some men are like [English bulldogs---]{.class_4095}slow, tenacious, stubborn, ugly, good-natured, and gentle. Others are like [French ]{.class_4095}poodles---dainty, prissy, showy, high‑strung, and good for nothing. Others are more like [bull mastiffs]{.class_4095}---small, solid, tough, dependable, brave, and common looking. Some are more like [Great Danes---]{.class_4095}large, fearful looking, muscular, slow, and not too bright. And some are like [German shepherds---]{.class_4095}alert, strong, powerful, aggressive, quick, and brutal.

But a dog is still a [dog, ]{.class_4095}and a lost man is a [lost ]{.class_4095}man no matter what kind of CHARACTER he has. Some unsaved men have more character than some saved men. Beethoven, Rommel, Schweitzer, Field Marshall Ney, General Model, Yamamoto, Walt Disney, Madam Curie, and Enrico Caruso had standards and principles that many Twentieth‑Century Christians could not keep or even attempt to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_379}keep; and their personal failures were successes when compared to the standards of discipline and ethical principles of 90 percent of the "new breed of Christians" (see remarks under Eph. 6:10--17).

["Worship God\...rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have NO CONFIDENCE in the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3). Again, the reader should notice how the word ["flesh"]{.class_4096} is used. The "old nature" is identified as the SKIN in which you walk around, and this is apparent from the context: ["concision" ]{.class_4096}and ["circumcision." ]{.class_4096}The addition of ["what we are]{.class_4095} in the flesh" [(Amplified New Testament) is, ]{.class_4095}indeed, an [amplification]{.class_4095}.

Three times in the passage Paul uses the expression ["in the flesh"]{.class_4096}: ["no confidence in the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3), ["1 might also have confidence in the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4), and ["he might trust in the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). The Christian, then, places ["NO confidence in the flesh,"]{.class_4096} and this implies [anything connected with it ]{.class_4095}or to it, [directly ]{.class_4095}or [indirectly. ]{.class_4095}Our flesh had nothing to do with our salvation (Rom. 8:12), and it has nothing to do with our security (Gal. 5:7).

During a broadcast in Tampa, Florida (Pastor Roy Mason), a sermon on a record got "hung up." The needle went back over one groove for seven minutes, and the part of the record that kept repeating was a quotation of Ephesians 2:9.              What the radio audience heard was, ["Not of works ‑‑ not of works ‑- not of works ‑‑ not of works ‑‑ not of works\...."]{.class_4096} The announcer had placed the record on the Gates turntable and had gone out for coffee! When a telephone call finally told him what was going out over the air, the Tampa area had already heard seven minutes of ["not of works ‑ ‑ not of works ‑ ‑ not of works\...."]{.class_4096}

["WE\...have no confidence in the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3). Furthermore, our "rejoicing" is not in the Jets, Colts, Redskins, Packers, Dodgers, Reds, Browns, Dolphins, 49'ers, Chiefs, Yankees, or Red Skelton, Jack Benny, Danny Thomas, Lucy, Flip Wilson, Martin and Rowen, the Smothers Brothers, Queen for a Day, or the Reader's Digest Jackpot Prize. ["We\...rejoice in Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3). The commentators can only understand the expression as it stands within the context, opposed to "bragging about salvation from works in the flesh." But this only shows that the commentators, revisers, "modern editors," etc., have failed to bear ["the fruit of the Spirit" ]{.class_4096}(Gal. 5:22 and comments). We ["rejoice in Christ" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_380}as an OBJECT instead of ["the flesh"]{.class_4096} as an object, and He is the "joy and rejoicing" of our hearts.

Now, observe how the "newer" translations avoid the truth as though the truth were the Black Death. To get around the clear teaching of the passage in regard to "rejoicing" [(and "rejoice" was the key word of this epistle according to ALL the commentators!), ]{.class_4095}the words ["in the flesh"]{.class_4096} have been confined to a [past doctrinal ]{.class_4095}meaning only. ["Good News" ]{.class_4095}translates ["external ceremonies,"]{.class_4095} which is found in no Greek manuscript of any family. The [NEB ]{.class_4095}translates ["anything external,"]{.class_4095} and Phillips incorporates the words which the [Amplified ]{.class_4095}suggested as part of the text, although no Greek manuscripts contain the words. Wuest has added, in brackets, ["human worthiness and attainment,"]{.class_4095} and various other human inventions have been added, such as "outward rites," [(Pulpit Commentary), ]{.class_4095}"external privileges" (Dummelow), and "self' (Rev. Rendall).

Now, all of this sidestepping and catwalking has been done to draw your attention from the fact that "the flesh" is a reference to SKIN on people. When Paul says ["Beware of dogs," ]{.class_4096}he is talking about people, not "external privileges," "outward rites," "human attainment," "self," or anything like it.

You see, the Christian is not only to abandon confidence in his own flesh, [he is to abandon confidence in anybody's flesh, ]{.class_4095}in the sense of [skin on bones]{.class_4095} (1 Pet. 4:6).[ ]{.class_4095}When Paul said ["rejoice in the Lord" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1), he was talking about the OBJECT of a Christian's joy, exactly as it is found in 4:4 and 1:18. This is said in contrast to trusting or confiding in the flesh. The implications are obvious. Even if the Christian "trusts Christ" instead of works for salvation, the text is only half expounded, for the subject matter of verse 3 is true in worship (John 4:24) and rejoicing.

When a Christian rejoices in Academy Awards, Miss America contests, Major League playoffs, attendance and giving, promotion and praise, the ecumenical movement, or the "All Stars," he is putting ["confidence in the flesh," ]{.class_4096}and the fact that he is not trusting the flesh for salvation is [immaterial. ]{.class_4095}["Where your treasure is, there will your heart be also" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 6:21). ["Keep []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_381}thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life" ]{.class_4096}(Prov. 4:23, see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs,]{.class_4095} 1972).

Many Christians who were "saved by faith," trust, confide, and rejoice ["in the FLESH."]{.class_4096} The failure of our modern commentators to see this salient point speaks eloquently of [their ]{.class_4095}spirituality.

["Though I might also have confidence in the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). All that follows is clear. If anybody in the world had a right to brag about the flesh, Paul had the right. He not only had the right to brag about it [before ]{.class_4095}he was saved (vss. 5--6), he had the right to brag about it AFTER he was saved (Gal. 6:17 and comments). This reinforces what we have said under verse 3. The issue is not closed with some pious mouthing about "external rites" and "what we are in the flesh." ["Rejoicing in the flesh" ]{.class_4095}can be a Christian pastime even when the Christian is [uncircumcised ]{.class_4095}and is [resting in the finished work of Christ. ]{.class_4095}We repeat: the failure of the modern commentators---take the first thirty at random---to grasp this essential truth is in itself a comment on their [spiritual condition.]{.class_4095}

The ["gain" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 7), which Paul[ "counted loss,"]{.class_4096} consisted of seven things:

1\. Circumcision.

2\. Native of Israel.

3\. From the tribe that furnished Israel with her first King---Benjamin.

4\. A ["Hebrew of the Hebrews."]{.class_4096}

5\. The highest type of Hebrew---["a Pharisee."]{.class_4096}

6\. A zealous Pharisee (Acts 26:5) who persecuted heretics.

7\. A righteous man (cf. Acts 10:2, 22), blameless as touching the law.

That is, Paul was a thoroughbred German shepherd before he was saved (see notes on vs. 2), and as an unsaved man, he had more character and religion than any Bishop or Cardinal in the United States of America. But like a dog, he was lost.

The word ["blameless" ]{.class_4096}is used as it is used in 1 Timothy 3:2, 10 and Luke 1:6. Before the eyes of man (and the world), Paul kept the ceremonial observances to the letter. This is the righteousness spoken of in Romans 10:1--4. If Christ had not come (John 15:22--[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_382}24), it would have been "passable." But when the perfect, sinless Man showed up, ["made under the law"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 4:4),[ "the outward appearance"]{.class_4096} no longer sufficed (1 Sam. 16:7). An inner man was required who fulfilled ["the righteousness of the law"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:1--5).

The modern Catholic or Seventh‑day Adventist represents accurately the things involved in the text. Both of them (as Church of Christ people and Jehovah's Witnesses) are busy trying to develop a righteous man [by availing themselves of the FLESHLY means of purifying the FLESH]{.class_4095} (meaning "skin on your bones")! None of them can understand the dog's affinity for dung (see vs. 2, 8), and all make the mistake of thinking that a man can earn Heaven if he walks ["blameless."]{.class_4096} Paul was [blameless ]{.class_4095}and he was [doomed.]{.class_4095}

A number of alterations have been suggested by the modern editors and dead‑orthodox apostates. Among them, we are to suppose that "eight days old in circumcision" is clearer than ["circumcised the eighth day" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5), "exulting in Christ Jesus" was meant by the Holy Spirit instead of ["rejoice in Christ Jesus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3),[ "in the spirit" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 3) should have been "by the Spirit," Paul was circumcised when he was "a week old," not ["the eighth day," "beware" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2)[ ]{.class_4096}should have been "look to" or "mark," and ["Hebrew of the Hebrews"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5)[ ]{.class_4096}should have been "sprung from Hebrew ancestors" (Alford, Schnedermann, Davidson, Wuest, [NEB, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, Good News, ]{.class_4095}Clarke, Lange, Ellicott, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Rendall, Caffin, Custer, Lightfoot, Vincent, Neal, DeWette, Meyer, and other dead‑orthodox apostates). Since none of the correctors or corrections will be of any aid to the reader in understanding anything about the passage, they may be dispensed with without any "sweat." The [AV ]{.class_4095}text is brilliantly clear, and those who profess to be "updating" it in the "living language" of [Living Letters ]{.class_4095}for the modern "man on the street" are all lying anyway, which is perfectly apparent by what every one of them does with the word ["dung"]{.class_4096} which shortly shows up!

The picture of verses 4--6 is vivid. Before you got saved, you had a little stick horse religion that you used to gallop around the house, and you pretended that it was a live horse. You watered it and curried it and trotted it to the "sacraments" and put it through its []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_383}paces for the grandstand. When an intelligent person called it to your attention that it was a [dead stick,]{.class_4095} you[ ]{.class_4095}became quite put out (if not infuriated); and you baptized it, fed it, and placed it on top of a table where it would be higher than any dead stick in the room. You were highly indignant about people attacking your stick and insisted that in "freedom‑loving America," people should "respect each other's faith," and a man who would "knock somebody else's dead stick" was a Communist, Fascist, Polak, dirty name, etc. Then one day you found Jesus Christ, as Paul did, and the stick horse was thrown in the river where the DUNG eventually winds up!

3:7 But what things were gain to me, those I counted loss for Christ.

8 Yea doubtless, and I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord: for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and do count them but dung, that I may win Christ,

9 And be found in him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ, the righteousness which is of God by faith:

10 That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death;

11 If by any means I might attain unto the resurrection of the dead.

12 Not as though I had already attained, either were already perfect: but I follow after, if that I may apprehend that for which also I am apprehended of Christ Jesus.

13 Brethren, I count not myself to have apprehended: but this one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind, and reaching forth unto those things which are before,

14 I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.

Now comes a full statement of Paul's attitude towards religions since he found Christ. What he considered ["gain"]{.class_4096} (vs. [7) ]{.class_4095}becomes ["loss,"]{.class_4096} and what he esteemed to be ["heresy"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_384} (Acts 24:14), he now esteems "gain."

Paul desires to ["win Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8), to ["be found in"]{.class_4096} Christ (vs. 9), to get excellent knowledge of Him (vs. 8), to ["know the power of his resurrection"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10) and ["the fellowship of his sufferings"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10), and to participate in the resurrection from the dead (vs. 11).

["The excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus" ]{.class_4096}(cf. Eph. 1:17) is more valuable than any religion or anything connected with any religion. This is the thing that clearly sets off Biblical Christianity from every religion on the face of the earth that ever existed. No religion desires ["the excellency of the knowledge" ]{.class_4096}of its FOUNDER. Not even an orthodox Jew is "gung ho" about knowing Moses as a [person; ]{.class_4095}his [teach]{.class_4095}ings---yes, his [precepts---]{.class_4095}yes, his [writings]{.class_4095}---perhaps, but HIM? No Buddhist has to have any knowledge about Buddha's [present condition as a person ]{.class_4095}in order to know anything. No Confucian studies Confucius' [present state as a person, ]{.class_4095}and no Mohammedan wastes five minutes to attain a knowledge of Mohammed as he NOW lives. The phenomenon is unique to Biblebelieving Christianity, and there is nothing like it in Roman Catholicism or Liberal Protestantism.

["I have suffered the loss of all things, and do count them but DUNG"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8). And here, once again (see Gal. 3:27, 5:4, 11, 21; Eph. 1:6, 3:18, 4:9, 5:9, 6:17 for similar catastrophes), the Sword of the Spirit slices the scholars, critics, editors, revisers, commentators, and professors to the bone and discerns ["the thoughts and intents of the heart"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 4:12).[ ]{.class_4096}For 2,200 pages (see commentaries on Genesis, Exodus, Proverbs, Matthew, Revelation, etc.) we have been listening to the dead‑orthodox apostates whining about "the need for accuracy" when correcting [clear ]{.class_4095}passages in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611, and then reversing field and going around left end hollering "clarity" where the [AV]{.class_4095} was more [accurate. ]{.class_4095}But you see, the double‑bladed broadsword of the Holy Spirit of God Almighty is so forged, tempered, honed, and oiled that it can cut in either direction and eventually SLAY any critic of any persuasion and drop him in his own blood. It is merely a matter of time (see comments under Col. 2:8). The old [King James 1611 ]{.class_4095}is the greatest, most scientific, infallible, analytical [critic ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_385}that ever lived; and simpletons like Dr. A. T. Robertson, Kenneth Wuest, Machen, Warfield, Wilbur Smith, E. Schuyler English, Weigle, Tregelles, Souter, Gaussen, Moulton, Custer, Milligan, Nestle, Neal, Westcott, Hort, Trench, Vincent, Poteat, and "the majority of scholars" are simply no match for it.

Look at the word: ["DUNG" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8)! Is that "archaic"? Then what is the plain modern four‑letter word in English that would "clarify the obscure Elizabethan English"? [Then why doesn't one translation on the market dare translate it?]{.class_4095}

What happened to all our brave, crusading pioneers who were engaged in "liberating the reader" from the "archaic expressions" of the [King James]{.class_4095} Bible?[ ]{.class_4095}Lose your guts, son? What happened? Did you run into a truckload of rabid police dogs? Where is all this "clarity" Bob Jones III talks so much about? [None of you wrote ]{.class_4095}["DUNG."]{.class_4096} What, then, did you do to the text?

The word ["skubala"]{.class_4095} (Greek) means "offal" or "dung" in the first two primary meanings in any Greek lexicon on the market. [What happened to "accuracy"? ]{.class_4095}"Uniformity"? "True to the original manuscripts," etc.?

Isn't it strange how "reverent" our translators have suddenly gotten after tearing up the Bible and stomping their dirty feet over every word in the Reformation text? My, my, what "class" we have here!

My, my, aren't we civilized!

Where did all our destructive critics (Conservatives foremost) get all this polish and tact from? One would be tempted to think that they still put a little value on ["all things" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8)! Certainly ["refuse"]{.class_4095} and ["garbage"]{.class_4095} are higher class commodities than ["dung." ]{.class_4096}If ["dung"]{.class_4096} was difficult to understand, I wonder why Phillips, Moffatt, Weymouth, Fenton, Goodspeed, Lamsa, Berkeley, Montgomery, and [Living Letters ]{.class_4095}didn't give us the Twentieth‑Century American equivalent in the "everyday language" of the "common man"? It is no time to stop now! If you have already made 31,000‑36,000 changes in the text (see the [RV ]{.class_4095}of 1881--1885), [why back out now?]{.class_4095}

No, you can be certain that Paul placed the religious trappings of the "sacraments" in the same place that Isaiah placed them (Isa. 64:6). Did you ever look up ["filthy rags" ]{.class_4096}in the "original Hebrew"?

Our modern commentators don't want to get their little pinkies dirty, and this is more than evident by their work here and their work on Philippians 3:21, which see. The two Roman Catholic editions, this time, are truer to Paul's intent than the premillennial Conservatives; both of the Catholic versions (1582 and 1945) read[ "dung."]{.class_4101}

["And be found in him" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 9). But Paul was already IN Him (see Gal. 3:27 and comments). Therefore, to ["win Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8) does not mean to "GAIN Christ" as the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961) wrongly translates. C. H. Dodd and his Socialistic humanitarians have confounded the "earning" of religion (vs. 7) with "earning Christ," and this is quite typical of self‑righteous sinners who are trying to justify themselves with good works. Nobody GAINS Christ at salvation. You can gain[ "knowledge" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8), and you can count as "gain" what you formerly considered "dung" and vice versa; but you cannot earn Christ. The "winning" spoken of in verse 8 is the accomplishment of a winner (1 Cor. 9:24--27) who is after a prize vs. 3:14).

Bishop Hall's comment, cited by Ellicott (Vol. 20, [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_4095} pg. 113), is completely beside the point, for every Christian is already IN Christ, engrafted into Him as part of His bone and flesh (see comments under Eph. 5:30). The Christian of ["Good News"]{.class_4095} (pg. 445) is only loosely joined to Christ by a temporary association and is looking for a [future time ]{.class_4095}where he will be []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_387}"completely united" with Him. There is no such relationship except in the minds of men like the translators of the [New ASV, ]{.class_4095}recommended by Bob Jones University (Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer), who have translated Galatians 5:4 as ["severed from Christ" ]{.class_4095}(see comments on the verse). But every Christian reading this print is [completely united ]{.class_4095}to Jesus Christ as part of His body, [bone of His bone and flesh of His flesh ]{.class_4095}(Eph. 5:30). \[Phillip's translation of Gal. 5:4 is superior to the [ASV, New ASV, ]{.class_4095}and similar apostate corruptions.\]

Observe the tense of the verbs in the infallible [King James ]{.class_4095}text: ["I COUNT all things\...I HAVE SUFFERED the loss\...and DO COUNT\...that I MAY WIN" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8). He is speaking as a saved man already united completely with Christ as a part of His body. Any further references (vss. 9--14) must refer to something in the future, something beyond salvation that is connected with ["the resurrection of the dead" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown are wise in commenting that the expression ["be found" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 9) refers to the Second Coming; it has nothing to do with getting "completely in Christ" lest you be "severed from Him," and similar nonsense.

To get a full understanding of the passage, a knowledge of verse 11 is essential, and getting a knowledge of verse 11 is nearly as difficult as getting a knowledge of Ephesians 3:18. When confronted with a real problem, no amount of "original manuscripts," "archaeological studies," "better manuscripts," or "scientific research" seems to avail any of the modern commentators or revisers. Phenomenon No. 9.

1\. The ["resurrection"]{.class_4096} referred to is the last general resurrection of the unsaved dead (Kennedy, Ex[positor's Greek Testament, ]{.class_4095}pg. 457). Note that the Reverend is worried about the "hazards" of stating that Paul denied a general resurrection. The Reverend is more worried about the "hazards" of [telling the truth ]{.class_4095}than the hazards of [altering the words of God. ]{.class_4095}Every epistle Paul ever wrote regarding the resurrection spoke of a special resurrection APART from a general resurrection. Not even the apostles (Acts 4:2) spoke of a "general resurrection" for Christians.

2\. The resurrection is the resurrection of the Christian [after []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_388}death.]{.class_4095}

3\. The resurrection is the [Rapture ]{.class_4095}of the Christian [before death.]{.class_4095}

4\. The resurrection is a [spiritual resurrection ]{.class_4095}after the New Birth that gives the Christian [a higher ]{.class_4095}level of spirituality.

5\. The resurrection is a super‑heavenly state above the heavenlies which Paul hoped he would attain because this was the place the              Church was supposed to occupy (Williams, pg. 833). \[Williams doesn't really buy this, since he refused to make any comment on vs. 11 after commenting on vss. 9--14. Like Kenneth Wuest, the brother seems to be prone to leave out verses without notifying the reader! See [Expanded Translation, ]{.class_4095}Wuest, pg. 59--60.\]

To avoid the embarrass ment of having to explain verse 11, the [RSV]{.class_4095}, [ASV, New ASV, RV]{.class_4095}---with Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown---have altered the word ["attained" ]{.class_4096}in verse 12 to "obtain" so that it can be divorced from the ["attain" ]{.class_4096}in verse 11! But the change accomplishes nothing, for the word ["attain"]{.class_4096} in verse 11 means "to come upon" or "to arrive at" or "to come to." It is apparent that Paul had not yet arrived (or come) to this resurrection, so the divorcement of ["Not as though I had already attained" ]{.class_4096}would have applied to the verse whether you altered that ["attain" ]{.class_4096}to "obtain" or left it the way it stood. Phenomenon No. 8.

"Accuracy" in translation doesn't solve any problem many times. The word ["apprehended" ]{.class_4096}is in the same class with "obtain" or ["attain."]{.class_4096} It is therefore apparent that Paul is not making any reference to the [event ]{.class_4095}of any PHYSICAL resurrection or rapture.

It would be rather stupid to say: "I don't think I've been resurrected [from the grave yet or caught up in the Rapture, ]{.class_4095}but I'm going to keep on working in hopes that one or the other might [happen." Both will happen. ]{.class_4095}Whether you "attain" or don't "attain," you will rise, [living or dead ]{.class_4095}(1 Cor. 15:49--55), no matter what your attitude toward "prizes" and "high callings" are. ["The dead in Christ"]{.class_4096} rise and the "living" rise (1 Thess. 4:16--17), and you couldn't "obtain" or ["attain"]{.class_4096} either of these prophesied events if you "pressed forward" (vs. 14) like a snow plow on a locomotive.

The correct interpretation, then, will lie within in the English text, and attempts to clarify the muddle with the "original Greek" will end where the United Nations ends: Babel.

1\. Paul had a ["high calling"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_389}(Heb. 3:1; 1 Cor. 1:26; 2 Thess. 1:11; 2 Tim. 1:9).

2\. There was a ["prize"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}to be obtained if this calling was fulfilled (Col. 4:17), and this prize came in the form of CROWNS (see 1 Cor. 9:25; 1 Thess. 2:19; James 1:12; Rev. 2:10; 2 Tim. 4:8; and 1 Pet. 5:4).

3\. To obtain this prize, a man must not merely be saved and IN Christ, he must know Christ's experience from his own living (vs. 10). The power of Christ's resurrection is available [in this life ]{.class_4095}for that "living" (see comments under Eph. 3:20; 1:19).

4\. When Paul says ["conformable unto his death,"]{.class_4096} he is not referring to a literal crucifixion, for he never received a literal crucifixion, Paul was [decapitated.]{.class_4095}

5\. The "attainment," therefore, of verse 11 is in [connection ]{.class_4095}with the ["better resurrection" ]{.class_4096}of Hebrews 11:35 (see Acts 4:2; Luke 16:31; Mark 12:25; and Luke 20:35).

6\. To obtain this ["better resurrection,"]{.class_4096} the following things are necessary:

A. The Christian must count sacramental religion as EXCREMENT. [(How's that for "clarity"?)]{.class_4095}

B. He must ["win Christ" ]{.class_4096}in the following manner:

a\. By getting to ["know Him"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}personally (vs. 10).

b\. By claiming ["the power"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}that raised Him from the dead (vs. 10).

c\. By actually [suffering ]{.class_4095}with Christ as He suffered (vs. 10).

d\. By [daily dying ]{.class_4095}and mortifying of the body (Gal. 2:20).

e\. By continually [following ]{.class_4095}God's will (vs. 12).

f\. By ["forgetting"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}the past (vs. 13).

g\. By reaching forward (vs. 13).

h\. And by [pressing forward ]{.class_4095}(vs. 14).

This is the only interpretation that will match the text as it stands. Alterations of the text to prove private interpretations will leave the student (and the professor) in the same confusion with which they started.

The resurrection, as an EVENT, is in 1 Thessalonians 4:15--16 and 1 Corinthians 15:23, but "arriving at" and "attaining to" this resurrection is not living on and on until you get caught up without dying (Kennedy, Ibid., pg. 457). The man who has "arrived" and "attained" this resurrection is the man who is ["found in him" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_390}(vs. 9), when this resurrection takes place, full of faith (vs. 9) and fruit (vs. 10) and a course well‑run (vs. 14) in response to his calling (vs. 12).

Many Christians cannot "press forward," for they have been backsliding so long that they couldn't move forward with a hurricane behind their backs. Many cannot forget any of the "things which are behind" if those things persist in the form of arguments, defeats, and victories. Most Christians down South live in the past, and the only revival they ever had was the one ten years ago. Instead of saying "This one thing I do," they are singing, "These twenty things I dabble in." Their "race" looks like a bunch of ants that got out of an ink well and crawled across a piece of paper.

Perhaps the most unique interpretation of the passage is the one by Bullinger, who states that Paul hoped to get raptured [ahead of the rapture scheduled for the Church ]{.class_4095}([Companion Bible,]{.class_4095} pg. 1778). (If he had made it, he could have gone up with Mary, if we are to believe the silly Popes of 1920--1960!)

The price for power is [suffering (vs. 10): ]{.class_4095}no cross, no crown. Christ does not merely come out of the grave at His resurrection (vss. 10--11), He is made both Lord and Saviour (Acts 2:36) when He "attains" the "resurrection from the dead." These things were the prizes for a race run well (Heb. 12:1--3), and they were WON in fair combat (Col. 2:15). Paul has not "attained" Christian perfection in the sense of verse 10---["Not as though I had ALREADY ATTAINED, either were ALREADY PERFECT" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12).

He has not ["apprehended" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 13) but desires to ["apprehend"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12) because God has ["apprehended"]{.class_4096} him (vs. 12) for a task. It is good to know exactly why your boss hired you or why your Company Commander sent you on the mission on which you find yourself (Col. 1:9, 28). Christ Jesus laid hold on him for something, so he is bent on "laying hold" of Christ. (For the Bible's own definition of ["perfect,"]{.class_4096} see the text of the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 in Job 8:20, 9:20--22; Gen. 6:9, 17:1; Matt 19:21; James 3:2, etc.)

Before closing the section, the reader should take note of the work put out by the Tyndale House Publishers, Wheaton, Illinois. This "Living Letter" states that the resurrection of verse 11 is PAST (2 Tim. 2:17--18), and that Paul is already "alive from the dead." []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_391}[This is the heresy for which Hymenaeus was delivered to Satan ]{.class_4095}(1 Tim. 1:20) [that his flesh might be destroyed]{.class_4095} (1 Cor. 5:5). Very often, ["Living Letters" ]{.class_4095}turn out to be ["Deathly Epistles."]{.class_4095}

3:15 Let us therefore, as many as be perfect, be thus minded: and if in any thing ye be otherwise minded, God shall reveal even this unto you.

16 Nevertheless, whereto we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule, let us mind the same thing.

17 Brethren, be followers together of me, and mark them which walk so as ye have us for an ensample.

18 (For many walk, of whom I have told you often, and now tell you even weeping, that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ:

19 Whose end is destruction, whose God is their belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who mind earthly things.)

20 For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ:

21 Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body, according to the working whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself.

If a man is ["perfect"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) in the sense of his attitude toward the past and the future (vs. 13--14), let him have the same mind about verses 9 and 10 that Paul has, and let him not think that he has been "perfected" simply because he is ["perfect." ]{.class_4096}The man who thinks that the race is over is ["otherwise minded" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15), for Paul has said in verse 13 that he himself did not believe that he had yet even grasped the full significance of his calling.

["God shall reveal even this unto you" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 15). God will show the Christian where he is in error and where he is "off base" in his attitude toward the things mentioned above, if indeed he really is.

["Let us walk by the same rule, let us mind the same thing" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 16; cf. Gal. 2:2, 6:16; Eph. 4:1--4). Even though the Christian has not attained ["the prize]{.class_4096} [of the high calling," ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_392}and even though he has not apprehended fully ["the high calling,"]{.class_4096} and even though he has not "won Christ" (in the sense of full reward at the resurrection), he should keep the ground that he has gained and should walk peaceably with those who have attained the same ground. He should be self‑reliant, but not [self‑sufficient; ]{.class_4095}steadfast, but not [stubborn; ]{.class_4095}tactful, but not [timid; ]{.class_4095}serious, not [sullen; ]{.class_4095}unmovable, but not [stationary; ]{.class_4095}tender, but not ["touchy"; ]{.class_4095}generous, but not [gullible; ]{.class_4095}meek, but not [weak; ]{.class_4095}and gentle, but not ["hypersensitive."]{.class_4095}

According to the commentators, we should: 1) omit ["let us mind the same," ]{.class_4096}2) change ["same thing" ]{.class_4096}into "let us walk," 3) omit ["by the same rule," ]{.class_4096}and 4) substitute "let us hold true." Since [Good News ]{.class_4095}would have us read[ "let us go forward to the same rules,"]{.class_4095} and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}would have us read ["our conduct should be consistent with the level"]{.class_4095}---plus ["go forward in the light of such truth" ]{.class_4095}(Phillips)[ ]{.class_4095}and ["obey the truth you already have"]{.class_4095} [(Living Letters), ]{.class_4095}plus "let us keep to the same path marching in file" [(Expositor's Greek Testament, ]{.class_4095}pg. 460)---a "clearer translation of the original" would be as follows:

"Since we have walked around to some rule of principle whereby some of us have attained to, our conduct should go forward with consistent rules that would hold true to the truth we already marched in file to keep the same path of truth going forward at the same level to obey what we already have."

(If that isn't clear, neither is any translation since 1611!)

The reason for all the confounded chaos is that the school at Alexandria (Origen, Philo, Clement, et al.) has removed six to seven words from the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text even though these words are found in the Hesychian family ([Aleph ]{.class_4095}"c"), the Byzantine family ([K, P]{.class_4095}), the Western family (D original), and the vast majority of Greek manuscripts. (The reader may disregard the last thirty versions and stick to the [King James ]{.class_4095}text.)

Verse 17 is self‑explanatory (see Eph. 5:1, Phil. 2:22 and comments; also 1 Cor. 4:16 and Phil. 4:9). ["Now tell you even weeping, that they are the enemies of the CROSS of Christ" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18). Those whom Paul is grieving over are enemies of the CROSS. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_393}The text implies that they are unsaved people going to Hell (vs. 19---["Whose end is DESTRUCTION\..."]{.class_4096}),[ ]{.class_4096}but it is remarkable to note that there are many bornagain Christians who are ["enemies of the CROSS" ]{.class_4096}(Gal. 2:20, 6:12). They are not enemies of Jesus Christ Himself; what they hate and shrink back from is the CROSS. The cross‑bearing life (Matt. 10:38) is not the life taught and recommended from many Fundamental pulpits today. One can tell in a moment from feeling out the "spiritual tone" of a congregation that crossbearing has not been mentioned very much; if it has been mentioned, it has been an afterthought on the part of the leaders. One can hear a great deal about "revival meetings," "congresses on evangelism," "power through prayer," "turning loose your faith," "trying the blessing plan," "speaking with other tongues," and "total evangelism in depth," etc.; but you will twist the dial and spin the knobs of your radio a long time before you will pick up a message on suffering reproach for Christ's sake (see vss. 10--11) and "going outside the camp" (Heb. 13:11--13) as a condemned criminal. Modern Christianity (of nearly any brand) has a peculiar "hail‑fellow‑well‑met‑friendliest‑church‑in‑a-friendly-community‑for‑friendly‑loving‑friends" to it that reminds one of Demas and Diotrephes.

["For MANY walk\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 18), not a "few." If there were[ "many"]{.class_4096} in Paul's day, what is the count now? Jesus said, ["many\...many\...many\...many\...many" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 24:5, 10--12). The enemies referred to here are the enemies of 2 Peter 2:1. Since they may be Jews (Rom. 11:28), they are "beloved enemies" (Rom. 11:28); this explains why Paul says, ["and now tell you even WEEPING."]{.class_4096}

Dummelow takes it for granted that those referred to are libertine Christians (Antinomians), and they are the ones referred to in Jude 4; Galatians 5:13; Romans 6:1; and Ephesians 4:19. This may be, but again a fine distinction is missed in the cross-references. Many [saved ]{.class_4095}people glory in [earthly things, ]{.class_4095}and many of them are not looking for "the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ" to come (1 John 3:1--3). It is taken for granted that when Paul says, ["whose God is their BELLY," ]{.class_4096}that this must be a reference to someone who is grossly licentious and obsessed with fleshly pleasures. However, this []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_394}shows a little naivete in the Christian who is supposed to know human nature.

For example, there is one religion on earth whose priests address a piece of BREAD as "my Lord and my God" (see any missal) and then put it into their BELLY (Rom 16:18)! Now how did Lange, Clarke, Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Davidson, DeWette, Lipsius, Rendall, Williams, Kennedy, Bullinger, and the [New Bible Commentary ]{.class_4095}miss that one?! The 31st article in the Anglican Creed ([The Thirty-nine Articles]{.class_4095})[ ]{.class_4095}for Episcopal ministers states that the Mass is "a blasphemous fable and a dangerous deceit." Could those who offer it be ["the enemies of the CROSS"]{.class_4096}? If they are, the "libertine" line is out. [Some Roman Catholics are Trappist Monks!]{.class_4095}

While Dr. Mounce ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}) raves about "shameful lusts" and "licentious profligates" and "sensual indulgences" (pg. 1329), he, like the others, missed the obvious reference. Those who talk the most about the "cross" and "crossing themselves" and the "stations of the cross" follow the leadership of the most un‑Pauline man who ever lived (see vs. 17), and their reputation for "minding earthly things" is so universal and so well known that to this day a Jew thinks that [Christianity ]{.class_4095}is [Catholicism ]{.class_4095}and that the New Testament was written and preserved by "Catholics." Universal political dominion has always been, [and is ]{.class_4095}and shall be, the goal of the Roman Catholic Church (see [Vatican Imperialism in the Twentieth Century, ]{.class_4095}Avro Manhattan).

The greatest enemy that a cross‑bearing Christian ever had in his life, outside of his own flesh, was that great Mother of Abominations, that earthly, worldly, fleshy, proud, diamond‑studded, scarlet-clothed, religious Whore of Revelation 17. If the truth were told, every Bible published since 1881 was her work, or her [workers ]{.class_4095}or that of her [satellites, ]{.class_4095}attempting (undercover) to restore her "Bible" which cursed the world for 1,000 years. The reason why no modern commentator found the obvious truth of Philippians 3:18--19 is because he ate at her table, glutted himself on her dainties, and then reinforced his hatred for the Holy Bible by feasting on her sacred cow---[Vaticanus]{.class_4095}.

["Whose glory is in their shame, who mind earthly things" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_395}(vs. 19). They glory in their ability to alter the word. They glory in the number of young men they can turn from the Reformation text. They glory in the findings of Westcott and Hort. They glory in their ability to correct the Holy Spirit and usurp His authority. They [mind ]{.class_4095}the taboos and traditions of the Scholars' Union. They [mind ]{.class_4095}their diplomas and accredited standards. They [mind ]{.class_4095}the comments of the bloated egotists who betrayed their sacred trust. They [mind ]{.class_4095}the criticism and suggestions of God's enemies---the ["enemies of the CROSS of Christ." ]{.class_4096}That's how they make a living.

[Their God is their belly. ]{.class_4095}You make a [living ]{.class_4095}to feed your [belly]{.class_4095} (Eccl. 6:7).

The shameful, disgraceful, wretched conduct of these puffed and bloated egotists is no different in essence from the vainglorious boasting of Hugh Hefner, Joe Fletcher, Errol Flynn, "Ringo," or Artie Shaw. You can find as much truth in an "X‑rated" picture from Italy as you can find at the table of a modern "Revision Committee."

The man of the world (Psa. 17:14) speaks of the world, and the world hears him (1 John 4:5). The Bible rejecter---any Conservative or Fundamental scholar who alters the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 in this age---speaks of the "errors" and "mistakes" in God's word; and the Hell‑bound, belly‑worshipping, earth‑glorying sinners hear him and hear him gladly! The price of making a name in the field of Bible commenting and revision is [the destruction of a nation and the Body of Jesus Christ. ]{.class_4095}There are 100 Christians who are ready to pay that price [while you are reading these lines, ]{.class_4095}and the only difference between them and the "slow bellies" of Crete (Tit. 1:12) is that the ["slow bellies" ]{.class_4096}spoke [plainly ]{.class_4095}and these Christians talk [in circles.]{.class_4095}

Art Linkletter helped sponsor a dinner in Hollywood for a gourmets' club, where the total cost was \$300 per member. Chefs, with their wines, pots, and pans, were flown in from Paris for the dinner; and the total cost was over \$12,000. Now that is really worshipping the belly!

Then there is the boy who turned down his fourth hamburger at 3 o'clock in the afternoon, because it might cause him to "lose his appetite for dinner."

But these crass illustrations do not exhaust the text. The text is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_396}also dealing with people who are going to Hell because they reject the finished work of Christ on the cross (Gal. 6:14--15 and comments). These people, like many Christians, see just as far as the end of their noses and no further (see Col. 3:1--2 and comments). They are found throughout the Bible. Esau is one of these. Reuben, Gad, and the half tribe of Manasseh exhibit the symptoms. The rich man of Luke 12:16--20 is a good example. Demas lingers near the borderline. They are characterized in every age by:

1\. A love for publicity and recognition.

2\. A lack of interest in prophecy and eternity.

3\. An implicit faith in education and science.

4\. A cheerful, optimistic view of fallen man.

5\. A shrinking from anything emotional connected with real worship.

6\. A desire to constantly hear or tell something new.

7\. A refusal to believe there is anything evil with the contemporary games, fads, customs, and dress of their age.

8\. A dislike for sermons on Hell or the Second Advent.

9\. An undying hatred for the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611.

["For OUR CONVERSATION is in heaven\..." ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20).(See Gal. 1:4 and comments; also see [The]{.class_4095} [Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} pg. 151, on "citizenship" and "state.")

Our Saviour is there, our affections are there, our home is there, our treasures are there, and many of our loved ones are there. Why shouldn't our conversation be in Heaven? The belly‑worshippers have their treasures here, their homes here, and their loved ones here. Education, Religion, and Science are their "saviors," so they converse about earthly things and glory in them.

The end of the belly‑worshippers is ["destruction" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19), where they will be conformed to the image of their father (John 8:44; Rev. 12:9, 20:14; Mark 9:44, 46, 48). The end of those whose ["conversation is in heaven" ]{.class_4096}is Glory (vs. 21) and conformation to the 33‑year‑old, male, sinless body of Jesus Christ (Rom. 8:29; 1 John 3:1--2). Since no major commentator or reviser from Origen to English---of any profession---was able to believe the text (vs. 21), it would do well to examine it and dig up the "unsearchable riches" of the "English nuggets," which, for nineteen centuries, have been []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_397}obscured by the "original Greek."

["Who shall change our VILE body" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 21). This attack on the flesh, in the sense of [skin and bones ]{.class_4095}(see 3:3--4), so deeply disturbs the orthodox "defenders of the faith against the Gnostics" that they decide something must be done. Verses such as this and Luke 16:15; Genesis 6:5; Isaiah 64:6; and James 3:6 are taken to be "mistranslations." Since they speak so disparingly of "mankind," they are usually altered or twisted to meet the demands of private interpretation. Conservatives and Fundamentalists both adopt this procedure when dealing with verses that are just a little too "tough" (see comments under 3:8). So to "cool it" (as the original Aramaic reads!), the word ["vile"]{.class_4096} has been toned down for the Christian day school and the Christian Parent‑Teachers Association---composed of old maids and money donators.

1\. ["Vile"]{.class_4096} should have been ["dying bodies"]{.class_4095} ([Living Letters, ]{.class_4095}1962).

2\. ["Vile"]{.class_4096} should have been ["weak, mortal"]{.class_4095} ([Good News, ]{.class_4095}1966).

3\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been ["the body of our humiliation"]{.class_4095} ([Amplified New Testament,]{.class_4095} 1958).

4\. ["Vile"]{.class_4096} should have been ["wretched"]{.class_4095} (Phillips[, ]{.class_4095}1958).

5\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been ["our humble state"]{.class_4095} ([NEB,]{.class_4095} 1961)!

6\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been ["which has been humiliated"]{.class_4095} (Wuest, 1956).

7\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been[ "lowly"]{.class_4095} ([RSV,]{.class_4095} 1946).

8\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been ["the body of our humiliation"]{.class_4095} ([RV, 1881; ASV, 1901]{.class_4095}).

9\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been ["humiliation"]{.class_4095} (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown Commentary).

10\. ["Vile"]{.class_4096} should have been ["lowly body"]{.class_4095} ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}).

11\. ["Vile" ]{.class_4096}should have been ["body of humiliation"]{.class_4095} (Dummelow and the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}).

12\. Ditto---Lipsius, Rendall, Bullinger, Lightfoot, Ellicott, and the next 350 you might pick up.

Anything but believe the Bible.

You know, if we were not broad‑minded, we might get the mistaken impression that somebody didn't like that word ["vile"]{.class_4096}! But []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_398}wouldn't we be naughty‑naughty to think such a thing? So let's just pretend that we didn't see [the Roman Catholic Rheims of 1582, published by the Jesuit priests. ]{.class_4095}You see, three centuries before Phillips, Weymouth, Moffatt, Lange, Clarke, Goodspeed, Williams, Berkeley, Mickey Mouse, Fenton, Gobey, Charlie Brown, the [NEB, ]{.class_4095}the [RV, ]{.class_4095}the [ASV, ]{.class_4095}Captain Midnight, the [New ASV, ]{.class_4095}and the Green Hornet decided to attack the [King James ]{.class_4095}text, [the Jesuits had already done it. ]{.class_4095}The Jesuit Bible reads as the [New American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}(1963), [recommended ]{.class_4095}by the reactionary faculty at Bob Jones University in Greenville, South Carolina (see recommendation given in the publication against the [TEV ]{.class_4095}by Dr. Custer and Dr. Neal). It must be gratifying indeed for the Pope to know that the[ ]{.class_4098}most ardent promoters of his work are the Premillennial, soulwinning Fundamentalists, but we mentioned this eleven years ago in [The Bible Babel ]{.class_4095}(1964).

Now, why the sudden fits of hysteria at the word [vile"]{.class_4096}? Didn't Westcott and Hort [use this word ]{.class_4095}in referring to the [Textus Receptus ]{.class_4095}of the Reformation (see pg. 223, [Which Bible?,]{.class_4095} D. O. Fuller, 1970)? Why then the sudden attack of acid indigestion when the word was placed on the corruptible (1 Cor. 15:40--48), degenerate (1 Pet. 1:24) humus of rotting flesh (Job 7:5)? Is rotting flesh to be spoken of in more gentle terms than the living word of the living God?

You see, very often what is called "overstatement" or "exaggeration" by the Conservatives who hate, fear, and despise the [King James ]{.class_4095}text, is merely the [plain, unvarnished truth. ]{.class_4095}No Bible could come from [any set of manuscripts ]{.class_4095}more corrupt than those used for the [New ASV ]{.class_4095}(1963---recommended by Bob Jones University). These corrupt Alexandrian forgeries are the basis of Nestle's text, which states the doctrinal convictions of [Arius ]{.class_4095}(A.D. 256--336). The text of Nestle, [used for the New ASV, ]{.class_4095}states that Jesus Christ is a [begotten God ]{.class_4095}(John 1:18). To call the Greek manuscripts of the [NEB ]{.class_4095}and ["Good News" ]{.class_4095}"inferior," in the face of [this evidence, ]{.class_4095}is like a polecat telling a possum he has bad breath. (The [New ASV ]{.class_4095}is just as corrupt as the old one, and any future "ASV" will be more so. \[See Vance's work on the [New ASV "Update"---Double Jeopardy,]{.class_4095} 1998\].)

But why waste time going back over something that has been []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2726.xhtml_page_399}proved on more than forty occasions (see Commentaries on Genesis, Exodus, Proverbs, Matthew, Revelation, Galatians, and Ephesians)? Let us cast aside these superficial attempts of the dead‑orthodox apostates to create a "Bible," and let us see what God has for us in the text of the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible[, ]{.class_4095}which all of the revisers on all the committees---[RV]{.class_4095}, [ASV, ]{.class_4095}RSV, [New ]{.class_4095}RSV, [New ASV, Jerusalem, New Jerusalem, Amplified, ]{.class_4095}New Amplified, Rinso, and New Rinso---missed.

1\. Your body of skin and bones is ["vile,"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}as the text states. It corrupts, it rots, it falls apart, it decays and decomposes, [and WORMS eat it ]{.class_4095}(Job 21:26).

2\. Christ's glorious body is [incorruptible, ]{.class_4095}holy, sinless, perfect, glorious, and eternal; and when He appears (vs. 20), ["we shall be like him"]{.class_4096} (1 John 3:1--2).

a\. We will all be MALE: see Genesis 2:7, 5:1--2; Judges 13:3--13; Revelation 21:17; Luke 24:36--44; Matthew 22:30; and Galatians 4:14. Every angel in the Bible is a MALE, and you couldn't find a "sexless angel" in the Bible with a microscope, binoculars, forceps, laser beams, and tweezers even if you had the "original manuscripts." The fact that the greatest premillennial Conservatives of this century (Weston, Gray, Erdman, Pierson, Moorehead, Harris, and Gaebelein) imagined such a thing only proves again that when a man rejects the authority of the Holy Spirit through His [Authorized ]{.class_4095}Bible, the man can no longer interpret sixth‑grade English.

b\. To teach the private interpretation of "sexless angels," the Scofield board of editors---any edition in any decade---have omitted ["in heaven" ]{.class_4096}from the quotation of Matthew 22:30 when citing it, and have given a false cross-reference to Isaiah 43:6 when expounding Genesis 6:4. They have completely ignored the creedal statement of the ["Ordo Saludis"]{.class_4095} in Romans 8, which states that we are to be ["conformed to the image of his Son"]{.class_4096} (Rom.[ ]{.class_4096}8:29). Jesus, the Angel of the Lord, was NOT "sexless."

c\. Your resurrection body will be 33‑years‑old, as Christ's.

d\. It will be able to travel faster than the speed of light, as Christ's (John 20:17 cf. Matt. 28:9).

e\. It will be able to go through solid objects, as Christ's (John 20:19, 26), and appear and disappear at will (Luke 24:31, 36).

f\. It will be able to eat and drink without worrying about digestion, assimilation, or elimination (Luke 24:41--43).

g\. It will be flesh and bones (Luke 24:39), [without blood]{.class_4095} (1 Cor. 15:50).

h\. It will be equipped with an omniscient mind which can recognize any saved person, from any age, at sight (1 Cor. 13:12), without the aid of distinctive physical features (1 Cor. 13:10).

i\. It will be an immortal, deathless, eternal body of sinless perfection!

A sexless" angel would be a synthesized, neutralized, intermediate man‑woman. These things are the subject matter of modern science and abnormal psychology, and God would have no more to do with a "sexless man" (or woman!) than He would with a brain transplant or goat glands (Dr. Brinkley: 1930--1940).

When the Holy Spirit says that you will ["be fashioned LIKE UNTO HIS GLORIOUS BODY]{.class_4096}" (vs. 21), He means exactly what He says; and He would not hesitate to blind the spiritual eyesight (and mind) of any man who came to the text messing with "lowly, dying, humble, wretched, humiliated folks." Your body is VILE, and if you don't believe that, watch what the undertaker and the mortician do with it a week after you're dead. If you can't [burn ]{.class_4095}garbage, you [bury ]{.class_4095}it. (You won't find that in the "original Greek" which Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer lie about in their pamphlet on the [TEV ]{.class_4095}\[pg. 19\]; but you will be able to find it in a rifle company in Vietnam, Korea, Germany, Tarawa, or your nearest [garbage disposal unit. ]{.class_4095}Sometimes, the city sanitation department can put more light on a text than [The Expositor's Greek New Testament!)]{.class_4095}

["According to the working ]{.class_4096}\[see Eph. 3:20\] [whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto HIMSELF" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 21). The Lord will do it (Rom. 8:29). While Scofield, Pettingill, Epp, English, McClain, Smith, Ockenga, Graham, and Thieme are still trying to make sexless angels out of "lowly" bodies, ["our vile body" ]{.class_4096}will ["be fashioned like unto his glorious body"!]{.class_4096}

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml}

## Chapter 4 {.heading_116}

4:1 Therefore, my brethren, dearly beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand fast in the Lord, my dearly beloved.

2 I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntyche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.

3 And I entreat thee also, true yokefellow, help those women which laboured with me in the gospel, with Clement also, and with other my fellowlabourers, whose names are in the book of life.

4 Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.

5 Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand.

6 Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.

7 And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.

Verse one is self-explanatory. The converts at Philippi are Paul's ["dearly beloved\...longed for\...joy and crown"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1; cf. 1 Thess. 2:19 for a similar thought). The "victor's festal garland" (Wuest) was substituted for the [crown]{.class_4095}, but then Wuest altered it to "victor's festal LAUREL" in 1 Thessalonians 2:19. Since 1 Thessalonians 2 was the proper cross-reference, all Kenneth Wuest has done is destroy the connection between the verses. The word ["crown"]{.class_4096} in the [King James]{.class_4095} maintains the connection. Phenomenon No. 8.

Verse 2 is self-explanatory. The two women are members of the local church. They have been at odds with each other (a very common thing in any local church) and Paul admonishes them to patch things up. He evidently had gotten news about their contention from a messenger or letter from the church.

Dr. Mounce ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}) is anxious to tell us that the spelling of ["Euodias"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_402} is wrong and should be "Euodia." This is rather odd, since all Greek manuscripts read "Euodian." (Of course, this is due to the accusative ending on the singular.) Mounce tells us that the [AV]{.class_4095} word is a man's name and therefore must be altered to "Euodia" to refer to a woman. Nor is Mounce the first one to get upset about this passage in the Bible. It seems that Hitzig, Schmiedel, Winer, Alford, Davidson, DeWette, Ellicott, and Kenneth Wuest also object to the spelling.

But the case is quite typical of the dogmatic and bigoted fiats laid down by dead-orthodox apostates. In spite of the fact that six Christian bishops were named ["Euodias"]{.class_4095} ([Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, Vol. III, pg. 30), we are told "it should read as the [RV]{.class_4095}," or "read as the [ASV]{.class_4095}," or "the [RSV ]{.class_4095}is better," or "not EUODIAS but Euodia."

Don't you think it rather odd that the modern editors, revisers, and commentators never heard of a Nineteenth- (or Twentieth-) Century man named "Francis" or "Jeanne"? Or a woman called "Jackie" or "Dean" or "Bobbie"? [How about "a boy named SUE"!]{.class_4095} Isn't it peculiar how the common "man on the street" has access to information forbidden to Wuest, Hitzig, Lightfoot, Davidson, Lipsius, DeWette, Thieme, Trench, Vincent, and Meyer?

Euodias and Syntyche are "conversation pieces" in any local church. The remarks of Schintz ([Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 464) on the subject are naive in the extreme. Williams' remark that both of them were [preachers ]{.class_4095}(Commentary, pg. 933) is a little farfetched, and the ["deaconess"]{.class_4095} of the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} (Vol. 20, pg. 155) is about as Scriptural as Romans 16:1 in the [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095} (1958)[, ]{.class_4095}Phillips (1958), and the [NEB ]{.class_4095}(1961). All of these apostate corruptions ordain women to hold office in the local church. Serious students of 1 Timothy 3 know this is just Catholic nonsense.

["With CLEMENT ALSO, and with other my fellowlabourers, whose names are in the book of life"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). This is supposed to be a reference to the apostate Arminian of Rome who wrote "First and Second Clement"---the New Testament apocryphal books recommended by Origen and found (in part) in the corrupt [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}manuscript. However, Origen is the author of this []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_403}fable (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown Commentary, pg. 1311), and like most of Origen's fables ("Letter to Aristeas," etc.), the whole story smacks of "continued revelation through the Holy See of the Apostle Peter, etc." (Once you've met Rome, you recognize her anywhere.)

(The Epistles of Clement are not quite as ridiculous as "The Shepherd of Hermas" or "The Epistle to Barnabas" (more literature found in [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}!), but you could learn more by reading Tatian's [Diatesseron]{.class_4095} or even [Moby Dick]{.class_4095}.)

Since the ["yokefellow"]{.class_4096} of the verse could not be identified (Bullinger, pg. 1779), he is called "SYZYGOS" by the [Wycliffe ]{.class_4095}commentator, along with Westcott and Hort and Rev. Kennedy. He is probably Epaphroditus (see 2:25) or Silas (Acts 15:40, 16:19). I do not believe that "Syzygos" (Gk., ["Suzuge"]{.class_4095}) was very much worried about his own name, for Westcott and Hort and Jerome have had to capitalize the first letter, which is NOT capitalized in any Greek edition on the market. Both Euodias and Syntyche [are capitalized]{.class_4095} in Kurt Aland's text and Nestle's. The reading "Syzgos" or "Syzygos" (proper name) is found only in the margin of Westcott and Hort, Jerome, and a liberal Bible published at Zurich in 1942.

["Whose names are in the book of life"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). This famous book seems to be one of several ["books"]{.class_4096} that are opened in Daniel 7:10 and Revelation 20:12.

1\. We have the ["book which thou hast written"]{.class_4096} (Exod. 32:32), mentioned by Moses before Israel entered the promised land.

2\. Job desired a book to be written (Job 19:23), before Moses was born.

3\. One book is settled in Heaven and abides forever (Matt. 24:35).

4\. There is a book which God wrote for those who ["thought upon his name"]{.class_4096} (Mal. 3:16).

5\. There is a book which has the names of those ["written in the earth"]{.class_4096} (Jer. 17:13) in contrast to those whose ["names are written in heaven"]{.class_4096} (Luke 10:20).

6\. There is a book of life which [determines ]{.class_4095}the destinies of men (Rev. 20:12--15).

7\. Somebody can risk the possibility of having his name removed from this book (Rev. 3:5).

8\. A man can lose ["his part out of the book of life,"]{.class_4096} even if his name is not removed (Rev. 22:19).

We seem to be dealing with several books. There is the Book of Life---the Bible---called by Chrysostom, "THE BOOK" (Gk.: ["To Biblion!"]{.class_4095})! It has hundreds of names and numbers in it which indicate how many people there are having the same name (see Num. 1--10; 1 Chron. 1--10, etc.). All of these Hebrew names have meanings which match the meanings of Spanish, French, English, Japanese, German, Chinese, and Russian names. Each saved person has ["a new name"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 2:17) in Glory, and "in Glory" the words are HEBREW (Acts 26:14; Rev. 19:3). [Wouldn't you be a fool to mess with those names in Numbers and Chronicles if God's word is "settled forever in heaven"]{.class_4095} (Psa. 119:89)?

Then there is a book in which WORKS are written (Rev. 20:12), and there seems to be an Old Testament book (Mal. 3:16) and a New Testament book (Phil. 4:3). So there is a possibility that the ["books"]{.class_4096} are 66 in number (!) and include the NAMES and the WORKS (Luke 10:20; Dan. 12:1; Exod. 32:32; Psa. 69:28). God is a "bookkeeper," and at the end of the day, every human being on this earth must bow his head and say with Pilate:[ "What I have written, I have written"]{.class_4096} (John 19:22)!

"The moving hand having writ moves on, nor all thy piety nor all thy wit can draw it back to cancel half a line of it" (Omar Khayyam)!

When Hendley ("Invictus") writes about his indifference toward the "scroll" charged with "punishment," he writes as a [fool]{.class_4095}, not as a brave man. "Hell is not for heroes," as someone has wisely said. When the "books are opened," men like H. L. Menken and Harry Truman are going to be surprised to find that apologies are not in order, repentance and restitution are not in order, reform and emendation are not in order, and confession and belief are no longer effective even though required (see Phil. 2:9--10). All that is left after the "inventory" is the quality of punishment to be received; the [length ]{.class_4095}of the punishment has already been determined (Matt. 25:41; Rev. 14:11).

["Those women which laboured with me in the gospel"]{.class_4096} []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_405}(vs. 4). Lydia was the first "worker" in this church (Acts 16:14--15), and of course, there were others. Women seem to have a finer appreciation of the benefits of the Gospel (Luke 23:49, 55) than men do. It was a woman who first announced the coming of Christ in Jerusalem (Luke 2:38), and women were the first to believe in the resurrection of Christ (Luke 24:9--10). By virtue of their nature and constitution, women feel more helpless and dependent than men, so the chances are that they will outnumber the men when the trumpet sounds and the "high calling" is given (1 Thess. 4:16). Church workers like Martha who are "cumbered about with much serving" are not as helpful as the great woman of Shunem (2 Kings 4:8--10) nor as spiritual as Mary (Luke 10:42), but for a single man devoted to the Gospel 365 days a year (Paul), a button-sewer, patch-mender, meal-server, dishwasher, gift-sender, or letter-writer is a real blessing.

Euodias and Syntyche (vs. 2) are always there to argue about who is really "laboring in the Gospel," but every pastor is acquainted with them. Euodias thinks that Syntyche's prayers are "in the flesh." Syntyche thinks that Euodias is "using" the Sunday School class or the church for her own ends. Euodias thinks that Syntyche's skirt is much too short, and Syntyche knows what only Euodias' hairdresser is supposed to know, etc.

["Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4). Paul is back on this theme song; his prison bars can't cut out the sunlight. "Two men looked out through prison bars: one saw mud and the other saw stars." Like the man said: "When God gives you lemon juice for a diet, make lemonade out of it."

The old English ["alway"]{.class_4096} is superior to "always," as both meanings are found in the word---"in every kind of way" and "at all times." This is quite typical of the "King's English," and the lack of flexibility of modern English (since 1900) is one of the reasons why the Holy Spirit is not particularly interested in [Living Letters]{.class_4095} or "Dead Epistles." The real Bible believer should never forget that the [sales and acceptance]{.class_4095} of the "new translations" are all coming from professing Christians and dead-orthodox apostates. The [unsaved masses]{.class_4095} of humanity who used to believe in the word of God (whether they obeyed it or not), no longer believe there is any such book. The []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_406}"sales" of the last thirty "new translations" mean nothing to them at all; nor do they have any effect on their lives, beliefs, churches, works, thoughts, goals, or convictions.

["Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). The dead-orthodox apostates alter the word ["moderation"]{.class_4096} to bring it into line with Aristotle's opinion about the word (Rendall, pg. 466).

1\. [Living Letters]{.class_4095}---["unselfish."]{.class_4095}

2\. [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095}---["your considerateness."]{.class_4095}

3\. [Good News]{.class_4095}---["gentle attitude."]{.class_4095}

4\. [RV, ASV, New ASV]{.class_4095}, and [RSV]{.class_4095}---["forbearance.]{.class_4095}"

5\. [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}---"gentleness."

6\. [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}---"yieldingness."

7\. Lipsius---"friendly manner."

8\. Schnedermann---"mildness."

There is about as much unanimity on the subject as there is on the Vietnam foreign policy (1961--1975).

We shall dispense with the Aristotelian theories of these impractical critics and stick to the text.

1\. Be moderate in using the world (1 Cor. 7:31).

2\. Be moderate when you are deeply sorrowful (1 Thess. 4:13).

3\. Be moderate when rejoicing in anything but the Lord (1 Cor. 7:29--30).

4\. Be moderate in personal habits (1 Cor. 9:25).

Did you notice that when Kennedy, Mounce ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}), and Williams (Commentary, pg. 934) got through restoring Aristotle's interpretation, that none of them could give another verse of Scripture to [illustrate, explain, or apply it?!]{.class_4095} This is the modern approach of Conservative scholars: i.e., get rid of the Bible by any means possible. By altering the word, [every Biblical truth connected with it]{.class_4095} was obliterated, and the dirty rascal who changed it could not even make [an application of his own after he changed it!]{.class_4095} Phenomenon No. 9.

["The Lord is at hand"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). Observe how the highly accurate and scientific text of the infallible [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible corrects all of the Greek texts on the word ["moderation."]{.class_4096} You see, every reference given above (under ["moderation"]{.class_4096}) has the [Second Coming of Christ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_407} in the immediate context! First Corinthians 7:31 was on the passing of this world system, 1 Thessalonians 4:13 was on the Rapture, 1 Corinthians 7:29 was on the time being short, and 1 Corinthians 9:25 was dealing with rewards at the Judgment Seat of Christ! Moral: any alteration of the word ["moderation"]{.class_4096} to [any other word]{.class_4095} destroys the entire verse (vs. 5), for ["The Lord is at hand"]{.class_4096} is part of the verse! Since the Lord is at the point of returning and is "on hand" to return at any time, the word "[moderation]{.class_4096}" is the ONLY word which can properly describe the Christian's conduct where it is related to worldly sorrows, joys, and relationships.

["Be careful for nothing"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). The "King's English" is, again, better than the modern "anxious" inserted by the [RV, ASV, New ASV, RSV, New RSV]{.class_4095}, New Duz, New Lux, etc. The word is a compound word meaning "full of care" ("[careFUL]{.class_4096}"), and this is what is cautioned against by the apostle. (Observe the recurrence of the word in the context---vs. 10.) To tell the truth (which is quite a stunt for a critic of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611), the Christian SHOULD BE "anxious" about some things. The alteration of the word to "anxious" (any and all commentators of any profession) is not merely a confounding of English words, it is LYING. A Christian who is not anxious to see Jesus Christ return and anxious to see people trust Him as Saviour and anxious to please God in all things is a BACKSLIDDEN Christian. The "care" that one takes with things (1 Cor. 7:32) is what is forbidden. Although one should be "anxious" to please God, one should never be "full of care" (["careful"]{.class_4096}), for this plainly indicates ["worry."]{.class_4095} Notice that the Philippians were "full of care" and [worried ]{.class_4095}about Paul's state (vs. 10), and the very words which Paul writes in the passage (vs. 6, 11--12, 17) tells them [NOT to worry.]{.class_4095}

["But in every thing by prayer\...supplication\...thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). God's "cure for anxious care" is constant communication with God (1 Pet. 5:7). The Christian is to talk everything "over" with his Lord (1 Thess. 5:17; James 5:13) and is to commune of "all that is in his heart" (1 Kings 10:2). The ["prayer"]{.class_4096} is the worshipping of God by approaching Him in adoration and praise, and the "[supplication]{.class_4096}" is the begging and beseeching for favors and needs from His hand. All of this is to be done with ["thanksgiving"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_408} (1 Thess. 5:18).

Even where the requests are not all granted, the ["peace of God, which passeth all understanding" ]{.class_4096}will keep the Christian's heart and mind ["through Christ Jesus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7). Many times, this peace is BETTER than "[understanding,]{.class_4096}" and when prayers go unanswered, this "[peace]{.class_4096}" is designed to keep the heart and mind from being "full of care" (see vs. 6).
