The English text stands in infallible perfection without an error of any kind in it, and if you had the "originals," you could do no more with them than the pitiful expositors of the [Cross Reference Bible]{.class_4095} or the [Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095} have done with the passage.

After a head-on collision of two passenger trains, two men extracted a colored Pullman porter from a pile of twisted metal and broken glass. He was astonished to see the ambulances, fire trucks, police cars, and the crowd when he emerged to daylight. "Didn't you know you were in a wreck?" said one of the rescuers. "No sah, Boss," the dark one replied. "I did feel sumpin kinda jolty, but ah thought dey was jes puttin on de dinah!" ["Be careful for nothing."]{.class_4096}

Once in an art exhibit, the judges gave an award to the artist who could portray the term "peace" best in painting. The winning picture showed a bird's nest on the thin limb of a tree which hung out over a roaring cataract. Silhouetted against the tons of pouring water and foam was a robin, head tilted back, mouth open, obviously in the midst of a concerto for whistlers. ["And the PEACE OF GOD\...shall KEEP YOUR HEARTS AND MINDS"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7).

4:8 Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are honest, whatsoever things are just, whatsoever things are pure, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report; if there be any virtue, and if there be any praise, think on these things.

9 Those things, which ye have both learned, and received, and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.

10 But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly, that now at the last your care of me hath flourished again; wherein ye were also careful, but ye lacked opportunity.

11 Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content.

12 I know both how to be abased, and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need.

13 I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.

14 Notwithstanding ye have well done, that ye did communicate with my affliction.

15 Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving, but ye only.

16 For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity.

17 Not because I desire a gift: but I desire fruit that may abound to your account.

18 But I have all, and abound: I am full, having received of Epaphroditus the things which were sent from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, wellpleasing to God.

19 But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.

Norman Vincent Peale's philosophy now shows up, but the reader should observe that the "positive thinking" is only recommended for a born-again believer, not an unsaved man. The positive thinking is not coupled with a raise in salary, meeting new people, selling your personality, or attaining your goals; it is connected with street preaching, getting thrown in jail, and getting your head cut off! (["Those things, which ye have\...seen in me, DO\..."]{.class_4096} vs. 9.) Now, I'll bet you won't find that in any book that Peale, Cronin, Douglas, Oursler, or Fosdick ever wrote!

Paul can "practice what he preaches," and "[the]{.class_4096} [God of peace]{.class_4096}" (vs. 9) is obviously with him (see vss. 11--12). If he can "think beautiful thoughts" in jail, the Philippians can think them in their []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_410}homes.

The "things that are true" are naturally unnoticed by all of the modern commentators, since their main purpose was to destroy the authority of the word. But! ["Thy word is truth"]{.class_4096} (John 17:17) and ["every one of thy righteous judgments endureth for ever"]{.class_4096} (Psa. 119:160) for ["thy word is true from the beginning" ]{.class_4096}(Psa. 119:160). When Paul told the Philippians to ["think on THESE things,"]{.class_4096} no commentator since 1880 dreamed that he was telling them to think about the word of God! You see, the destructive critics of the Reformation Bible never LOVED the word when they devoted their lives to studying and analyzing it; they merely got "entangled" with the word and were unable to get loose!

The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} makes the verse read as Barth and Brunner would retranslate it (Vol. 20, pg. 157). The [Wycliffe]{.class_4095} commentator passes the buck by talking about the nature of "reality." Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown alter the word to "sincere." Bullinger runs you back into Appendix No. 175, and Dummelow limits the word to mean "honorable" character.

How did the commentators miss Revelation 19:9, 11, 21:5, 22:6; John 19:35; 2 Samuel 7:28; Psalms 33:4, 119:160; and 2 Thessalonians 2:13? You would think that if a man devoted his life to commenting on 31,102 verses of truth that he would understand what a Christian said when he told him to "think on the things that were TRUE"! How does one explain this fantastic incongruity between a profession of "seeking the truth" and "loving the truth" and a total inability to recognize it [while one is dealing with it?!]{.class_4095}

The [true things ]{.class_4095}are the eternal things in Heaven (Col. 3:1--2), the things that are invisible (2 Cor. 4:18), and the living words of the living God (John 6:63). The [things that are honest]{.class_4095} are conversations and dealings that are open and "above board" and men and women who are frank and sincere in their search for truth. The [things that are just]{.class_4095} are the judgments of God (Psa. 9:16, 10:5) and the things that are lawful for man. The [things that are pure]{.class_4095} are the words of God (Psa. 12:6, 119:140), the Holy Spirit (Eph. 4:30), and "[the wisdom that is from above"]{.class_4096} (James 3:17). The [things that are lovely]{.class_4095} are things that God has created (Psa. 19:1) and the things that need and deserve love. The [things that are of good report]{.class_4095} (not "THAT which has a good ring," [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_411}) are the spiritual growth of new converts (1 Thess. 1), the maturity of saints (Eph. 4:13--14), the salvation of souls (John 4:27--38), the answers to prayer (Phil. 1:3--10), and the fulfillment of prophecy. If there is ["]{.class_4095}[any virtue"]{.class_4096} in thinking about these things or anything in them for which a Christian can praise God (vs. 8), then ["think on these things"]{.class_4096} (vs. 8).

Since all of the new translations have gotten rid of our ["virtue"]{.class_4096} (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, Prov. 31:10) in both Testaments, the word here has been turned into "[excellence]{.class_4095}" ([RSV]{.class_4095}), "[good]{.class_4095}" ([Good News]{.class_4095}), "[admirable]{.class_4095}" ([NEB]{.class_4095}), and Phillips[ ]{.class_4095}and [Living Letters]{.class_4095} have paraphrased the verse so far out of its original wording that it is impossible even to tell which words were mutilated, which ones were added, and which ones were omitted. Kennedy, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, and the [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095} all respect Lightfoot's pagan speculation that "heathen virtue" is the "[virtue]{.class_4096}" of the text. The idea we are to get here, according to Lightfoot who was one of the major destructive critics on the [RV ]{.class_4095}committee (1881--1885) is: "Whatever value may reside in [your old heathen conception of virtue]{.class_4095}, whatever consideration is [due to the praise of MEN\..."!]{.class_4095} But this is just Aristotle, Socrates, Plato, Anaximander, Herodotus, Sophocles, Empedocles, and Anaximenes all over again, with a professing Christian trying to turn them into "evangelists." Lightfoot's "idea" is about as Biblical as Astruc's "due" or Wellhausen's "theory."

Verse 9 is self-explanatory. We have commented on verse 10 under 2:30 and 2 Corinthians 8:1--2.

Verse 11 is one of those remarkable statements like verses 13 and 19. If Paul could truly be "[content]{.class_4096}" in "any state," he had attained a spiritual maturity (see 1:18) so close to perfection that he was just about half way home to Glory without leaving the body (2 Cor. 12:2--4). ["Whatsoever state"]{.class_4096} includes not only Alabama, Michigan, New York, and California, but the state of hunger, thirst, nakedness, and want described in 2 Corinthians 11:23--33. A man who is "happy on the rack" has got the problem whipped. If intensive prolonged pain cannot kill the rejoicing of verse 4, there is nothing more that the Devil can do with a man. He knocked the shout out of Job's mouth, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_412}and even though he could not get rid of the man's trust in God, he at least got rid of his "praise and thanksgiving." But Satan could never even do this to Paul, and God knows he tried everything (2 Cor. 11:13--30) short of roasting him over a slow fire. When Paul says ["all things"]{.class_4096} in verse 13, he has just mentioned ["abased \... hungry \... suffer \..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). Another list he gives mentions [weariness \... painfulness \... hunger \... thirst \... fastings \... cold \... nakedness \... offended \... infirmities"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 11:27--30), and another list reads ["tribulation \... distress \... persecution \... famine \... nakedness \... peril \... sword"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:35). If you can't quench praise and thanksgiving with those items, what in the world can you do with the culprit but KILL him?

Not even American Christians are "content" with "[food and raiment]{.class_4096}" (although they were commanded to be---1 Tim. 6:8). What a universal grumble would go up if ["weariness, painfulness, hunger, thirst, fasting, cold, nakedness, tribulation, persecution, distress, famine, and THE SWORD"]{.class_4095} were added to their problems! Success is getting what you want; happiness is WANTING what you GET. Paul was a happy failure. He wanted to go home (Phil. 1:23), but he couldn't; yet he wanted what he got (text), so he was a happy failure.

["I can do ALL THINGS THROUGH CHRIST which strengtheneth me"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13). After three centuries of bellyaching about the word ["which"]{.class_4096} in the text, the "highly scientific" translators of the "more accurate" editions based on "better manuscripts" simply knock Jesus Christ clean out of the verse and attribute the SOURCE of strength to an unidentified "whom" of "him" (see comments under 3:3).

The [ASV ]{.class_4095}knocks ["Christ"]{.class_4096} out and mumbles "in him," without indicating WHO the "him" is (and there is certainly no immediate reference within two verses either side of the verse). The [New ASV]{.class_4095}, recommended by the reactionary faculty at Bob Jones University, follows the Jesuit, Dark-Age Bible of 1582; and the same reading is found in the Communist [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946).

As a matter of fact, 99 percent of the "leading scholars" adopt the Roman Catholic reading of 1582 in one form or another. God gave the "conservatives" 59 years (between 1901 and 1960) to forsake this God-forsaken piece of trash, but they went right ahead and copied it down again. The omission of the word "[Christ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_413}" comes, as most omissions do, from the depraved faculty at that great godless sink of spiritual corruption---Alexandria, Africa.

Kurt Aland refuses even to discuss the manuscript evidence, although the vast majority of manuscripts give Christ the honor due Him. But Kennedy (pg. 470) tells us that the [King James]{.class_4095} reading is found in all three families of manuscripts---Hesychian, Byzantine, and Western---so we know that Aland and Nestle "fudged" a little. Nestle deceived us when he told us that we could "infer" from the absence of "[Aleph]{.class_4095}," "[D]{.class_4095}," and "[B]{.class_4095}" that they read as the text, for this is not true. ["D" and "Aleph" both read with the King James,]{.class_4095} but they were not listed in the critical apparatus of Nestle (pg. 508) because Nestle (as Westcott and Hort) was a deeply prejudiced bigot when it came to the German Bible of the Reformation. He led you to believe that only the [Receptus]{.class_4095} (Syrian or Byzantine family) had the reading. [ALL THREE FAMILIES have the reading, plus Athanasius, Chrysostom, and Cyprian.]{.class_4095}

All of the amateurs (1880--1980) have received their orders from the African church in Rome, so all say: "The best manuscripts omit ['Christ.']{.class_4096}" This, as always, simply means: "The forged spurious [Vaticanus]{.class_4095}, in the Vatican, containing the Apocrypha as part of the Old Testament, has attacked Jesus Christ again." (To borrow one of the clichés of the dead-orthodox apostates, we may say: "This is the true meaning of the original which is obscured in the books written by Alford, Clarke, Lange, Dummelow, Mounce, Caffin, Lipsius, Rendall, Phillips, Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Williams, Young, Lamsa, Fenton, Kennedy, Lightfoot, Meyer, Wordsworth, Schnedermann, Ellicott, Luken, Bengel, Trench, Vincent, Lumby, and DeWette.")

I can give up cigarettes ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096} I can live one day at a time ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096} I can overcome a bad habit ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096} I can finish my work without help or encouragement ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096} I can go on living when loved ones and friends are gone or have turned against me ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096} I can work a fourteen-hour day ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_414} I can take the ridicule and opposition of "respectable Christianity" and withstand the storms of criticism and slander ["through Christ which strengtheneth me."]{.class_4096}

Isn't it amazing how much light the King's English puts on "the best manuscripts"?

The alteration of ["Christ"]{.class_4096} to "in him," found in the Vatican manuscript, is a startling witness to the fact that the same scribe who changed it objected to the Deity of Christ, [for the same thing was done in 1 Timothy 3:16,]{.class_4095} where the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, recommended by Bob Jones University, changed ["God"]{.class_4096} to "he who." Mature Christians (see Eph. 3:13--14 and remarks) can analyze Biblical handwriting easily. It is the same rascal in both places, and he is the rascal who removed Christ's name or title from 1 Corinthians 16:22; Ephesians 3:9; Colossians 1:2; 1 Thessalonians 1:1; and Matthew 8:29. If a manuscript omits ["Christ,"]{.class_4096} catalog it with "The Yellow Submarine."

Verses 14--18 are self-explanatory and deal with Paul's past ministry and the help he received from the Philippians. (See Gal. 6:6 for "[communicate]{.class_4096}.") ["The beginning of the gospel"]{.class_4096} refers to Acts 16 when he first preached to them. The Philippians helped him when he left, although none of the other groups did (see Acts 16:40; 2 Cor. 11:8--9). Scriptural comments on verse 18 may be found in Ephesians 5:2 and Hebrews 13:16. "[Spiritual sacrifices]{.class_4096}" are the order for the Christian (1 Pet 2:5), and the literal "sacrifice of the Mass" is, as the 31st Article (of the "Thirty-nine Articles") has stated, "A blasphemous fable and a dangerous deceit."

["But my God shall supply ALL YOUR NEED according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 19).

This is one of the great "prayer promises" of the Bible, and it ranks alongside Romans 8:28; Philippians 4:13; 1 Corinthians 1:7--8; 2 Corinthians 2:14 and 9:8.

Since the verse has been prayed over, wept over, and rejoiced over for three centuries, you might know that the "modern editors" could not allow it to stand as the blessing it has been. Not one major reviser or commentator since 1700 will allow the verse to stand as it has been authorized, used, and blessed by the Holy Spirit through the greatest period of missionary work, evangelism, soulwinning, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_415}and Bible teaching the earth has ever seen.

The "Big Four"---[RV, RSV, ASV]{.class_4095}, and [New ASV]{.class_4095}---all change ["by"]{.class_4096} to "[in]{.class_4095}," then alter ["all"]{.class_4096} to "[every]{.class_4095}" and ["your need"]{.class_4096} to "[every need of YOURS]{.class_4095}." [Anything but believe the text.]{.class_4095}

Dummelow "fills up the need" instead of supplying it. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} tells us that the verse should not read ["by"]{.class_4096}; it should be "in" Christ Jesus (ditto Bullinger). Wuest will "[satisfy to the full]{.class_4095}" instead of ["supply."]{.class_4096} [Good News]{.class_4095} has no ["glory."]{.class_4096} The [NEB ]{.class_4095}supplies your "[wants]{.class_4095}" instead of your ["need"]{.class_4096} (!). Phillips has no ["glory,"]{.class_4096} [Living Letters]{.class_4095} doesn't pay any attention to any Greek text, and the [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095} will "[liberally supply]{.class_4095}" instead of ["supply]{.class_4096}." Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown think that you get "every need" supplied because you are "in Christ," but [Living Letters]{.class_4095} corrects Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, and says it is because of what Christ DID for you, while the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} makes "[glory]{.class_4096}" an adjective or an adverb instead of a PLACE. But as always, the text preserved by the Holy Spirit is much better than the one preserved by the destructive critics of the Reformation Bible.

A man NEEDS four things (and "wants" is not a part of the discussion):

1\. He needs Salvation (Eph. 2:5).

2\. He needs Comfort (Gen. 50:20).

3\. He needs Assurance (1 Cor. 15:58).

4\. He needs Wisdom (Dan. 2:28).

God promises to supply NEED, not "greed." Those who have suffered NEED---Paul, Richard Wurmbrand, Ian Paisley, Haralan Popov, Huss, Luther, Hubmaier, Bunyan, the Huguenots, the Waldensians, and the Albigenses---know a great deal more about the [King James]{.class_4095} reading than the impractical, inconsistent, unrealistic, subjective, biased critics of the [ASV ]{.class_4095}committee or the [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095} committee.

A man can get by without HEALTH (McCheyne, Brainerd, Martha Snell Nicholson), a man can get by without WEALTH (Wesley, Paul, John Bunyan), a man can get by without EDUCATION (Billy Bray, Sammy Morris, John Jasper), and a man can get by without SOCIAL STANDING (Billy Sunday, Sam Jones, Mel Trotter, and Pappy Reveal); but a man cannot get by without Jesus Christ (salvation), []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_416}assurance of salvation, the comfort of the Holy Ghost in times of sorrow and trouble, and divine wisdom to know what to do at the right time in the right way. The "essentials" are not the essentials presented, promoted, suggested, or recommended by any leading educator or scientist in the world.

Williams, Mounce ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}), and the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} do not devote the proper space to "comments on the verse." We get a definite impression from studying the length of the comments on the various verses that the primary "burden" of Clarke, Lange, Mounce, Ellicott, Caffin, Williams, and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown is to overthrow the authority of the Bible. Why did the commentators run line after line on Philippians 3:2--3 and then suddenly drop Philippians 4:19 with less than five words per each word in the verse? Have our commentating "brethren" ever been in NEED?

They seem to be kin to the kind of people who would select a millionaire for the "Commander in Chief" of the American Army. Why would anyone in their right mind want a man to be President of a country \$200,000,000,000 in debt when the man never had to sweat out a gas bill or a light bill? Conversely, how could any commentator give you a "nugget" from Philippians 4:19 if he has not been sued in court, forced into bankruptcy, declared incurable, threatened with foreclosure, tortured for his faith, or deserted in an hour of need? Philippians 4:19 is not written for pseudo-sophisticated asses sitting around a table arguing about "tenses" and "iota subscriptum"; it is written for [Bible-believing Christians ]{.class_4095}who need something infallible and absolute on which to rest.

If you NEED a revival, God will give you one. If you NEED to pay your bills, God will supply the money. If you NEED to be healed, you will get well. If you NEED to go home to Glory, you will drop dead in your tracks. If you NEED to be sick, no healer on this earth can pray you out of your infirmity. If you NEED to be humbled, the Lord will do it. If you NEED comfort and encouragement, the Lord will supply it. If you NEED pain, the Lord will send it.

"Wants" ([NEB]{.class_4095}, 1961) are completely out of the question. Many a child of God has burned at the stake or roasted on the coals (see [Foxe's Book of Martyrs]{.class_4095}) because God was supplying his "[NEED]{.class_4096}." But []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_417}if you think most Christians "WANT" to be crucified, strangled, wracked, roasted, and sliced, you've spent too much time reading [Man Against Himself]{.class_4095} (Karl Menninger) or some similar superstitious publication ([The Varieties of Religious Experiences]{.class_4095}, William James) put out by men who had about as much spiritual understanding as John Dewey and Bertrand Russell.

When children are two to three years old, they say, "Daddy, I WANT some ice cream!" But somewhere between three and five, they learn to "revise the original" and say, "Daddy, I NEED some ice cream!!" A lot of us Christians try this line when dealing with our Father, don't we? Don't we?! (If you can get 30 percent of your congregation to "amen" that, you have a spiritual congregation these days!)

4:20 Now unto God and our Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

21 Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.

22 All the saints salute you, chiefly they that are of Caesar's household.

23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

All is clear in the closing admonitions and benediction. The believers are ["saints"]{.class_4096} (see Eph. 1:1 and comments), and they ["salute"]{.class_4096} each other with a greeting of some kind or other. When present, this is often ["an holy kiss"]{.class_4096} (1 Thess. 5:26); when absent, it is merely a verbal greeting passed on by word of mouth (Phil. 4:22) or letter (1 Thess. 5:27).

["They that are OF CAESAR'S HOUSEHOLD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 22) shows that Paul's testimony has reached out through all the PALACE (see comments under 1:13), not "the praetorian hall," etc. The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} now contradicts itself (pg. 160) by admitting that the ["palace"]{.class_4096} was affected, but the [Wycliffe]{.class_4095} commentator (Dr. Mounce) remains adamant and announces boldly, bravely, and stubbornly (with no support of any kind for his statement, from any source) that the[ "household"]{.class_4096} was not the emperor's family, "as formerly thought" (!!), but only his [employees]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ2913.xhtml_page_418}. (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown follow the same line, and [Good News]{.class_4095} and [Living Letters]{.class_4095} contradict themselves by saying "[palace]{.class_4095}" where they just finished wiping the ["palace"]{.class_4096} out of 1:13. Very typical. Phenomenon No. 8.)

For verse 23, see Galatians 6:18 and comments. Williams has some interesting remarks on verse 19, and he notes that the word [sin]{.class_4095} does not occur one time in the epistle.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3029.xhtml}

# Colossians {.heading_86}

## Introduction {#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3029.xhtml_Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ4089 .heading_1314}

The book of Colossians is by far the most interesting of the Pauline Epistles for this age. Although no major "commentator" from Origen to English grasped the prophetic significance of the epistle, the ordinary Bible believer will have very little trouble in seeing the truth of the matter.

1\. John wrote to seven churches.

2\. Paul wrote to seven churches.

3\. John's first church was [Ephesus.]{.class_4095}

4\. There is an epistle to[ the Ephesians.]{.class_4101}

5\. John's seventh church was [Laodicea.]{.class_4095}

6\. The words ["Laodicea"]{.class_4096} or ["Laodiceans" ]{.class_4096}occur five times in Colossians.

The book of Colossians is plainly applicable to the Twentieth-Century church of 1900--2000, since Laodicea is the last church on earth preceding the Rapture. Its content and subject matter, then, is crucial for the believer in 2010 who desires the truth, and its remarks are pointed and aimed---as no other epistle in the Bible is---directly at the Twentieth- and Twenty-first Century "Christian."

If this is not clear to the reader, it will become abundantly clear when he engages to digest Colossians 2:6--14. In Colossians 2, the Holy Spirit finally backs the dead-orthodox apostates up to the edge of the cliff, strips them naked for the centuries to gaze upon, and then slays them in one mighty blow (1 Kings 18:40) which they are unable to delay (1 Sam. 31:3--4) or deflect (Judg. 3:22).

William's Introduction (Commentary, pg. 935) has some good spiritual material about the general nature of the book. The Introductions by E. E. Ellis ([Wycliffe]{.class_4095}), Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, and Dr. Peake are quite similar to each other.

Colosse was a city of Phrygia in western Asia Minor on the banks of a river called the "Lycus." It was a mighty metropolis in the days of the kings of Persia, but declined somewhat before the Christian era. By the First Century, it had been eclipsed by LAODICEA (see []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3029.xhtml_page_420}remarks above) which lay eleven miles south of Colosse. The Talmud and the writings of Josephus indicate that a good number of Jews populated the region, having been brought there before the time of Christ. But the church there was mainly composed of Gentiles (2:13). Paul had not founded this church (2:1), but his convert Epaphras (4:12) had probably evangelized the area (see Acts 19:10; Col. 1:6--7, 4:12--13).

Throughout the epistle, one finds warnings aimed at four contemporary problems, which were THE problems of the revision committees of 1881, 1901, 1946, 1952, 1963 (and the next ten committees).

1\. Ebionites: people who believed in "following the example" or "imitating the lowly Galilean" (see Eph. 5:1 and comments). They loved to talk about "the historical Christ" as the "real revolutionary" in contrast with the "distorted picture" of Christ given by Peter, James, John, Matthew, Mark, Luke, and Paul. [Three of Origen's fellow-translators were Ebionites:]{.class_4095} Symmachus, Theodotian, and Aquilla. "Jesus Christ Super Star" (1971) is an Ebionite production.

2\. Essenes (described in detail by Philo and Josephus 1,800 years before "the Dead Sea Scrolls" were unearthed): people who tried to work their way to Heaven by "mortifying the deeds of the body" and "putting the flesh to death" by self will. They lived lives of extreme asceticism and sometimes mutilated themselves, [as Origen (A.D. 184--254) did,]{.class_4095} to prove their "other-worldliness."

3\. Judaizers: as those of Galatians 2--4, who sought to bring the Gentile convert back under Exodus, Leviticus, and Deuteronomy.

4\. Philosophers: who came from Alexandria, Egypt ([Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_4095} Vol. 20, pg. vii, Introduction to Colossians), and brought with them the teachings of Aristotle, Plato, Zeno, Socrates, Sophocles, Wuest, Euripedes, Demosthenes, Thieme, Archimedes, Aristophanes, A. T. Robertson, Simonides, Solon, Trench, Alford, Ellicott, Acreon, Pythagoras, Dummelow, Dr. Custer, Dr. Neal, Archilocus, and the [New American Standard Version.]{.class_4095}

The epistle was written between A.D. 62 and 64. It has 4 chapters, 95 verses, and 1979 words. The word [Colossians]{.class_4095} means (fittingly) ["the punishments."]{.class_4095}

Rev. Peake devotes seven pages to a discussion of "angels." This []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3029.xhtml_page_421}peculiar angel-fixation pops up in nearly all of the lengthy commentaries, but all of the writers seem to think that the problems concerning it are limited to the remote past where a combination of Gnosticism and apostate Judaism was at work. Notice that Dummelow confines the problem to "the doctrine of angels current in\...St. Paul's time" (Commentary, pg. 979). This "common view" is attributed to IMAGINATION by Brother Dummelow (Ibid.), and on this foundation, Dummelow---as 95 percent of the commentators---alters ["rudiments of the world"]{.class_4096} (Col. 2:8) to "elemental spirits" (see the same horseplay in Gal. 4:3). Since none of the "leading scholars" from 1880 to 1980 even knew what an angel was (see comments under Gen. 6:1--4 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis,]{.class_4095} 1970), it is rather ridiculous for them to make any notes on the subject, let alone several pages dealing with "angelic" worship.

Even before we get into the text of Colossians, we have a premonition of what is going to happen, for on page 487 of Volume 3 of the [Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, Rev. Peake has already begun covering up the truth with error. The insertion of "so-called" before the word ["philosophy"]{.class_4096} is a dead giveaway to the Bible believer as to what is going to take place when the revisers go to work on the Colossian epistle.

The Colossian epistle mounts an attack on Conservative scholarship and the Roman Catholic Church, so we should not be unnerved to find at Colossians 2:8 that every Fundamental, Premillennial commentator in the world has refused to abide by ANY Greek text in Colossians and has manufactured his own private text to divert the reader from the truth. At Colossians 2:8, no critic of the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 is able to conceal his besetting sin any longer. All he can do is pervert the "original Greek" and reject all the manuscript evidence at his disposal. Colossians is proof that in a sure-enough knock-down, drag-out case, where "push comes to shove," no critic of the [AV]{.class_4095} is really sincere in his comments, attitude, or writings. Phenomenon No. 13.

Colossians nearly matches Ephesians, as we have said before, and it differs only in the fact that it is primarily applicable to the church of the "end time." The "Body mystery" of Ephesians is PAST TRUTH for the Body of Christ, but the material in Colossians is PRESENT []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3029.xhtml_page_422}TRUTH for the Body of Christ. The greatest danger, therefore, for the Bible believer, between now and the Rapture, is not "misunderstanding the Body of the mystery" or "failing to magnify the Church of the one Body" or "failing to make known the fellowship of the mystery," etc. (see Eph. 3). The danger in the last thirty years of church history will arise from Bible-rejecting Conservatives and Fundamentalists who love philosophy and tradition, and Roman Catholics who encourage salvation by works and homage to angels and saints. (We may also include the "Church of Christ" and other groups who teach baptismal regeneration, for Colossians 2:20--23 has some pointed remarks to make to this group.)

Our practice in handling the English text will be as always: we shall assume that the [motives for altering it]{.class_4095} are the love of honor, the fear of man, the praise of man, self-righteous pride, intellectual egotism, resentment of authority, and pro-Catholic tendencies. We shall leave it as it stands and correct the "correctors," calling the reader's attention to their numerous errors in transcription, interpretation, exposition, textual emendation, choice of manuscripts and readings, and, above all, their vicious subjective bias against the greatest Bible the world has ever seen.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml}

## Chapter 1 {.heading_116}

1:1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus our brother,

2 To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you.

4 Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which ye have to all the saints,

5 For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel;

6 Which is come unto you, as it is in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the grace of God in truth:

7 As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;

8 Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.

The opening of the epistle is quite similar to the openings found in 1 Thessalonians, Ephesians, and Philippians. Nothing is difficult to understand. ["Timotheus"]{.class_4096} is the "Timothy" of the two epistles of Timothy. For ["the hope which is laid up" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5), see comments under Ephesians 1:18. Verse 6 shows that "fruit bearing" (see Gal. 5:22 and comments) can only follow BELIEF in the word (1 Thess. 2:13; Matt. 13:23). They heard ["the grace of God in truth"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) from Epaphras (vs. 7), who was a ["dear fellowservant"]{.class_4096} and ["a faithful minister"]{.class_4096} (see comments under Phil. 2:25).

Various changes of the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text have been made by the egotistical scribes of Alexandria, London, New York, Chicago, and Rome; but this is to be expected. The Lord Jesus Christ has been shoved out of His preeminent place by the [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095} (1963), recommended by Bob Jones University; but this "hustling" was approved of long ago by the apostate [RV ]{.class_4095}(1885), [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901), and [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946)---the sister translations of the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_424}[New American Standard "Version."]{.class_4095} The English Bibles of 1525, 1535, 1539, 1540, 1560, 1562, 1568, and 1602 have preserved the infallible text of the Holy Spirit.

The [King James]{.class_4095} reading (in vs. 2) is found in ALL families of manuscripts and the majority of manuscripts, yet the corrupt [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and "[D]{.class_4095}" (Western) have omitted the words. The [King James]{.class_4095} text is called "an attempt to [remedy a defect"]{.class_4095} ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20, pg. 2); but of course, the truth is that the reading of the[ New ASV]{.class_4095}, New Duz, and New Morality is an attempt by apostates to [overthrow the truth]{.class_4095}. The [NEB]{.class_4095}, [Living Letters]{.class_4095}, Phillips, Williams, Wuest, Knox, Rotherham, Berkeley, Montgomery, and the Jehovah's Witness Bible all omit ["the Lord Jesus"]{.class_4096} exactly as the Roman Catholics threw it out of their [Confraternity]{.class_4095} edition.

The word ["also"]{.class_4096} (vs. 7) should be "elided" according to Williams, and the words ["who is for you"]{.class_4096} should be "for us" or "on our behalf" according to Lachmann, Lightfoot, Westcott and Hort, Tregelles, Alford, and other "leading" destructive critics of the Holy Bible. This alteration is called "a finer and more fitting sense," but of course, it isn't anything of the kind. Epaphras was in Colosse BEFORE Paul (see Introduction), and he was representing [nobody ]{.class_4095}but Jesus Christ Himself. He was not sent as a "representative" ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}) but was [one of the Colossians]{.class_4095} (4:12) and a servant of [Jesus Christ]{.class_4095} (4:12), not Paul. The emendation, then, which came from "[Aleph]{.class_4095}" and "[B]{.class_4095}" in Rome (from Alexandria, via Caesarea) is to be ignored---enthusiastically. The fact that the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, old [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RSV]{.class_4095}, and [RV ]{.class_4095}have adopted this conjecture means nothing except that they, as Origen, Eusebius, Pamphilus, Westcott, Lightfoot, Lachmann, Hort, Tregelles, Alford, and "the majority of well-known scholars," had a difficult time reading their native tongues.

1:9 For this cause we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;

10 That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_425}in the knowledge of God;

11 Strengthened with all might, according to his glorious power, unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness;

12 Giving thanks unto the Father, which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light:

13 Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of his dear Son:

14 In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins:

15 Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:

Verses 9 and 10 are such a perfect match-mate to Ephesians 3:16, 19 and Ephesians 1:17--19 that the verses need no comment. Scriptural verses which help illuminate verses 9--15 are found in Ephesians 1:4--5, 11, 3:18--19, 4:1; Philippians 1:9, 27; 1 Thessalonians 2:12, 4:1, 5:15; 1 Corinthians 1:9, 14:20; 2 Thessalonians 2:17; 1 Timothy 5:10; and Titus 3:8. Verse 11 is commented on under Ephesians 1:19, 3:16, and 6:10; and Scriptural cross-references will be found in 2 Timothy 1:7--8 and 2:9--10.

["Which hath made us meet to be PARTAKERS OF THE INHERITANCE OF THE SAINTS IN LIGHT"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12). This is the [spiritual inheritance ]{.class_4095}of Galatians 3:18 and Ephesians 1:18 and is not to be confounded with the [literal inheritance ]{.class_4095}of Romans 8:17 and 2 Timothy 2:12. This is apparent by the reference to ["the kingdom of his dear Son,"]{.class_4096} which follows in verse 13. By confounding this kingdom with the Kingdom of Heaven (Acts 1:3; Mark 1:14; Matt. 4:17), the Campbellite, Presbyterian, Catholic, Methodist, Episcopalian, and Lutheran get a PRESENT reigning (see comments under Phil. 1:28). The light referred to is spiritual light (Acts 26:18), and it qualifies the saint to be a "child of light" (1 Thess. 5:5) in contrast to darkness (2 Cor. 4:4).

["The kingdom of HIS DEAR SON"]{.class_4096} (vs. 13) sounds a little too "homey" for the destructive critics (cf. comparative attitude on "child" in Acts 4:27), so the adjective has been transferred from the clear English to the obscure Greek idiom, and we come up with ["Son of his love." ]{.class_4095}The originator of this reading, which is found in the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_426}[New American Standard Version]{.class_4095} and similar Catholic corruptions, is the Jesuit [Rheims ]{.class_4095}edition of 1582. Here the translators have resorted to "accuracy" instead of "clarity." Phenomenon No. 10.

In the very next chapter you will observe all of them changing the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text to make it "clearer" instead of "accurate." (This works well when dealing with gullible suckers who suppose that they can overcome the authority of the Bible, but in a court of law it will fail every time. The Ultra-Supreme Court \[Rom. 3:4\] will make "short shrift" of any Bible patterned after the Dark-Age [Rheims ]{.class_4095}edition.) More discriminating Greek scholars will observe the glaring inconsistency of the corrupt [New American Standard]{.class_4095} by translating the [same construction ]{.class_4095}in the [same chapter ]{.class_4095}(vs. 7) as ["dear fellowservant"]{.class_4096} instead of ["fellowservant of love"]{.class_4095}! Why the sudden reversal, doctor? [The adjective in the genitive follows both names!]{.class_4095} Phenomenon No. 4.

You see, the perversion was neither "clearer" nor more "accurate"; it was inaccurate, inconsistent, muddled, confounded, and CROOKED. Why was it done? Because God's Son was the subject of the verse! (And some of you are still naive enough to believe that a profession of Conservatism or Evangelicalism is to be taken at face-value!) The [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095} is just about as accurate as a 1920 telephone book.

["In whom we have redemption THROUGH HIS BLOOD, even the forgiveness of sins"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). The verse is a checkpoint placed by the Holy Spirit to enable the Bible believer to spot the corrupt Bibles when they appear. Other checkpoints are Luke 2:33; 1 Timothy 3:16; Acts 20:28, 8:37; and John 9:35.

The average layman has neither time nor money to study manuscript evidence, so God, in His providential mercy, has allowed the Bible to be divided into numbered verses. This makes it possible for the crudest Christian to spot immediately a forgery of the truth. For this reason, the verse system is usually obscured as much as possible in the "modern translations." They wish to destroy the check system the Lord set up. Students who take Bible study seriously never fail to notice the confusion that is produced by erasing the verse numbers or hiding them within the paragraph. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_427}(Kenneth Wuest used this cover-up to omit several verses from the Bible while still retaining the correct number of verses! This was done by placing the numbers in the margin and then jamming the text together indiscriminately with nothing in it to mark off one verse from another. Phillips and the [New English Bible]{.class_4095} do the same thing, and [Good News]{.class_4095} follows suit.) If the modern "Conservatives" and Liberals can just get rid of the verse-number system (by appealing to the "unnumbered originals"!), they can destroy hundreds of verses and even omit dozens of verses without being detected. Phenomenon No. 17.

A good rule for the intelligent Christian to follow is to reject, at sight, any Greek manuscript or translation that omits ["through his blood"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). This is the unfailing sign of a demoniac hand tampering with the word of God. You see, ["forgiveness of sins"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14), in the Catholic Communion, is tied up with John 20:23 and Matthew 16:19. ["Through his blood"]{.class_4096} (Heb. 10:1--14) is so obnoxious to Rome that she has torn out everything in the book of Hebrews from 9:14 on (see "[Vaticanus]{.class_4095}," in the Vatican).

Now, no man in this age has "redemption" any other way than ["through his blood"]{.class_4096} (see Rom. 3:25, Heb. 9:15). Furthermore, nobody was ever redeemed by the [forgiveness of sins]{.class_4095} (Exod. 34:7). The reading, therefore, of every Bible on the market since Origen's Hexapla is a [Roman Catholic reading]{.class_4095} misleading the sinner into thinking that ["redemption"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 3:25; Heb. 9:15) is [synonymous with]{.class_4095} ["forgiveness of sins." ]{.class_4096}[But it is not.]{.class_4095}

At the Judgment Seat of Christ, Bible perverters like Westcott, Hort, Dr. A. T. Robertson, Warfield, Machen, Davis, Trench, Vincent, Souter, Milligan, Wuest, Neal, E. Nestle, Custer, Thieme, Alford, and Zodhiates are going to have a time explaining why they adopted Nestle's text when this reading (Col. 1:14) is a direct contradiction and denial of one of the main fundamental truths of New Testament salvation and the finished work of Jesus Christ. Phenomenon No. 13. If every man on the committee of the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [New ASV]{.class_4095} was a Christian, all one can say is: "It is a tragedy and a calamity that men in whom the Holy Spirit dwells have so given themselves over to Satan that they are not even aware of being used as tools in the destruction of Biblical Christianity."

["Forgiveness of sins"]{.class_4096} is NOT ["redemption." ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_428}Israel was forgiven (Luke 23:34) but not redeemed (Acts 3:19). A man can be forgiven (Matt. 18:32--34) and go to Hell. Old Testament saints were forgiven (Exod. 34:1--7), but none of them were redeemed at that time, or within 400 years of that time (Gal. 4:5).

["Through his blood" ]{.class_4096}is the truth of God given by the Holy Ghost and preserved through nineteen centuries via the [King James 1611 Authorized Version]{.class_4095}. The omission of the words, occurring in all Roman Catholic versions of "the Bible," was due to some Second Century Jehudi (Jer. 36:23) who thought that the confessional guaranteed salvation. If the words were found in no Greek manuscripts extant, they would have to appear in the text to prevent the New Testament from contradicting itself. Phenomenon No. 10.

Kurt Aland, Peake, and Nestle slyly avoid any detailed discussion of the text. It is taken for granted by all---Catholics, Liberals, and Conservatives alike---that the words are an "interpolation" from Ephesians 1:7. Wuest is so desperate to preserve his orthodoxy---at the expense of the text---that he alters ["forgiveness"]{.class_4096} to "putting away"; and then, realizing that this is a complete redemption, he quickly inserts ["payment of ransom"]{.class_4095} (!) into the text, after removing ["through his blood"]{.class_4096}! (Houdini and Blackstone never topped that one!)

One of the few men who survived the infamous Death March of Bataan (1942) was a Christian man who is now pastoring a small church in Pennsylvania. He was a born-again believer when he entered the service back in the late 1930s, but the army being what it was (see [From Here to Eternity]{.class_4095}, James Jones), his life was soon leveled to the lowest common denominator. After two years you couldn't have told whether he was a Christian or a Catholic. He did a lot of repenting on the hideous road to Bildad, Cabanatuan, Subic Bay, and Moji, Japan. One night when the Japanese crews on his prison ship were stoned with saki, he slipped through a porthole, dropped into the sea, and, after an exhausting struggle, gained the shore of an island nearby. He crawled up on the beach and passed out. The next day he "came to" around high noon and looked out into the bay; the prison ship was gone. He crawled into the jungle to []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_429}get some shade and passed out again. After four or five weeks in the bushes, living off coconuts, coconut milk, raw fish, and plants, he saw and heard signs of some kind of military activity on the back side of the island. He went cautiously through the brush on a trip that took him all day, and fearing that the activity was [Japanese]{.class_4095}, he stayed under cover until sundown.

The first time this young man poked his head out of the tropical undergrowth, he saw American helmets, several bulldozers, and the outlines of LCTs and LCIs standing offshore. Almost mad with joy, he broke out of the bushes and raced for the familiar silhouettes. He hadn't taken 100 steps when a nasty voice yelled: "Halt you\-\-\-\--!" He "put on the brakes" in midair, and raising both hands over his head, he cried, "I'm an American! I'm an American! [Don't shoot!"]{.class_4095}

Ahead of him in the dusk, he could see two G.I.s---one kneeling, one standing. The one standing had an M-1 leveled at him, and even before he had been able to cry "I'm an American," he had heard that terrifying "click-clack" as a bolt-handle came back and went forward. "For God's sake don't shoot me," he screamed. "I'm a G.I. I've been a prisoner\...I got\...off the ship\...I'm not a Jap\...I\...!"

About fifteen feet ahead of him, he sensed two pairs of eyes searching him over, but it was nearly dark, Suddenly he realized that five weeks on the island---nearly naked---had given him the appearance of a Japanese "hold out." (He was down to 110 pounds, and with his small stature \[about 5 feet, 6 inches\], he made a perfect "gook.")

"Gimmee the password, bud!"

"I-I don't know the password. But look, my name is \-\-\-\--. My serial number is \-\-\-\--. I was with the \-\-\-\-- Company of the \-\-\-\-- Battalion, \-\-\-\--the Infantry. I'm a G.I.!"

"Password. "

"My God, man, let me in! I was raised in \-\-\-\--, Maryland. I enlisted in 1938. I've been a Jap prisoner. Listen, for God's sake don't kill me now. I got through two years of it; don't kill me now. If you only knew what I've been through!"

"Sorry, bud. I got orders to shoot anybody from a General down that don't know the password. Gimmee the password and I'll let ya in."

"But listen. I'm not a spy! I'm not a Jap. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_430}[Look at me\...!"]{.class_4095}

He took three steps nearer, and the rifleman opposite him put the butt of the rifle to his shoulder. He was standing less than ten feet from (and looking into) the business-end of a Garand.

Without lowering the weapon the sentry said, "Sorry, buddy. I got orders. Get ready to die!"

Before the sentry could pull the trigger, the young man, with his hands still raised, screamed: "Let me pray! If you're going to shoot me, let me at least pray first!"

"Okay," said the hard voice. "Gowan and pray. I'll give ya ten seconds."

And there, under the muzzle of a loaded weapon, the young man knelt, and with tears pouring down his cheeks, he prayed: "Father, they're going to kill me. I've done my best. I've tried to get back home, but You won't let me. And now I'm going to see You in a minute, and [when I do]{.class_4095}, I don't want You to remember my sins!"

His ten seconds were up, but now the sentry had become unnerved, and he failed to fire at the kneeling man in front of him. The one-man prayer meeting went on.

"I've lived like the devil, Lord, and I deserve to go to Hell. But I'm Your child, and I'm trusting Your Son, and I have nothing good to say for myself. Lord, don't remember my good deeds any more than my bad ones. When I step into Your presence and You look on me, Lord, don't remember anything but THE BLOOD!"

He bowed his head and waited for the shattering blast. It never came. The sentry had lowered his rifle, and he said quietly: "That's the password. Come on in."

We often used words with "l"s in them because a Jap couldn't pronounce it. "Lallapallooza" was a good one because it would always be pronounced, ["Rarraparrooza."]{.class_4095}

That young man has pastored Bible-believing churches in Pennsylvania for more than twenty years. But his terrible experience confirms the truth of our text beyond "the original Greek." Redemption is ["THROUGH HIS BLOOD,"]{.class_4096} and when God sees ["THE BLOOD,"]{.class_4096} He will pass over the sinner (Exod. 12:13). Outside of that, you are just as good as stone-cold "dead in the market" or rotting on the backside of a tropical island. No amount of []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_431}"identification" will get you into the "base camp." All it contains is sinners who came ["THROUGH HIS BLOOD."]{.class_4096}

Any Bible text---Greek or English---that omits ["THROUGH HIS BLOOD"]{.class_4096} came from the pen of a man who should have spent his time revising Homer and Plato---not the living words of the living God. The fact that "THE Greek text" of Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer (Bob Jones University) excludes the words means nothing except that Dr. Neal and Dr. Custer are sympathetic to apostates.

["Who IS THE IMAGE of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). With this clear definition (["the image,"]{.class_4096} see Heb. 1:2--3), one would think that the theologians could get Adam straightened out, but to this day, Seminaries, of any persuasion, invent an "image" for Adam to be "created in" and shy away from the clear definitions given in 2 Corinthians 4:4 and here. ["The IMAGE of God"]{.class_4096} is Jesus Christ (John 14:8--13); and that is clearer than any textbook on Theology written by Strong, Dabney, Kuyper, Chafer, Hodge, Berkhof, Calvin, or Arminius.

Rev. Peake ([Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}) handles the "original Greek" exactly as any Bible-rejecting heretic would handle Greek, Latin, German, English, French, Spanish, or Basque. With the exact definition set clearly before his face where a blind man could not miss it at midnight, he goes right on with the inane doggerel about the[ "image"]{.class_4096} being the "manifestations of God's [qualities or attributes.]{.class_4095}" That is, he [spiritualizes ]{.class_4095}it, exactly as Philo, Clement, and Origen would have, and proves once again that "Christian education"---where it deals with the [King James]{.class_4095} text---is about as wholesome as "Myra Breckenridge." Philip and Thomas (John 14:8--9, 20:28) have evidently been grossly mistaken in their concepts of "images" if we are to believe the nonsense put out by the "Conservative" M.A.s, Ph.D.s, and Th.D.s of this century.

E. E. Ellis ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}), citing Simpson and F. F. Bruce, goes right on with this absurd private interpretation without even having read the comments by Rev. Peake. We are told here ([Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, pg. 1338) that the fallen Adamic race "bears the image of his Creator." And this is said with the most insolent barbarity, right in the face of the Holy Spirit's clear statement that [no man on this earth "bore" God's image for 4,000 years]{.class_4095} (Gen. 5:1--3; Col. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_432}3:10). But truth never deterred the dead-orthodox apostates who hated the word of God.

The [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} (pg. 8) goes right on with the fable as though no Bible had ever been written to contradict it. After giving all of the proper cross references (2 Cor. 4:4; John 1:1--3; Heb. 1:1--3), the writer goes right on altering the clear sense and continues to brainwash the believer into thinking that ["image"]{.class_4096} doesn't mean IMAGE; it means merely "the moral and [redemptional attributes ]{.class_4095}of the Godhead manifested in\
\...etc." Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown would never withdraw their cards from the "party" now, so on they go with the impudent perversity (Commentary, pg. 1316) that the image is a reference to "his essential [CHARACTER."]{.class_4095}

[Has anyone seen Adamantius Origen around here anywhere? ]{.class_4095}I detect his B.O. We must be dealing with the [Deity of Christ]{.class_4095} (see the next four verses), for there has suddenly arisen that peculiar stinking smog that is so characteristic of the [New American Standard]{.class_4095} (1963) and the Hexapla (A.D. 200--240). It smells like North Avenue in Baltimore, Maryland, on a hot summer evening.

What happened to the "original GREEK"? The "original" Greek said ["eikon"]{.class_4095} in [Vaticanus]{.class_4095}, [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}, [Alexandrinus]{.class_4095},[ D]{.class_4095}---Western, Syrian, Egyptian, cursives, uncials, and Caesarean, plus [E, F, G, H]{.class_4095}, etc. Tell us something, ye erudite geniuses who have been correcting the Bible every other word in Galatians, Ephesians, and Philippians. "What is an ['eikon']{.class_4095}?" How is it that THIS TIME---after citing these depraved philosophers time after time after time (see [Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, pp. 37--502)---you suddenly forgot how the word ["eikon"]{.class_4095} was used in [classical Greek literature]{.class_4095}? Phenomenon No. 3. (If we didn't know you any better, we might say, in jest, that your "lapses of memory" are nearly as convenient as those experienced by a man being examined by the Internal Revenue Service.)

When was an "image" anything but an IMAGE? Phenomenon No. 5. If we are predestinated to be ["conformed to the]{.class_4096} [image of his Son"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:29), why, in Purgatory, would you think that this was a "spiritual goal" to be obtained in [this life]{.class_4095}? (See comments under Phil. 3:21 and Eph. 4:15.)

You see, the men who holler the longest and loudest about the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_433}"GNOSTIC heresy" (see comments under Phil. 3:21) really believe that [matter is evil ]{.class_4095}themselves! When God says ["image,"]{.class_4096} they immediately divorce the word from the context in which it appears if it has any reference to God Himself (see Exod. 20:1--3 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus]{.class_4095}, 1973). They are so frightened by the implications of the word (in any text) that they feel it must be [spiritualized ]{.class_4095}to absolve them of the charge of being "idolators." (Notice how this confusion can arise from reading John 6:57 into John 14:9.)

[Philosophy ]{.class_4095}has corrupted the "leading translators and commentators." They blush to think that any of their critics might catch them confining the "eternal" God---who is a SPIRIT---to a [body of flesh and bones]{.class_4095}, but that is [exactly what happened]{.class_4095} (1 Tim. 3:16). (And that is why 1 Tim. 3:16 was butchered by the [New American Standard, American Standard,]{.class_4095} [Revised Standard]{.class_4095}, Revised American Standard, New Revised, [New Revised Standard, ]{.class_4095}and the New Revised New American New Standard New Clairol.) Students who take their Bible seriously recognize the hands of Marcion and Valentinus when they turn the pages of these "highly recommended" corruptions.

["Who is the image of the invisible God"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). Since ["the invisible things\...are clearly seen\...even HIS ETERNAL power and GODHEAD" ]{.class_4096}(Rom. 1:20), the verse is to be taken literally to say what it means and mean what it says. If 100 percent of Conservative scholarship in America placed a different interpretation on the verse (see Col. 2:8 for example), it would mean nothing except that 100 percent of Conservative Scholarship was infected with an incurable disease. Jesus Christ was the VISIBLE appearance of the INVISIBLE God. He was the Godhead, ["manifest]{.class_4096} [in the flesh"]{.class_4096} (1 Tim. 3:16), and the Godhead dwelt in him BODILY (Col. 2:9). This is what a "Conservative" is [supposed to believe ]{.class_4095}when he speaks of the "DEITY" of Christ.

If you don't have that in mind, you are nothing but a deluded backslider who is trying to keep the temperature at 70 degrees (see Rev. 3:16), where you won't get "froze out" or "scorched." The "middle of the road" approach is quite manifest in the "newer translations" where the obnoxious word ["image"]{.class_4096} has been modified to the more acceptable[ "likeness"]{.class_4095} (see Phillips) and []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_434}["expression" ]{.class_4095}([Living Letters]{.class_4095}, [Good News]{.class_4095}, and similar apostate corruptions).

Every real Bible believer is an IDOLATER of sorts: his "idol" is Jesus Christ. Furthermore, every real orthodox Jew or atheist knows this to be true and is more honest in expressing his feelings about this matter than such plastic, pliable, vacillating chameleons as Lightfoot, Westcott, Ellicott, Alford, Rendall, Hort, and other brethren who must have been raised on a diet of Post Toasties and Oatmeal.

["The FIRSTBORN OF EVERY CREATURE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). Now we are on familiar ground! This is where Origen, Arius, and Augustine used to camp; and the area is still littered with cigarette butts, beer cans, and chewing gum wrappers! Having rejected the clear statements of 1 Timothy 3:16; John 14:9, 20:28; Colossians 1:15; 2 Corinthians 4:4; and Hebrews 1:1--3, the "Christian" faculty at the "Christian" University now has something they can argue about till Hell freezes over. The confusion at this point is apparent by one fugitive glance over the paper work.

1\. If we are to believe Phillips, Christ existed ["before CREATION began."]{.class_4095}

2\. If we are to believe the [NEB]{.class_4095}, Christ ["has the primacy"]{.class_4095} over all created things.

3\. If we check these off and accept [Living Letters,]{.class_4095} God did not create His Son, for He existed ["before God made anything at all."]{.class_4095} (A beautiful attempt to restore yourself to the ranks of the believers, after altering the first clause in the verse!)

4\. But Bengel, Wordsworth, Olshausen, Meyer, and Ellicott would alter it to read: ["he is the firstborn BEFORE every creature."]{.class_4095}

5\. Soden and Abbott think that it really meant: ["he has a priority before all creation."]{.class_4095}

6\. Haupt and Weiss think that it should have said: ["he was the ruler of all creation."]{.class_4095}

7\. Arius conjectures: ["the first begetter of a creation."]{.class_4095}

8\. Schleiermacher thinks it should have been: ["the first begetter of the new MORAL creation. "]{.class_4095}

It is apparent that when a man destroys the plain truth of half a verse, he is liable to flounder awhile in the second half.

Before further study, the reader should notice the peculiar mania that the scholars have for retranslating ["CREATURE"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:19, 2 Cor. 5:17) as "CREATION." Since verse 15 is still a part of a sentence that goes into our next section, we shall reproduce it here.

1:16 For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him:

17 And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.

18 And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.

19 For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell;

The reason for this necessary division of sections is due to the fact that Paul has so written Colossians One that it makes nearly one theme from verses 9 to 29. You will notice that the concluding thoughts of verse 11 are not allowed to stop but are only part of a sentence that goes on to verse 17. Even though there is a transition of thought between verses 14 and 15, the sentence is one.

And even though the material in verse 16 is referring to ACTS instead of a STATE (vs. 15), it is tied to verse 15 by the second clause---["every creature."]{.class_4096} And although the sentence ends at verse 17, the thought continues into verses 18 and 19 and goes right into another discussion in verse 20. Even though this sentence stops at verse 20, the subject matter of verse 20 goes right on into verses 21 and 22, and this time the sentence does not stop until verse 29.

Now, the [American Standard Version]{.class_4095} (1901) states that Jesus Christ was a "creature" (margin, John 9:35). This heresy is condemned by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (Commentary, pg. 1316), but these worthy gentlemen wrongly accuse the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible of suggesting this heresy while the [ASV ]{.class_4095}actually [teaches ]{.class_4095}it. This should give the reader some cause for deep thought.

For centuries (dating from the Council of Nicaea), "orthodoxy" is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_436}supposed to have been marked by profession in the Deity of Christ. Strangely enough, the champions of this "orthodoxy" (the Roman Church) came out with the most Christ-dishonoring, Christ-rejecting, Christ-nullifying system of all (Mark 7:6--13). Catholicism, while professing to believe in the "Deity of Christ," has never "elevated" Him anywhere except by a hypocritical pretense when a sinner "elevates" a cup full of fermented liquor. In the Catholic system (as any informed person knows), Mary shares with Christ a sinless nature, a resurrection, and authority as a mediator. In South America, [the Madonna]{.class_4095} holds [the child,]{.class_4095} not vice versa.

Now, whenever Rome wishes to exert her political power as a State-Church, she brands as "heretics" (or "demagogues") anyone who opposes her political designs (see North Ireland, 1960--1971); and the newspapers, magazines, and TV back her up, exactly as the European Monarchs backed her up between 1000--1500. This is most interesting, for when John Calvin, the great dead-orthodox Conservative, set up his Church-State system in Geneva, he had a converted Jew burned at the stake for not accepting [Origen's interpretation]{.class_4095} of Colossians 1:15 and Psalm 2:7 (see Schaff's [Church History]{.class_4095}, Vol. VII, pp. 65--70).

You see, Origen---as nearly every "conservative" teacher in America today---suggested that Christ was BORN in [eternity ]{.class_4095}and that the word ["begotten"]{.class_4096} referred to ["eternal generation."]{.class_4095} Anyone who thought that the word ["born"]{.class_4095} (as here) or ["begotten"]{.class_4096} (as Heb. 1:6, Psa. 2:7) referred to BIRTH or BEGETTING in a [temporal sense]{.class_4095} was called "ARIAN." Servetus (burned at the stake, 1553) believed that Jesus Christ was God of very God, manifested in the flesh. However, he did NOT believe what Lightfoot, Ellicott, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Clarke, Lange, Williams, Dummelow, Rotherham, Knox, Phillips, Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Robertson, Davidson, Schaff, Weigle, Meyer, Hort, Machen, Warfield, or Peake said about the passage.

1\. Origen (A.D. 185--254) began the whole controversy by insisting that Christ's BIRTH took place [before time.]{.class_4095}

2\. Arius (A.D. 256--336) knew this was nonsense, and he was right. He knew, as well as you know you're alive, that there is no time element in eternity and that Psalm 2:7 clearly said ["THIS DAY []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_437}have I begotten thee."]{.class_4096}

So Arius took the opposite point of view and said that sometime before Genesis 1 and sometime AFTER eternity (!) Christ was brought forth as a lesser god (see Jehovah's Witness Bible, [New World Translation]{.class_4095}, John 1:1--2). The [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095}, recommended by Bob Jones University, takes this view (see John 1:18), and this is apparent by its attempt to get rid of the connection between "Jehovah" and "Jesus." You will observe in the [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1963) and similar corruptions that the word "Jehovah" occurs nowhere in the New Testament. The word "LORD" occurs in both Testaments in the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 so the reader can see the connection between "Jehovah God" and "Jehovah Jesus" (see Rev. 19:16, for example). (A conservative [committee ]{.class_4095}does not guarantee a conservative [translation]{.class_4095}. Most "conservative committees" have been out of contact with real Biblical Christianity for half a century or a whole century.)

3\. Servetus (1511--1553) took the middle ground. He knew Jesus was God, so he was NOT an "Arian," although he was burnt at the stake for being an "Arian." But he, as Arius, knew that the words [begotten,]{.class_4095} [begat,]{.class_4095} [begetting,]{.class_4095} [birth]{.class_4095}, and [born]{.class_4095} referred to an ACT that took place. He was not an allegorizing spiritualist like Origen and his Amillennial followers---Calvin, Hodge, Machen, Warfield, A. T. Robertson, Mauro, Berkhof, et al.---so he came to the only conclusion that a real Bible believer could come to: [he came to the conclusion that Christ was BORN on a certain day at a certain time and was BEGOTTEN of the Holy Ghost through the Virgin Mary.]{.class_4095} Before that time, He was only a manifestation of the Godhead, who appeared as the Angel of the Lord (Gen. 16; Judg. 13; etc.). Although we know NOW that God's tripartite nature (body, soul, spirit) included "the Son" (John 1:18), we also know that some day "the Son" will merge back into the Godhead (1 Cor. 15:28) as He originally was BEFORE GENESIS 1:1.

["Firstborn"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) can have a reference to Christ's pre-incarnate state only if one accepts the private interpretation of Calvinism which teaches that the "decree" of Psalm 2:7 was an ETERNAL DECREE before Genesis 1:1 (and Berkhof says that "all of God's decrees are eternal" and that they have their source in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_438}eternity). This forces the Bible believer into another one of the numerous hopeless contradictions that are found in Calvin's system (see comments under Eph. 1:4--5). He is to believe that ["THIS DAY"]{.class_4096} (Psa. 2:7) refers to [NO DAY. ]{.class_4095}But even worse, ["THIS DAY"]{.class_4096} (Psa. 2:7) cannot even refer to a [period of time! There is no time in eternity.]{.class_4095}

Now, this is the problem with which our text deals. The insertion of "before" for ["of"]{.class_4096} (see any modern commentator, including the Hyperdispensationalist Williams!) was done to teach the private interpretation of John Calvin. No such Greek text exists anywhere. Phenomenon No. 5.

Furthermore, the changing of ["by"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16) to[ "in"]{.class_4095} ([New ASV, RV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and similar corruptions) forces the believer to accept the private interpretation of Origen that all of the ["things"]{.class_4096} in verse 16 refer to saved spiritual things in the "new creation." This is the ACME of private interpretation, and it can only be matched by the deluded Italian princes of Rome (see [Rome---The Great Private Interpreter]{.class_4095}, 1969).

One more comment before digging the infallible truth out of the mire, morass, and debris of "conservative scholarship." Observe how the [New ASV]{.class_4095} (and other erroneous distortions of truth) has taken the trouble to alter ["creature"]{.class_4096} to "creation," yet the Greek text---any set of manuscripts of any family, in any century---go right on with ["ta panta\...ta panta\...ta panta\...ta panta"]{.class_4095} (["ALL THINGS,"]{.class_4096} vss. 16--17), listing the things [in the creation]{.class_4095}, not the "creation" (see comments under Gal. 6:15).

For diversion, we shall look into the Holy Bible and see what it says about the passage, instead of what dead-orthodox apostasy THINKS the passage says.

1\. ["The IMAGE of God"]{.class_4096} never appeared on this earth until Christ was BORN. Notice that when the scholars refused to take ["eikon"]{.class_4095} literally, they lost the context for the second clause. There is no discussion at all in verse 15 of anyone's pre-eternal state: ["IMAGE\...FIRSTBORN."]{.class_4096} John saw the Image (John 1:14), Peter saw it (2 Pet. 1:16), Thomas saw it (John 20:28), and Philip saw it (John 14:8--9). Verse 14, which preceded the controversial passage, was dealing with the [earthly ministry ]{.class_4095}of Jesus Christ as a man---[born ]{.class_4095}and dying among men who are [born ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_439}and die. Phenomenon No. 9.

2\. The term ["firstborn"]{.class_4096} has no reference to eternity in one passage out of 65 passages (see Gen. 10:15, Exod. 4:22, Num. 3:2; Deut. 21:15; Josh. 6:26; Judg. 8:20; 1 Sam. 8:2; 2 Sam. 3:2; 1 Kings 16:34; and the other 56 passages). To conjecture that ["firstborn"]{.class_4096} is a reference to Christ [before Genesis 1]{.class_4095} is to deny deliberately and outrightly [every verse in the Bible that deals with the subject.]{.class_4095} (You see, Origen no more believed the Bible he had was infallible than John R. Rice or Noel Smith!)

3\. Where the term ["firstborn"]{.class_4096} is applied to Jesus Christ by the Divine Author of the inspired and infallible "originals," it is always a reference to His relationship to Mary (see Matt. 1:25; Luke 2:7; Rom. 8:29).

4\. Only in Colossians 1:18 (which we shall shortly exposit) is the ["firstborn"]{.class_4096} ever connected with anything but [earthly birth ]{.class_4095}(see Rev. 1:5 and comments).

5\. The salient text of Hebrews 1:6 says ["when he bringeth into the world,"]{.class_4096} giving a definite [time element ]{.class_4095}that took place in a [temporal setting.]{.class_4095}

6\. The crux of the argument (Psa. 2:7) states ["THIS DAY HAVE I BEGOTTEN THEE."]{.class_4096} The context of ["this day"]{.class_4096}---regardless of your opinion about it, or the opinion of any man who taught you---is the visible, literal Second Coming of Jesus Christ (vss. 2--6, 9--11). Remembering that the two Advents are concurrent until Acts 7 (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew]{.class_4095}, Matt. 24, 1970), it is perfectly clear that nobody was ["firstborn"]{.class_4096} or ["begotten"]{.class_4096} until they were, first, [BORN ]{.class_4095}or [BEGOTTEN]{.class_4095}.

Servetus was burned at the stake for the truth, and dead-orthodox apostates today (exactly as their Roman counterparts) would burn any real Bible believer for the truth if they thought they could get away with it.

Paul's lengthy sentence (vs. 9 through 17) threw the scholars, for none of them could "rightly divide the word of truth" when it came to a knockdown, drag-out contest to find real truth. They joined the second half of verse 15 with verse 16 and then refused to accept the first half of verse 15 as having any reference to the [bodily incarnation ]{.class_4095}of 1 Timothy 3:16. This was done in their MINDS, even []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_440}when they cited the cross-refer-ences for the student to read! Phenomenon No. 9.

Jesus Christ was the first man ever BORN of the Holy Spirit. (No, your professor's reference to Luke 1:15 and Jeremiah 1:5 is not relevant; don't waste God's time trying to overthrow the truth.) He, therefore, is ["the FIRSTBORN among many brethren"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:29) who will be born by the Spirit. [No one in the Old Testament was born of the Spirit]{.class_4095} (see comments under Gal. 3:6, 9 and 4:29); but since none of the "leading scholars" among Conservatives, Liberals, or Catholics could find this most simple truth, it is not surprising that they should make such a holocaust of Colossians 1:15. Jesus Christ is the ["FIRSTBORN"]{.class_4096} of every creature in the sense that He is the first in a series of "NEW CREATURES" (2 Cor. 5:17---not "creation" as the corrupt [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095}), and those in Him (vs. 27) are "new creatures" (Rom. 8:16). He is ["the FIRST BEGOTTEN of the dead"]{.class_4096} (Rev. 1:5) in that He is the first man who ever died and came back up, never to die again. (Moses came up, like Dorcas, Eutychus, Jonah, and Lazarus, only to die in the future; see Rev. 11:3--7.)

The verse that the scholars were looking for was the famous Russellite passage of Revelation 3:14. There Christ is ["the beginning of the creation of God,"]{.class_4096} and this is obviously a reference which matches Colossians 1:16. However, as anyone can plainly see, it is not a cross-reference to Colossians 1:15. In Colossians 1:15, it is ["creature"]{.class_4096} ([King James]{.class_4095}); but in Revelation 3:14, it is ["creation"]{.class_4096} ([King James]{.class_4095}). In Colossians 1:15, it is ["firstborn"]{.class_4096} ([King James]{.class_4095}); but in Revelation 3:14, it is ["beginning"]{.class_4096} ([King James]{.class_4095}).

Observe how the infallible [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text neatly "divides the word of truth" so that the simplest can understand it and the most erudite can do nothing but curse, pray, holler, scream, cry, weep, wail, and gnash their teeth. In both passages, the English clarifies "the Greek." Phenomenon No. 10.

Jesus Christ was the source or medium through which everything created was created (John 1:3; Col. 1:16; Rev. 3:14), but He was never ["begotten"]{.class_4096} one day before Matthew 1:25. In His preexistent, eternal relationship, He was that counterpart of the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_441}Trinity answering to the "body" of man, but that body does not show up as an ["image"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) until 1 John 1:1--2. The body of the "Angel of the Lord" (Gen. 1--Matt. 1) HAS NO BLOOD, and the resurrection body HAS NO BLOOD (see Phil. 3:21 and comments).

Servetus had it right, and John Calvin was as fouled up as a Chinese fire-drill when it came to clear exposition of the truth. The sophisticated theological argument for which Origen was responsible sidetracked the Body of Christ for nearly fourteen centuries, and at the time of the Reformation---when the Body of Christ was supposed to have gone "back to the Bible" (Theodore Epp!)---the same old Alexandrian philosophers slipped back into the driver's seat and sidetracked it again!

Verse 16 is a statement on the work of the Lord Jesus Christ BEFORE He was born, or before He became ["the firstborn"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15). Verse 16 is plainly PAST, verse 17 is plainly PRESENT, and verses 22---24 are plainly FUTURE. Verse 15 deals with Christ's [earthly life ]{.class_4095}and [atonement ]{.class_4095}(see vs. 14), and the colon at the end of verse 15 is a clear warning to the impetuous and rash "Conservative" that he had better slow down. There is another colon at the end of verse 16 showing a change in TIME, and this is observed by all of the scholars. But they did not observe it [because of the colon]{.class_4095} (for all of them despise the [AV ]{.class_4095}punctuation system!); they observed it this time because [the time change was CLEAR]{.class_4095}, whereas in verse 15 it [was not]{.class_4095}! In a "shakedown," the critics of the [AV 1611]{.class_4095} will fail the inspection every time. You can count on them. Phenomenon No. 14.

But our bungling "brethren" are not through yet trying to inject their private opinions into the text and usurp the authority of the Holy Spirit as the interpreter (Luke 24:45). By changing ["For BY him were all things created\..."]{.class_4096} to "IN him" ([Amplified, RV, ASV, RSV, New RSV, NEB,]{.class_4095} Dummelow, Bullinger, Moffatt, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Wordsworth, Haupt, Meyer, [Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_4095}, Lange, Clarke, [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Rev. Peake, Bengel, etc.), the ridiculous doctrine is now stated that the "principalities and powers" (vs. 16) were made IN Christ. The intelligent reader can see at once that the "principalities and powers" are the [Satanic and demoniac dominions]{.class_4095} of Ephesians 1:21 and 6:12. (If they were created IN Christ, they must have fallen out of Him, or else Ethelbert Bullinger []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3058.xhtml_page_442}was right in his remarks on universal restoration \[Eph. 1:10\]!)

The lame alibi for this perversion of truth is (again!) "accuracy." The Greek word ["en"]{.class_4095} has been translated as "in," following the Papal dictates of the Jesuits from 1582 ([Rheims]{.class_4095}). (The same Roman Catholic stunt was pulled in 1 Corinthians 12:13, since all Roman Catholics depend on WATER for the New Birth instead of the Holy Spirit.)

Now, "in" does not always convey the sense as ["by"]{.class_4096} does, by any stretch of the imagination. Both [Living Letters]{.class_4095} and ["Good News"]{.class_4095} understand this if the [RV, ASV, RSV, NEB, New ASV]{.class_4095}, and the New Math do not. If we desired, we could simply retain the [King James]{.class_4095} text on the grounds of "clarity" instead of "accuracy," for the destructive critics of the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}text do this many times without batting an eye. However, in this case, the [King James]{.class_4095} is not merely PLAINER, it is also ACCURATE.
