With this much light flooding the pavilion, one would think that the commentators would find something appropriate to say about the scenery. But instead, we find that "the clause is obscure," and "the passage is exceedingly corrupt," and "the bravest of the brave" []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_508}(Williams---not Field Marshall Ney!) do not dare apply the passage to the Roman Catholic hierarchy which has practiced the abuses listed for more than fifteen centuries.

Before boring the reader with the negative aspects of the passage (the comments of the commentators and the perversions of the revisers), we shall pan out the gold (Psa. 119:127; Prov. 16:16) which God the Holy Spirit has placed in the pure mountain streams of the [Authorized Version.]{.class_4095}

1\. ["A voluntary humility" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18) resembles "voluntary abandonment" (grounds for divorce). It means that the Christian willingly subjects himself to some man's sacramental rules and regulations as a "selfhumbling process" to show his "consecration." When this is done, the Christian is "subverted" (Tit. 3:10--11) to the puffed‑up fleshpot of the text: ["vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind."]{.class_4096}

2\. ["Worshipping of angels" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 18) goes on regularly in Spain, Italy, South America, Central America, and the Philippines in 1970; and there is no reason at all for the cowardly commentators to run back nineteen centuries to First‑Century Persian religions and apostate Judaism to find an application. ["The wicked flee when no man pursueth" ]{.class_4096}(Prov. 28:1), and this eternal scurrying and hurrying [backwards in time ]{.class_4095}to deaden the authority of the Holy Bible is only indicative of the insincerity and infidelity of the commentators. "[Angels]{.class_4096}" are "appearances" (see Rev. 1--2, [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation, ]{.class_4095}1970). If you had a dollar for every "appearance" that Papists claim to have seen, you could buy the gold plating in the Vatican library.

["The star of your god Remphan" ]{.class_4096}(Acts 7:43) is still the national emblem of the nation of Israel, and the sex sign of Isis and Osiris is still the official emblem of the Vatican's Pope. Don't ever be duped into thinking that The Epistle to the Colossians was aimed only at [First‑Century inhabitants ]{.class_4095}of Asia Minor! Jean Dixon has "communicated" since 1950, and so has Bishop Pike and Timothy O'Leary. A man who thinks the text of the 1611 Bible is "archaic" is a fool.

3\. ["Subject to ordinances" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20) implies that the self‑humbled man is trying to stay saved or get "sanctified" by []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_509}submitting to rules and regulations. The ordinances listed are not "[the ordinances]{.class_4096}" of 1 Corinthians 11:2, and have nothing to do with the MEMORIAL of the Lord's Supper or the FIGURE of Baptism.

The references say:

a\. [Don't TOUCH IT. ]{.class_4095}The Pharisees couldn't touch one brick inside of Pilate's Hall, or they would be "unclean" (John 18:28). It is quite similar to a Roman Catholic touching the inside of an independent Baptist church. (No priest in America told his congregation to go to Protestant churches, even if the press reported Pope John XXIV \[not "XXIII": [John XXIII lived in A.D. 1410]{.class_4095}\] as recommending it,)

b\. [Don't TASTE IT. ]{.class_4095}Don't eat pork if you are a Jew. Don't eat "meat" on Friday if you are a Catholic.

[c. Don't HANDLE IT. ]{.class_4095}Don't handle a Bible, don't handle a hymn book, don't handle a Gospel tract, etc.

The original "[handwriting]{.class_4096}" is Leviticus 5--27, and this clearly marks the Rabbi and the Priest as legalists who are out to force the Christian back under the Law by "trodding underfoot" the blood of Calvary (Heb. 10:26--29).

In Acts 15, the Christians are not given ordinances ["after the commandments and doctrines of men"]{.class_4096};[ ]{.class_4096}they are given recommendations and [suggestions ]{.class_4095}by the Holy Spirit (Acts 15:6--28). Notice that the advice is not "if any man keep not these, let him be anathema" or "unless you do these, ye cannot be saved." The advice is ["From which if ye keep yourselves, ye shall do well" ]{.class_4096}(Acts 15:29). This distinction must be kept, for Antinomianism is the result of making a blanket application of Colossians 2:20--21 to every case.

The careful reader of Scripture will observe that Paul has some "touch nots and taste nots" [of his own ]{.class_4095}in Romans 12 and 14; but these are not Levitical ordinances, nor are they given to save any man or keep him saved (see comments under Gal. 5:1--5).

4\. "[A]{.class_4096} [shew of wisdom in will worship" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 23). The ascetic appears to be "a man of God," but he has simply exerted his willpower over the flesh and is maintaining his "separation" by WILLPOWER, not by the power of the Holy Spirit (see Gal. 5:22 and comments). This displays the man's "[wisdom,]{.class_4096}" and it appears that he is a great "saint" or "holy one"; however, all he has done is magnify [his own ability ]{.class_4095}to control himself! This is "[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_510}[WILL WORSHIP,]{.class_4096}" pure and simple, and the remarks of the commentators---which we shall shortly examine---only show that criminals tend to cover up for one another.

5\. ["Neglecting of the body\...not\...to the satisfying of the flesh" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 23). The great pole‑sitter Simon Stylites and the great ascetic Saint Anthony well illustrate the verse. Martin Luther also worked on this shift for several years before the [Textus Receptus ]{.class_4095}of Romans 1:17 liberated him from the corrupt Roman Catholic Vatican manuscripts of Jerome. Where the body is neglected, abused, lacerated, punished, denied, and controlled by the WILL, the will‑worshipper imagines he is making progress. The Holiness people actually believed that long hair and absence of lipstick proved something, i.e., until 1965--1970. Then--lo and behold!--out [came the dope heads, whoremongers, gonifs, revolutionaries, and prostitutes with---LONG HAIR AND NO LIPSTICK! ]{.class_4095}(You see, the [King James ]{.class_4095}text is more relevant to 1970 than the preaching of the Twentieth‑Century preachers who are trying to get "sanctified"!)

When a man refuses to TASTE beer and to TOUCH cigarettes and to HANDLE the flesh, he gets the immediate impression that he is "growing in grace" and is becoming "holy." But Proverbs 14:12 was written long before Colossians 2. No matter what you quit handling, tasting, and touching, you cannot grow three inches in grace if you are still SELF‑WILLED. As a matter of fact, SELF‑WILL (Isa. 14:12--16) determines every act of sin ever committed, including the first one on earth (Gen. 3:1--5). Men who are ["lovers of their own selves" ]{.class_4096}(2 Tim. 3:2) are capable of committing ANY SIN.

While the puffed‑up fleshpot of the passage appears to be carrying out the instructions of Colossians 3:5 (which see), he is merely displaying SELF. As Millard Box once said, "You cannot completely crucify yourself, because you will have to have [one hand free ]{.class_4095}to pound the nails in!"

Mahatma Gandhi neglected the body and wasted very little time "satisfying the flesh." He had about as much spiritual power with God as Elizabeth Taylor. What his biographers call "spiritual power" was political [influence, ]{.class_4095}personal [magnetism, ]{.class_4095}and [communication with spirits: ]{.class_4095}there is a difference, you know. (Or maybe you don't.) []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_511}Gandhi and Albert Schweitzer are perfect examples of the "genuine counterfeit." Personal sacrifice, self‑denial, humiliation, and idealism are manifest in every page of their life histories; however, there are ten essential ingredients missing. The fact that the world is also missing these ingredients only means that they will approve of such men and accept them as "spiritual giants" (2 Cor. 4:4).

1\. Neither man [believed ]{.class_4095}the revelation of God in the Bible.

2\. Neither man believed he would go to Hell if he [rejected ]{.class_4095}this revelation.

3\. Neither man believed what God said about man or [man's nature.]{.class_4095}

4\. Neither man judged himself [the way God told him to.]{.class_4095}

5\. Neither man trusted [the grace of God ]{.class_4095}for salvation.

6\. Neither man abandoned [his own self‑righteousness.]{.class_4095}

7\. Neither man was regenerated or ["born again."]{.class_4095}

8\. Neither man was [spiritually]{.class_4095} [circumcised.]{.class_4095}

9\. Neither man was in Christ or ["in the Spirit."]{.class_4095}

10\. Neither man [believed in blood atonement ]{.class_4095}for sins.

(Outside of that, they were splendid "Christians"!)

Paul's neglect of the flesh (2 Cor. 11:24--26) is an entirely different thing than that practiced by the Trappist Monks of Madison, Georgia. Paul's abuse of the flesh (2 Cor. 11:23--30) was entirely different from that of the Baalite sun‑worshippers (1 Kings 18) who worshipped ["the queen of heaven" ]{.class_4096}(Jer. 44:25). Things different are not equal.

6\. ["The rudiments of the world" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 20) have been discussed before under 2:8, which see. If the Christian is [dead ]{.class_4095}to these things (see 3:3 and comments), then why does he pay any attention to the commandments of the people who still direct and guide their lives by these rudiments? What good can come of giving up chocolate if you still drink shinny in the Mass? What is the point of giving up cigars for Lent if you still play bingo for money? What is the purpose in putting a hanky on your head on Sunday when the man who taught you this is still guiding his life by the rudiments we listed under 2:8? Aren't you a little confused?

We have commented on verse 19 under Ephesians 4:15--16. The believer "lives" in Christ, not "in the world" (see vs. 20). He is only []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_512}"in the world" and "in the flesh" GEOGRAPHICALLY, as we have stated on numerous occasions when correcting the Gnostic errors of Wuest, Dummelow, Lipsius, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Ellis, Lange, Clarke, Bullinger, Phillips, Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Berkeley, Montgomery, Alford, Wordsworth, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Luken, Peake, Meyer, Vincent, Riddle, Schnedermann, Haupt, and other Bible‑rejecting "Conservatives" (see comments under Gal. 2:20, 4:29, 5:25, etc.).

The passage (vss. 16--23) is a negative warning telling the Christian what kind of advice and leadership to avoid in his struggle to get the mastery over the flesh. In the next chapter, we learn that the solution for the problem lies in a positive ACTION[ ]{.class_4096}taken through the Holy Spirit, not the exertion of the will. Before studying this solution, we shall examine, very briefly, some of the numerous attempts of the "modern editors" to overthrow the words of truth.

The biggest boner pulled out of the boneyard by the critics of the [AV ]{.class_4101}1611 is the one unearthed in 1881--1885 by Philip Schaff, Ellicott, Lightfoot, Westcott, and Hort. The "dead man's bone" (see Matt. 23:27) is to the effect that verse 23 should have been worded so as to teach that the practices listed before are "not of any value [against ]{.class_4101}the indulgence of the flesh." (The [RSV ]{.class_4101}alters this to "no value in CHECKING the indulgence of the flesh," and the [NEB ]{.class_4101}tries "no use at all in combatting sensuality.") All of these attempts are the efforts of puffed‑up fleshpots to salvage some of their own "ordinances" from the passage, for these readings show that none of them believe that the [will is able to combat sensuality (NEB) ]{.class_4101}or check indulgences ([ASV,]{.class_4101} [New ASV,]{.class_4101} and [RV). ]{.class_4101}It then follows that if the translators of the [NEB, RSV, ASV]{.class_4101}[, ]{.class_4096}COD, TVA, RCA, and IBM believe this, they think the problem is limited to "indulgences of the flesh" and "sensuality." (See also Berkeley's "[lower nature]{.class_4101}" and the [Good News ]{.class_4101}translation: "[controlling physical PASSIONS]{.class_4101}.")

[But that was not the problem. ]{.class_4095}The problem was a man with a puffed‑up mind worshipping his own WILL. Not even Ellis [(Wycliffe Commentary) ]{.class_4095}nor Phillips nor Jamieson, Fausset and Brown (Commentary, pg. 1324) are deceived into thinking that the problem is sensuality, fleshy indulgences, physical passions, or any of the "cover ups" used by the committees of the [ASV]{.class_4095}, [RV, New ASV,]{.class_4095} or []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_513}other puffed‑up fleshpots. Willpower CAN successfully combat fleshy indulgence, physical passions, and sensuality; and that has been proved on more than 500,000,000 occasions. The reading, therefore, of the [New ASV, ASV,]{.class_4095} and other corrupt Bibles is baseless and defunct. Haupt and Hort gave the [ASV ]{.class_4095}committee the alibi for perverting the text by saying that it was "corrupt" (see Dummelow, Commentary, pg. 984). But we have been on board the "[Romana del Vaticano]{.class_4095}" just a few days back (see Col. 2:8 and comments), and we know a corpse when we see one. The [AV]{.class_4095} text slew Hort and Haupt because both of them were [will worshippers ]{.class_4095}and both of them deeply resented the exposure of their sin (Jer. 23, 36).

Since the [New World Translation ]{.class_4095}(Jehovah's Witness Bible) has also adopted this peculiar interpretation of Haupt and Hort, which they received from the [LXX]{.class_4095} and Philo (see [Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_4095}Vol. 20, pp. 96--97), we can be certain that we are again dealing with dead‑orthodox apostates who have no understanding whatsoever about either the spiritual warfare or the spiritual dangers of the Christian life.

The danger is very well stated by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, and even the unrestrained paraphrase of [Living Letters ]{.class_4095}gets the point. The point is that selfinflicted asceticism, which DOES control passions and DOES combat the lower nature and DOES check indulgences, [does NOT solve the problem of the puffedup mind ]{.class_4095}(vs. 18). The "fleshy mind" of the passage is without Christ (vs. 19), in spite of the attempts of the Berkeley translation to intimate that it merely slipped in "holding on to Christ," and since it "[is enmity against God"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:7) and ["is not subject to the law of God"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:7), its inventions can only counterfeit the New Birth (John 1:13).

The "remedy" for the fleshy mind that is puffed up is a [renewed mind ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 12:1--3) that places its affections (Col. 3:1--3) ["on things above."]{.class_4096} There is no permanent "remedy" for the flesh, per se, short of death or the Rapture, and Kenneth Wuest's ridiculous reading ([Expanded Translation,]{.class_4095} pg. 473) is simply more of Haupt, Hort, Lightfoot, et al., pawned off on Bible believers as ["sound doctrine."]{.class_4096} (It is about as "sound" as Wuest's rendering of Matt. 5:22!)

Paul's ["mind"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 7:25) was as ["the mind of Christ" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_514}(see Phil. 2:5; 1 Cor. 2:16), and this is NOT the "fleshy mind" of the seducer of Colossians 2:18 who is trying to draw off the Christian into the tenets of Roman Catholicism and kindred beguilements. The ["fleshly mind"]{.class_4096} can put down the fleshly passions, but this only magnifies the power of the ["FLESHLY MIND."]{.class_4096} The only thing flesh can produce is flesh; marvel not that I say unto you, ["Ye must be born again" ]{.class_4096}(John 3:6--8).

The [ASV ]{.class_4095}translators have mistaken the ["no man"]{.class_4096} of Colossians 2:18 to be a saved man seeking to master passion and being unable to do it because his willpower wasn't strong enough. This is as far from the truth of the text as you can get. The seducer is an unsaved man who [HAS conquered his passions by willpower ]{.class_4095}and is trying to get the Christian to exchange true worship (John 4:24) [for worship of the human will ]{.class_4095}(Col. 2:23). Since this has been the seascape in all directions since we sailed off from the massacre of verse 8, ["the dream is certain, and the interpretation thereof sure" ]{.class_4096}(Dan. 2:45)!

Philosophy, tradition, and the rudiments of this world are the products of man's WILL. When they are [secularized,]{.class_4095} we find ourselves dealing with Plato, Aristotle, Hegel, Spinoza, Westcott, Hort, Schaff, Lightfoot, Kant, Horace Mann, Dewey, Einstein, Russell, Darwin, Gentile, Haeckel, Huxley, Freud, and Nietzsche. Where philosophy, tradition, and the rudiments are [spiritualized, ]{.class_4095}we find ourselves dealing with Abelard, Aquinas, Anselm, Fulton Sheen, Cardinal Newman, Fatima, Lourdes, Joan of Arc, St. Anthony, Peter the Hermit, Francis of Assisi, the Council of Trent, the ecumenical council, Oursler, A. J. Cronin, Norman Vincent Peale, Lloyd Douglas, Dean Weigle, Origen, Augustine, Calvin, and Oral Roberts.

The human will (Gen. 3:3--5) is the source of the entire array of depraved claptrap from the sons of God (Gen. 6:1--6) to the moon landings of 1969--1972. Controlling "physical passions" and fleshly indulgences is not the problem. The problem is the carnal ["fleshly mind" ]{.class_4096}(Col. 2:18) that ["is enmity against God"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 8:7), and this is the mind of the "old man," whether or not he controls, curbs, adjusts, directs, curtails, or frustrates "fleshly passions" and "fleshly indulgences." The [AV ]{.class_4095}text is correct and the [New American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_515}(1963) is just as corrupt as its twin sister---the [Revised Standard Version ]{.class_4095}of the National Council of Christian Churches.

You see, the second chapter of the Epistle to the Colossians is the great "negative stroke" (hippy cliché) against the American educational system in the last century preceding the Advent. Its rebuke of "Christian" education is as strong as its rebuke of [secular education, ]{.class_4095}and this accounts for the obvious meddling and tampering with the text that we find in all of the versions. Bible‑rejecting Christian "scholars" resent certain passages as deeply and as bitterly as Tom Paine and Bob Ingersoll ever resented other passages, and Colossians 2 is proof of this great spiritual truth. The second chapter of the Epistle to the Colossians undermines the foundations of Western and Eastern "education," and it will stand in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 text as a severe reprimand to the great educational‑cultured‑respectable-safe Christianity which arose from the "Day School" movement (1940--1980).

This respectable‑educational‑cultured‑nice‑safe Christianity is a new bird in the zoo. Not even the Christianity of Constantine and Eusebius could compete with it, for their Christianity at its worst was at least [masculine. ]{.class_4095}The new bird has a plumage which is plainly [feminine. ]{.class_4095}It is more the product of the ["devout\...women" ]{.class_4096}(Acts 13:50) and Noadiah (Neh. 6:14) than the product of a heathen King and a compromising historian.

In vain will the Bible believer search the New Testament for these "nice" Christians. Barnabas (Acts 11:22--24) is about as close to being "nice" as anyone, and he fellowships with a backslidden missionary (Acts 15:39) after his defection from Paul. He also is "drawn away with the dissimulation" that arises around Peter's conduct (see Gal. 2:13 and comments). New Testament Christianity is [dangerous ]{.class_4095}(Acts 14:19), [disreputable ]{.class_4095}(Acts 28:22), [despised ]{.class_4095}(Acts 13:50), [dumb ]{.class_4095}(John 7:15; Acts 4:13), and [denounced ]{.class_4095}(Acts 24:2--9).

We can only arrive at the conclusion that there is a radical and revolting difference between modern "Fundamentalism" (or "Conservatism") and Biblical Christianity. Although the gap may not be as great as that between Biblical Christianity and Roman Catholicism (or Seventh‑day Adventism or Liberalism), the gap is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_516}there nonetheless, and Colossians 2 was written to show you how, why, when, and where that gap began, and who created it. It began in the First Century, and its source was the [Alexandrian ]{.class_4095}scholars (Acts 17:18) and pagan Libertines (Acts 6:9) who were at heart PHILOSOPHERS. The [ascetics ]{.class_4095}among them tended to Gnosticism and the supernatural, and the [naturalists ]{.class_4095}among them tended to a belief in evolution and "situation ethics." In either class, the MIND and the WILL were made the objects of worship, exactly as they are presented by the National Education Association in America today.

The basic difference between a modern "Christian" education and this secular idolatry is that "Christian" education makes a [profession ]{.class_4095}of believing a dozen things extracted from [the Bible. ]{.class_4095}Five of these are found in the Roman Catholic creed of Nicea (A.D. 325). Premillennialists often add four more to this Catholic creed, but both groups spend most of their time attacking the Reformation Bible of 1611. This is apparent to anyone with any reading background in the versions. (See evidence in [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970.)

We leave Colossians 2 with the indelible impression that some great and terrible warnings have been proclaimed, and they have been spoken with feeling and certainty by an educated man who knew the dangers of his subject matter. "Christian philosophy" is to be found [in the book of Proverb;, ]{.class_4095}not the writings of Howison (1834--1916), Husserl (1859--1938), Royce (1855--1916), Bergson (1859--1941), Whitehead (1861--1947), Ayer (1910--1989), Sartre (1905--1980), Heidegger (1889--1976), and Bertrand Russell (1872--1970). A Christian "education" is obtained by PRACTICING Romans--Philemon; and if the Lord leads the Christian young man (or woman) to obtain secular degrees (B.A., B.S., M.A., Ph.D., etc.), he should pursue all of his courses in every curriculum throughout the entire period with one central thought dominating his studies: [where any scholar, writer, teacher, reviser, commentator, scientist, philosopher, or educator alters, changes, slights, ignores, revises, or misquotes the AV 1611, those remarks or writings are to be set aside exactly as you would set aside "Bullfinch's Mythology."]{.class_4095}

This should be done continuously with full knowledge that the motive behind any and all changes is [to replace the authority of the word of God with the authority of the TEACHER. ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3273.xhtml_page_517}(This has been the motive behind every change in the [AV ]{.class_4095}1611 from 1800 to the present date.) Bible believers in the Twentieth Century should approach [all forms of education ]{.class_4095}with the Bible as the infallible and absolute authority for judging [all forms. Christian education ]{.class_4095}is never to be "excepted"; and the approach of the real believer to the writings of Wuest, Thieme, Rice, Machen, Neal, Warfield, Payne, Robertson, Trench, Custer, Davidson, Vincent, Thayer, and the Conservative commentators should be the same approach that he uses in judging the writings of Voltaire, Rousseau, Spencer, Hobbes, Hume, Pierce, Mach, Fichte, Bentha, Schelling, Descartes, Bacon, Berkeley, Leibnitz, Comte, and Charles Darwin.

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml}

## Chapter 3 {.heading_116}

3:1 If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.

2 Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.

3 For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.

4 When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.

5 Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:

6 For which things' sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:

7 In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.

8 But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.

9 Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;

10 And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:

11 Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.

We now find the solution to the problem of "putting the flesh down." The entire chapter is [practical ]{.class_4095}and deals with the [positive approach ]{.class_4095}that the Christian should take in order to increase ["with the increase of God" ]{.class_4096}(2:19).

["If ye then be risen\...seek those things which are above\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). ["Set your affection ON THINGS ABOVE, not on THINGS ON THE EARTH" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2). The passage states further that one part of the Christian remained ["DEAD"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3) after the other part "arose" (see Rom. 7:4). ["The new man"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10) who []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_519}came up out of the grave with Christ (see Eph. 1:20, 2:6, and comments) is already ["above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 1); therefore, his affections should be located in that place, not the place where his dead corpse has been buried (see Rom. 6:1--6).

The passage is another touchstone which deeply grieves the "Christian" of the Twentieth Century. You see, ["affection" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2 cf. Gal. 5:24) deals with the heart, not the head. The puffed‑up fleshpots of Colossians 2:8 and 18 are majoring in the MIND, like the puffed‑up fleshpots who translated the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901). ["Where your treasure is, there will your heart be also" ]{.class_4096}(Matt. 6:21). The Lord is interested in the heart (Prov. 4:23) primarily, and the word always appears before the word ["mind"]{.class_4096} in any list, except when the "newer translations" are mishandling the words of truth (see Prov. 23:26 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}1972).

Now, how many ["things"]{.class_4096} can you name ["where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God"']{.class_4096}? If you cannot name any, how on earth are you going to obey the admonition to ["set your affection ON THINGS ABOVE"]{.class_4096}?[ ]{.class_4096}Another problem arises: since the editors of the ["New" Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_4095}(actually a revision of the [King James ]{.class_4095}text) cannot tell which direction is ["above"]{.class_4096} (see footnote on pg. 1261 of that "Bible") how do you even start to obey the admonition?

The corrupt [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) knocked Jesus Christ out of Heaven when He was on earth (see John 3:13), and our study of Ephesians 2:6 has shown us that many modern "Conservatives" wouldn't know ["heavenly places"]{.class_4096} from Broadway Joe's. Since Kenneth Wuest (Moody Bible Institute) is in agreement with the Roman Catholic version of John 3:13 (["Jerusalem Bible"]{.class_4095}), how is a seeker for truth going to find out what is going on? Well, we have found only one way out of the morass of Conservative, Liberal, and Catholic errors before, and we will take it again.

1\. New Jerusalem is ["above"]{.class_4096} (Gal. 4:26).

2\. The throne of God is ["above"]{.class_4095} (Rev. 4:1‑4).

3\. The departed saints are ["above" ]{.class_4095}(1 Thess. 4:14).

4\. Jesus Christ Himself is ["above"]{.class_4095} (1 Thess. 4:16).

5\. Our rewards at the Judgment Seat are ["above" ]{.class_4095}(2 Cor. 5:1--10).

6\. Many of our loved ones and relatives are ["above"]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_520} (1 Thess. 5:10).

7\. The eternal truth of God's eternal word is ["above"]{.class_4095} (Psa. 119:89).

8\. ["Above"]{.class_4096} is straight north beyond Alpha Draconis (Isa. 14:13; Psa. 48:2; Ezek. 1:4), and this direction is ["UP"]{.class_4096} (2 Cor. 12:1--4; Rev. 4:1).

["The things which God hath prepared for them that love him" ]{.class_4096}are ["REVEALED\...unto us by his Spirit" ]{.class_4096}(1 Cor. 2:9--10), and the feigned agnosticism and hypocritical piousness of the Conservative commentators in regard to these things is as sickening as the smell of the Lewis, Delaware, fish factory on a sultry day in August.

The old‑time Christians of America were a heavenly‑minded people, and Heaven was real to them. This is perfectly apparent from the "country songs" that survive in some of the hymnals from 1800--1900, and it is more than apparent in the sermons preached by DeWitt Talmage, Sam Jones, and J. Frank Norris. This heavenly anticipation and this love of the life "beyond" is nearly absent from the modern "Fundamentalist movement"; and only on rare occasions do we find evangelists, missionaries, pastors, or teachers (of any profession of faith) bragging, glorying in, rejoicing in, and magnifying the "things which are above" (vs. 1). Appeals to departed loved ones, triumphal entries, the glories of New Jerusalem, the fragrance of the garden of God, the happiness of the celestial inhabitants, and "land of fadeless day" are now considered to be "melodramatic," "tearjerkers," "mid‑Victorian," "sensationalism," and "appeals to emotions." The thing that Catholics and Liberals appreciate the most about Billy Graham is his lack of drama.

All unsaved people like their feelings connected with Hollywood, TV, magazines, and soap operas; they do not want God getting into their AFFECTIONS. As Bob Jones Sr. so aptly said: "The biggest trick the Devil ever pulled on this country he pulled when he took drama out of the pulpit and put it in the theater." The "new breed" of Christians (the nice-safe-respectable-cultured‑educated crowd of Col. 2:8 and 18) keep their affections [on earth ]{.class_4095}when they teach the Bible, and even when they exalt Jesus Christ. They have evidently []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_521}succeeded in "bringing Heaven down to earth" without having to die or go up in the Rapture! (See [Between Heaven and Earth, ]{.class_4095}Fieldhouse, 1970.)

The Christian, like Daniel, should always have his windows open toward (New) Jerusalem (Gal. 4:26). His look is an [upward ]{.class_4095}look (Luke 10:20) unto the Author and Finisher of his faith (Heb. 12:1--4). The day that Heaven ceases to be a real place to him, with a real population, located in a real location (John 14:1--3), is the day that he may hand his sword (Eph. 6:17) over to the ecumenical council of "Christian" churches and worship the Antichrist.

I know of a man living in Pensacola, Florida, who spent his lifetime looking for lost golf balls. He found 143 in one day, on one golf course, and found a total of 31,800 on four courses in a period of ten years. He must have spent a lot of time looking DOWN.

I heard of a young man who found \$5.00 on the ground one time, so he spent the next 30 years of his life hunting for five dollar bills---on the ground. At the end of thirty years, he had lost the glory of the sun and the sheen of the stars, the bursting blooms of springtime, the blaze of autumn's leafy pallet, the smiles of friends, and the confidences of loved ones. He had found 29,000 buttons, 50,000 cigarette butts, 4,000 pins, \$89.00 in change, \$120.00 in bills, 35 pocket combs, 20 empty billfolds, 12 pencils, 8 pens, and 600 "unmentionables." He inherited a bent back, a miserly disposition, and a set of trifocals.

["SET YOUR AFFECTION ON THINGS ABOVE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 2).

Every Christian is an "astronaut" in the truest sense of the word, and he is constantly engaged in research into "outer space." His life is dedicated to learning and knowing about things beyond the reach of the observatory at Palomar, and beside Cape Canaveral, Paul (2 Cor. 12:1--4) and John (Rev. 4:1--2) appear as supermen from outer space.

The Christian's real life is in Heaven, where he is seated with Christ ["in heavenly places" ]{.class_4096}(Eph. 2:5--6). The life that he lives here (Gal. 2:20) is only BY Christ and THROUGH Christ (see Phil. 2:13 and comments). (Kenneth Wuest's translation of the article "THE Christ" \[vs. 4\] means nothing since he refused to translate, the article "THE philosophy" in the preceding chapter.)

Williams' Hyperdispensational bias appears in his exposition of the text, for he limits the "things on earth" to "ordinances and sacraments\...religious rites." But this is an attempt to reserve the heart's affections for money, sex, power, popularity, sports, property, family, education, or something else ["on the earth."]{.class_4096}

Williams, as O'Hair, Stam, Bullinger, and Baker, has fallen into the snare of reading HEAD DOCTRINE into every passage. He mentions ordinances and sacraments here because he has taken the position that no Christian should be baptized in water, even though Peter, James, John, and Paul were! ["Things on the earth" ]{.class_4096}in the context covers a great deal more than some doctrinal hobbyhorse that Bullinger or Calvin trotted around the track. To tell the truth, the immediate context (vs. 5) is on things that the flesh enjoys DOING and enjoys GETTING.

The [Wycliffe]{.class_4095} commentator (Dr. Ellis) gives us the original Alexandrian perversion of the great Bible‑rejecting "conservative"---Origen (A.D.185--254). In this highly fanciful (and pagan) speculation, the ["things above" ]{.class_4096}and the ["things on the earth" ]{.class_4096}are privately interpreted to mean "the new age of total Christianity" compared with the "old age of legalism and ritualism." Dr. Ellis is careful to warn his reader (pg. 1342) that "SPATIAL" relationships are not what Paul has in mind. That is, OUTER SPACE and distance in the universe are NOT the subject under discussion, according to the [Wycliffe Commentary.]{.class_4095}

[But that is exactly what the subject is, both here and in Ephesians 3:18, which see. ]{.class_4095}SPATIAL relationships are precisely what Paul is speaking of, and SPATIAL distances (with SPATIAL objects in SPATIAL locations) is the direct intention of the Holy Spirit in writing the passage. Note: ["WHERE Christ sitteth\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). This is a [geographical location ]{.class_4095}in outer space (2 Cor. 12:1--3; Heb 12:1--3; 1:1--3; Acts 2:33--34) and is as "spatial" a reference as longitude and latitude on this planet. Dr. Ellis---as other dead-orthodox apostates---has been tricked into thinking that "science" is the authority on "spatial" relationships in dealing with space and the universe (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_4095}1969, Gen. 1--3).

Verses which will shed light on the passage are found in the [AV ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_523}1611 text of Philippians 1:23, 3:20; Ephesians 1:3, 2:6, 4:22; Matthew 6:19--20; John 12:26; 2 Corinthians 5:14--15; and 1 Corinthians 3:23.

["When Christ, who is our life, shall APPEAR, then shall ye also appear with him IN GLORY" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4). For a change, the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}has an excellent exposition of the passage. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have no trouble with the passage, and the comments by Clarke and Lange are as sound as these gentlemen ever make when dealing with the [Authorized]{.class_4095} text. The reader will notice that the Lord's "appearance" (Gk.: ["phaneroo") ]{.class_4095}is the same word used in 1 Peter 5:4; 1 John 3:2, 2:28; Titus 2:13; 2 Timothy 1:10, 4:1, 8; and 1 Timothy 6:14. This is "most embarrassing" for Bullinger, for all Bullingerites believe in a "rapture of the one Body" ("the Body of the mystery," etc.), and yet, the [term for this "rapture" ]{.class_4095}is found in the Hebrew‑Christian Epistles. Bullinger taught that the passages in the Hebrew‑Christian Epistles were NOT for "the Church of the one Body"! (George Ladd, a dead‑orthodox Conservative, takes advantage of this confusion in his book "[The Blessed Hope]{.class_4095}" and uses it to prove that the Rapture and the Advent are [simultaneous.]{.class_4095})

But the "manifestation" of 1 John 3:1--3, as here, is the time when ["we shall see him,"]{.class_4096} and this "manifestation" (["When Christ\...shall appear\..."]{.class_4096}) is like the post‑Resurrection manifestations of 1 Corinthians 15:4--8. (Note that the same Greek word is used in Mark 16:12, 14.) There are appearances to the WORLD (Rev. 1:7; Zech. 12:10). (We should always allow the English text to correct the errors of men like George Ladd who are out to destroy the authority of the word of God with the Alexandrian "Greek" of dead orthodoxy.)

We appear with Christ ["IN GLORY" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 4) as Moses and Elijah did (Matt. 17:3; Mark 9:4). This is ["IN GLORY" ]{.class_4096}(2 Pet. 1:17) at a rapture, not "ON EARTH" at an Advent (Zech. 14). Where the Greek [obscures ]{.class_4095}the sense (notice the change of the Greek word for "appear" between Matt.17:3 and Mark 16:12), the English of 1611 can often clarify the meaning. Phenomenon No. 10.

["Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). This "putting to death" is taken by Alexandria and Rome to mean "penance" or "asceticism." Origen used the passage with Matthew 5:30 as a proof text for emasculating himself. Others []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_524}have used the verses for flagellation, mutilation (1 Kings 18:28), isolation from society, and every ghastly form of sadism and masochism possible to imagine. The Roman Catholic Martin Luther "mortified his members" before he became a Christian, and the names of Monks, Nuns, and other ascetics are legion (Mark 5:4--9!) who have tried to gain Heaven or please God by abusing themselves and others. To this day, the Roman Church has an income of several thousand dollars annually from the sale of coarse clothing (like "sack cloth"), braided whips, crowns of thorns, and chains to "penitents" who wish to "mortify the deeds of the flesh."

But the mortification of which the apostle speaks is done by two operations, and [neither one ]{.class_4095}involves corporal punishment or self‑abuse.

1\. The "[AFFECTION]{.class_4096}" (not "mind"---as in the apostate [New American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}recommended by Bob Jones University, Greenville, SC) is placed on heavenly things instead of earthly things. (The alteration of "[affection]{.class_4096}" to "mind"---[ASV]{.class_4095}, [RSV, ]{.class_4095}Charlie Brown, [RV, New ASV, ]{.class_4095}Mickey Mouse, and Donald Duck---is quite typical of the "heart motives" behind the new versions \[see comments under 2:23\].)

2\. The "putting off" of ["the old man" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 9) is done by simply refusing to honor his requests (Rom. 6:6--14). This is not done by drawing up a check list of regulations (see 2:21--23 and comments), but is done by yielding to the Holy Spirit (Gal. 5:22 and comments) and allowing the Holy Spirit to subvert the flesh (Gal. 5:5--21; see also Rom. 8:13, 13:14; Eph. 4:21--22).

Most of the words used in verse 5 are self-explanatory. ["Inordinate affection" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5) is a reference to love that has "gone to seed." What was affection becomes ["unnatural atffection"]{.class_4095} (2[ ]{.class_4095}Tim 3:1--4), and "lovingkindness" becomes ["lustful passion."]{.class_4095} The[ ]{.class_4095}sin is quite common among married couples, and it is the logical outcome of a sex‑saturated society where "love" is interpreted (24 hours a day) as meaning "an exchange of flesh." This is no overstatement of the matter, for the careful observer of this type of degeneration will have noticed the word [SEX]{.class_4095} is now universally accepted to mean something that the dictionary never intended it to mean. Webster said: "either of the two divisions of organisms" or "the character of []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_525}being male or female" or "the attraction between sexes."

However, the current filth publication by Dr. David Reubin is an omnibus of: "What you always wanted to know about [fornication, adultery, birth control, intercourse, perversion, irregularity, ]{.class_4095}menopause, and [excess ]{.class_4095}in regard to [fleshly contact with one or more bodies." ]{.class_4095}The word [SEX]{.class_4095} in Reubin's publication---as in any College periodical, pornographic magazine, TV program, modern novel, or street conversation---means an ACT involving the [sex organs ]{.class_4095}of male or female. This propaganda has been brainwashing the American public for more than fifty years, and the result of it is a conception of marriage where one climax and thrill must precede another (in the fleshly sense) until ["inordinate affection" ]{.class_4096}is part of the marriage bed.

Ninety percent of the women who seek divorces on grounds of "cruelty" complain about this type of thing. And it seldom occurs to these women that the depraved passions of men that lead to irregularities and perversion are the same passions that lead women to fits of jealousy, extreme covetousness, and self‑worship. Observe how neatly the Holy Spirit has inserted ["covetousness, which is idolatry" ]{.class_4096}right in the same verse with ["inordinate affection, evil concupiscence" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 5).

The last term---["evil concupiscence"]{.class_4096}---refers to shameful, unholy, or hurtful desires that greedily seek to satisfy the ["inordinate affection." ]{.class_4096}Although Wuest has altered ["covetousness"]{.class_4096} to "[avarice]{.class_4095}" and the [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}has altered it to "[ruthless greed]{.class_4095}," the Holy Spirit has still preserved the correct set of crossreferences (Exod. 20:17; Rom. 7:7) in the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible[. ]{.class_4095}Phillips tells us that the sin of "[lust for other people's goods]{.class_4095}" is "[as serious a sin AS idolatry", ]{.class_4095}but of course, that is an attempt to justify the sin somewhat. Covetousness IS idolatry, according to the inspired writer; it is not "as" or "like" anything---it IS [idolatry.]{.class_4095} (The careful reader will observe that this time, none of the "article‑maniacs" translated the article before ["covetousness."]{.class_4096} Further, they did not translate it as "your covetousness" or "HIS covetousness." We mention this because back in Colossians 2:8, when we came upon the word ["philosophy"]{.class_4096} with [the article, ]{.class_4095}the modern editors refused to translate the article---as the [King James]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_526}---but then inserted "his" where no "his" was in any manuscript. With the same thing occurring here, why did not our "consistent" [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [New A]{.class_4095}SV interpolate "his" or "your" before ["covetousness"]{.class_4096}? Phenomenon No. 4.)

No man can put ["covetousness"]{.class_4096} to death unless he actively opposes the "lusts and affections" (see Gal. 5:24 and comments) by applying the cross to them (Gal. 6:14). This is the "crucified life," set forth so well in the writings of Andrew Murray, Ruth Paxson, E. L. Maxwell, and Watchman Nee.

To counteract every effort of the Christian to gain mastery over the flesh, Satan has at last produced the greatest lust‑machine the world has ever known. It is called "Tell A Vision" (see Matt. 17:9!). The vision of desirable objects is now commonplace, worldwide, continual, and in "living color" so the "lust cycle" may continue in the breast of man, woman, and child, black or white, young or old, 24 hours a day. We shall not spend a great deal of time commenting here, but the reader should refer to the remarks under Genesis 20:9--10 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis ]{.class_4095}(1969). Commercial advertising is based on the idea that you can create a feeling of "need" where there is none, and thus it appeals to the basic instinct of the depraved pagan---IDOLATRY. Covetousness IS idolatry, according to the text. When it comes to idolatry, the difference between the African cannibal in the Congo and the business executive of the Chase-Manhattan National Bank is that one [has money ]{.class_4095}and the other [doesn't.]{.class_4095}

The Christian used to "live" in these things (vs. 7), and he ["walked"]{.class_4096} in them[ ]{.class_4095}(vs. 7) because he was spiritually "in the flesh" as well as geographically (see comments under 2:11--12). At that time, he was a "child of disobedience" (vs. 6; cf. Eph. 5:6), and the wrath of God abided on him (John 3:36). To prevent this wrath from falling on him anew after his conversion (1 Cor. 3--6), the Christian is told to put the former practices away (Rom. 8:1, 13). When these things are committed (see Gal. 5:19--21 and comments), they should be [judged ]{.class_4095}(1 Cor. 11:31--32), [confessed]{.class_4095} (I John 1:9), and [put away---]{.class_4095}["But now ye also PUT OFF ALL THESE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8).

Several alterations have been made in the passage (vss. 5--7) by the dead‑orthodox apostates.

1\. The sterile [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_527}(1961) has toned down the ["wrath" ]{.class_4096}of verse 6 to "God's dreadful judgment," in keeping with its complete removal of that word from the Bible.

2\. The [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946) and the Jehovah's Witness Bible (the [New World Translation]{.class_4095}) have stricken ["the children of disobedience" ]{.class_4096}out of the text altogether. This was done on the advice of Tregelles, Tischendorf, Alford, Lightfoot, Westcott, Hort, and other destructive critics of the Bible (see [Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_4095} Vol.[ ]{.class_4095}20, pg. 149). Kenneth Wuest has gone along with this destructive criticism, but the intelligent student of manuscript evidence may be interested to know that the [King James ]{.class_4095}reading is found in every family of Greek manuscripts known to man. It is only in the isolated Alexandrian corruptions of Origen that we find the words omitted (P46 and [Vaticanus]{.class_4095}), and also in the Coptic and Ethiopic translations of Africa. The Western, Syrian, Hesychian, and Caesarean manuscripts read as the [King James]{.class_4095} Bible.

3\. The [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}(G. G. Findlay) presents the reader with one of those rare and incredible examples of naivete for which [AV ]{.class_4095}critics are famous. It tells us that the sins of verse 5 were the vices of [Colossian Gentiles]{.class_4095}\![ ]{.class_4095}And taking the past‑historicizing tradition further, we are told that these vices were only for ["some of them at least"]{.class_4095}! I wonder where Brother Findlay lived in the threescore and ten years allotted to him on earth? England? America? Europe? Does he really expect us to think that the vices of verse 5 were the vices of COLOSSIAN GENTILES? Why not American or English Christians?

What is all this infernal blabbing about past history unless it is a desperate attempt by the writer to dislocate the truth from his own person and make ["the]{.class_4096} [word of God of none effect"]{.class_4096} (Mark 7:13) by directing it at someone else?! You may be certain that some of the vices listed in verse 5 were practiced by [the religious leaders of every group of Christians on the face of this earth from A.D. ]{.class_4095}100--2000. Is there a Christian on earth who has not coveted something forbidden to him of God? If Paul was guilty (Acts 22:18, 21), are YOU innocent?

["But now ye also PUT OFF ALL THESE" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 8). Observe the numerous mentions of the commandment:

1\. ["Put off all these"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_528} (vs. 8).

2\. ["Put off the old man"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9).

3\. ["Put on the new man"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10).

4\. ["Put on therefore"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12).

5\. ["Put on charity" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 14)

6.[ ]{.class_4095}["Put on the whole armour of God"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 6:11).

7\. ["Put on the new man"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 4:24).

8\. ["Put off concerning the former conversation"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 4:22).

The text of verse 8 is self‑explanatory and should be clear to anyone. ["Filthy communication" ]{.class_4096}covers anything omitted under ["blasphemy." ]{.class_4096}Dirty jokes, lewd stories, hurtful gossip, and obscene talk of any kind is verbotten. ["If it's doubtful it's dirty" ]{.class_4095}is the rule for the Christian, and no amount of brainwashing about "sex being clean," "God instituting sex," "meaningful sexual relations," and "sexual maturity" can disguise the modern filth peddled by SIECUS and the National Education Association. God created [male and female ]{.class_4095}and honored [the marriage relationship ]{.class_4095}(Heb. 13:2--4), but modern sex educators are making no reference to either of these things when they say "sex" (see comments under vs. 5).

Six items are to be discarded exactly as clothes are taken off and hung up in the closet: 1. ["anger,"]{.class_4096} 2. ["wrath,"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}3. ["malice,"]{.class_4096} 4. ["blasphemy,"]{.class_4096} 5. ["filthy communication,"]{.class_4096}[ ]{.class_4095}6.[ "lying" ]{.class_4095}(vs. 9). (Cf. Eph. 4:14--15 and 1 Tim. 1:6.) These are the deeds of ["the old man"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9) who was crucified, dead, and buried with Christ, having received the recompense of judicial judgment from God and falling under it. The Christian is still "housed" in this living "dead man," and the carcass of its putrefying corpse is his "vessel" until the redemption of the [body ]{.class_4095}(Rom. 8:23, 7:24). Hence, this corpse cannot actually be "[put off]{.class_4096}' in the sense of getting rid of him completely; however, since the Christian's soul has been cut loose [from this corpse ]{.class_4095}on the inside (see comments under 2:11--12), he can "put it off' in the sense of refusing to listen to it, refusing to obey it (Rom. 6:11--16), and refusing to satisfy its demands.

The [New English Bible ]{.class_4095}reading (["discarded the old nature"]{.class_4095}) is more than an "unfortunate rendering" (dead‑orthodox cliché); it is a direct denial of the fundamental truth of the two natures of the believer. [No believer has discarded his old nature, ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_529}and if a Christ-ian thinks he has, he is a liar and "the truth is not in him" (1 John 1:8--10). The old nature is present until you trip over the lid of your coffin or leave the solar system behind at the Rapture. Nor does the "[stripping off of the personality"]{.class_4095} (Jehovah's Witness Bible) have anything to do with anything in the Pauline Epistles. A "personality" is the total effect of a person on his environment and social contacts; this effect can be favorable and positive while the person is a God‑defying, Christ‑rejecting, Bible‑denying, Hell‑bound sinner: e.g., Pontius Pilate.

The term is ["put off," ]{.class_4096}as in: ["I'm not ready yet; put him off a little longer."]{.class_4095} The term is ["put on,"]{.class_4096} as in: ["Aw, cut it out, you're jes' puttin' on!" ]{.class_4095}"The old man" is the "God of the vessel" [in the United States Navy, ]{.class_4095}and the "old man" is the C.O. of a rifle company [in the United States Army. ]{.class_4095}The terminology of the [King James ]{.class_4095}1611 is a good bit ahead of its nearest 35 rivals, and the reader should retain it and discard them lest he get something "archaic" (like the [New American Standard Version)]{.class_4095}!

Kenneth Wuest has a misleading exposition of the text. The redoubtable "Teacher Emeritus" of Moody Bible Institute tells us [(Expanded Translation,]{.class_4095} pg. 473) that ["the]{.class_4096} [old man" ]{.class_4096}of the text is the person you were "before you were saved" and "[the]{.class_4096} [new man" ]{.class_4096}is the person you are "after you are saved." This is not quite the truth. ["The OLD MAN"]{.class_4096} was old man Adam (1 Cor. 15:22) and ["the NEW MAN"]{.class_4096} is Jesus Christ Himself (1 Cor. 15:47). This is perfectly clear from the deliberate statement of the Holy Spirit: ["the NEW MAN, which is renewed in knowledge AFTER THE IMAGE OF HIM that created him" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10).

In Colossians 1:15 and 3:10, we thoroughly covered the truth of this statement by comparing the verses with Hebrews 1:2--3 and 2 Corinthians 4:4, which see. ["The old man"]{.class_4096} was[ "put off" ]{.class_4096}by God Himself at Colossians 2:9--12, and ["the new man" ]{.class_4096}is sinless (1 John 3:9) for he has been born of the Spirit (John 3:5--7) and ["is one spirit"]{.class_4096} with the Lord Himself (1 Cor. 6:17). The mistaken notion of Kenneth Wuest came from refusing to observe the next verse (vs. 11), which reads: ["Where there is neither Greek nor Jew\...."]{.class_4096} This ["new man" ]{.class_4096}is Jesus Christ, for the next statement says: ["but Christ is ALL, AND IN ALL" ]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_530}(cf. Eph. 1:23). The putting on of a new "character" or "personality" or "what you are since you got saved" is found in verses 12 to 17, [not verses 9 and 10]{.class_4101}. It does not take a Ph.D. to see that verse 11 is the matchmate to 1 Corinthians 12:13 and Galatians 3:28. In any of the three contexts ["the new man" ]{.class_4096}is Jesus Christ Himself. One would think that since most of the dead‑orthodox Conservatives make the ["creature"]{.class_4096} of 2 Corinthians 5:17 a "creation" (i.e., the Body, the Church), they would have had no trouble with the text. Williams has no trouble getting the point and says that ["the new man"]{.class_4096} is in the heavens (Commentary, pg. 940). "[The]{.class_4096} [new man" ]{.class_4096}is Jesus Christ in the believer, who has joined the Spirit of the believer to the one Body: ["Where there is neither Greek nor Jew."]{.class_4096}

A. S. Peake ([Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095}, pg. 539) discards the whole doctrinal truth on the grounds that a repetition of the word ["Christ"]{.class_4096} in verse 11 "would be a strange tautology" if Christ were referred to in verse 10. Readers stupid enough to mistake this kind of nonsense for "scholarship" should get a good dose of "tautology" in Ephesians 1:17--23 and 2:5--7. "Tautology" (where the person of Christ is at issue) is Paul's [habitual method of thinking and writing, ]{.class_4095}and it is a crying shame that a man versed in manuscript evidence and the voluminous works of Ellicott, Lightfoot, Alford, Davidson, Haupt, Lipsius, Westcott and Hort, Winer, Moulton, Meyer, Olshausen, et al., cannot even identify the style of the writer he is reading.

["The new man"]{.class_4096} in the believer is ["renewed"]{.class_4096} (vs. 10) from day to day (2 Cor. 4:16), and Peake's absurd remark that "there is no reference to a restoration to a former state" is as far off base as any man could get without leaving the ball park. "Restoration to a former state" [is the whole point of the passage, ]{.class_4095}and only where Adam is [not ]{.class_4095}replaced in the sinner with Christ (1 Cor. 15:47) is there no restoration to a "former state." Adam is a SON of God (Luke 3:38), and that is exactly what every believer is in Jesus Christ (John 1:12--13). Romans 5 is a lengthy dissertation on this fundamental Bible truth, and Peake's spurious comment is as out of place in an [Expositor's Greek Testament ]{.class_4095}as a grease monkey is in the Taj Mahal.

Unsaved people are not ["renewed\...after the IMAGE OF HIM that created him" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 10); they are made after []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_531}["the similitude of God" ]{.class_4096}(James 3:9). Unsaved men enter the world in Adam's image---as Seth (Gen. 5:1--3)---and their dead spirits must be ["renewed\...after the IMAGE OF HIM that created" "the new man."]{.class_4096}

["But Christ is all, and in all" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 11). Notice the remarkable double application given by the Holy Spirit and preserved in the [King James ]{.class_4095}text in spite of the bungling of the Conservative commentators and Fundamental critics.

1\. ["CHRIST IS ALL." ]{.class_4096}He is the Body into which the believer is placed, and this Body is a new creation (see Eph. 1:23 and comments).

2\. ["CHRIST\...IN ALL."]{.class_4096} He is the regenerator within the individual believer who restores the image which Adam lost when he sinned. This image is plainly the Lord Jesus Christ Himself, and this is exactly what Paul told us in the epistle (1:27).

3:12 Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;

13 Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.

14 And above all these things put on charity, which is the bond of perfectness.

15 And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.

16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.

17 And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.

Now (and only now) does the Holy Spirit begin to deal with "personalities," "character," and "the person you used to be," etc. The DEEDS of ["the old man"]{.class_4096} are in verses 8 and 9, with []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_532}["the old man"]{.class_4096} identified in verse 9. ["The elect of God" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 12; see comments under Eph. 1:5, 11). ["Bowels of mercies"]{.class_4096} (vs. 12; see Phil. 1:8 and comments).

Verse 15 is self‑explanatory. ["The peace of God"]{.class_4096} is to [rule ]{.class_4095}and [reign ]{.class_4095}in the affections of the believer (see Rom. 5:1, 10 and 2 Cor. 5:18--21). The substitution of "umpire" ([Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_4095}pg. 153) and ["control"]{.class_4095} (Jehovah's Witness [New World Translation) ]{.class_4095}and ["be judge"]{.class_4095} ([Good News for Modern Man]{.class_4095})[ ]{.class_4095}and ["arbiter"]{.class_4095} ([New English Bible]{.class_4095}) means nothing except that confusion is the rule (and the reaping) for the critics of the [AV]{.class_4095}.

Williams tries hard to bolster this sagging conjecture of the men who resent the [AV ]{.class_4095}reading, but his attempts to make the ruler an "umpire" (Williams Commentary, pg. 940) look like a midget trying to play center with the Globe Trotters. ["The peace of God"]{.class_4096} does not have to "umpire" any disputes [in your heart; ]{.class_4095}He (Eph. 2:14)should RULE your heart so friction with the brethren (vss. 12--13) can be avoided.

This Greek "nugget" ("umpire") is quite typical of lists given by Wuest and Trench for the purpose of destroying the Reformation []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_533}text. The process consists of the following:

Williams has an excellent comment on the verse (Commentary, pg. 940), and since it is very simply worded, none of the commentators go far astray on it.

However, one should never underestimate the inability of the scholars when it comes to plain English or Greek. The excuse given for this omission is that the word ["crept in from Ephesians 5:20": ]{.class_4095}"[Thanks to God AND the Father "]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). ["Unto God AND the Father"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 5:20).

Now, what do you suppose it is that makes a professing Christian think that if a Bible writer says something twice the same way, it "has crept in" from somewhere? Doesn't this remind you of all that burping and belching about "the two documents" and "the Aramaic gospel" and the "Q document" and "Deutero-Isaiah" and "Luke and Matthew using Mark" and Mark using his undershirt or a paper towel or something like that?! Then, in Colossians 3:17, with the [same writer ]{.class_4095}writing to the [same audience ]{.class_4095}(Gentile converts in Asia Minor) about the [same subject matter ]{.class_4095}(cf. Col. 3:17 with Eph. 5:20), we are to believe that the same word "CREPT IN"! Well, if it crept in, it "crept in" with enough company to break down the front door.

The Western manuscripts ("[D]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_539}") said it was "in the original." [Seidelianus ]{.class_4095}manuscript says it is part of the Greek text. Where did Acts 8:37 "creep in from" in the [Textus Receptus?]{.class_4095} Isn't it more logical to believe that it was ERASED at Alexandria from Constantine's vellum manuscripts? (See [The Christian's Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_4095}1970.)

Well, if ["kai" ]{.class_4095}crept in from Ephesians 5:20, John 1:18 (Nestle's Greek Text) slithered in from Hell, and the cowardice of Jamieson, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_540}Fausset, Brown, Dummelow, Williams, and Bullinger (yes, Bullinger!) in failing to condemn this obvious corruption of the text is unexplainable.
