J. Ray, are taken [from Vaticanus and Sinaiticus.]{.class_4095}

You will do well to correct all Greek manuscripts with the English text of 1611 and ignore Conservative crackpots who worship the Devil's "Greek text."

What the Christian says or does (speech or action), is to be said and done ["in the name of the Lord Jesus"]{.class_4096};[ ]{.class_4096}and when he says or does it, he is to give ["thanks to God and the Father" ]{.class_4096}by Jesus Christ. This means that anything the Christian cannot truthfully thank God for is not to be indulged in (see Rom. 14:22 for the same thought). This will take care of ["those things that are pleasing in his sight" ]{.class_4096}(1 John 3:22), and such conduct (1 Cor. 10:33) goes considerably beyond the mere "keeping of commandments" (Rom. 8:3--5). The "doubtful things" of Romans 14:23 can be settled by applying the text. Try it the next time something "doubtful" shows up.

18 Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.

19 Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them.

20 Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_541}well pleasing unto the Lord.

21 Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged.

22 Servants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:

23 And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;

24 Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.

25 But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.

["Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). See comments under Ephesians 5:22, and especially Proverbs 31 in[ The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs ]{.class_4095}(1972).

Typical of the ["past‑historicizing" ]{.class_4095}tendencies of[ ]{.class_4095}Bible adulterators (see 3:5 and comments) is the effort of Dr. Ellis [(Wycliffe Commentary) ]{.class_4095}to place the admonitions [back in the First Century. ]{.class_4095}Why any man in his right mind would limit discussions of "the conduct of the household" to Jewish and pagan writers of the B.C. era and Paul's time is just too much. The "conduct of the household" has been a subject of discussion for every major writer and philosopher who ever lived, from Job to the Kinsey Report. But as we have said before, this is very typical.

Thinking like that of Dr. Ellis' breathes through every line of the dead‑orthodox commentators, even where they do not intend to give away their feelings about the matter. It is SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) when approaching the Bible to [relegate it to the past ]{.class_4095}to get rid of its [present authority. ]{.class_4095}This accounts for the numerous comments similar to Dr. Ellis' which one will find throughout thousands of Bible expositions.

"The conduct of the household" has run second only to politics or religion as the main subject of conversation among people since Eve messed up the household and Adam followed her leadership. The "conduct of the household" is found in more than fifty verses in []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_542}Proverbs, which was written more than five centuries before the "pagan writers" learned how to commit their thoughts to writing.

Once again, our "creeping creepers" are telling us that something "crept in" from Ephesians 5:22. This time, the culprit is the word ["own"]{.class_4096} (see Jamieson, Eausset, and Brown, Commentary, pg. 1326), which is missing from the Alexandrian manuscripts of Origen and Eusebius. However, the majority of Greek manuscripts read as the [King James. ]{.class_4095}One can see, at a reading, that the word ["own"]{.class_4096} is an excellent forethought. The ["husbands"]{.class_4096} are not merely "your husbands" (see John 4:18; Rom. 7:1--3), but[ "your OWN HUSBANDS," ]{.class_4096}as in 1 Corinthians 7:2.

Mature Christians, who have learned to ["search the scriptures" ]{.class_4096}(John 5:39), will have already noticed the ["married wives"]{.class_4096} in Luke 17:27, where the "wives" were [other men's wives! ]{.class_4095}["Your OWN husbands" ]{.class_4096}is obviously the intention of the apostle, and it is unfortunate that the word crept OUT of the corrupt Alexandrian counterfeits.

["As it is fit" ]{.class_4096}matches the ["help MEET"]{.class_4096} of Genesis 2:18, implying that it is "fitting and proper" for them to do this and it "meets" the proper requirements. (The word "mate" is kin to ["meet"]{.class_4096}; hence, helpmate.)

["Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 19; see 1 Pet. 3:7 and Eph. 5:25--33). Bitterness is a root (Heb. 12:15) which can spring up at a moment's notice, and women seem to have a talent for cultivating the plant. Very few American men have not been driven to distraction (at some time) by the peculiar "feminine irrationale" which refuses to face facts, distorts information, makes unreasonable demands, causes unnecessary expenditures, and makes decisions on the spur of the moment according to intuition instead of common sense. European men (especially German and Russian) and Japanese men very rarely spoil their daughters; consequently, they make better wives than American women make. The things that cause "bitterness" in the European and Japanese home are rarely the same sources of trouble in the American home, where "women's liberation" has been going on since "women's emancipation." (For a lengthy series of illustrations dealing with the problem, see the comments in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_543}1972, on Prov. 9:13, 11:16, 12:4, 21:9, 19, 27:15, and 30:20.)

["Children, obey your parents in all things"]{.class_4096} (vs. 20 cf. Eph. 6:1 and comments). (Detailed analysis in Prov. 8:32, 17:6, 20:11, 22:6, 23:13, and 29:15 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972.) ["All things,"]{.class_4096} of course, refers to any commandment not clearly contrary to the word of God. For a reverse, notice 1 Kings 15:12--14.

Now the Alexandrian scribes decide to do a little "creeping," so they "creep" in the word "IN" ("IN the Lord") from Ephesians 6:1, which see. This revised TEXT (not "version") is used by the [RV, ASV]{.class_4095}, [RSV, ]{.class_4095}the [New ASV]{.class_4095}, and other corrupt Vatican productions recommended by John Rice and Wilbur Smith in the Twentieth Century.

["Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 21; see Eph. 6:4 and comments; also Prov. 22:6, 15 and 23:13 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972).

["Servants, obey in all things your MASTERS\...fearing God" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 22). Suddenly, the "highly accurate" [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1963) forget all the Greek they learned in 59 years and decide that the [AV ]{.class_4095}has the right reading after all! Instead of "slaves" (Greek manuscripts---["doulos"]{.class_4095}), they decide to say ["servants"]{.class_4096}! Phenomenon No. 3. Not even the [Amplified ]{.class_4095}can amplify enough amplifications to find the "original Greek" this time, and due to its racial and religious prejudice, it also refuses to translate ["doulos"]{.class_4095} as [slave]{.class_4095}.

However, Phillips[, Good News,]{.class_4095} and the [New English Bible]{.class_4095}---with the apostate [RSV ]{.class_4095}(1946)---give the word[ "SLAVE" ]{.class_4095}exactly as it appears in all the Greek lexicons used by the committees of the [RV, ASV]{.class_4095}, and [New ASV]{.class_4095}. The word means "bondman," LITERALLY, so no ministerial student should be kidded into thinking that a "more literal translation" was the true motive behind the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [New ASV]{.class_4095}. They simply translated "literally" where they thought best, and where they lacked the guts to translate literally---Colossians 2:8 and 2:16, for example---they ignored the matter as neatly as any of their opponents or competitors ignored it. Phenomenon No. 4.

Observe how the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_544}(Dr. Findlay) refused to translate ["with eyeservice" ]{.class_4096}literally, but instead put "with ACTS of eyeservice." If the critics of the [AV ]{.class_4095}take these liberties, how do they fancy that they can criticize the [AV ]{.class_4095}for taking the same liberties? (You cannot do it in [court; ]{.class_4095}you can only do it in a "Conservative" school.)

Since the oldest Papyrus (P46) agrees with the [AV ]{.class_4095}text this time on ["fearing God,"]{.class_4096} it[ ]{.class_4096}is shelved, and the corrupt Vatican reading has been inserted (["fearing the Lord"]{.class_4095}) in the [ASV, RSV, RV, New ASV, ]{.class_4095}Phillips[, ]{.class_4095}Weymouth, Moffatt, Goodspeed, Findlay, Ellis, Wil-liams, etc. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown blithely remark that the "oldest manuscripts" read[ "Lord."]{.class_4095} However, the oldest manuscript this time is Papyrus 46, which does NOT say ["Lord"]{.class_4095}; it says ["GOD"]{.class_4095} as the [Authorized Version ]{.class_4095}of 1611. (Naughty, Naughty!) Phenomenon No. 17.

However, we have something much more reliable to go by than some vacillating papyrus, for Paul's style is ["the fear of God," ]{.class_4096}not ["the fear of the Lord."]{.class_4101} Note 2 Corinthians 7:1; Romans 3:18; and Ephesians 5:21. What is the "style of the writer"? Well, his style is ["THE FEAR OF GOD."]{.class_4096}

The expression ["the fear of the Lord" ]{.class_4095}is an Old Testament expression, and Paul drops it exactly as he drops ["the Son of man" ]{.class_4095}when speaking of the risen Saviour. The insertion of this archaic expression---"the fear of the Lord"---is as dispensationally out of place in Paul's mouth as a quotation from Cassius Clay.

Furthermore, this is so obvious that one can only account for the appearance of the substitution in the writings of Kenneth Wuest and the [Confraternity ]{.class_4095}Bible of Rome on the grounds that these people (and any major commentator from 1880 to 2000) had an idolatrous reverence for the Vatican manuscripts which completely blinded them to the simplest principles of manuscript evidence. The constant reference to [Vaticanus ]{.class_4095}and [Sinaiticus ]{.class_4095}by the [AV ]{.class_4095}critics as "the best manuscripts" or "the better manuscripts," for example, shows how spiritually blind these men are. [Vaticanus and Sinaiticus are two of the most botched, bungled, and written‑over manuscripts ever discovered; see Appendix to The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence,]{.class_4095} 1970.

Leaving the "blind to lead the blind," we now turn to infallible truth.

1\. [Bondservants ]{.class_4095}are still found in the dispensation of Grace.

2\. They are [returned to their masters ]{.class_4095}when they escape (e.g., Philemon).

3\. They are commanded to [obey their masters]{.class_4095} (1 Tim. 6:1--3).

4\. They are told to [stay in servitude ]{.class_4095}if they have a chance to escape (1 Cor. 7:20--22)!

5\. They are to serve God in their capacity as [slaves ]{.class_4095}(1 Cor. 7:22--24).

6\. They are to do their service ["AS TO THE LORD, and not unto men" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 23).

7.[ ]{.class_4095}They will be rewarded by the Lord at the Judgment Seat of Christ (vs. 24).

8\. They will suffer for wrongdoing exactly as anyone else (vs. 25), and their abject condition or poverty will not influence the Lord one way or another in dealing with them (Deut. 1:17). (Some people think that the only people who should have "civil rights" are people who won't work and who live off the government.)

The great Laodicean campaign for the "underdog" (see Rev. 3:14 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation,]{.class_4095} 1970) is teaching that special privileges and special rights and special dispensations should be given to revolutionaries, bums, loafers, jailbirds, the insane, and the immoral [at the expense ]{.class_4095}of the lawabiding, working majority. But God will charge no one for your "expenses" but His Son, and you will give account for your expenses whether you are a slave or the Secretary of the United Nations. ["THERE IS NO RESPECT OF PERSONS" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 25, see comments under Eph. 6:8--9; and 1 Pet. 1:17 with Gal. 2:6.)

["The reward of the inheritance" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 24) is the Millennial reign (see Eph. 5:5; Gal. 5:21; and 1 Cor. 6:9). Since the writer of the [Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_4095}is a Postmillennialist (as Jerome, Origen, and Augustine), he cannot make any intelligent comment on ["the inheritance." ]{.class_4096}He "passes" and lets his partner do the bidding. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown trip mightily over the verse and go crashing down the cellar stairs by saying ["the inheritance" ]{.class_4096}is of GRACE. Yet verse 24 called it a ["reward," ]{.class_4096}and that word "reward" []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_546}occurs in every Greek manuscript of the text ever found on earth. Phenomenon No. 12. ["The inheritance" ]{.class_4096}(vs. 24) is an [earned reward, ]{.class_4095}and it is the absolute OPPOSITE of "salvation by grace" (Rom. 4:14).

But we are only getting under way! The postmortem crew of ["Romana del Vaticano"]{.class_4095} (see Col. 2:8 and remarks) is having a case of rigor mortis. Along comes the [Wycliffe Commentary ]{.class_4095}with the astounding comment that the ["inheritance" ]{.class_4096}is a Father's APPROVAL for getting a playroom cleaned up! Nor does the commentator (Dr. Ellis) stop with this ridiculous illustration. He tells us that a [Platonist ]{.class_4095}would take the passage literally. But Plato (as Augustine and Philip Schaff) was a [Postmillennialist. ]{.class_4095}Plato (as Darwin, Hitler, Lenin, and Marx) believed that you "brought in the kingdom" (see [The Sure Word of Prophecy, ]{.class_4095}1969) by war, education, and "science." An "inheritance" for Plato would have been an ideal "Republic" [before the return of Jesus Christ. ]{.class_4095}Dr. Ellis has unwittingly taken Plato's side without realizing it, for he has made ["the inheritance" ]{.class_4096}to mean "approval for action" NOW, [before the return of Jesus Christ. ]{.class_4095}This is Plato's position, and it is as "polarized" (educational cliché) from Biblical truth as the position of the Lockman Foundation in California.

"Rewards" (vs. 24) come from "service" (vs. 24---"[for ye SERVE the Lord Christ"]{.class_4096}).[ ]{.class_4096}Ellis, Findlay, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have no more idea what they are talking about than Abbie Hoffman or Angela Davis (1969--1971).

Among many errors recorded by the [AV ]{.class_4095}critics, one will find ["slave for the Master, Christ"]{.class_4095} given as a sentence (!) in the Jehovah's Witness [New World Translation ]{.class_4095}(1961) instead of ["for ye serve the Lord Christ." ]{.class_4096}Since this blooper is not in any manuscript, it should be handled like the [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) reading of 1 Timothy 3:16---which is not even a sentence.

Kenneth Wuest has knocked the Lord out of His own inheritance by translating ["kurios" ]{.class_4095}(Gk.) as "Master"; hence, "the heavenly MASTER Christ ye are serving." But this is too much. There is no "heavenly" in any Greek manuscript (cursive or uncial), and the conjecture that this[ "Lord"]{.class_4096} (Lord Christ) should have been "Master" on the grounds that the first ["kurios" ]{.class_4095}(Gk.) was translated as []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_547}"master" in verse 22 is the heighth of something or other. You see, the word (Gk.: ["kurios") ]{.class_4095}has already occurred THREE times between the [human ]{.class_4095}master of verse 22 and the ["Lord Christ"]{.class_4096} of verse 24, at least according to Wuest. \[Wuest, as the Roman Catholic Bible, substituted ["kurios" (]{.class_4095}Lord) for ["God"]{.class_4096} in verse 22, making THREE "Lords" before the ["Lord" ]{.class_4096}of verse 24.\]

Suddenly, as though to "bring out the comparison between earthly and heavenly masters," Wuest decides that "MASTER" should be substituted for ["LORD"]{.class_4096} in verse 24. But isn't this a century too late? How does one read "master" (vs. 22) and "master" (vs. 24) when the word ["LORD" ]{.class_4096}has been used for the "heavenly master" [THREE times before the translator decides to ]{.class_4095}change it? Are we to take this kind of thing seriously, or what?

All is clear without the benefit of any "Bible" or commentator since 1800. Bondslaves are to serve God in their capacity as a man's servant. They are to do what they do heartily, as though God were the "Captain" or "Boss man." This means that they should work even when the "Massah" is not on the job WATCHING THEM. Service done ["to the Lord" ]{.class_4096}in this fashion will count as "suffering for Christ" (Rom. 8:16--22), and it entitles the slave to a literal, visible, earthly piece of property at the Advent (Luke 19:11--27), which might exceed his "Master's" real estate by 10--10,000 acres! But all of this is hidden to a Postmillennialist or an Amillennialist or any other "kingdom builder" who is trying to wear a crown without carrying a cross (see Gal. 6:12 and comments).

In closing, the reader should observe that "present truth for the Church of the one Body" (Bullingerite cliché) includes a literal, visible, earthly, millennial inheritance (vs. 24) that is earned by WORKS. Nowhere in the Bible, therefore, is the major error of Bullinger pointed out any more clearly than here. The invisible "spiritual" inheritance of ["spiritual blessings"]{.class_4096} in the "heavenlies" (see Eph. 1:1--6 and comments) is NOT the only place for the Christian, nor is it the only ["inheritance," ]{.class_4096}for that inheritance (Eph. 1--2) is by GRACE; this inheritance (Col. 3:24) is by WORKS.[ ]{.class_4096}The isolation, then, of Ephesians for the Christian as "present truth" for "the Church of the one Body" in order to SPIRITUALIZE the promises of the Millennial inheritance is as gross an error as the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3707.xhtml_page_548}Postmillennialism of the Roman Catholic Church.

In order to limit the Christian's future inheritance to the ethereal, indefinite, spiritual "heavenly" of the "spiritual heavenlies," Bullinger had to cancel Romans 8:16--17, for again, the context of that passage was the literal, visible Second Advent of Christ to reign on earth. When Bullinger cancelled this passage (and made it part of the "Acts" ministry), he failed to cancel 2 Timothy 2:12, which is on the same subject, and 2 Timothy 2:12 was written AFTER Ephesians! Some "present truth," then, for the "Church of the one Body" is found even in Matthew 25:31! The "joint heir" of Galatians 3:29 (see comments) shares the Jewish, earthly, literal, visible, political kingdom for 1,000 years (see Rev. 20:1--4 and comments).

[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml}

## Chapter 4 {.heading_116}

4:1 Masters, give unto your servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven.

2 Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;

3 Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:

4 That I may make it manifest, as I ought to speak.

5 Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time.

6 Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.

With the exception of these six verses, the last chapter of Colossians is [personal ]{.class_4095}and [informative, ]{.class_4095}and deals with Paul's friends and his own ministry.

["Masters\...knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1). (One can see, at a glance, that Kenneth Wuest's reading on 3:24---["Master"]{.class_4101}---has "crept in" from this passage!) All is clear in the [King James]{.class_4101} text. A Christian slaveholder is to remember that he himself is a slave (1 Cor. 7:20--24), and his "Massah" will pay him off just like he pays off his own hired help (Col. 3:25).

["That which is just and equal"]{.class_4096} (vs. 1) matches Colossians 3:25, and "receiving for the wrong that is done" is considered to be a part of disciplining slaves as well as "paying off" the Christian at the Judgment Seat of Christ. All of this sounds strangely like "Uncle Tom's Cabin" to the discipline-rejecting, authority-defying, man-exalting, Bible-rejecting humanists of the Twentieth Century, but at the Judgment Seat of Christ (2 Cor. 5:3--10), many of these "Christian Libertines" will get their New Testament doctrines straightened out.

You will observe that the bondservant of 1 Timothy 6:1--3 is PAID for his services, and he is paid that which is ["just and equal."]{.class_4096} Nowhere does the Bible condone the [mistreatment of slaves]{.class_4095}, nor does it even condone the [underpaying of slaves]{.class_4095}. There are Christian []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_550}businessmen and Christian educators in America today who do NOT even give their employees that which is ["just and equal,"]{.class_4096} and for such people to condemn the institution of slavery on the grounds that it is not "Christian" is a little too much to swallow. A Christian master of Colosse was obligated to treat his slaves better than most Christian educators pay their TEACHERS. The verse said: ["that which is JUST and EQUAL"]{.class_4096}; and if any man ["go beyond and defraud his brother"]{.class_4096} (1 Thess. 4:6), the Lord is ["THE AVENGER"]{.class_4096} of all such! Much of what passes for "Christian education" is nothing but sanctified [extortion ]{.class_4095}and [embezzlement]{.class_4095}.

Rome, in America, set the pattern for the Christian schools to follow. Nobody but an insane man would call the salaries paid to nuns for teaching ["just and equal,"]{.class_4096} at least not the salaries paid by the Roman Church. Where they were able to extort Protestant tax money (as in Ohio, New York, Maryland, Indiana, and Illinois), the teachers were offered a fair wage.

["Continue in prayer\...with thanksgiving"]{.class_4096} (vs. 2; see Phil. 4:6; 1 Thess. 5:17--18; Eph. 6:18; Rom. 12:12, etc.) A Christian farmer can see more [on his knees]{.class_4095} than a philosopher can see on his tiptoes. Of prayer, it has been said: "He who does not pray when the sun shines, knows not how to pray when the clouds come"; "When the OUTLOOK is bad, try the UPLOOK"; "God cannot honor a draft where there is no deposit"; "If God is not first in your thoughts in the morning, He will be last in your thoughts all day"; "Pray hardest when it is hardest to pray"; "Prayer is discussed more and practiced LESS than any other Bible doctrine"; "You are not on ASKING grounds till you are on PRAYING grounds"; "Strength in prayer is better than length in prayer"; "Prayer must come from the root of the heart, not the roof of the mouth"; and "Anchor yourself to the throne of God and then shorten the rope."

["Withal praying also for us, that God would OPEN UNTO US A DOOR OF UTTERANCE, to speak the mystery of Christ"]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). Paul desires to make this mystery ["manifest"]{.class_4096} (vs. 4). The serious student of Scripture will note that this mystery is NOT the "mystery of the one Body" or the "mystery of the Church of the one Body" (Bullingerite cliché); it is the mystery of Colossians 1:26--27. As we have said before (see Eph. 1, 3:1--6 and comments), []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_551}hyperdispen-sationalists and hyper-Calvinists eventually subvert the whole Bible to one or two pet doctrines. We call these doctrines ["hobbyhorses,"]{.class_4095} and Bullinger, as Calvin, rode his to death. John Gilpin (the [Baptist Examiner]{.class_4095}, Ashland, Kentucky) is gullible enough to think that Paul was in jail [to get the elect saved]{.class_4095}; the proof text he uses for this is 2 Timothy 2:10. Bullinger, on the other hand (along with Stam and O'Hair), thought Paul was in jail because [he preached the "Body mystery"]{.class_4095}; the proof text is Colossians 4:3--4. May we suggest also (reverently!) that Paul was in jail because he was [arrested and confined there]{.class_4095}?

Paul was in jail for:

1\. Preaching salvation by faith (Acts 16:30--31).

2\. Preaching the JOINT BODY of Jew and Gentile (Eph. 3:1--3).

3\. Preaching ["Christ in you"]{.class_4096} (Col. 1:27).

4\. Preaching the Second Coming of Christ (Acts 20:27).

5\. Preaching against idols (Acts 19).

6\. Walking into the Temple at Jerusalem (Acts 21:27--33).

7\. A ransom which Felix hoped would be paid (Acts 24:26).

8\. Preaching the resurrection FROM the dead (Acts 26:23).

You see, Bullinger, Calvin, Stam, and O'Hair (like any Campbellite or Catholic in "Christendom") never really learned [the Bible]{.class_4095}. They discovered one or two "doctrines" and then relegated both Testaments to those doctrines. If you are a big enough fool to follow them for any length of time, you will miss three-fourths of the New Testament and nine-tenths of the Old Testament---or maybe [all of it]{.class_4095}. Modern "Fundamentalism" is also much like this: you extract FROM the Bible a list of things which you consider to be [essential beliefs]{.class_4095} and then teach your church members (or your students) to [throw their Bible out the window]{.class_4095} (see [Mark of the Beast]{.class_4095}, chapter 1, 1969).

["Open unto us a door of utterance\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 3). A ["door" ]{.class_4096}in Scripture is defined (by Scripture) as an opportunity to preach (note Rev. 3:8; 1 Cor. 16:9; and 2 Cor. 2:12). Although this is not always the meaning (see Rev. 3:20, 4:1, etc.), the verse before us is plainly this case.

The old English ["withal"]{.class_4096} means "together" and indicates that Paul is saying, "While you're praying, don't forget to pray for me, because I am in jail, in handcuffs, and I want a chance to preach. So []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_552}pray that God will open a door for me so I can preach like I ought to (Eph. 6:18--20!), whether I'm bound or loose!" (Paul preached in chains \[Acts 26\], as boldly as he preached from a pulpit \[Acts 13\].)

["Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time"]{.class_4096} (vs. 5). (See Eph. 5:16 with comments and 1 Cor. 7:29 with Gal. 6:10.) The man who "redeems" the time will not "itch" for something without "scratching for it." The lazy Christian aims at nothing and generally hits the target. Stagnant pools of water usually gather filth and germs, and no Christian should be "waiting for something to turn up"; he ought to be turning things upside down (Acts 17:6). Teachers and preachers cannot work in the "harvest field"; the prayer was for LABORERS (Matt. 9:38)! "What a man IS depends largely on what he does when he has nothing to do." It is better for a pot to boil OVER than never reach a boil, and in Christian service, it is always better to BURN OUT than to RUST OUT.

It takes wisdom in dealing with the unsaved (["THEM that are without"]{.class_4096}), for they are by disposition (from their rejection of the truth) naturally hypercritical and hypersensitive in dealing with a Bible believer. If you "live it," they will call you a fanatic, and if you don't "live it," they will call you a hypocrite. You can't win either way, and the best you can do is pray for wisdom in dealing with them and ["walk in wisdom" ]{.class_4096}(text) when you are around them (see 1 Cor. 10:27, for example).

["Let your speech be alway with grace, SEASONED WITH SALT, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6). Christians are ["the salt of the earth"]{.class_4096} (Matt. 5:13); therefore, their speech should be "salty." "God's prophets are not 'sugar sticks'" (Dr. Bob Jones Sr.)--- they are "salty." Where the wound has been opened (Jer. 48:10; Psa. 149:6), the salt is rubbed in (Mark 9:50). First Peter 3:15 and Philippians 1:27--28 go with the verse.

For a perfect example of how NOT to speak, observe the [Amplified New Testament's]{.class_4095} distortion of the verse to read exactly what it did NOT read in any Greek manuscript: ["pleasant and winsome"]{.class_4095}! That is exactly what the verse did NOT say, and this reading is a private interpretation inserted by a Bible-rejecter who was determined to overthrow the truth of the passage.

You have to be more than an infidel to do such a thing. You have to have a proud, self-righteous, carnal spirit that is DEDICATED to overthrowing the authority of the word of God. The "pleasant" and "winsome" Christian who thought he was doing God a favor by converting Paul's speech into that of a day-school teacher at a tea party has produced a reading that could only be found in "Alice in Wonderland." ["Pleasant and winsome speech"]{.class_4095} would have been as out of place in the mouth of Jeremiah, Isaiah, Moses, Peter, James, John, Paul, or Jesus Christ as a pork chop in the mouth of David Ben Gurion.

["With GRACE"]{.class_4096} (vs. 6) has been taken by A. S. Peake to mean "pleasantness," but Brother Peake is about as sure of this private interpretation as Einstein was sure of the origin of man. Observe the great Athenian agnosticism (Acts 17:23) breathing through every word of the [Expositor's Greek Testament ]{.class_4095}(pg. 35): ["probably\...some think\...but this does not suit\...wisdom is probably meant\...there may be a secondary meaning\...." ]{.class_4095}But we know[ "a double-minded man" ]{.class_4096}(James 1:8) when we see one, and so we shall set Brother Peake aside and go to the root of the matter.

"The root of the matter" is found where most roots are found---Alexandria. In the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} (Vol. 20, pg. 210), we "tree the coon." Three citations are given for the alteration of ["grace" ]{.class_4096}and ["salt" ]{.class_4096}to ["pleasant and winsome."]{.class_4095} [Two of the three quotations]{.class_4095} are from the Roman Catholic apocryphal books, and [all three]{.class_4095} are from the mythological [LXX]{.class_4095}. (The ["LXX"]{.class_4095} quoted was written more than 200 years [after the New Testament was completed]{.class_4095}.) Not even Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (Commentary, pg. 1327) are deceived into accepting this apocryphal reading, but Phillips and ["Good News"]{.class_4095} adopt the Alexandrian emendation of the [Amplified Version]{.class_4095}.

Since the Holy Spirit has given us an infallible cross-reference in the infallible [AV ]{.class_4095}(3:16), it might do well to shelve all of the suggestions of Peake, Findlay, Ellis, Lipsius, Olshausen, Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Westcott, et al., and learn the truth of the text.

In Colossians 3:16, the same expression occurs---["WITH GRACE in your hearts,"]{.class_4096} and Ephesians 4:29 explains what this means when dealing with people---["that it may MINISTER GRACE unto the hearers."]{.class_4096} The speech recommended in our text []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_554}is speech that comes from a heart that is aware of (and rejoicing in) the GRACE of God. Speech coming from this source will reveal God's grace to the man witnessed to (and the man witnessed to is ["without"]{.class_4096}; see vs. 5), but the revelation of this grace is neither "pleasant" nor "winsome." It is either shocking or [stinging ]{.class_4095}(Jer. 23:29).

The text of the [King James]{.class_4095} compared with the corrupt text of the A.D. [LXX ]{.class_4095}and the [Amplified Version ]{.class_4095}only shows us again the gap that exists between an aggressive, militant, Pauline, Biblical Christianity and the modern "Christian education" movement. Modern Christian educators are not training SOLDIERS; they are training [politicians]{.class_4095}. They are not educating TROOPS for battle; they are organizing old maids for teaching [children]{.class_4095}. They are not producing Bible [believers]{.class_4095}; they are producing Bible [critics. ]{.class_4095}They are not developing spiritual giants; they are turning out executives and office boys. The man who wrote Colossians 4:6 spoke of grace (Rom. 11:6) from a heart acquainted with it (Gal. l--2); and when he spoke to those ["without," ]{.class_4096}he poured on the salt till their wounds burned (Gal. 1:8--10) three months after he left town (Acts 14:19). Before Felix and Agrippa, Paul shows that he knows "how\...to answer every man"; and if you can find a lot of "winsome and pleasant things" ([Amplified Version]{.class_4095}) in these two speeches (Acts 24, 26), you either need glasses, or you need to see the headshrinker.

How do you like Acts 13:41 for a ["winsome]{.class_4095} [speech"?]{.class_4095} How about Acts 13:46 for ["pleasantness"]{.class_4095}? How do you like Acts 17:23 for an "interesting" (see [Good News for Modern Man]{.class_4095} on Col. 1:6) speech? How ["winsome"]{.class_4095} can you get!? Perhaps the most ["winsome"]{.class_4095} speech Paul ever made to ["them that are without"]{.class_4096} was made to the high priest at Jerusalem: ["GOD SHALL SMITE THEE, THOU WHITED WALL"]{.class_4096} (Acts 23:3)\![ How is that for "winsome"?]{.class_4095}

You see, the people who wrote the [Amplified Version,]{.class_4095} and the people who believe it, read it, teach it, preach from it, and use it, are not ready for BATTLE. They are ready to sell out to the enemy as soon as he gets in sight, and the thought of losing their esteem (or financial support) terrifies them. The big trick in Christianity today is how to appease the world system so you won't lose radio or TV time or your tax exempt status. No church in the New Testament had tax exempt status. ([Pray about that one]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_555}!)

In a [manly ]{.class_4095}Christianity, where a [masculine ]{.class_4095}salvation is preached (God the Father---MALE, God the Son---MALE, God the Holy Spirit---MALE) by apostles who were all males, one will find the salty speech of the "old salt" which not only irritates the lost, but also whets his appetite for food and preserves the food that is on hand. In a [womanly ]{.class_4095}Christianity, where a [feminine ]{.class_4095}salvation is preached by [society climbers]{.class_4095}, one will find ["winsome and pleasant"]{.class_4095} speech, but you may also find the same speech coming from a garbage-mouth prostitute! (See Prov. 6:24 and 9:17 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs]{.class_4095}, 1972.)

The ["good words and fair speeches"]{.class_4096} (Rom. 16:15) of the Bible-perverting "Christian" are not to be taken seriously. When a Bible critic gets especially saccharine and mouthy in his praise of the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible---["poetic beauty, rhythmic cadences, stately language, etc."]{.class_4095}---you can get ready for a load of garbage: after the GAS comes the operation, after the SUGAR comes the pill, and after the BREAD comes the [baloney]{.class_4095}. Those of us who believe the Bible don't have to throw kisses at it before we take it apart. We elevate it to its rightful place (Psa. 138:2) and [leave it there]{.class_4095} as it stands.

There are numerous corrections made on the passage by the dead-orthodox Conservatives, but as we have discovered on eight dozen occasions before, none of them give any light on the text, and none are necessary. Williams tells us that verse 2 should "read as the [RV]{.class_4095}." (Naturally, it shouldn't, so ignore the remark.) The conversion of ["every man"]{.class_4096} to ["each one"]{.class_4095} ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}) does nothing but destroy the entire sense of the passage which is talking about your answers to unsaved people when they put you on the spot (["them that are WITHOUT"]{.class_4096}).

This time, the [RV, ASV, RSV]{.class_4095}, and [New ASV]{.class_4095} (same family) have translated the word ["logou"]{.class_4095} (vs. 3) literally ("a door for THE WORD"), after absolutely [refusing to translate 2:8 and 2:16 literally]{.class_4095}. But we have observed this Phenomenon (Phenomenon No. 3) on numerous occasions, and we should not be surprised by it. Where the dead-orthodox apostates cannot get rid of the text by translating ACCURATELY, they translate LITERALLY; and where the [King James]{.class_4095} has already been translated LITERALLY, it is altered to make it more ACCURATE. That is, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_556}[most hypocrites are incurable.]{.class_4095}

Kenneth Wuest takes the position of Barth and Brunner in translating "word" and translates it as the "Word" of John 1:1--3. Various other changes are found: "opportunity to preach the message," "a door for the Gospel," etc. But none of these substitutions can conceal the hypocrisy of men like the translators of the [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1963), recommended by Bob Jones University and other dead-orthodox schools, for lo and behold, the same word ["Logos"]{.class_4095} (Gk.) has been translated as ["utterance" ]{.class_4095}by the [ASV, RV, RSV,]{.class_4095} [New ASV]{.class_4095}, etc., in 2 Corinthians 8:7! Phenomenon No. 4.

Every translation since 1880 was translated at the whim and caprice of a body of men devoted to [destroying the authority of the AV 1611.]{.class_4095} When they blab about "uniform translating," they are simply LYING. Phenomenon No. 7.

The founders of Bob Jones University and Moody Bible Institute never read "the word" for ["utterance"]{.class_4096} in Colossians 4:3; they read it as the Reformers read it \[see Tyndale (1525), Geneva (1560), and [Bishop's Bible]{.class_4095} (1568)\]: ["A DOOR OF UTTERANCE."]{.class_4096} Don't ever make the mistake of thinking that because a man, church, or school has a "name that liveth" (Rev. 3:1) that there is anything around breathing. The church that "kept His word" (Rev. 3:8) was [not ]{.class_4095}the church of 1901--2001.

Dummelow imagined that the ["speech"]{.class_4096} of verse 6 should be "winning and wise" (Commentary, pg. 984). ["Redeeming the time"]{.class_4096} should have been ["making your market full from the occasion"]{.class_4095} (Wow!) according to Dr. Peake, and Wuest has inserted ["as it is necessary in the nature of the case"]{.class_4095} in the middle of verse 4, thus expanding it to an expansion that blows out the seams and collapses the sense to little or nothing.

(Fortunately, we have the correct text preserved in plain English for us in the [AV 1611]{.class_4095}, and we may stick with it and use it as a point of reference for studying the more inaccurate "translations" such as the [New ASV, New English, New Jerusalem,]{.class_4095} [New American, ]{.class_4095}New Rinso, [New Scofield,]{.class_4095} New Duz, and "The Expanded, Amplified, Laminated, Contaminated, Lanolized, Everyday Modern English Speech of the Common, Up-to-date, Authorized, Revised, Standard, Universal, Ecumenical Crap-out of the American, English, Roman, []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_557}and Latin Vulgate.")

4:7 All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:

8 Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;

9 With Onesimus, a faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They shall make known unto you all things which are done here.

10 Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and Marcus, sister's son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)

11 And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.

All that follows is [personal ]{.class_4095}and deals with Paul's associates.

1\. Tychicus is a messenger to tell the Colossians how things are going (vs. 7). He is ["a faithful minister and fellowservant."]{.class_4096}

2\. Onesimus will go with him to Colosse (vs. 9) and help bear tidings.

3\. Aristarchus and John Mark (with Paul at this time) send their greetings also by means of Tychicus and Onesimus (vs. 10).

4\. Jesus (Justus), Epaphras, Luke, and Demas (vs. 11--14) also send their greetings to the brethren in Colosse.

5\. Archippus (vs. 17) gets a special message from Paul in regard to his call to the ministry (and we shall comment on this at length).

Tychicus (vs. 7) appears first in Acts 20:4 where he is called "an Asian." He is probably with Trophimus in Acts 21:29 at Jerusalem. Tychicus is with Paul in Rome, although he is not in prison; hence, he can carry tidings to the Colossians. He is: 1. ["a beloved brother,"]{.class_4096} 2. ["a faithful minister,"]{.class_4096} 3. a ["fellowservant"]{.class_4096} (see 1:7, 4:10, and Phil. 2:25 with comments).

Onesimus is the returned slave of Philemon 10--16. He is to be "TRUSTED" and "LOVED" (Phile. 17) as a Christian brother, even []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_558}though he was returned to serve his master (see 1 Tim. 6:1--2 and comments under Col. 3:22--24). He is ["one of you"]{.class_4096} (vs. 9), which would indicate that Onesimus came from this city.

Aristarchus (vs. 10) was a Macedonian who accompanied Paul through some trying times in Acts 19:29, and he accompanied Paul to Rome in Acts 27:2. Technically, Aristarchus is from Thessalonica (Acts 20:4), and in Philemon 23--24, his name follows Mark as ["fellowlabourers."]{.class_4096} Evidently, Aristarchus suffered a prison term with Paul (see Rom. 16:7; Eph. 3:1; 2 Tim. 1:8; 2:3--4; and 2 Cor. 10:3--6) and was therefore not free to travel with Tychicus and Onesimus.

Marcus (["sister's son to Barnabas"]{.class_4096}) is the John Mark of Acts 13:13 who defected on the missionary trip. The break in fellowship between Mark and Paul was not a permanent thing which is evidenced by 2 Timothy 4:11; Philemon 24; and 1 Peter 5:13.

["And Jesus, which is called Justus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 11) is a converted Jew, as is also Mark (["who are of the circumcision"]{.class_4096}). Since Aristarchus is included in the sentence (vs. 10), we come to the conclusion that he, as Cornelius (Acts 10:1--2), was a Jewish proselyte before his conversion to Christ.

["These ONLY are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God\..."]{.class_4096} (vs. 11) shows that these men are the only ones [who have stuck with him]{.class_4095} in his present predicament. This predicament is counted to be part of the "sufferings" through which we enter the MILLENNIAL KINGDOM (see Acts 1:3 and 14:22). Again, Bullinger is sadly deficient in his Bible study. The sufferings, [here, ]{.class_4095}are for the earthly, literal, [visible inheritance ]{.class_4095}when the Kingdom of God appears, as in Luke 19:11, and have nothing to do with the Kingdom of God as found in Romans 14:17. People hung up on "PRESENT TRUTH" (Stamite cliché) are unable to expound the text, so Williams (a hyperdispensationalist) refuses comment. Bullinger's reference to Appendix No. 112 ([Companion Bible]{.class_4095}) solves nothing for his system of private interpretation.

4:12 Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_559}will of God.

13 For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them that are in Laodicea and them in Hierapolis.

14 Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, greet you.

15 Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.

16 And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea.

17 And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.

18 The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.

Epaphras is another Colossian (as Onesimus), and he is mentioned in Philemon 23 and Colossians 1:7, which see. For ["perfect and complete,"]{.class_4096} see Colossians 1:9, 28 and Ephesians 1:9 and 3:16--20.

Epaphras has a "great zeal" for those at Colosse, Hierapolis, and LAODICEA (see remarks in the Preface). Again, we can apply types and get a beautiful picture of Christ ["always labouring fervently"]{.class_4096} in His prayers (John 17; Rom. 8:26--27) that the Christian ["may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God,"]{.class_4096} since He has a great zeal for the saved people in the church of Laodicea (Rev. 3:14--22) at the end of this age: 1901--2009.

["Luke, the beloved physician"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). Since the ["circumcision"]{.class_4096} has been listed before (vs. 11), it is assumed by most scholars that Luke and Demas are UNCIRCUMCISED. This is the tradition that is responsible for teaching that ["Luke was the only Gentile writer in the Bible."]{.class_4095} However, as any fair-minded man can see, this deduction is arrived at by stretching something near the breaking point. The opening words of Luke point to a [Judean Jew]{.class_4095} living in Jerusalem (Luke 1:1--5). If Luke was a Gentile, he was a circumcised Gentile with access to more information than John Mark, Justus, or Aristarchus (["the circumcision,"]{.class_4096} vs. 10--12).

["Luke, the beloved physician"]{.class_4096} doesn't match the theological format of A. A. Allen, "Reverend Ike," Oral Roberts (ex-healer), Ewing, Branham, Jack Coe, and some others. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_560}["They that are sick"]{.class_4096} have need of a physician according to the Lord Jesus Christ (Matt. 9:12), and when the apostle calls a physician a ["beloved"]{.class_4096} brother, he certainly is not "of the Devil." Luke, as a matter of fact, is the last man to leave Paul before his head rolls in the basket (2 Tim. 4:11). If his ministry had not been a blessing and comfort to Paul, he never would have referred to him as ["beloved."]{.class_4096} Isaiah believes in [medicine plus prayer]{.class_4095} (Isa. 38), and "faith healing" is not all that it is "cracked-up-to-be" if we are to believe 1 Kings 13:6, 26 and Revelation 16:13--14 (!).

A man who seeks physicians without prayer is very unwise (2 Chron. 16:12). Although it is possible to spend a fortune on medicine and doctor bills without being healed (Mark 5:26), still, medicine is recommended on occasion in the New Testament (1 Tim. 5:23), and the man who rejects all medical help when he is sick is either a fool or a suicide. (See extensive comments under Exod. 4:6--7 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus]{.class_4095}, 1973, and Matt. 8:17 in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Matthew]{.class_4095}, 1970.)

In addition to the conjecture that Luke was "uncircumcised," we have the "highly imaginative" guesswork of Plumptre who thought that Luke was connected with the family of Seneca and Lucan (a Roman [philosopher ]{.class_4095}and a Roman poet). By now, that word [philosopher ]{.class_4095}sounds like a stink bomb going off every time it appears (see Col. 2:8 and comments), and we strongly suspect that Plumptre---one of the greatest destructive critics of the Reformation Bible who ever lived---is simply trying to add a little prestige to his own classification. (See "The Pauline Obsession," footnote 96, chap. 6, [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_4095}, 1970.)

Not everybody for whom Paul prayed got healed (2 Tim. 4:20), and Paul could not heal himself (2 Cor. 12:7--10): Luke still traveled with him (Acts 27--28) and ministered to him for many years. Doctors are evidently used by the Lord to maintain EFFICIENCY even where complete healing is not "in the books." Although we cannot classify Jarman's "Obstetrics," Barker's "Medicine," Mayo's "Surgery," Gray's "Anatomy," Holt's "Pediatrics," Kelly's "Gynecology," or Welch's "Practice of Medicine" with revealed truth by the Holy Spirit, we should not ignore or defer help offered from []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_561}men whom God has used at times to assist His people. Aesculapius, Hippocrates, Galen, Paracelsus, Harvey, Leeuwenhoek, Jenner, Priestly, Pasteur, Lister, Locke, Dunant, Oschner, Mayo, and Salk were not [spiritual giants]{.class_4095}; and the majority of them were as lost as Dave Gardner and Duke Ellington, but you can borrow their brains if the Lord wants you to!

["And Demas"]{.class_4096} (vs. 14). This is the famous defector of 2 Timothy 4:10 who[ "loved this present world."]{.class_4096} Here, in our text, there is not one good word said about him. Although Luke is ["beloved"]{.class_4096} and Tychicus is ["a faithful minister"]{.class_4096} and Onesimus is ["faithful"]{.class_4096} and Aristarchus is a ["fellowprisoner,"]{.class_4096} poor Demas is a blank; [he is nothing.]{.class_4095} He is a perfect picture of the Christian who was not a ["fellowworker"]{.class_4095} (as Jesus and Marcus) and did not labor on his knees (like Epaphras)---except to tune the television set. Demas could not reconcile a "Christian testimony" and "a successful ministry" with dying alone in jail (2 Tim. 4:11) and leaving nothing behind for an inheritance. Demas was a "builder of bridges" who believed in visible, physical, literal monuments to "orthodoxy." If he [began ]{.class_4095}by building MEN, it is certain that he [wound up]{.class_4095} building BUILDINGS. Demas was fascinated by the gods of Rome---Education, Science, Culture, Art, Music, and Religion. He was hypnotized by culture, refinement, social status, Greek scholarship, social image, and "respectability." ["This present world"]{.class_4096} (2 Tim. 4:10) is THAT world, and no amount of veneer or whitewash can cover its true condition (Gal. 1:4) in the sight of God (1 John 2:16). Demas was not ["a devil"]{.class_4096} like [Judas ]{.class_4095}(John 6:70--71), nor was he a Christ-rejector like [Pilate]{.class_4095}. He was a premillennial Fundamentalist who followed Paul. But he served mammon (Luke 16:13). The world is full of Demases; and whether they go by the name of Eusebius, Augustine, Jerome, Robertson, Moulton, Milligan, Trench, Vincent, or \-\-\-\-\--(!), they are kin to DEMAS.

One must not be too hard on Demas; there are too many of his kinfolk around! Consider the man: he was saved, "washed in the blood," born again, an orthodox, evangelistic, "Bible-believing Fundamentalist" who believed in the "verbal inspiration" of the "originals." He was [Pauline ]{.class_4095}in his approach and outlook and "took his stand" where Rome had a theological argument with Paul. However, he ["loved this present world"]{.class_4096}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_562} (cf. 2 Tim. 4:10 and 1 John 2:15); therefore, ["the love of the Father"]{.class_4096} (1 John 2:15) was NOT in him!

He was one of those peculiar types of "career Christians" who puts up with suffering and persecution as long as [glamor ]{.class_4095}and [glory ]{.class_4095}are connected with it. However, when all of the glamor and glory disappeared and there was no Sunday School record attendance to publish, no number of graduates "matriculated," no honors in the "teachers' aptitude tests," no gorgeous buildings to exhibit (like Rome!), no major works of art to display (like Rome!), no trained musicians to perform (like Rome!), no scholarship to boast in (like Rome!), and no thronging multitudes to address (like Rome!), Demas "crossed his Rubicon" (like Rome!) and [left Paul in jail to die]{.class_4095} (2 Tim. 4:10). Undoubtedly, Demas began an inter-senatorial, non-denominational "witness" in "Christian dialogue" with the men who supported Nero---[Nero had Paul beheaded.]{.class_4095} For a similar comparison, one should study the infamous Congress on Evangelism (Berlin, 1966) where Richard Wurmbrand was refused permission to speak on the subject of "The Underground Church in Russia." The Russian "establishment" (the official Communist Pastors, paid by the State) was present with Billy and Dr. Theodore Epp, but Wurmbrand was consigned to the limbo of "undesirables." That is, Demas was [on the platform]{.class_4095} with the representatives of the Caesaro-papacy when Paul's head was rolling off the block. [Both Billy and Epp were "Fundamentalists." ]{.class_4095}There seems to be a division within the "Fundamentalists" themselves when it comes to their ideas about practicing the New Testament.

If we may be allowed to make one more comment on Demas, it would be simply this: Where Heaven is not as REAL to the believer as the world around him is, he will sell the Bible out every time [under the proper pressure.]{.class_4095} If Satan wishes to turn a Christian intellectual against the Lord, he simply holds before him the terrific prospect of ridicule from the "intelligentsia" if he stands by the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible. The gangland method is simple: "You either adopt the centuries-old traditions and philosophy (see 2:8 and comments) of Alexandria or we'll blacklist you, blackball you, and blackmail you until no one in Christendom will listen to your teachings or read []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_563}your writings." If Heaven is not REAL for this scholar, he will sell out every time, exactly as Demas. There is [no essential difference]{.class_4095} between the pastor who compromises with the Southern Baptist Convention by putting his church in the Association (to avoid persecution), and Warfield, Wuest, Robertson, and Thieme selling out the [AV]{.class_4095} 1611 for "the original Greek." Every man involved feared ridicule and opposition.

There is not the slightest bit of difference between Billy Graham cooperating with Modernists in a "crusade" (in order to avoid opposition) and Bob Jones University and Tennessee Temple University recommending the [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095} (1963) (in order to avoid ridicule and opposition from "Conservative scholarship"). All involved feared ridicule and opposition. The pot cannot call the kettle black, and any man who gets on the Eusebius-Jerome-Augustine-Calvin-Warfield-Lightfoot-Nestle-Gregory-Westcott-Vincent-Trench-Hort-Ellicott-[RV-ASV-RSV-New ASV]{.class_4095} airline is flying with DEMAS. These are the world-loving, world-pleasing, world-honoring, world-fearing segments of "orthodox Christianity." [Heaven was real to none of them.]{.class_4095}

You cannot fear God AND man; and if you fear the fiats of education, science, philosophy, and religion, you cannot fear God (Eccl. 12:13). You cannot love Heaven and this world (Luke 16:15); and if you fear the ridicule, opposition, and persecution of this world, you will forget your heavenly home and fall into goosestep with ["the course of this world,"]{.class_4096} obeying the dictates of ["the prince of the power of the air"]{.class_4096} (Eph. 2:2--3).

Demas is the perfect representative of the Laodicean Fundamentalist. Hence, ["Salute the brethren which are in LAODICEA\...the church of the LAODICEANS\...the epistle from LAODICEA"]{.class_4096} (vss. 15--16).

Now we have the picture. ["Present truth"]{.class_4095} for this time has nothing to do with "the Church of the Body mystery" or "the Church of the one Body." That was a truth given more than 1,900 centuries back for the Ephesian church period (Eph. 3:1--3; Rev. 2:1--7). ["Present truth"]{.class_4095} is Colossians, written for the Laodicean church of Revelation 3:14.

1\. It is a [sick church]{.class_4095} and needs medical care (note[ "Luke"]{.class_4096} in Col. []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_564}4:14).

2\. It is plagued with [education ]{.class_4095}and [philosophy ]{.class_4095}(Col. 2:8).

3\. It has inherited nineteen centuries of [phony traditions]{.class_4095} in the line of "Biblical" scholarship (Col. 2:8).

4\. It is nearly [Roman ]{.class_4095}(see 2:18) in its approach to sacraments and Angelology.

5\. It has to deal with saved [Russians ]{.class_4095}(see 3:11).

6\. It is a [worldly ]{.class_4095}church that has lost its heavenly vision (3:1--3 and comments).

7\. It has a problem with [African music]{.class_4095} (3:16 and comments).

8\. It is living by the ["rudiments"]{.class_4096} (2:8 and comments) set down by Satan and preserved through Greek and European philosophers.

9\. Its average church member "loves this present world."

10\. And last, but not least! ["Say to Archippus, TAKE HEED TO THE MINISTRY which thou hast received in the Lord, THAT THOU FULFIL IT"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). The last church has a [shortage of ministers]{.class_4095}, and those called are wavering, for Conservative scholarship has STOLEN their authority by using philosophy and the traditions of men (see 2:8 and comments)!

The last chapter of Colossians closes with this thunderous roar, which is muffled as much as possible by Clarke, Lange, Ellicott, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, Findlay, Ellis, Peake, and the other 300 "leading" dead-orthodox apostates.

["And Nymphas, and the church which is in his house"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15; cf. 1 Cor. 16:19; Acts 12:12; Rom. 16:5). The verse can be a reference to the saved people within the household of Nymphas, or it can refer to a local assembly which met in that house; the latter case is probably the right one.

At this point, the believer should pay heed to the text and observe (again) the remarkable potency of the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible where it blasts holes through its critics while they are commenting on it. You see, DEMAS has just showed up (see last verse and comments), and the sudden appearance of DEMAS, [right before the mention of Laodicea,]{.class_4095} has a subconscious affect on the minds of the dead-orthodox apostates. This is what is known in psychology as a "controlled response," and Pavlov's experiment (which was converted to "psycho-politics" in Russia \[1920--1970\]) is now []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_565}demonstrated beautifully.

No sooner has the word ["Demas"]{.class_4096} appeared than all of the critics of the Holy Bible go into a spasm. The spiritual shock waves which pass (electronically) over the minds of the critics when they read ["Demas"]{.class_4096} attack the "central nervous system." Without being aware of their spasmodic twitching, all of them suddenly decide that something [has to be wrong]{.class_4095} with the next verse. But since all of the manuscripts read the same way on all of the verse [except on one word]{.class_4095}, this [one word]{.class_4095} is pounced upon with the madness of a Treblinka inmate finding a piece of meat.

The first man to do this was Origen (A.D. 185--254), who altered the words ["HIS house"]{.class_4096} to "HER house." The gullible and highly superstitious writer of the [Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_4095} (pg. 547) calls the evidence for this reading ["very strong."]{.class_4095} This is to mislead the reader into thinking that the manuscript evidence for ["HIS house"]{.class_4096} ([AV]{.class_4095} 1611) is ["very weak."]{.class_4095} But those of us who can still remember the gallant crew of the ["Romana del Vaticano" ]{.class_4095}(see Col. 2:8) trying to lie their way through the Bible, are not going to take the word of a M.A. or Th.D. simply because he is a born-again Conservative who "believes in the inspired originals." We have seen these Bible-rejecting idiots performing right alongside the infidels and Jesuits, and they have ceased to impress us.

What is the "VERY STRONG" attestation for "HER house" instead of the ["HIS house"]{.class_4096} found in the [Authorized Version]{.class_4095} of 1611? [Well, the Roman Catholic Vatican manuscript reads with Origen---"HER house."]{.class_4095}

You say, "What else?" [Well, that is about it.]{.class_4095} (Phenomenon No. 17.)

It's very much like the readings in John 14:7 and 5:29. That is, "[B]{.class_4095}" is considered to overrule the Western family of manuscripts, the Caesarean family of manuscripts, the Hesychian family of manuscripts, and the Syrian family of manuscripts.

There is not much doubt about Dr. Peake's relation to Demas. He, as the [New American Standard Version]{.class_4095} (1963) and the old [American Standard Version ]{.class_4095}(1901), is quite susceptible to ANYTHING that disagrees with the [King James ]{.class_4095}text. The [ASV ]{.class_4095}(with the [RV ]{.class_4095}and [New ASV]{.class_4095}) has written "THEIR HOUSE." [(Anything but []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_566}believe the text!)]{.class_4095}

Kenneth Wuest (Moody Bible Institute) prefers the Roman Catholic reading of the Vatican to the Reformation text of the Protestants. The [Amplified New Testament]{.class_4095} (Theodore Epp's main version) also adopts this Roman Catholic corruption ("HER house"), and this brings the [Amplified ]{.class_4095}into line with C. H. Dodd's Roman Catholic [New English Bible.]{.class_4095} Phillips also uses the reading, as does the Jehovah's Witness Bible ([New World Translation]{.class_4095}). This reading places Arians, infidels, Catholics, Protestants, Fundamentalists, Liberals, and Jehovah's Witnesses [all on the same footing]{.class_4095} and demonstrates, again, the remarkable quality of "ecumenicy" found among the enemies of the [Authorized ]{.class_4095}Bible of 1611.

Origen turned ["Nymphas" ]{.class_4096}into a WOMAN and then was forced to change the ["his"]{.class_4096} (Gk., ["autou"]{.class_4095}) of the passage to "her" (Gk., ["autas"]{.class_4095}). But this is VERY typical of Origen. His error was copied out by Eusebius (A.D. 260--340) and Pamphilus (A.D. 240--309) for the Roman "Catholic" Constantine (A.D. 288--337), and it is still preserved for us in the corrupt texts of Nestle (pg. 516) and Metzger, recommended by the duped faculty at Bob Jones University, Greenville, South Carolina.

The reading ["THEIR house"]{.class_4095} is a compromise arrived at around A.D. 330--350 by the writer of the Sinaitic manuscript who saw that Origen had messed with the text. Since all dead-orthodox apostates will do ANYTHING rather than accept an [AV ]{.class_4095}reading, the [ASV ]{.class_4095}and [RV ]{.class_4095}have gone "halfway to the truth" and written "THEIR HOUSE." (The manuscript evidence for this latter corruption is [Sinaiticus]{.class_4095}, [Alexandrinus]{.class_4095}, manuscript "[P]{.class_4095}," and [Ephraemi ]{.class_4095}\[manuscript "[C]{.class_4095}"\].)

However, one can see, at a glance, that all of these mighty efforts to destroy the truth were nothing but the outworkings of a momentary convulsion which took place in verse 14. This subconscious agitation caused a sort of "seizure" in the minds of the dead-orthodox apostates and the outworkings of their spiritual paroxysm produced the "funnyisms" before us.

Consider:

1\. How can it be "THEIR" when the word "HOUSE" (Gk., ["oikon"]{.class_4095}) is SINGULAR in every Greek manuscript extant? [There is no plural subject in the sentence;]{.class_4095} Nymphas was an [individual]{.class_4095}---not a chain-[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_567}gang!

The reading ["their"]{.class_4095} ([New ASV]{.class_4095} and sister corruptions), [if it had been a reference to the "brethren"]{.class_4095} (see text), would have to have been ["their HOUSES."]{.class_4095} But no! We are expected to think that we are studying "accurate translating" when we read the corrupt [ASV ]{.class_4095}(1901) and [New ASV]{.class_4095} (1963) and find "their" referring to ONE MAN! He must have been a ["behemoth"]{.class_4096} (Job 40:15) like the one in Revelation 13:1--2!!

2\. How can it be "her" when [there hasn't been a woman named in fifteen verses]{.class_4095}, and every name listed is [a man's name]{.class_4095} (vss. 7, 9--15)?

No! By all the laws of context, common sense, grammar, and style, the word is masculine and requires ["HIS"]{.class_4096} for ["HIS house."]{.class_4096} This is the reading of the inspired and infallible text, faithfully preserved in the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible through the majority of Greek manuscripts, in all the [Receptus ]{.class_4095}manuscripts, in "[D]{.class_4095}" (Western) and "[G]{.class_4095}," [Cyprius ]{.class_4095}("[K]{.class_4095}") and manuscript ["Psi."]{.class_4095} Again, we should observe that the Greek fathers of the Greek church---from Antioch through Constantinople ([not Alexandria through Rome]{.class_4095})---have used the [King James]{.class_4095} text: Theodoret, Chrysostom, and John of Damascus all read ["HIS house."]{.class_4096} (If you ever have to toss a coin between Chrysostom and Origen, save yourself the trouble; Chrysostom was a soulwinning, Bible-preaching missionary. Origen was a Platonic Arian who devoted his life to destroying the [Textus Receptus]{.class_4095}.)

["The church in his house"]{.class_4096} was an assembly of Christians who met on the first day of the week (Acts 20:7) ["to break bread"]{.class_4096} and pray together, and their "primitive Christianity" is well documented in the writings of the Church Fathers. Any history by Vedder, Newman, or Schaff will give the reader the clear picture. Water baptism was by IMMERSION, the Scriptures were read and commented on, collections were taken to help poor saints, and there is no evidence in any writing of any church father before A.D. 325 that the [Roman ]{.class_4095}bishop was anything more than a pastor of a local church. Cyprian (A.D. 195--258) and Irenaeus (A.D. 130--202) are the first to distort the Biblical picture of the pastor, and Justin Martyr (A.D. 100--165) and Tertullian (A.D. 160--220) distort the ordinances. But behind these distortions of truth sat the great []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_568}mastermind of dead orthodoxy-Origenes Adamantius. Origen called the pastor a ["high priest!"]{.class_4095}

Early worship services were simple and spiritual. They resembled the meetings held by the early Quakers and Pietists (America and Germany) and the Waldensians (Italy and France). ["The church which is in HIS house"]{.class_4096} (vs. 15) has about as much resemblance to "the church at St. Peter's" as a rose garden resembles a used car lot.

["The epistle from Laodicea"]{.class_4096} (vs. 16) has been thoroughly discussed under Ephesians 1:1 and the prefatory remarks on the Epistle to the Ephesians (which see). The reader may gain some information from page 215 of the [Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095} (ibid., vol. 20), but naturally, this information will seek to bolster the dead-orthodox view of [Marcion the heretic]{.class_4095} (A.D. 160), which view is accepted as [truthful fact]{.class_4095} by the majority of the commentators. (For the other side of the picture, see the last Appendix in [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_4095}, 1970.)

["And say to Archippus"]{.class_4096} (vs. 17). Readers of Revelation 2:6 cannot fail to note that here we have the LAITY exhorting the CLERGY. There is no "conquering the laity" (see [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation]{.class_4095}, Rev 2:6, 1970), here. The Colossians are told to tell Philemon's ["fellowsoldier" ]{.class_4096}(see Phile. 1--2) to watch his step and ["make full proof" ]{.class_4096}(2 Tim. 4:5) of his ministry. (The free reading of [Living Letters]{.class_4095} \[1962, pg. 195\] is so "free" that it tempts one to translate verse 18 as: ["Send me a letter, send it by mail, send it in care of Birmingham Jail. Bye Bye!"]{.class_4095})

Archippus was given a job to do, and the Lord gave him this job exactly as He gave out the jobs in Ephesians 4:11 and 1 Corinthians 12:1--11. No ministry is "fulfilled" until the minister has done what he was supposed to have done and "finished the course with faith" (2 Tim. 4:7--8), having ["fought a good fight"]{.class_4096} (2 Tim. 4:7). (To the prospective young minister who has "received a charge in the Lord," we suggest that he thoroughly ground himself in [Which Bible? ]{.class_4095}\[David Otis Fuller, 1970\], [Believing Bible Study]{.class_4095} \[Dr. Edward Hills, 1969\], and [The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence]{.class_4095}, \[Ruckman, 1970\]. These three books will save a man's ministry---from the standpoint of authority and fidelity---before "Christian education" []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_569}has time to destroy it.)

["Take heed to the ministry"]{.class_4096} is not limited to watching over a flock, writing a book, supporting missionaries, or traveling on evangelistic tours. If you don't take heed to the BOOK that saved you---the Book that told you of your calling and the Book that you were told to minister---you will ["fulfil"]{.class_4096} nothing. And woe be to the dead-orthodox apostate in the Seminary chair who cuts your ground out from under you before you have time to ["take heed to the ministry]{.class_4096}!"

Never forget 1 Kings 13. The lying prophet professed to be "called," but he was called for nothing but [to test a young man's fidelity to the word of God]{.class_4095}! He had no ministry against [the apostasy of his day]{.class_4095} (1 Kings 13:1--5).

["The salutation\...of me Paul\...AMEN"]{.class_4096} (vs. 18). The last verse is self-explanatory and is commented on in Philemon 9, 13, 19; Ephesians 3:1, 13, 4:1; 2 Timothy 2:9; 2 Thessalonians 3:17--18; 1 Corinthians 16:21--24; and Galatians 6:11--18.

We may get one last chuckle from the dead-orthodox apostates if we note the comment of Dr. G. G. Findlay ([Pulpit Commentary]{.class_4095}, Vol. 20). Entirely overlooking the cross-reference (2 Cor. 8:9) to ["Grace be with you"]{.class_4096} (see Gal. 6:18 and Phil. 4:23 with comments), Dr. Findlay tells us that in 2 Corinthians 13:14 we find "the FORMULA in its full LITURGICAL AMPLITUDE"! That is as fine an ending for a Laodicean church member as you will ever read in your life.

None of the "new versions" ([Berkeley, Amplified, Good News, NEB, RV, ASV, RSV, New ASV, Living Letters]{.class_4095}, or Phillips) translate the article before ["grace,"]{.class_4096} although Wuest and the Jehovah's Witnesses try it---naturally it produces a nullity. "THE grace be with you," without the added ["the grace OF OUR LORD JESUS CHRIST be with you"]{.class_4096} (see Phil. 4:23) is only "accuracy" without "legibility." The [King James ]{.class_4095}text has the correct reading, proving (for the hundredth time) that "the translation or omission of the article" is not one of the [AV]{.class_4095}'s faults; [all of its critics do the same thing]{.class_4095}, continually.

Here ends the Epistle to the Colossians. Since no major commentator from Origen to English paid any real attention to the "order of books" in the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible, it is not surprising that []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_570}Ellicott, Lange, Clarke, Dummelow, Bullinger, Williams, Stam, Thieme, Wuest, Robertson, Larkin, Scofield, Lightfoot, Haupt, Lipsius, Tregelles, Trench, Schnedermann, Alford, Aland, Nestle, Findlay, Peake, Ellis, Riddle, Westcott, Luken, Hort, and Meyer missed one of the greatest "nuggets" that ever came up from a New Testament gold mine.

You see, [1 Thessalonians]{.class_4095} follows Colossians. Colossians matched the Laodicean church of Revelation 3, and exactly as a rapture followed [that church]{.class_4095} (Rev. 4:1--2 and comments), [the Rapture here]{.class_4095} (1 Thess. 4:14--17) [follows the Colossian epistle]{.class_4095}!

"Present truth," then, for this period of church history (1900--2009) is the Epistle to the Colossians, if one wishes to be technical about it. But those of us who believe our Bible (instead of some agnostic "original" nobody ever found) know that "present truth" is often found in Job, Proverbs, Exodus, and Genesis. Still, if there is one book in the Bible that [bears directly ]{.class_4095}on the modern philosophical, traditional, sacramental, educational, cultured, safe, respectable Christianity of the Twentieth Century, it is COLOSSIANS---"THE PUNISHMENTS!"

Colossians is the perfect antidote for the modern, feminine, "day-school" type of Christianity, which is devoted to the appeasement of the educational world in which it lives or conformity to the community in which it has "established a testimony." Colossians contains the last warnings the Holy Spirit will ever give to this Twentieth-Century church. These warnings clearly deal with the following:

1\. [Settling down]{.class_4095} in the community (Demas).

2\. Judging the Bible by [tradition ]{.class_4095}(2:8).

3\. Using [philosophy ]{.class_4095}to overthrow Scripture (2:8).

4\. Adopting [a set of standards]{.class_4095} where YOU are the final judge (2:8).

We have known of modern Christians in this century (who were "Fundamental, Premillennial, etc.") who professed to believe in the absolute authority of the Bible and then stated their personal opinions as divine fiats of God's decrees. Every one of them rejected the [AV ]{.class_4095}as an "infallible authority." Every one of them was a social climber, and all of them were dependent on their social reputation for their income. I recall one great "Christian leader" (who was given an honorary doctorate for his work in "Christian education") who used 1 Timothy 6:1--2 (!!!) to get his employees to obey orders. I recall another one who told young people that if they didn't go to the school from which she graduated, they were being "led by the Devil," and that the Lord only led young people to go to "Christian Colleges."

[The Lord led Savonarola to a STAKE. ]{.class_4095}The Lord got Paul as far from a "College" as He could get him.

You can lose your faith in a [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible quicker in the average "Christian" College today than you can at the University of Chicago. The apostasy today is not among [the Liberals; the apostasy today is within the Body of the Lord Jesus Christ]{.class_4095} (Matt. 13:33; Lev. 23:17).

Demas is still with us, and he is no more heavenly-minded now than he was nineteen centuries ago when he deserted the writer of Colossians in the Roman dungeon. The only difference between the modern Conservative Demas and the Postmillennial heretic Augustine is that Augustine believed the world would be converted [before ]{.class_4095}Christ came back and the Premillennial Demas knows better than that. However, [to all practical purposes]{.class_4095}, the modern Demas approaches "the ministry" in this same fashion. He is only using the Bible as a TOOL to present an earthly, literal, visible "testimony." He doesn't believe it any more than some of you do; he only finds it "expedient" to use it and profitable to [profess faith ]{.class_4095}in it. This is the opportunist of 2 Timothy 4:10, and Colossians is written to warn this class of Christians that they are NOT fulfilling the ministry given to them.

The Colossian Christian of this century is boasting about his belief in the "Fundamentals," blissfully unaware of the fact that the first Roman ecumenical council aimed at a Church-State government boasted [in the same things]{.class_4095} (Nicea, A.D. 325). He is also boasting about "plenary" and "verbal" inspiration of the "original," while openly defying the authority of the Reformation Bible which gave him his present position of liberty and blessing. In short, the []{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_572}Colossian Christian of this century is a dead-orthodox apostate who has been deceived by the Devil into thinking that knowledge (1 Cor. 8:1), science (1 Tim. 6:20), and tradition (Col. 2:8) have equipped him so thoroughly that he can now sit in judgment on the only infallible authority left to the Christian. But without this authority, the Christian is abandoned to the Catholicism of Rome, the Communism of Russia, or some pagan god like Education, Culture, Humanitarianism, Philosophy, or Socialism.

It doesn't take a year's research among the "Fundamentalist" literature published between 1900--2000 to see what has happened. Following the fixed cycle of Atheism, Evangelism, Education, Culture, Apostasy, Ecumenicy, Catholicism, Atheism, Evangelism, etc., the Laodicean church finds itself at the point where Education and Culture have replaced the authority of the Bible. For example, when John R. Rice (1970) writes of ["OUR God-Breathed Book]{.class_4095}," he is careful not to mention three terrible truths to the reader.

1\. No one has such a book in their possession, according to Dr. Rice, Dr. Weniger, Dr. English, Dr. Neal, and Dr. Custer.

2\. The word "OUR" must be a reference to the revision committees of 1901 and 1963 ([ASV ]{.class_4095}and [New ASV]{.class_4095}), as the corrupt publications of these men are promoted in the book ([Our God-Breathed Book]{.class_4095}).

3\. The author (Dr. Rice) has never seen any "Our Bible" by his own profession, and [neither have any of his associates. ]{.class_4095}Neither has Norman Vincent Peale or Nels Ferre.

The modern "word of God" mentioned by the leading Conservatives in America is a book that [was]{.class_4095}, not a book that [is]{.class_4095}; and the alteration of tense ("WAS" to "IS") is a perennial lying confession that passes off as smooth as honey on glass; i.e., "We believe the Bible [is ]{.class_4095}the word of God," when what they mean is: "We believe the Bible [was ]{.class_4095}the word of God." (Greek scholars always did have trouble with verb tenses! See Col. 2:17 and comments.)

When one presses these great "contenders for the faith," "bastions of orthodoxy," or "strongholds of Fundamentalism," one finds that their promoters are holding up the [King James ]{.class_4095}Bible as "the best translation" while they are out trying to get young people to attend the INSTITUTION. [Once inside the walls of the institution]{.class_4095}, the [King James ]{.class_4095}[]{#Qv_tx9OySRyw7xPkbX14HQ3906.xhtml_page_573}Bible is attacked with the ferocity and tenacity of a rabid wild cat. When the student leaves the institution, he graduates with the distinct impression that the final court of appeals IS THE INSTITUTION, [not the Bible]{.class_4095}!

Furthermore, charges like this can be brought against every major "Christian" University in the country without fear of any type of "false accusation" or "misrepresentation." All one has to do is talk to the graduates of these schools, and 99 percent of them will bear witness to the truth of the accusation. [They have no Bible.]{.class_4095} They have ["reliable]{.class_4095} translations" that deny the Deity of Christ and the Virgin Birth (like the [ASV ]{.class_4095}of 1901) and ["thoroughly reliable]{.class_4095} translations" that attack the Bodily Resurrection and the Incarnation (like the [New ASV]{.class_4095} of 1963). They have ["original]{.class_4095} Greek texts" which match the official Dark Age "Bible" of the Jesuit Priests ([Rheims]{.class_4095}, 1582), but they have no authority except a faculty composed of sinners who sit in judgment on the Holy Bible 24 hours a day. The final authority in all matters of faith and practice for Bob Jones University, for example, is [the opinions of the Greek professors. ]{.class_4095}In the language of evangelical theology, that simply means APOSTASY.

This is the Laodicean church of Colossians, and it is the last church on earth before the Rapture.
